subject: Need invoice split after transferring users to a new subsidiary workspace
description: We created a separate workspace for our EU subsidiary and moved 22 users out of the parent account. I now need help from both a billing and admin perspective: I want to make sure those users are no longer billed on the original account and I need the invoices separated correctly between the two workspaces.

comments.message: I transferred 22 users from the GlobalOps workspace to the new GlobalOps EU workspace using the admin tools. Right now the old account still shows 85 billable seats, and the new one shows 22. I’m worried we’ll be double charged on the next invoice.

User transfers between workspaces don’t always change committed billing quantities immediately, especially if the source account is on annual billing and the destination is monthly. I can confirm the 22 users now exist only in the EU workspace from an administration standpoint, but I can’t yet confirm the invoice impact.
Can you verify whether the parent account will still be billed for them this month?

I’ve escalated this to our billing operations team because they need to review both contracts and the transfer logs together. In the meantime, I’ve added a note to prevent duplicate invoicing if both workspaces generate charges before the review is complete. You should receive an update within 2 business days.

subject: Configuration des rôles et visibilité des tableaux de bord
description: Je viens de configurer notre espace pour une équipe de 25 personnes et je n'arrive pas à faire en sorte que les chefs d'équipe voient uniquement les tableaux de bord de leur service. En plus, certains utilisateurs standard peuvent encore modifier des vues enregistrées alors que je pensais avoir bloqué cela. J'ai probablement raté quelque chose dans la configuration des rôles, mais la documentation n'est pas très claire pour mon cas.

comments.message: Bonjour, nous migrons depuis un autre outil et je dois former rapidement nos managers. J'ai créé des rôles “Manager Support” et “Agent”, puis limité l'accès à certains tableaux de bord, mais les permissions ne semblent pas se comporter comme prévu. Pouvez-vous m'expliquer la bonne configuration étape par étape ?

Bonjour, oui bien sûr. Pour obtenir ce résultat, il faut configurer à la fois les rôles et les droits sur les dossiers de rapports. Je vous conseille : 1) aller dans Paramètres > Utilisateurs > Rôles, 2) désactiver pour le rôle Agent l'autorisation “Modifier les vues partagées”, 3) placer chaque tableau de bord dans un dossier par service, 4) partager chaque dossier uniquement avec le rôle concerné. Ensuite, vérifiez aussi dans Paramètres > Groupes que les managers sont bien affectés au bon groupe, car la visibilité peut hériter de là.
Merci. J'avais bien fait les rôles, mais pas les dossiers de rapports. En revanche, un manager du service Finance voit encore un tableau de bord RH.

Dans ce cas, il reste probablement un partage direct sur ce tableau de bord. Ouvrez le tableau concerné, cliquez sur Partage, puis supprimez les accès individuels ou le lien public si activé. Pour former vos managers, je vous suggère aussi de tester avec “Aperçu en tant que rôle” afin de valider ce qu'ils verront avant déploiement. Si vous voulez, je peux vous fournir un modèle de configuration recommandé pour des équipes multi-départements.

subject: Aide pour configurer les SLA par priorité et former mon équipe
description: Je dois mettre en place des SLA différents selon la priorité et le type de client, mais je ne comprends pas comment éviter les conflits entre règles. J'ai aussi besoin d'une explication simple pour pouvoir former mes superviseurs sans leur donner de mauvaises consignes.

comments.message: Bonjour, nous voulons 1 heure de première réponse pour les VIP, 4 heures pour les clients standard, et des délais différents selon la priorité. J'ai commencé à créer plusieurs politiques, mais quand je teste un ticket urgent VIP je ne sais pas quelle règle s'applique réellement. Comment configurer cela proprement ?
Bonjour, le plus simple est de créer une seule politique SLA pour tous les tickets urgents. Comme ça, vous évitez les conflits. Ensuite vous pourrez ajuster si besoin.
Mais cela ne répond pas au cas des VIP versus standard. Je dois justement expliquer à l'équipe comment prioriser sans mélanger les deux.

Vous avez raison, ma réponse précédente était trop simplifiée. Pour votre cas, il faut ordonner les politiques SLA de la plus spécifique à la plus générale. Exemple : 1) VIP + Urgent, 2) VIP + Haute, 3) Standard + Urgent, 4) Standard + autres. Le moteur applique la première règle correspondante. Pour la formation, montrez à vos superviseurs comment lire les conditions de chaque politique et faites un test avec 3 tickets exemples. Je vous conseille aussi d'ajouter un champ “Segment client” obligatoire pour éviter qu'un ticket VIP parte dans une règle standard.

subject: La integración con Slack no respeta el canal configurado
description: Configuré la integración con Slack para que las alertas de nuevos tickets lleguen al canal #soporte-latam, pero los mensajes siguen apareciendo en un canal antiguo que usamos para pruebas. Ya eliminé el webhook anterior en Slack, así que no entiendo si quedó alguna configuración guardada dentro de la plataforma o si existe otra regla activa.

comments.message: En la configuración de la integración seleccioné #soporte-latam y guardé los cambios, pero las notificaciones siguen yéndose a #sandbox-alertas.

Gracias por reportarlo. Lo más probable es que exista una automatización heredada apuntando al canal anterior. Además de la integración de Slack, revise Configuración > Automatizaciones y busque reglas con acción "Enviar a Slack".
Encontré una regla vieja, la desactivé, pero aun así algunos eventos van al canal equivocado y otros al correcto.

Entonces probablemente tiene dos conexiones de Slack activas en el espacio de trabajo, una global y otra por equipo. Entre en Integraciones > Slack y verifique si aparece más de una instalación. Si ve una conexión marcada como "Predeterminada del workspace", edite esa instalación y cambie el canal por defecto o desconéctela si ya no la usa. Con eso debería unificarse el destino de las alertas.

subject: I can log in as a delegate but my workflow changes won't save
description: I'm covering for our operations manager while she's on leave. I have delegate access and can open the workflow editor, but any edits I make to the escalation flow fail with a save error. I need to know whether delegates are supposed to be able to modify automations and, if not, what the right workaround is.

comments.message: I can access the escalation workflow through delegated login, review the steps, and even make changes, but when I hit Save it errors out. Is that expected behavior?

Delegated access usually allows review and troubleshooting, but not always ownership-changing actions like saving automation edits. That's intentional in many accounts because workflow edits can have broad impact. Can you tell me the exact error message?
It says, 'You may not publish changes while acting on behalf of another user.'

That confirms it. Delegate sessions are meant for inspection, not for changing live workflow configuration. The cleanest option is for an admin to grant you direct Automation Manager access on your own account for the coverage period, or have the current workflow owner transfer ownership temporarily. To prevent confusion, I also suggest documenting this in your internal runbook so backups know that delegated login is not equivalent to full workflow edit rights.

subject: Need tenant-level admin separation for two subsidiaries
description: Our company recently added a second subsidiary to the same tenant, and we need to make sure the admins from one subsidiary cannot add, deactivate, or edit users in the other. Right now our current user admin roles appear to be global across the whole tenant. If there isn’t a way to scope this correctly, I need this escalated because we may have to change our setup before rollout.

comments.message: We need user administrators from Subsidiary A to manage only their own users, not users from Subsidiary B. Right now the admin permissions seem to apply across the entire tenant.

At the moment, standard user admin roles are tenant-wide unless you use scoped administration through managed groups or a partitioned account design. To make sure we don’t misguide your rollout, I’m escalating this to our solutions team to confirm whether your current plan and configuration support admin scoping by subsidiary. They can review whether managed groups, separate workspaces, or another structure is the right model.

We already use managed groups, but some shared services staff need visibility into both subsidiaries while local HR admins should stay separated. We need something practical, not a generic recommendation.

That nuance is exactly why I’ve sent this to the solutions team rather than guessing from frontline support. I added your requirement for mixed visibility: local subsidiary-only user administration for HR, with broader cross-subsidiary access for shared services. They’ll assess whether your current managed group structure can enforce that split cleanly or whether tenant segmentation is needed, and they’ll follow up with a prescriptive setup.

subject: Usuários sem licença aparecem no relatório de adoção
description: Convidei alguns usuários novos, mas ainda não atribuí licença para todos. Mesmo assim, o relatório de adoção semanal está contando esses usuários pendentes como se estivessem ativos. No painel de administração eles aparecem como 'convidado' e sem assento. Eu preciso que o relatório reflita apenas quem realmente tem licença ativa.

comments.message: Hoje temos 43 usuários com licença e 6 convidados pendentes. O relatório está mostrando 49 usuários ativos na semana.
Olá! Pelo que descreveu, o relatório de adoção pode estar usando a definição de 'usuário provisionado', que inclui convidados criados pelo administrador, mesmo sem licença atribuída.
Então não existe um jeito de ver só os licenciados? Esse é justamente o número que a diretoria pede.

Existe sim, mas não no cartão padrão da dashboard. Crie uma visão customizada do relatório e adicione o filtro 'Tipo de acesso' = 'Licenciado' ou 'Status de assento' = 'Ativo'. Assim os convidados ficam fora da contagem. Se quiser, posso te mandar os campos exatos para montar esse relatório.

subject: HubSpot contacts missing from activity report after reconnect
description: I reconnected our HubSpot integration after reauthenticating the app, and now our weekly activity report is missing a large chunk of contact touchpoints from the last two months. New contacts are syncing, but historical engagements seem incomplete, which makes the report useless for our sales managers. I need to know if this data can be re-imported or if we need to rebuild the report another way.

comments.message: After reconnecting HubSpot on Monday, our 'Sales Activity by Rep' report dropped by about 30%. Calls and emails tied to older contacts are gone, while new records keep coming in normally. Can you help?

Thanks for reporting this. A reconnect should resume sync for supported objects, but engagement history can depend on which scopes were granted during reauthorization. If the reconnect restored contact sync without historical engagement access, reports based on those events would show gaps. Could you confirm whether the app was reauthorized by an admin with full activity permissions?
Yes, our HubSpot super admin did the reauth. We compared scopes and they seem identical to before. The missing data is mostly older emails and call logs from April and May.

That helps. Since current sync is working but older engagement records are absent from reporting, this likely needs backend investigation rather than a front-end report fix. I'm escalating to our integrations team to inspect the reconnect job and to reporting support to confirm whether historical activities were excluded from the dataset after the reauth. I'll attach your report name and date range so they can assess whether a backfill is possible.

subject: Service account can create users but cannot assign them to teams
description: We built an internal onboarding tool that calls your API to create user accounts and add them to the right teams. Account creation works, but the team assignment endpoint returns 404 for the same user IDs that were just created. In the admin UI I can see the users, so I'm not sure whether this is a permissions issue or some delay after creation.

comments.message: The failing request is POST /api/v1/teams/77/members with the new user ID. We usually send it within a second of creating the user.
A 404 generally means the team ID is invalid. Please double-check that team 77 exists in your workspace and that you're using the correct environment.
The team definitely exists. If we wait a few minutes and rerun the exact same request, it succeeds for the same user and team IDs.

Got it — that points to a propagation delay after user creation rather than a bad team ID. Newly created users can take 30-90 seconds to become assignable in downstream membership services. For automation, add a short retry loop on 404 for membership assignment, or poll GET /users/{id} until the lifecycle_status is fully provisioned before adding them to teams. If this delay becomes a blocker, I can log feedback with engineering.

subject: Locked out of admin account after enforcing SSO
description: I turned on SSO enforcement for our workspace and now I can’t get back into the admin account I used to configure it. I’m the only org admin listed in our account. I need to regain access and also make sure we don’t end up with no administrators again.

comments.message: I tested SSO with my company email and it failed because the domain mapping was wrong. After that, password login was blocked as expected, so I’m completely locked out. There are no other active org admins in our directory.

I can help with that. For security reasons, we can’t disable SSO enforcement solely from chat, but I can initiate an emergency admin recovery. Please reply with your workspace URL, the affected admin email, and a screenshot from your identity provider showing you administer the domain. Once verified, we can temporarily lift SSO enforcement or assign a backup admin login.
I’ve attached the workspace URL and a screenshot from Okta showing I’m the super admin for our domain. The affected email is jlee@harborgrid.io.

Thanks, I’ve submitted this to our Account Access team as a priority recovery case. They’ll manually verify ownership and contact you at the same email with next steps. Once you’re back in, please create at least one additional org admin and test SSO on a non-admin account before re-enabling enforcement.

subject: Fusion d’utilisateurs en double et chiffres doublés dans les rapports
description: Après un changement de nom de famille, mon compte a été recréé au lieu d’être simplement mis à jour. Nous avons maintenant deux profils pour la même personne, et les rapports d’activité semblent compter certaines actions deux fois. J’aimerais fusionner ou corriger le compte utilisateur, puis savoir comment nettoyer les données de reporting pour notre bilan mensuel.

comments.message: Bonjour, j’ai changé de nom et l’équipe IT a créé un nouveau compte au lieu de modifier l’ancien. Depuis, mes actions semblent apparaître sous deux noms dans les rapports, et certains totaux sont doublés. Peut-on fusionner mes deux profils ?

Bonjour, oui, nous pouvons généralement désactiver l’ancien profil et renommer le nouveau. Pour les rapports, les données historiques restent souvent attachées aux deux identités, donc il faut vérifier la période concernée. Pouvez-vous m’envoyer les deux adresses e-mail et un exemple de rapport où le doublon est visible ?
Bien sûr : claire.martin@entreprise.fr et claire.dupont@entreprise.fr. Le problème se voit surtout dans le rapport "activité par agent" sur le mois en cours.

Merci. J’ai fait désactiver l’ancien compte et défini le nouveau comme utilisateur actif unique. En revanche, la fusion historique n’est pas rétroactive dans ce rapport standard : les actions passées continueront à apparaître sous les deux noms pour le mois déjà entamé. Pour votre bilan, utilisez un filtre excluant l’ancien utilisateur à partir de la date de bascule, ou exportez les données détaillées pour regrouper manuellement ce mois-ci. J’ai aussi ajouté une note interne pour éviter qu’un troisième profil soit recréé.

subject: Teams calendar integration connected, but meeting links aren't appearing in reminders
description: I connected Microsoft Teams so our customer success managers can insert meeting links into automated reminders. The integration status says connected, yet reminders still go out without Teams join URLs. I need help troubleshooting the setup and understanding whether there is another feature we need to enable for this to work.

comments.message: The Teams integration is green, but our reminder emails still say "meeting link unavailable." We expected the Teams URL to be inserted automatically when a success plan review is scheduled.

A successful Microsoft Teams connection only authorizes the tenant; it does not automatically attach meeting links to reminder templates. You'll also need to enable calendar event enrichment under Automations > Meeting Settings and choose Teams as the default conferencing provider. After that, reminders only include a join URL if the underlying event was created or updated after the integration was enabled.
We turned that on and new bookings now work, but the reminders for meetings already on the calendar are still blank. Is there a way to backfill them?

Existing events keep the metadata they had at the time they were created, so they won't backfill automatically. The workaround is to resave those meetings from the scheduler or update them via the calendar sync so our system fetches the Teams join data again. If you have many affected events, I can escalate a bulk re-sync request to our integrations team.

subject: CSV user import overwrote managers and broke Slack channel routing
description: I ran a bulk CSV update to fix department values in our user directory, but it also changed manager assignments for dozens of people because the file had stale values. Now approval chains are wrong, and our Slack integration is routing notifications to the wrong team channels based on the manager hierarchy. I need to revert the user data if possible, or at least understand the fastest way to restore the previous manager structure without touching unaffected records.

comments.message: A bulk user import accidentally overwrote manager fields. Since then, approval routing and Slack channel targeting are wrong. Can the user data import be rolled back?

There isn't a one-click rollback for CSV imports. You'll need to upload a corrected file with the proper manager assignments, and that should restore the hierarchy used by notifications and approvals. For Slack routing, the integration will follow the current user hierarchy after the next sync cycle.
We don't have a clean file with the old manager values. Is there an import history export or audit log we can use to reconstruct what changed?

Yes — if your plan includes the audit log, you can export user profile changes to see previous manager values and affected accounts. I recommend pulling only the changed users, preparing a corrective CSV, and then forcing a Slack directory resync once the hierarchy is fixed. If you can't access the audit log, we can escalate to see whether a backend snapshot is available.

subject: Scalenie dwóch kont po połączeniu działów
description: Po połączeniu dwóch działów mamy teraz dwa osobne workspace w Państwa systemie, a chcemy zostawić jedno konto i przenieść użytkowników do wspólnego środowiska. Nie wiem, czy da się przenieść ludzi z zachowaniem historii, czy trzeba ich zaprosić od nowa.

comments.message: Mamy dwa konta: Sprzedaż-PL i Obsługa-PL. Chcemy zostawić tylko Obsługa-PL, ale przenieść tam 14 użytkowników ze starego workspace. Czy da się zmienić ich przypisanie bez ponownego zakładania kont?

Dziękuję za szczegóły. Użytkowników można przenieść między workspace tylko częściowo: same konta da się ponownie zaprosić do nowego środowiska pod tym samym adresem e-mail, ale uprawnienia, grupy i historia aktywności nie migrują automatycznie. Jeśli zależy Państwu na zachowaniu konfiguracji, potrzebne będzie wsparcie naszego zespołu wdrożeniowego.
Rozumiem. Najważniejsze, żeby nie utracili dostępu i żeby dało się usunąć stare konto po migracji. Czy mogą Państwo pomóc zaplanować kolejność?

Tak. Proponuję najpierw dodać wszystkich użytkowników do Obsługa-PL, przypisać im role testowo, a dopiero po potwierdzeniu logowania dezaktywować ich w Sprzedaż-PL. Samo zamknięcie starego workspace i ewentualne przeniesienie części ustawień zgłosiłem już do wdrożenia. Otrzymają Państwo osobny kontakt w tej sprawie.

subject: Nie mogę zmienić adresu e-mail admina, a reset hasła nie dochodzi
description: Chcę zmienić adres e-mail głównego administratora z prywatnego na firmowy, ale formularz zapisuje się w nieskończoność. Dodatkowo po tej próbie nie dochodzą wiadomości resetujące hasło na nowy adres. Sprawdziłem spam i nic tam nie ma. Potrzebuję pomocy z samą zmianą konta oraz ustaleniem, dlaczego formularz i mailingi nie działają.

comments.message: Próbuję zmienić e-mail admina na firmowy i po kliknięciu Zapisz mam ciągły spinner. Potem próbowałem resetu hasła na nowy adres, ale mail nie dochodzi. Czy możecie to naprawić?
Proszę wyczyścić cache przeglądarki i spróbować ponownie jutro. Jeśli mail nie dochodzi, to zwykle kwestia po stronie dostawcy poczty.
Wyczyściłem cache, testowane na dwóch komputerach i dwóch przeglądarkach. Problem nadal jest. Stary adres działa, nowy nie. Czy możecie ręcznie zmienić login administratora albo sprawdzić logi?
Niestety nie mamy wglądu w takie logi. Proszę dalej korzystać ze starego adresu e-mail, dopóki system nie zacznie działać poprawnie.

subject: Merge rule update caused company records to combine incorrectly
description: We changed our company deduplication settings from email-domain matching to a custom tax ID field, hoping to reduce false positives. Instead, a batch sync merged several unrelated subsidiaries together because many records had blank tax IDs and apparently fell back to another rule. Now we have corrupted company profiles with mixed contacts and notes. I need to know how the merge logic handled blanks and whether this can be reversed.

comments.message: After switching dedupe to Tax ID, our sync merged companies that definitely should not have merged. Quite a few of those records had no Tax ID populated. Why would blank values match anything?

Blank Tax ID values should not match each other by themselves. If fallback matching remained enabled in your merge configuration, the system may still have used secondary rules like normalized company name or website domain when the primary field was empty. Can you confirm whether legacy fallback rules were disabled?
No, normalized name matching was still enabled as a secondary rule because we were afraid of creating duplicates.

That combination likely caused the unexpected merges. To prevent further damage, disable the fallback rule temporarily and pause the sync. For the already merged companies, we can sometimes restore pre-merge records from audit snapshots, but because notes and associations may have been consolidated, I've escalated this to our data recovery team rather than giving you risky manual unmerge steps.

subject: Former employees still able to reach shared portal after access policy update
description: I updated our portal visibility and user deactivation rules, but it looks like two former employees can still access a shared client portal link after being disabled in the main app. They cannot get into the core workspace, but the portal still loads for them if they had a bookmarked URL. I am worried I missed a configuration tied to authentication.

comments.message: Disabled users are blocked from the main workspace login, but two ex-employees can still open the shared client portal from old bookmarked links. We changed deactivation settings yesterday and expected all access to stop.
If they are truly disabled, they shouldn't have any access anywhere. It may just be a browser cache issue on their side.
One of them sent us a screenshot from today with live client data, so it is not just cache. We do have the portal set to allow persistent sessions for 30 days.

Thanks, that helps. The main app login block and portal session policy are separate. If portal sessions remain valid after user deactivation, you likely need to enable 'Revoke portal sessions on account disable' under Portal Configuration, then manually invalidate active sessions for those users. I recommend doing both immediately and reviewing whether portal visibility is set to authenticated users only.

subject: Zmiana nazwy organizacji nie zapisuje się po połączeniu z CRM
description: Po rebrandingu firmy próbuję zmienić nazwę organizacji i domenę kontaktową w naszym koncie. Formularz zapisuje zmianę tylko częściowo: nowa nazwa pojawia się na górze aplikacji, ale w sekcji rozliczeń i integracji nadal widać starą. Dodatkowo przy zapisie domeny dostaję błąd „request timeout”. Potrzebuję pomocy z aktualizacją danych konta i ustaleniem, czemu synchronizacja się rozjechała.

comments.message: Chcemy zmienić nazwę organizacji po rebrandingu, ale system zapisuje ją tylko w części miejsc. W CRM i w rozliczeniach nadal jest stara nazwa, a przy zmianie domeny czasem wyskakuje timeout.

Dziękuję za zgłoszenie. Czy mogą Państwo potwierdzić starą i nową nazwę organizacji, nową domenę oraz czy integracja z CRM jest natywna czy przez API? Timeout przy zapisie domeny może wskazywać na problem z walidacją lub synchronizacją integracji.
Stara nazwa: Datahouse Sp. z o.o., nowa: Northlane Data. Nowa domena: northlane.io. Integracja z CRM jest natywna, Salesforce. Timeout pojawia się po około 20 sekundach.

Sprawdziłem wstępnie: nazwa organizacji została zaktualizowana tylko w warstwie aplikacyjnej, ale rekord rozliczeniowy i mapowanie CRM nadal wskazują starą wartość. Zmianę danych konta przekażę do zespołu odpowiedzialnego za konto i billing, a technicznie proszę na razie nie ponawiać zapisu domeny, żeby nie tworzyć duplikatów synchronizacji. Załączam też notatkę o timeoutach do zespołu integracji.

subject: Tag normalization rule overwrote source values during import
description: We enabled a new normalization rule for customer tags to force lowercase and merge synonyms, but after today's import a lot of records lost the original segmentation tags we rely on. For example, "VIP-EMEA" and "vip_emea" were collapsed, which was expected, but several unrelated tags also disappeared or were replaced by a default category. I'm trying to determine whether the import mode, overwrite settings, or the normalization rule caused us to lose data.

comments.message: We turned on tag normalization and then imported an update file. Now many accounts only have one generic tag instead of the full tag set from before. Did the normalization rule erase the old values?

Normalization only changes formatting, so it shouldn't remove tags. This sounds more like the import replaced the multivalue field instead of appending to it. Was the tag field import set to "overwrite existing values"?
Yes, the import template is set to overwrite. The file only had the current campaign tag, because we assumed the existing tags would remain and just be normalized.

Then the overwrite behavior is the main cause. The system replaced each record's tag list with only the values present in the file, and the normalization rule standardized what remained. You may be able to recover the prior tag sets from yesterday's export or audit history, then reimport using append mode if you want to preserve existing tags while applying the new configuration.

subject: Quebra na paginação do relatório detalhado após aplicar ordenação decrescente
description: Tenho um relatório detalhado com cerca de 30 mil linhas. Quando aplico ordenação decrescente por data de atualização e avanço para a página 2, o sistema repete parte das linhas da página anterior e pula outras. Em ordem crescente isso não acontece. Como usamos esse relatório para conferência operacional, a paginação incorreta está atrapalhando bastante. Podem investigar?

comments.message: Verifiquei com dois colegas e todos conseguiram reproduzir. O problema só acontece no modo detalhado, não no resumo agregado.
Olá! Obrigado pelo contexto. Isso pode estar ligado ao limite do navegador ao lidar com muitas linhas. Tente reduzir o volume com filtros adicionais para evitar duplicidade visual.
Não parece ser visual, porque os IDs realmente se repetem na página 2 e alguns da sequência esperada desaparecem. Em ordem ascendente, com o mesmo volume, a paginação funciona.

Você tem razão, com essa diferença entre ascendente e descendente há forte indício de bug na paginação ordenada do relatório detalhado. Vou repassar ao time técnico com a observação de que a chave de ordenação por data de atualização pode estar instável entre páginas. Como alternativa provisória, use ordem crescente ou exportação completa até corrigirmos isso.

subject: Cannot reset MFA for locked-out admin
description: Our only other workspace admin got a new phone and is locked out because her authenticator app wasn't transferred. I went into user settings to reset MFA, but the reset option is grayed out for her account. We still need to get her back in without disabling security for everyone else.

comments.message: Our only other workspace admin got a new phone and is locked out because her authenticator app wasn't transferred. I went into user settings to reset MFA, but the reset option is grayed out for her account. We still need to get her back in without disabling security for everyone else.

The MFA reset button is disabled when the target admin is protected by the "admins require verified recovery method" policy and the acting admin has not completed a fresh step-up verification. Please sign out and back in, complete the step-up prompt, then go to Admin > Users > Security and try the reset again. If your tenant uses enforced SSO, the reset may also be blocked until the identity provider confirms she is still in the admin group.
I logged back in and completed the step-up verification. The reset button is active now, and I was able to clear her MFA. She's back in.

Perfect. No global security settings needed to change — it was just the step-up requirement on admin-to-admin MFA resets. I recommend adding a second recovery method for all admins to avoid this scenario.

subject: Workflow action fails only for records created with API token auth
description: Our automated onboarding workflow calls your "Generate Invite" action when a new contact is created. If the contact is created in the app UI, the workflow succeeds. If the same contact is created through the API using a service account token, the workflow fails with "actor lacks permission to execute action." We need to understand how API-created records interact with workflow permissions.

comments.message: We create contacts through POST /contacts with a service account that has write access. The onboarding workflow triggers immediately, but the Generate Invite step errors out. The audit log says the actor is our integration user.

That behavior is expected. Workflow actions that perform privileged operations run under the initiating actor unless the workflow is configured to run as workflow owner. For API-created records, that means your service account's permissions are evaluated. Please check whether the integration user has permission to send invites, or switch the workflow execution context to the workflow owner if your plan includes that feature.
We don't see any run-as setting in this workspace. Is that enterprise-only?

Yes, the run-as-workflow-owner option is only available on Enterprise. On your current plan, the practical fix is to grant the service account the 'Manage Invites' permission or change your process so invite generation is done by a user or scheduled job with the required role.

subject: Need help understanding API rate limit errors in our sync job
description: Our nightly sync to your API started failing with 429 errors, but only on larger customer accounts. I can see from the logs that we burst a lot of requests during the initial backfill, and I’m not sure whether the right fix is retries, batching, or some other pattern your platform expects. Can you help me troubleshoot what’s happening and explain the recommended way to design around your rate limits?

comments.message: Our nightly sync to your API started failing with 429 errors, but only on larger customer accounts. I can see from the logs that we burst a lot of requests during the initial backfill, and I’m not sure whether the right fix is retries, batching, or some other pattern your platform expects. Can you help me troubleshoot what’s happening and explain the recommended way to design around your rate limits?

429 means you are sending too many requests in a short window. The immediate troubleshooting step is to inspect the response headers for remaining quota and reset timing, then confirm whether the spikes happen during paginated reads, per-record updates, or both. Best practice is usually a combination of server-respected backoff, queueing, and reducing call volume: batch writes where supported, cache unchanged records, and spread initial backfills over time instead of firing everything at once. If your client ignores Retry-After headers, you’ll keep hitting the same wall.
We are not currently honoring Retry-After, and our job does one update call per record. We also re-fetch full records every night even if nothing changed.

Then you have at least three likely contributors: no adaptive backoff, a write pattern that amplifies request count, and no incremental sync strategy. I’d recommend changing the job to use updated-since filters for reads, batching writes if you’re using endpoints that support bulk operations, and implementing exponential backoff with jitter using the Retry-After value as the minimum wait. For troubleshooting, test on one large account and log request rate, 429 frequency, and average retry delay so you can confirm the new behavior actually smooths the burst pattern.

subject: Data residency request stuck in approval
description: I submitted a request last week to move our account's hosting region from the US to the EU for compliance reasons. The request has been sitting in "pending approval" for six days and our legal team is asking for an update. I need to know who can actually approve this and whether the migration can still happen before month-end.

comments.message: This is blocking our compliance sign-off. Can support approve the region move or at least tell me why it's stalled?
Region changes are reviewed by our infrastructure operations team, and support can't approve them directly. I don't see any internal notes explaining the delay, but I can reach out on your behalf.
Please do, because we've already provided the legal entity details and expected downtime window.

I've escalated your request to Infrastructure Ops and included your original submission, target deadline, and compliance impact. I wasn't able to get an immediate ETA from them, but this is now in the right queue and they'll update the ticket once they've reviewed migration capacity.

subject: Unable to re-enable suspended user account
description: I suspended one of our sales reps last week while she was on leave, and now I can't get her back into the workspace. I switched her status back to active in Admin, but she still gets an error saying her account is disabled when she tries to sign in. We need her back online today because she owns several open deals.

comments.message: I suspended one of our sales reps last week while she was on leave, and now I can't get her back into the workspace. I switched her status back to active in Admin, but she still gets an error saying her account is disabled when she tries to sign in. We need her back online today because she owns several open deals.

I checked the behavior you described. In our platform, reactivating the user in Admin restores access only after all active sessions and the cached suspension flag are cleared. Please go to Admin > Users > select her profile > Security, click "Revoke Sessions," then ask her to sign out completely and retry in an incognito window. If SSO is enabled, also confirm she is assigned to the application in your identity provider, because suspended users are sometimes removed there as well.
Revoking sessions did it. She was also missing the SSO app assignment in Okta, so we added that back. She's in now.

Glad that worked. The issue was a combination of the suspended-session cache and the missing SSO assignment. If you'd like, I can also share a checklist for temporarily suspending users without breaking access restoration later.

subject: API access lost after converting a member to a light user
description: To save licenses, we changed one of our automation accounts from a full member to a lighter seat type. Right after that, our integration started getting permission errors on create and update calls, even though read requests still work. I need to know if this account change removed API write access and whether we have to switch the account back.

comments.message: We downgraded the user tied to one of our integrations from a standard seat to a light seat this morning. Since then POST and PATCH calls return 403, while GET calls still succeed. Did the account type change remove write scopes?

A lighter seat type can restrict product access and admin capabilities, which often affects what the API can do under that user's context. If the integration relies on that user account directly, the simplest fix is to restore a full seat or move the integration to a service account with the required permissions.
I restored the full seat and writes started working again. Is there a way to keep the lower-cost seat but grant API write access separately?

Not through a separate API-only entitlement on most plans. Write capability follows the account's role and product access, so if that account needs to create or update records through the API, it generally must remain a fully licensed user. If cost is the concern, sales may be able to advise on service-account options for your subscription.

subject: SCIM provisioning created duplicate users and broke Salesforce sync
description: I turned on SCIM provisioning with Okta last week so we could automate user onboarding, but since then we've ended up with duplicate user records for about 18 employees. A few of them now show as deactivated in our workspace even though they're active in Okta, and our Salesforce integration is assigning records to the wrong user profile because the email on one of the duplicates is outdated. I need to know how to merge or clean up the bad user records without losing activity history, and how to stop the sync from creating more duplicates.

comments.message: We enabled SCIM from Okta on Tuesday. Since then, users who previously existed in the platform are getting recreated instead of linked, and the Salesforce integration is pushing ownership to the wrong account. Can you help us clean up the user data safely?

Yes — this usually happens when SCIM is matching on a different identifier than your existing users. Please do not delete anything yet. First, export your user list from Admin > Users so you have current IDs, then check whether Okta is sending a different primary email or external ID than the one stored on the existing records. Duplicate users can generally be merged by our support team to preserve history, while manually deleting them can break integration mappings.
I checked and Okta is sending the employee's new work email, while the older users in your system still have their pre-domain-change email addresses. Is there a way for us to remap them ourselves?

You won't be able to merge those records from the UI. I've opened an escalation for our user operations team to merge the duplicates based on the current and former email pairs you identified, and to restore the deactivated primary records where applicable. To prevent new duplicates, update the SCIM matching field to immutable employee ID in Okta if available, or bulk-update the existing users' email addresses in our platform before re-enabling provisioning for affected accounts. For the Salesforce side, once the merges are complete, re-run the user sync so ownership maps to the surviving user IDs.

subject: Annual plan renewed even though I thought auto-renew was off, and admin login uses an old email
description: I’m trying to sort out an unexpected annual renewal. We intended to switch to monthly, but the account renewed for another year yesterday. To make this harder, the only billing admin on file is my old work email, and I can’t receive the login code there anymore. I still have access to the workspace with my new email as a regular user, but not to billing settings. Can you help change the billing admin login and review whether this renewal can be reversed?

comments.message: I’m trying to sort out an unexpected annual renewal. We intended to switch to monthly, but the account renewed for another year yesterday. To make this harder, the only billing admin on file is my old work email, and I can’t receive the login code there anymore. I still have access to the workspace with my new email as a regular user, but not to billing settings. Can you help change the billing admin login and review whether this renewal can be reversed?

I can help with both, but I’ll need to verify ownership before changing the billing admin email. Please reply with the last 4 digits of the card on file, the latest invoice number, and confirm the domain on your new company email. Regarding the annual renewal, our policy usually allows review within 7 days if auto-renewal settings were unclear.

Last 4 are 2048, invoice is INV-90117, and my new email is on the same company domain as before. Please note we tried to turn off auto-renew during offboarding of our old IT manager, which may be why this was missed.

Thanks, I’ve completed the ownership check and updated the billing admin login from your old address to your new one. You should receive a fresh verification email shortly. I’ve also submitted a billing exception request to convert the renewal from annual to monthly, but that requires billing team approval, so I can’t promise the outcome yet. Your access issue is fixed on our side; the renewal review is still pending.

subject: Archived projects missing from audit export totals
description: I archived 87 completed projects at the end of last month so our workspace would be easier to navigate. Since then, the audit export report is showing fewer total projects than expected, and finance is asking why last month's count changed. I need to understand whether archived items are supposed to remain in reporting and, if not, whether there's a way to include them in the export without restoring everything manually.

comments.message: Our audit export total dropped after archiving projects. We expected archived records to stay in historical reports. Did we misunderstand how archiving works?
Archived projects are excluded from all reports by default, including audit exports.
That seems odd, because your help article says audit exports include active and archived records unless a status filter is applied.

You're right, and I checked further. In your account, the export template has an old hidden filter on Project Status = Active that was carried over from a cloned report. That's why the archived items disappeared after your cleanup.
Can that filter be removed without recreating the whole export?

Yes, I removed the hidden filter and saved a corrected version of the export template. Future audit exports will include archived projects again, and the previous month total now matches your pre-archive count.

subject: Slack notifications stopped after reconnecting workspace
description: After reconnecting our Slack workspace, alerts from your platform stopped posting to our #ops-alerts channel. The integration test says 'successful,' but no real notifications are coming through when incidents are created. I've tried removing and re-adding the channel in the notification settings, but it still doesn't work.

comments.message: We reconnected because our previous Slack app token expired. Since then, the test ping shows up in Slack, but actual incident alerts do not.
If the test ping works, the integration itself is fine. Please recreate the channel and try again.
I already did that twice. The issue is only with live incident alerts, not test messages.
In that case it may be a Slack outage. I'd suggest waiting a few hours and trying again.

subject: Need invoice corrected after ownership transfer and plan downgrade
description: I became the new admin for our nonprofit account and downgraded us from Enterprise to Pro on the same day because we no longer need SSO. The invoice we received still shows Enterprise pricing for part of the month and lists the previous director as the bill-to contact. I need the billing contact corrected and want to confirm whether the downgrade credit was applied accurately.

comments.message: I became the new admin for our nonprofit account and downgraded us from Enterprise to Pro on the same day because we no longer need SSO. The invoice we received still shows Enterprise pricing for part of the month and lists the previous director as the bill-to contact. I need the billing contact corrected and want to confirm whether the downgrade credit was applied accurately.

I verified the ownership transfer completed on the 6th, but the billing profile update didn't sync to invoicing until the next day, so the old director remained on the generated invoice. The downgrade itself was applied mid-cycle, so one partial month of Enterprise plus a prorated Pro credit is expected. I can update the billing contact going forward, but to reissue the finalized invoice with the correct bill-to name I need to involve our accounts team.
Please do that, and can you tell me whether our nonprofit discount stayed in place after the plan change?

Yes, I included that question in the escalation because discounts can behave differently across plan tiers. I can confirm the discount is still attached to your account record, but the accounts team will verify whether the prorated credit used the discounted rate and send a revised invoice if necessary.

subject: Relatório de SLA inclui tickets excluídos e distorce a média
description: Percebi que nosso relatório de SLA da operação está incluindo tickets que já foram excluídos permanentemente por engano no começo da semana. Eles não aparecem mais na busca normal, mas seguem impactando a média de primeira resposta e resolução no relatório executivo. Isso parece um bug sério de consistência de dados. Gostaria de confirmar se vocês conseguem corrigir ou ao menos remover esses registros do relatório.

comments.message: Consegui identificar pelo menos três IDs excluídos que ainda aparecem agregados na média do relatório de SLA. O dashboard piorou logo depois da limpeza que fizemos na terça-feira.

Olá! Obrigado pelos IDs. Esse comportamento não é esperado: tickets excluídos não deveriam continuar sendo contabilizados nos relatórios de SLA após a remoção definitiva. Vou abrir uma investigação com engenharia de dados para verificar se o índice analítico não foi atualizado corretamente. Enquanto isso, se você me enviar os IDs afetados, adiciono ao chamado para priorização.
Os IDs são 45821, 45877 e 45902. Todos foram apagados definitivamente por um admin na terça às 14h, horário de Brasília.

Perfeito, anexei os três IDs e o horário da exclusão ao incidente. Como mitigação temporária, você pode aplicar um filtro excluindo manualmente esses IDs em relatórios detalhados, mas reconheço que isso não resolve o dashboard executivo agregado. Assim que o time confirmar o reprocessamento do índice, retorno por aqui.

subject: Confused about why login-based workflow triggered for test users only
description: I'm trying to learn the workflow tool, and I built a simple automation that should tag accounts after login. It worked for the test accounts I created yesterday, but not for existing users. I need help understanding whether that's expected and what rule I should actually use if I want this behavior across all active users.

comments.message: I made a workflow with the trigger 'user logs in' and action 'apply adoption tag.' It fired for two fresh test users, but none of our existing customers got tagged after logging in today. Is the automation broken or am I misunderstanding the trigger?
That trigger should fire on every login, so it sounds like the workflow may not be published correctly. Try cloning it and republishing from scratch.
I republished it already and got the same result. The audit log only shows the two new users.

I gave you the wrong guidance earlier. I checked the trigger behavior, and in your workspace that event is effectively first-login only for customer portal users, not every session. If your goal is to tag all active users, a login trigger is the wrong tool; use a segment-based automation or a scheduled workflow that evaluates last-seen status and applies the tag in bulk. Sorry for the confusion — I can send a quick example of how to build that.

subject: Import template confusion for products and price book data
description: I’m trying to train a new rev ops hire on importing products, but the template fields are not obvious and we already uploaded one bad file that created inconsistent SKUs. Can you explain the correct order for preparing product data and how to teach someone to validate the spreadsheet before importing?

comments.message: I’m trying to train a new rev ops hire on importing products, but the template fields are not obvious and we already uploaded one bad file that created inconsistent SKUs. Can you explain the correct order for preparing product data and how to teach someone to validate the spreadsheet before importing?

Sure. The cleanest process is to normalize the spreadsheet before import: one row per product, one unique SKU column, consistent currency formatting, and separate columns for list price, status, and product family. If you also use a price book, import the core product records first and the price book entries second so associations map correctly. For training, have the new hire check for duplicate SKUs, blank required fields, mixed currency symbols, and accidental spaces before uploading. A 10-row test import is worth doing every time.
We already created duplicate SKUs. Should we edit them in place or delete and reimport?

If those products are already attached to quotes or deals, editing in place is usually safer than deleting, because deletion can break historical references. If they’re unused test records, delete them and reimport a clean file. If you’re unsure, export the affected products first and compare where they’re referenced before deciding.

subject: Can't transfer ownership after disabling user
description: I disabled a project manager's account before transferring the workflows she owned, and now the ownership transfer tool won't let me select her name as the source user. We need those workflows reassigned today because several automations are failing. Is there a way to troubleshoot this without reactivating her for too long?

comments.message: I disabled a project manager's account before transferring the workflows she owned, and now the ownership transfer tool won't let me select her name as the source user. We need those workflows reassigned today because several automations are failing. Is there a way to troubleshoot this without reactivating her for too long?
The transfer tool only lists active users as ownership sources, so the quickest option is to temporarily reactivate the account, perform the transfer, and then disable it again.
I reactivated her, but the tool still doesn't list her. The account is active again and I waited about ten minutes.

Then the ownership index probably hasn't caught up yet. Open the user profile and make sure she still has the Project Manager role, because reactivation without the original role can exclude her from the transfer list. If the role is correct, wait up to 30 minutes for the owner index refresh, then retry. If you are blocked beyond that, send the workflow IDs and I can hand this off for a backend reassignment.

subject: Sandbox data is different from production after our migration test
description: We ran a migration test from our old CRM into your sandbox, and several record counts don’t match production anymore after a second import. Some contacts were updated, some were duplicated, and a few linked notes are missing. I need help troubleshooting what likely went wrong, but I also want guidance on how to structure repeatable migration tests so we can validate mapping changes without contaminating the sandbox each time.

comments.message: We ran a migration test from our old CRM into your sandbox, and several record counts don’t match production anymore after a second import. Some contacts were updated, some were duplicated, and a few linked notes are missing. I need help troubleshooting what likely went wrong, but I also want guidance on how to structure repeatable migration tests so we can validate mapping changes without contaminating the sandbox each time.

For migrations, the second import often behaves differently if the upsert key changed or if related objects are loaded in the wrong order. I’d start by checking whether contacts were matched on the same external ID both times, whether notes were imported after their parent records existed, and whether duplicate prevention was enabled in the sandbox. For repeatable tests, the cleanest approach is to reset the sandbox or use a fresh one, freeze a source export snapshot, document your field mappings, and import in a fixed sequence: accounts/companies first, then contacts, then activities or notes.
We used email as the match key on the first pass, then switched to an external legacy ID on the second pass after finding email conflicts. Notes were imported in parallel with contacts.

That would create exactly the kind of mismatch you’re describing. Changing the match key mid-test means the second run no longer recognized some first-run records as the same objects, so duplicates were created instead of updates. Importing notes in parallel also risks orphaned or skipped records if the parent IDs are not yet available. For a reliable test cycle, pick one stable unique key before the first load, keep it unchanged across reruns, import dependent records only after parent IDs are confirmed, and either reset the sandbox between runs or tag each batch so you can isolate and compare results cleanly.

subject: How do I train team leads to use saved report filters without giving admin access?
description: We want our team leads to open a shared dashboard and use preset filters for their own teams, but we do not want them editing the report or managing users. I’m trying to set up the right permissions and create a short training note for them. Right now I’m unclear on whether they need a special role, viewer access, or something else to use saved filters properly.

comments.message: The team leads only need to view dashboards, switch between saved filters, and export what they see. They should not be able to edit the underlying reports or change user settings. What’s the simplest setup?

The simplest setup is a view-only role with report viewing and export permissions enabled, but no report editing and no user administration rights. Then share the dashboard folder with a group containing those team leads. If saved filters are already on the dashboard, they can switch between them without needing edit access.
Great — can you give me one or two sentences I can send them as basic instructions?

Sure: 'You have access to view the shared dashboard and select the saved team filters from the filter menu at the top. You can export the filtered results if needed, but any changes you make to filters are temporary unless an admin updates the dashboard for everyone.'

subject: Checklist não salva itens marcados no template do projeto
description: Eu editei o template padrão dos projetos e adicionei uma checklist com 12 itens. O problema é que quando duplicamos o projeto, alguns itens já vêm desmarcados ou desaparecem depois de salvar. Não sei se a configuração do template está corrompida.

comments.message: Isso acontece principalmente quando o projeto é criado a partir do template pelo time de implantação. No editor do template parece tudo certo.
Oi! Você poderia me dizer se esses itens da checklist foram criados dentro de uma seção repetível ou se há automações mexendo nesse template após a criação do projeto?
Sim, a checklist fica dentro de uma fase repetível e existe uma automação que renomeia a fase conforme o tipo de cliente.

Entendi. Já vimos comportamento parecido quando checklists dentro de blocos repetíveis são alteradas junto com automações na criação do projeto. Como isso envolve configuração avançada e possível defeito, vou encaminhar para nossa equipe técnica com o contexto do template, fase repetível e automação de renomeação. Enquanto isso, como alternativa temporária, crie a checklist fora da fase repetível para validar se a perda dos itens para.

subject: Erreur 403 sur l'API Slack malgré l'application reconnectée
description: Notre intégration Slack servait à poster des alertes dans plusieurs canaux privés. Après avoir reconnecté l'application suite à une demande de permissions supplémentaires, certains canaux publics fonctionnent mais les canaux privés renvoient maintenant des 403. J'ai pourtant réinvité l'application dans les canaux concernés. Est-ce que vous gérez différemment les permissions pour les canaux privés ?

comments.message: Bonjour, après reconnexion de Slack, les alertes repartent bien vers les canaux publics mais plus vers les privés. Nous recevons des 403 côté intégration.

Bonjour, oui, Slack traite différemment les canaux privés. Même si l'application est réautorisée au niveau workspace, elle doit aussi être explicitement membre de chaque canal privé. Pouvez-vous confirmer que l'app elle-même, et pas seulement votre utilisateur, a bien été invitée avec /invite dans chacun des canaux concernés ?
Oui, je viens de le faire sur un canal test et celui-ci fonctionne à nouveau. En revanche, deux anciens canaux renvoient toujours 403.

Merci, c'est cohérent avec des références de canaux devenues obsolètes après la reconnexion. Dans Paramètres > Intégrations > Slack, éditez chaque destination en erreur puis re-sélectionnez le canal privé dans la liste au lieu de garder l'ancien ID mémorisé. Les 403 persistants après réautorisation viennent souvent de là.

subject: Preciso restaurar uma versão anterior de um conjunto de registros
description: Eu usei uma automação para atualizar o campo 'fase do onboarding' de centenas de clientes, mas a regra estava errada e moveu muita gente para a etapa incorreta. Já desativei a automação, porém agora preciso voltar os registros ao valor anterior. Não encontrei uma opção de desfazer em massa no histórico. Como posso restaurar isso?

comments.message: Eu usei uma automação para atualizar o campo 'fase do onboarding' de centenas de clientes, mas a regra estava errada e moveu muita gente para a etapa incorreta. Já desativei a automação, porém agora preciso voltar os registros ao valor anterior. Não encontrei uma opção de desfazer em massa no histórico.
Você pode editar os registros manualmente pelo histórico individual de cada cliente.
São mais de 900 clientes. Fazer isso manualmente não é viável. Existe exportação do histórico ou rollback?

Entendo. Não temos um botão de rollback em massa no produto. Se o log de auditoria estiver habilitado na sua conta, nossa equipe pode extrair os valores anteriores do campo e orientar uma restauração por importação ou script. Vou encaminhar o caso para análise porque depende do período afetado e das permissões do seu plano.

subject: Stripe subscription cancellations missing from churn report
description: Our churn report is undercounting cancellations after we connected Stripe. The cancelled subscriptions are visible in the customer records, and the Stripe webhook logs look fine, but they don't show up in the churn dashboard unless I manually edit the record afterward. We need this fixed or at least explained, so please escalate if there's any chance it's a processing bug.

comments.message: Our churn report is undercounting cancellations after we connected Stripe. The cancelled subscriptions are visible in the customer records, and the Stripe webhook logs look fine, but they don't show up in the churn dashboard unless I manually edit the record afterward. We need this fixed or at least explained, so please escalate if there's any chance it's a processing bug.

Thanks for the details. That behavior suggests the Stripe integration is writing the cancellation status correctly, but the churn dashboard may only recalculate when one of the report's indexed fields changes. Manual edits can unintentionally trigger that recalculation, which matches what you're seeing. I don't have a direct fix from support, so I've escalated this to our reporting engineering team with your webhook event samples.
Is there any workaround other than manually touching records? We have too many cancellations for that.

The least painful workaround is to build a custom report on subscription status changed date instead of the prebuilt churn dashboard until engineering reviews the recalculation issue. I've added that recommendation to the escalation and marked the case as month-end impact so it gets prioritized.

subject: Exported contacts missing custom field values
description: I'm exporting our contacts to CSV and the file is missing values for two custom fields we added last month: "Territory" and "Renewal Owner." The fields appear correctly on the contact records in the app, but the export shows those columns as blank for most rows. I tried filtering to just one segment and got the same result. We need this for a QBR tomorrow, so can you check whether this is a bug or if there's something wrong with our data setup?

comments.message: I'm exporting our contacts to CSV and the file is missing values for two custom fields we added last month: "Territory" and "Renewal Owner." The fields appear correctly on the contact records in the app, but the export shows those columns as blank for most rows. I tried filtering to just one segment and got the same result.

I checked your workspace and can reproduce that the export job is returning blank values for those two fields even though the values exist on the records. It looks like both fields were converted from single-line text to lookup fields on May 14, and the export service is still reading the old schema for some records. As a workaround, I refreshed the field index and re-ran the export from our side; the new file now includes the values. I've also logged a bug with engineering because exports should not require a manual reindex after a field type change.

subject: Pflichtfelder im Anfrageformular lassen sich nicht pro Gruppe steuern
description: Ich versuche, unser internes Anfrageformular so zu konfigurieren, dass das Feld "Kostenstelle" nur für die Gruppe Einkauf verpflichtend ist. Im Form Builder kann ich das Feld zwar global als Pflichtfeld setzen, aber nicht abhängig von der Gruppe. Wir müssen das diese Woche live nehmen. Wenn das so nicht geht, brauche ich bitte eine belastbare Aussage und ggf. eine Eskalation.

comments.message: Zur Ergänzung: Wir nutzen den Professional-Plan und haben dynamische Formulare aktiviert. Ich sehe Bedingungen für Sichtbarkeit, aber nichts für Pflichtstatus nach Gruppe.
Hallo, das sollte mit den Sichtbarkeitsregeln eigentlich möglich sein. Wenn das Feld für Einkauf sichtbar ist, ist es automatisch auch verpflichtend.
Nein, genau das ist bei uns nicht der Fall. Sichtbar ja, aber absenden kann ich trotzdem ohne Kostenstelle. Ich brauche eine saubere Antwort, weil unser Go-Live davon abhängt.

Sie haben recht, ich habe das eben in einer Testumgebung nachgestellt. In Ihrem Tarif lassen sich gruppenabhängige Pflichtfelder derzeit nicht separat konfigurieren; nur Sichtbarkeit ist regelbasiert. Ich habe Ihre Anfrage als Produktlücke an unser Konfigurations-Team und den Produktmanager eskaliert. Als Workaround könnten Sie ein eigenes Formular nur für Einkauf anlegen oder die Validierung nachgelagert per Workflow prüfen lassen. Sorry für die Verwirrung.

subject: Wrong person was made owner during acquisition
description: Our company was acquired, and while consolidating workspaces we accidentally set the new owner role on our old IT consultant instead of our internal systems lead. That consultant can still log in and is currently the only owner. I’m an admin and can manage users, but I can’t demote him or assign the owner role myself. We need this corrected urgently and also want his admin access removed afterward.

comments.message: The current owner is tim@externalit.co, which should never have happened. The intended owner is sara.mendez@brightforge.com. I need ownership reassigned and Tim downgraded or removed.

I understand the urgency. Because the only owner is a third party, I can’t change that immediately from a standard support request. I’m escalating this to our account security team for ownership verification and admin-role review. They may ask for a company officer or billing contact to approve the transfer.
Please escalate it. Our CFO can confirm today, and we’d like Tim fully removed once Sara is owner. Is there anything else we should avoid changing in the meantime?

Yes—please do not delete Tim’s account or alter SSO settings until the review is complete, since that can delay ownership reassignment. I’ve opened the escalation and marked it urgent with your requested follow-up to transfer ownership to sara.mendez@brightforge.com and remove Tim’s elevated access afterward.

subject: No puedo eliminar filas del reporte porque siguen apareciendo en el dashboard
description: Eliminé manualmente varias filas erróneas de una tabla de datos importada, pero siguen apareciendo en los widgets del dashboard y en una exportación programada. Ya probé refrescar el navegador y esperar unas horas. Necesito confirmar si el borrado fue real o si hay otra copia de esos datos en el sistema.

comments.message: Eliminé manualmente varias filas erróneas de una tabla de datos importada, pero siguen apareciendo en los widgets del dashboard y en una exportación programada. Ya probé refrescar el navegador y esperar unas horas. Necesito confirmar si el borrado fue real o si hay otra copia de esos datos en el sistema.
Si ya las eliminó, debería desaparecer todo de inmediato. Seguramente su navegador está mostrando información en caché. Le recomiendo borrar cookies y volver a iniciar sesión.
Lo hice en dos equipos distintos y la exportación programada que llega por correo también las incluye. Por eso no parece caché del navegador.
Entiendo. Entonces probablemente el dashboard tarda en actualizarse más de lo normal. Espere 24 horas y debería corregirse solo.

subject: Automation says report delivered but recipients never got it
description: We have an automated workflow that sends a custom usage report to customer success managers when account health drops below a threshold. The workflow activity log says the report email step completed successfully, but none of the recipients received anything this week. I need help determining whether the report generation failed, the automation email step is broken, or if this needs to be escalated to your email team.

comments.message: The health-monitor workflow generated 14 report sends overnight, all marked successful, but nobody on the recipient list got the emails. We checked spam and there is nothing. The attached report link in the workflow log also errors out for me.

I reviewed the workflow logs and can confirm the automation executed the 'Send report' action for all 14 accounts. However, the linked report asset is returning a permission error instead of a generated file, so the email step likely completed without a valid attachment. This points to a reporting permissions problem triggered inside the automation context rather than an outbound email issue.
Can you fix the permissions on your side? These reports are used in our renewal calls today.

I can't change report execution permissions directly from support, so I've escalated this to our platform team as a priority case and included the failing report template ID, workflow ID, and one impacted recipient. As a workaround, please run the report manually from an admin account and export it for today's calls. I'll update you once engineering confirms why automated runs lost access.

subject: Unsure how to teach my team to use tags vs folders for file records
description: We store onboarding documents in your file area, and it’s gotten messy because half the team creates folders by client and the other half tags everything by department. I need advice I can pass along to the team on what structure scales better and how to clean up the existing data without making it worse.

comments.message: We store onboarding documents in your file area, and it’s gotten messy because half the team creates folders by client and the other half tags everything by department. I need advice I can pass along to the team on what structure scales better and how to clean up the existing data without making it worse.

A practical rule is to use folders for stable hierarchy and tags for cross-functional metadata. For example, folders by client or lifecycle stage can work well if users always know the primary location, while tags like Finance, Legal, or Renewal make files easier to find across clients. To clean up, pick one source of truth first — I’d recommend standardizing folder structure — then create an approved tag list and migrate a small batch before changing everything. For team education, publish 4-5 examples of where common document types should live.
Can we bulk retag existing files, or does that have to be done one by one?

Bulk tag editing is available from the file list view if you multi-select records, though moving folders in bulk is more limited. If your library is very large, I’d suggest exporting a metadata report first so you can identify the worst inconsistencies before doing mass changes.

subject: Zmiana adresu e-mail administratora i utrata dostępu
description: Chcę zmienić adres e-mail głównego administratora naszego konta z anna@stara-firma.pl na admin@nowa-firma.pl, bo zmieniliśmy domenę. Po próbie aktualizacji w ustawieniach zostałam wylogowana i teraz nie mogę zalogować się ani na stary, ani na nowy adres. Potrzebuję też upewnić się, że faktury i powiadomienia będą przychodziły już na nowy adres.

comments.message: Dzień dobry, próbowałam zmienić e-mail właściciela konta i system poprosił o ponowne logowanie. Od tego momentu żaden adres nie działa. Czy mogą Państwo przywrócić dostęp i zmienić login na nową domenę?

Dzień dobry, dziękuję za wiadomość. Mogę pomóc ze zmianą adresu logowania właściciela konta, ale najpierw muszę zweryfikować uprawnienia. Proszę przesłać nazwę workspace, obecny adres administratora oraz potwierdzenie domeny firmowej z nowego adresu e-mail. Po weryfikacji zaktualizujemy login oraz adres powiadomień.
Workspace to Northwind Polska, obecny adres to anna@stara-firma.pl. Piszę z admin@nowa-firma.pl i mogę potwierdzić, że mamy dostęp do obu domen, ale stara będzie wyłączona do końca tygodnia.

Dziękuję, zweryfikowałem konto i zleciłem zmianę adresu właściciela na admin@nowa-firma.pl. Stary login pozostanie aktywny jeszcze przez 24 godziny jako zapas, a powiadomienia systemowe i faktury ustawiłem już na nowy adres. Proszę spróbować zalogować się przez link resetu hasła wysłany na nowy e-mail.

subject: Need a cleanup plan for old opportunities before training the sales team
description: We have thousands of stale opportunities that were never closed properly, and before I retrain the sales team I want a cleanup plan that won’t destroy historical reporting. I’d like guidance on what to bulk close, what to archive, and how to explain the rules to reps so we don’t end up back in the same state.

comments.message: We have thousands of stale opportunities that were never closed properly, and before I retrain the sales team I want a cleanup plan that won’t destroy historical reporting. I’d like guidance on what to bulk close, what to archive, and how to explain the rules to reps so we don’t end up back in the same state.

That’s a good use case for a staged cleanup. First, segment stale opportunities by age, stage, and last activity date. In most cases, deals that are clearly dead should be bulk moved to Closed Lost with a standardized loss reason rather than deleted, so they remain in reporting. Archive only if your workspace has a separate archival state for records you truly don’t want in active views. For rep training, define clear exit criteria for every pipeline stage and a rule like: no opportunity can sit untouched for more than 30 days without next steps or closure.
We have some opportunities from a migration that were never real deals at all. Should those still be marked Closed Lost?

Not necessarily. If they were placeholder records from migration and not actual pipeline, I’d separate them with a source flag and handle them in a one-time cleanup so they don’t contaminate sales-loss reporting. If your archive option excludes them from active workflows while preserving auditability, that’s usually better. If you want, I can also outline a sample policy your managers can send to reps.

subject: Required field added to form, but existing bulk updates now fail
description: We made a new custom field mandatory on the account object for all records created from our onboarding form. Since that change, a bulk update process that only modifies account owners has started failing on older accounts, even though it doesn't touch the new field. The error says the required field is missing. I thought the requirement would apply only to new form submissions, not to unrelated data updates, so I need help sorting out whether we configured the requirement at the object level instead of just the form.

comments.message: We added a required field called Implementation Tier to the account setup form. Now our bulk ownership reassignment import fails because old accounts don't have that field populated. Why is an owner-only update being blocked?

If the field was marked required at the object schema level, every create or update operation can validate against it, including imports that only change another field. To limit it to the form, the requirement should be configured in the form layout or submission rules instead of the core field definition.
I checked and yes, the field itself is globally required on the Account object. We only wanted it required for new onboarding submissions.

Then you'll need to remove the global required setting or backfill Implementation Tier on existing accounts before bulk updates will pass. The cleaner option is usually to make the field optional in the data model and enforce it only on the onboarding form. That preserves data integrity for new submissions without breaking maintenance updates on historical records.

subject: Former admin removed, but we're still paying for her seat
description: I deactivated our old workspace admin at the start of this month and transferred admin rights to our COO. Even so, the latest bill still includes her full seat, and I want to make sure she is no longer tied to the account in any billing role either. Can you remove any leftover account access and refund the unused portion?

comments.message: I deactivated our old workspace admin at the start of this month and transferred admin rights to our COO. Even so, the latest bill still includes her full seat, and I want to make sure she is no longer tied to the account in any billing role either. Can you remove any leftover account access and refund the unused portion?

I can see the admin transfer was completed, but the user record is still marked as suspended rather than deleted, which keeps the paid seat allocated on your current subscription. I’ve now converted that seat to unassigned and removed her as a backup billing contact. Since the change happened on day 3 of your billing cycle, I applied a prorated credit of $86.40. The COO is now the only admin and billing contact on file.

subject: Can’t log in after changing primary email address
description: I changed my login email in my profile from my old company address to my new one after our rebrand. Since then I can no longer sign in consistently. Sometimes the old email says no account found, and the new email accepts the password but sends me back to the sign-in screen. I’m confused about which address is actually tied to my account now and whether your email change flow is broken.

comments.message: I can still see automated notifications coming to the new address, so part of the change seems to have worked.

It sounds like the email update may have only changed your notification address and not your login identity. Our system keeps those separate in some plans. Please continue signing in with your old email and use the new one only for notifications.
That doesn't match what the settings page said. It specifically said 'Primary email used for sign-in and notifications,' and now the old email isn't accepted either.

You’re right, and I gave you an incomplete answer. I tested this in a sandbox and found a bug affecting email changes on accounts created before the unified identity update: the visible profile email updates, but the authentication record may not finish migrating. I’ve opened a bug with engineering and attached your case. As a temporary workaround, I’ve sent a secure account recovery link to your new address so you can rebind the login identity. After you complete that flow, please sign in only with the new email.

subject: Locked out after changing primary admin phone number
description: I updated my profile with a new phone number because I changed carriers, and now I can’t complete two-factor login consistently. Sometimes the code goes to the new number, sometimes the old one, and from the admin console my profile page errors out when I try to save any further changes. Since I’m the primary admin, I’m worried I may lock everyone out if this gets worse.

comments.message: I can still get in using backup codes, but only barely. The account settings page throws “failed to load security methods” half the time, and my team wants me to add a second admin today.

Because this involves both access and account profile changes, let’s troubleshoot carefully. First, please avoid making more edits to your security settings. I checked and your account currently has two MFA enrollments attached — one to the old phone and one to the new one — which explains the inconsistent code delivery.
