The settings page error appears to come from that conflict.
Can you remove the old phone enrollment without disabling MFA entirely? I don’t want to weaken the account.

Yes. I’ve removed only the stale SMS enrollment for the old number and kept your authenticator app backup intact. The security settings page should now load normally, and you can add the second admin once you log back in and confirm the new phone number is the only SMS method listed.

subject: Slack alerts stopped after workflow step update
description: I updated one of our approval workflows yesterday to add a conditional branch, and since then the Slack notification at the end never fires. The workflow itself completes, but no one gets notified in the channel. We use the Slack integration with a shared service account, and this was working before the change.

comments.message: The workflow run log shows all internal steps as successful, but the Slack action is marked as skipped even when the condition should be true. Can you help me understand why the integration stopped working after a workflow edit?
It sounds like the Slack connection itself may have expired. Please disconnect and reconnect the Slack integration from Settings > Integrations, then rerun the workflow.
I reconnected Slack and tested again. The message still does not post, and the action is still showing as skipped rather than failed.

Thanks for checking. If the action is skipped, the issue is likely in the workflow logic rather than the integration token. I reviewed similar cases and adding a new branch can reset downstream step conditions to reference the old output path. Please open the Slack step and verify the run condition is pointing to the branch that now ends in Approved. If you want, I can escalate this with your workflow ID so our automation team can inspect the rule configuration.

subject: Merged contacts still referenced by old campaign members
description: We merged duplicate contacts last month, but campaign membership reports still list some of the obsolete contact IDs instead of the surviving records. Clicking through sometimes opens the merged contact, and sometimes gives a not found error. I need to know whether campaign membership data was not remapped during merge and whether reporting on old IDs is now corrupted.

comments.message: We merged duplicate contacts last month, but campaign membership reports still list some of the obsolete contact IDs instead of the surviving records. Clicking through sometimes opens the merged contact, and sometimes gives a not found error. I need to know whether campaign membership data was not remapped during merge and whether reporting on old IDs is now corrupted.

Can you share a campaign ID and two obsolete contact IDs that are still appearing? That will help me check whether the merge redirect exists only at the contact layer or whether membership rows were left pointing to retired IDs.
Campaign ID is CMP-481, and old contact IDs are CT-19044 and CT-19102. Both were merged into surviving contacts on May 3.

I checked those examples. The contact merge redirects are present, which is why one path resolves correctly, but the campaign_member records still reference the retired contact IDs. So this is not just a UI issue; the related membership data was not fully rewritten during merge. I’ve raised this with engineering as a data consistency bug. I don’t recommend editing those campaigns in bulk until we hear back, because it could make the reporting mismatch harder to unwind.

subject: How do I stop new users from seeing everything in our workspace?
description: I’m the new admin for our account and I think I set things up wrong. When I invite people, they can see projects and dashboards that should only be visible to managers. I’ve clicked around in settings but I’m not sure what the difference is between workspace roles, team permissions, and dashboard sharing. Can someone explain the right way to lock this down without breaking access for everyone?

comments.message: I’m especially confused because I made someone a Viewer, but they still saw a shared dashboard link from another team.

Happy to clarify. In our product, workspace roles control broad account abilities, while team permissions control access to team-owned resources. Dashboard visibility is separate again: if a dashboard is shared by link, anyone in the workspace with the link can open it unless link sharing is disabled. To lock things down, I recommend: 1) review each user’s workspace role, 2) move sensitive content into manager-only teams, 3) change dashboards from link-shared to specific users or teams, and 4) disable public link sharing in Admin Settings > Sharing if you don’t want that behavior.
That helps. Is there a safe order to make those changes without accidentally blocking our exec team?

Yes — start by auditing dashboard sharing first, since that’s the most likely source of overexposure. Next, create or confirm the manager-only team and test access with a non-manager test user. After that, tighten workspace roles for anyone who is over-permissioned. I’d also suggest exporting your current member list before changes so you can roll back assignments if needed. If you want, I can outline a sample permission model for admins, managers, and viewers.

subject: Prorated charge after adding temporary users doesn't match admin logs
description: We added 18 temporary support reps for a product launch and removed them three days later. The prorated charge on this month’s invoice looks too high compared with what I see in the user activity logs. I need someone to reconcile the billing math against the actual add/remove timestamps.

comments.message: The users were created on Aug 4 and all deactivated by Aug 7. In Admin > Audit Log, I count 18 temporary users, but the invoice appears to bill us for 21 prorated seats. Can someone explain the difference?
Proration charges are based on the number of days each seat was active, so some variance can happen. If the invoice says 21, that likely includes the temporary reps plus your baseline seat count.
Our baseline is already billed separately on the main subscription line. I’m specifically asking about the proration line item.

Understood. I don’t have enough visibility into the invoice calculation details from support tools alone, but I’ve attached your admin audit export and escalated the discrepancy to billing engineering for a seat-by-seat reconciliation. They’ll confirm whether three additional users were activated outside the list you mentioned or if the proration line is incorrect.

subject: Nowy administrator nie widzi ustawień konta po awansie
description: Nadałem pracownikowi rolę administratora konta, ale po zalogowaniu nadal nie widzi sekcji ustawień i zarządzania użytkownikami. W logach audytu zmiana roli jest widoczna, więc wygląda jakby interfejs albo cache nie pobierały nowych uprawnień. Potrzebuję pomocy z naprawą uprawnień i sprawdzeniem, czemu panel admina się nie pojawia.

comments.message: Awansowałem użytkownika na administratora konta, ale on nadal nie widzi żadnych ustawień administracyjnych. W audycie zmiana jest zapisana poprawnie.

Dziękuję za szczegóły. Proszę o adres e-mail tego użytkownika, godzinę wykonania zmiany oraz informację, czy loguje się przez SSO. Czasami nowe role nie pojawiają się od razu, jeśli sesja nie została odświeżona albo SSO nadpisuje mapowanie grup.
Użytkownik to karol@verdan.pl, zmiana była dziś o 8:52, logowanie przez SSO. Wylogowanie i ponowne zalogowanie nic nie zmienia.

Sprawdziłem i w tym przypadku lokalna zmiana roli została wykonana poprawnie, ale przy następnym logowaniu SSO profil jest nadpisywany przez grupę bez uprawnień administracyjnych. Tymczasowo mogę utrzymać rolę tylko do czasu kolejnej synchronizacji, więc właściwa poprawka wymaga zmiany mapowania grup w Państwa konfiguracji SSO. Na teraz eskaluję zgłoszenie z naszym zespołem technicznym i przesyłam zalecenie, którą grupę trzeba zmapować na admina.

subject: Les données des sous-groupes n'apparaissent plus dans le rapport équipe
description: Je gère une équipe avec trois sous-groupes, et depuis hier le rapport équipe n'affiche plus que le groupe parent. Les agents des sous-groupes ont pourtant bien traité des tickets. J'ai besoin d'un correctif ou d'un contournement, sinon notre suivi hebdomadaire est inutilisable.

comments.message: Bonjour, le rapport équipe n'affiche plus les données des sous-groupes depuis hier. Seul le groupe parent remonte.
Bonjour, avez-vous récemment modifié la structure des groupes ou activé la nouvelle hiérarchie d'équipes ? Les rapports agrégés peuvent changer de comportement dans ce cas.
Oui, nous avons activé la nouvelle hiérarchie lundi soir, mais je pensais justement que les sous-groupes resteraient visibles dans le détail.

Vous avez raison de le signaler. Avec la nouvelle hiérarchie, certains rapports standards basculent en agrégation au niveau parent tant que le backfill des dimensions d'équipe n'est pas terminé, ce qui prend généralement 24 à 48 heures. Comme cela dure depuis hier seulement, je vous conseille d'attendre demain. Si les sous-groupes restent absents après 48 heures, répondez ici et j'ouvrirai une escalade avec votre identifiant de compte.

subject: Admin locked out after enforcing security key login
description: I turned on required security keys for admins this morning. My two other admins had keys enrolled already, but I was using TOTP and got signed out during the policy save. Now I can enter my password, but I can’t complete login because it only offers a security key that I never registered. I’m the one who configured the policy and I’m now locked out.

comments.message: The other admins are in different time zones and not online yet. I need access before our audit meeting.

I checked the audit trail and can confirm the new admin authentication policy was enabled from your account just before the failed login attempts. Unfortunately, support cannot bypass enforced MFA or enroll a factor on behalf of an admin user.
So what am I supposed to do right now?

I’m escalating this to our secure access team for emergency admin recovery guidance. If one of your other admins becomes available sooner, they may be able to temporarily exempt your account from the security key requirement. Otherwise, the recovery team will verify your identity and review what options are permitted under your security settings.

subject: Guest users were given member access after CSV import
description: I uploaded a user list for external reviewers and selected Guest access in the template, but after the import finished, 11 of them were created as full Members. I need those accounts corrected without losing the documents they've already commented on. I also want this reviewed quickly because it affects our license count.

comments.message: I tried editing one manually, but the role dropdown is greyed out for imported users in our plan.

Thanks for the details. Since imported accounts on your plan can't be role-changed in the UI, this needs a support-side review. I can escalate the 11 wrongly provisioned users to our provisioning team to see whether they can be converted from Member to Guest without removing their existing comments and document access. Please send a CSV or list of the affected email addresses.
I've attached the list of 11 emails and the original import file.

Received. I've escalated the case with both attachments and called out the need to preserve existing document comments while correcting the user type and license impact. Our provisioning team will review whether an in-place conversion is possible on your subscription.

subject: Le rapport SLA hebdomadaire exclut des tickets sans raison
description: Depuis lundi, notre rapport SLA hebdomadaire affiche un taux de 96 %, alors qu'en ouvrant le détail il manque plusieurs tickets prioritaires créés par e-mail. J'ai vérifié les statuts et les tags, ils devraient être inclus. Nous devons envoyer ce rapport à notre client final et le chiffre est faux.

comments.message: Bonjour, le rapport SLA hebdomadaire ne prend pas en compte certains tickets prioritaires reçus par e-mail. Le pourcentage affiché est donc incorrect.

Bonjour, merci pour ces précisions. Les rapports SLA peuvent exclure des tickets si le calendrier métier associé a changé ou si le rapport est filtré sur la date de résolution plutôt que la date de création. Pouvez-vous partager un ou deux identifiants de tickets manquants ?
Oui, par exemple les tickets #54821 et #54902. Ils sont bien en priorité haute et ont été créés mardi matin par e-mail.

Je vois le problème: ces tickets sont rattachés à une politique SLA mise à jour mercredi, et votre rapport est basé sur "politique SLA actuelle" au lieu de "politique appliquée au moment de l'événement". C'est pour cela que les lignes disparaissent du détail. Je n'ai pas la main pour corriger ce paramètre côté backend, donc j'escalade au support produit avec vos exemples. En attendant, créez une copie du rapport et choisissez la dimension historique de politique SLA si elle apparaît dans votre compte bêta.

subject: Invoice still includes deleted users from a bulk offboarding
description: I removed 14 former sales reps in a CSV user update before renewal, but the renewal invoice still billed us for 28 seats instead of 14. I may have deactivated them incorrectly because some records were only marked inactive in our employee export. Can someone clarify what data state actually affects billing and how to offboard users properly in bulk?

comments.message: Our finance team is asking why we paid for seats we don't use. I uploaded a user file last week and set status to inactive for the reps who left. The invoice still shows 28 paid seats. Did the data import fail?
Inactive users are basically removed, so they should not count. It sounds like billing may just need time to refresh. Please wait 24 hours and check again.
It has already been 6 days, and I can still see the inactive reps under user management. Also, our invoice is finalized. We need the exact rule here.

I reviewed this more closely and I gave you an incomplete answer earlier. In your plan, seats are billed for users in either Active or Inactive status; only Deactivated users stop billing. Your CSV changed the profile status field, but not the billable seat state. To offboard in bulk, use the 'Deactivate user' action in the admin export/import tool or the SCIM deprovision endpoint. Because the renewal already closed, I can't edit the invoice directly, but I've escalated it to Billing to review whether a credit is possible based on the offboarding date.

subject: Invoice includes SSO add-on after turning off SAML login
description: We switched our team back to email/password login and disabled the SAML configuration in the authentication settings before renewal. Despite that, we were still billed for the SSO add-on this cycle. If the integration is off in the product, why are we still paying for it?

comments.message: The IdP metadata was removed and SAML is disabled in our auth settings. We haven't used SSO since mid-month, but the renewal invoice still includes it.
If SAML is disabled in the authentication page, the SSO add-on should usually stop billing as well. It sounds like the invoice may just need to be regenerated.
Can you regenerate it then? I don't want to pay for something we intentionally turned off.

At the moment I can't regenerate invoices from support. Please try re-enabling and disabling SAML once more in case the status didn't sync, and if the next invoice still shows the add-on, reply back. Billing should sort itself out after the config refresh.

subject: Okta SSO integration loops back to login
description: We enabled Okta SSO for our users, but after entering credentials in Okta they get redirected back to your login page instead of being signed in. This only started after we changed the app from test to production in Okta. I need help troubleshooting whether our SAML settings are mismatched.

comments.message: A few users can still log in with password, but SSO users are stuck in a loop. Our ACS URL and audience value were copied from your setup guide, although we may have multiple environments mixed up.

A login loop after moving from test to production often points to the IdP sending assertions to the wrong ACS URL or using the wrong audience/entity ID for the target workspace. Please compare the production values in our SSO setup page with the current Okta app settings, especially the ACS URL, audience URI, and default relay state. Also confirm that the users are assigned to the production Okta app.
We found the ACS URL was still pointing to our sandbox subdomain. After updating it, some users can sign in, but others get 'user not found'.

That means the integration path is mostly fixed. The remaining 'user not found' errors usually happen when the NameID or email attribute sent by Okta doesn't match an existing user record in the production workspace. Check the SAML attribute statement for email/username and confirm those users exist in the correct workspace or have JIT provisioning enabled.

subject: Invited user never receives magic link and can't access dashboard
description: I'm trying to onboard a contractor into our workspace. She gets the invitation email, but when she clicks the magic link it opens your site and immediately says the link is invalid or expired. We resent it three times and tried different browsers. She still can't get in. I'm not sure whether this is a login problem or a bad invite flow, but I need her account active before tomorrow.

comments.message: For context, she was previously added to another workspace under the same email last year, but that workspace was deactivated. Could that matter?

Yes, that could matter. If the email was attached to an older inactive org, the invite may be colliding with an existing identity record. As a first step, ask her to use the "Sign in with password" option instead of the magic link and choose "Forgot password." That often reactivates access.
She doesn't have a password option on the screen, only the emailed link. Also, our workspace is configured for passwordless login only.

Understood. In that case I checked and there is a known issue where passwordless invites can fail for emails tied to archived tenants. I manually cleared the stale invite token on our side and generated a fresh invitation. Please have her use the newest email only, preferably in an incognito window. If she gets in, I'd also recommend removing any duplicate user record from your deactivated workspace so future invites don't hit the same edge case.

subject: Audit export API does not show who changed user permissions
description: We're investigating an internal access issue and need to know who changed a user's permissions last Friday. I can pull user records from the API and see the current role, but I can't find which admin made the change or which endpoint captured it. We also need a repeatable way to query these permission changes for future reviews. Where is that data exposed?

comments.message: I looked at the user object and the general activity endpoint, but neither clearly identifies the actor for role changes. This is specifically about admin-driven changes to user access.

The user API itself only shows current state, not the actor history. For administrative permission changes, you’ll want the audit logs endpoint with the category filtered to user_admin or role_change, depending on your account version. Those records include the acting admin, target user, timestamp, and before/after values for roles in most plans. If your token can read users but not audit data, make sure it also has audit:read scope.
I don't think our token has audit:read today, which may be why we missed it. We'll try the audit logs endpoint with that category filter.

That should be the right source. If the role_change category doesn’t appear in your tenant, use user_admin and filter events where the changed fields include role or permissions. Reach back out if you want help building the query.

subject: Scheduled reports are arriving with yesterday's numbers
description: I'm seeing something odd with our daily scheduled KPI report. The email goes out at 6:00 AM as expected, but the revenue and new signup totals look like they stop around midnight UTC instead of reflecting the full prior business day in Pacific time. If I run the same report manually later in the morning, the numbers are different. Can you check whether the scheduler is using the wrong time zone, and also explain how scheduled reports determine the date range?

comments.message: Our finance team noticed the mismatch three days in a row. The report is set to 'Yesterday' and scheduled for 6:00 AM Pacific. Manual runs after 9:00 AM Pacific show higher totals. I'd like to know if this is a bug or if we're misunderstanding how 'Yesterday' works in scheduled emails.

I checked your account settings and I can reproduce the behavior. Scheduled reports currently evaluate relative date filters like 'Yesterday' using UTC at send time, while manual runs use the workspace time zone in the report UI. That explains why the emailed version can exclude late Pacific-time activity. I've logged this as a product bug with our reporting team.

As a workaround, please change the filter from the relative preset 'Yesterday' to a fixed rolling window based on your business hours, or schedule the email after 8:00 AM Pacific so the UTC day boundary has passed. Also, to clarify the feature behavior: scheduled reports resolve relative dates at the moment the job runs, not when the recipient opens the email. Once engineering ships a fix, scheduled and manual runs should align.

subject: Need a beginner-friendly explanation of custom report access
description: I’m onboarding two new workspace admins and they keep asking why creating a report isn’t the same as sharing it. They can build reports, but some users still can’t see them unless I step in. I know this has something to do with user groups and report folders, but I need a simple, accurate explanation plus the exact admin settings they should review.

comments.message: Can you help me explain the difference between report creation rights and report visibility? One of our admins created a dashboard for QA leads, but the QA leads can’t see it even though the admin can.

Of course. Creating a report means the admin has permission to build content, but visibility is separate and usually controlled by where the report is saved and which users or groups have access to that folder/dashboard. So an admin can create something successfully, but end users still won’t see it until the content is shared to the right audience.
What should the admins check first when this happens?

Have them check three things in order: the user’s role includes report viewing, the dashboard/report is saved in a folder shared with that audience, and the intended users are in the correct group or team. A simple way to teach it is: create permission lets you build; share permissions and audience membership let others see it.

subject: Jira integration brings in tickets, but SLA breach report is empty
description: Our Jira integration is pulling issues into the platform, and I can see them in the records view, but the SLA breach report shows zero rows for the entire month. That's definitely wrong because we had multiple overdue tickets. We added the integration specifically for reporting, so can you help troubleshoot and escalate if there is a known issue?

comments.message: Our Jira integration is pulling issues into the platform, and I can see them in the records view, but the SLA breach report shows zero rows for the entire month. That's definitely wrong because we had multiple overdue tickets. We added the integration specifically for reporting, so can you help troubleshoot and escalate if there is a known issue?
SLA reports only work for tickets created natively in our app. Imported Jira issues are not supported there, so there isn't really anything to troubleshoot.
Your setup guide says Jira fields can be used in custom reports, and we specifically mapped due date and resolved date. Are you saying the documentation is wrong, or just this built-in report?

Thanks for clarifying. I was referring to the built-in SLA breach report, which does not currently evaluate imported Jira issue timestamps the same way as native tickets. Custom reports can use those mapped fields, but the built-in SLA widget won't. I've escalated your account to product support because the documentation could be clearer, and they can confirm whether your use case matches a known limitation or a bug.

subject: Deprovisioning automation removed access but left orphaned tasks
description: We set up an automated offboarding flow so when a user is deactivated it revokes app access, transfers ownership, and closes outstanding approvals. The access removal works, but for a subset of deactivated users their open tasks remain assigned to them and block downstream workflows. We recently changed both our user deactivation policy and the task reassignment rules, so I need help untangling where the gap is.

comments.message: The workflow trigger is 'when user status changes to deactivated.' Action 1 disables login, action 2 reassigns open tasks to the manager, action 3 cancels pending approvals. For sales users it works, but for service users the task reassignment step shows '0 records updated' even though they had tasks open.

I checked the pattern you described, and a common cause is that service tasks may belong to a different object type than the generic reassignment action targets. If your recent product configuration narrowed reassignment to 'owned business records' only, workflow tasks might be excluded. Also, if the deactivated service users no longer have a manager value at the moment the workflow runs, the reassignment destination would be blank. Can you confirm whether service users still have managers assigned and whether the action is scoped to tasks as well as records?
You're onto something. The action is scoped to records only — not tasks — because we copied it from another workflow. Service users do still have managers.

Then the issue is in the workflow configuration, not the deactivation itself. Add a task reassignment action or expand the existing step to include task objects, and keep the manager-based destination. Since User Administration is already providing a valid manager, the tasks should transfer correctly once the automation includes that object type.

subject: Namensänderung eines Benutzers wird in alten Tickets nicht übernommen
description: Eine Kollegin hat geheiratet und ich habe ihren Namen in der Benutzerverwaltung aktualisiert. In neuen Bereichen wird der neue Name angezeigt, aber in älteren Tickets und @Erwähnungen steht weiterhin der alte Name. Ist das so gewollt oder hängt die Profilaktualisierung irgendwo fest?

comments.message: Es geht uns vor allem darum, dass Kollegen sie in alten Vorgängen noch unter dem alten Namen suchen müssen.

Das Verhalten kann gemischt sein: Das Profil selbst wird aktualisiert, aber historische Ticketereignisse und bestehende Erwähnungen werden aus Audit-Gründen oft nicht rückwirkend umgeschrieben. In Suchvorschlägen und neuen Kommentaren sollte jedoch der neue Anzeigename erscheinen.
Kann ich irgendwo einen Reindex anstoßen, damit die Suche schneller den neuen Namen bevorzugt?

Einen manuellen Reindex können Sie als Admin normalerweise nicht selbst auslösen. Die Suchindizes aktualisieren sich automatisch, teils mit Verzögerung. Wenn der alte Name auch in neu erstellten Erwähnungen oder der aktuellen Benutzersuche morgen noch Vorrang hat, melden Sie sich bitte mit der Benutzer-ID, dann lassen wir den Index gezielt prüfen.

subject: Abhängiger Workflow-Schritt wird nach Aufgabenabschluss nicht fortgesetzt
description: Wir haben einen internen Prozess, bei dem nach Abschluss einer Aufgabe automatisch ein Folge-Task und eine Slack-Benachrichtigung erzeugt werden sollen. Seit zwei Tagen endet der Workflow aber nach dem ersten Task, obwohl dieser sauber abgeschlossen wird. Es betrifft mehrere Benutzer. Ich finde im Ablauf keinen Fehlerhinweis.

comments.message: Sobald die Aufgabe als erledigt markiert wird, sollte die nächste Aktion starten. Im Run-Log steht danach aber nur "wartet", ohne weitere Details.

Danke. Das Verhalten sehen wir öfter, wenn der Workflow auf einen bestimmten Abschlussstatus wartet, zum Beispiel "Done", Ihre Nutzer aber inzwischen "Erledigt" oder einen benutzerdefinierten Status verwenden. Können Sie prüfen, welcher genaue Statuswert im Automationsschritt hinterlegt ist?
Tatsächlich wartet der Schritt auf "Done", wir haben die Statuswerte letzte Woche lokalisiert und nutzen jetzt "Erledigt".

Dann ist die Ursache gefunden. Aktualisieren Sie die Bedingung im abhängigen Schritt auf den neuen Status "Erledigt" oder verwenden Sie, falls verfügbar, die interne Status-ID statt des angezeigten Texts. Danach sollten Folge-Task und Slack-Benachrichtigung wieder ausgelöst werden.

subject: SSO-Rollen greifen nach Umstellung nicht mehr
description: Ich habe gestern unsere SSO-Konfiguration und die Rollenzuordnung für zwei Teams angepasst. Seitdem werden neue Benutzer zwar erfolgreich angelegt, landen aber immer mit der Standardrolle "Viewer" statt in den konfigurierten Gruppen. Ich habe die Attribut-Mappings mehrfach geprüft, finde aber keinen offensichtlichen Fehler. Das blockiert gerade unser Onboarding für 40 Kollegen. Bitte dringend prüfen.

comments.message: Wir haben Azure AD als IdP. Vor der Änderung hat die Zuordnung über das Feld "department" funktioniert, jetzt scheint Ihre Plattform den Wert zu ignorieren. Können Sie nachsehen, ob auf Mandantenebene etwas falsch konfiguriert ist?

Danke für die Details. Ich habe mir Ihre Mandantenkonfiguration angesehen und sehe, dass die Regel für die Rollenzuordnung zwar aktiv ist, aber noch auf das alte Attribut "dept" zeigt, während Ihr aktuelles Mapping "department" sendet. Bitte ändern Sie unter Admin > Sicherheit > SSO > Rollenregeln das Quellattribut auf "department" und speichern Sie die Regel neu.
Das habe ich gemacht. Bei einem Testnutzer funktioniert es jetzt, aber bestehende Benutzer bleiben weiterhin Viewer. Müssen wir jede Person manuell neu provisionieren?

Für bereits angelegte Benutzer werden Rollenregeln nicht rückwirkend angewendet. Ich habe Ihren Fall deshalb an unser Provisioning-Team eskaliert, damit geprüft wird, ob wir für Ihren Tenant eine erneute Synchronisierung anstoßen können. Bis dahin können Sie betroffene Benutzer entweder neu synchronisieren oder die Rolle manuell anpassen. Ich melde mich, sobald ich vom Team Rückmeldung habe.

subject: Campo removido ainda aparece no schema da API
description: Removemos um campo personalizado do módulo de oportunidades há três dias, mas ele ainda aparece no endpoint de metadata e nosso pipeline continua tentando preencher esse campo, gerando erros de mapeamento. Também precisamos limpar os valores antigos desse campo no data mart para manter o catálogo coerente. Gostaria de entender o tempo de propagação da remoção e se existe alguma forma de invalidar cache do schema.

comments.message: O campo não aparece mais na interface, só na API de metadata. Nosso ETL faz refresh de schema a cada 6 horas.
Olá! Alterações de schema costumam propagar rápido, mas caches podem durar algumas horas. Tente forçar uma nova autenticação do token e refazer a chamada sem cache no seu lado.
Já fizemos isso ontem e hoje de manhã. O campo continua vindo com status active.

Entendi. Isso sugere que a deleção do metadata não concluiu corretamente no backend. Vou escalar para nossa equipe responsável pelo catálogo de campos para verificar o resíduo no schema. Como workaround, filtre esse field_id na sua ingestão até a correção e marque a coluna correspondente no data mart como descontinuada para evitar novos preenchimentos.

subject: No puedo autorizar la integración con Google Drive
description: Al intentar conectar Google Drive, después de aceptar permisos Google me devuelve a su aplicación pero aparece una pantalla en blanco y la integración queda como "pendiente". Ya borré cookies, probé en incógnito y con dos cuentas distintas. Necesitamos adjuntar archivos desde Drive hoy mismo.

comments.message: En la consola del navegador veo un error que menciona redirect_uri_mismatch, por si ayuda.

Sí, eso ayuda mucho. Un redirect_uri_mismatch suele indicar que la URL de retorno configurada para OAuth no coincide con la que está enviando la aplicación. Para descartar algo local, ¿me confirma si están entrando desde su dominio personalizado o desde nuestro dominio estándar?
Estamos entrando desde nuestro dominio personalizado, app.empresa-central.com.

Gracias. Encontré el problema: su espacio de trabajo todavía tenía registrada la URL antigua de callback del dominio estándar, por eso Google aprobaba el acceso pero no completaba la vuelta. Ya actualicé la configuración del conector para su dominio personalizado. Cierre la ventana pendiente y vuelva a iniciar la autorización; si todo sale bien, Drive debería pasar a "conectado" en menos de un minuto.

subject: Automation stopped after SSO login change
description: I changed our team over to Google SSO on Monday, and since then our lead-routing automation has stopped assigning new records. Some users also get bounced back to the login screen when they try to open the automation settings. I need to understand whether this is a permissions issue or if the workflow itself broke during the auth change.

comments.message: After we enabled Google SSO, only admins can get into the workflow editor consistently. Sales managers keep hitting the login page again, and meanwhile the round-robin assignment workflow is no longer firing on new leads. Can you explain what changed and how to fix it?

What likely changed is that SSO created a mismatch between your identity provider groups and the roles in our app. The workflow is still published, but if the service account that owns it lost access during the login migration, the trigger can fail. Please ask an admin to go to Settings > Access Control, confirm Sales Managers still have the Automation Editor permission, and in Workflow Automation open the lead-routing flow to check whether the owner shows as inactive.
I checked that. The owner listed on the workflow is a former employee whose account was deactivated when we switched to SSO. Sales Managers also only have Viewer now, which I did not notice before.

That explains both symptoms. Please have an admin reassign the workflow owner to an active user or a shared automation service account, then restore the Sales Manager role to include Automation Editor if they need to manage the flow. Once the owner is active again, republish the workflow and test with a new lead. I also recommend mapping your IdP group to the correct in-app role so users do not get sent into a login loop when trying to access restricted pages.

subject: Can someone walk me through importing contacts from CSV?
description: I’m trying to migrate our customer list from a spreadsheet into your platform and I’m nervous about messing it up. The import page shows field mapping options, deduplication settings, and an overwrite checkbox, but I don’t really understand the consequences. I need a beginner explanation of the safest way to do the first import.

comments.message: My biggest worry is creating duplicates or overwriting good data we already entered manually.

A safe first import is definitely possible. The simplest approach is to start with a small test file — maybe 10 records — before importing your full spreadsheet. During mapping, make sure one column contains a stable unique value like email address or customer ID. That’s what the system uses for deduplication. If you’re unsure, leave overwrite turned off for the first pass so existing records won’t be replaced by CSV values. After the test import, review a few records to confirm fields landed where you expected.
If one of my CSV rows has the same email but a different company name, what happens?

Usually, with deduplication by email, that row would match the existing contact instead of creating a second one. Whether the company name changes depends on whether overwrite is enabled and whether company is mapped as an updatable field. Since you’re being cautious, I’d suggest: test with one known duplicate, keep overwrite off, and verify the import summary before doing the full upload. If you want, I can also give you a sample CSV template with recommended columns.

subject: Round-robin assignment broke after adding a new queue
description: I added a new SMB queue and updated our round-robin automation so incoming chats should assign to the next available agent within the selected queue. Since that change, chats in the Enterprise queue are all going to the same rep while SMB seems fine. I may have changed something in the queue configuration that affected the shared assignment workflow.

comments.message: Before adding SMB, Enterprise round-robin was balanced. Now every Enterprise chat is assigned to Dana. The workflow chooses queue based on customer segment, then uses round-robin assign.

It sounds like the queue order reset. Please go to Product Configuration > Queues and make sure all Enterprise agents are active and eligible for chat, then inspect the round-robin node in the workflow. If the assignment action is set to "last assigned in selected pool," adding a new queue can sometimes rebuild the pool membership. Re-saving the Enterprise queue membership usually fixes it.
All Enterprise agents are active. I re-saved membership and tested three chats; they still all went to Dana.

Thanks for checking. I’m not confident this is a simple config issue anymore. There may be a defect in how the round-robin cursor was recalculated after the new queue was added. I’ve escalated this to our automation team with your findings; in the meantime, a temporary workaround is to switch Enterprise to load-balanced assignment or recreate the Enterprise queue and point the workflow to the new queue ID.

subject: No puedo restaurar registros archivados en lote
description: Archivamos por error unos 900 registros de oportunidades usando una automatización mal configurada. Ahora puedo restaurarlos de uno en uno, pero no encuentro ninguna acción masiva para traerlos de vuelta. Además, algunos dejaron de aparecer en listas relacionadas con cuentas activas. ¿Hay un modo de desarchivar en lote sin recrearlos manualmente?

comments.message: Archivamos por error unos 900 registros de oportunidades usando una automatización mal configurada. Ahora puedo restaurarlos de uno en uno, pero no encuentro ninguna acción masiva para traerlos de vuelta. Además, algunos dejaron de aparecer en listas relacionadas con cuentas activas. ¿Hay un modo de desarchivar en lote sin recrearlos manualmente?
Sí, debería poder seleccionarlos en la vista de archivados y usar "Restaurar" en acciones masivas. Si no aparece, pruebe desde la vista clásica en lugar de la nueva interfaz.
No aparece en ninguna de las dos vistas. Solo me deja eliminar permanentemente o abrir cada registro.

Gracias por confirmarlo. Entonces es probable que su rol o su plan no tengan restauración masiva habilitada para oportunidades archivadas, aunque la restauración individual sí esté permitida. Para no arriesgar relaciones con cuentas y pipelines, no le recomiendo recrearlas. Puedo escalar una restauración por backend si me envía el ID de la automatización y, si es posible, una exportación de los registros archivados por error.

subject: Need account owner changed but API client secrets must stay active during merger
description: Our company was acquired, and we need to change the legal account owner and primary administrator to the parent company's admin team. We have several API clients in production, and leadership wants assurance that the existing client IDs and secrets won't be invalidated during the transition. I need written guidance and, if needed, an escalation because this is a regulated change window.

comments.message: We're preparing an ownership transfer due to a merger. Before we make the change, can you confirm whether existing API clients and secrets remain valid, and whether any clients tied to individual users need to be migrated first?

In general, workspace-owned API clients remain valid through an account owner change, but user-owned clients may lose manageability or fail if the underlying user is deactivated. From what I can see, you have both types: two workspace clients and one legacy user-owned OAuth app. Because this is a regulated change and you need formal confirmation, I'll escalate to our account transitions team to review the tenant and provide a migration plan.
Please do. We also need to know whether we can complete the legal owner update first and migrate the legacy app afterward, or if that order is risky.

I've opened the escalation and documented that sequencing question specifically. My recommendation until they respond is to postpone deactivating any user tied to active API clients, but the transitions team will confirm the safest order of operations and any temporary safeguards they can apply during the change window.

subject: Zmieniliśmy domenę i część użytkowników ma zduplikowane konta
description: Po zmianie firmowej domeny z @alto.pl na @alto.eu kilku pracownikom utworzyły się nowe profile zamiast podmiany starego adresu. Teraz mają po dwa konta, a w jednym są stare zgłoszenia i uprawnienia. Chcemy to uporządkować bez utraty danych.

comments.message: Dzień dobry, po zmianie domeny i ponownym zaproszeniu zespołu część osób ma podwójne konta. Na przykład j.krawiec@alto.pl i j.krawiec@alto.eu istnieją równolegle. Jak najlepiej to scalić?
Najprościej będzie usunąć stare konta i zostawić nowe.
Ale na starych kontach są przypisane sprawy, notatki i role. Nie możemy ich po prostu usunąć. Czy da się zmienić login istniejącego profilu zamiast tworzyć nowy?

Ma Pan rację, samo usunięcie byłoby niewłaściwe. W tym przypadku należy zaktualizować adres logowania na istniejących profilach i dopiero potem zamknąć duplikaty. Nie zrobię tego zbiorczo z poziomu czatu, ale proszę przesłać listę par stary/nowy adres oraz wskazać, które konto ma pozostać źródłowe. Przekażę to do operacyjnej zmiany kont.

subject: SSO users disappeared after changing default role mapping
description: I updated our default SSO role mapping because new hires were being created as viewers instead of contributors. Right after that, several users lost access and now some of them don’t appear in the active user list at all until they attempt another login. I need to understand whether this is expected and how to restore the affected accounts.

comments.message: We use SAML with Okta. Yesterday I changed the mapping rule from Viewer to Contributor, and this morning 11 people reported missing projects. Three users also vanished from the People page unless I search by exact email.

Changing the default mapping should not remove users, but it can change permissions on next login. Please have the affected users sign out and sign back in so the role sync can complete. For the missing entries, try disabling any filters on the People page and refresh the browser.
We already did that. After relogin some came back, but two users are still hidden and one person was recreated as a duplicate account with Contributor access.

I reviewed the audit logs and the role mapping change triggered a partial reprovisioning event for users whose NameID changed format. That’s why you’re seeing hidden legacy records and one duplicate. I’ve escalated this to our identity team to merge the duplicate and restore visibility on the affected accounts. In the meantime, please avoid further mapping edits so we don’t create additional duplicates.

subject: Seat count went up after we bulk reassigned ownership to shared users
description: We were trying to clean up data by moving thousands of records from former reps to a few shared queues. After the reassignment, our seat count and invoice both increased. I think we may have created new placeholder users incorrectly. Please explain why that affected billing and what the right way is to manage ownership on inactive data.

comments.message: We added five 'regional queue' users so imported records could have an owner, then reassigned old accounts and contacts to them in bulk. Now billing says we added five paid seats. Could those have been non-billable somehow?
Any user record in your workspace can create a seat charge, so yes, adding queue users would increase billing. You should delete them and leave the owner blank.
The system doesn't allow blank owners on accounts in our setup, and deleting them might orphan automation. What's the supported approach?

You're right; my previous answer was too simplistic. In your edition, standard users are billable, but service queues should be configured as routing pools, not human user accounts. For owned records that must stay assigned, you can also use one existing operations seat plus team-based visibility instead of creating multiple placeholders. I can't remove the current invoice charge, but if you convert those queue users to routing pools before renewal, next cycle's seat count will drop. I've included the admin guide for bulk reassigning records from users to pools.

subject: Los tickets no llegan a Salesforce desde su integración
description: Desde esta mañana los tickets cerrados ya no se están creando como casos en Salesforce. La integración estuvo funcionando por meses sin problemas. Revisé las credenciales y aparentemente siguen activas, pero en el registro solo veo un mensaje genérico de fallo de API. ¿Me pueden orientar?

comments.message: También noté que el problema empezó justo después de que un administrador cambió permisos en Salesforce, aunque no sabemos si está relacionado.

Entiendo. Si las credenciales siguen activas, probablemente no sea un problema de autenticación. Le recomiendo desconectar y volver a conectar Salesforce para refrescar la sesión. Normalmente eso resuelve los fallos de API.
Ya lo hicimos dos veces y sigue igual. Además ahora la integración muestra menos objetos disponibles que antes.
En ese caso podría ser una incidencia temporal de Salesforce. Les sugiero esperar unas horas y probar de nuevo más tarde.

subject: Arquivo exportado veio com acentuação quebrada e separador errado
description: Baixei uma exportação de contatos para tratar no Excel e os nomes vieram com acentuação quebrada, além de as colunas abrirem todas bagunçadas por causa do separador. Estou no Brasil e preciso entregar esse arquivo hoje. Qual configuração devo usar para exportar corretamente?

comments.message: Baixei uma exportação de contatos para tratar no Excel e os nomes vieram com acentuação quebrada, além de as colunas abrirem todas bagunçadas por causa do separador. Estou no Brasil e preciso entregar esse arquivo hoje. Qual configuração devo usar para exportar corretamente?

Isso costuma acontecer quando o Excel abre CSV em codificação ou delimitador diferente do esperado. Para evitar, prefira exportar em XLSX quando disponível. Se precisar de CSV, escolha UTF-8 e depois importe o arquivo no Excel usando 'Dados > De Texto/CSV', selecionando origem UTF-8 e delimitador vírgula ou ponto e vírgula conforme o arquivo gerado.
Eu preciso de CSV mesmo porque outro sistema vai consumir. O arquivo de vocês parece usar vírgula, mas meu Excel em português interpreta tudo em uma coluna.

Perfeito. Nesse cenário, o arquivo pode estar correto para integração, e o problema é só a abertura local no Excel por causa da configuração regional. Para validar, abra o CSV em um editor de texto e confirme o delimitador. Se for vírgula, mantenha assim para o outro sistema e use a importação guiada do Excel em vez de abrir com duplo clique. Se você quiser compatibilidade local imediata, também pode gerar uma cópia convertida para ponto e vírgula sem alterar a base original.

subject: Subsidiary admin lost billing access after ownership change
description: I took over as the primary admin for our workspace after our finance manager left. I can manage users just fine, but I can’t view invoices or update the card on file, and our payment failed today. I need to restore billing access and make sure the disabled former owner is no longer the billing contact.

comments.message: The previous owner’s user account has already been deactivated in the admin console. I’m listed as Workspace Admin, but the Billing tab says I don’t have permission. Meanwhile invoice payment failed because the old company card expired.

Billing permissions are separate from standard workspace administration. I can see you’re a Workspace Admin but not a Billing Admin, and the deactivated user is still set as the billing owner on the subscription.
Can you switch that to me? We need to pay the invoice today.

I’ve sent a billing ownership transfer form to your company email and temporarily unlocked invoice download access for your user so you can retrieve the failed invoice. Once you return the signed form, we can assign you as Billing Admin and remove the former owner permanently. If payment is urgent, use the invoice link I included to pay manually today.

subject: No se actualizan los estados en Jira después de cambiar el flujo
description: Tenemos sincronización bidireccional con Jira. La semana pasada cambiamos el flujo de estados en nuestro proyecto y desde entonces, cuando una incidencia se mueve a "En validación" en su plataforma, en Jira se queda en el estado anterior. Otras actualizaciones sí pasan, como comentarios y prioridad. ¿Qué puede estar bloqueando solo los estados?

comments.message: Revisé la conexión general y está sana. Parece algo específico del mapeo del workflow, pero no estoy seguro.

Correcto, cuando solo fallan los estados después de un cambio de workflow, lo primero es revisar el mapeo de estados porque los nombres visibles pueden seguir iguales aunque el ID interno cambie. Entre en Integraciones > Jira > Mapeo de estados y verifique si "En validación" aparece como no asignado o vinculado a un estado archivado.
Sí, aparece como no asignado. El estado viejo fue reemplazado y nunca lo remapeamos.

Ahí está la causa. Asigne "En validación" al nuevo estado de Jira y guarde los cambios. Después haga una transición manual en un ticket de prueba para confirmar que la sincronización vuelve a funcionar. Tenga en cuenta que los tickets que fallaron antes no siempre se reenvían solos, así que quizá deba volver a cambiarles el estado para disparar el evento otra vez.

subject: Token da API funciona em sandbox mas falha em produção
description: Estou a finalizar a configuração da nossa integração e o mesmo script funciona na sandbox, mas em produção recebo "invalid audience" ao tentar obter dados de tickets. Copiei o client_id e o secret corretos, por isso suspeito de alguma definição errada no produto. Onde é que se define o audience para cada ambiente?

comments.message: Estou a finalizar a configuração da nossa integração e o mesmo script funciona na sandbox, mas em produção recebo "invalid audience" ao tentar obter dados de tickets. Copiei o client_id e o secret corretos, por isso suspeito de alguma definição errada no produto. Onde é que se define o audience para cada ambiente?

Esse erro normalmente indica que o token foi pedido ao servidor de autenticação certo, mas com o audience da sandbox. No seu tenant, a sandbox está em api-sandbox.seudominio.com e a produção em api.seudominio.com. Em Configurações > Aplicações > OAuth, o audience de produção aparece no detalhe da aplicação e não é herdado da sandbox. Confirme também se o redirect URI e o ambiente selecionado no seu script não estão misturados.
Tinha mesmo o audience antigo hardcoded no ficheiro de configuração. Corrigi isso e já consigo listar tickets em produção. Obrigado.
Ótimo. Como prática, sugiro separar as variáveis por ambiente e usar também chaves OAuth distintas para sandbox e produção para evitar esse tipo de troca.

subject: Slack notifications stopped for one project
description: I'm the workspace admin for our team, and since yesterday our Slack integration has stopped posting ticket updates for just one project. Other projects are still sending messages normally. I already reconnected Slack once and the app shows as authorized, but no new comments or status changes are appearing in the channel. Can you help me figure out what broke?

comments.message: I'm the workspace admin for our team, and since yesterday our Slack integration has stopped posting ticket updates for just one project. Other projects are still sending messages normally. I already reconnected Slack once and the app shows as authorized, but no new comments or status changes are appearing in the channel.

Thanks for the details. Since it's isolated to one project, the Slack authorization is probably fine. Please check that project's automation settings and confirm the Slack channel is still selected under Notifications > Destinations. We recently saw cases where a channel mapping was removed after a project template update. Also, send me one example ticket ID that should have posted but didn't.
You were right that the channel field was blank in that project's notification settings. I re-selected the channel, but older updates still didn't appear. Example ticket is INC-1842.

That makes sense. Reconnecting the channel restores future events but won't replay past ones automatically. I checked the behavior against the example you sent, and new events should now post normally for that project. If you need historical activity in Slack, you'll have to post those manually or use an export. Let us know if a brand-new ticket update still fails.

subject: Salesforce sync is skipping closed-won deals in pipeline report
description: I'm trying to build a quarterly pipeline report in your platform using data synced from Salesforce, but any opportunity that moved to Closed Won after the initial sync is missing from the report. We use the Salesforce integration and the report is filtered by stage history, so finance is now seeing lower revenue than what's actually in Salesforce. I already forced a sync twice and the numbers still don't match. Can someone help figure out whether this is a reporting issue or an integration issue?

comments.message: Our Q2 pipeline report is short by 17 deals. All of them are visible in Salesforce as Closed Won, but they never appear in your report even after I ran a manual sync. The report groups by stage change date. This is blocking our finance review today.

Thanks for the detail. I checked our sync behavior and standard opportunity fields should update on manual sync, but stage history objects are handled separately from the base opportunity record. If the report is built on stage transition dates, it's possible the integration imported the current stage but not the related history entries for those records. Could you send 2-3 example opportunity IDs and the report URL so I can verify against the sync logs?

Sure, examples are 0068A00000K1dP2, 0068A00000K1dRm, and 0068A00000K1dVm. The report is our 'Q2 Pipeline by Stage Movement' report in the RevOps folder. We need to know whether we should trust this report at all.

I reviewed the examples and can confirm the integration is updating the opportunity status, but the historical stage-change events for those records were not backfilled into reporting after the initial import. Because this affects how the report is calculated, I'm escalating this to our integrations team to run a history reprocessing job and to our reporting team to confirm whether the metric definition needs adjustment. For now, I would not use that stage-history report for finance signoff; use a report filtered on current Closed Won date in Salesforce until we update you.

subject: HubSpot contact sync stuck in pending
description: Our HubSpot integration has been showing 'sync pending' for new contacts for almost six hours. Existing contacts are still visible, but nothing new is coming over. I'm trying to troubleshoot whether we've hit a rate limit, an auth problem, or a mapping issue.

comments.message: We onboarded a new SDR team today, so there were a lot of new contact creates in HubSpot this morning. The queue in your app keeps growing, and manual retry doesn't seem to do anything.
This is usually caused by browser caching on the integrations page. Please clear your cache and reload the contact sync dashboard. After that, the pending count should update.
I cleared cache and checked from another browser. The pending count is still increasing, and no new contacts are appearing.
In that case, it may just take more time for HubSpot to process the backlog. I'd suggest waiting a few more hours and checking again tomorrow.

subject: Can't remove extra paid seat after contractor offboarding
description: I removed a contractor from our workspace yesterday, but our subscription page still says we have 12 active seats and the upcoming invoice preview hasn’t changed. I’m both the billing contact and workspace owner, so I should have permission to fix this. What exactly do I need to do to stop paying for that seat?

comments.message: The user was fully removed from User Administration, not just suspended. However, Billing still shows 12 seats with renewal next week. I assumed deleting the user would lower the count automatically.
If the user is gone, the invoice should update by itself at the next sync. Please wait 24 hours and it will likely correct automatically.
It has already been more than 24 hours. Also our invoice preview still shows the old quantity. Is there a separate place where I have to reduce seats?
You may need to archive the user instead of deleting them. If that doesn’t work, try clearing your browser cache and checking the billing page again.

subject: Ancien collaborateur toujours visible comme propriétaire de l’espace
description: Je viens de reprendre la gestion de notre compte et je vois que l’ancien directeur opérationnel apparaît toujours comme propriétaire de l’espace dans plusieurs sections, alors qu’il a quitté l’entreprise il y a deux mois. J’ai pu lui retirer l’accès utilisateur, mais pas son statut de propriétaire. Cela pose un problème pour notre audit interne.

comments.message: Bonjour, même après avoir désactivé l’utilisateur, l’ancien dirigeant reste affiché comme propriétaire dans les paramètres avancés. Comment retirer complètement cette mention ?
Bonjour, si l’utilisateur est désactivé, il ne devrait plus apparaître nulle part. Je vous invite à vider le cache de votre navigateur.
Je l’ai déjà fait sur deux navigateurs différents, et le statut de propriétaire est toujours visible. Je ne peux pas le modifier moi-même.
Dans ce cas, il est possible que l’affichage se mette à jour tout seul d’ici quelques jours. Si cela persiste, contactez votre administrateur interne.

subject: Charged for deactivated users after admin handoff
description: We deactivated several users before month-end, but the new invoice still includes all of them. Our previous admin is out on leave and I'm taking over subscription management. I need access updated to my account and a correction to this invoice if possible.

comments.message: We deactivated 7 users on the 30th, but invoice INV-77219 billed us for 24 active seats instead of 17. The previous admin, Simon, is on leave and can't respond, so please transfer billing permissions to me. Can this invoice be corrected?

Seat reductions usually need to be made before the end of the billing period to affect the next invoice. Since the changes were on the 30th, they may have missed the invoice sync job depending on timezone. I can start the admin transfer if your email domain matches the account domain.
It does match, and I can verify by DNS or email. But I still need to know whether we have to pay this invoice as-is.

Please pay the invoice to avoid interruption. If the seat reduction timestamp is confirmed before renewal cutoff in your account timezone, we may be able to apply an adjustment afterward. I've started the transfer request, but billing review is still needed before I can promise any correction.

subject: Past-due invoice but our SSO rollout locked out the people who can pay it
description: We are getting past-due notices for an invoice that needs to be paid today, but during our SSO rollout the finance admins lost access to the platform. I can sign in as a standard user, but the billing page says unauthorized, and the two owners can’t get through SSO because their accounts were never provisioned in the identity provider. We need to know the outstanding balance and get one person back into billing before the service is interrupted.

comments.message: We are getting past-due notices for an invoice that needs to be paid today, but during our SSO rollout the finance admins lost access to the platform. I can sign in as a standard user, but the billing page says unauthorized, and the two owners can’t get through SSO because their accounts were never provisioned in the identity provider. We need to know the outstanding balance and get one person back into billing before the service is interrupted.

Your outstanding invoice balance is $3,860.00 across the current renewal and one prorated seat increase. Because this involves both SSO enforcement and billing admin roles, I’ve temporarily exempted one owner account from SSO for 12 hours and sent a magic login link to the email ending in @northpeakhealth.com. Once that owner signs in, they’ll be able to update payment immediately.
We received the link and were able to enter billing. Payment is submitted now. Can you also prevent this from happening again when we re-enable SSO?

Yes. I added both owner accounts to the bypass list and documented that they must be assigned to the Billing-Admins group in your IdP before you remove the bypass. I also attached our SSO billing-access checklist to this case. Since payment is processing, your interruption notice should clear automatically.

subject: Zwei Benutzerkonten für dieselbe Person nach SSO-Einführung
description: Seit wir SSO aktiviert haben, hat ein Mitarbeiter plötzlich zwei Konten: eins mit seiner alten lokalen Anmeldung und eins mit dem SSO-Profil. Tickets und Erwähnungen verteilen sich jetzt auf beide Profile. Ich muss wissen, wie wir das sauber zusammenführen oder eines der Konten stilllegen, ohne Datenverlust zu verursachen.

comments.message: Die E-Mail-Adresse ist inzwischen in beiden Profilen identisch. Im Adminbereich sehe ich aber weiterhin zwei unterschiedliche Benutzer-IDs.
In solchen Fällen können Sie normalerweise einfach das ältere Konto löschen, dann bleibt nur das SSO-Konto übrig. Die Tickets sollten automatisch auf das verbleibende Profil übergehen.
Das klingt riskant. Ich sehe keine Hinweise, dass historische Aktionen wirklich zusammengeführt würden.

Sie haben recht, das sollte ich so nicht pauschal empfehlen. Eine echte Kontenzusammenführung ist oft nicht automatisch möglich, besonders wenn bereits unterschiedliche Benutzer-IDs aktiv verwendet wurden. Ich eskaliere das an unser Identitäts-Team, damit geprüft wird, welches Konto autoritativ bleiben sollte und ob ein Merge oder eine kontrollierte Deaktivierung ohne Verlust der Historie möglich ist.

subject: Upgraded account after removing a team, invoice looks wrong
description: I removed our APAC team from the workspace and then upgraded the remaining users to the Business plan in the same afternoon. The invoice now shows both a downgrade credit and two separate upgrade charges, and the total is much higher than the estimate I saw before confirming. Please check whether the account changes were applied in the right order and fix the invoice if needed.

comments.message: I removed our APAC team from the workspace and then upgraded the remaining users to the Business plan in the same afternoon. The invoice now shows both a downgrade credit and two separate upgrade charges, and the total is much higher than the estimate I saw before confirming. Please check whether the account changes were applied in the right order and fix the invoice if needed.

I reviewed the audit trail and see the team removal completed at 2:14 PM, while the Business upgrade processed at 2:09 PM. Because the plan change went through first, your invoice correctly includes the temporary higher seat count, followed by a prorated credit once APAC was removed. That said, I also found a second upgrade event caused by a retry after the page timed out. I've voided that duplicate charge and sent a corrected invoice to your billing email.

subject: CSV import succeeded but standard users get access denied after password reset
description: We imported a new batch of leads this afternoon and the admin team can see them, but our SDRs get an access denied message after they reset their passwords. One SDR can log in on mobile but not desktop. I need to know if the import changed permissions or if something is wrong with user sessions.

comments.message: The CSV job says completed with 4,532 leads created. Right after that, several SDRs who had to reset expired passwords started seeing 'access denied' on the Leads page.

A lead import does not change page permissions, so the data job and the password resets are likely coincidental. Since one user can access on mobile, please have affected users clear cookies or sign out and back in. Also confirm their role still includes Leads view access.
We cleared cookies for one user and desktop works again. Another still gets access denied, and I noticed their role somehow changed from SDR to Viewer.

That would explain the remaining issue. Restore that user to the SDR role and have them sign in again so their permissions refresh. The imported leads should then be visible; the import itself appears healthy, and the desktop symptom was likely stale session data after the password reset.

subject: Latence très forte sur les recherches avancées
description: Depuis lundi, les recherches avancées mettent entre 25 et 40 secondes à répondre pour notre équipe support. Le problème touche plusieurs bureaux et plusieurs comptes. Nous avons un pic d’activité en ce moment, donc j’ai besoin d’un diagnostic ; si c’est un incident connu, merci de l’indiquer et de lancer les actions nécessaires.

comments.message: Bonjour, la recherche avancée est devenue presque inutilisable. Les filtres par tag et période finissent parfois par expirer.

Bonjour, merci du signalement. Je vois qu’aucun incident global n’est affiché publiquement, mais comme vous mentionnez plusieurs sites et des expirations, cela mérite une analyse plus poussée. J’escalade ce ticket à notre équipe performance avec votre plage horaire, les temps de réponse observés et l’impact métier sur votre centre de support.
Très bien. Le plus gros ralentissement est entre 9h et 11h CET, surtout sur les recherches de plus de 30 jours.

Merci, j’ai ajouté ces détails techniques à l’escalade. En attendant, la seule mesure de contournement que je peux suggérer est de réduire temporairement la plage de dates ou de lancer les requêtes hors pic, le temps que nos spécialistes performance analysent le problème.

subject: Webhook trigger starts workflow but HubSpot sync step never maps company ID
description: I am using an incoming webhook to start a workflow whenever our app sends a new trial signup. The workflow starts correctly, but the HubSpot create/update company step fails because the company ID field is blank, even though the webhook payload includes it. I think the mapping broke after I edited the trigger schema.

comments.message: In the payload preview I can still see company.external_id, but in the workflow field picker that path no longer appears. I tried deleting and re-adding the HubSpot step and it still only shows top-level fields.

This is expected. When you change the webhook schema, nested fields are not always backfilled into existing workflow variables. Please recreate the webhook trigger from scratch so the integration step can read the new payload shape.
Recreating the whole trigger is pretty disruptive because this automation has several branches and post-sync actions. Is there any less destructive fix?

There is. Instead of rebuilding the entire workflow, add a 'Transform data' step immediately after the webhook trigger and map company.external_id into a flat field such as hubspot_company_key. Downstream integration actions usually recognize transformed outputs even when the original nested schema picker does not refresh correctly. If the field is still missing after publishing, send us the workflow ID and a sample payload and we can have engineering resync the trigger schema on the backend.

subject: Report duplicates after workflow reassigns ticket owner
description: I'm seeing duplicate rows in a support performance report after we turned on an automation that reassigns tickets between queues based on SLA. The report is grouped by owner and status, and now some tickets appear twice with two different owners even though it's the same ticket. I need this fixed before our ops review, or at least explained clearly enough that I can trust the numbers.

comments.message: We enabled a workflow yesterday that moves overdue tickets from Tier 1 to Escalations. Since then, our resolution report is counting the same ticket under both the original owner and the escalated owner. Is the workflow creating duplicate records or is the report wrong?

This usually happens when a report includes historical owner changes. If you edit the report and remove the 'Owner history' dataset, it should stop showing duplicates. The workflow automation itself shouldn't be creating extra tickets.

I don't see any field called 'Owner history' in this report. It's just the default ticket dataset with current owner, status, and resolved date. Also, the issue only started after we enabled the reassignment workflow.

Thanks for clarifying. In that case, the reassignment workflow may be writing to both 'Assigned team' and 'Owner' in separate steps, and the reporting cache can temporarily surface both transitions as rows when grouped by owner. I can't confirm from support-side tooling, so I'm escalating this to our reporting team along with the report ID and the workflow run logs. For now, if you filter the report to 'Current status = Resolved' and remove owner grouping, your totals should be closer to expected.

subject: Se cobró un addon que habíamos quitado y las automatizaciones fallan
description: Quitamos el addon de analítica avanzada el mes pasado, pero volvió a aparecer cobrado en la renovación de hoy. Además, nuestras automatizaciones dejaron de ejecutarse y en el registro sale "permission denied" para reglas que antes funcionaban. ¿Pueden revisar ambos temas?

comments.message: Quitamos el addon de analítica avanzada el mes pasado, pero volvió a aparecer cobrado en la renovación de hoy. Además, nuestras automatizaciones dejaron de ejecutarse y en el registro sale "permission denied" para reglas que antes funcionaban. ¿Pueden revisar ambos temas?

Hola. Estoy revisando la cuenta y veo algo extraño: el addon fue desactivado en la interfaz, pero siguió marcado como activo en la renovación porque estaba ligado a un paquete heredado. No puedo corregir el cobro yo mismo, así que ya lo escalé a facturación con captura de la configuración.

Sobre las automatizaciones, los logs muestran que el usuario de servicio "bot-ops" perdió el rol Admin ayer a las 18:02, por eso las reglas devuelven "permission denied". Pueden restaurarle el rol o cambiar las automatizaciones para que usen otro propietario con permisos suficientes.
Restauramos el rol Admin y las reglas volvieron a correr. Bien. ¿Tienen plazo para resolver el cobro del addon?

Me alegra que la parte técnica quedara resuelta. La incidencia de cobro está asignada al equipo de billing, pero no tengo un plazo firme; normalmente responden en 1 o 2 días hábiles. Si mañana no ven actualización, puedo dejar un recordatorio interno.

subject: Jira-Integration erstellt Issues im falschen Projekt
description: Wir haben Regeln eingerichtet, damit Tickets aus Ihrer Plattform automatisch als Bugs in Jira angelegt werden. Seit der letzten Änderung landen neue Issues aber im Standardprojekt statt im Projekt unseres Entwicklerteams. Ich finde nicht, wo diese Zuordnung überschrieben wurde. Können Sie mir helfen?

comments.message: Früher ging alles in das Jira-Projekt DEVOPS. Jetzt wird plötzlich jedes neue Ticket als Bug im Projekt OPS angelegt, obwohl wir die Regel nicht bewusst geändert haben.
Bitte prüfen Sie in Jira, ob das Projekt DEVOPS noch aktiv ist. Wenn ein Projekt archiviert wurde, weicht unsere Integration automatisch auf das Standardprojekt aus.
DEVOPS ist aktiv und erreichbar. In der Integration sehe ich auch noch DEVOPS als Zielprojekt.
Dann liegt es wahrscheinlich an Jira selbst. Unsere Integration übernimmt nur den Projektnamen, den Sie auswählen. Wenn trotzdem OPS verwendet wird, müssten Sie das direkt bei Atlassian klären.

subject: Admin login works, but custom app users see blank screen after MFA setup
description: We enabled MFA and also changed a feature flag for our embedded dashboard module. Since then, standard users can authenticate but land on a blank page instead of the app. Admins can still log in and load everything. I am trying to figure out whether the access policy or one of the dashboard configuration changes is causing this.

comments.message: Standard users complete login and MFA, but after that they just see a blank screen. Admin users are fine. The only changes today were enabling MFA and changing the default dashboard from Legacy View to Embedded Analytics.

A blank screen after successful login usually points to a post-login routing or permission issue rather than MFA itself. Can you confirm whether the affected role has access to Embedded Analytics? In Settings > Modules, if Embedded Analytics is set as the default landing page but the Standard User role lacks that module permission, they can authenticate successfully and then hit a blank route.
That seems likely. I enabled the dashboard globally but did not update role permissions. Is there a quick fix?

Yes. Either grant the affected roles access to Embedded Analytics under Role Permissions, or switch the default landing page back to Legacy View until permissions are updated. After saving, have one affected user sign out and back in so the new navigation config is applied to their session.

subject: Owner account removed during cleanup and invoice is overdue
description: During a user cleanup, our IT team accidentally deactivated my owner account instead of an old contractor. Now the latest invoice is overdue, and I can’t log in to pay it or even see the billing contact details. Another admin can access the workspace but not the billing tab. We need the owner account reinstated or another way to settle the invoice today so the account doesn’t get suspended.

comments.message: During a user cleanup, our IT team accidentally deactivated my owner account instead of an old contractor. Now the latest invoice is overdue, and I can’t log in to pay it or even see the billing contact details. Another admin can access the workspace but not the billing tab. We need the owner account reinstated or another way to settle the invoice today so the account doesn’t get suspended.

I found the deactivation event and confirmed your owner profile was disabled yesterday. I can’t directly reactivate an owner without security verification, but I can prevent suspension while we sort this out. I’ve placed a 3-day hold on collections and sent you a secure ownership verification link. Once completed, we can restore your login or transfer billing admin rights to the other admin.
I’ve completed the verification form. If possible, transferring billing rights to our operations manager may be faster than waiting for the owner reactivation.

Verification passed. I’ve granted Billing Admin to your operations manager and sent them an invite to set a password. Your overdue invoice remains payable immediately through their account, and the suspension hold will stay in place for the next 3 days while your owner login reactivation is processed separately.

subject: Locked out after turning on SSO for some users
description: I enabled SSO for our company yesterday, but now two contractors can't sign in at all. Internal employees are getting in through Okta, but the contractors don't have company identities there. I may have assigned them to the wrong login policy, and I don't understand how to let external users keep password login without opening that up to everyone. What should I change?

comments.message: I enabled SSO for our company yesterday, but now two contractors can't sign in at all. Internal employees are getting in through Okta, but the contractors don't have company identities there. I may have assigned them to the wrong login policy, and I don't understand how to let external users keep password login without opening that up to everyone. What should I change?

This usually happens when SSO enforcement is applied to all users instead of a specific domain or group. To allow contractors to keep password login, create or move them into an External Contractors group, then in Security > Authentication set SSO Required only for your verified company domain or for the employee groups synced from Okta. After that, review each contractor account in Admin Settings > Users and make sure their login method is set to Local Password instead of SSO Only.
I found the contractors and changed one of them to Local Password, but she still gets redirected to SSO when entering her email on the login page.

That suggests the domain-level routing rule is still forcing SSO before the user-level setting is checked. Please send me the contractor email domains and I'll escalate this to our identity team, because tenant-level routing rules sometimes need to be adjusted on the backend. In the meantime, don't re-invite those users, since duplicate accounts can make access mapping worse. Once the routing rule is reviewed, they should be able to use the password reset flow normally.

subject: Mobile app crashes when opening task details
description: Our field team says the iOS app crashes every time they tap into task details for one specific project. Other projects open fine. I've tried reinstalling the app on one phone and the crash still happens, so I need help troubleshooting whether the issue is data-related or a bad app version.

comments.message: Our field team says the iOS app crashes every time they tap into task details for one specific project. Other projects open fine. I've tried reinstalling the app on one phone and the crash still happens, so I need help troubleshooting whether the issue is data-related or a bad app version.

Thanks — since it's limited to one project and survives reinstall, this does sound data-specific rather than a general app install problem. Can you tell me the iOS app version, whether all users on that project are affected, and if the crash happens on a particular task or every task in that project? A screen recording or the project ID would also help.
App version is 4.18.2. It happens for all users we've tested, but only when opening tasks that have a long checklist attached. Project ID is PRJ-7731.
