That is helpful. We recently fixed a crash in 4.19 related to rendering unusually large checklists in task details. Please update one affected device to the latest app version and test again. I've also attached your project ID to an internal bug in case the checklist payload there triggers the same issue even after upgrading.

subject: SSO users stuck in login loop after domain change
description: I updated our company domain in the admin settings yesterday, and since then our Okta users get redirected back to the login page over and over. Local admin login still works for me, but none of our regular users can access the workspace. I also changed the default sign-in method to SSO during the same maintenance window, so I'm not sure which setting caused it.

comments.message: Our users are in a login loop after I changed our company domain from northstar.io to northstarhealth.io and enabled SSO as the default. Okta authenticates them, then they land back on your sign-in page. I can still get in with a password on the owner account.

Thanks for the details. This usually happens when the IdP ACS URL or allowed domain list no longer matches the workspace configuration. Since you changed both the verified domain and default auth method, please log in with your local owner account, go to Security > SSO, and confirm the Audience URI and ACS URL in Okta exactly match the values shown there. Also under Workspace Settings > Domains, make sure northstarhealth.io is verified and marked as the primary domain. If users are still looping, temporarily disable 'Enforce SSO' so they can sign in while you update the IdP configuration.
The ACS URL in Okta was still pointing to the old subdomain. I fixed that and users can now sign in, but a few people with the old email suffix are getting 'user not found'.

That means authentication is working again, but account matching is now failing for users whose email no longer matches the verified domain. In Admin > User Provisioning, either update those users' email addresses to the new domain or enable Just-in-Time provisioning with email alias mapping if your plan includes it. If you want, I can also help you export the affected users and bulk update them.

subject: Need to restore admin rights for one user without reactivating everyone
description: I accidentally bulk-edited our team and removed the Admin role from one of our IT managers while also suspending three contractor accounts. I can reactivate the contractors if needed, but I only need to restore admin permissions for the IT manager. Since I no longer have permission to change roles myself, can support step in or route this to whoever can verify and fix it?

comments.message: For context, I'm still listed as a workspace owner in our contract, but the UI now says I don't have permission to manage user roles. The affected admin is Mark Ellison.

I can help with the user administration side, but role restoration for admins requires an ownership verification review. Please reply with your workspace URL and the affected user's email address. Once I have that, I'll escalate the request to our account security team to verify the authorized owner and restore Mark's admin role without changing the suspended contractor accounts.
Workspace is northstar-ops.acmeapp.com and Mark's email is mark.ellison@northstarops.com. Please do not unsuspend the contractors.

Got it. I've submitted an escalation with those details and specifically noted that only Mark's admin role should be restored, with no changes to the suspended contractor users. Our account security team will review the owner authorization and handle the permission update from their side.

subject: Xero integration lost connection after seat reduction and owner cannot sign in
description: I reduced our user count before renewal because a few people left the company. The new invoice still charged the old amount, and now our Xero integration says the connection owner is inactive. To make matters worse, the person listed as workspace owner also can’t sign in because their magic link emails never arrive and password reset says the account uses social login. I need to recover access for the owner and get the billing and Xero issues sorted out.

comments.message: I reduced our user count before renewal because a few people left the company. The new invoice still charged the old amount, and now our Xero integration says the connection owner is inactive. To make matters worse, the person listed as workspace owner also can’t sign in because their magic link emails never arrive and password reset says the account uses social login. I need to recover access for the owner and get the billing and Xero issues sorted out.

I checked the renewal event and it appears the seat reduction request missed the processing cutoff by a few hours, which is why the old count was invoiced. Separately, the Xero integration owner was one of the deactivated users, so the connector lost its active token context. For the owner login issue, I can see the account is configured for Microsoft social sign-in, not magic link, and the mailbox suppression list is also preventing our emails from arriving. I’ve removed the suppression, sent a fresh owner-invite flow, and reopened the invoice for a possible courtesy adjustment. Once the owner signs in, they’ll need to reconnect Xero because I can’t complete that OAuth step on your behalf.
The owner got in through Microsoft now. We reconnected Xero, but the old invoices from the last two days didn’t backfill.

That can happen when the connection owner changes. I’ve manually queued a historical resync for the past 72 hours, which should repopulate the missing Xero records. Billing has not approved the invoice adjustment yet, but the review is attached to this case so you won’t need to re-explain the seat timing.

subject: Accidentally changed analyst to viewer and now shared reports fail
description: I mistakenly changed one of our analysts from Editor to Viewer while cleaning up user roles. I switched her back within a few minutes, but now the reports she built for our customer success team open in view mode only and some scheduled emails stopped overnight. I need to know whether that temporary account change can alter report ownership or publishing rights, because she still needs to administer user groups for her team too.

comments.message: I mistakenly changed one of our analysts from Editor to Viewer while cleaning up user roles. I switched her back within a few minutes, but now the reports she built for our customer success team open in view mode only and some scheduled emails stopped overnight. I need to know whether that temporary account change can alter report ownership or publishing rights, because she still needs to administer user groups for her team too.

A temporary downgrade can interrupt edit rights and scheduled delivery if the system revalidates ownership while the lower role is active, but it normally should not permanently change ownership. Since she was restored to Editor, please have her open each affected report and republish it, then check whether the schedules show as active. User-group administration is separate, so that part should remain based on whichever admin permission set she currently has.
Republishing fixed two reports, but one schedule still shows paused and there’s no resume button for her.

If the schedule is paused without a resume option, it may have been detached from her report-edit permission during the downgrade window. Have a full admin duplicate that schedule under her restored account or under a service owner account, then remove the paused version. That should restore delivery without changing her user-group admin permissions.

subject: Child records duplicated after moving parent account
description: We moved a regional account under a new parent in our hierarchy, and afterward several child locations appeared twice in the hierarchy view. The duplicates share the same external IDs, but only one copy is editable. Reports now double-count those locations. Can you check whether the move operation duplicated relationship rows instead of just updating the parent reference?

comments.message: We moved a regional account under a new parent in our hierarchy, and afterward several child locations appeared twice in the hierarchy view. The duplicates share the same external IDs, but only one copy is editable. Reports now double-count those locations.

I reviewed the affected hierarchy and can confirm there are duplicate relationship entries for 14 child records, while the underlying child records themselves are not duplicated. This appears to be a bug in the parent-move job writing a new relationship row without removing the previous one. I've removed the extra relationship rows in your workspace and the hierarchy view should be correct now. Engineering also has a defect report to prevent future moves from duplicating those links.

subject: Invite links fail for new hires and the approval automation never starts
description: We hired two new agents today. Their invite links either expire immediately or send them to a blank login page. Because they can't complete setup, our new-hire approval workflow hasn't started for their equipment requests. We are onboarding today, so please either fix this or escalate it to whoever handles account access and automations.

comments.message: We hired two new agents today. Their invite links either expire immediately or send them to a blank login page. Because they can't complete setup, our new-hire approval workflow hasn't started for their equipment requests. We are onboarding today, so please either fix this or escalate it to whoever handles account access and automations.

I checked one of the invite records and it shows the email was already verified, so the link behavior may be normal. For the approval workflow, that usually starts only after the user submits the request themselves, so if they can’t log in yet there isn’t really an automation issue.

That doesn't make sense. They never got in, and the blank page happens right after clicking the invite link. Also, the workflow is supposed to create the request automatically when a new user is activated.

In that case, please resend the invites and ask them to use Chrome. If the automation still doesn't run after they activate, you may need to recreate it. I’m not escalating this yet because it sounds environmental.

subject: HubSpot integration keeps disconnecting for one user account
description: Our HubSpot integration disconnects every couple of days, but only for one regional workspace tied to a specific HubSpot user. Other connected workspaces stay active. I need help troubleshooting whether this is a permission issue, token refresh problem, or something else in the integration setup.

comments.message: When it disconnects, the error banner says 'refresh token invalid_grant.' The affected user recently had SSO enforced in HubSpot, but they can still log in normally.

That error often appears when the OAuth grant is revoked or the installing user loses the required scopes after a security policy change. Since SSO was enforced, HubSpot may have invalidated the existing refresh token for that user-specific connection. Please reconnect the integration using a service account or a non-personal admin account if possible.
We reconnected yesterday with the same user and it failed again this morning. We do have a service account available but haven't tried it yet.

Using the service account is the right next step because it avoids user-level token churn tied to SSO or password policy changes. Before reconnecting, remove the old app authorization in HubSpot and then authorize from our Integrations page with the service account to generate a fresh token set. If it still disconnects after that, send the connector ID and I'll have engineering review the refresh logs.

subject: No entiendo cómo limitar qué datos pasan a un ERP por la integración
description: Estamos explorando una integración con nuestro ERP mediante API, pero me preocupa que se envíen más datos de los necesarios. Quiero una explicación básica de cómo se filtran registros, qué se puede mapear y cómo hacer pruebas sin afectar producción. No necesito que me lo implementen todavía; necesito entender el enfoque correcto.

comments.message: El equipo financiero solo quiere que viajen clientes aprobados y ciertos campos de facturación. Yo veo opciones de filtros, eventos y payloads, pero no sé cómo combinarlas.

Lo ideal es pensar la integración en tres capas. Primero, el disparador: por ejemplo, solo cuando un cliente cambia a estado 'aprobado'. Segundo, el filtro: excluir registros sin NIF o sin dirección fiscal completa. Tercero, el mapeo: enviar únicamente los campos necesarios al ERP, como razón social, identificador fiscal y condiciones de pago.
¿Y cómo probamos eso sin mandar datos reales al ERP?

Hazlo en un entorno de prueba o con un endpoint sandbox del ERP. Si no lo tienen, pueden usar temporalmente un receptor de pruebas para inspeccionar el payload antes de activar producción. También recomiendo probar con 3-5 registros representativos: uno válido, uno incompleto y uno que debería quedar excluido. Así aprenden si la lógica de filtros está funcionando antes de abrir el flujo completo.

subject: Overbilled for suspended users, Dropbox sync paused, and 2FA reset not working
description: We were charged this month for several users who had already been suspended, and around the same time our Dropbox integration stopped syncing attachments. I’m also locked out of my admin account because I changed phones and the 2FA reset link sends me to a page saying my session is invalid. Another admin can still get in, but they can’t see the billing details. Please help us regain access and figure out why suspended users are still being billed while integrations are failing.

comments.message: We were charged this month for several users who had already been suspended, and around the same time our Dropbox integration stopped syncing attachments. I’m also locked out of my admin account because I changed phones and the 2FA reset link sends me to a page saying my session is invalid. Another admin can still get in, but they can’t see the billing details. Please help us regain access and figure out why suspended users are still being billed while integrations are failing.

I reviewed the account and the suspended users are still billable because they were not fully deactivated before the invoice snapshot; they were only access-suspended. Your Dropbox integration paused because the app owner is your locked admin account, and when 2FA recovery was attempted multiple times, our security service invalidated the active sessions tied to that owner token. I’ve issued a manual 2FA recovery for your admin login, promoted the secondary admin to billing visibility temporarily, and transferred the Dropbox integration ownership so sync can resume.
I got back in, thanks. Dropbox started syncing again, but finance wants to know if the suspended users will be credited.

I can confirm the current invoice is still based on the snapshot taken before full deactivation, so the charge was expected by system rules, but I’ve submitted a billing exception request because the users were intentionally suspended and non-operational. I can’t guarantee approval, though the note includes the exact suspension dates and the fact that the access loss and integration interruption happened together.

subject: Salesforce sync creating duplicate accounts
description: We connected Salesforce to sync accounts and contacts into your platform, but over the last two days it's started creating duplicate account records instead of updating the existing ones. I already checked that the Salesforce account IDs are present on the original records. I need help troubleshooting what changed and how to stop more duplicates from being created.

comments.message: This seems to happen mostly when customer records are updated in Salesforce, not when they're created. Our team noticed duplicates for about 18 accounts so far. We recently changed the field mapping for Account Owner and added a custom domain field.

Duplicate creation after an integration mapping change can happen if the sync key falls back from external ID to name matching. Please go to the Salesforce integration settings and verify that Account ID is still set as the primary matching field, not the custom domain field. Also check whether the custom domain field is marked unique in Salesforce.
Account ID is still listed as the primary match field. The custom domain field is not unique in Salesforce, but I don't see why that would create a new account instead of updating the old one.

You're right that it should not on its own. I reviewed the pattern you described, and this likely needs a deeper sync-log inspection because updates should still anchor on Account ID. I've opened an escalation to our integrations team to inspect the last 24 hours of Salesforce upsert events and temporarily recommend pausing account sync to prevent additional duplicates. We'll follow up once we confirm whether a mapping regression is involved.

subject: SSO login loop after enabling new security policy
description: I turned on the new session timeout and IP restriction settings for our workspace yesterday, and now several users are stuck in a login loop. They authenticate through Okta, briefly land in the app, and then get sent back to the sign-in page. A few admins can still get in, but most regular users cannot. I need to know if this is a bug on your side or something we misconfigured, and I also need a way to get my team back in today.

comments.message: We changed nothing in Okta besides what I mentioned in the admin settings in your product. The affected users all show successful authentication in Okta logs, but your app doesn't keep them signed in.

Thanks for the detail. I checked our incident board and I don't see a platform-wide outage, but what you're describing could happen if the new IP restriction policy is evaluating the IdP redirect IP instead of the end-user IP. Could you confirm whether the blocked users are behind a VPN or corporate gateway, and send one HAR capture from a failed login?
Yes, almost all of them are behind our Zscaler gateway. I attached a HAR from one user. I also tested from my home network and I can log in there without an issue.

That helps a lot. I reproduced the behavior in a test tenant with SSO + IP restrictions enabled behind a secure gateway, so this does appear to be a product bug rather than an Okta misconfiguration. As an immediate workaround, please add your gateway egress IPs to the allowlist or temporarily disable IP restrictions for non-admin users. I've escalated the bug to engineering with your HAR and reproduction steps, and I'll update you on the permanent fix. For now, users should be able to access the app once the gateway IPs are allowlisted.

subject: Webhook de deleção não bate com os dados exportados
description: Configuramos webhooks para sincronizar exclusões com nosso banco, mas alguns registros chegam no evento delete sem aparecer mais na API, enquanto outros somem da exportação completa sem que o webhook tenha sido disparado. Isso está deixando nossa rotina de gestão de dados incompleta, porque não sabemos se devemos confiar no webhook ou fazer varredura completa todo dia. Preciso entender a fonte da verdade e a melhor prática para manter consistência.

comments.message: Temos uma tabela de clientes espelhada localmente. Para criação e atualização está ok, mas exclusão está inconsistente há cerca de uma semana.

Olá! O webhook de deleção deve ser considerado a fonte principal para exclusões, e a API pode deixar de retornar o registro imediatamente após a remoção, então essa parte é esperada. Se alguns sumiram da exportação sem webhook, provavelmente o endpoint que você usa filtra por permissões ou status arquivado.
Não usamos filtro nenhum e a conta do token é admin. Você pode confirmar se exclusões em massa disparam um webhook por registro?
Sim, sempre disparam um webhook por registro excluído, então se você não recebeu é provável que tenha havido perda no seu receptor. Sugiro revisar os logs do seu endpoint e aumentar o timeout.

subject: Need to pause automation during bulk API imports
description: We're importing about 40,000 historical records through the API for a migration, but every imported record is triggering our active workflow automations and sending customer emails. We need a way to load the data without firing the normal automations. I couldn't find a suppress flag in the API docs, and right now we've had to stop the import.

comments.message: The import uses the standard create endpoint. The problem is our automations for welcome emails, owner assignment, and escalation timers all run on those imported records, which we definitely don't want for backfilled data.

At the moment there isn't a global API parameter to suppress all workflow automations on create. The usual workaround is to add a condition like imported_via_api = true and exclude those records from email and assignment rules during the migration. You can set that flag in your API payload and remove or ignore it afterward.
That would mean editing 17 separate workflows, which is risky in production. Is there really no account-level import mode?

I checked our standard settings and I don't see an account-level switch for disabling automations during import. Because this affects multiple rules, I'm escalating this to our platform team to confirm whether a temporary maintenance mode can be enabled on your workspace or if the flag-based approach is the only supported option.

subject: Automation retries inflated report counts for failed syncs
description: We use a workflow to retry failed CRM syncs up to three times, and our operations report for sync failures now shows far more failures than actually occurred. It looks like each retry attempt is counted as a separate failed sync event in the report, which makes the team look worse than it is. I need help determining whether the report can be adjusted or whether the automation should be changed.

comments.message: Since enabling automatic retries for failed sync jobs, our weekly failure report has almost tripled. Looking at individual records, one bad job can appear multiple times before it finally succeeds.
That sounds expected because the report probably counts all failure events, including retries. You can just tell your team to ignore the inflated numbers.
I need a usable report, not something people have to mentally adjust. Can we report unique jobs that ever failed, instead of every retry event?

Yes, a better approach would be to report on unique job ID with a condition like 'had failure before success,' or to have the workflow stamp a final outcome field once retries are exhausted. I don't have access to rewrite the event model myself, so I'm escalating this to our reporting consultants and automation team with your current report ID and sample job IDs. They can advise whether to deduplicate in reporting or modify the retry workflow to produce cleaner summary fields.

subject: Admin swap completed but API pagination changed unexpectedly
description: We replaced our workspace owner and updated the main technical contact. Since then, one of our API integrations started receiving a different default page size, which broke an old parser. I don't know whether this is related or coincidence, but it happened immediately after the account update, and I'd like someone to verify before we rewrite code.

comments.message: Right after we changed the account owner, GET /v2/tickets started returning 50 records per page instead of 100 when no limit parameter is sent. Did the new admin profile change an API default somewhere?
API pagination defaults are documented and shouldn't be tied to account ownership. Please specify the limit explicitly in your requests; relying on defaults can cause variability.
That's fair, but it still changed at the same time as the admin swap. Is there a tenant setting for legacy pagination behavior?

I don't see any tenant-level legacy pagination toggle available to support, and I may be missing an internal migration flag tied to older accounts. Rather than guess, I'm escalating this to our API team to confirm whether the ownership change triggered a version-normalization job on your workspace.

subject: Custom field visibility broke automated team assignment
description: I changed our request form so the 'Region' field is only visible to certain departments, and now the automation that assigns tickets to regional queues is failing for some users. At the same time, a few newly added supervisors also can't see the admin page to override the queue manually. I think I may have created a conflict between field configuration, permissions, and the workflow.

comments.message: Before the change, every request had Region and the workflow sent APAC items to the APAC queue, EMEA to EMEA, etc. After restricting field visibility, requests from Finance come in with Region blank, and our new supervisors say they don't have the 'Queue Override' option anymore.

This sounds like two related configuration issues. If Region is hidden from Finance users and not defaulted anywhere, the workflow automation has no value to evaluate, so queue assignment can fail. Separately, the Queue Override option is usually tied to a supervisor permission set in User Administration, not just job title. Did the new supervisors receive the Supervisor Admin role, or were they only placed in a supervisor group?
They were added to a group called Supervisors, but I don't think we assigned the actual admin role. Also, Region isn't defaulted for Finance because we assumed their office mapping would fill it.

Got it. To fix this, either make Region available to those submitters, add a default/mapping rule before the assignment workflow runs, or update the automation to derive Region from office explicitly. And in User Administration, assign the proper supervisor permission role if they need Queue Override. Group membership alone won't expose that control. If you'd like, I can outline the safest order of changes so routing doesn't break again during the update.

subject: Overage charges after enabling event tracking
description: I turned on event tracking in our admin settings to support some workflow automations, and now our bill is much higher than normal. I didn't realize the configuration would start counting internal test events and webhook retries. I need help understanding which setting caused the overage and whether there is a way to change the configuration so we are only billed for the events we actually need.

comments.message: Last month we were around 40k events, but after I enabled event tracking for three pipelines, this month's invoice shows 168k billable events. Most of that seems to be QA traffic and failed webhook retries from one integration. Is there a config flag to exclude those? Right now billing says we're over plan.

Event tracking counts all processed events once the feature is enabled, including retries, so the invoice is probably correct. You can reduce usage by turning off the feature if you don't want charges.

Turning it off completely doesn't really help because we need it for production automations. I'm asking whether sandbox traffic and retries can be filtered out by configuration so we don't get charged for noise.
I don't believe there is a distinction there. Billing can't adjust event charges based on source, so you may just need to upgrade to the next plan.

subject: Cargo prorrateado inesperado y error 500 al crear usuarios
description: Añadí 3 usuarios nuevos y nos llegó un cargo prorrateado que no esperábamos porque pensábamos que se facturaba al siguiente ciclo. Desde ese mismo momento, cuando intento crear más usuarios desde el panel de administración, aparece un error 500. Probé en dos navegadores y con otro administrador.

comments.message: Añadí 3 usuarios nuevos y nos llegó un cargo prorrateado que no esperábamos porque pensábamos que se facturaba al siguiente ciclo. Desde ese mismo momento, cuando intento crear más usuarios desde el panel de administración, aparece un error 500. Probé en dos navegadores y con otro administrador.

Hola. En los planes mensuales, los asientos extra se cobran prorrateados de inmediato, así que ese cargo es esperado. Respecto al error 500, ¿podrían compartir la hora exacta y un correo de uno de los usuarios que intentaron crear? Quiero revisar los logs del backend.
Fue hoy sobre las 10:14 CET. Intenté crear a lucia@grupoalbor.es y luego a soporte2@grupoalbor.es. Necesito saber si el cargo se puede revertir porque aún no empezarán hasta el mes que viene.

Revisé los logs de las 10:14 y encontré que el directorio SCIM de su cuenta estaba devolviendo un atributo "department" vacío, lo que provoca el error 500 al crear usuarios manualmente. Desactivé temporalmente esa validación y ya deberían poder crearlos. Sobre el cargo prorrateado, en este tipo de plan no se revierte automáticamente, pero si quitan esos asientos antes de la próxima renovación puedo solicitar una nota de crédito excepcional. No está garantizada.

subject: Custom field values updated by API are not reflected in utilization report
description: We used the API to bulk update a custom numeric field called Billable Capacity across roughly 3,000 employee records. The values in the employee profile view are correct now, but the utilization report still shows the old percentages from before the update. I need to know whether API-written values are treated differently in reporting or if the report model needs to be refreshed after bulk edits.

comments.message: The API update succeeded and the source records look right, but the utilization report is still using the old Billable Capacity values.

Thanks for the detail. API updates and manual edits should both flow into reporting, but custom numeric fields used in calculated reports are only recomputed during the nightly metrics job unless the report is rebuilt manually.
We can't wait until tonight. Is there any workaround?

I wasn't able to safely trigger a recalculation from support because your report is based on a locked shared model. I've opened an internal request to the reporting team for an expedited rebuild, but for the moment the live report will remain stale. I'll update the ticket once they confirm timing.

subject: Importação duplicou contatos após upload CSV
description: Fiz uma importação de contatos por CSV ontem à noite e hoje percebi que vários registros foram criados em duplicidade. Eu marquei a opção para atualizar registros existentes pelo e-mail, mas mesmo assim apareceram dois ou três contatos com o mesmo e-mail em listas diferentes. Preciso limpar isso sem perder o histórico de atividades e entender o que causou o problema.

comments.message: Fiz uma importação de contatos por CSV ontem à noite e hoje percebi que vários registros foram criados em duplicidade. Eu marquei a opção para atualizar registros existentes pelo e-mail, mas mesmo assim apareceram dois ou três contatos com o mesmo e-mail em listas diferentes. Preciso limpar isso sem perder o histórico de atividades e entender o que causou o problema.

Entendi. Quando a opção de correspondência por e-mail é usada, o sistema só atualiza o registro existente se o campo e-mail estiver exatamente igual ao que já está salvo. Diferenças como espaços extras, e-mails em campo secundário ou importação para outro workspace podem gerar novos registros. Para corrigir sem perder histórico, vá em Contatos > Ações em massa > Mesclar duplicados e use o e-mail como chave. Se quiser, exporte primeiro uma amostra dos duplicados para validar quais registros serão mantidos.

Verifiquei aqui e alguns e-mails no CSV tinham espaço no final. Também notei que a importação foi feita pelo meu colega em uma visualização compartilhada. A mesclagem preserva notas e tarefas dos dois registros?

Sim. Na mesclagem, o registro principal mantém a linha do tempo e o sistema incorpora notas, tarefas e atividades vinculadas do registro secundário, embora campos conflitantes fiquem com o valor do registro escolhido como principal. Como houve espaços no final, isso explica boa parte das duplicidades. Recomendo limpar o CSV, executar a mesclagem em lote e depois ativar a validação de campos na importação para bloquear espaços em branco ocultos.

subject: Archived user reactivation brings back account without permissions
description: I reactivated a previously archived employee account, but it came back as a blank member with no group assignments, no project access, and no custom role settings. The reactivation screen said it would restore prior access. I retried with another archived test account and saw the same thing, so I’m worried this is not just one bad record.

comments.message: I reactivated a previously archived employee account, but it came back as a blank member with no group assignments, no project access, and no custom role settings. The reactivation screen said it would restore prior access. I retried with another archived test account and saw the same thing, so I’m worried this is not just one bad record.

I’m sorry about the disruption. To troubleshoot, can you confirm whether both archived users were deactivated before or after your workspace enabled the new permissions model last month? Also, do you see any restoration warning in the audit log?
Both were archived before we enabled the new permissions model. The audit log says "reactivation completed" and then a separate line says "0 assignments restored." No warning beyond that.

That’s enough to narrow it down. It sounds like older archived accounts don’t map correctly to the new permissions model during reactivation, which would explain why the UI promises restoration but none occurs. I’ve reported this as a bug with your audit log evidence. For now, restoring prior access will need to be done manually account by account; I know that’s tedious, but I don’t want to suggest another reactivation test until engineering confirms the fix.

subject: Se mezclaron registros entre dos datasets después de cambiar la clave primaria
description: Ayer cambiamos la clave primaria de un dataset de "email" a "customer_id" porque había muchos correos compartidos. Después de eso, varias filas quedaron asociadas al cliente equivocado y algunos registros antiguos desaparecieron de las vistas filtradas. Necesito ayuda para entender si el sistema reindexó mal o si perdimos relaciones al cambiar la clave.

comments.message: Ayer cambiamos la clave primaria de un dataset de "email" a "customer_id" porque había muchos correos compartidos. Después de eso, varias filas quedaron asociadas al cliente equivocado y algunos registros antiguos desaparecieron de las vistas filtradas. Necesito ayuda para entender si el sistema reindexó mal o si perdimos relaciones al cambiar la clave.

Cuando se cambia la clave primaria, el sistema vuelve a reconciliar registros y las relaciones dependientes pueden reescribirse usando el nuevo índice. Si había filas con customer_id vacío, duplicado o con formato inconsistente, es posible que se hayan colapsado registros o roto asociaciones. Lo primero es validar un muestreo de IDs afectados y revisar en el log de reindexación cuántos conflictos detectó el proceso.
Eso no lo puedo ver yo desde la interfaz. Solo veo que el cambio terminó "correctamente".

Tiene razón, ese log no es visible para todos los roles. Voy a pedir al equipo técnico una revisión del reindexado y si encuentran conflictos podrán indicarnos si conviene restaurar el índice anterior o relanzar la reconciliación con una tabla limpia de customer_id. Envíeme 5-10 ejemplos de registros mezclados y la hora aproximada del cambio para acotar la auditoría.

subject: Former employee still listed as primary contact
description: Our old finance lead left months ago, but she's still showing as the primary contact on the account. I want to replace her with our controller and make sure future invoices and admin notifications go to the right people. I'm not sure whether I should edit her profile, deactivate her, or assign someone else. Can you walk me through the correct setup?

comments.message: Our old finance lead left months ago, but she's still showing as the primary contact on the account. I want to replace her with our controller and make sure future invoices and admin notifications go to the right people. I'm not sure whether I should edit her profile, deactivate her, or assign someone else. Can you walk me through the correct setup?

Sure — the cleanest approach is not to edit the former employee's profile to impersonate the new person. Instead, invite your controller under their own email address, assign the needed admin or billing permissions, then update the primary billing/contact fields under Billing Settings. After confirming the controller is receiving notifications, you can deactivate the former employee. That preserves your audit trail and avoids confusion in activity history.
We don't want the controller to have full admin rights. Can they still receive invoices without being a workspace admin?

Yes, in most cases you can set someone as a billing contact without giving them full workspace admin rights. If your current permission model separates billing from admin access, assign the billing role only. If your account is on the legacy permissions model, you may need a broader role, so check the Permissions page first. If you send me a screenshot of the roles available, I can point to the exact option.

subject: Users with viewer role get 'Access Denied' after successful login
description: Several of our viewer-only users can log in successfully, but once they land in the app they immediately see an 'Access Denied' page instead of the reports they should have access to. Admins and editors are unaffected. I already checked that the users are active and assigned to the correct team. Can you help figure out whether this is a permissions bug and what we should change?

comments.message: One strange detail: if I temporarily promote one of these users to Editor, they can get in. When I switch them back to Viewer, the error returns on next login.

Thanks, that pattern suggests the login itself is working and the issue is happening during post-login authorization. Can you confirm whether your default landing page for the workspace was recently changed to a section that viewers cannot open, such as Settings or Audit Log?
Yes, we changed the default landing page to Audit Log last week so our ops team would land there first.

That explains the behavior. Viewer users are authenticating correctly, but after login they're being redirected to a page their role can't access, so they hit Access Denied. Please change the workspace default landing page to Home or Reports, or create a role-based bookmark for viewer users. Separately, I agree the product should handle this more gracefully by redirecting viewers to an allowed page instead of showing a hard denial, so I've filed that as a bug report with our product team.

subject: Merged duplicate users and now neither account can be edited
description: We had two profiles for the same employee, so I used the merge account tool. The merge said it completed, but now the resulting account opens with missing fields and any attempt to change the manager, title, or seat type throws a generic save error. We need to clean this up before our access review tomorrow.

comments.message: We had two profiles for the same employee, so I used the merge account tool. The merge said it completed, but now the resulting account opens with missing fields and any attempt to change the manager, title, or seat type throws a generic save error. We need to clean this up before our access review tomorrow.
Thanks for the context. Please try exporting the user list and reimporting that employee row with the correct fields populated. That usually repairs missing profile data after a merge.
I tried a CSV reimport already. The import log says the row was skipped because the account is in a locked state. That seems related to the save errors.

Understood. In that case, there may be a stuck merge transaction on the account. I don’t have a direct fix from support, but I’ve sent this to our data operations team to unlock the profile and verify the merged record integrity. I can’t promise it will be completed before tomorrow’s review, though.

subject: Bulk update stuck on processing and partially overwrote custom fields
description: I ran a bulk update on 2,100 opportunities to populate a new custom field for territory assignment. The job sat in "Processing" for almost an hour, then completed, but only about half the records were updated and some existing values in another custom text field were replaced with blanks. I retried with a 20-row file and saw the same blanking behavior on records where the source column was empty. I need help figuring out whether this is expected import behavior or a regression, and whether the original values can be restored.

comments.message: I ran a bulk update on 2,100 opportunities to populate a new custom field for territory assignment. The job sat in "Processing" for almost an hour, then completed, but only about half the records were updated and some existing values in another custom text field were replaced with blanks. I retried with a 20-row file and saw the same blanking behavior on records where the source column was empty. I need help figuring out whether this is expected import behavior or a regression, and whether the original values can be restored.

Blank source cells usually clear the destination field on update, so that part is expected. For the records that didn’t update, it may have timed out because the file was too large. Please split the file into smaller batches and re-run the import.
That doesn’t explain why the UI preview said "skip empty values" was enabled. Also, these same records updated fine in smaller tests except for the blanking issue.

If the preview showed that option, then it may have been cached incorrectly in the UI. Unfortunately we can’t restore overwritten field values from support, so your best option is to re-import from a backup export if you have one. I don’t see a bug on our side at the moment.

subject: How should we archive old tickets before a data retention purge?
description: We need to reduce old support data before our renewal, but I don’t want my team deleting closed tickets in a way that removes reporting we still need. What’s the best practice for archiving vs deleting, and how can I train the team to do this consistently?

comments.message: We need to reduce old support data before our renewal, but I don’t want my team deleting closed tickets in a way that removes reporting we still need. What’s the best practice for archiving vs deleting, and how can I train the team to do this consistently?
You can probably just delete any closed tickets older than a year. Most customers do that and it keeps things simple.
That’s what I’m worried about — we still use annual trend reports and audit older escalations. Is there an archive workflow instead?
You may want to export them to CSV first. I’m not sure whether archived tickets remain in reports, so I’d suggest checking with your admin internally.

subject: Custom admin role can edit users but not build headcount report
description: I set up a custom admin role for our HR operations lead. The role works for adding and deactivating users in her business unit, but she gets an error when trying to create a headcount report from the employee dataset. She can view an existing headcount dashboard just fine. I’m not sure if custom user administration roles inherit reporting limitations by default or if there’s a missing permission toggle somewhere.

comments.message: I set up a custom admin role for our HR operations lead. The role works for adding and deactivating users in her business unit, but she gets an error when trying to create a headcount report from the employee dataset. She can view an existing headcount dashboard just fine. I’m not sure if custom user administration roles inherit reporting limitations by default or if there’s a missing permission toggle somewhere.

Viewing dashboards and building reports are controlled separately. A custom admin role can absolutely allow user administration while still blocking report creation if the reporting-builder permission or dataset access wasn’t included. Please review the custom role and any attached permission groups for: report creation, dataset access to Employee/Headcount, and export rights if needed.
I see report viewing and export, but not report creation. If I turn on report creation, will she be able to build reports for all business units?

Not necessarily. Report creation lets her build reports, but the data scope still follows the datasets and row-level access assigned to her account. If her business-unit filter is configured correctly, she should only be able to build headcount reports for that unit while continuing to manage users there. After enabling the permission, test with a simple employee count report to confirm the scope is correct.

subject: Changed company name but contract emails still go to former controller
description: Our company was acquired last month, and I'm trying to update the legal entity details and the main account contact. Right now invoice reminders and renewal notices are still going to our former controller, who no longer works here. I also need to understand whether changing the account owner will affect our team's admin access.

comments.message: I updated our company display name in the app, but contract-related emails are still going to the old finance contact. Can you switch the account owner to me and confirm what else we should update?
If you changed the company name, the rest should usually follow automatically. The old emails may stop on their own after the system syncs.
It's been two weeks, and they are definitely still going to the old person. Also, I don't know if account owner and billing contact are the same in your system.

You're right to question that, and I should have been clearer. Display name changes do not automatically update account owner or billing/renewal contacts. Those are often separate records. I can explain the difference: the account owner/admin role affects in-product administration, while billing and contract contacts determine who receives financial and renewal communications. Because changing legal entity and contract contacts can touch subscription records, I'm escalating this to our billing/account operations team to update the authorized contacts securely. In the meantime, please send the new contact name, title, and email from your company domain, and keep your existing admins in place since admin access won't automatically transfer with the billing contact update.

subject: Dashboard metric changed after automation started backfilling fields
description: After we launched a workflow that backfills missing region and segment values on account records, one of our dashboard reports started showing a much higher conversion rate for EMEA than before. I'm trying to understand whether the automation corrected bad data and changed the report legitimately, or whether historical records are being rewritten in a way that distorts period-based reporting. If needed, please escalate this because leadership is questioning the integrity of the dashboard.

comments.message: Our EMEA conversion dashboard jumped from 21% to 34% immediately after we turned on a field-backfill workflow. The workflow updates region and segment based on account owner rules. Did the automation rewrite historical records that the report uses?

Yes, that is likely. Your report is using current account properties, not values as of the time of conversion, so the workflow's backfill changed the dimensions for historical records too. The metric increase may be mathematically correct under current-state reporting, but it won't match your previous period snapshots.
Can we preserve the automation and still report using the values at the time of conversion?

You would need either a historical snapshot report or a workflow change that stamps fixed fields like 'Region at conversion' when the conversion event occurs. I don't see snapshot reporting enabled on your plan, so I've escalated this to your account team to confirm options and to our technical specialists to review whether your current workflow can be modified to stamp immutable values without breaking existing automations.

subject: Need a simple explanation of API rate limits for our developers
description: I'm the admin for our account, and our engineering team keeps asking me why some API imports fail in the afternoon but work fine in the morning. I need an explanation I can actually relay to non-specialists, especially how the per-minute limits and concurrent job limits work. Also, is there a recommended way to avoid hitting those limits during a large backfill?

comments.message: I'm the admin for our account, and our engineering team keeps asking me why some API imports fail in the afternoon but work fine in the morning. I need an explanation I can actually relay to non-specialists, especially how the per-minute limits and concurrent job limits work. Also, is there a recommended way to avoid hitting those limits during a large backfill?

Rate limits basically mean the system only accepts a certain number of requests before telling you to slow down. So if imports fail later in the day, your team is probably just sending too many requests. They should reduce volume and try again at night.
I need something more specific than that. We have multiple integrations, and I want to teach the team how to design around the limits instead of guessing.

Understood. I don't want to oversimplify this further because the exact limits can vary by endpoint and job type. I'm escalating your ticket to our technical support team so they can provide the current per-minute and concurrency guidance for your plan, along with recommended backoff and batching practices you can share with engineering.

subject: Can’t figure out why our email replies are attaching to the wrong ticket
description: When customers reply to one of our support emails, the message sometimes ends up on an older ticket instead of the open one. It doesn’t happen every time, which makes it hard to pin down. I need help troubleshooting the threading behavior, and I’d also like a plain-English explanation of what identifiers your system uses to decide which ticket an incoming reply belongs to.

comments.message: When customers reply to one of our support emails, the message sometimes ends up on an older ticket instead of the open one. It doesn’t happen every time, which makes it hard to pin down. I need help troubleshooting the threading behavior, and I’d also like a plain-English explanation of what identifiers your system uses to decide which ticket an incoming reply belongs to.
Usually the system matches by subject line, so if the customer keeps the same subject it will go onto the same ticket. You can ask customers to change the subject when they open a new issue.
That doesn’t really fit what we’re seeing, because some replies with changed subjects still attach to old tickets. We need to know what the system is actually checking.

You’re right — subject alone is not the primary method, and my earlier explanation was incomplete. Incoming email threading typically relies first on hidden message identifiers in the email headers, such as In-Reply-To and References, plus the unique reply address or ticket token embedded in the original outbound email. Subject is more of a fallback signal. If customers start a new issue by replying to an old thread, their mail client often preserves those headers, which can cause the reply to attach to the older ticket even if they edit the subject. To troubleshoot, capture one misthreaded email’s raw headers and compare them with the original outbound ticket email. A practical prevention step is to train agents to send a fresh outbound message or use a 'new ticket' mailto link when the issue is unrelated.

subject: Guest collaborator blocked after accepting invite
description: We invited an external consultant as a guest collaborator, but after accepting the invite she gets 'your account is not permitted to access this workspace.' She can log in, just not open our workspace. We need this escalated because the project starts today and the invite flow appears broken.

comments.message: Her email is from a different company domain, which is normal for guest access in our plan. I checked and the guest seat is available.
External domains are often blocked by workspace security settings. You may need to upgrade your plan to allow guest users.
We already have three other guests active in this workspace on the same plan. This seems specific to this new invite, not a plan limitation.

Understood. In that case I’ve escalated the ticket to our access provisioning team to inspect the guest entitlement on this specific user record. Please don’t delete and recreate the guest yet, because they may want to inspect the current failed state first.

subject: Need report access retained while changing regional admin structure
description: We’re restructuring our account so two regional admins will become one global admin plus three site managers. I need to change the user roles this week, but each of those people owns custom reports used by their sites. I’m worried the role changes will either break the reports or expose data they shouldn’t see afterward. Can you outline the safest order of operations so we preserve reporting and keep user administration aligned with the new org structure?

comments.message: We’re restructuring our account so two regional admins will become one global admin plus three site managers. I need to change the user roles this week, but each of those people owns custom reports used by their sites. I’m worried the role changes will either break the reports or expose data they shouldn’t see afterward. Can you outline the safest order of operations so we preserve reporting and keep user administration aligned with the new org structure?

Yes — the safest sequence is: 1) inventory which reports and schedules each current admin owns, 2) transfer ownership of any cross-site reports to the future global admin before role changes, 3) confirm each site manager has a role that includes only site-level user administration plus access to the specific reports they need, and 4) only then downgrade the old regional admins. If a report uses data outside a site manager’s eventual scope, it may still exist after transfer but will render partially or fail for that manager unless it’s redesigned.
That helps. Is there a way to test what a site manager will see before we finalize the role changes?

If you have the impersonation feature, use that after assigning the draft role to a pilot user account; otherwise, create a temporary test user with the same site-manager role and reporting groups. Review both user admin pages and the key reports before making the production changes. If you send a list of critical reports, I can suggest which ones are most likely to break due to scope changes.

subject: Google Sheets row update automation writes to wrong tab
description: I set up a workflow to update a row in Google Sheets whenever a customer success task is completed. The integration is connected correctly and the automation runs without errors, but the data is being written into an older tab with a similar name instead of the active quarter tab. I changed the sheet selection last week, so I expected future runs to use the new tab.

comments.message: The workflow says it updated the spreadsheet successfully, but all the new entries keep landing in Q1 Archive instead of Q2 Active. I already opened the Google Sheets step and reselected the correct tab.
If the step reports success, then Google may be caching the worksheet reference. Please wait a few hours and test again after disconnecting and reconnecting your Google account.
We waited overnight and reconnected the account this morning. It still writes to the archive tab.

Thanks. I may have sent you in the wrong direction. This is usually caused by the workflow step retaining the old worksheet ID even though the tab label was changed in the editor. Please duplicate the Google Sheets action, remap the fields in the duplicated step, and remove the original one. That forces a fresh worksheet binding. If it still targets the wrong tab after duplication, send the workflow URL and we’ll inspect the stored sheet ID.

subject: Nie mogę zalogować się po usunięciu starego telefonu
description: Zmieniłem telefon i nie mam już dostępu do aplikacji uwierzytelniającej. Hasło pamiętam, ale bez kodu nie wejdę do systemu. Nie zapisałem też kodów zapasowych.

comments.message: Zmieniłem telefon i straciłem dostęp do kodów 2FA. Nie mam kodów zapasowych, a potrzebuję się zalogować.

Rozumiem. W celu wyłączenia 2FA muszę potwierdzić tożsamość. Proszę podać nazwę workspace, ostatnią datę udanego logowania oraz cztery ostatnie znaki numeru karty używanej do płatności, jeśli ma Pan rolę administratora rozliczeń.
Workspace: Atlas Ops, ostatnie logowanie 3 czerwca około 9:00, końcówka karty 1842.

Dziękuję, dane zgadzają się. Wyłączyłem 2FA dla tego konta na najbliższe logowanie. Po wejściu do systemu proszę od razu skonfigurować nową aplikację uwierzytelniającą i zapisać kody zapasowe. Dla bezpieczeństwa oznaczyłem też konto do dodatkowej kontroli przy następnym logowaniu z nowego urządzenia.

subject: How do I teach agents the difference between internal notes and customer replies?
description: I’m onboarding several new support agents next week, and I want to avoid the classic mistake where someone sends an internal comment to a customer or leaves a public reply when they meant to discuss the case internally. Can you help me explain the difference clearly and suggest a simple training exercise?

comments.message: We had this happen in our old system, so I’m trying to make the lesson really stick.

That’s a smart topic to emphasize early. The simplest explanation is: customer replies are visible to the customer and become part of the external conversation, while internal notes are only for your team and are used for collaboration, context, or handoff information. A good exercise is to present five example messages and ask trainees to decide whether each belongs in a public reply or an internal note — for example, “Please try clearing your cache” versus “Customer is upset; escalate if they mention cancellation.”
I like that. Any quick way to reduce mistakes beyond training?

Yes — combine training with process cues. Encourage agents to pause before sending, use clear labels in macros, and if your workspace allows it, set default composer behavior carefully so it opens on the safer option for your team’s workflow. You can also have supervisors review a sample of early replies from new hires during their first week.

subject: Audit report shows disabled users as active managers
description: I was reviewing our quarterly user administration audit and noticed that two disabled users still appear as active managers in a permissions report. When I click into their profiles they are clearly deactivated, but the exported report is wrong and leadership is asking whether our access review can be trusted. One of those users also used to own a compliance report schedule, so I’m not sure if stale reporting data is tied to the account change history.

comments.message: I was reviewing our quarterly user administration audit and noticed that two disabled users still appear as active managers in a permissions report. When I click into their profiles they are clearly deactivated, but the exported report is wrong and leadership is asking whether our access review can be trusted. One of those users also used to own a compliance report schedule, so I’m not sure if stale reporting data is tied to the account change history.

This can happen when the user status field in reporting lags behind account updates or when the report is based on historical role snapshots rather than current user state. First, check the report definition to see whether it uses “Current Status” or an audit/event dataset. If it’s tied to a historical dataset, disabled users can still appear as active for the time period covered.
The export is supposed to be current-state only, and it was run today. We disabled those users last month.

Got it. That suggests either a stale reporting cache or a mismatch between the user directory table and the permissions-report dataset. I can’t verify the backend from here, so I’m escalating this to engineering with your export timestamp and the affected user IDs. For now, I’d avoid using that specific permissions export for the audit and rely on the user directory view as the source of truth until we confirm the reporting sync status.

subject: Zendesk ticket fields not appearing in support performance report
description: We connected Zendesk to pull custom ticket fields into your analytics, but our support performance report still only shows standard ticket properties. The custom priority and escalation reason fields are visible in the integration mapping screen as synced, yet they aren't available as report dimensions. We need those fields for our weekly operations review.

comments.message: I mapped two Zendesk custom fields last week and the sync completed. However, when I edit our support performance report, those fields are missing from the list of dimensions and filters. Standard fields are there, custom ones are not.

Custom Zendesk fields should become reportable after the next schema refresh, which usually happens automatically within 24 hours. Since it's been longer than that, could you send the field keys and tell me whether they appear in any other report builder screens?
The keys are custom_priority_level and escalation_reason_code. They don't appear anywhere in reporting, but they do show up under synced fields in the integration admin page.

I checked your account notes and don't see a completed reporting schema refresh after those fields were added. The integration mapping looks fine, but the reporting layer hasn't exposed the new columns yet. I'm escalating this so our team can trigger a schema sync manually and verify whether those custom fields are eligible dimensions in your plan's reporting model.

subject: Overage fees after changing ticket retention settings
description: We reduced our ticket retention from 24 months to 6 months because we were trying to lower storage costs, but this month's bill still includes data overage charges. I expected old ticket attachments to be purged after the setting change. Is the retention configuration not tied to billing, or is there another cleanup step we need to run?

comments.message: I changed retention in admin settings two weeks ago and assumed old data would be removed automatically. Our invoice still has storage overages and it's actually higher than last month.

Retention settings control how long new records are kept going forward. They don't immediately purge historical attachments unless a cleanup job runs, and billing reflects current stored volume until that happens.
I don't remember seeing anything about a cleanup job. Can you trigger it?

I can't trigger the purge from my side, but I can see your workspace hasn't had the archival cleanup scheduled yet. I've opened an internal request for our platform team to run it and asked billing to review the overage once the storage recalculates. In the meantime, the invoice is still technically valid based on current usage.

subject: API import is creating contacts that no admin can see
description: We built a custom integration that imports customer records nightly through your API. Since we tightened role permissions, the import still says it succeeds, but the new records aren't visible to our regional admins and some appear with no owner at all. I suspect this is related to the service account and workspace permissions. I need help figuring out whether the data is landing in the wrong business unit, whether ownership can be set during import, and what admin setting is blocking visibility.

comments.message: Our API import continues to create records after a permissions change, but admins in EMEA can't see most of them and some records show owner = blank. The integration uses a service account. How should this be configured?

This sounds like a scoping issue between the integration user and your admin roles. Records created by the API can inherit the default business unit or visibility scope of the service account if ownership isn't explicitly passed. Please check whether your payload includes owner_id and business_unit_id, and whether the service account is limited to a single workspace.

The payload only passes email, company, and custom fields. We don't send owner_id or business unit today. The service account is in the global IT team, but regional admins only have unit-level access.

That would explain both symptoms. The records are likely being created under the service account's default scope with no direct owner, so regional admins outside that scope won't see them. Update the integration to assign an owner or business unit on create, or broaden the relevant admin roles to include those imported records. If you'd like, I can share the API fields and a sample payload for ownership mapping.

subject: Need admin training advice for deactivation workflow confusion
description: I'm training our HR admins on how to deactivate users properly, and I'm getting stuck on the difference between deactivating, suspending, and fully deleting an account. We also had one case where a deactivated user still appeared as an approver in an old workflow, which makes me nervous about teaching the wrong cleanup steps. Can you help me explain this simply and tell me what we should do next?

comments.message: I need a plain-English explanation for admins, plus guidance on why a deactivated user might still show up in a workflow.

In plain English: suspending temporarily blocks access, deactivation removes active access and generally takes the user out of day-to-day operations, and deletion permanently removes the account record where allowed. A deactivated user may still appear in historical records or as a referenced step in an older workflow design, especially if the workflow stores the person object rather than dynamically looking up active users.
Should admins manually replace those references before deactivating someone?

As a best practice, yes — for training I'd recommend telling admins to review ownerships, approval chains, scheduled reports, and workflow references before deactivation. Because you already saw a deactivated user remain in a workflow, I'm escalating this to our workflow operations team so they can confirm whether that behavior is expected in your account and whether any cleanup tools are available.

subject: Slack-Benachrichtigungen kommen nur in einem Channel an
description: Ich habe die Slack-Integration für drei verschiedene Projekte eingerichtet. Benachrichtigungen werden aber nur in einem Channel gepostet, obwohl in den anderen beiden Projekten dieselben Trigger aktiv sind. Ich habe die App in Slack bereits neu autorisiert. Woran kann das liegen?

comments.message: Im Projekt 'Support DACH' funktioniert alles. In 'Onboarding' und 'Enterprise' wird dagegen nichts an Slack gesendet, obwohl dort dieselben Automationen aktiv sind.
Bitte prüfen Sie zuerst, ob die Slack-App in allen drei Workspaces installiert wurde. Wenn sie nur in einem Channel postet, ist meistens der Slack-Webhook abgelaufen.
Wir haben nur einen Workspace, aber mehrere Channels. Die App ist installiert, und die Verbindung wird in Ihrem Admin-Bereich bei allen Projekten als aktiv angezeigt.
Dann sollte es reichen, die Automationen einmal aus- und wieder einzuschalten. Mehr können wir auf unserer Seite bei Slack leider nicht beeinflussen.

subject: Login page branding change seems to have broken invite acceptance
description: We customized the login page with our own domain and branding, and after that I changed the self-serve registration toggle for a campaign. Since then, invited users are hitting a message that registration is closed, even though they were directly invited. Existing users can sign in normally. I need to get invites working again without opening general signup.

comments.message: After setting up our branded login page and turning off self-serve registration, invited users can no longer accept their invites. They are being told registration is closed. Current users can still log in.

That is odd. Invite acceptance should not depend on the public registration setting. Can you share whether your custom domain is pointing to the correct workspace and whether 'Treat invites as registrations' is enabled in your onboarding configuration?
I found that option and it is enabled. We turned it on months ago for a partner rollout and forgot about it.

That explains the behavior. With 'Treat invites as registrations' enabled, invited users are routed through the registration gate, so disabling self-serve registration blocks them too. Turn that option off in Onboarding Configuration, resend one invite, and the user should be able to create their account without reopening public signup.

subject: Permissions mismatch after Marketo sync created records under disabled reps
description: We connected Marketo for lead sync, and after a recent user cleanup we disabled several sales reps who had left the company. New leads are still arriving assigned to those disabled reps, and our current team managers can't edit some of the synced records because ownership and region visibility don't line up anymore. I need to know whether Marketo is still mapping to old user IDs, how to reassign incoming records to active users, and whether we can update access rules without corrupting historical lead data.

comments.message: Marketo is still assigning new synced leads to reps we disabled last quarter. Managers also can't edit some of those leads because the records sit in the old region scope. How do we stop this?

This is usually caused by stale owner mapping in the Marketo integration plus record-level visibility rules. Check the Marketo-to-platform user mapping first and replace any disabled reps with active users or a routing queue. Then review whether your managers' roles include edit rights across the legacy regions where those records are being created.
If we change the mapping now, will existing leads move too, or only new ones? We don't want to lose attribution history.

Changing the mapping will generally affect new syncs only; existing leads usually keep their current owner unless you run a separate bulk reassignment. To preserve attribution history, reassign ownership without altering the original created-by fields. If you'd like, I can escalate this to our integrations team to validate the safest sequence for remapping and backfilling access.

subject: Locked out after MFA reset and workflow approvals are piling up
description: I got locked out after resetting my phone, and now all purchase requests are stuck waiting on my approval workflow step. Our team needs both a way to get me back in and guidance on how to avoid one person blocking automations in the future.

comments.message: I can't log in because MFA is tied to my old device, and I'm the approver in a workflow that blocks PO requests over $10k. Is there a way to reset my access and make sure this process doesn't stall every time one approver is unavailable?

I have reset your MFA and you should receive a new enrollment email shortly. For the workflow itself, I suggest deleting your approver step and replacing it with another manager so items can move again.
I got the MFA email, thanks, but deleting my step doesn't sound right. We still need finance review when I'm out.

You're right, I oversimplified that. A better pattern is to use an approval group or an 'any of these approvers' step with a fallback assignee, instead of one named user. Once you're back in, go to the PO approval workflow, edit the approval node, and replace the individual approver with your Finance Leads group plus a 24-hour escalation rule. That keeps requests moving even if one person's login is blocked.

subject: CSV import integration mapped correctly but executive report still wrong
description: We use the scheduled CSV import integration to load renewal data every night. The import summary says the rows are accepted and the account pages show the latest renewal dates, but our executive renewals report still shows old ARR values for a subset of accounts. I need support to sort out whether this is due to the import mapping, the report cache, or something deeper that should be escalated.

comments.message: Nightly CSV imports are succeeding, but the 'Executive Renewals Forecast' report is not picking up updated ARR for about 50 accounts. Can someone check both the import and report pipeline?

I reviewed the latest import job and the ARR column is mapped correctly. The issue is that your report uses a derived metric called 'Current Renewal ARR,' which only recalculates when both ARR and renewal status change in the same processing cycle. Your CSV import updates ARR nightly, but status is synced from another source later in the day, so the report can lag or stay stale for some records.
So is there any fix, or are we just stuck with a bad exec report?

I manually triggered a recompute for the affected dataset, and those 50 accounts should now reflect the latest ARR in the report. This is a product limitation rather than a mapping mistake, and it could recur until the metric logic is changed. I've escalated the behavior to our reporting team with your import configuration details so they can review a more permanent fix.

subject: Addon charge remains after disabling multilingual help center
description: We turned off the multilingual help center and unpublished all translated articles because we were consolidating to English only. However, this month's invoice still includes the language localization add-on. Is there another setting outside the help center config that keeps billing active?

comments.message: All secondary languages are disabled in the help center settings and the translated content is unpublished. We expected the add-on to fall off automatically.
Usually when all languages are disabled, the localization add-on should no longer be used. It may just take one billing cycle to update.
That doesn't really answer whether we need to cancel something manually. We don't want this charge next month too.

You shouldn't need to do anything else if the product configuration is off. I'd recommend waiting for the next invoice, and if it still appears, contact billing directly since support can't see add-on state changes in detail.

subject: Campo obrigatório não aparece no formulário para alguns usuários
description: Criei um campo personalizado obrigatório no formulário de onboarding, mas alguns usuários do meu time dizem que não conseguem vê-lo nem preencher. Para mim aparece normalmente no editor. Já limpei cache e continua igual para eles.

comments.message: O campo se chama "Segmento atendido" e deveria aparecer logo abaixo de "Número de funcionários". O problema acontece com usuários com perfil de vendedor.

Oi! Isso geralmente acontece quando o campo foi adicionado ao formulário, mas a visibilidade dele está restrita por permissão ou por layout. Você pode abrir Configurações > Campos > Segmento atendido e verificar se ele está visível para o perfil Vendedor?
Fui lá agora. Estava visível só para administradores e gerentes. Mudei para todos, mas no formulário deles ainda não aparece.

Obrigado por conferir. Então o próximo ponto é o layout condicional do formulário. Se houver uma regra para exibir o campo apenas quando "País" ou outro campo tiver um valor específico, o vendedor pode não vê-lo. Peça para eles atualizarem a página em janela anônima e confirme se existe alguma condição de exibição nesse campo. Se quiser, posso te orientar a localizar essa regra passo a passo.

subject: Freigabe-Workflow überspringt zweite Genehmigungsstufe
description: Ich habe einen zweistufigen Freigabe-Workflow für Rabattangebote gebaut. Seit heute werden Anfragen mit mehr als 20 Prozent Rabatt direkt als genehmigt markiert, ohne dass die zweite Stufe beim Vertriebsleiter landet. Das ist für uns kritisch, weil dadurch bereits zwei Angebote falsch freigegeben wurden. Ich brauche Hilfe bei der Ursache.

comments.message: Unser Workflow sollte ab 20 Prozent Rabatt zuerst den Teamleiter und dann den Vertriebsleiter ansprechen. Im Audit-Log steht aber nur, dass die Anfrage automatisch abgeschlossen wurde.

Verstanden. Bitte öffnen Sie den Schritt nach der ersten Genehmigung und prüfen Sie, ob dort eine Verzweigung definiert ist. Häufig springt der Workflow direkt zum Abschluss, wenn für die zweite Stufe kein gültiger Empfänger gefunden wird oder die Bedingung falsch formuliert ist.
Ich sehe eine Bedingung "Rabatt > 20" für die zweite Freigabe. Einer der Fälle hatte genau 20 Prozent. Ein anderer hatte 25 Prozent, dort steht beim Genehmiger-Feld "leer".

Dann gibt es vermutlich zwei Probleme: Exakt 20 Prozent fällt mit "> 20" aus der zweiten Stufe heraus, und bei 25 Prozent kann kein Freigabe-Task erzeugt werden, wenn das Feld für den Vertriebsleiter leer ist. Ich empfehle, die Bedingung auf ">= 20" zu ändern und im Workflow statt des leeren dynamischen Felds einen festen Fallback-Genehmiger zu hinterlegen. Ich gebe den zweiten Punkt zusätzlich an unser Produktteam weiter, weil der Schritt in so einem Fall nicht einfach als abgeschlossen enden sollte.

subject: Locked out after downgrade and still being charged for extra seats
description: I downgraded our account from 25 seats to 10 last week because several contractors rolled off. Since then, two things are wrong: I still got billed for 25 seats on the renewal invoice, and I can no longer sign in as the billing admin to update the subscription because the system says my access is restricted. I’m the original owner on the account and need to get back in so I can fix the invoice before finance pays it.

comments.message: I downgraded our account from 25 seats to 10 last week because several contractors rolled off. Since then, two things are wrong: I still got billed for 25 seats on the renewal invoice, and I can no longer sign in as the billing admin to update the subscription because the system says my access is restricted. I’m the original owner on the account and need to get back in so I can fix the invoice before finance pays it.

Thanks for reporting this. I checked your workspace and can see the seat reduction was submitted after the renewal invoice was generated, so the invoice still reflects 25 seats. I’ve opened a billing adjustment request for the 15-seat difference. On the access side, your account was flagged because SSO enforcement was turned on and your user was not mapped to the IdP group with admin rights. I’ve temporarily restored local admin login for your user for 24 hours so you can sign in and review billing.
I’m back in now, thanks. Do I need to pay the full invoice first, or can you send a corrected one?
