Please hold payment on the current invoice. Our billing team will void it and issue a corrected invoice for 10 seats within 1 business day. I’ve added that note to the case and you’ll receive the updated invoice by email.

subject: Usunięcie byłego pracownika z konta nie zwolniło miejsca
description: Wczoraj dezaktywowałem pracownika, który odszedł z firmy, bo chcemy dodać nową osobę bez kupowania kolejnej licencji. System nadal pokazuje brak wolnych miejsc. Nie wiem, czy trzeba coś jeszcze zmienić w koncie.

comments.message: Wyłączyłem konto użytkownika m.kulesza@firma.pl, ale przy dodawaniu nowej osoby nadal mam komunikat, że wykorzystano wszystkie licencje. Czy dezaktywacja nie zwalnia miejsca?
Dzień dobry, zależy od typu planu. W większości planów samo wyłączenie użytkownika zwalnia miejsce dopiero na początku kolejnego cyklu rozliczeniowego.
To trochę mylące, bo w panelu jest napisane tylko „użytkownik nieaktywny”. Potrzebuję dodać nową osobę jeszcze dziś.

Rozumiem. Sprawdziłem konto i u Państwa licencje są rozliczane według maksymalnej liczby aktywnych stanowisk w cyklu, więc miejsce technicznie może być zajęte do końca miesiąca. Mogę jednak tymczasowo dodać jednego użytkownika testowego na 7 dni bez naliczania dodatkowej opłaty i równolegle zasugerować zmianę na model z elastycznymi miejscami. Czy mam to uruchomić?

subject: La integración con Slack manda demasiadas notificaciones y no sé cómo educar al equipo
description: Activamos Slack para avisos de tickets nuevos, pero ahora el canal está lleno de mensajes y mi equipo ya los ignora. Necesito entender mejor qué tipos de eventos conviene enviar y cómo configurar algo más útil. Busco orientación, no solo una respuesta técnica.

comments.message: Ahora mismo se publica cada comentario, cada cambio de estado y cada asignación. Queríamos visibilidad, pero se volvió ruido. ¿Cuál sería una configuración recomendable?

La mejor práctica suele ser empezar con pocos eventos de alto valor. Por ejemplo: tickets nuevos de prioridad alta, menciones directas y tickets sin respuesta después de cierto tiempo. Evitaría publicar cada comentario interno o cada cambio menor de estado, porque eso satura al canal.
Tiene sentido. ¿Y cómo se lo explico al equipo para que no piensen que estamos quitando información importante?

Puedes presentarlo como una mejora de señal vs. ruido. Una forma sencilla es crear dos canales o dos flujos: uno operativo con alertas críticas y otro opcional para actividad completa. Así educas al equipo a usar Slack para acción inmediata y a revisar la plataforma para el historial detallado. Si quieres, te comparto una configuración base por tipos de evento para soporte y ventas.

subject: Deleted contacts still appear in search results
description: I deleted about 300 old contacts from our workspace yesterday, and they no longer show up in the main Contacts list. However, when my team searches for some of those names, they still appear in global search and can even be opened from the result. We need to know whether the records are actually deleted or if search is indexing stale data.

comments.message: I deleted about 300 old contacts from our workspace yesterday, and they no longer show up in the main Contacts list. However, when my team searches for some of those names, they still appear in global search and can even be opened from the result. We need to know whether the records are actually deleted or if search is indexing stale data.

Thanks for reporting this. It sounds like the contact records may have been soft-deleted in the database but are still present in the search index cache. Can you confirm whether these contacts were deleted individually or through a bulk import rollback, and share 2-3 example contact IDs?

They were deleted via bulk action from a filtered list, not an import rollback. Example IDs are C-10482, C-10497, and C-10511. When I open one from search, the profile loads with a banner saying it was removed.

I checked the examples and the source records are marked deleted correctly, so your data is not active in the primary table. The issue is that the search index has not fully reprocessed the deletions. I’ve triggered a reindex for your workspace and this should clear the stale search results within the next hour. If any of those IDs still appear after that, reply here and I’ll attach the case to our indexing bug report.

subject: Workflow branch removed and now saved report errors out
description: I deleted an old branch from a lead routing workflow because we no longer use one of the territories, and now a saved report that tracks leads routed by automation is throwing an error instead of loading. The report was built on workflow action outcomes, so I'm worried the deleted branch broke the underlying reporting dataset. Please help me understand whether the report can be repaired and whether this needs to be escalated.

comments.message: Our 'Leads Routed by Automation' report stopped loading right after we removed a branch from the routing workflow. The error says one or more referenced values are unavailable.

That report is likely referencing a workflow outcome value tied to the deleted branch. When those branch labels are removed, saved report groupings can lose their source values. This is a known limitation with reports built directly on workflow outcome dimensions.
Can you restore the deleted value or map it to something else so the historical data still renders?

Support can't restore deleted branch metadata once the workflow is republished. I've escalated this to our reporting engineering team to see whether the historical values can still be queried in the backend. For now, you may need to duplicate the report and regroup by the stable lead owner or territory fields instead of the workflow outcome label.

subject: Quiero aprender a probar una integración antes de activarla para todos
description: Estamos evaluando la integración con Microsoft Teams, pero no quiero habilitarla a toda la empresa sin entender bien qué eventos se publican y cómo hacer pruebas seguras. ¿Tienen una forma recomendada de pilotear esto? Necesito orientación paso a paso.

comments.message: Siempre terminamos activando cosas demasiado rápido y luego toca apagarlas. Esta vez quiero hacerlo bien con un grupo pequeño.

Muy buena idea. La práctica recomendada es hacer un piloto controlado. Empieza creando un canal de Teams solo para pruebas, conecta la integración con una cuenta administradora, y limita los eventos a uno o dos casos, por ejemplo tickets nuevos y escalaciones.
¿Y cómo validamos si el piloto fue suficiente?

Define criterios simples antes de empezar: si las notificaciones llegan a tiempo, si contienen el contexto necesario y si el volumen es manejable. Durante una semana, recoge feedback del grupo piloto y ajusta filtros o plantillas. Cuando ya tengas una configuración estable, amplíala por departamentos. Si quieres, también puedes documentar internamente qué eventos van a Teams y cuáles se revisan solo en la plataforma para evitar expectativas confusas.

subject: Duplicados al importar oportunidades desde Pipedrive
description: Conecté Pipedrive para traer oportunidades a su sistema, pero cada vez que corre la importación automática se duplican varios registros. No pasa con todos, solo con algunos que fueron actualizados recientemente. Ya desactivé y activé la integración una vez y el comportamiento sigue igual.

comments.message: Si sirve de algo, los duplicados comparten el mismo ID de Pipedrive en el campo externo, pero aparecen como dos oportunidades distintas en su plataforma.

Gracias, ese detalle ayuda bastante. Si dos registros comparten el mismo ID externo, no deberían duplicarse; normalmente eso ocurre cuando la regla de deduplicación está usando el correo o el nombre en vez del ID del conector. ¿Puede revisar en Configuración > Importaciones si la fuente de Pipedrive está configurada para "fusionar por ID externo"?
No, estaba en "fusionar por nombre de oportunidad". Lo cambié a ID externo y corrí una importación manual, pero los duplicados viejos siguen ahí.

Perfecto, entonces ya corregimos la causa de los nuevos duplicados. Los registros ya duplicados no se consolidan automáticamente. Puede usar la herramienta de fusión masiva filtrando por origen Pipedrive, o si prefiere, le abro una tarea interna para ejecutar una deduplicación asistida. Mientras tanto, las próximas sincronizaciones deberían respetar el ID externo y no crear más copias.

subject: Archived users still counted as record owners in dashboard
description: We archived a group of former reps last quarter and reassigned their accounts. In the account records, the new owners look correct, but the sales dashboard still attributes 87 open deals to the archived users. I've checked a few records manually and can't find any live data pointing to them. Is there a reporting bug or hidden ownership data somewhere?

comments.message: We archived a group of former reps last quarter and reassigned their accounts. In the account records, the new owners look correct, but the sales dashboard still attributes 87 open deals to the archived users.
That usually happens when browser sessions cache old dashboard data. Please have your team refresh the dashboard and clear local storage.
This is happening for multiple users and in scheduled emailed dashboards too, so it seems server-side.

Thanks, that helps narrow it down. I checked one archived user and found their user ID still present in the dashboard fact table even though ownership on the deal records was reassigned. I've escalated this as a likely backfill bug in the ownership sync. I don't have a workaround besides building a custom report off current record owners for now.

subject: Need emergency lockout for compromised admin account
description: One of our user admins reported suspicious login alerts, and we believe their account may be compromised. I’ve already reset the password in our IdP, but I need to make sure any active sessions in your platform are invalidated and that no additional users were created or modified from that account. This is urgent and should be treated as a security escalation.

comments.message: We think one of our admin accounts was compromised. I need the account locked down immediately and I need to know whether that admin changed any user permissions today.

Thank you for reporting this. Since this involves a potentially compromised admin with user management privileges, I’m treating it as an urgent escalation. From your side, please disable the account in your identity provider and remove any admin group assignments if you haven’t already. I’ve escalated the ticket to our security operations team to review active sessions and audit logs for user creation, permission changes, and deactivations tied to that admin.
We disabled the account in Google Workspace a few minutes ago. Is there anything else we should do right now to protect the rest of the admins?

Yes—please have a current super admin review recent user administration changes, rotate credentials for any shared admin integrations if applicable, and enforce re-authentication for other privileged users in your IdP. Our security team will determine whether there are still valid platform sessions to revoke and will review the audit trail for actions taken from the compromised account. I’ve marked the escalation as high priority due to the admin scope involved.

subject: SSO users are stuck in a login loop after session timeout
description: Our SAML users can sign in initially, but if they leave the app idle and come back later, they get bounced between your login page and our identity provider until the browser eventually errors out. Can you investigate whether the session refresh is broken, and explain what the expected SSO timeout flow should be for SP-initiated logins?

comments.message: This started after we changed the session timeout from 12 hours to 2 hours last week. It seems worse in Chrome, but we've seen it in Edge too.
I wasn't able to reproduce it on my side, and login loops are usually caused by IdP configuration. You may want to ask your IT team to check the SAML certificate and relay state settings.
Our IT team already checked that. Initial login works every time; only the re-login after timeout loops. Can you at least tell us what sequence should happen when the SaaS session expires?

When the app session expires, users should be redirected to the identity provider and then back into the product with a fresh session if their IdP session is still valid. If the IdP session is not valid, they should log in there first. I don't have evidence of a platform bug right now, so I can't escalate this yet.

subject: Escalation queue report and workflow SLA timer don't match
description: We have an automation that moves high-priority incidents into the Escalation queue and starts a separate SLA timer, but the report for escalation response time is showing negative or zero-minute first responses on some tickets. I need help reconciling the workflow timing with the reporting logic because leadership thinks our SLA numbers are broken. If support can't verify this, please escalate it.

comments.message: For some incident tickets, the escalation response report shows first response as 0 minutes or even negative values after the workflow re-queues them. These are tickets auto-escalated after hours, and the SLA timer should start when the workflow moves them.

I checked a couple of examples and the report is calculating from the original ticket creation timestamp, while your workflow starts the escalation SLA from the moment the queue changes to Escalation. If the report uses one timestamp and the automation uses another, the numbers won't line up.
That explains the mismatch, but can the report be changed to use the escalation timestamp instead of created time?

Possibly, if you have the queue-entry timestamp available as a reportable field. I don't see it exposed in the standard builder on your workspace, so I've escalated this to our advanced reporting team and included the sample ticket IDs and SLA policy details. In the meantime, I'd avoid sharing that report as an SLA source of truth because it is measuring a different event than your workflow timer.

subject: Zmiana adresu e-mail zablokowała logowanie
description: Po zmianie firmowego adresu e-mail w naszym koncie nie mogę się teraz zalogować do panelu. Stary adres już nie działa, a po wpisaniu nowego dostaję komunikat, że użytkownik nie istnieje. Muszę odzyskać dostęp jak najszybciej, bo nie mogę wejść do ustawień zespołu.

comments.message: Wczoraj administrator zmienił mi adres z anna.kowalska@oldfirma.pl na anna.kowalska@nowafirma.pl. Od tego momentu nie działa ani logowanie hasłem, ani link resetujący.

Dziękuję za zgłoszenie. Sprawdziłem konto i widzę, że zmiana adresu została zapisana, ale zaproszenie do nowego loginu nie zostało potwierdzone. Wysłałem ponownie wiadomość aktywacyjną na anna.kowalska@nowafirma.pl. Proszę kliknąć link, a następnie ustawić nowe hasło.
Link zadziałał i mogłam ustawić hasło, ale nadal dostaję błąd przy logowaniu do aplikacji webowej.
Widzę jeszcze stary wpis SSO powiązany z poprzednim adresem. Usunąłem go i odświeżyłem profil logowania. Proszę spróbować ponownie w trybie incognito — dostęp powinien już działać poprawnie.

subject: Export PDF tronqué sur le rapport de performance des agents
description: Quand j'exporte le rapport de performance des agents en PDF pour notre comité hebdomadaire, la deuxième page coupe les noms des agents et une partie des graphiques disparaît. Sur l'écran tout est correct. J'ai testé Chrome et Edge, même résultat.

comments.message: Bonjour, l'export PDF de notre rapport de performance des agents est tronqué à partir de la deuxième page. Les noms sont coupés et certains graphiques manquent.

Bonjour, merci pour le détail. Ce type de problème arrive souvent quand le rapport est en mode paysage avec trop de widgets larges. Pouvez-vous me dire si vous utilisez un zoom navigateur différent de 100 % et combien de widgets sont présents sur la page ?
Le zoom est à 100 %, format paysage, et il y a 7 widgets dont un grand histogramme en haut.

Merci. J'ai reproduit un comportement similaire sur les exports PDF avec plus de 6 widgets en paysage. La solution de contournement est de réduire la largeur de l'histogramme ou de déplacer un widget vers une deuxième page avant export. Ce n'est pas idéal, mais cela évite la troncature. Je signale aussi le bug à l'équipe reporting, sans délai garanti pour un correctif.

subject: Quero renomear um valor de picklist sem quebrar relatórios históricos
description: Temos um campo de segmento com um valor antigo escrito 'Mid Market' e queremos padronizar para 'Mid-Market'. Meu receio é alterar isso e perder consistência nos relatórios antigos ou nos registros já preenchidos. Como fazer essa mudança do jeito certo?

comments.message: Temos um campo de segmento com um valor antigo escrito 'Mid Market' e queremos padronizar para 'Mid-Market'. Meu receio é alterar isso e perder consistência nos relatórios antigos ou nos registros já preenchidos. Como fazer essa mudança do jeito certo?
Você pode editar a opção do campo diretamente e trocar o rótulo. Isso atualiza o valor em todos os registros sem mexer nos dados históricos.
Mas nossos relatórios usam esse campo ao longo do tempo. Se mudar o rótulo, o histórico vai aparecer com o nome novo também? Precisamos manter rastreabilidade.

Boa observação. Em muitos casos, o rótulo novo passa a aparecer inclusive em relatórios históricos, porque o sistema guarda a opção como uma mesma chave de valor. Se vocês precisam distinguir o antes e depois, a alternativa mais segura é criar uma nova opção 'Mid-Market', atualizar os registros em massa e documentar a data da mudança; porém isso pode fragmentar tendências históricas. Como o comportamento exato varia por módulo de relatório, eu sugiro testar em sandbox ou relatório clonando antes de aplicar em produção.

subject: Contractor accounts cannot accept invite after session timeout change
description: I changed our session timeout and invite policy this morning, and now external contractor accounts cannot finish the invite flow. They set a password, then get sent to a screen saying their invitation is invalid or expired. Internal employees are mostly fine. I need to know whether this is an access issue or a bad configuration on our side.

comments.message: After I changed security settings today, contractors invited with external domains cannot complete account setup. They click the invite, choose a password, and then the page says the invitation is invalid or expired.

Thanks. Did you also change domain restrictions or the external collaborator policy under Workspace Access? If external domains are no longer allowed, invited users can appear to complete the password step but fail during final account creation. Please check whether 'Allow guest users from unverified domains' was disabled, and also whether the invite TTL was shortened below your normal onboarding window.
Yes, I disabled guest users from unverified domains because I thought it only affected file sharing. Our contractors use partner-mail.com. Invite expiration is still 7 days.

That would explain it. Re-enable guest users from unverified domains, or add partner-mail.com to the approved external domains list if you want to keep the restriction tighter. Existing invite links may still be rejected, so resend those invitations after updating the policy. Once you do that, contractors should be able to finish setup normally.

subject: Role changes not syncing after SCIM update
description: We turned on SCIM provisioning yesterday, and since then new hires are being created correctly, but role updates inside the app keep getting overwritten. I promoted one supervisor to user admin for the Sales org three times, and each sync changed him back to a normal member. I need to understand whether that role has to come from our identity provider now and how to update it without breaking the rest of our account changes.

comments.message: The affected user is leo.martin@everbranch.com. I can add him to the Sales org, but his User Admin role never sticks after SCIM runs. We also renamed two users this morning, so I’m trying to avoid more changes until I know the right source of truth.

Once SCIM is enabled, certain account attributes and role mappings may be controlled by your identity provider. If Leo’s app role is being overwritten after each sync, the User Admin assignment is likely coming from the SCIM mapping rules rather than the in-app setting.
That makes sense, but I don’t know which field our IT team needs to map. We need Leo to manage Sales users only, not become a full admin.

I can confirm this requires an IdP-side update to the SCIM role mapping for scoped administration, and I don’t want to guess at the exact attribute name because it varies by provider setup. I’m escalating this to our provisioning specialists with your notes about Leo, the Sales org scope, and the recent account renames so they can advise the correct mapping and any order-of-operations concerns.

subject: Genehmigungsworkflow startet nicht bei Schwellenwert über 5.000 EUR
description: Ich habe unseren Freigabeworkflow so konfiguriert, dass bei Bestellungen über 5.000 EUR automatisch eine zweite Genehmigung erforderlich ist. Bei Testanfragen mit 7.200 EUR wird aber nur die erste Freigabe ausgelöst. Ich brauche eine schnelle Einschätzung, ob wir die Bedingung falsch gesetzt haben oder ob das ein Fehler in der Workflow-Engine ist.

comments.message: Die Bedingung lautet: Wenn Feld "Betrag" größer als 5000, dann zusätzliche Genehmigungsstufe. Das Feld ist vom Typ Währung.
Danke. Bei Währungsfeldern wird der Wert intern häufig als Text gespeichert. Bitte ändern Sie das Feld auf Zahl, dann sollte die Bedingung korrekt greifen.
Das können wir produktiv nicht einfach ändern, und laut Ihrer Hilfe sind Währungsfelder in Bedingungen unterstützt. Können Sie das bitte verifizieren?

Sie haben recht, Währungsfelder sollten unterstützt sein. Ich möchte hier nichts riskieren und habe den Fall an unsere Workflow-Spezialisten eskaliert. Bitte teilen Sie mir noch mit, ob Ihr Betrag mit Komma oder Punkt eingegeben wird, da die Engine in einigen Regionen unterschiedliche Formate verarbeitet. Sobald ich eine technische Rückmeldung habe, melde ich mich.

subject: Bulk role change triggered the wrong approval path
description: I did a bulk update to move several users from Contractor to Employee, and now expense requests from some of them are still following the contractor approval workflow while others use the employee workflow. We also renamed a permission role during the same cleanup, so I'm unsure whether the automation is reading the user type, the access role, or stale data.

comments.message: For context, our workflow should route Contractors to procurement review and Employees to manager approval. After the bulk change, six users switched correctly, but four still hit procurement even though their profiles say Employee and they have employee access.

This can happen when the workflow condition is tied to a different user attribute than the one updated in bulk. In some environments, 'employment type' and 'security role' are separate fields. If you renamed the permission role but only changed employment type for some users, the automation may still be evaluating the old contractor-related field. Can you check one affected user's audit history to see which attributes changed, and confirm what exact field the workflow condition references?

I checked. The affected users had Employment Type updated, but the workflow condition is actually based on a custom profile field called Worker Category. That field stayed as Contractor for those four users.

There we go. Update Worker Category for the affected users or change the workflow to evaluate Employment Type if that's now your source of truth. Also review whether the renamed permission role is used anywhere else, but for this routing issue the mismatch between user administration fields and workflow configuration is the root cause.

subject: Admin API says role update succeeded, but user still can't export data
description: I changed one of our analysts from Viewer to Data Manager using the API, and the response came back 200. However, when she logs in she still cannot run exports or edit data sources, and the UI still seems to show her old role in some places. We are also seeing stale department values in the user directory export. I'm not sure whether the update didn't stick, or whether there is some second permission layer I'm missing.

comments.message: I used PUT /users/8871/role with role=data_manager. The response body reflected the new role. Then I pulled /users/8871 and saw the new role there too, but in the app she can't export datasets.

It sounds like the role update worked at the user record level. In our platform, export and source-editing permissions can also be restricted by workspace-level permission sets and team scope. Please have a workspace admin check whether the user is assigned a limited permission set or belongs to a team with restricted dataset access. The stale department values in exports may be unrelated cache lag from the directory index.

We checked, and she still had the old permission set attached from when she was a Viewer. After removing that, exports worked. But the directory export still shows her old department from before our sync yesterday.

Great, that confirms the issue was a combination of role and permission set assignment. For the department field, the directory export is generated from the indexed user profile store and can lag behind API writes for several hours after a sync. If it remains stale beyond 12 hours, re-run the sync for that user and we'll investigate the indexer.

subject: Former employee still has access and our termination workflow didn't run
description: We offboarded an employee yesterday, but she was still able to log in this morning through Google SSO. Also, the offboarding workflow that should revoke assigned approvals and transfer ownership never ran for her open requests. This is a security issue, so if you can't fix it directly please escalate it to the right team right away.

comments.message: We offboarded an employee yesterday, but she was still able to log in this morning through Google SSO. Also, the offboarding workflow that should revoke assigned approvals and transfer ownership never ran for her open requests. This is a security issue, so if you can't fix it directly please escalate it to the right team right away.

Understood — I’m treating this as urgent. I checked the audit trail and the user was deactivated in our app, but your Google IdP continued asserting membership in the 'Support-App-Users' group until 08:43 UTC, which allowed a fresh session. I’ve revoked all active sessions for that user on our side. Your offboarding workflow is triggered only when the user status changes from Active to Suspended inside the app, and because the user was provisioned through SCIM the state moved directly to Deactivated, so that automation never fired. I’ve escalated this to our identity engineering team to review the SSO/SCIM behavior and to our workflow team for a safer offboarding trigger recommendation.
Thanks. Is there anything we should change immediately so this doesn't happen again for the next termination?

Yes. Until the specialists finish their review, please remove users from the Google access group before deprovisioning them, and update your offboarding automation to trigger on both Suspended and Deactivated statuses if your plan supports multi-condition triggers. I’ve attached those recommendations to the escalation and marked it as a security-impacting case.

subject: User can log into main app but gets access denied in one workspace
description: We have a multi-workspace setup. One employee can sign into the main application successfully, but when she switches to our Finance workspace she gets an access denied page. I’m an org admin and I can see her as a member in the company account, just not sure whether she’s missing a workspace assignment or if this is something else.

comments.message: Her name is Sofia Romero. She can access the Sales workspace fine, so login itself is working. The Finance workspace says she is not authorized. I need her in before month-end close starts.

If she can access one workspace, then her account is working and this is likely a User Administration issue specific to Finance. Please open the Finance workspace member list and confirm Sofia is assigned there with the correct workspace role; org membership alone does not grant workspace access.
She wasn’t assigned directly, but she is in a group that should sync into Finance automatically. Other people in that group got access.

That suggests the group sync may not have applied to her record. As a workaround, add Sofia directly to the Finance workspace now so she can log in immediately. Then re-run the group sync or remove and re-add her to the source group to repair the mapping. If the sync still skips only her account, send the user ID and group name and we’ll inspect the provisioning logs.

subject: Não consigo ativar SSO para um grupo específico de usuários
description: Estamos configurando SSO via SAML e quase todos conseguem entrar, mas um grupo do domínio subsidiaria.empresa.com fica preso em loop na tela de login. Revisei o provedor de identidade e aparentemente os atributos estão corretos. Preciso validar se falta alguma configuração no produto.

comments.message: No nosso lado, o NameID vem com o e-mail completo e o domínio alternativo está verificado na conta.

Olá! Obrigado pelos detalhes. Quando há loop apenas para um subconjunto, normalmente o problema está no mapeamento do domínio para a política de autenticação. Na sua conta, o domínio secundário está somente verificado ou também marcado como permitido para provisionamento e login SSO?
Acho que estava só verificado. Onde eu habilito isso?

Vá em Segurança > SSO > Domínios da organização. Selecione subsidiaria.empresa.com e habilite as opções de login via SSO e provisionamento just-in-time, se desejado. Depois salve e peça para um usuário testar em janela anônima. Se ainda houver loop, precisaremos revisar a resposta SAML completa, então eu escalaria para nosso time de autenticação.

subject: OAuth token refresh works once, then subsequent API calls fail
description: Our public app can complete the OAuth flow and the first batch of API requests succeeds. After the access token expires, we refresh it successfully according to the token endpoint response, but later API calls start failing with invalid_token. I need troubleshooting help to understand if we are storing the refresh response incorrectly.

comments.message: We call the refresh endpoint, get a new access token and what looks like a new refresh token, then continue using the old stored refresh token for the next cycle. Is that supported?

For our OAuth implementation, refresh token rotation is enabled by default. Each successful refresh invalidates the previous refresh token. You need to atomically store both the new access token and the new refresh token from the response; otherwise the next refresh attempt may succeed inconsistently during overlap and then fail with `invalid_token` later. Also make sure only one worker performs refresh for a given installation.
That makes sense. We had two workers racing and we were not persisting the replacement refresh token reliably. We'll fix the locking and token storage.
Perfect. After you deploy that change, re-authorize any installations already stuck with an invalidated refresh token, because those won't recover automatically.

subject: Repeated timeout during CRM sync import
description: Our initial CRM import has been timing out for three days. The import gets to around 78% and then fails with no actionable error besides "request expired." We have a launch date coming up, and I need this looked at by someone who can inspect backend job logs because the standard troubleshooting steps haven't changed anything.

comments.message: Can you escalate this to engineering? We reduced the batch size twice and removed custom fields, but the import still dies in the same place.
Timeouts usually mean the file is too large. I'd recommend splitting the CSV into smaller chunks and retrying from scratch.
We already did that, and the failure point is consistent. That's why I'm asking for escalation.
At this point there's nothing else support can do from here. If the smaller file also fails, please wait a day and try again in case the system is busy.

subject: Zmiana nazwy firmy po rebrandingu
description: Nasza firma zmieniła nazwę i chciałbym zaktualizować nazwę organizacji widoczną w aplikacji, na fakturach oraz w stopce e-maili systemowych. W ustawieniach udało mi się zmienić tylko nazwę zespołu, ale stare dane dalej pojawiają się w kilku miejscach.

comments.message: Dzień dobry, zmieniliśmy nazwę z DataPoint sp. z o.o. na Vistaline sp. z o.o. W panelu zmieniłem nazwę workspace, ale faktury nadal są wystawiane na starą nazwę i klienci widzą starą nazwę nadawcy w e-mailach.

Dziękuję za zgłoszenie. To są trzy osobne ustawienia: nazwa workspace, dane rozliczeniowe i nazwa nadawcy wiadomości. Nazwę workspace może Pan zmienić samodzielnie, ale dane na fakturach wymagają aktualizacji w sekcji Rozliczenia, a nazwa nadawcy jest w Ustawienia > E-mail. Jeśli chce Pan, mogę opisać kroki dokładniej.
Poproszę o dokładne kroki, bo w sekcji Rozliczenia nie widzę pola do edycji nazwy prawnej firmy.

Jasne. Proszę przejść do Rozliczenia > Dane firmy > Edytuj profil rozliczeniowy. Jeśli pole nazwy prawnej jest zablokowane, oznacza to, że konto ma ręcznie ustawiony profil przez nasz zespół finansowy. W takim przypadku już przekazałem prośbę o zmianę na Vistaline sp. z o.o. Zmianę nazwy nadawcy może Pan zrobić sam od razu, a aktualizacja danych fakturowych powinna być widoczna na kolejnej fakturze.

subject: Duplicate charge after importing archived contacts
description: I was charged for 3,200 active contacts on this month's invoice, but about 900 of those were old records we imported only to keep historical notes. I archived them the same day, and they are not usable in campaigns. I need to understand why they counted toward billing and what I should do differently when importing cleanup data so this doesn't happen again.

comments.message: We imported a CSV of legacy contacts on May 2, archived the duplicates within an hour, and our invoice on May 3 still jumped to the 3k-5k tier. Can you explain what counted and whether that charge can be reversed?

I checked your account and the invoice tier is based on the highest active contact count recorded during the billing period. Because the imported records were active at the time of import, they pushed the account into the higher tier before being archived. I can submit a one-time billing review for the prorated difference, although I can't guarantee approval.
Okay, but we need to merge old notes into existing contacts next time without briefly inflating our count. Is there a safer process?

Yes. For historical data, I recommend importing into a test workspace first, deduplicating by email or external ID, then using the update-existing-records option instead of create-new. If you need to preserve old records only for reference, you can also import them directly with an archived status through our bulk importer API so they never become billable active contacts. I've included our deduplication and archived-import guide in the follow-up email, and I've opened billing review case BR-18422 for the charge adjustment.

subject: Transfert de propriété du compte et filtres de rapport incohérents
description: Je suis directrice financière et j’ai besoin de reprendre la gestion du compte parce que notre ancien responsable IT est parti. Je dois aussi préparer un rapport par département, mais les totaux changent selon que je filtre par équipe ou par tag, alors que cela devrait correspondre. Je voudrais à la fois faire modifier le propriétaire du compte et comprendre quelle vue de reporting est fiable.

comments.message: Bonjour, nous devons changer le propriétaire du compte immédiatement car l’ancien contact n’est plus chez nous. En plus, dans les rapports, j’obtiens 184 tickets avec le filtre équipe Support, mais 163 seulement avec les tags de cette même équipe. Le comité de direction me demande une explication aujourd’hui.

Bonjour, pour changer le propriétaire du compte, il suffit de supprimer l’ancien utilisateur puis de créer le nouveau. Pour la différence dans les rapports, les tags et les équipes sont censés donner le même total si la configuration est correcte.
Je ne peux pas supprimer l’ancien propriétaire car je n’ai pas ses droits, c’est justement le problème. Et si les rapports sont censés être identiques, cela ne m’aide pas à savoir lequel utiliser.

Je comprends. Je vous conseille d’attendre que l’ancien propriétaire revienne ou retrouve son accès afin qu’il fasse le changement lui-même. Pour les rapports, utilisez plutôt le chiffre le plus bas par prudence.

subject: SSO login loops back to sign-in page
description: Several users are unable to log in through SSO this morning. They authenticate with Okta, get redirected back to your app, and then land on the sign-in page again instead of entering the workspace. Password login works for local admins, but most of our staff use SSO only. We haven't changed our Okta setup recently. I tested from two networks and in a private browser session with the same result.

comments.message: Our SSO users are stuck in a login loop after authenticating in Okta. What should we look at first?
Please verify whether third-party cookies are blocked in the browser, since that can interrupt the session handoff. Also confirm if this affects all SSO users or only one group.
It affects all SSO users we tested, across Chrome and Edge. Third-party cookies are allowed in at least one of those test browsers.

Given that scope, this may be related to a session cookie issue on our side rather than your IdP config. I'm seeing a few recent reports of SAML logins returning to the sign-in page when the account has the new domain-based routing enabled. I can't safely change that from support, so I'm escalating this to our authentication team now. As a temporary measure, local admins can still access the workspace and create password-reset invites for critical users if needed.

subject: Board columns disappeared for one team but not another
description: Our support board looks normal for the customer success team, but the implementation team says half the columns disappeared this afternoon. I’m trying to figure out whether this is a permissions issue, a saved view problem, or someone changing the board configuration. I’d appreciate help troubleshooting it and also a quick explanation of how global board settings differ from personal views, because that part is still confusing to me.

comments.message: Our support board looks normal for the customer success team, but the implementation team says half the columns disappeared this afternoon. I’m trying to figure out whether this is a permissions issue, a saved view problem, or someone changing the board configuration. I’d appreciate help troubleshooting it and also a quick explanation of how global board settings differ from personal views, because that part is still confusing to me.

If some users see columns and others don’t, it’s often a view-level issue rather than the underlying board being changed. Personal views affect only the individual user’s filters, visible columns, sort, and grouping. Global board settings change the actual shared board structure for everyone with access. Ask one affected user to reset to the default view, then compare whether the missing columns return. Also check whether their role has permission to see fields tied to those columns, since restricted fields can appear as missing columns in some layouts.
Resetting to default brought two columns back, but not all of them. The remaining missing columns are based on custom fields that implementation can’t currently edit.

That points to permissions. Even if a team can use the board, field-level visibility can hide columns built from custom fields they don’t have access to. Have an admin review the implementation team role under Permissions > Fields and grant at least view access to those custom fields. After that, the users should refresh the board. So in short: personal views changed some of what they saw, but the rest is field visibility, not a broken board configuration.

subject: Droits administrateur retirés après réorganisation et rapport planifié non envoyé
description: Je gérais auparavant l’espace de notre filiale France, mais après une réorganisation interne mes droits ont été réduits sans demande de notre part. Depuis, je ne peux plus modifier les utilisateurs ni consulter la configuration des rapports programmés. En plus, notre rapport hebdomadaire n’a pas été envoyé aux managers lundi matin. Merci de m’aider à rétablir les bons droits et à vérifier l’échec d’envoi.

comments.message: Bonjour, je ne suis plus admin complet depuis ce week-end alors que je n’ai rien changé. Je ne peux plus gérer les utilisateurs, et le rapport hebdomadaire planifié n’est jamais parti lundi.

Bonjour, je vois qu’un rôle "gestionnaire" vous a été attribué lors d’une mise à jour des groupes. Je peux restaurer vos droits d’administratrice locale, mais pas de super-admin sans validation. Pour le rapport non envoyé, j’ai besoin de savoir si la planification existe encore dans l’onglet Rapports programmés.
Justement, je n’ai plus accès à cet onglet à cause de la baisse de droits. Nous avons besoin du même niveau qu’avant, au moins pour notre entité.

Compris. J’ai rétabli vos droits d’administratrice locale sur l’espace France, ce qui vous redonne l’accès aux utilisateurs et aux rapports programmés. En vérifiant la configuration, la planification existait toujours mais l’option "envoyer aux nouveaux managers ajoutés automatiquement" avait été désactivée, et la liste de destinataires était vide après la réorganisation. J’ai remis vos trois managers principaux et relancé l’envoi manuellement. Si vous avez besoin du rôle super-admin global, je dois faire valider la demande par le propriétaire du compte.

subject: I removed an admin and now no one can update settings
description: I deactivated a former manager yesterday, and I think he was the only full admin on the account. I'm still able to log in, but I can't access the settings pages I need to manage users. How do I promote myself, or how can we recover admin access without creating a new workspace?

comments.message: I deactivated a former manager yesterday, and I think he was the only full admin on the account. I'm still able to log in, but I can't access the settings pages I need to manage users. How do I promote myself, or how can we recover admin access without creating a new workspace?

If no active admin remains, standard members usually can't self-promote for security reasons. The fastest path is to submit an admin recovery request from the login page or have someone from your company's verified domain contact support with proof of authority. Once ownership is confirmed, we can restore admin access without affecting your workspace data. Do not create a new workspace, because that would split your history and subscriptions.
What kind of proof do you normally need? I need to tell our IT director what to send.

Typically we ask for a request from a company email on the verified domain, the workspace URL, the name of the organization, and sometimes supporting documentation such as an internal authorization or a billing record that ties your company to the account. For security, the recovery team reviews each case manually, so I can't grant access in this chat, but I've linked the recovery form below and flagged your case as urgent.

subject: No coincide la factura con el número de licencias y el panel va lentísimo
description: Nuestra factura de este mes muestra 48 licencias, pero solo tenemos 41 usuarios activos desde hace semanas. Además, el panel de proyectos tarda más de 30 segundos en cargar cada vista y a veces termina con un timeout. Ya desactivamos extensiones del navegador y sigue igual.

comments.message: Nuestra factura de este mes muestra 48 licencias, pero solo tenemos 41 usuarios activos desde hace semanas. Además, el panel de proyectos tarda más de 30 segundos en cargar cada vista y a veces termina con un timeout. Ya desactivamos extensiones del navegador y sigue igual.

Gracias. Sobre el rendimiento, probablemente sea por demasiados proyectos archivados; les sugiero borrar los que no usen. En cuanto a las licencias, la factura siempre refleja el máximo de usuarios del período, no necesariamente los activos al final del mes.

No podemos borrar proyectos por cumplimiento, y nunca nos explicaron que se cobrara por el máximo del período. Además el problema de lentitud empezó esta semana, no tiene sentido que sea por archivados antiguos.

Entiendo. En ese caso, lo mejor sería probar desde otra red para descartar latencia local. Si persiste, puedo pasarles un artículo sobre optimización del espacio de trabajo. Para la factura, si creen que el conteo es incorrecto, escriban a cobranzas@empresa.com.

subject: New teammates can't find the shared dashboard after I invited them
description: I invited three new sales managers to our workspace this morning, but two of them say they only see an empty home screen and not the pipeline dashboard we use every day. I’m the workspace admin and I expected the dashboard to appear automatically for everyone on the team. Can you help me figure out what I missed and what setting controls this?

comments.message: I invited three new sales managers to our workspace this morning, but two of them say they only see an empty home screen and not the pipeline dashboard we use every day. I’m the workspace admin and I expected the dashboard to appear automatically for everyone on the team. Can you help me figure out what I missed and what setting controls this?

Happy to help. The most common cause is that the dashboard was shared only with a specific team or role, rather than the whole workspace. Could you ask the affected users to confirm whether they are in the same team as the users who can see it? Also, from the dashboard, click Share and check whether it is shared with 'Everyone', a team, or only specific people.

I checked and the dashboard was shared only with our old 'Revenue Ops' team. The new managers were added under 'Sales East' and 'Sales West'. I changed the share setting to Everyone, but one user still doesn’t see it.

That narrows it down. If one user still can’t see it after the share change, have them refresh the browser and sign out/sign back in so their permissions reload. If it still doesn’t appear, go to Admin > People, open that user, and confirm they have a seat with dashboard access rather than a limited collaborator role. For future invites, dashboards do not appear automatically unless they’re shared broadly, so using 'Everyone' or a default team is the safest setup.

subject: Okta SCIM provisioning skips users in one group
description: Our Okta SCIM integration provisions most users correctly, but members of one specific group are being skipped and never created in your app. The integration is active and other groups work fine. I need help troubleshooting whether the group mapping, attribute payload, or SCIM endpoint response is causing the issue.

comments.message: The skipped group is our contractors group. They have a different employeeType value than full-time staff, but they should still be provisioned.

Thanks — that difference is important. If only one group is affected, the first thing to check is whether your Okta group assignment rule is sending a profile attribute our SCIM endpoint rejects, such as an unsupported employeeType enum or a missing required manager field. Can you open one skipped user in the Okta provisioning logs and see whether the POST or PATCH call shows a 400 response body?
Yes, one example says 400 with 'employeeType must be one of Employee, Admin, or External'. Our contractors are being sent as 'Contractor'.

That explains the behavior. Our SCIM integration is rejecting those users because 'Contractor' is not an accepted employeeType value on your current schema. The fastest fix is to map that Okta value to 'External' in the app profile mapping, then retry provisioning for the failed users. If you actually need a separate Contractor value, that would require a schema change request with our integrations team.

subject: SFTP import job failing on files with accented characters
description: Our nightly SFTP import integration has started failing whenever the uploaded filename contains accented characters like é or ñ. Files with plain ASCII names import normally. We need help troubleshooting whether this is a filename encoding issue or a parser bug on your side.

comments.message: The job log just says 'file not found after listing,' even though I can see the file on the server. The server uses UTF-8 and nothing changed on our side except a new naming convention from another team.

Thanks, that error is useful. It suggests our importer can list the directory but fails when it tries to open the file path, which often happens when the SFTP server returns UTF-8 filenames but the client session negotiates a different encoding. As a quick test, can you rename one failing file to ASCII only and rerun the import?
Yes, I renamed 'revisión_año.csv' to 'revision_ano.csv' and it imported immediately.

That confirms it's tied to filename encoding rather than the file contents. I don't have a same-day fix, but I've documented this as an integration bug and escalated it to engineering with your example filename. For now, the only reliable workaround is to keep uploaded filenames ASCII-only before the nightly pickup runs.

subject: Gerente removido ainda aparece como dono nos relatórios agendados
description: Removi o acesso de um gerente que saiu da empresa, mas os relatórios agendados continuam sendo enviados com o nome dele como proprietário. Eu sou superadmin e já transferi a equipe dele para outra gestora. No módulo de usuários ele está como desativado, porém em Relatórios > Agendamentos ainda consta como dono de 5 envios. Tenho receio de isso quebrar os agendamentos no fim do mês.

comments.message: Tentei editar um dos agendamentos e só consegui mudar os destinatários, não o proprietário. A usuária nova precisa assumir tudo.
Olá! Quando um usuário é desativado, os agendamentos mantêm o proprietário original até que a propriedade seja reatribuída por um administrador da conta usando a tela de relatórios avançados.
Não achei nenhuma opção de reatribuição nessa tela. Só vejo pausar, editar frequência e destinatários.

Você tem razão — para agendamentos antigos essa opção não aparece na interface atual. Vou abrir uma escalada para nossa equipe técnica reatribuir esses 5 agendamentos para a nova gestora no backend. Enquanto isso, eles não devem falhar automaticamente só por o antigo dono estar desativado.

subject: Marketo campaign membership not matching attribution report
description: I'm seeing a mismatch between Marketo and your attribution reporting. Campaign membership counts synced over, but the attributed pipeline report only includes some of those members, especially for campaigns that were updated in bulk last Friday. We rely on this report for marketing ROI, so I need help understanding whether the integration dropped updates or whether the attribution report has some hidden logic.

comments.message: Our Marketo campaign 'Q3 Webinar Series' has 1,842 members in Marketo, but the attribution report in your platform only shows 1,219 associated responses. Bulk status updates were pushed last Friday. Manual sync didn't change anything.

Attribution reports often apply additional model rules, so membership and attribution totals won't necessarily match one-to-one. That said, bulk updates should still be reflected if the integration processed the activity changes. Can you confirm whether the missing members share a specific status like Registered or Attended?
Most of the missing records are people whose status changed from Invited to Attended in the bulk update. The integration log says the sync succeeded with no errors.

Got it. That pattern suggests the Marketo integration may have brought in the campaign membership record but not reprocessed the status-change event that your attribution report depends on. I can see why the metric looks wrong, but I can't validate the event pipeline from the support side. I'm escalating this to the integrations team with the campaign ID and to reporting specialists so they can confirm whether attribution is reading stale status data.

subject: Changed team names and permission groups no longer apply correctly
description: I renamed several teams during an internal reorg and updated the associated permission groups, but users are still inheriting access from the old team names. Some dashboards are missing while others are visible when they shouldn’t be. I’m not sure if the rename process broke the mapping or if permissions just haven’t refreshed yet.

comments.message: For example, “Customer Success West” became “CS North America,” but members still show the old group badge in some places. We also have two new hires who got added after the rename and they can’t see the shared dashboard at all.

Team renames can take a little time to propagate through cached permission views. Please ask users to sign out and sign back in, and make sure the shared dashboard is assigned to the renamed group rather than the previous one.
We already checked that. Existing members have mixed access, and the two new hires are definitely in the renamed group but still blocked.

Understood. I inspected your org settings and the rename created a new display label, but the underlying permission group ID for the dashboard share is still attached to the retired team object. I’ve updated the mapping so new hires should inherit access correctly now. Existing users may need a permissions refresh job, which I’ve queued, but it can take up to an hour to fully normalize.

subject: La integración con Microsoft Teams marca conectada pero no entrega notificaciones
description: Conectamos Microsoft Teams para recibir avisos de incidencias críticas en un canal, pero aunque la integración aparece como activa no se publica nada. Hicimos una prueba creando una incidencia crítica y no llegó ningún mensaje. Esto es urgente porque el equipo de soporte depende de esas alertas.

comments.message: En el registro de eventos solo veo "webhook delivered" de su lado, por eso no entiendo dónde se está perdiendo.
Si el webhook figura como entregado, entonces nuestra plataforma ya hizo su parte y el problema debe estar en Teams. Seguramente Microsoft está filtrando el mensaje.
¿Hay alguna forma de validar la URL del webhook o el formato del payload? Necesito algo más concreto para revisar.
No tenemos más visibilidad sobre eso, pero puede intentar crear otro canal y pegar un nuevo webhook por si acaso.

subject: La limpieza automática borró valores válidos de una columna
description: Activamos una regla de limpieza para normalizar la columna "Teléfono" y desde entonces varios registros quedaron vacíos aunque antes tenían número. Sospecho que la regla eliminó formatos internacionales con prefijo +34. Necesito recuperar esos valores y entender cómo evitar que vuelva a pasar en la próxima ejecución nocturna.

comments.message: Activamos una regla de limpieza para normalizar la columna "Teléfono" y desde entonces varios registros quedaron vacíos aunque antes tenían número. Sospecho que la regla eliminó formatos internacionales con prefijo +34. Necesito recuperar esos valores y entender cómo evitar que vuelva a pasar en la próxima ejecución nocturna.

Eso puede ocurrir si la regla está configurada para aceptar solo 9 dígitos nacionales y descartar caracteres no numéricos sin conservar el prefijo de país. Para recuperar datos, abra el historial de transformaciones del dataset y verifique la ejecución de anoche; si la regla admite rollback, podrá restaurar la versión previa de la columna. Después edite la validación para permitir formato E.164 o al menos conservar códigos de país como 34.
Veo el historial, pero solo me deja comparar versiones, no restaurarlas. Además la próxima ejecución corre en 3 horas.

Entonces haga dos cosas: desactive temporalmente la automatización nocturna desde el programador del flujo y exporte una muestra afectada para confirmar el patrón. Si su entorno no tiene rollback habilitado, la recuperación probablemente requiera restaurar desde un snapshot interno. He abierto una escalación para que revisen si existe una copia previa de la columna antes de la limpieza. También le sugiero duplicar la regla y probarla sobre un subconjunto antes de volver a activarla.

subject: Need help explaining audit logs to our compliance lead
description: Our compliance lead asked me to give her access to audit logs, but I’m not fully sure what those logs include in your system. I don’t want to promise more than the feature actually provides. Can you explain in straightforward language what audit logs are useful for and any limitations I should mention?

comments.message: She specifically wants to know whether audit logs show who changed permissions and when.

Audit logs are typically used to track important account activity such as login events, admin changes, permission updates, and configuration changes, depending on your plan. So yes, permission changes are often included with a timestamp and the user who made the change. The main limitation to mention is that audit depth varies by subscription tier and event type, so not every field-level action may be captured. It’s best to verify exactly which event categories your plan includes before presenting it as a complete compliance record.
Where do I check that in the app?
You should find it under settings somewhere in the admin area.

subject: Invited users see “no account found” after accepting invite
description: I invited six contractors to our workspace this morning. They received the email and set passwords, but when they try to sign in, they get “no account found for this organization.” I removed and re-sent one invitation and now that user gets the same error. These contractors start onboarding in an hour.

comments.message: All six are using the exact email addresses I invited. They can complete the password setup page, but the login screen says the account doesn’t exist.

This can happen if the invitations are pending in a different region shard than your workspace. I can confirm three of the contractor accounts are in a provisioning state that should normally finish within 15 minutes.
It has already been over an hour for the first batch. Can you force the sync?

I don’t have the ability to re-run provisioning jobs from support, so I’ve escalated the ticket to our platform operations team with the affected email addresses and invite timestamps. In the meantime, please avoid deleting and recreating the users again, as that can create duplicate pending records.

subject: Preciso restaurar registros excluídos em massa
description: Apaguei cerca de 1.200 leads por engano ao aplicar um filtro e usar exclusão em massa. Isso aconteceu há umas 3 horas. Eu preciso recuperar esses registros com as tags e o responsável original, porque a equipe comercial já está cobrando. Onde faço essa restauração?

comments.message: Apaguei cerca de 1.200 leads por engano ao aplicar um filtro e usar exclusão em massa. Isso aconteceu há umas 3 horas. Eu preciso recuperar esses registros com as tags e o responsável original, porque a equipe comercial já está cobrando. Onde faço essa restauração?

Sinto muito por isso. Exclusões em massa recentes podem ser revertidas pela lixeira do workspace por até 30 dias, desde que seu perfil tenha permissão de administrador. Vá em Configurações > Dados > Lixeira, filtre por Leads e pela hora da exclusão, selecione os registros e clique em Restaurar. Tags, proprietário e campos personalizados costumam ser recuperados junto com o registro.
Encontrei a lixeira, mas o botão de restaurar está desabilitado para mim. Sou gerente comercial, não admin.

Perfeito, então o bloqueio é de permissão. Peça a um administrador para fazer a restauração por esse mesmo caminho. Se quiser agilizar, já abri uma nota interna com o horário informado e a quantidade aproximada para que nosso time confirme se houve alguma exclusão em cascata ligada a listas. Se o admin não localizar todos os registros, podemos orientar uma restauração por backup lógico, mas isso precisa de análise do suporte avançado.

subject: Filtros salvos no relatório não persistem e a contagem muda sozinha
description: Eu salvei um relatório com filtros de status, canal e região, mas quando volto algumas horas depois os filtros aparecem corretos visualmente e mesmo assim a contagem de resultados vem diferente do que estava salvo. Se eu removo e adiciono os mesmos filtros de novo, os números mudam pela terceira vez. Isso está deixando impossível confiar no relatório semanal. Preciso de ajuda para investigar.

comments.message: O nome do relatório é “Pipeline LATAM semanal”. Ele deveria mostrar apenas tickets abertos de WhatsApp e Email para a região Sul, mas hoje de manhã apareceu um volume muito maior. Os chips dos filtros parecem normais.

Olá! Pelo que descreveu, pode ser que o relatório esteja incluindo dados atualizados em tempo real e por isso a contagem varie ao longo do dia. Recomendo fixar um intervalo de datas encerrado e verificar novamente.
Eu já deixei o intervalo travado em 06/05 a 12/05 e mesmo assim a contagem muda sem nenhuma alteração de dados. Além disso, se eu editar e salvar de novo, volta ao número “certo” por um tempo.

Obrigado pelo complemento. Isso sugere um problema na aplicação dos filtros salvos, possivelmente na camada de persistência do relatório. Ainda não tenho correção imediata, mas registrei a investigação com exemplos do seu caso. Até termos retorno, a orientação é regravar o relatório antes de compartilhar ou exportar, embora eu entenda que isso não resolva a causa raiz.

subject: Bulk deactivation failed for suspended users
description: I uploaded a CSV to deactivate 27 suspended users, and the job report says completed, but only 19 were actually deactivated. The remaining 8 still show Active, and they all belong to our Support team. I need help figuring out why the bulk action skipped them.

comments.message: I uploaded a CSV to deactivate 27 suspended users, and the job report says completed, but only 19 were actually deactivated. The remaining 8 still show Active, and they all belong to our Support team. I need help figuring out why the bulk action skipped them.

Thanks for reporting this. A common reason is that some users are assigned to protected roles or are the last admin on a team, which prevents bulk deactivation. Please open one of the eight user profiles and check whether they are listed as team managers, billing contacts, or owners of required queues. If so, those assignments need to be reassigned first.
I checked two of them. They are not billing contacts, but they are assigned as default approvers for a Support workflow. I didn’t see any warning in the import results though.

That would explain the behavior. The bulk job currently skips users tied to active approval workflows, but I agree the reporting should be clearer. Reassign those default approver steps to another active user, then rerun the CSV for the remaining eight. If you want, I can also log product feedback about the missing warning in the import summary.

subject: Service account can no longer authenticate to the API
description: Our nightly sync job stopped around 2 AM UTC with authentication failures. The integration uses a dedicated service account, not a personal login. We also noticed that the service account can no longer sign into the UI because it gets redirected to Okta, which it was never configured for. Did something change with access policies that would affect both the API and login?

comments.message: The account is sync-bot@ourcompany.com. API calls now return 401, and trying to log in through the browser sends us to SSO and fails because this isn't a real user mailbox.

It looks like your org enabled mandatory SSO for all users yesterday. That includes service accounts, so the password-based login and any API credentials tied to that user were invalidated. For non-human integrations, we recommend converting the account to a machine user and using an org-level API client instead of a user session.
We can't change the integration today. Is there any immediate workaround to get tonight's sync running?

I've temporarily excluded sync-bot@ourcompany.com from SSO enforcement for 24 hours and reset its local authentication flag. You should be able to log in once to generate a new API token, and existing password-based UI access should also work during that window. After that, you'll need to migrate to a machine user because the exemption will expire automatically.

subject: Webhook step in workflow fails with 401 after token rotation
description: Our automated workflow posts ticket data to an internal webhook when a case is escalated. After we rotated the API token yesterday, every webhook action started failing with 401 Unauthorized. I updated the token in what I thought was the right place, but the automation is still broken. I need help finding where the workflow is actually pulling credentials from.

comments.message: We have several automations, but this specific one sends a POST request to our incident bridge. Since rotating credentials, all executions show 401 in the workflow log. I updated the token under Integrations > API keys, but new runs are still unauthorized.

I checked the usual setup for webhook actions. In most accounts, workflow webhook steps use the credential stored in the connection object, not the generic API key list. Please go to Automation Builder > Connections, open the connection attached to that webhook step, and update the Authorization header or secret there. If you only changed the account-level API key, existing workflow connections would continue using the old token.
That was it. The connection still had the old bearer token saved. I've updated it and test runs are now returning 200.

Great, glad that confirmed it. As a follow-up, I'd recommend naming shared connections with the system they target and documenting token ownership, since rotated secrets often stay hidden in workflow-level connections.

subject: Webhook setup page loads, but API key creation is blocked for my admin
description: I need to configure outgoing webhooks and test them with the API, but I can't create an API key anymore. I can still log in and browse most admin screens, so I thought my permissions were intact. However, the Developers page says I don't have access, and our deployment automation is now failing because it can't fetch a token.

comments.message: My role is Workspace Admin. I can edit workflows, but Settings > Developers shows an access denied banner. The CI job that uses my old API key started getting 401 yesterday.

Your login itself is okay, but your workspace changed to a restricted admin model. In that model, Workspace Admins can manage webhooks but cannot create new API credentials unless they also have the Developer Credentials permission. Your old key likely expired under the new security policy.
Can you grant that permission to me directly? We need the deployment job back up today.

I can't grant it from support because your account requires owner approval for credential-issuing privileges. I've sent a request to the workspace owner listed on the account and noted the CI outage. If they approve it, you'll be able to create a new API key immediately after logging out and back in.

subject: Training users on dashboards, but scheduled emails are failing
description: I'm creating internal training materials for our team on how to build dashboards and schedule email digests. The dashboard part is straightforward, but the scheduled emails are failing for several users with no clear error beyond 'delivery issue.' I need to teach people what they can troubleshoot themselves versus what needs admin help.

comments.message: Can you give me a basic troubleshooting flow we can include in our training guide?

Yes. A good user-facing flow is: first confirm the dashboard is shared with the intended recipients, then verify the schedule is still active, then check whether the recipient list contains external domains, and finally ask your IT team to allowlist our sending domain. If those checks don't fix it, admins should review org-level email restrictions and any bounced-recipient notices.
We already checked sharing and the schedules are active. There are no bounce notices anywhere in the app. Does that point to a platform issue?

Possibly, but not conclusively. Since there isn't enough detail in the ticket to isolate whether this is caused by your mail policy, recipient suppression, or a backend delivery problem, I'm escalating this to our deliverability team. They'll inspect recent send logs for the affected users. For your training guide, I'd still separate it into user checks (sharing, schedule ownership, recipient list) versus admin checks (domain policy, org restrictions, suppressed recipients).

subject: Need help setting up access for department leads
description: We're expanding our account and I need to add five department leads. They all need to log in and manage users within their own teams, but they should not be able to edit company-wide billing or security settings. I couldn't tell from the role names which one fits, and I don't want to accidentally over-permission them. Can you explain which role to use and how to assign team-specific admin access?

comments.message: We're expanding our account and I need to add five department leads. They all need to log in and manage users within their own teams, but they should not be able to edit company-wide billing or security settings. I couldn't tell from the role names which one fits, and I don't want to accidentally over-permission them. Can you explain which role to use and how to assign team-specific admin access?

For that setup, use the Team Admin role rather than Workspace Admin. Team Admins can sign in, add and manage users for the teams they are assigned to, but they can't change billing, SSO, or global security settings. To configure it, go to Admin Settings > Users, invite each lead, assign Team Admin as the role, then under Team Scope select the departments they should manage. If someone should only review membership without inviting users, use Manager instead.
That helps. One more thing: if a Team Admin invites someone, can they choose any role or only roles within their scope?

Only roles allowed within their scope. A Team Admin can invite users into their assigned teams and grant non-global roles such as Viewer, Commenter, Member, or another Team Admin if your workspace allows delegated admin creation. They cannot assign Workspace Admin or access billing settings. If you want tighter control, you can disable delegated admin creation in Security > Role Controls so they can manage users but not promote peers.

subject: OAuth app users stuck in login loop before API consent screen
description: We're testing our OAuth integration and several users in our customer portal can't get past your login screen to authorize the app. They enter valid credentials, get redirected back to sign in once, and never reach the consent page. Existing tokens still work, but new authorizations are blocked. I suspect something broke in the auth flow.

comments.message: The issue only affects new OAuth authorizations. Users sign in at the authorize endpoint, briefly see the dashboard, then are redirected to login again instead of getting the consent page. If I use an already-issued refresh token, the API still works.

Could you share the exact authorize URL including redirect_uri, scopes, and whether PKCE is enabled? A login loop before consent can happen if the session is established on one domain and the authorize request is sent to another.
We're using the same domain as before and PKCE is enabled. I sent the URL in the private attachment. Also, this began right after your status page mentioned a fix to authentication caching.

I reviewed the URL and it looks valid. I also found elevated errors tied to our auth cache rollout that can prevent the consent session from being recognized after login. This is a platform bug on our side, not your OAuth configuration. I've attached your examples to the incident and temporarily extended the rate limit on your existing client so you can lean on current tokens while we work on the fix.

subject: Slack integration alerts report not capturing all delivered messages
description: We're using the Slack integration to track alert deliveries, and the weekly operational report is missing a chunk of messages from one workspace. The alerts are visible in channel history and some appear on user activity pages, but the report totals don't line up. Please review whether this is a Slack integration issue, a reporting aggregation issue, and whether this should be escalated because our compliance team reviews these counts.

comments.message: Our 'Alert Deliveries by Workspace' report is short by roughly 1,800 Slack messages for the last seven days. Can you verify the sync?
I checked the Slack connector status and it shows green, so the integration is fine. The report may just need time to recalculate.
We've waited since Monday. Compliance needs exact numbers, not 'maybe later'.

You're right to push on that. Looking deeper, messages from your secondary Slack workspace are being ingested under a new workspace token after your admin rotated credentials, but the report's workspace join table hasn't fully updated. Some message records exist, but they are orphaned from the aggregation layer. I can't repair that relationship from support, so I've escalated it to engineering with example channel IDs and requested a priority reindex.

subject: Can't access admin panel after password reset
description: I reset my password this morning because I forgot it, and now I can log in but the admin panel just throws me back to the dashboard every time. Regular pages load, but anything under Settings > Admin is inaccessible. I've tried Chrome and Edge and get the same result.

comments.message: I reset my password this morning because I forgot it, and now I can log in but the admin panel just throws me back to the dashboard every time. Regular pages load, but anything under Settings > Admin is inaccessible. I've tried Chrome and Edge and get the same result.
This sounds like a browser cache issue. Please clear cookies and cached files completely, then log back in and test the admin panel again.
I already did that before opening the ticket. Still happening, and my colleague can open the admin panel on her account from the same machine.

In that case it may be related to your role permissions not refreshing after the password reset. I can't change roles from this queue, so I've forwarded this to our access team to verify whether your admin grant is still attached to your user record. They should follow up shortly.

subject: Erro 400 ao atualizar campos personalizados em lote
description: Estou tentando atualizar campos personalizados de empresas via API em lote, mas recebo erro 400 quando o payload inclui um campo de data e um campo numérico no mesmo request. Além disso, quando a operação falha, alguns registros parecem ter sido atualizados parcialmente e outros não, o que deixou nossos dados inconsistentes. Preciso de orientação para corrigir o payload e também de uma forma segura de reconciliar os registros já afetados.

comments.message: O endpoint é /api/companies/bulk_update. O retorno diz 'invalid value type', mas não informa qual campo causou o erro. Temos cerca de 12 mil empresas para corrigir.

Olá! Esse erro normalmente ocorre quando campos personalizados são enviados sem respeitar o tipo esperado pelo schema. Para campos de data, envie no formato YYYY-MM-DD. Para campos numéricos, envie número sem aspas. Sobre a atualização parcial, esse endpoint processa os registros individualmente dentro do lote, então alguns podem ser aplicados mesmo se outros falharem.
Certo, mas como descubro qual registro falhou e quais foram aplicados? A resposta só traz contagem agregada.

Nesse caso, peço desculpas, mas não temos como identificar pelo retorno atual exatamente quais itens falharam nesse endpoint legado. A recomendação é dividir o lote em blocos menores e consultar os registros depois para comparar os valores. Vou encaminhar sua solicitação ao time de produto, porque realmente falta granularidade no log de erros. Enquanto isso, para reconciliar os dados, você pode exportar as empresas afetadas, comparar com sua fonte original e reenviar apenas as divergências em lotes de 100.

subject: Historical data export incomplete and admin login blocked by suspicious activity
description: I tried exporting all opportunities for the last two years, but the file stops around March of last year even though the filter is correct. When I went to check settings, my admin account got blocked for suspicious activity after a few login attempts from VPN. I need to regain access and figure out whether the export is actually missing data or timing out.

comments.message: The export UI says complete, but the CSV only has 18,204 rows and we should have closer to 31,000. Then I got a suspicious activity message and my login was blocked after retrying from our VPN endpoint.

The login block was likely an automated security lock because the VPN IP changed or looked high-risk. An owner on your account can unblock you, or if you're the only owner we'll need to verify identity and escalate to Trust & Safety. On the data side, large exports can silently split by date or exclude archived opportunities depending on the selected view. Did you run the export from a saved filtered view or from the raw Opportunities table?
It was from a saved view that excludes archived items, which I missed. I'm still locked out though, and I'm the only owner.

That explains the incomplete export result. For access, I can't unlock an only-owner admin directly in chat, so I'm opening a secure verification case with our account access team. They'll contact you at the billing email to restore login after identity checks.

subject: Users trapped in login loop when workflow sends approval link
description: Our approvers receive the email from an automated workflow, click the link, and get sent back to the sign-in page over and over. A few eventually get in, but most give up, so approvals are delayed. I need help understanding whether the automation link is wrong or our access settings are.

comments.message: The workflow sends an approval email with a direct link to the record. When users click it, they authenticate with Okta and then land back on login instead of the approval page. Can you explain what causes that?

This usually happens when the workflow email uses a deep link to a page the user does not have permission to open after SSO completes.
