The authentication succeeds, but the app redirects them because the target route requires a role they do not hold. Please compare one affected user's role with one of the people who eventually gets in, and also check whether the workflow email template is using the internal admin URL instead of the standard app URL.
You were right about the URL. The template had the admin domain, and most approvers are not admins. Their role is just Approver.

Great catch. Update the workflow template to use the standard approval link token instead of the hard-coded admin URL, then send a test to an Approver role user. As a general rule, avoid pasting browser URLs into automation emails; use the workflow's built-in record or approval link variables so the system generates a destination appropriate for the recipient's access level.

subject: Locked out after enabling SCIM, API provisioning also failing
description: We turned on SCIM provisioning from Azure AD this morning. New users are not being created through the API, and our IT team also lost access to the admin console because their local passwords no longer work. We expected SSO to handle interactive login, but now both provisioning and admin access are a mess.

comments.message: The SCIM test connection passed yesterday. Today POST requests to the provisioning API return 403, and the admins are seeing "local auth disabled" on the login page.

Once SCIM is enabled, local auth is disabled by design, so your admins should sign in through your identity provider instead of with local passwords. For the 403 on provisioning, can you confirm the bearer token belongs to an org admin and not a regular member?
The token was created by one of the admins before we turned SSO on. He can still access Azure, but he can't get into your app anymore to recreate anything.

I can confirm the old personal token lost its admin scope after your access policy changed. I’ve escalated this to our identity team because SCIM tokens should ideally be service-based and not tied to an interactive admin login. For now, have a current IdP-assigned org admin sign in via SSO and create a new provisioning token under Settings > Directory Sync. That should restore API provisioning while the escalation is reviewed.

subject: Lead routing rule ignores region override field
description: I'm configuring lead assignment so that inbound leads normally route by country, but sales ops wants a manual Region Override field to take priority when it's filled in. I added the override field and updated the automation, but leads are still being assigned by country even when Region Override has a value. I need to know whether the field is not available to the workflow yet or whether the routing order is wrong.

comments.message: Example: a lead from Canada with Region Override set to EMEA still goes to the North America queue. The rule order is Override first, then country mapping. The field is a dropdown we added yesterday.

Thanks. New fields can take a few minutes to propagate to workflow evaluation, but if this has been longer than that, the more likely issue is the field value format. In Product Configuration, can you confirm whether Region Override stores `EMEA` as text or as an internal option ID? In the routing workflow, the first condition should be "Region Override is not empty," and then route based on the specific stored value.
It has definitely propagated by now. The stored values are plain text. I already have "is not empty" plus a nested check for EMEA.

In that case, try moving the country rule set into an else block under the override check. If both rule groups are top-level and your assignment action doesn't stop further processing, the later country mapping can overwrite the queue selected by the override branch. Publish the workflow again and test with the lead activity log open; you should see only the override branch execute.

subject: New automation rule saves but does not fire on ticket reopen
description: I created an automation rule that's supposed to notify our account queue whenever a closed ticket is reopened, but it never fires. The rule validates and saves without errors. I tested with multiple tickets and waited over an hour, and there are no runs in the automation history. Could there be a bug with reopen events?

comments.message: The trigger is status changes from Closed to Open and the action is post to queue #accounts. Similar rules for New to Open work fine.

Thanks. I checked the rule syntax and your condition looks valid. One thing to note is that tickets in Closed status may be immutable depending on your workspace settings, so some "reopens" are implemented as follow-up tickets instead of status changes. Could you confirm whether the affected records keep the same ticket ID or generate a new one?
They generate a new follow-up ticket ID. I didn't realize that mattered.

That explains why the rule never runs: there is no actual Closed-to-Open transition on the original ticket. This isn't a product bug in the rule engine, but I did test the behavior because your description suggested one. You’ll want a trigger based on follow-up ticket creation instead. If you'd like, I can escalate a product feedback request because the UI doesn't make this distinction very clear when building reopen-related automations.

subject: Need to move one user to a different subscription and fix duplicate charge
description: I downgraded one of our contractors from a paid seat to viewer access last week, and I also changed the billing admin on the account. We were still charged for the full paid seat on yesterday's invoice, and I think I may also be seeing a duplicate prorated line item. Can someone review the account changes and correct the billing?

comments.message: I downgraded one of our contractors from a paid seat to viewer access last week, and I also changed the billing admin on the account. We were still charged for the full paid seat on yesterday's invoice, and I think I may also be seeing a duplicate prorated line item. Can someone review the account changes and correct the billing?

I checked your workspace and can confirm the seat change for Marcus was made on the 12th, but the billing cycle closed on the 11th, so the full seat charge on the latest invoice is expected. However, there is an extra prorated charge from when the billing admin change triggered a plan sync in error. I've removed that duplicate line item and issued a $48.00 credit to your next invoice. Your current billing admin is now listed as Priya Nair. Anything else I can help with?

subject: Duplicate charges after cloning a workspace template
description: We cloned our support workspace to create a new region-specific instance, including forms, macros, and SLA policies. After that change, our invoice appears to show two Platform Enterprise charges. I expected the clone to copy configuration only, not create a second paid subscription. Did the setup process accidentally provision another billable workspace?

comments.message: The new instance was created from the template tool last Thursday. We only wanted a separate config for EMEA, but finance now sees what looks like a second full subscription charge.

Cloning a workspace template can create a separate billable instance if it was completed through the multi-workspace wizard instead of the sandbox/template preview tool. I'm checking which path was used on your account.
Can you confirm whether that's what happened? If so, we need to undo it quickly.

I confirmed a second production workspace was provisioned, which is why the extra Platform Enterprise charge appeared. I've submitted a deprovision request for the duplicate instance and asked billing to reverse the charge if the workspace is removed within the grace period. Until operations completes that request, both workspaces will still show as active.

subject: La integración con Slack no crea canales para nuevos proyectos
description: Tenemos activada la integración con Slack para que cada proyecto nuevo cree su canal automáticamente, pero desde el lunes ya no se están creando. Los proyectos sí se crean bien en su sistema y el bot sigue instalado en Slack. ¿Qué deberíamos revisar?

comments.message: Además, los proyectos creados manualmente en Slack sí funcionan, solo falla la creación automática desde su plataforma.

Gracias. Cuando la creación automática falla pero el bot sigue instalado, normalmente revisamos permisos del bot y el log de automatizaciones. ¿Puede entrar en Integraciones > Slack > Registro y decirme si aparece algún error como "missing_scope" o "channel_name_invalid"?
Sí, veo varios errores "missing_scope: channels:manage" desde el lunes.

Eso explica el fallo. Slack revocó o cambió el alcance necesario para crear canales. Deben volver a autorizar la integración aceptando el permiso channels:manage. Después de eso, los proyectos nuevos deberían crear canal otra vez. Los proyectos ya creados durante la falla no se recuperarán solos, así que tendrán que ejecutar la acción "Crear canal" manualmente o desde una automatización en lote.

subject: Need to change service account email without breaking API client
description: We need to rename the employee attached to our service account because she left the company, but our internal apps authenticate to your API through that account. I'm worried that changing the account email or profile details will break the client credentials flow or webhook subscriptions tied to it. What's the safest way to make this account change without disrupting production?

comments.message: Our API integration uses a dedicated user account, but HR needs us to change the email on that account to a generic address. Before I do that, I need to know whether changing the login email will affect the client ID/secret or invalidate current access tokens.

You can safely update the user's email address from the Admin > Users page. That should not change your client ID or client secret, and existing webhook subscriptions usually stay in place. Access tokens that are already issued may continue until they expire, so we still recommend doing the change during a low-traffic window and then testing a fresh token request.
I changed the email and now our token endpoint is returning invalid_grant for the password-based flow we still use for one legacy script. The client credentials integration still works.

That makes sense: if one script still uses the user's username/password rather than pure client credentials, changing the login email will also change the username it must send to the token endpoint. Update that script to use the new email, and if the password was reset during the account change, enter the new one there as well. Longer term, I'd strongly suggest retiring the password-based flow for that script.

subject: Deleted projects still appear in API sync results
description: We hard-deleted 67 test projects from our workspace before turning on our nightly API sync, but the sync endpoint still returns many of those deleted project IDs with a null name and stale timestamps. I confirmed they are gone in the admin UI and not just archived. Our downstream job treats them as active rows because they still exist in the payload. Can you help determine if your deletion process is lagging somewhere and how we should handle the bad data until it’s fixed?

comments.message: We hard-deleted 67 test projects from our workspace before turning on our nightly API sync, but the sync endpoint still returns many of those deleted project IDs with a null name and stale timestamps. I confirmed they are gone in the admin UI and not just archived. Our downstream job treats them as active rows because they still exist in the payload. Can you help determine if your deletion process is lagging somewhere and how we should handle the bad data until it’s fixed?

Thanks — I checked your workspace and can confirm the UI deletion completed, but the incremental sync feed still contains tombstoned project IDs without a deletion flag. That’s not expected. For now, please ignore records where name is null and updated_at is older than the deletion time. I’m opening an incident with our API team because the feed should either omit them or mark them as deleted.
Do you have any estimate for when the incident might be fixed? We use that feed for finance reporting, so filtering on null names is risky if legitimate bad data ever appears.

I understand. I don’t have an ETA yet, but I’ve marked the incident as customer-impacting and linked your reporting use case. As a safer temporary measure, I’ve attached a list of the 67 deleted project IDs from the audit log so your team can exclude only those specific records until engineering updates the sync response.

subject: Factura incorrecta y error al exportar reportes
description: Nos cambiaron al plan Enterprise en abril, pero la factura de mayo sigue mostrando el precio anterior más un cargo por usuarios excedentes que no entendemos. Al mismo tiempo, cuando intento exportar reportes en CSV aparece el mensaje "export failed". Quiero saber cuánto debemos realmente y cómo arreglar la exportación.

comments.message: Nos cambiaron al plan Enterprise en abril, pero la factura de mayo sigue mostrando el precio anterior más un cargo por usuarios excedentes que no entendemos. Al mismo tiempo, cuando intento exportar reportes en CSV aparece el mensaje "export failed". Quiero saber cuánto debemos realmente y cómo arreglar la exportación.

Hola. El cargo por usuarios excedentes suele aparecer cuando hay más miembros activos de los permitidos por el plan. Si no quieren ese cargo, pueden eliminar usuarios. En cuanto al error de exportación, por favor borren caché y cookies del navegador.
Eso no responde mucho. En Enterprise nos dijeron que no había límite de usuarios con nuestro contrato anual, y ya borré caché. El error solo pasa con reportes de más de 90 días.

Entiendo. Entonces puede ser una limitación del sistema con archivos grandes. Les recomiendo dividir la exportación por rangos de fecha más cortos. Sobre la factura, tendría que revisarlo el equipo comercial porque desde soporte no veo detalles del contrato.

subject: Refund request for add-on after failed restore of deleted records
description: We purchased the advanced backup add-on specifically to restore deleted opportunity records from last quarter. We were charged immediately, but the self-service restore only brought back partial records and left related activities missing. Since we paid for the add-on because of this recovery need, I want either a full restore or a refund. Please escalate if support cannot perform the restore directly.

comments.message: We bought the backup add-on to recover deleted opportunities, but the restore brought back only partial records and no related activities. Can you either complete the restore or refund the add-on?
The backup add-on fee is non-refundable once activated. Related activities are not always included in restores, so this may be expected behavior.
That was not stated anywhere in the purchase flow, and the sales rep specifically said we'd be able to recover the deleted opportunities with history.

I've escalated both concerns: first, to our recovery specialists to verify whether related activities should have been included for your backup snapshot; second, to billing leadership for an exception review based on the product representation you received. I can't promise a refund, but the case now reflects both the incomplete restore and the disputed add-on charge.

subject: Échec de synchronisation Salesforce après rotation de clé API
description: Je suis administrateur RevOps chez un client Enterprise et notre intégration Salesforce ne synchronise plus les nouveaux comptes depuis hier soir. Nous avons fait une rotation de la clé API côté plateforme, mais dans les logs d'intégration je vois surtout des erreurs 401 et quelques messages "mapping introuvable" sur l'objet Compte. J'ai relancé la connexion sans succès et notre équipe commerciale commence à travailler avec des données incomplètes. Pouvez-vous nous dire quoi vérifier et si une resynchronisation est possible sans créer de doublons ?

comments.message: Bonjour, depuis la rotation de notre clé API, la synchro Salesforce > votre plateforme échoue. Les nouveaux comptes ne remontent plus, et j'ai des erreurs 401 dans le connecteur. J'ai peur de casser le mapping si je reconnecte complètement.

Bonjour, les erreurs 401 indiquent presque toujours que l'ancienne clé est encore utilisée par l'intégration. Vous pouvez aller dans Paramètres > Intégrations > Salesforce, cliquer sur "Reconnecter", puis coller la nouvelle clé. Vérifiez ensuite dans l'onglet Mapping que l'objet "Compte" est toujours associé à "Account". Une reconnexion ne crée pas de doublons tant que l'ID externe Salesforce est inchangé.

J'ai reconnecté avec la nouvelle clé et le 401 a disparu, mais les comptes créés ce matin ne remontent toujours pas. Dans le mapping, je vois que le champ identifiant externe est vide pour l'objet Compte.

Merci, cela explique le blocage. Sans identifiant externe, la synchro incrémentale ne sait pas faire la correspondance. Remettez le champ Salesforce Account ID comme identifiant externe, enregistrez, puis lancez une resynchronisation des enregistrements échoués uniquement depuis "File d'attente > Réessayer". Cela devrait reprendre sans doublons. Si vous voyez encore des éléments bloqués après ça, je peux ouvrir une vérification côté intégrations.

subject: Lista dinâmica não remove contatos com opt-out
description: Criei uma lista dinâmica para campanhas ativas e defini uma regra para excluir contatos com opt-out de e-mail. Mesmo assim, ainda aparecem pessoas que já cancelaram inscrição. Isso está afetando nossa segmentação e preciso entender se a lista está com atraso ou se o campo certo não é esse.

comments.message: Criei uma lista dinâmica para campanhas ativas e defini uma regra para excluir contatos com opt-out de e-mail. Mesmo assim, ainda aparecem pessoas que já cancelaram inscrição. Isso está afetando nossa segmentação e preciso entender se a lista está com atraso ou se o campo certo não é esse.
Listas dinâmicas podem levar alguns minutos para reprocessar, então é normal haver atraso. Aguarde um pouco e veja se a segmentação corrige sozinha.
Eu esperei desde ontem. E quando abro o registro do contato, o status de inscrição está como cancelado, mas na lista ele continua.

Entendi. Nesse caso, verifique se a regra da lista está usando o campo 'Opt-out de e-mail' do perfil do contato e não o evento de marketing. Algumas contas antigas têm dois campos parecidos: um booleano operacional e um status de assinatura por canal. Se você puder, edite o filtro para excluir 'Status de assinatura de e-mail = cancelado'. Se ainda persistir, pode haver reindexação travada e eu recomendo abrir um chamado técnico com o ID da lista.

subject: Le centre d'aide affiche la mauvaise base de connaissances selon le client
description: Nous avons plusieurs marques et j'essaie de configurer le centre d'aide pour que chaque client voie la bonne base de connaissances, avec les bons articles et le bon branding. Pourtant, certains clients de notre marque premium voient encore des articles génériques. Je dois aussi préparer un guide de formation pour nos content managers.

comments.message: Bonjour, nous utilisons deux marques dans la plateforme : Standard et Premium. J'ai créé des catégories séparées et un thème spécifique pour Premium, mais quand je me connecte avec un compte client Premium, je vois encore des articles Standard dans la recherche. Pouvez-vous m'aider à comprendre la bonne configuration ?

Bonjour, cela peut venir de la recherche, qui indexe parfois plus large que la navigation. Avez-vous bien affecté les articles Premium à la bonne marque et limité leur visibilité aux bons segments d'utilisateurs ? Il faut généralement configurer à la fois la marque, le segment, et parfois le thème du centre d'aide.
Je pense que les segments sont la partie que nous maîtrisons le moins. Comment l'expliquer simplement aux content managers pour éviter des erreurs de publication ?

Expliquez-leur que la marque détermine “où” le contenu vit, et le segment détermine “qui” peut le voir. Concrètement : 1) vérifier la marque de l'article, 2) sélectionner le bon segment d'audience, 3) tester avec un compte client de chaque marque, 4) republier puis attendre la réindexation. Comme la recherche semble impliquée ici, je vais aussi escalader ce ticket à notre équipe spécialisée centre d'aide afin qu'elle vérifie l'indexation de votre marque Premium.

subject: No se aplicó el cupón anual y la app móvil no sincroniza
description: Renovamos ayer con un cupón de descuento del 15%, pero el cobro llegó por el total sin descuento. Además, desde la renovación la app móvil de Android no sincroniza tareas nuevas; se queda en "sincronizando" indefinidamente. Ya reinicié el teléfono y reinstalé la app.

comments.message: Renovamos ayer con un cupón de descuento del 15%, pero el cobro llegó por el total sin descuento. Además, desde la renovación la app móvil de Android no sincroniza tareas nuevas; se queda en "sincronizando" indefinidamente. Ya reinicié el teléfono y reinstalé la app.

Hola, gracias por los detalles. Veo que el cupón ANUAL15 se introdujo después de que la renovación ya se hubiera procesado, por eso no se aplicó al cobro. No puedo ajustar ese cargo directamente desde este chat, así que abriré una solicitud al equipo de facturación para que evalúe un crédito equivalente.

Sobre Android, revisé los logs y la renovación dejó el workspace en estado pendiente durante unos minutos; eso suele romper el token de sincronización. Por favor vayan a Ajustes > Cuenta > Restablecer sincronización en un dispositivo afectado y luego inicien sesión de nuevo.
Hicimos eso en dos teléfonos y volvió a sincronizar. ¿Cuánto tarda lo del crédito?

Perfecto, me alegra que la sincronización se haya recuperado. El caso de facturación quedó escalado con prioridad normal y suele resolverse en 24 a 48 horas hábiles. Les comparto la referencia BIL-77219; recibirán correo cuando confirmen si aplican el crédito por el 15%.

subject: Duplication de champs après import de configuration
description: J'ai importé une configuration depuis notre environnement de test vers la production pour gagner du temps sur le déploiement, mais certains champs personnalisés apparaissent en double dans les formulaires. Je dois comprendre ce qui s'est passé pour corriger la configuration et expliquer à mon équipe comment éviter ce problème à l'avenir.

comments.message: Bonjour, après l'import, j'ai maintenant deux champs “Centre de coût” et deux champs “Application concernée”, avec des identifiants différents. Les formulaires montrent parfois l'un, parfois l'autre. Quelle est la bonne manière de nettoyer cela sans casser les automatisations ?

Bonjour, merci. Dans ce type de cas, je préfère ne pas vous faire supprimer des champs directement, car cela peut affecter les règles, vues et workflows existants. La cause la plus fréquente est que l'import a créé de nouveaux champs au lieu de faire une correspondance avec ceux déjà présents en production.
Oui, c'est probablement ce qui s'est produit. Mais comment procéder maintenant, et que dois-je dire à l'équipe qui va refaire des imports plus tard ?

Je vais vous proposer une approche prudente : 1) identifiez quel champ est réellement utilisé par les automatisations, 2) retirez le doublon des formulaires avant toute suppression, 3) mettez à jour les règles qui pointent vers le mauvais identifiant, 4) supprimez seulement le champ inutilisé après vérification. Pour prévenir ce problème, formez votre équipe à comparer les identifiants système avant import et à utiliser, si disponible, l'option de correspondance par clé externe. Je préfère aussi escalader votre cas à notre équipe technique pour confirmer qu'aucune dépendance cachée ne bloquera la suppression.

subject: Custom field values shifted after list deduplication
description: We used the deduplication tool on our lead list yesterday, and afterward several merged records have the wrong custom field values. In multiple cases, the surviving record kept the older lead score and region instead of the newer values. I need to understand whether the merge logic is buggy and whether there is any audit trail or rollback for the field-level changes.

comments.message: We used the deduplication tool on our lead list yesterday, and afterward several merged records have the wrong custom field values. In multiple cases, the surviving record kept the older lead score and region instead of the newer values. I need to understand whether the merge logic is buggy and whether there is any audit trail or rollback for the field-level changes.

Our deduplication process normally keeps the primary record’s values unless a field is blank, so some field differences can be expected. Can you share two merged record IDs and tell me how you selected the primary record during dedupe?

Merged lead IDs are L-22014 and L-22109. We selected 'most recently updated record wins,' which is why I wasn’t expecting older values to survive. The field history on region also shows the change happened during merge.

I reviewed your examples and agree the outcome doesn’t match the selected merge rule. The primary record was chosen correctly, but the custom field resolver appears to have applied the default 'keep existing value' behavior instead of the 'most recently updated' rule for non-standard fields. There isn’t a one-click rollback for merged field values, but the audit log preserves the previous values. I’ve opened a bug with engineering and exported the pre-merge values for the affected records; I can send you a CSV so you can bulk restore the fields if that works for you.

subject: Need card updated and former admin removed
description: Our corporate card expired at the end of last month, and the last payment failed. At the same time, the employee who used to manage our subscription is no longer with us, but she is still listed as the billing admin. I need to replace the payment method, remove her access, and make sure the invoice doesn't keep retrying the old card.

comments.message: Payment for invoice INV-44821 failed because the Visa ending 1142 expired. The issue is I can't update the card because the billing admin on file is Priya, and she left the company two weeks ago. Please remove Priya's billing access and let me add our new Mastercard ending 8821.

You can update your payment method under Settings > Billing once you are an admin. I have removed Priya from the list of billing contacts so she should no longer receive invoices. To make you the billing admin, please ask an existing workspace owner to assign the role, and then retry payment manually from the invoice page.

There isn't another workspace owner available right now—that was Priya too. Removing her from notifications didn't give me any permissions, and the failed invoice is still showing automatic retries against the expired card.

In that case, please wait for the next retry attempt. The system usually refreshes card details automatically after a failed charge, and if not, your bank can often forward the payment from the expired card to the replacement card.

subject: CSV import fails on row 214 with invalid date error
description: I'm importing a customer list via CSV and the upload keeps failing on row 214 with an invalid date error. The date in that row looks normal to me, and I already re-saved the file as UTF-8 CSV. The import screen doesn't tell me which field is the problem, so I'm stuck troubleshooting this blind.

comments.message: I'm importing a customer list via CSV and the upload keeps failing on row 214 with an invalid date error. The date in that row looks normal to me, and I already re-saved the file as UTF-8 CSV. The import screen doesn't tell me which field is the problem, so I'm stuck troubleshooting this blind.
Usually that means the system wants MM/DD/YYYY. Please change the date format in that row and re-upload the file.
The value is already 03/14/2024, so it's already in that format. I also removed the whole row and then it failed on the next row with the same message.
In that case the file may just be corrupted. Please create a new CSV from scratch and try again.

subject: Can’t add supervisors to report subscriptions
description: I’m trying to subscribe our supervisors to a weekly performance report, but their names don’t appear in the recipient picker. They can log in and view the report manually, so I don’t understand why I can’t add them. I’m also putting together an internal how-to guide for our admins, so I need a clear explanation of what controls who can be added to report subscriptions.

comments.message: The supervisors are active users and part of the Support department. They can open the dashboard from the app, but when I edit the weekly subscription only a few admins are available as recipients. What setting controls that?
That happens when users are not marked as report recipients in their user profile.
I don’t see any report recipient setting in the user profile. Is that in roles or groups?

You’re right, I misspoke. There isn’t a separate 'report recipient' toggle. In most accounts, available recipients are limited by license type and whether the user has access to the report’s underlying folder. Please check whether those supervisors have the same paid seat type as the admins who do appear, and whether the report sits in a folder shared with the Support department. For your guide, I’d describe subscriptions as depending on both active user status and report access.

subject: Annual invoice shows tax twice after plan change
description: We switched from monthly to annual billing last week, and the invoice generated today appears to include VAT twice. The subtotal looks right, but there are two separate tax lines and the final amount is much higher than the quote our sales rep sent. Also, when I click into billing details, the tax breakdown page throws a generic error. Can someone check whether this is a calculation bug or if I am reading the invoice wrong?

comments.message: We switched from monthly to annual billing last week, and the invoice generated today appears to include VAT twice. The subtotal looks right, but there are two separate tax lines and the final amount is much higher than the quote our sales rep sent. Also, when I click into billing details, the tax breakdown page throws a generic error. Can someone check whether this is a calculation bug or if I am reading the invoice wrong?

I reviewed the invoice and I believe the tax is correct. One line is probably for the subscription and the other is likely for the billing period adjustment. The error page may just be a browser cache problem, so please try incognito mode and clear cookies.
I already tried two browsers. The tax amount is exactly duplicated, not prorated, and the quote from sales only had one VAT entry.
Understood. I’m not able to validate tax logic from support, so I recommend paying the invoice as-is to avoid interruption and contacting your account manager if you want a credit later.

subject: Mandantenweite Spracheinstellung wird nach Import überschrieben
description: Ich habe unsere Produktkonfiguration für die DACH-Region angepasst und danach per CSV mehrere Standorte importiert. Seitdem ist die Standardsprache für neue Unterkonten wieder auf Englisch statt Deutsch. Ich finde keine Regel, die das verursacht. Das ist kritisch, weil neue Kunden falsche Systemtexte sehen.

comments.message: Im Import selbst gab es keine Spalte für Sprache. Vorher war auf Mandantenebene überall Deutsch als Standard gesetzt.

Guten Tag, ich habe in der Dokumentation nachgesehen: Der Standortimport sollte die Mandantensprache nicht ändern. Könnten Sie bitte unter Einstellungen > Regionen prüfen, ob dort Englisch als Fallback gesetzt ist?
Dort steht Deutsch als Fallback. Trotzdem werden neue Unterkonten auf Englisch angelegt. Können Sie bitte tiefer prüfen oder weitergeben?

Ich habe Ihren Fall an die Plattformtechnik eskaliert, weil das Verhalten nicht der erwarteten Konfiguration entspricht. Nach erster Prüfung könnte ein bekannter Fehler beim Legacy-Importer vorliegen, der den Sprachwert beim Erstellen von Unterkonten auf den globalen Systemdefault zurücksetzt. Bis das Team den Tenant geprüft hat, empfehle ich neue Unterkonten vorerst manuell anzulegen, wenn Deutsch zwingend erforderlich ist. Ich aktualisiere das Ticket, sobald ich eine Rückmeldung habe.

subject: Relatório mensal mostra valores duplicados após exportação
description: Eu gerei o relatório mensal de atividades para abril e os totais por equipe estão vindo praticamente em dobro na exportação CSV. Dentro da tela de Relatórios os números parecem corretos em alguns widgets, mas no arquivo exportado o total de horas e de tickets fechados fica duplicado para alguns departamentos. Já testei com dois filtros diferentes e aconteceu a mesma coisa. Preciso entender se isso é um bug e como posso fechar meu relatório financeiro hoje.

comments.message: Notei isso hoje de manhã. Se eu filtro por 01/04 a 30/04 e agrupo por equipe, o dashboard mostra 148 tickets fechados para Suporte N1, mas no CSV saem 296 linhas somadas no total consolidado. Isso está afetando meu fechamento do mês.

Olá! Obrigado pelos detalhes. Isso pode acontecer quando há atualização incompleta do cache do relatório. Você pode tentar recriar o relatório do zero e limpar os filtros salvos antes de exportar? Se possível, me diga também o nome exato do relatório e o fuso horário da conta.

Recriei do zero em uma aba anônima e o problema continua. O relatório se chama “Fechamento Operacional - Abril”. Nosso fuso é America/Sao_Paulo. Também percebi que a duplicação só aparece quando incluo a métrica de horas registradas.

Consegui reproduzir internamente um comportamento parecido quando o relatório combina “tickets fechados” com “horas registradas” e agrupamento por equipe. Isso parece ser um bug na lógica de exportação, não no widget da tela. Abri o chamado de engenharia BI-4821 com a sua reprodução. Como alternativa temporária, exporte sem a métrica de horas e faça uma segunda exportação apenas com horas registradas; na interface os valores continuam corretos. Assim que eu tiver retorno da engenharia, atualizo você.

subject: Unexpected charge after disabling premium workflow rules
description: I turned off the premium workflow rules in our workspace before our renewal because we no longer needed them, but our invoice that came through yesterday still includes the Advanced Automation add-on for 25 seats. I also can't find a clear place in the admin settings that shows whether the add-on is actually disabled at the account level or just hidden from users. Can someone confirm what we're actually subscribed to and fix the billing if this was charged in error?

comments.message: We disabled the automation rules on May 28 and renewed on June 1, but the invoice still shows the add-on. In Settings > Workflows the rules are all inactive, so I'm confused why we're billed.

I checked your account and can see the workflow rules are inactive, but billing is based on whether the Advanced Automation add-on is enabled in subscription settings, not whether individual rules are turned off. Right now the add-on was still attached to your renewal order.
I wasn't aware those were separate. Where exactly do I remove it so this doesn't happen again, and can the current charge be reversed?

You can remove it under Admin Center > Billing > Subscription > Add-ons, where it should show as enabled for 25 seats. I've submitted a credit request for the June invoice because the feature was not in use and noted that you intended to cancel before renewal. The credit isn't automatic, but our billing team usually reviews these within 1-2 business days.

subject: Changed company domain and admin user, API callbacks still pointing to old tenant
description: We updated our company domain after a rebrand and switched the main admin account to a new email at the same time. Our outbound API callbacks are still sending the old tenant identifier in payloads, and our middleware is rejecting them. Can someone confirm whether tenant metadata should have changed automatically or if this needs a backend update?

comments.message: We changed the admin email/domain from oldco.com to newco.com and reassigned the account owner. Since then, callback payloads still include tenant_slug=oldco, while the UI shows the new branding. Is the API metadata cached somewhere?
The tenant slug is usually independent from admin email changes, so callbacks may continue using the original identifier even after a rebrand. Have you updated your middleware to accept the old slug?
We can patch that, but our concern is consistency because the account manager told us the tenant name would be updated globally. Right now some endpoints show the new name and callbacks don't.

That makes sense. I reviewed your workspace and this looks like a partial account-change migration: display name updated, but event payload metadata did not. I'm escalating to our backend team to confirm whether the tenant slug can be renamed or whether they need to enable an alias. I'll note the conflicting guidance you received.

subject: Can workflow branches use values returned by an API action?
description: We're trying to automate contract review. Our workflow calls an external API through your HTTP action to run a risk score, and then we want the next branch to route high-risk contracts to Legal. Right now the API call succeeds, but I can't figure out how to use the returned JSON value in later workflow conditions. Is that supported, or do we need to push the result back through your API separately?

comments.message: The HTTP action returns 200 and the response body includes {"risk_score":82}. Ideally we'd like the workflow to evaluate that response directly and assign the record if score > 70.
You can do that by referencing the action response body directly in the next branch condition. Try using something like http_action.response.risk_score > 70 in the workflow builder.
I don't see any response variables exposed in the condition builder after the HTTP action. Only record fields are available.

Got it. In that case, our workflow builder may not expose raw HTTP response values in conditional branches in your version. The supported approach would be to have your external service call back into our API to write risk_score onto a field, then let the automation branch on that field. If you'd like, I can flag your account for the newer action-output feature when it becomes available.

subject: Assinatura programada envia PDF vazio para alguns destinatários
description: Configurei uma assinatura semanal de um relatório de performance e parte dos destinatários recebe o PDF normalmente, mas outros recebem um anexo vazio, com apenas a capa e sem dados. O agendamento é o mesmo e a lista de destinatários está no mesmo envio. Revisei permissões básicas e todos deveriam ter acesso ao conteúdo. Isso parece intermitente e difícil de explicar. Preciso de ajuda para descobrir a causa.

comments.message: Hoje de manhã 3 pessoas receberam o PDF completo e 2 receberam o arquivo vazio. Todos estão no mesmo grupo executivo. Se eu faço download manual do PDF, ele sai correto.

Olá! Se o download manual está correto, então o relatório não parece ter problema. Talvez os usuários com PDF vazio estejam abrindo o arquivo em leitores desatualizados. Peça para testarem outro aplicativo.
Um deles abriu no Adobe, outro no navegador, e os dois continuam vendo só a capa. Além disso, o tamanho do arquivo recebido por eles é muito menor do que o PDF completo.

Entendi. Isso indica que o problema pode estar na geração ou personalização do anexo no envio programado, não no leitor local. Vou escalar para o time que cuida de assinaturas de relatórios para verificar por que o mesmo job está produzindo anexos diferentes entre destinatários. Enquanto isso, recomendo compartilhar o download manual quando o relatório for crítico.

subject: Want to convert a contractor to employee without losing their data
description: One of our contractors just became a full-time employee, and I'd like to update her account so it reflects her new role and internal team. She currently has limited access, and I need to know whether I should edit her existing user, change permissions, or create a brand-new account. I want to preserve her activity history and also explain to her what, if anything, will look different after the change.

comments.message: One of our contractors just became a full-time employee, and I'd like to update her account so it reflects her new role and internal team. She currently has limited access, and I need to know whether I should edit her existing user, change permissions, or create a brand-new account. I want to preserve her activity history and also explain to her what, if anything, will look different after the change.

Use her existing account if the person is the same and she should keep her historical activity. From the user management page, update her team assignment and permission level rather than creating a new profile. That way her comments, ownership history, and reporting attribution remain intact. The visible changes for her will depend on the new role — she may see additional navigation items, settings, or queues after her next login.
Her email address is also changing from her agency domain to our company domain. Does that still count as the same account?

Yes, usually. If your workspace allows email changes, update the existing user record before sending any new invite so the account keeps the same identity and history. If you're on SSO, confirm the new company email is what your identity provider will send, otherwise she could get locked out or end up with a second profile. After the update, it's a good idea to have her sign out and back in so she can confirm the new access level.

subject: Need to teach our team the difference between tags and custom fields
description: I'm cleaning up our ticket workflow and want to train agents on when to use free-form tags versus structured custom fields. Right now people are mixing both, which is breaking reports and automations. Can you give me a practical explanation with examples I can share, and tell me the safest way to migrate without disrupting live queues?

comments.message: I'm cleaning up our ticket workflow and want to train agents on when to use free-form tags versus structured custom fields. Right now people are mixing both, which is breaking reports and automations. Can you give me a practical explanation with examples I can share, and tell me the safest way to migrate without disrupting live queues?
Use tags for anything and custom fields for everything else. If reports are breaking, I'd recommend just deleting the old tags and telling everyone to use fields going forward.
That feels risky, and it doesn't really help me teach the difference. Some tags still drive routing today.

You're right to push back. A better practical rule is: use custom fields for standardized values you want users to select consistently and report on reliably, like region, product line, or severity. Use tags for lightweight behind-the-scenes labeling, temporary workflow markers, or automation hooks where free-form flexibility is acceptable. Since you still have routing tied to existing tags, don't remove them abruptly. I'm escalating this so we can get you a migration sequence for converting report-critical tags into fields while preserving current automations during the transition.

subject: Need to transfer workspace ownership before our admin leaves
description: I'm the current admin for our workspace, and I'm leaving the company on Friday. We need to transfer ownership to our operations manager, but I'm not seeing a clear way to do that without disrupting SSO or our user permissions. Can you explain the right process and help make sure nothing breaks?

comments.message: I'm the current workspace owner and also the person who originally set up billing and SSO. I need to hand everything over to our operations manager before I leave. If I just change my role, will that affect our integrations or invoices?
You can just demote yourself and promote the other user to admin. That should take care of it. Integrations usually stay as they are.
We already tried changing her to admin, but there is still a banner saying only the owner can manage certain settings. I don't want to lock us out of billing or SCIM provisioning.

Thanks for clarifying. Admin and owner are separate in your plan, and ownership transfer has to be done from Settings > Workspace > Ownership if a second eligible admin already exists. Billing contact and technical contacts remain separate unless you update them manually. SCIM and SSO configs normally persist because they are workspace-level, not tied to your personal login, but I don't want to risk giving you incomplete guidance since you're also the original provisioning contact. I'm escalating this to our account management team so they can review your specific tenant and confirm the safest sequence before your departure.

subject: Slack alerts stopped after switching workspaces
description: I moved our team from a Slack sandbox to our production workspace yesterday, and now none of our workflow alerts are posting. I reconnected the app from the Integrations page, but the test message only works for me and not for the channels our automations use. I need help figuring out what broke and what I should reconfigure so the team can start receiving alerts again.

comments.message: We switched Slack workspaces and reinstalled your app. The connection shows as healthy, but workflow alerts are no longer going to #sales-ops or #renewals. Can you tell me what settings I need to update?

When a Slack workspace changes, the app connection can stay valid while old channel mappings become orphaned. Please go to Settings > Integrations > Slack > Notification Routes and reselect the destination channel for each workflow, then run the test from each route rather than the top-level connection test. Also make sure the app has been invited to those channels with /invite @YourApp, because private and newly recreated channels won't receive posts until the bot is added.
That helped for #sales-ops, but #renewals is still failing with "channel_not_found" even though I can see it in Slack.

That usually means the original channel was deleted and recreated during the workspace move, so our stored channel ID no longer matches. Remove the existing #renewals route, create it again from the dropdown, and confirm the app is a member of the channel. If it still fails after recreating the route, send me the workflow name and approximate timestamp and I can check the delivery logs.

subject: Accidentally deleted the wrong user and need role restored
description: While cleaning up inactive accounts, I deleted the wrong person. She was one of our limited user admins for the Support org, and I recreated her account, but now all her permissions and managed users are gone. I need to know if the original account can be restored or if her admin scope has to be rebuilt manually.

comments.message: The user is katie.holm@redcinder.io. I meant to delete a contractor with a similar name. I recreated Katie already, but she came back as a basic user and no longer manages the Support org users.

If the original account was hard-deleted, recreating the user does not restore prior admin scopes automatically. I can check whether the deletion is still within the recoverable window. If it is, we may be able to restore the original account and its user-administration role.
Please check. The deletion happened about two hours ago, and if possible I’d rather restore the original because she had a lot of delegated user-management rules.

Good news: the deletion is still within the recovery window. I’ve restored the original katie.holm@redcinder.io account and reinstated her Support org user-admin scope. The duplicate recreated basic-user account is still present, so please remove that extra account to avoid login confusion.

subject: Charged for deactivated users on our annual renewal
description: I’m our account admin, and when our annual invoice renewed this week we were billed for 148 seats even though I had already deactivated about 30 users in the admin panel last month. I need to understand whether deactivated users still count toward billing and whether this invoice can be corrected.

comments.message: I deactivated 31 users on June 2 under Admin > Team Members because those employees left the company. Our renewal invoice from June 15 still shows 148 paid seats. Can you explain why those inactive accounts were included and adjust the bill if this is a mistake?

Thanks for the details. I checked your subscription and you’re on an annual plan billed to your committed seat count at renewal. Deactivating users frees up licenses for reassignment, but it doesn’t automatically reduce the contracted seat count before renewal unless the seat reduction request is submitted prior to the renewal lock date. In your case, the lock date was May 31, so the June 2 deactivations happened after that cutoff.
I wasn’t aware of any lock date. We definitely don’t need those seats anymore. Is there anything you can do?

I understand the frustration. I can see the users were genuinely deactivated, so I’ve opened a billing review with our accounts team to see whether they can issue a prorated credit as a one-time exception. I’ve also reduced your available seat count in the admin settings going forward so those licenses can’t be reassigned accidentally. Billing will follow up within 1 business day.

subject: Impossible d'activer le formulaire simplifié pour nos clients
description: Je souhaite activer le formulaire de demande simplifié pour nos clients externes, mais après publication ils voient encore l'ancien formulaire complet avec des champs internes. J'essaie de préparer une session de formation demain et je ne veux pas leur montrer une configuration incorrecte.

comments.message: Bonjour, j'ai créé un nouveau type de formulaire avec seulement 5 champs obligatoires et j'ai coché l'option pour le portail client. Pourtant, sur le portail, c'est toujours l'ancien formulaire qui apparaît. Pouvez-vous me dire où je me trompe ?

Bonjour, si l'ancien formulaire apparaît encore, c'est souvent lié au cache du navigateur. Pouvez-vous vider le cache et réessayer ? Le système met parfois un peu de temps à prendre en compte les changements.
J'ai testé en navigation privée et avec un autre collègue. C'est pareil. Je dois aussi expliquer à nos équipes comment publier correctement un formulaire, donc j'aurais besoin des étapes exactes.

D'accord. Vérifiez alors dans Paramètres > Portail > Formulaires quel formulaire est défini comme “par défaut” pour la catégorie concernée. Le fait de publier un formulaire ne remplace pas automatiquement l'ancien. Il faut aussi l'associer à la bonne file ou catégorie de demande. Pour votre formation interne, dites à vos équipes de suivre cet ordre : créer le formulaire, publier, définir comme formulaire par défaut, puis tester avec un compte client. Si après cela l'ancien formulaire reste visible, je peux transmettre au support produit car il pourrait y avoir une règle héritée de votre ancien portail.

subject: Kod 2FA nie dochodzi na telefon
description: Nie mogę zalogować się do aplikacji, bo nie przychodzi mi SMS z kodem weryfikacyjnym. Hasło jest poprawne, ale zatrzymuję się na drugim kroku. Próbowałem kilka razy i nadal nic.

comments.message: Nie dostaję SMS-a z kodem 2FA i nie mogę wejść do konta. To pilne, bo potrzebuję pobrać raporty.
Proszę sprawdzić, czy telefon ma zasięg oraz czy nie jest włączony tryb samolotowy. Czasem pomaga też ponowne uruchomienie urządzenia.
Telefon działa normalnie, inne SMS-y przychodzą. Problem dotyczy tylko Waszych kodów.
W takim razie proszę spróbować ponownie za kilka minut. Jeśli problem będzie się powtarzał, proszę użyć kodów zapasowych, jeśli były wcześniej zapisane.

subject: New hire invite says expired and she can't sign in
description: I invited a new employee last night, but when she clicks the invite link this morning it says the invitation is expired. If she tries the normal sign-in page with the same email, she gets 'account exists but no password set.' I need her onboarded today and I'm not seeing a way to recover the invite on my side.

comments.message: I invited a new employee last night, but when she clicks the invite link this morning it says the invitation is expired. If she tries the normal sign-in page with the same email, she gets 'account exists but no password set.' I need her onboarded today and I'm not seeing a way to recover the invite on my side.

I checked the invite status and it looks like the original email was clicked through a secure email scanner before your employee opened it, which consumed the one-time token. I revoked that pending invite. Please go to Team > Users, delete the pending user entry if it still shows 'Invited,' then re-invite her. After the new invite is sent, ask her to use it within the hour and avoid forwarding the email.
I deleted and re-invited her, but now she gets a different error: 'password setup token invalid.'

I manually generated a fresh activation link from the backend and sent it to the email on file. That one bypasses the expired token state. I also disabled one-time consumption for the next 24 hours on her invite because your domain appears to be link-scanning. She should be able to complete setup now.

subject: Removing a user from the API did not free up the paid seat
description: We removed five contractors through the API at the end of the month, but our billing page still shows the same seat count and the user admin page lists them as deactivated rather than deleted. We expected those licenses to free up automatically. Is there a separate API step to fully remove users from the workspace?

comments.message: Our script currently sends a deactivate call because we need an audit trail, but Finance is asking why those users still count toward seats.

In our system, deactivation and seat release are different states. A deactivated user loses access but may still consume a billable seat depending on your contract and retention settings. To free the seat immediately, you generally need to archive or permanently remove the user after deactivation, either via the DELETE user endpoint or from the admin console, unless your plan includes retained inactive users. Because billing behavior varies by subscription, I’m escalating this to our billing specialists to confirm how your workspace is configured.
Okay, that explains why the API behavior and the invoice view didn't match. Please escalate it — we need to know whether deleting those users will affect our audit history.

I've handed this off to billing with your workspace details and the note about audit retention. In the meantime, I would not bulk delete additional users until they confirm whether your current plan preserves historical records after permanent removal.

subject: Angebot sagt monatliche Zahlung, Rechnung ist jährlich
description: Wir haben laut unterschriebenem Angebot eine monatliche Zahlung vereinbart. Im System wurde aber heute eine Jahresrechnung über den Gesamtbetrag erstellt und direkt belastet. Das weicht klar von unserem Vertrag ab. Bitte stoppen Sie das und korrigieren Sie den Abrechnungszyklus sofort.

comments.message: Wir haben laut unterschriebenem Angebot eine monatliche Zahlung vereinbart. Im System wurde aber heute eine Jahresrechnung über den Gesamtbetrag erstellt und direkt belastet. Das weicht klar von unserem Vertrag ab. Bitte stoppen Sie das und korrigieren Sie den Abrechnungszyklus sofort.

Es tut mir leid für die Verwirrung. Ich sehe in Ihrem Account tatsächlich einen jährlich hinterlegten Abrechnungszyklus, obwohl im CRM-Vermerk monatliche Zahlung erwähnt ist. Ich kann die bereits erzeugte Rechnung nicht selbst stornieren. Ich habe den Fall mit hoher Priorität an Billing Operations eskaliert und um Prüfung einer Rückerstattung sowie Umstellung auf monatliche Abrechnung gebeten. Bis dahin bleibt die Rechnung leider offen im System, aber das Team sollte sich kurzfristig melden.

subject: Google Ads cost data delayed in executive marketing report
description: Our executive marketing report is missing yesterday's spend from Google Ads even though the integration dashboard says the sync completed at 6:10 AM. The campaign rows exist, but cost is blank until two days later, which makes the daily report inaccurate. Please check whether this is expected latency or an integration problem, and escalate if needed because leadership is asking about the discrepancy every morning.

comments.message: Our executive marketing report is missing yesterday's spend from Google Ads even though the integration dashboard says the sync completed at 6:10 AM. The campaign rows exist, but cost is blank until two days later, which makes the daily report inaccurate. Please check whether this is expected latency or an integration problem, and escalate if needed because leadership is asking about the discrepancy every morning.

I reviewed your connector settings and this appears to be expected behavior from the Google Ads API for some cost metrics, especially when accounts use account-level currency conversions and late attribution updates. Your report is ingesting campaign entities on schedule, but cost finalization can lag by 24-48 hours. To avoid the morning discrepancy, I recommend adding a one-day reporting delay or excluding 'today minus one' from the executive dashboard.
Understood, but we'd still like confirmation that this is not a bug specific to our account. Can you have someone verify our sync logs?

Absolutely. I've escalated the case to our integrations team with your account ID and sample campaign IDs so they can confirm the latency matches normal Google Ads API behavior in your tenant. For now, the report logic itself looks healthy, but I'll follow up once they review the connector logs.

subject: API-only users unexpectedly blocked from login after role template change
description: I modified one of our role templates and access policies because we wanted API service accounts to stop using the web app. Unfortunately, several human users tied to that template are now unable to log in at all, and some dashboards disappeared for others who can still access the system. I need help untangling the login restriction from the permission changes.

comments.message: We edited the 'Integration Operator' role template to disable interactive login, assuming it only affected service accounts. Now a few actual employees assigned to that role get blocked at sign-in, and others lost dashboard access after we cloned permissions around.

It sounds like the role template is controlling both login eligibility and module permissions. First, identify which human users are assigned to the Integration Operator role and move them to a role with interactive login enabled. Then compare the cloned role's module permissions to confirm dashboard access wasn't removed when you copied the template.
We have about 18 users on that role because ops used it for convenience. Is there a bulk way to fix both issues?

Yes. From User Management, filter by role and bulk-assign those 18 users to a corrected human role, then update the Integration Operator template so only service accounts retain it. For the dashboard issue, edit the replacement role and re-enable the relevant analytics modules before users sign back in. If you want, export the affected user list first so you have an audit trail of the changes.

subject: Mudei o idioma padrão da conta e agora não consigo salvar perfis
description: Eu alterei o idioma padrão da conta para português e também atualizei algumas preferências globais do workspace. Depois disso, ao editar perfis de usuários, o botão de salvar fica desabilitado ou retorna erro genérico. O problema não existia antes dessa mudança de configuração da conta. Gostaria de entender se a alteração acionou algum bug e se há uma solução temporária.

comments.message: Depois de mudar o idioma padrão da conta para português, editar perfis de usuários ficou impossível: o botão salvar trava ou dá erro genérico.
Olá! Isso provavelmente é relacionado ao navegador traduzindo campos automaticamente. Desative a tradução automática e tente novamente.
Não estamos usando tradução automática. O idioma da própria conta foi alterado no admin e o problema acontece em diferentes máquinas.

Entendi. Nesse caso, peço desculpas pela orientação inicial. Parece mesmo um bug disparado pela alteração de configuração da conta. Vou encaminhar para investigação e incluir que o erro ocorre em múltiplas máquinas e navegadores após a mudança do idioma padrão.

subject: Can log into dashboard, but PAT creation endpoint says session not allowed
description: I'm trying to automate token issuance through the API for our internal tooling. I can access the dashboard with my normal account, but when I call the personal access token creation endpoint it returns 403 with a message about interactive session requirements. We recently enabled passwordless login and disabled basic auth, so I'm unsure whether this is expected.

comments.message: The exact response is "PAT creation not permitted for this session type." I logged in using a magic link, then copied the session cookie into my request.

That is expected. Dashboard access via passwordless login gives you an interactive web session, but the PAT creation endpoint does not accept browser session cookies as API authentication. You need to already authenticate with a supported admin API credential to create or rotate tokens programmatically.
But I don't have any existing admin API credential—that's why I'm trying to create one. Is there another route?

At the moment, no self-bootstrap route exists for your plan. A workspace owner can create the first API credential from the Developers page after signing in interactively, and then your tooling can use that credential for future rotation calls. If you'd like, I can escalate a feature request, but I can't bypass the access model from support.

subject: How do I hand off admin duties while on parental leave?
description: I'm going on parental leave for four months and need someone else to handle user invites, permission changes, and account notices while I'm away. I want to understand the difference between making my colleague an admin versus changing the primary owner/contact. We don't need a permanent transfer if it's avoidable.

comments.message: Is there a temporary delegation option, or do I have to fully transfer ownership? We mainly need coverage for account changes and onboarding new hires while I'm out.

There isn't a temporary vacation delegate feature for ownership in most accounts, but you may not need a full transfer. In many cases, adding a trusted colleague as admin is enough for user invites, permission updates, and day-to-day workspace management. Ownership or primary account contact changes are usually only needed for sensitive account-level actions like subscription changes, renewal communications, or certain billing settings.
That helps. If I leave myself as owner but change the primary contact, will they get admin alerts too?

They'll receive the account-level communications tied to that contact field, but operational/admin alerts depend on the specific notification type and the user's in-product role. A practical setup is: keep you as owner, grant your colleague admin rights, update the primary account contact if they should receive renewal/account notices, and test one admin workflow before you leave. If your plan includes settings restricted only to the owner, reply with your plan tier and I can flag whether a temporary ownership change should be considered.

subject: Need help transferring ownership of dashboards after admin left
description: Our analytics admin left the company last week. I deactivated her account, and now six shared dashboards show her as the owner. Editors can view them but cannot update schedules or permissions. I need those dashboards reassigned to another admin, but I don't see a bulk transfer option anywhere.

comments.message: We have a replacement admin already active in the workspace. Ideally we want all assets owned by Priya Raman moved to Daniel Cho.

Dashboard ownership can be changed individually if you open each dashboard, go to Share, and select a new owner. If that option is unavailable because the original owner is deactivated, we need to escalate the transfer to our content operations team. Please confirm the old and new admin email addresses and approximately how many dashboards are affected.
Old owner is priya.raman@lumenbi.com, new owner is daniel.cho@lumenbi.com, and there are 6 dashboards plus 2 scheduled reports.

Thanks — I've escalated the ownership transfer request for Priya's 6 dashboards and 2 scheduled reports to be reassigned to Daniel. I also noted that schedule and permission edits are blocked because the original owner is deactivated. That team will complete the reassignment from the backend and follow up once finished.

subject: MFA reset not working for former admin account
description: I’m the new IT admin for our workspace. The previous admin left the company, and his account still appears to be the only one with full admin rights. I can log in with my own user, but I can’t access the admin console, and when I try to reset MFA on the old admin account, I get an “insufficient permissions” error. We’re effectively locked out of admin functions.

comments.message: I need to get into admin settings to add two new managers and rotate API credentials, but the only super admin was our former operations lead. I can access the main app, just not the admin console.
You should be able to have the old admin log in and transfer permissions from Settings > Team.
He no longer works here, and we do not have access to his phone or email. That’s why I’m contacting support.

Understood. Since this is an account access and ownership issue, I’m escalating it to our Account Recovery team for secure verification of company ownership. They’ll likely request proof such as your company domain email, billing contact details, and possibly a recent invoice before they can restore admin access.

subject: Cannot demote user because system says they are the last admin
description: I’m trying to remove admin rights from one of our team members who changed roles. The problem is the admin page says she is the last admin, which is not true because I’m also an admin and I’m currently logged in. Because of that, I can’t change her permissions or deactivate her account.

comments.message: The user is Melissa Grant. We need to change her from Admin to Viewer. The error says 'You cannot modify the final administrator on this account.' But my profile clearly shows Org Admin.
That message appears when the user is the last admin, so you would need to promote someone else first.
That doesn’t make sense because I already am someone else. There are at least two admins visible on the list.

You’re right, that sounds inconsistent. I checked our known issues list and there is a bug affecting role changes when one admin was granted access through a legacy custom role. Please try editing your own account and confirming the Org Admin permission is direct rather than inherited. If it’s inherited, assign Org Admin directly to another user first. If you’d like, I can escalate this with the affected user IDs so engineering can correct the admin count on the backend.

subject: Merged two departments, changed account structure, and now charges are duplicated
description: We consolidated our Support and Success departments into one workspace and updated our ticket-routing automation so enterprise accounts get assigned to a new shared queue. After the account merge, I’m seeing duplicate subscription charges for both legacy departments, and the automation is still creating tasks under the old Support account owner. I need confirmation of which account is the billable parent now, whether the duplicate invoice will be voided, and how to make the automation use the new merged ownership model.

comments.message: There are two invoices this month—one for the old Support workspace and one for the merged workspace. We were told the merge would retire the old billing account.

Account merges don’t automatically cancel prior subscriptions unless the legacy workspace is explicitly closed. I can confirm the merged workspace is active, but the old Support workspace remains billable because it still has automation jobs running under it.
That’s likely because the routing workflow still references the old owner and queue. Can you shut off billing on the old workspace now?

I can’t terminate a subscription without authorization from the current billing admin listed on that legacy workspace, and that’s still your former Support director. I’ve temporarily paused the active automation jobs there to prevent further task creation, and I’ve escalated the duplicate charge review plus ownership transfer request to our billing team. For the new workflow, replace the destination workspace ID in the enterprise routing step with the merged workspace ID; right now it still posts to the retired environment.

subject: SCIM-Provisionierung legt deaktivierte Nutzer wieder aktiv an
description: Ich habe in unserer Benutzerkonfiguration festgelegt, dass deaktivierte Mitarbeiter aus Okta auch in Ihrem System deaktiviert bleiben. Seit letzter Woche werden einige dieser Konten bei einer erneuten Gruppenzuweisung aber wieder als aktiv angelegt. Das ist aus Compliance-Sicht heikel. Bitte prüfen Sie das sofort oder leiten Sie es an die richtige Stelle weiter.

comments.message: Wir haben nichts Grundsätzliches geändert, nur eine neue Standardgruppe für externe Berater konfiguriert. Seitdem passiert es vereinzelt.

Ich sehe in Ihren Einstellungen, dass bei neuer Gruppenzuweisung automatisch die Basisrolle "Mitarbeiter" gesetzt wird. Es könnte sein, dass dadurch ein Reaktivierungsereignis ausgelöst wird, wenn der SCIM-Connector den Benutzer erneut sieht. Bitte deaktivieren Sie testweise die automatische Gruppenzuweisung.
Das ist als Sofortmaßnahme möglich, aber wir brauchen eine belastbare Ursache und am besten eine Bestätigung, dass das kein normales Verhalten ist.

Verstanden. Das geschilderte Verhalten sollte bei deaktivierten Konten nicht auftreten. Ich habe den Vorgang mit hoher Priorität an unser Identity-Team eskaliert und Ihre neue Gruppenregel als möglichen Auslöser dokumentiert. Bis zur Analyse empfehle ich, die automatische Gruppenzuweisung deaktiviert zu lassen und Reaktivierungen täglich zu prüfen. Ich update Sie, sobald das Team die Logs ausgewertet hat.

subject: Training materials for teaching my team to use automations
description: I manage a support team and we just upgraded to a plan that includes workflow automations. I understand the basic idea, but I need a practical way to teach my agents when to use triggers versus scheduled actions versus macros. Do you have any beginner-friendly guidance or examples I can reuse for training?

comments.message: My agents keep asking whether macros and automations do the same thing, and I don’t want to explain it badly.

They’re related, but not the same. A macro is a manual shortcut an agent runs on demand, like applying a tag and sending a templated reply. A trigger runs automatically as soon as a ticket meets conditions, and a scheduled action runs later based on time rules. For training, I usually suggest one simple example of each: macro for common password reset replies, trigger for routing urgent tickets, and scheduled action for following up on unresolved requests after 48 hours.
Do you have a ready-made deck or checklist?
We have help center articles.

subject: Billing contact updated, tax exemption not carrying over
description: I took over as account owner from our previous operations lead and updated all the billing details, including our company address and tax ID. Since then, our newest invoice no longer reflects our tax-exempt status and includes sales tax. Can you make sure the account ownership change didn't wipe out our billing exemption and reissue the invoice?

comments.message: I took over as account owner from our previous operations lead and updated all the billing details, including our company address and tax ID. Since then, our newest invoice no longer reflects our tax-exempt status and includes sales tax. Can you make sure the account ownership change didn't wipe out our billing exemption and reissue the invoice?

When account details are changed, tax settings sometimes need to be revalidated. I can see your old exemption certificate expired last month, so the system charged tax on the latest invoice. If you upload the renewed certificate in Billing Settings, future invoices should be fine.
The certificate doesn't expire until December, and we already uploaded it last quarter. The issue started only after I changed the account owner and address.

Thanks for clarifying. I may be looking at the wrong document version from your file list. I've opened an internal review with our tax and billing team to verify whether the ownership change detached the exemption profile. Once confirmed, they can reissue the invoice if tax was applied incorrectly.

subject: Duplicate contacts created after custom field change
description: I updated one of our contact schemas yesterday and since then our nightly import has been creating duplicate contacts instead of updating existing ones. We recently changed the external ID mapping from "customer_code" to a new custom field called "ERP Account ID" in the admin settings. Now records that used to merge correctly are showing up as brand new contacts, and some lifecycle data is split across duplicates. I need to understand whether this is a data issue or if we misconfigured the matching rules, and ideally how to clean this up without deleting recent activity.

comments.message: We changed the identifier field in Settings > Data Model yesterday afternoon. Last night's CSV import created about 1,800 duplicate contacts. The import file includes the ERP Account ID column and those values are populated. Can you tell me what setting we broke?

It sounds like the import is no longer matching on the same unique key. When you switch the mapped external ID field, our importer treats that as a new record identity unless the new field is both populated on existing records and marked as the primary deduplication key before the import runs. Please check whether "ERP Account ID" is configured as unique in Admin > Product Configuration > Record Matching and whether your existing contacts had that field backfilled prior to the import.

I just checked and the field was mapped in the import template, but it was not enabled under Record Matching. Existing contacts also only had that value on about half the records because the sync to backfill them was still running.

That would explain the duplicates. I've outlined the safest recovery path: 1) enable "ERP Account ID" as the primary matching key, 2) finish backfilling that field on all existing contacts, 3) rerun the import in update mode, and 4) use the merge tool on duplicates created in the last import batch so activity is retained. Because this touches a large dataset, I've escalated the merge portion to our data operations team and attached your import job ID so they can help avoid accidental data loss.

subject: Import keeps failing on the date column
description: I’m trying to import a CSV of customer renewals and the upload keeps failing with a generic validation error. After a few tries I noticed it seems to stop on the 'renewal_date' field. The file has dates like 03/07/24 and 11/12/24. I’m not sure what date format your importer expects or how I can test this before re-uploading the whole file again.

comments.message: I’m trying to import a CSV of customer renewals and the upload keeps failing with a generic validation error. After a few tries I noticed it seems to stop on the 'renewal_date' field. The file has dates like 03/07/24 and 11/12/24. I’m not sure what date format your importer expects or how I can test this before re-uploading the whole file again.
The importer can be picky about dates. Usually you just need to use Excel to reformat the column, then upload again. Try changing the cells to a date format and that should fix it.
I already did that in Excel, and the values still export as the same text in the CSV. I need to know the exact accepted format because the error message isn’t specific.

Understood. The accepted import format is ISO-style YYYY-MM-DD, for example 2024-07-03. Two-digit years and ambiguous slash formats often fail because the parser can’t tell whether 03/07/24 means March 7 or July 3. A quick way to test is to create a 2-row CSV with just required columns plus renewal_date in YYYY-MM-DD format and import that first. If that succeeds, convert the full file the same way. If you want, send a sample of 3-5 rows with headers and I can point out any other columns that may still block the import.

subject: Duplicate invoice after SCIM provisioning and Slack app stopped posting
description: We turned on SCIM provisioning from Okta this week, and right after that we got billed for 18 new seats that should have replaced existing users, not added to them. At the same time, the Slack integration stopped posting notifications, and two managers say they can’t log in because their accounts now appear twice with different statuses. I need to fix the access mess and understand why provisioning created a billing spike.

comments.message: We turned on SCIM provisioning from Okta this week, and right after that we got billed for 18 new seats that should have replaced existing users, not added to them. At the same time, the Slack integration stopped posting notifications, and two managers say they can’t log in because their accounts now appear twice with different statuses. I need to fix the access mess and understand why provisioning created a billing spike.

SCIM can create duplicate users if the identity matching attribute in Okta doesn’t line up with the existing login email in our system. That would explain both the extra billed seats and the managers seeing duplicate account states. I also checked the Slack app and it was installed by one of the newly duplicated admin identities, which is why posting stopped when that record was disabled. I’ve merged the two manager records, excluded the 18 SCIM-created duplicates from billing pending review, and transferred the Slack integration ownership to your primary admin. New Slack notifications should resume shortly, but I’m escalating the full SCIM reconciliation so the remaining duplicates can be cleaned up safely.
Slack is posting again, but several of the duplicate users still cannot sign in because Okta sends them to the disabled records.

Understood. I’ve added that detail to the escalation and attached the affected SSO identifiers. Until the identity remap is completed, those users may continue landing on disabled profiles. Our provisioning specialists will need to correct the external IDs on the backend; I’m leaving the billing exclusion in place so you won’t be charged for those duplicates while that work is ongoing.
