subject: Can’t map external manager IDs to users during API import
description: We're importing employees from our HRIS and trying to populate each user's manager in your platform so approvals route correctly. Creating the users works, but when we send the manager_external_id in the same API request, it gets ignored for many records. If we try again later for the same users, some update and some don't. We need a reliable way to establish reporting lines during provisioning.

comments.message: A pattern we noticed is that failures happen when both the employee and the manager are created in the same batch. The manager account often exists by the end of the run, just not at the exact moment the employee is processed.

That behavior is expected if the referenced manager user does not exist yet at write time. Our import API does not queue unresolved manager_external_id references for later reconciliation. The safest pattern is a two-pass sync: first create or update all users, then run a second pass to set manager relationships once the target users can be resolved by external_id. You can verify each mapping with GET /users?external_id= before applying the manager field.
That explains the inconsistency. We'll split the job into a create pass and a relationship pass instead of trying to do everything in one request.

Exactly. That should make the hierarchy updates deterministic and easier to retry when a manager record is missing. If you'd like, I can share a sample sequence for bulk imports with manager assignment.

subject: Merged accounts still showing duplicate companies in reports
description: I merged several duplicate company records on Friday, and the company list now looks correct in the UI, but our pipeline report is still showing the old duplicates as separate rows. The open opportunities seem attached to the right surviving account, so this looks more like stale reporting data than a bad merge. Can you help figure out whether the merge didn't fully apply to reporting tables?

comments.message: I merged several duplicate company records on Friday, and the company list now looks correct in the UI, but our pipeline report is still showing the old duplicates as separate rows. The open opportunities seem attached to the right surviving account, so this looks more like stale reporting data than a bad merge.

Thanks for reporting this. I looked at one of the merged accounts and confirmed the transactional data points to the surviving record, but the reporting dataset still contains the pre-merge company IDs. That suggests the nightly sync did not backfill merged IDs correctly. I manually triggered a rebuild of your reporting model, which should remove the duplicate rows within the next 30 minutes. I've also attached this to an existing bug around post-merge report caches.
The duplicate rows are gone now, but one of the merged companies is missing its ARR total in the report.

I can see that too. The rebuild fixed the duplicate IDs, but the ARR aggregate did not recalculate for one merged record. I've escalated this to our data platform team with the affected company ID and merge timestamp. For now, the account page has the correct opportunity values, but the report metric is still inaccurate until they patch the rollup job.

subject: Custom automation token not populating for API-submitted forms
description: We have a workflow that sends a Slack message using a template token for "submitted by email." It works when employees submit the request form in the portal, but when the same form data is sent through the API from our intranet, the Slack message shows the token as blank and the workflow falls into the fallback branch. We're trying to understand whether API submissions populate a different actor context.

comments.message: The API request includes requester_email and requester_name. The record is created correctly, but the workflow condition and message template that reference 'submitted by' don't seem to have a value.

Most likely the token you're using refers to the interactive submitter session, not the stored requester fields on the record. For API-created records there may be no portal submitter context, so automation tokens like 'submitted by email' can be empty even when requester_email is set.
So should we switch both the condition and the Slack template to the requester fields instead?

Yes. Update the workflow to branch on the record's requester_email field and use the requester.email token in the notification template. That way the automation works consistently for both portal submissions and API-created records.

subject: Changed from annual to monthly and added seats, charged for both
description: I switched our subscription from annual billing to monthly because our headcount is changing, and at the same time I added three temporary users for a client rollout. My card was charged the annual renewal amount plus a monthly prorated amount for the new seats. I need to know which plan is actually active and whether the annual charge can be corrected.

comments.message: I switched our subscription from annual billing to monthly because our headcount is changing, and at the same time I added three temporary users for a client rollout. My card was charged the annual renewal amount plus a monthly prorated amount for the new seats. I need to know which plan is actually active and whether the annual charge can be corrected.

I checked the subscription status and it currently shows as monthly with 27 active seats, including the three users you added. The annual renewal captured before the billing frequency change finished processing, which is why you see both charges. I’m not able to reverse that directly from frontline support, so I’ve escalated this to our billing specialists to either refund the annual renewal or convert it into account credit. They usually respond within 1 business day.

subject: Bulk role update left users without dashboard access
description: I used the bulk user administration tool to move 18 people from Viewer to Analyst, but about half of them now land on a blank page after login instead of the analytics dashboard. Their accounts show the new role correctly, so I'm not sure if permissions actually propagated. Can you help figure out what broke?

comments.message: I used the bulk user administration tool to move 18 people from Viewer to Analyst, but about half of them now land on a blank page after login instead of the analytics dashboard. Their accounts show the new role correctly, so I'm not sure if permissions actually propagated. Can you help figure out what broke?

Yes — this can happen when the role value updates but the workspace-level product entitlement does not. For the affected users, open Admin > Users and compare both Role and Product Access against a working Analyst. If Product Access still shows "Core Only," switch it to include Analytics, save, and then have the user log out and back in. If there are many affected users, export the user list and filter for accounts where role is Analyst but analytics_access is false.
That seems to be it. 7 of them had Analyst role but no Analytics product access. I fixed those manually, but the other 2 still see a blank page.

For the remaining 2, I suspect their default landing page was saved while they lacked access. Please edit each profile, temporarily change the home page to "Overview," save, then switch it back to "Analytics Dashboard" after they can log in. If that doesn't correct it, reply with their user IDs and I'll escalate it as a provisioning sync issue.

subject: Relatório de logins por usuário está com fuso horário errado
description: Eu preciso de um relatório de logins por usuário para auditoria e notei que os horários estão 3 horas adiantados. Como admin, revisei as configurações dos usuários e do workspace, tudo está em horário de Brasília. Mesmo assim, a coluna de último acesso no relatório exportado vem em UTC. Isso afeta principalmente os usuários recém-criados este mês.

comments.message: Na interface, algumas telas mostram o horário certo, mas no relatório CSV o último acesso aparece diferente. Para auditoria isso é um problema sério.
Olá! Se o CSV está em UTC, isso é esperado porque exportações sempre usam o horário do navegador do usuário que gera o arquivo.
Mas não faz sentido, porque testei em dois navegadores diferentes e os dois vieram em UTC. Além disso, na tela aparece BRT.

Entendi. Talvez seja necessário alterar individualmente o fuso horário de cada usuário em Administração para que o relatório respeite. Recomendo revisar os perfis dos usuários recém-criados e ajustar manualmente.

subject: Necesito fusionar cuentas cliente sin perder historial
description: Tenemos dos cuentas del mismo cliente creadas por error: una con el dominio antiguo y otra con el nuevo. Queremos fusionarlas porque los usuarios y oportunidades están repartidos entre ambas, pero no queremos perder el historial de actividad ni los permisos por equipo. No encuentro una opción clara para combinar cuentas padre con sus registros relacionados.

comments.message: Tenemos dos cuentas del mismo cliente creadas por error: una con el dominio antiguo y otra con el nuevo. Queremos fusionarlas porque los usuarios y oportunidades están repartidos entre ambas, pero no queremos perder el historial de actividad ni los permisos por equipo. No encuentro una opción clara para combinar cuentas padre con sus registros relacionados.

Sí, le ayudo. Desde la vista de cuentas puede usar la opción "Fusionar" si ambas cuentas están en el mismo workspace. El sistema conserva la cuenta principal, mueve el historial de actividades, notas y oportunidades, y luego desactiva la secundaria. Los permisos por equipo no siempre se heredan automáticamente, así que después de la fusión conviene revisar la pestaña de acceso.
No veo el botón "Fusionar" en ninguna de las dos cuentas. Soy administrador del workspace.

Gracias. En algunos planes esa función solo aparece si está habilitado el módulo de deduplicación avanzada. Revise en Configuración > Gestión de datos > Detección de duplicados. Si no aparece, puedo escalar una solicitud para que soporte técnico haga la fusión asistida desde backend. Envíeme los IDs de ambas cuentas y cuál debe quedar como principal.

subject: Escalated cases report not picking up workflow-tagged tickets
description: We created a workflow to auto-tag urgent customer issues as escalated when certain keywords and SLA conditions are met. The operational report that tracks escalated cases is still missing many of those tickets unless someone manually checks the escalation box. I need to know whether the report is pointed at the wrong field or whether the automation isn't setting data the same way the manual action does.

comments.message: The report for escalated cases is undercounting tickets that were auto-escalated by workflow. If an agent manually flags a ticket, it appears in the report. If the workflow adds the 'escalated' tag, it often doesn't.

I looked at your report and it's filtered on the boolean field 'Escalated = true,' not on the presence of the 'escalated' tag. Your workflow currently only adds the tag and reassigns the ticket to the Escalations queue, so the report is behaving consistently with its setup.
Got it. Can the workflow set the boolean field too, or is that restricted?

It can, provided the field is editable in automations. I checked and in your account that field is locked because it also triggers downstream SLA logic. I've escalated this to our workflow team to confirm whether we can safely allow automation updates there. In the meantime, you can build a temporary report based on the tag plus queue assignment to better approximate escalated volume.

subject: Legal hold export request not moving
description: We're under a legal hold and submitted a data export request through our customer success manager four business days ago. We were told support could track the status, but nobody has updated us and the deadline is court-driven. I need help getting this in front of the correct internal team today.

comments.message: Can someone please chase this? We don't need general guidance — we need movement on the actual export request.

I checked but I couldn't find the request in the standard support view, so it may have been submitted outside the support system. You may want to follow up directly with your customer success manager.
That defeats the purpose of opening this ticket. Can you escalate internally instead of sending us in circles?

I've reached out to our Data Services queue and created an internal escalation referencing your company name, legal hold context, and deadline. I still don't have the original request ID, so there may be some delay while they match it up, but this should at least get the request in front of the operational team.

subject: Erreur 500 lors de la suppression d’un espace de travail
description: Je dois supprimer un ancien espace de travail de test avant notre revue mensuelle, mais l’application renvoie une erreur 500 à chaque tentative. Le problème survient quel que soit l’utilisateur administrateur. Comme je ne veux pas manipuler la base ou laisser des données de test visibles, merci de prendre en charge rapidement.

comments.message: Bonjour, quand je clique sur “Supprimer l’espace”, la page charge puis affiche une erreur 500. Nous avons essayé avec deux admins sur le workspace “Sandbox Avril”.
Bonjour, une erreur 500 indique généralement un incident serveur. Pouvez-vous réessayer plus tard ? Si cela persiste, nous verrons ensuite.
Nous réessayons depuis ce matin et cela bloque toujours. J’ai besoin d’une vraie prise en charge, pas seulement d’attendre.

Je comprends. Je vais faire remonter le cas à notre équipe plateforme avec le nom exact du workspace et signaler l’impact sur votre revue mensuelle. Pour accélérer l’analyse, merci d’ajouter si possible l’heure approximative de votre dernière tentative et une capture d’écran de l’erreur.

subject: Invited users getting 'no account found' error
description: I invited six contractors to our workspace, but when they click the invite link and create passwords, they end up on a screen saying no account found. Two of them tried multiple browsers and got the same result. We have a training session today, so if this is a provisioning problem please escalate it.

comments.message: The invite emails were delivered, and the links aren’t expired. They can set a password, but after that the app says there’s no account associated with their login.
That usually happens when users sign up from the public registration page instead of the invite. Can you ask them to use the correct link from the email?
They are using the link from the email. I watched one of them do it over screen share. It looks like the invite acceptance completes and then fails to attach them to our workspace.

Thanks for confirming. I checked and I’m seeing several invite records in a pending-but-unbound state, which suggests a backend provisioning issue rather than user error. I’ve escalated this to our account access team with the affected email addresses so they can repair the memberships. As a temporary workaround, don’t resend the same invites yet, since duplicates can make reconciliation harder.

subject: Password reset works but user still cannot access tenant
description: A user from our finance team can reset her password successfully and log in with the new password, but after that she gets a message saying her account isn't assigned to any workspace. She definitely belongs to our tenant, and I can see her in the user list as active. We need help figuring out whether this is an access mapping problem or something broken in your authentication flow.

comments.message: She was originally invited with her Gmail address, then later we changed her login to her company address. The issue started after she used the password reset on the company address.

That usually means the password reset created a fresh standalone identity instead of linking back to the existing tenant membership. Can you confirm whether you now see two user entries for her under different email addresses?
Yes, I see the old Gmail user with all the history and a newer company-email user with no role assigned.

Thanks, that confirms it. The reset flow appears to have exposed a duplicate-identity bug rather than a simple permissions issue. For immediate access, assign the correct role to the company-email user if you need her working today, but be aware her historical ownership may remain on the Gmail identity until records are merged. I've escalated this to our identity team to merge the accounts properly and investigate why the reset path created a new workspace membership instead of attaching to the existing one.

subject: SSO login failures after domain cutover
description: I'm the IT admin for Northline Health. Since we switched our email domain yesterday, about 40 users can't log in through SSO and they're getting a generic "authentication failed" error. I already updated the IdP metadata on our side, but the problem is still happening for a subset of users. We have onboarding sessions this afternoon, so I need to know whether this is something your team can fix or if I should roll back the change.

comments.message: Several users are blocked from accessing the platform after our domain change. The error only affects some accounts, not all, and I can't see a clear pattern.

Thanks for the details. Domain changes can affect the SSO tenant mapping on our side, especially when only a subset of users is impacted. I checked your workspace and I can see failed assertions tied to the old domain for some accounts, but I can't update that mapping directly from frontline support.
Can you confirm this needs backend intervention? I don't want to waste time troubleshooting the wrong system.

Yes — this needs to be escalated to our Identity team. I've opened a priority escalation with your tenant ID, the affected domain, and examples of failed users so they can update the mapping and review the assertion logs. In the meantime, if you need urgent access for today's sessions, I can also ask them to enable temporary password login for specific users if you send the list.

subject: Webhook de atualização não dispara após alterar o status do ticket
description: Estou configurando a integração da nossa conta com o seu API e preciso receber um webhook sempre que um ticket muda para "Resolvido". Criei o endpoint, gerei o token e ativei o evento no painel, mas nada chega ao nosso lado. Se eu consulto o ticket manualmente pelo API, o status já foi alterado. Pode verificar se a configuração está errada ou se existe alguma limitação?

comments.message: Estou configurando a integração da nossa conta com o seu API e preciso receber um webhook sempre que um ticket muda para "Resolvido". Criei o endpoint, gerei o token e ativei o evento no painel, mas nada chega ao nosso lado. Se eu consulto o ticket manualmente pelo API, o status já foi alterado. Pode verificar se a configuração está errada ou se existe alguma limitação?

Claro. Verifiquei um ponto comum nesse cenário: no módulo de automações, o webhook só é enviado se o evento estiver publicado no ambiente ativo e se o filtro incluir transições feitas por agentes e por regras. Na sua conta, o webhook estava configurado apenas para mudanças manuais. Ajustei a configuração para incluir mudanças feitas por automação também. Além disso, o seu endpoint estava respondendo com 302, e nosso sistema exige resposta 2xx para considerar a entrega válida.
Fiz um novo teste mudando um ticket para Resolvido por uma regra e agora recebemos a chamada. Também corrigi o redirect no nosso endpoint. Obrigado.

Perfeito. Então o problema era uma combinação da configuração do gatilho no produto com a resposta do endpoint no API. Se quiser, posso enviar um exemplo de payload e a lista completa de eventos disponíveis para você validar os próximos webhooks.

subject: Dashboard export dropped rows after deduplication rule update
description: We changed a deduplication rule on our accounts object last week, and since then the dashboard export for "Renewals at Risk" is missing around 150 rows compared with the live view. I already cleared filters, tested as another admin, and downloaded both CSV and XLSX with the same result. It looks like records with merged child accounts are showing in the app but not making it into the export file. Is there a known issue, and is there any safe way to recover the missing data for this quarter's report?

comments.message: We changed a deduplication rule on our accounts object last week, and since then the dashboard export for "Renewals at Risk" is missing around 150 rows compared with the live view. I already cleared filters, tested as another admin, and downloaded both CSV and XLSX with the same result. It looks like records with merged child accounts are showing in the app but not making it into the export file. Is there a known issue, and is there any safe way to recover the missing data for this quarter's report?

I wasn’t able to reproduce a platform-wide issue, but your description suggests the export job may still be reading pre-merge account references while the dashboard UI resolves the merged record IDs dynamically. Could you send one or two example account IDs that appear on-screen but not in the file?
Examples are ACC-10492 and ACC-11807. Both are visible in the widget totals and detail table, but neither appears in the exported CSV.

I checked those IDs and both have merge histories with child records rolled into a surviving parent. The export service is likely excluding child-linked rows after deduplication. As an immediate workaround, use a custom list filtered on current parent account ID and export from the Accounts page instead of the dashboard. I’m escalating this to our reporting team as a suspected bug because the dashboard and export counts should match.

subject: Importação cria contatos duplicados apesar da regra de deduplicação
description: Estou importando uma base CSV e o sistema está criando contatos duplicados mesmo com a deduplicação por e-mail ativada. Já testei com um arquivo pequeno e acontece igual. Preciso descobrir se o problema é no mapeamento da importação.

comments.message: Os e-mails no CSV parecem iguais aos que já existem, mas alguns vieram com letras maiúsculas e espaços no final.

Oi! A deduplicação normalmente ignora maiúsculas, mas espaços extras podem atrapalhar dependendo de como o arquivo foi salvo. Você chegou a mapear a coluna de e-mail para o campo principal de e-mail ou criou um campo personalizado?
Mapeei para um campo personalizado que usamos no legado, não para o e-mail principal.

Aí está o motivo. A regra de deduplicação por padrão só compara o campo principal de e-mail do contato. Reimporte o arquivo mapeando a coluna para o campo padrão de e-mail e, antes disso, remova espaços no final da célula. Se quiser manter o campo legado, você pode mapear os dois, mas o principal precisa estar preenchido para evitar duplicados.

subject: HubSpot-Felder werden nach dem Sync überschrieben
description: Ich nutze die HubSpot-Integration, und seit letzter Woche werden unsere benutzerdefinierten Felder in HubSpot mit alten Werten aus Ihrer Plattform überschrieben. Besonders problematisch ist das Feld für Vertragsstatus. Ich brauche eine Möglichkeit, die Synchronisierungsrichtung pro Feld festzulegen oder den betroffenen Sync rückgängig zu machen.

comments.message: Wir haben gemerkt, dass nach jedem Sync der Vertragsstatus in HubSpot wieder auf 'Angebot' springt, obwohl der Deal dort bereits als 'gewonnen' markiert wurde. Das passiert nur bei einem bestimmten Custom Field.

Verstanden. Bei der HubSpot-Integration lässt sich die Synchronisierungsrichtung tatsächlich pro Feld steuern, aber nur, wenn das Feld als benutzerdefiniertes Mapping und nicht als Standard-Mapping angelegt wurde. Können Sie unter Einstellungen > Integrationen > HubSpot > Feldzuordnung prüfen, ob 'Vertragsstatus' als Standardfeld markiert ist?
Ja, es ist als Standard-Mapping drin. Ich kann die Richtung dort nicht ändern.

Dann ist das Verhalten erwartbar: Standard-Mappings in dieser Integration sind bidirektional mit Priorität auf dem zuletzt geänderten Datensatz. Wenn Ihre Plattform den Datensatz später aktualisiert als HubSpot, überschreibt sie den Wert wieder. Einen automatischen Rollback gibt es leider nicht. Ich habe den Fall an unser Integrations-Team eskaliert, damit geprüft wird, ob wir das Mapping in ein benutzerdefiniertes Feld umwandeln können oder den Sync für dieses Feld serverseitig ausschließen. Bis dahin würde ich den Feld-Sync für Deals pausieren, um weitere Überschreibungen zu vermeiden.

subject: Admin-Rechte versehentlich entzogen und kein weiterer Admin verfügbar
description: Ich habe bei einer Bereinigungsaktion in der Benutzerverwaltung versehentlich meine eigenen Admin-Rechte entfernt. Ich kann mich noch anmelden, sehe aber die Admin-Einstellungen nicht mehr. Soweit ich weiß, bin ich der letzte Volladmin in unserem Konto. Wie kommen wir da wieder raus?

comments.message: Wir sind ein kleines Team mit nur drei Nutzern. Die beiden anderen haben normale Agentenrollen und können keine Benutzerrechte ändern.

Wenn kein anderer Admin vorhanden ist, können Sie die Rolle leider nicht selbst wiederherstellen. In diesem Fall benötigen wir aus Sicherheitsgründen eine manuelle Prüfung des Kontoinhabers oder eines verifizierten Ansprechpartners. Bitte antworten Sie mit Ihrer Account-Subdomain, der betroffenen Benutzer-E-Mail und möglichst einer Rechnung oder Vertragsreferenz.
Die Daten kann ich senden. Können Sie ungefähr sagen, wie lange so eine Wiederherstellung dauert? Wir müssen heute noch einen neuen Mitarbeiter anlegen.

Sobald die Verifizierung vollständig ist, wird so etwas meist noch am selben Werktag bearbeitet, ich kann es aber nicht garantieren. Ich markiere das Ticket als dringend und leite es an das Team für Admin-Zugriffsherstellung weiter. Bis zur Bestätigung kann ich selbst keine Rechte direkt zurücksetzen.

subject: Troca do identificador da conta apagou filtros salvos na busca
description: Eu alterei o identificador da conta no sábado para alinhar com nosso novo nome comercial. Hoje percebemos que vários filtros salvos na busca avançada desapareceram para alguns gestores, enquanto para outros aparecem mas ao abrir geram erro. Como isso começou logo após a alteração da conta, queria confirmar se existe bug conhecido e se vocês conseguem restaurar os filtros.

comments.message: Após trocar o identificador da conta, filtros salvos da busca sumiram para alguns gestores e para outros dão erro ao abrir.
Olá! Vamos verificar. Esses filtros eram compartilhados ou privados? E o problema afeta somente gestores ou também usuários comuns?
A maioria era compartilhada. Até agora notamos só em gestores, principalmente os que criaram os filtros antes da mudança do identificador.

Obrigado, isso aponta para um cenário bem específico. Há indícios de um bug em referências internas de filtros compartilhados quando o identificador da conta é alterado. Infelizmente não consigo restaurar manualmente do suporte de primeiro nível. Já escalei para a equipe responsável pela busca e pedi análise de possível recuperação dos filtros. Assim que tiver retorno, atualizo este chamado.

subject: Export des logs incomplet pour un audit sécurité
description: Je tente d’exporter les logs d’activité pour notre audit ISO et l’export s’arrête toujours après environ 12 000 lignes. J’ai essayé sur deux navigateurs et avec deux plages de dates différentes. L’auditeur attend les fichiers demain, donc j’ai besoin d’aide ou d’une extraction côté support.

comments.message: Bonjour, l’export CSV des journaux d’audit est tronqué et impossible à finaliser. Nous avons besoin des événements complets sur les 90 derniers jours pour un audit externe.
Bonjour, avez-vous essayé de vider le cache du navigateur et de relancer l’export en navigation privée ? Cela règle souvent ce type de souci.
Oui, comme indiqué, j’ai déjà testé sur deux navigateurs différents. Le problème semble lié au volume, pas au cache.

Merci pour la précision. Dans ce cas, je vais transmettre votre demande à notre équipe technique pour vérifier s’il existe une limite connue sur les exports volumineux et voir s’ils peuvent générer le fichier côté serveur. Je ne peux pas vous donner de délai exact pour l’instant, mais le ticket est escaladé.

subject: Jira tickets are syncing to the wrong project after our admin changed defaults
description: Our support escalation workflow creates Jira issues from high-priority cases. Since our Jira admin changed the default project last night, new issues from your platform have been landing in an engineering backlog instead of the customer support project. I need help tracing where that mapping lives and guidance on how to prevent this kind of misrouting in the future.

comments.message: We expected escalations to create issues in the CS-OPS Jira project, but they're suddenly going into ENG-PLAT. Our Jira admin changed some defaults yesterday. What should I check?

Go to Integrations > Jira and review both the global default project and any workflow-level overrides. If the default changed to ENG-PLAT, any automation without an explicit project mapping would inherit that. I recommend opening the escalation workflow, setting CS-OPS directly on the Jira action, and then submitting a test issue to confirm the destination.
I found one workflow override, but another workflow doesn't have any Jira action settings exposed. It still creates issues somehow.

That usually means the workflow is using the legacy escalation template, which stores the Jira target in the template configuration instead of the action editor. Open Templates > Escalations, edit the template used by that workflow, and reset the project there. For prevention, avoid relying on the global default for production flows and document explicit mappings per workflow.

subject: Can someone walk me through audit log filters for our admins?
description: I'm preparing a short enablement session for our regional admins, and I need to show them how to use audit log filters to trace changes to user access. I can find the audit log, but the filter behavior is not very intuitive, and I don't want to teach them something incorrect. Also, when I filter by 'User updated,' I don't always see role changes that I know happened yesterday.

comments.message: Could you explain how the audit log filters are supposed to work and why some role changes might not appear under that event type?

Happy to help. For training purposes, I'd recommend telling admins to start broad and then narrow by actor, date range, and object type, because some event labels are more granular than they appear. Role changes may not show under 'User updated' if they were recorded as a separate permission or membership event, depending on whether the change was made directly on the user profile, via SCIM sync, or through a group mapping.
That helps. Is there a simple way to explain that without confusing everyone?

Yes — I'd frame it as: 'Think of the audit log as recording the action the system saw, not always the business outcome you expected.' So if access changed through a sync or group rule, admins should search for related events, not just user edits. Since you mentioned a known role change from yesterday that you can't find, I'm escalating this to our technical support team to validate whether those entries are present in your tenant and whether any logging delay or sync behavior is involved.

subject: Service account lost admin scope after ownership change
description: We transferred workspace ownership to a new primary admin, and now our automation service account can still log in but can no longer create users through the API. In the UI it shows as a normal member instead of Service Admin. None of us can edit that role because the setting is locked. Can you confirm whether this role change is expected and escalate to the right team to restore it?

comments.message: The service account provisions users nightly, so this is blocking onboarding for tomorrow morning.
Service accounts usually inherit permissions from the current owner, so this may be expected after an ownership transfer. Try reauthorizing the API token and it should regain the missing permissions.
We already rotated the token. The API still returns permission_denied on user creation, and the UI role is definitely changed.

Thanks for confirming. Since the issue is specifically with the service account's user-administration role after the ownership change, I've escalated this to our platform team to review the locked role mapping and restore the correct Service Admin scope if appropriate. I've also noted the onboarding impact for tomorrow morning.

subject: Need help archiving old tickets without losing reporting
description: I’m trying to clean up data in our account because we have years of closed tickets cluttering searches and views. I want to archive or otherwise reduce the noise, but I don’t want to break our historical reports or dashboards. I’m not clear on the difference between deleting, archiving, and changing retention settings, and I need guidance before I touch anything.

comments.message: Ideally I want anything closed before 2022 out of day-to-day views, but finance still needs quarterly trend reports. What’s the safest approach?

The safest approach is not to delete those records. Deletion permanently removes ticket data from reporting, exports, and audit history. Instead, use either archive status—if your plan includes it—or create a filtered view that excludes tickets closed before 2022. That keeps the data available for dashboards while reducing clutter for agents.
I don’t see an archive option on our ticket statuses. Does that mean we can’t do it?

Possibly. Archive is only available on certain plans and may need to be enabled. As a workaround, I recommend creating a saved view with conditions like 'Status is not Closed' or 'Closed date is after 2021-12-31' for daily operations. For education on retention settings: retention policies affect how long data is kept, not how it appears in views. If you want, I can walk you through building the filtered view and checking whether archiving is included in your subscription.

subject: Blank values grouped as 'Other' after field cleanup in executive report
description: I standardized several text fields in our customer table and replaced null values with blanks so downstream exports would be cleaner. Since doing that, the executive segmentation report has a much larger 'Other' bucket, and I can't tell whether blank strings are being treated differently from nulls in the grouping logic. This matters because leadership uses that chart directly in board slides. Can you explain how the report handles those cleaned values?

comments.message: We replaced null segment values with blank strings, and now the executive report's 'Other' category jumped. Did reporting start counting blanks differently?
The report should treat blanks and nulls exactly the same, so the increase in 'Other' must be from some other data change.
I only changed those values. Before the cleanup they were excluded from the grouped chart, and now they're definitely included.

I checked the report definition and I gave you the wrong guidance earlier. In grouped charts, nulls are omitted when 'Hide empty values' is on, but blank strings are treated as actual values and get folded into 'Other' when they don't match a named segment. Converting nulls to blanks is what changed the chart.

subject: Permissão de administrador não libera edição de métricas do relatório
description: Promovi uma colaboradora para administradora hoje de manhã para que ela pudesse editar um relatório executivo. O acesso administrativo já aparece no perfil dela, mas a opção de editar métricas calculadas continua bloqueada. Outros administradores conseguem. Já comparei as permissões básicas e não entendi o que está faltando.

comments.message: Ela consegue criar usuários e ver configurações da conta, então a promoção aparentemente funcionou. O bloqueio acontece só em relatórios com campos calculados.
Olá! Administrador da conta sempre tem acesso total aos relatórios, então provavelmente basta limpar o cache do navegador ou testar em janela anônima.
Ela já testou em dois navegadores e segue sem editar. Continua aparecendo 'somente leitura para métricas calculadas'.
Nesse caso pode ser uma falha temporária da sessão. Sugiro remover e readicionar a permissão de administrador amanhã para ver se normaliza.

subject: Reembolso pendiente y error de verificación en SSO
description: Nos aprobaron un reembolso parcial hace más de una semana por un ajuste de licencias, pero todavía no aparece en la tarjeta ni como crédito en la cuenta. Además, desde esta mañana varios usuarios no pueden entrar por SSO porque sale "invalid audience". Ya confirmamos que el proveedor de identidad no cambió la configuración ayer.

comments.message: Nos aprobaron un reembolso parcial hace más de una semana por un ajuste de licencias, pero todavía no aparece en la tarjeta ni como crédito en la cuenta. Además, desde esta mañana varios usuarios no pueden entrar por SSO porque sale "invalid audience". Ya confirmamos que el proveedor de identidad no cambió la configuración ayer.

Hola. Revisé el caso financiero y el reembolso fue aprobado, pero quedó retenido porque el cargo original ya había sido liquidado en un lote cerrado; billing lo reemite manualmente en estos casos. Voy a dejar una nota para que lo prioricen.

En cuanto al SSO, veo en nuestros registros que el valor Audience URI cambió anoche durante una migración interna de dominios para su región. Ya restauré temporalmente el identificador anterior para su tenant y les recomiendo actualizar también la configuración del IdP al nuevo valor que les adjunto para evitar que vuelva a fallar.
Acabamos de probar y los usuarios ya pueden entrar otra vez. Gracias. Solo necesito confirmación de cuándo se verá el reembolso.

Perfecto, gracias por validar lo del SSO. Sobre el reembolso, no puedo dar una fecha exacta porque depende del reprocesamiento manual, pero dejé el ticket de billing marcado como urgente por antigüedad. En cuanto lo confirmen, recibirán el número de abono o referencia bancaria por correo.

subject: Po korekcie literówki w adresie nie mogę wejść na konto
description: W moim loginie była literówka i poprosiłem support o poprawienie adresu e-mail. Od tej zmiany nie działa ani stare, ani nowe logowanie. System raz pokazuje, że konto nie istnieje, a raz że hasło jest błędne.

comments.message: Nowy poprawny adres to j.kaczmarek@merxon.pl. Stary miał brakującą literę w nazwie domeny.
Rozumiem. Sprawdziłem tylko, że konto istnieje pod nowym adresem, więc problem powinien być rozwiązany. Proszę użyć opcji „Nie pamiętam hasła”.
Już to robiłem kilka razy i nie dostaję żadnej wiadomości resetującej.
W takim razie możliwe, że wiadomości są blokowane przez Państwa serwer pocztowy. Proszę skontaktować się z własnym działem IT.

subject: Réactivation d’un compte supprimé par erreur
description: J’ai supprimé par erreur le mauvais utilisateur pendant un ménage de printemps dans notre annuaire, et il s’agissait en fait de notre responsable conformité. Son compte avait aussi des droits spécifiques et une configuration SSO particulière. J’aimerais savoir s’il est possible de le restaurer exactement comme avant, ou au moins de rétablir son accès rapidement.

comments.message: Bonjour, j’ai supprimé par erreur le compte de lucie.perrin@biocare.fr il y a environ une heure. Pouvez-vous le réactiver avec ses anciens rôles ?

Bonjour, les comptes supprimés restent restaurables pendant 7 jours dans la plupart des cas. Je peux lancer la restauration, mais j’ai besoin de confirmer le nom de l’espace et si vous souhaitez rétablir aussi les groupes et permissions précédents.
Espace : Biocare France. Oui, il faut absolument remettre ses groupes et son rôle de responsable conformité, sinon elle ne pourra pas travailler aujourd’hui.

C’est fait : le compte a été restauré sur l’espace Biocare France avec ses appartenances aux groupes précédents et ses permissions. La reconnexion via SSO peut prendre jusqu’à 10 minutes pour se resynchroniser. Si elle voit encore une erreur après ce délai, envoyez-nous une capture et nous vérifierons le mapping SSO.

subject: Nie działa logowanie po przejęciu konta po byłym pracowniku
description: Przejęłam obowiązki po koledze i administrator zmienił dane konta na moje. Problem w tym, że po wpisaniu mojego e-maila system mówi, że konto jest nieaktywne. Bardzo proszę o pomoc, bo potrzebuję dostępu do historii zgłoszeń.

comments.message: Zależy mi też na tym, żeby stare dane kolegi nie były już widoczne jako login. Czy można to sprawdzić?
Wygląda na to, że konto po zmianie właściciela zostało oznaczone jako oczekujące na akceptację. Wysłałem nowe zaproszenie aktywacyjne na Pani adres i ukryłem poprzedni login z widoku użytkowników.
Zaproszenie przyszło, ale po kliknięciu linku dostaję stronę 404.
To dziwne. Proszę spróbować później albo otworzyć link w innej przeglądarce.

subject: Can't log in with Microsoft and onboarding workflow is creating duplicate tasks
description: Since we enforced Microsoft SSO this morning, I get stuck in a login loop after authentication. At the same time, our onboarding automation started creating two follow-up tasks for every new customer. I'm not sure if these are related, but I need both looked at because I can't get into the admin area consistently enough to troubleshoot.

comments.message: Since we enforced Microsoft SSO this morning, I get stuck in a login loop after authentication. At the same time, our onboarding automation started creating two follow-up tasks for every new customer. I'm not sure if these are related, but I need both looked at because I can't get into the admin area consistently enough to troubleshoot.

Login loops are usually caused by browser cache. Please clear cookies and try an incognito window. For the duplicate tasks, it sounds like your workflow may have been created twice, so you can delete one of them from the admin panel.
I already tried incognito on two browsers. Also, I can't get into the admin panel reliably, which is why I opened this ticket. I only see one workflow in the list.

If there’s only one workflow listed, then the duplicates may be expected behavior from the trigger firing twice on record updates. Unfortunately we don’t support detailed workflow debugging on standard plans. If login is still looping after cache clearing, please wait a few hours and try again.

subject: Users intermittently signed out and unable to log back in
description: Since this morning, several users in our EU team are being randomly signed out. When they try to log back in, some get a generic 'authentication failed' message and others get in after several attempts. It’s affecting customer-facing work, so please escalate if there’s a platform-side issue.

comments.message: We’ve seen this with at least 12 users so far. No recent browser changes, and both Google SSO and password users seem affected.
Sorry about that. This sounds like a temporary connectivity problem. Please have everyone clear cache, restart the browser, and try again in 30 minutes.
We already had people try that, and the pattern is still random across different offices and login methods. Is there an incident or something on your auth service?

I don’t currently see a posted incident, but given that both SSO and password logins are affected, I’m escalating this to our platform operations team for investigation. I should have done that earlier. Please send 2-3 affected user emails and timestamps if you can, since those help correlate authentication logs.

subject: Manager lost access to scheduled reports after role change
description: I changed our Operations Manager from Admin to Manager yesterday because we’re tightening permissions, and now all of the weekly scheduled utilization reports she used to receive are failing. She can still log in, but the report subscriptions show as owned by a disabled admin account from last year, and I can’t tell whether I need to transfer ownership, restore the old user, or update something in user settings. We need the Monday morning report to go out to leadership.

comments.message: I changed our Operations Manager from Admin to Manager yesterday because we’re tightening permissions, and now all of the weekly scheduled utilization reports she used to receive are failing. She can still log in, but the report subscriptions show as owned by a disabled admin account from last year, and I can’t tell whether I need to transfer ownership, restore the old user, or update something in user settings. We need the Monday morning report to go out to leadership.

Thanks for the detail. Scheduled reports are tied both to the report owner and the user’s current permission set. Since the original owner is a deactivated user, those schedules can fail after role changes. Please go to Admin Settings > Users, temporarily reactivate the old admin if possible, then in Reporting > Scheduled Reports transfer ownership to an active admin or another user with permission to view that report. After that, confirm the Operations Manager still has access to the underlying dashboards as a Manager.
I reactivated the old admin and transferred the two executive reports to our current Admin. One of them works now, but the utilization report still says the manager can’t access the dataset.

That means the role change removed access to at least one source object used by the utilization report. I’ve checked the behavior against our permissions model: Managers can receive scheduled reports only if they can open every underlying dashboard/filter combination. Please have an Admin open the report, review its sharing settings, and add the Operations Manager’s team to the report access list. If you want, I can also flag this for our reporting team to help identify the blocked dataset.

subject: Need to remove department admin access without removing product access
description: One of our managers should no longer be able to administer users in her department, but she still needs to log in and use the product every day. I can’t find a way to take away her admin rights without disabling the whole account. We use role-based access and department groups.

comments.message: Her account is active and should stay that way. She currently can add users to the Support department and reset their passwords, which we want to stop immediately, but she still needs access to tickets and reports.

You don’t need to deactivate her. In User Administration, edit her role assignments and remove the department admin role or group that grants user-management permissions, while keeping her product role intact. If your setup uses group-based admin rights, check both direct roles and inherited group membership.
I removed one admin group, but she can still open the department user list after logging back in.

She may still inherit that permission from another group or from a custom role bundle. Review the 'Effective permissions' panel on her user profile and remove any remaining scopes like 'manage users' or 'reset credentials' for that department. If the panel still shows no admin scopes but access persists, wait a few minutes for permission cache refresh; otherwise reply with a screenshot and I can escalate it.

subject: Need to bulk reassign dashboard ownership after offboarding analyst
description: Our reporting analyst left last week and we deactivated her account today. Since then, several department heads are saying their saved reports either disappeared or can’t be edited. I’m a user admin, but I don’t see a way to bulk transfer ownership of her custom reports and dashboards to another employee. We need to keep the reports but lock editing down to just two team leads.

comments.message: Our reporting analyst left last week and we deactivated her account today. Since then, several department heads are saying their saved reports either disappeared or can’t be edited. I’m a user admin, but I don’t see a way to bulk transfer ownership of her custom reports and dashboards to another employee. We need to keep the reports but lock editing down to just two team leads.

You should be able to solve this by changing the replacement employee to Super Admin. Once they’re Super Admin, all reports from the former analyst should show up automatically under their account, and then you can reduce permissions again later.
We tried that and nothing reappeared under the new person. The reports still only show when I search by the old analyst’s name in audit logs.
In that case the reports were probably deleted when the user was deactivated, so there may not be anything to transfer. I’d recommend recreating them manually.

subject: Offboarding automation removed users, but annual billing still counts them all
description: We ran a bulk offboarding after a reorg and used an automation to transfer account ownership, revoke app access, and archive each user profile. The process worked operationally, but our annual true-up report still counts those archived users as payable seats, and one of the ownership transfer steps seems to have reassigned several records back to a disabled manager account. I need help reconciling the true-up numbers and fixing the account-change logic so these users don’t keep affecting billing.

comments.message: Thirty-two users were archived in February, but the true-up export still shows them as licensable. We also found 14 customer records owned by a disabled manager after the offboarding workflow ran.

Archived users can still be counted toward annual committed seats if they were active at any point during the contract year, so the report may be expected. As for ownership reassignment, disabled users can’t receive new records, so I suspect those records were never updated.
The audit log shows the automation reassigned them on the day the manager was disabled, so something definitely updated them.

Understood. I checked further and there’s a race condition in your workflow: the transfer step runs before the directory sync fully removes the disabled manager from eligible owners, so the fallback rule still selects that account. I’ve escalated that bug to our automation team. On billing, I can confirm the true-up report is based on peak occupied seats during the annual period, not current archived status, so the 32 users remain part of this year’s count even though they’ve been offboarded.

subject: Webhook integration retries for hours after endpoint recovered
description: Our outbound webhook integration to our internal API went down briefly during maintenance, which I expected. The problem is that even after our endpoint came back healthy, your system kept retrying old failed events for hours and delayed new ones behind them. I need help troubleshooting the retry behavior and whether there's a way to flush or reprioritize the queue.

comments.message: The endpoint returned 503s for about 12 minutes, then was fine. New events were still delayed more than 3 hours after recovery. We saw repeated delivery attempts for the same payload IDs long after the API was healthy again.

Our webhook delivery system uses exponential backoff, so some retrying after an outage is expected. There isn't currently a self-service option to reorder queued events ahead of older retries. To troubleshoot whether this was normal backlog behavior or a stuck worker, could you share one or two webhook delivery IDs and the approximate recovery time?
Two IDs are wh_11872 and wh_11891. Recovery was around 14:22 UTC. This is problematic for us because stale events are less useful than fresh ones.

Understood. I can't manually flush the queue from support, but I've sent these delivery IDs to our integrations operations team to verify whether the retry workers drained abnormally slowly. I've also added your account to the feature request for queue controls/prioritization, since your use case makes sense.

subject: Slack integration stopped posting case updates
description: I'm the admin for our support workspace, and since yesterday our Slack integration has stopped posting ticket update notifications to the #customer-escalations channel. We didn't intentionally change anything, but we did rotate some internal app credentials last week. The connection page in your app still says Slack is connected, yet no messages are being delivered. I need help figuring out whether this is an auth problem, a channel permission issue, or something else.

comments.message: The last successful Slack notification was yesterday around 9:14 AM ET. Since then, ticket assignments and status changes are happening in the platform, but nothing is appearing in Slack. We use a custom workflow tied to priority=urgent if that matters.

Thanks for the detail. Since the integration still shows as connected, the most likely cause is that the Slack bot token is valid but missing permission to post in that specific channel. Could you confirm whether the app is still a member of #customer-escalations and whether any Slack admin changed channel restrictions? Also, please try sending a test notification from Settings > Integrations > Slack.
I checked. The bot had been removed from the private channel during a cleanup. I re-added it, but the test notification failed with 'channel_not_found'.

That helps narrow it down. 'channel_not_found' usually means the saved channel reference is stale, which can happen if the channel was converted or recreated. Please reselect #customer-escalations in the Slack integration settings and save the mapping again, then run the test. If it still fails after remapping, send us the integration log timestamp and I'll have engineering review the webhook lookup.

subject: Unexpected workspace suspension notice
description: Our workspace suddenly showed a suspension banner this morning saying we violated acceptable use rules, but we haven't changed how we're using the product. We have active customer cases in progress and the warning says service may be limited in 24 hours. I need someone to review this because the normal support articles don't explain what triggered it.

comments.message: Can you tell me why this happened and remove the suspension warning? We're a paying customer and this is disrupting our operations.

I checked the account and it does show a compliance review flag, but the specific trigger isn't visible in the standard support console. Those reviews are handled by our Trust & Safety team, so I can't lift the warning directly.
That's not very helpful. Is there at least a timeline? We need to plan for tomorrow.

I've escalated the ticket to Trust & Safety and noted the risk to your live customer operations. I can't promise removal within 24 hours, but escalated reviews are typically picked up the same business day. If they need documentation from you, they'll reply here — for now, your workspace still appears accessible.

subject: Błędnie zmieniona nazwa workspace po migracji
description: Po weekendowej migracji nasze środowisko ma złą nazwę i użytkownicy myślą, że trafili na testowe konto. Chcę zmienić nazwę workspace na właściwą, ale opcja edycji jest wyszarzona. Nie wiem, czy to ograniczenie po migracji, czy brak uprawnień.

comments.message: Nasze konto po migracji nazywa się „acme-temp-final”, a powinno „Acme Polska”. Próbowałem to poprawić, ale pole nazwy jest nieaktywne. Jestem administratorem.
Prawdopodobnie to tylko problem z pamięcią podręczną przeglądarki. Proszę wyczyścić cache i spróbować ponownie.
Sprawdziłem w dwóch przeglądarkach i w trybie incognito. Nadal nie mogę edytować nazwy.

Rozumiem. W takim razie możliwe, że nazwa została zablokowana na poziomie migracji i wymaga ręcznego odblokowania po naszej stronie. Przekazuję zgłoszenie do zespołu technicznego z prośbą o zmianę na „Acme Polska” albo odblokowanie pola edycji. Damy znać po aktualizacji.

subject: API rate limit banner shows wrong reset time
description: We're hitting API rate limits during our nightly sync, and the admin console banner says the limit will reset in 3 minutes, but requests continue getting 429 responses for closer to 15 minutes. I need to know whether the reset timer in the UI is wrong or if our integration is being throttled by some other rule. Please investigate and explain how the rate-limit window is calculated.

comments.message: Our developers backed off based on the banner and resumed too early, which just extended the failure window. We need accurate guidance.

The banner countdown should match the account's primary rate-limit bucket, but your logs show your sync is also hitting a secondary per-endpoint throttle on bulk upsert calls. The UI isn't displaying that secondary reset window, so the 3-minute message is incomplete and misleading. I've reported that as a product bug.
What should our developers do in the meantime? We only saw generic docs about 429 handling.

Until the UI is improved, please rely on the reset headers returned with each 429 response rather than the admin banner alone, and apply exponential backoff specifically to the bulk upsert endpoint. In general, the platform uses rolling windows by limit bucket, so different endpoints can recover at different times. That's why the banner looked wrong even though one bucket had technically reset.

subject: CSV import hangs at validating for files with international characters
description: Our CSV imports get stuck at the validating step whenever the file contains names with accented characters like José or Chloë. A file with only plain ASCII imports normally. I tested different CSV delimiters and both UTF-8 export options from Excel and Google Sheets. Is there a known bug with character encoding in the importer?

comments.message: One file with 2,300 rows has been validating for 45 minutes. A simplified file with the same columns but no accented characters completes in under a minute.
If the file uses special characters, the importer only supports ANSI encoding, so you'll need to remove those characters before uploading.
Your docs say UTF-8 is supported, and we've imported UTF-8 files before. This sounds wrong.

You're right, I spoke too broadly there. UTF-8 is supported, and your before/after test strongly suggests a parsing bug rather than unsupported characters. I’m escalating this to the imports team with your examples; if you can share a redacted sample file that still reproduces the hang, that will help engineering isolate where validation is stalling.

subject: Invoice includes API package after enabling custom field sync
description: I turned on custom field sync between our CRM and your platform, and now we're being billed for an API package that we didn't budget for. The setup guide implied this was a configuration change, not a separate paid feature. I need to understand whether the sync configuration triggered billable API access and what our options are.

comments.message: After enabling custom field sync for accounts and tickets, our invoice shows an added "API Throughput Pack." We are on the Standard plan. Did the sync feature automatically enroll us in a paid API tier?
Any integration uses the API, so yes, the system probably added API billing automatically once you turned it on.
Probably? I need a definite answer. The admin page said standard API limits were included, so I'm not sure why a throughput pack appeared at all.
I can't verify the exact trigger from here. You may want to reach out to sales if you don't want API-related charges.

subject: Invite says accepted but user still missing from team
description: I invited a consultant and she accepted the invite, but she can only see the homepage and none of the project boards she needs. She can log in, so this seems more like a permissions problem than a login problem. I think I added her at the workspace level but forgot to put her in the correct team, and I'm also not sure what role external consultants usually get in your system. How do I fix that?

comments.message: I invited a consultant and she accepted the invite, but she can only see the homepage and none of the project boards she needs. She can log in, so this seems more like a permissions problem than a login problem. I think I added her at the workspace level but forgot to put her in the correct team, and I'm also not sure what role external consultants usually get in your system. How do I fix that?

If she can log in but sees no boards, she probably isn't in the right team or project group yet. Go to Users, open her profile, and add her to the specific project team. For consultants, the most common role is Commenter so they can view and comment without making changes.
She actually needs to update task statuses and upload files, not just comment. Also I don't see a place called project group in Users.

Right, then Commenter would be too limited. Use Member or Contributor depending on which role name your workspace shows, then add her both to the team and directly to the board if the board is private. I was imprecise earlier: team membership is managed from her user profile, while board access is managed from the board's Share or Access panel. Once both are set, have her refresh or sign out/in to reload permissions.

subject: Webhook says user removed, but API still shows them as active
description: We're listening for your user.updated webhook to revoke downstream access when a user is removed from our account. The webhook payload said the user was deactivated, but when our workflow called the user API immediately after, the response still showed active=true for about a minute. Is that expected, and how should we handle this race condition in our user administration process?

comments.message: This caused a downstream system to re-enable the account because it trusted the follow-up API read over the webhook event. We're trying to make offboarding deterministic.

Thanks for flagging this. There can be a short eventual-consistency delay between the event bus and the read replica that serves some API GET requests, especially right after deactivation. The webhook is emitted from the primary write path, so it may arrive before all reads reflect the new status. We recommend treating the webhook as authoritative for revocation and retrying the GET with exponential backoff for up to 2 minutes if you need to confirm state.
Understood. We'll change our workflow so the webhook triggers revocation immediately and the API check becomes a delayed verification step.
That’s the safest pattern here. If you'd like, I can also share the event ordering notes for user.updated versus group membership events so your offboarding flow stays consistent across both.

subject: Sincronização criou empresas com CNPJ formatado de formas diferentes
description: Depois da sincronização com nosso ERP, a plataforma criou empresas separadas para o mesmo cliente porque alguns CNPJs vieram com pontuação e outros sem. Agora temos negócios espalhados entre registros duplicados e os dashboards ficaram errados. Como normalizar isso e impedir que volte a acontecer?

comments.message: Depois da sincronização com nosso ERP, a plataforma criou empresas separadas para o mesmo cliente porque alguns CNPJs vieram com pontuação e outros sem. Agora temos negócios espalhados entre registros duplicados e os dashboards ficaram errados. Como normalizar isso e impedir que volte a acontecer?

Isso acontece quando o campo usado como identificador não passa por normalização antes da criação/atualização. Para corrigir o histórico, exporte as empresas com o CNPJ, remova pontuação para ficar tudo no mesmo padrão, reimporte usando o CNPJ limpo como chave única e depois mescle as duplicadas que já existem. Para evitar recorrência, ative uma regra de transformação no mapeamento da sincronização para strip de caracteres não numéricos no campo CNPJ.
Conseguimos fazer a transformação na importação manual, mas não achei essa opção na integração nativa com o ERP. Existe mesmo?

Na integração nativa, essa transformação não fica disponível em todos os conectores. No seu caso, conferi aqui que o conector ERP Legacy não oferece normalização inline de CNPJ. Vou encaminhar para a equipe de integrações verificar se há um campo alternativo de chave ou ajuste no middleware. Enquanto isso, a mitigação mais segura é padronizar o CNPJ na origem ou passar a sincronizar por um ID externo imutável.

subject: Need a simple way to teach form logic to team leads
description: I'm responsible for training our team leads on building intake forms, and I'm struggling to explain conditional logic in a way they can actually remember. On top of that, one form in production is hiding a required field when 'Contractor' is selected, which is blocking submissions for a few managers. I need both a plain-English explanation and help with the broken behavior.

comments.message: Can you give me a user-friendly explanation of conditional logic and tell me why a required field would disappear?

A simple explanation is: conditional logic shows or hides questions based on earlier answers, so users only see fields that are relevant to them. If a required field disappears, it's usually because another rule is intentionally hiding it, and hidden required fields are ignored by the form.
That's not what we're seeing. The hidden field seems to still be required somehow because users can't submit.

Thanks, that detail changes things. Hidden required fields should not block submission in a normal configuration, so either the field is being referenced by another dependency or the form has a misconfigured validation rule. I'm escalating this to our forms team so they can inspect the exact logic path for the Contractor option. For training, I'd avoid saying 'required fields are always ignored when hidden' and instead tell team leads to test each answer path before publishing.

subject: API key stopped working after ownership transfer
description: I transferred ownership of our workspace from my account to our new IT manager yesterday, and since then our nightly sync to your API has been failing with 401 errors. The integration was created under my user and uses an API key generated a few months ago. Did the ownership change revoke something automatically? We need to know whether to rotate the key, update permissions, or recreate the app connection.

comments.message: We changed the primary owner of the workspace yesterday and now every request to the v2 API is coming back 401 Unauthorized. The key itself hasn't changed on our side. Can you confirm whether owner changes invalidate existing API keys?

Changing the workspace owner can affect credentials that were created under the previous owner's profile, especially if the integration was scoped to that user rather than to a service account. Please have the new owner go to Settings > Developers, verify the app is still authorized, and generate a new API key if the old one shows as inactive. If you share the last 6 characters of the failing key and a request ID, I can confirm whether it was revoked during the account change.

I checked and the old key now shows 'disabled'. The new owner can create keys, but our integration also needs access to the old custom objects endpoint. Will a newly generated key inherit the same scopes automatically?

Not always. After an ownership transfer, a newly generated key inherits the permissions of the account that creates it, so the new owner should verify that their role includes access to custom objects and API write scopes. Once they create the new key, update the integration credentials and test the /custom-objects endpoint first. If you'd like, reply with the endpoint path and I can verify the exact scope set you need.

subject: Duplicate charges tied to duplicated workflow executions
description: I noticed we were billed for two overage blocks this month, and the usage report lines up with a workflow automation bug we’ve been seeing. Our lead assignment workflow has been firing twice for a subset of records, which appears to have doubled both task creation and billable automation runs. I need help separating a billing problem from an automation defect, because we shouldn’t be paying overages for duplicate executions caused by the platform.

comments.message: We got charged for 18,000 automation runs, but our normal volume is closer to 9,000. Looking at the logs, a lead routing workflow has been executing twice on the same trigger for hundreds of records.

I checked your invoice and the overage charges were calculated from recorded workflow runs, so the billing itself is mathematically correct. For the duplicate executions, can you confirm whether the workflow has more than one trigger or if records are being updated by another automation?
It has one trigger: when lifecycle stage changes to MQL. We do have a follow-up workflow that updates owner fields, but that shouldn’t retrigger the original path.

That follow-up update may be causing the original workflow to run again if re-enrollment is enabled. I recommend disabling re-enrollment and monitoring usage for 24 hours. I’m also opening a ticket with our technical team to review whether duplicate runs from the same event should be credited back to billing if they were system-generated.

subject: Zendesk ticket volume report missing one channel after integration reconnect
description: We reconnected our Zendesk integration over the weekend, and now our support volume report is missing tickets from the chat channel. Email and web form counts look normal, but chat is zero in the report even though I can open those tickets in individual customer timelines. Please figure out whether the reconnect changed the integration scope, whether the report is filtering the wrong field, and whether this needs to be escalated.

comments.message: After reconnecting Zendesk, our 'Tickets by Channel' report shows no chat tickets from Saturday onward. We definitely had hundreds. What should we do?
I checked the connector and it appears to be syncing data successfully. The fastest fix is to clone the report and remove the channel filter.
We did that. Chat is still blank, and the ticket records themselves have chat transcripts attached.

Thanks. I dug further and found the Zendesk integration is now writing the source into a new field called 'origin_channel', while your report still groups on the deprecated 'channel' attribute. I updated the report field on this specific saved report, and current counts started populating. Historical chat data from the reconnect window still needs reindexing, so I've escalated a backfill request to our reporting operations team.

subject: Exportação XLSX de relatório truncando colunas calculadas
description: Estou exportando um relatório com colunas calculadas de SLA e tempo até primeira resposta. Na tela os valores aparecem completos, mas no arquivo XLSX algumas colunas vêm vazias e outras repetem o valor da linha anterior. Isso acontece só quando o relatório passa de mais ou menos 8 mil linhas. Preciso enviar essa base para auditoria ainda hoje.

comments.message: Já tentei duas vezes e o comportamento é sempre o mesmo. CSV sai com todos os registros, mas eu preciso do XLSX porque a auditoria usa o formato com abas.

Olá! Se o CSV está correto, então provavelmente não é um bug no relatório em si. O XLSX costuma ter limitações de formatação em grandes volumes. Minha sugestão é usar CSV e converter localmente para Excel.
Mas o problema não é formatação. As colunas calculadas ficam vazias ou copiam o valor anterior, então o conteúdo sai incorreto. Isso não deveria acontecer numa exportação padrão.

Entendo. No momento não tenho como validar esse comportamento do gerador XLSX daqui. Vou encaminhar seu caso para análise do time técnico, porque pode haver falha na serialização das colunas calculadas em arquivos grandes. Como saída temporária, a única opção disponível agora continua sendo exportar em CSV, onde você informou que os dados estão íntegros.

subject: CSV import created duplicate contacts and skipped custom fields
description: I imported about 4,800 contacts from a CSV into our workspace yesterday, and now I’m seeing two problems: some contacts were duplicated, and two of our custom fields (Renewal Date and Segment Owner) are blank for a large portion of the list. I need to understand what happened and how to clean this up without making things worse. I’m also not sure if I mapped the file correctly because the import screen was a little confusing.

comments.message: I imported the file from HubSpot export format. I matched email, company, and phone, but I may have mapped Renewal Date as text instead of date. Is there a way to roll back just that import?

Thanks for the detail. There isn’t a one-click rollback for a single import, but we can troubleshoot this safely. First, go to Contacts > Imports > Import History and open yesterday’s job. That page will show how many rows were created, updated, or skipped, plus any field-level errors. Duplicates usually happen when the unique identifier in the file doesn’t match the deduplication setting used during import.

For cleanup, export the affected contacts using the import timestamp filter, then sort by email to identify duplicated records. If email should be the unique key, re-import the corrected CSV with 'Update existing records' enabled and email mapped as the identifier. For Renewal Date, the system expects YYYY-MM-DD. 'Segment Owner' must map to a text field or user field depending on how that custom property was created. If you send a sample of 5-10 rows, I can help you verify the mapping before you run the correction import.

subject: Need to update account owner for audit API access
description: Our compliance team changed who should be the billing and security owner on the account, and at the same time our internal tooling lost access to the audit log API. The new owner can sign in fine, but requests using their token get a permissions error while the former owner's token still works for now. I'm trying to tell whether this is an account-role issue, a delayed permission sync, or something we missed during the owner change.

comments.message: We reassigned account ownership to our compliance lead. She can log in and see the admin area, but when she calls the audit log API with a personal token she gets 403 Forbidden. The previous owner can still call it. What else needs to be updated?
If ownership has already been transferred, the new owner should automatically inherit all admin API permissions, including audit access. It may just take a little while for the change to propagate.
It's been a full day. She still gets 403, and we have an audit export due this afternoon.

Understood. I shouldn't have implied it was only propagation. Audit endpoints are often restricted not just by ownership but by a specific admin role or security permission set. Please check whether the new owner also has the security-admin capability enabled in Roles, and if not, grant that or use a service token tied to an admin who does. If you'd like, send the exact endpoint and I can confirm the minimum role required.

subject: Merged contacts are still appearing separately in search
description: We merged several duplicate contacts today, but when I search by name I still see what looks like separate entries. I don’t know whether the merge failed, whether search indexing is delayed, or whether one of the records is just archived differently. I need help troubleshooting this because our team is losing confidence in the cleanup work.

comments.message: On the contact detail page I can see a merge note, but global search still shows both names. Is that expected?

Yes, sometimes search indexes lag behind record changes for a short period after merges. If the contact detail page shows the merge event, the merge likely completed successfully even if search still reflects older results.
How long is 'a short period'? My manager is asking if we need to redo them.

It varies, but typically search catches up within a few minutes to a couple of hours depending on system load. I would not redo the merges right away, because that can create more confusion. To verify, open the surviving contact record and confirm the secondary email or activity history was absorbed as expected. For your team, the key point is that data cleanup actions and search visibility are not always perfectly real-time. If the duplicate search entries remain tomorrow, reply here with two example contact IDs and we can investigate further.

subject: Can't log in after domain verification and imported attachments are missing
description: We verified our company domain and switched users to that login method, but since then I can't sign in with my old email alias. Separately, a bulk migration of support tickets finished, yet many attachments are missing from the imported records. I'm trying to determine whether both problems came from the domain change.

comments.message: I used to log in as sara@oldbrand.com, but after domain verification only sara@newbrand.com seems recognized and even that failed once before working. Also, imported tickets show the text content but not the PDF attachments for roughly 20% of records.

The domain verification can affect which email identity is accepted at login, especially if the old alias is no longer mapped to your user profile. Please use the newbrand address going forward and ask an admin to add the old alias back if needed. For the missing attachments, those are often skipped when the source URLs expire during migration. Can you confirm whether the import summary mentions attachment fetch failures?
Yes, the report has a column saying 417 attachment fetch failures due to 403 from source URLs. So I guess that's separate from the login issue.

Correct, these look like two distinct issues. Your access problem is explained by the identity change after domain verification, with the brief failure likely due to propagation or a stale session. The data migration itself imported the ticket records, but some attachments were not retrievable from the source system. You'll need to re-run those attachments with fresh source links or export them manually and attach them again.

subject: Restored user came back without previous permissions
description: I reactivated a manager who returned from leave, but their account only came back as a basic member. Before leave they were able to manage their team’s users and approve access changes. I need to know whether role history can be restored automatically or if I have to rebuild their permissions manually.

comments.message: I restored a returning manager, but their old admin permissions didn’t come back. They’re just a basic member now.

That is expected in some cases. When a user is reactivated, previous elevated permissions may not be restored automatically depending on how the account was deactivated and whether roles were assigned directly or through synced groups. Can you confirm whether their manager permissions were manually assigned or provisioned from your identity provider? I’m escalating this to our account configuration team because they can check the user history and tell us whether the prior role can be recovered.
I’m not sure. The user existed long before we enabled group sync, so it might have been a manual assignment originally.

Got it. I added that to the escalation.
