If the role was direct, the configuration team may be able to confirm it from the audit trail; if it was group-based, they’ll likely point you to the current mapping source. For now, you can manually grant the least-privileged admin role needed for their work, but I’d wait on restoring broader access until the team verifies the historical assignment.

subject: Webhooks non déclenchés après rotation de clé API
description: J’ai fait une rotation de clé API conformément à notre politique sécurité, et depuis plus aucun webhook ne part vers notre ERP. Les journaux montrent des tentatives sans détail utile. Comme ces flux alimentent la facturation interne, j’ai besoin d’un diagnostic et probablement d’une analyse plus poussée.

comments.message: Bonjour, depuis la rotation de clé d’hier soir, les événements “invoice.created” et “customer.updated” n’atteignent plus notre endpoint. Nous n’avons pourtant changé que la clé API.
Bonjour, si les webhooks ne partent plus, c’est probablement que votre endpoint est en panne. Vérifiez votre serveur puis revenez vers nous.
Notre endpoint répond 200 sur les tests manuels, et les échecs ont commencé exactement après la rotation côté plateforme. Pouvez-vous regarder plus loin ?

Je vais transmettre cela à notre équipe intégrations pour qu’elle vérifie s’il existe un lien entre la rotation de clé et la signature ou la file d’envoi des webhooks. Merci de laisser le ticket ouvert ; je n’ai pas de correction immédiate à vous proposer et le dossier est désormais escaladé.

subject: No se respetan las reglas de retención en archivos adjuntos
description: Configuramos una política para eliminar adjuntos de más de 180 días en tickets cerrados, pero siguen apareciendo archivos antiguos en búsquedas y también en exportaciones de cumplimiento. Lo extraño es que el texto del ticket sí se conserva como esperábamos. ¿La retención de adjuntos se administra por separado?

comments.message: Configuramos una política para eliminar adjuntos de más de 180 días en tickets cerrados, pero siguen apareciendo archivos antiguos en búsquedas y también en exportaciones de cumplimiento. Lo extraño es que el texto del ticket sí se conserva como esperábamos. ¿La retención de adjuntos se administra por separado?

Sí, en muchas cuentas la retención del contenido estructurado y la de adjuntos se administran por políticas distintas. La regla que describe puede estar aplicándose solo al objeto ticket y no al almacén de archivos. Revise en Administración > Gestión de datos > Retención de archivos si existe una política específica para adjuntos o blobs.
Entré ahí y veo una política global, pero está activa. Aun así, archivos de enero siguen accesibles.

En ese caso puede haber dos posibilidades: que la política no sea retroactiva y solo afecte adjuntos cargados después de activarla, o que el proceso de purga esté atrasado. Como las exportaciones de cumplimiento todavía los incluyen, conviene revisar el job de eliminación. Voy a escalarlo con nuestro equipo de plataforma. Si me comparte un par de IDs de tickets con adjuntos antiguos, lo adjunto para que validen si están marcados para purga o si nunca entraron en la política.

subject: Dashboard loads forever after adding custom field filter
description: After I added a filter for one of our new custom fields to a dashboard widget, the dashboard has been spinning for several minutes and never finishes loading. If I open a different dashboard it's fine, so it seems tied to this one widget. I need to troubleshoot whether the field itself is causing it or if the dashboard is stuck in a bad state.

comments.message: After I added a filter for one of our new custom fields to a dashboard widget, the dashboard has been spinning for several minutes and never finishes loading. If I open a different dashboard it's fine, so it seems tied to this one widget. I need to troubleshoot whether the field itself is causing it or if the dashboard is stuck in a bad state.

Thanks for reporting this. Please try opening the dashboard in an incognito window and, if possible, duplicate the dashboard and remove the new custom field filter from the copied widget. We have seen long loads when a newly created multi-select field is filtered before its index finishes building. Can you tell me whether the field is text, single select, or multi-select, and roughly how many records are in scope?

It's a multi-select field, created this morning, and the widget covers about 1.8 million records. In incognito the same widget still hangs, but a duplicate dashboard without that filter loads normally.

That points to the new field filter rather than the dashboard itself. Most likely the field index is still catching up on a dataset that large. As a workaround, remove that filter for now or narrow the widget date range, then retry later today. I'm also escalating this to our analytics team to verify whether the index job is stalled for your workspace, because it should not spin indefinitely.

subject: Dashboard filters disappear when switching between saved views
description: When I switch from one saved dashboard view to another, some of the filters disappear and the widget data becomes inconsistent. It seems to happen only on one of our finance dashboards, and mostly after editing a filter and then moving to a different saved view. I already cleared cache and reproduced it in an incognito window. Can you help figure out whether this is a bug?

comments.message: We rely on these saved views for weekly reporting, and right now the region filter vanishes when I jump between views. Then the numbers shown in two widgets no longer match the expected totals.
That sounds frustrating. This is usually caused by stale browser storage. Please clear your cookies and recreate the saved views, then test again.
I already mentioned I tried incognito, and this is happening for two teammates as well. Recreating the views is a lot of work if there is a product issue.

Understood. Since multiple users can reproduce it and incognito rules out local storage, this may be a defect in how saved-view state is applied. I’m escalating this to our dashboard team with your reproduction steps: edit filter, switch saved view, region filter disappears, widget totals drift. If you can send a screen recording, I’ll attach it to speed up their investigation.

subject: Knowledge base images fail to load for some published articles
description: A handful of articles in our help center show broken image icons for customers, but the images load fine when our team previews the draft internally. I need help figuring out whether this is a permissions bug or something wrong with how we inserted the files. Also, can you explain the correct way article attachments and embedded images are served publicly?

comments.message: The broken images are in articles copied from an older category. We used the rich text editor's image tool, not raw HTML. Customers reported this on mobile and desktop.

I reviewed two affected articles and found the image URLs still point to files attached to the original draft articles in the old category. Preview works for your team because authenticated users can access those assets, but published readers cannot if the source attachment isn't public. That part is expected behavior. What looks buggy is that the copy action preserved the old attachment references instead of duplicating the assets into the new published article.

The correct setup is that embedded article images should be uploaded so the published article references its own public asset URLs. As a workaround, edit each article and reinsert the affected images from your computer or the article's own attachments panel rather than relying on the copied reference. I've escalated the copy behavior to our content team because those links should have been rewritten automatically.

subject: Les événements Stripe arrivent avec 30 minutes de retard
description: Nous utilisons votre intégration Stripe pour déclencher l'activation d'un espace dès qu'un paiement est réussi. Depuis cette semaine, les événements payment_intent.succeeded arrivent avec un gros décalage, parfois 20 à 30 minutes, alors que dans Stripe ils sont marqués comme livrés immédiatement. Du coup nos clients paient mais n'ont pas accès tout de suite. Y a-t-il une file d'attente ou un paramètre à ajuster chez vous ?

comments.message: Bonjour, les webhooks Stripe semblent bien partir instantanément, mais votre plateforme ne les traite qu'avec beaucoup de retard. Cela impacte l'activation de nos comptes.

Bonjour, merci. Je viens de vérifier et nous n'avons pas d'incident global sur Stripe. Pouvez-vous me confirmer si vous utilisez le webhook standard ou notre connecteur Stripe natif avec file d'ingestion ?
Le connecteur natif. Dans l'historique, je vois bien les événements reçus tout de suite puis "mis en attente" avant traitement.

Merci, cela ressemble à une congestion de la file d'ingestion liée au mode de déduplication renforcée, activé sur certains comptes à fort volume. J'ai désactivé ce mode sur votre instance et lancé le retraitement des événements en attente. Les prochains paiements devraient être traités en temps quasi réel. Surveillez les deux prochaines heures; si le délai dépasse encore 5 minutes, je passerai le dossier à l'équipe plateforme.

subject: Agendamento de envio do relatório falha quando uso acentos no nome
description: Estou tentando programar o envio semanal de um relatório para a diretoria, mas sempre que o nome do agendamento contém acentos, como “Revisão Operação São Paulo”, o salvamento falha sem mensagem clara. Se eu tiro os acentos, salva normalmente. O relatório em si funciona, o problema parece estar só nessa etapa de agendamento. Podem verificar se isso é um bug?

comments.message: Testei com “São Paulo”, “Operação” e “Revisão Mensal”. Em todos os casos com acento o botão de salvar fica carregando e depois volta sem criar o agendamento.
Olá! Obrigado por avisar. Pode me confirmar se isso acontece em todos os navegadores? Às vezes caracteres especiais falham por causa do idioma configurado no sistema operacional.
Acontece no Chrome e no Firefox, em dois computadores diferentes. O idioma do sistema é português em ambos, mas se removo só o acento, o agendamento é criado na hora.

Obrigado pela confirmação. Isso reforça uma falha no tratamento de caracteres especiais no fluxo de agendamento de relatórios. Registrei o bug para o time responsável e incluí exemplos com “São Paulo” e “Operação”. Como contorno imediato, use nomes sem acentuação até a correção ser aplicada.

subject: Workflow mit Webhook sendet doppelte Benachrichtigungen
description: Ich habe eine Automatisierung eingerichtet, die bei Statuswechsel auf "Bereit zur Abrechnung" einen Webhook an unser ERP schickt. Seit der Reaktivierung des Workflows nach einem Testlauf gehen manche Datensätze doppelt rüber. Im ERP werden dadurch zwei Rechnungsentwürfe erstellt. Ich bin mir nicht sicher, ob der Fehler in der Schleife oder am Trigger liegt.

comments.message: Uns ist aufgefallen, dass einzelne Datensätze zweimal an den Webhook gesendet werden. Im Workflow sehe ich denselben Datensatz mit zwei Läufen innerhalb weniger Sekunden.

Das ist wahrscheinlich nur ein Anzeigeproblem im Log. Wenn der Datensatz zweimal auftaucht, bedeutet das nicht unbedingt, dass der Webhook auch doppelt gesendet wurde. Sie könnten die Einträge im ERP einfach manuell löschen.
Im ERP gibt es aber wirklich zwei Requests mit unterschiedlichen Run-IDs. Außerdem wird der Datensatz nach dem Webhook noch einmal aktualisiert, weil ein Feld zurückgeschrieben wird.
Dann deaktivieren Sie am besten den gesamten Workflow und bauen ihn neu auf. Mehr können wir an dieser Stelle leider nicht empfehlen.

subject: SCIM-Synchronisierung legt deaktivierte Benutzer wieder an
description: Ich verwalte unsere Nutzer über Azure AD und SCIM. Wenn ich einen Benutzer in Ihrem System manuell deaktiviere, wird er nach dem nächsten Sync wieder als aktiv angezeigt. Das führt gerade zu Verwirrung bei unserem Offboarding. Wie ist der korrekte Weg, Benutzer dauerhaft zu deaktivieren, ohne dass SCIM das überschreibt?

comments.message: Zur Einordnung: Es betrifft mehrere Konten. Manuelle Änderungen in Ihrer Benutzerverwaltung scheinen nach dem Verzeichnisabgleich verloren zu gehen.

Das klingt nach dem erwarteten Verhalten bei aktiviertem SCIM-Provisioning. In diesem Fall ist Ihr Identitätsanbieter die führende Quelle für den Benutzerstatus. Wenn Sie Benutzer dauerhaft deaktivieren möchten, sollten Sie sie in Azure AD entziehen oder dort auf inaktiv setzen, statt nur lokal in unserer Benutzerverwaltung.
Verstehe. Bedeutet das auch, dass Gruppen- und Rollenänderungen ebenfalls beim nächsten Sync überschrieben werden?

Ja, bei SCIM können sowohl Aktivstatus als auch bestimmte attributgesteuerte Rollen- oder Gruppenzuweisungen vom IdP zurückgeschrieben werden. Ich empfehle, Offboarding und Berechtigungen zentral in Azure AD zu steuern. Wenn Sie möchten, kann ich das an unser Provisioning-Team übergeben, damit geprüft wird, welche Felder in Ihrer Konfiguration autoritativ sind.

subject: Restored backup reintroduced users we had already anonymized
description: We restored a backup of our sandbox from two weeks ago, and now several user profiles that had been anonymized for testing are back with old names and email addresses. The audit log shows the restore completed successfully, but it seems to have overwritten only some profile fields, not all related activity. We need to understand whether restore is supposed to bring back historical personal data this way, and whether there's a reliable bulk method to re-anonymize the affected users without touching their activity records.

comments.message: We restored a backup of our sandbox from two weeks ago, and now several user profiles that had been anonymized for testing are back with old names and email addresses. The audit log shows the restore completed successfully, but it seems to have overwritten only some profile fields, not all related activity. We need to understand whether restore is supposed to bring back historical personal data this way, and whether there's a reliable bulk method to re-anonymize the affected users without touching their activity records.

A backup restore usually returns data to the point-in-time state captured in the snapshot, so old profile values coming back would be expected. If you only need to re-anonymize names and emails, you should be able to do that with a bulk user update using user ID as the key and leaving activity objects untouched. I do want to verify why only some profile-linked fields rolled back, because that may indicate inconsistent restore behavior.
Can support identify exactly which fields are restored versus excluded? We don't want another surprise on our production sandbox copies.

I don’t have the full field-level restore matrix in front of me, and I don’t want to guess. I’m escalating this to our data platform team to confirm the restore scope for user objects and whether anonymization flags should survive backup restores. For now, please hold off on another restore if preserving masked data is a compliance concern.

subject: Chcę usunąć siebie z konta, ale jestem jedynym adminem
description: Odchodzę z firmy i mam zostać usunięty z systemu, ale kiedy próbuję dezaktywować swoje konto, pojawia się błąd, że nie można usunąć ostatniego administratora. Firma chce zachować dostęp do danych, więc potrzebuję wiedzieć, co zrobić poprawnie.

comments.message: Nie mogę usunąć własnego konta, bo system blokuje ostatniego administratora. Nie mam już nikogo z rolą admina, a mój następca zaczyna dopiero za tydzień. Jak zamknąć temat zgodnie z procedurą?
W takiej sytuacji trzeba najpierw nadać uprawnienia administratora innej aktywnej osobie. Dopóki na koncie nie ma drugiego admina, system nie pozwoli Panu zdezaktywować siebie.
Czy mogę nadać rolę admina osobie, która jeszcze nie aktywowała zaproszenia?

Nie, nowa osoba musi najpierw zaakceptować zaproszenie i zalogować się przynajmniej raz. Jeśli to niemożliwe przed Pana odejściem, możemy tymczasowo odroczyć dezaktywację konta albo — po autoryzacji firmy — ręcznie przenieść własność konta na wskazaną osobę, gdy tylko aktywuje dostęp.

subject: Invited users can't activate accounts and Zendesk sync is failing on organization updates
description: We invited a new support team after enabling SSO, but some of them never finish account activation even though they were assigned in our identity provider. On top of that, our Zendesk integration is no longer updating organizations and is creating duplicate org records with no ARR values. This started right after we changed the integration credentials to a different admin during onboarding.

comments.message: The new hires appear in the app as pending, but clicking through from SSO just lands them on a blank activation page. Meanwhile, Zendesk ticket sync still works, but organization updates create duplicates and ARR is empty on the new org records.

The blank activation page can happen if invited users were provisioned before accepting the product terms, while the Zendesk problem points to a field mapping or permission issue on the new admin connection. Please clear browser cache for the activation problem and remap the ARR field in the Zendesk integration.
Cache didn't change anything, and ARR was already mapped. The sync log says 'organization lookup key not accessible' on updates.

Thanks, that log changes the diagnosis. If the lookup key isn't accessible, the new Zendesk-connected admin likely lacks permission to the org external key, which would cause duplicates; ARR being blank may be the same permission problem. For the users stuck on activation, I suspect their provisioning state is incomplete rather than a browser issue. I'm escalating both pieces to our access and integrations specialists because they'll need to inspect provisioning and connector permissions on the backend.

subject: Need to rename primary admin without breaking SSO provisioning API
description: I need to change our primary admin from a former employee to me, but I'm worried because our Okta provisioning flow and internal scripts still use the current admin's API client. Can support confirm what changes automatically and what needs to be migrated manually? If possible, please help coordinate because we cannot risk downtime.

comments.message: Our former operations lead is still listed as the primary admin, and we want to switch that to my account today. We also use the SCIM and user management APIs through a client he originally created. Will changing the primary admin break those integrations?
Changing the primary admin should not affect your APIs. The integrations are workspace-based, so you can go ahead and make the account change from the Admin page.
We already did, and now our SCIM calls to create users are failing with 403, while some older scripts still work. So it definitely changed something.

Thanks for testing that. I looked deeper and the SCIM client in your tenant is actually owned by the deactivated admin, so my earlier guidance was incomplete. Older scripts are working because they use a separate personal token. I'm escalating this to our identity team to transfer the SCIM app ownership and preserve the existing client ID if possible. In the meantime, please avoid deleting the former admin record completely.

subject: Charged for sandbox environment that was only enabled for testing
description: I enabled a sandbox last month to test a new ticket form and custom routing setup, but we only needed it for a few days. Our latest invoice includes a full month's Sandbox Environment charge. I deleted the test forms afterward, so I assumed the sandbox was off. Did I miss a separate configuration step, and can that fee be removed?

comments.message: The sandbox was used around June 3-5 for testing forms and assignment rules. We removed the test config, but the invoice dated June 30 still has the sandbox line item.

Deleting forms and routing rules inside the sandbox doesn't deactivate the sandbox subscription itself. That setting is managed in Billing, not in the configuration area where the test assets were created.
Okay, but I don't see a way to tell whether it's still active now. Can you verify it?

I can confirm the sandbox add-on is still marked active on your account. To prevent future charges, go to Account Settings > Environments and disable the sandbox, then remove the add-on from Billing > Manage Plan. I can't directly remove the past charge from support, so I've escalated your refund request to billing for review.

subject: API retornando registros duplicados na exportação de contatos
description: Estou usando a API para extrair nossos contatos para o data warehouse e, desde ontem, a resposta do endpoint /v2/contacts está trazendo vários registros duplicados quando uso paginação com updated_since. Isso bagunçou nossa carga incremental e criou milhares de linhas repetidas no banco. Preciso entender se houve alguma mudança no comportamento da API e como posso fazer a deduplicação correta sem perder atualizações reais.

comments.message: Estamos paginando com limit=500 e salvando o último updated_at processado. Mesmo assim, a página seguinte volta com alguns IDs já vistos. Antes isso não acontecia.

Olá! Em alguns cenários, registros atualizados durante a paginação podem reaparecer entre páginas quando o filtro usa updated_since. Para evitar duplicidade na ingestão, recomendamos ordenar por updated_at ascendente e fazer a deduplicação por id no destino, mantendo a versão com updated_at mais recente. Também vale usar um pequeno overlap na janela incremental, por exemplo 2 a 5 minutos, justamente para não perder alterações no limite do corte.
Entendi. Mas no nosso caso a ordenação já está ascendente e mesmo assim o mesmo contato vem duas vezes com o mesmo updated_at exato.

Obrigado pelo detalhe. Nesse caso, isso não é o comportamento esperado. Verifiquei internamente e houve um incidente curto no índice de contatos que pode ter afetado respostas entre 08:10 e 09:00 BRT. O problema já foi corrigido. Para limpar sua base, sugiro reprocessar esse intervalo e fazer um merge por id, preservando o registro mais recente por updated_at. Se quiser, também posso abrir uma solicitação para nosso time de API confirmar se sua conta foi impactada especificamente.

subject: Automation didn't send when deal moved stages
description: I built an automation that should send an internal Slack alert whenever a deal moves to 'Legal Review,' but it didn’t fire for two deals today. I’m new to setting up workflows in your system, so I may have misunderstood how trigger conditions work. I’d like help troubleshooting why it didn’t run and also a simple explanation of the difference between trigger-based and scheduled automations.

comments.message: I built an automation that should send an internal Slack alert whenever a deal moves to 'Legal Review,' but it didn’t fire for two deals today. I’m new to setting up workflows in your system, so I may have misunderstood how trigger conditions work. I’d like help troubleshooting why it didn’t run and also a simple explanation of the difference between trigger-based and scheduled automations.

Sure — trigger-based automations run immediately when a record changes and matches the condition, while scheduled automations run on a time-based schedule, like every hour or every day, and then look for matching records. For the missed alerts, please open the workflow and check three things: whether the trigger is 'Deal updated' versus a scheduled rule, whether the condition says 'stage changed to Legal Review' instead of just 'stage is Legal Review,' and whether the Slack connection is still authorized.

I checked. It was actually set to 'stage is Legal Review' under a scheduled automation that runs nightly. That explains why we didn’t get alerts right away. But one of the deals entered that stage yesterday and still no Slack message came through overnight.

That helps. Since it’s scheduled, the rule only evaluates during the nightly run, and depending on your plan, scheduled jobs can skip records if they were updated outside the selected pipeline filter or if the Slack step failed authorization. Please switch it to a trigger-based automation if you want immediate alerts. Then test with one sample deal after reconnecting Slack under Settings > Integrations. If the overnight deal still matters, check the workflow run history for that record ID; if you don’t see an entry, the pipeline or owner filter likely excluded it.
I found a pipeline filter on the workflow that excluded our enterprise pipeline. That was the missed piece. I’ve updated it and changed the workflow to trigger-based.

Perfect — that would definitely prevent those deals from being picked up. To summarize: trigger-based is best for immediate notifications, 'changed to' is safer than 'is' when you care about the moment of movement, and filters can silently exclude records if they don’t match. I’d recommend doing one controlled test in each pipeline you use so you can confirm the workflow behaves as expected before relying on it in production.

subject: OAuth app asks users to log in repeatedly and token exchange fails
description: We're building an internal app against your API using OAuth. Users can authenticate in the browser, but after consent the callback sometimes returns an error that the authorization code is invalid. Other times the token exchange works, but the access token only works for a few minutes before API calls start failing. On top of that, some users are being asked to log in again every time they reconnect the app.

comments.message: We registered one OAuth app for both US and EU workspaces and set the redirect URI to our common callback. The users who get prompted repeatedly are mostly in our EU tenant. API errors after token exchange are 401 invalid_grant or invalid_token depending on when it fails.
This sounds like your users may be clearing cookies or using the wrong redirect URI. Please ask them to use a consistent browser and make sure your callback URL matches exactly.
The callback matches exactly, and the pattern is region-specific. Could it be because EU users authorize against eu.app while our token exchange hits api.us?

Yes, that would do it. Your auth code is issued by the regional identity host and must be exchanged against the matching regional token endpoint. If EU users authorize on the EU login domain but your backend posts the code to the US token URL, you'll see invalid_grant. The repeated logins are also consistent with sending users to the wrong regional authorize host, which prevents session reuse. Split your OAuth configuration by region and store issuer-specific token endpoints.

subject: Salesforce sync creates duplicate contacts overnight
description: I'm seeing duplicate contacts being created in our CRM every night after the Salesforce integration runs. We turned on two-way sync last week, and since then several records are being duplicated instead of matched to existing contacts. I already checked that email is our matching field, but it keeps happening for a subset of records. Can you help me figure out what is causing this and how to stop it?

comments.message: The duplicates seem to be created between 1-3 AM UTC during the scheduled sync. It mostly affects leads that were converted to contacts in Salesforce earlier the same day.

Thanks for the details. This usually happens when the integration sees the converted lead and the new contact as separate objects before the external ID mapping updates. Please check whether lead-to-contact conversion is enabled under your Salesforce connector settings and whether 'create if no exact match' is turned on.
Yes, lead conversion sync is enabled, and 'create if no exact match' was on. We turned that off for a test last night, and duplicates dropped but one record failed to sync entirely.

That points to the matching logic. I recommend keeping 'create if no exact match' off and adding Salesforce Contact ID as a secondary mapping key alongside email. I also cleared the stale sync cache on your connector from our side, which should prevent the converted lead/contact pair from being treated as new records tonight. Monitor the next run, and if any records fail again, send me one example ID so we can inspect the sync log.

subject: Google-Kalender-Integration legt Termine eine Stunde verschoben an
description: Seit der Umstellung auf Sommerzeit werden Meetings, die aus Ihrer Plattform an Google Kalender gesendet werden, bei mehreren Kollegen eine Stunde zu früh eingetragen. In der App ist unsere Zeitzone auf Berlin gestellt. Ich muss wissen, ob das ein bekannter Fehler in der Integration ist oder ob wir etwas falsch konfiguriert haben.

comments.message: Der Termin steht bei uns intern auf 15:00 Uhr, in Google Kalender landet er aber bei manchen Nutzern um 14:00 Uhr. Betroffen sind nur Termine, die über Ihre Integration erstellt werden.

Das klingt nach einer Diskrepanz zwischen Konto-Zeitzone und Benutzer-Zeitzone in der Google-Kalender-Integration. Bitte prüfen Sie nicht nur die globale Zeitzone Ihres Arbeitsbereichs, sondern auch unter Profil > Sprache & Region die persönliche Zeitzone der betroffenen Benutzer. Wenn dort noch CET statt Europe/Berlin gespeichert ist, kann es nach der Sommerzeitumstellung zu Verschiebungen kommen.
Bei zwei betroffenen Nutzern stand tatsächlich noch CET. Reicht es, das einfach zu ändern?

Ja. Ändern Sie die persönliche Zeitzone auf Europe/Berlin und verbinden Sie danach die Google-Kalender-Integration für diese Nutzer einmal neu, damit die OAuth-Einstellungen aktualisiert werden. Bereits falsch angelegte Termine werden dadurch nicht automatisch korrigiert, neue Einträge sollten aber wieder mit der richtigen Uhrzeit erstellt werden.

subject: Can someone explain restoring deleted records from a bad bulk update?
description: One of our admins ran a bulk update this morning and several customer records disappeared from a filtered view. We’re not sure if they were deleted or just overwritten, and the junior admin on my team is asking me what to check first. I need step-by-step guidance so I can coach them through troubleshooting it.

comments.message: One of our admins ran a bulk update this morning and several customer records disappeared from a filtered view. We’re not sure if they were deleted or just overwritten, and the junior admin on my team is asking me what to check first. I need step-by-step guidance so I can coach them through troubleshooting it.

Start by checking the import or bulk action history to confirm whether the action was an update or a delete. Then compare one missing record by searching directly for its ID or email, not just the saved filter. If the records still exist, a field used by the view may have changed. If they were deleted, go to the recycle bin or deleted records log and restore a small sample first. For coaching your junior admin, I’d have them document: what action ran, what mapping or rule was used, and which fields the filtered view depends on.
If the values were overwritten instead of deleted, is there any rollback option?

There isn’t a universal one-click rollback for all bulk updates, but you can usually reverse it by exporting the affected records, identifying the changed fields in the audit log, and reimporting corrected values. If you have field history enabled, that will help reconstruct the original data. If you send the job ID to support, we can confirm whether your plan includes assisted rollback tools.

subject: Parent-child account import failing after relationship rule update
description: We imported a new batch of accounts this morning and all child accounts were rejected with relationship validation errors. The only recent change on our side was updating the account hierarchy settings so that subsidiaries must reference a parent by external account ID instead of account name. The source file was adjusted, but the import summary still says the parent reference is invalid for hundreds of rows. We need help figuring out whether the relationship field is mapped wrong or whether existing parent records aren't configured correctly.

comments.message: Every child account row failed with "parent reference not found" after we changed hierarchy settings. The CSV now contains the parent external ID, not the parent name. Parents already exist in the system.

Thanks for the detail. After changing the relationship rule, the parent lookup field on the import must be mapped specifically to the relationship reference expected by the object configuration, not just included as a normal text column. Did you remap the "Parent Account" relationship field to use "External Account ID" as the lookup source in the import wizard?
I mapped the CSV column to our custom field "Parent External ID," but I don't think I touched the actual Parent Account relationship mapping.

That's the issue. Storing the parent ID in a text field won't create the relationship. Re-run the import and map the CSV column to the "Parent Account" relationship field, then choose the lookup option that matches the configured unique field "External Account ID." Your data itself may be fine; this is an import mapping mismatch caused by the configuration change.

subject: Archived opportunities reappearing in pipeline reports
description: I'm seeing closed and archived opportunities show back up in our active pipeline dashboard after we changed the pipeline configuration last week. We added a new stage set for our EMEA team and adjusted some default filters, but now opportunity records that should stay archived are included in exports and widgets tied to the old pipeline. It looks like either the data state was altered during the reconfiguration or our report logic is reading the wrong status values. Can someone help us figure out what changed?

comments.message: After we split our sales pipeline into NA and EMEA, archived opportunities started showing in the "Open Pipeline" report. We didn't unarchive them manually. Is this a bug or did the new stage mapping change their status?

Most likely the pipeline split changed the report configuration rather than the underlying opportunity data. When new pipelines are created, saved reports sometimes inherit stage filters but not archived-state exclusions. Can you confirm whether the report is filtering on "stage is not closed" instead of "record status = active"?
The report is filtering by stages only. It includes every non-lost stage from both pipelines. I assumed archived records wouldn't qualify anyway.

Archived records can still retain one of those stage values, so they will appear unless the report explicitly excludes archived status. Please update the report filter to add "Record Status = Active" and republish the dashboard widget. If you still see archived rows after that, export one example record ID and we can check whether its archive flag failed to persist during the pipeline reconfiguration.

subject: Password reset sent, but user still gets 'account not found'
description: I created a new user yesterday and sent a password reset because they never got the welcome email. The reset email arrives, but when they follow it the page says the account can't be found. In Admin I can see the user profile, so I'm confused whether the account was only partially created.

comments.message: I created a new user yesterday and sent a password reset because they never got the welcome email. The reset email arrives, but when they follow it the page says the account can't be found. In Admin I can see the user profile, so I'm confused whether the account was only partially created.
It sounds like the user exists in the directory but may not have a completed authentication record yet. Can you confirm whether the profile status shows Pending Invite, Active, or Provisioned by SSO?
It says Pending Invite. We do use SSO for most employees, but this person is an external consultant so I made them as email/password.

Thanks. In that case the reset link should not be used before the initial invite acceptance creates the login credential. I recommend deleting the user and recreating them as a fresh email/password account.
I'd rather not delete them because I've already assigned projects and permissions.

Understood. Then please leave the current record in place and I’ll escalate this to our authentication team, because a Pending Invite user with valid reset mail but no resolvable auth record suggests the invite token and login record are out of sync. Include the user email and profile ID in your reply and we'll investigate without requiring recreation.

subject: Bulk status update stuck halfway and data is inconsistent
description: I ran a bulk update to change 12,000 tickets from "Pending Review" to "Closed - Imported" after a migration. The job showed 100% complete, but only about half the records actually changed, and the audit log lists the operation as successful. Some views show mixed statuses depending on where I look. I need help determining whether this is a UI caching issue or a failed write that left the dataset inconsistent.

comments.message: I ran a bulk update to change 12,000 tickets from "Pending Review" to "Closed - Imported" after a migration. The job showed 100% complete, but only about half the records actually changed, and the audit log lists the operation as successful.

I checked the bulk job metadata and it does show a completed status, but only 6,143 status-change events were committed to the database. The remainder failed after the worker hit a row-lock timeout, and the job incorrectly marked itself successful instead of partial failure. That's a bug on our side. I can't safely rerun the whole update without risking overwriting newer edits, so I've escalated this to our data team to generate a retry only for unchanged records from that batch.
Is there a way for us to identify which records were skipped before your data team gets back to us?

Yes. I've attached a temporary view to your account called "Migration status mismatches" that filters records updated by that bulk job ID where the current status is still "Pending Review." It should give you the skipped set, although please treat it as read-only until the retry plan is confirmed.

subject: Prorated charge question after increasing agent seats for a new queue setup
description: I added 12 agents last week while setting up a dedicated Escalations queue with its own permissions and views. The invoice shows a prorated seat charge plus a higher platform fee, and I'm not sure whether the queue configuration itself triggered an additional plan cost or if it's only the seats. Can someone explain what changed and whether I can assign those users without upgrading the whole account?

comments.message: We created a new queue, custom roles, and added 12 light agents, but the invoice jumped more than expected. I need to understand what exactly caused the increase.

I reviewed the billing events on your account. The new queue and views themselves are not billable, but converting those 12 users from requester status into agent roles created a prorated seat charge. In addition, your total active agents crossed the threshold where your contract includes the Collaboration pack, which added the platform fee.
So if I change some of them back to requesters and keep access through the queue email, would that remove the extra charge next month?

Yes, if those users don't need agent permissions in the product configuration, converting them back to requesters before the next billing cycle would reduce seat-based charges. The queue can still receive email, but only agents can log in to work tickets directly. I can also send you a report of which users triggered the role change if that helps with cleanup.

subject: Charged for extra records after failed deduplication job
description: We ran the deduplication tool to merge duplicate leads, but instead of reducing our count it seems to have created additional records in one segment. Our invoice now shows a higher usage tier based on record volume, and reporting is broken because some merged leads still exist as separate entries. I need someone to review both the billing impact and whether the duplicate data can be corrected.

comments.message: The deduplication job appears to have increased our record count, and now we've been charged for a higher usage tier. Some leads were merged, but others were duplicated again. Can support fix the records and the invoice?

If your record count increased, that usually means the job created net-new records from unmatched rows. The usage-based invoice would still be correct in that case. You may want to manually delete the duplicates.
We did not import anything new. This was your native dedupe tool, and the duplicate records share the same internal source IDs. Manual cleanup across thousands of leads isn't realistic.

Understood. I've escalated the deduplication run to our product support queue with the job reference so they can determine whether the merge process duplicated records incorrectly. I can't modify the current invoice today, but I've placed the billing charge under dispute pending that review. If the tool malfunctioned, billing can reassess the usage tier after the data count is corrected.

subject: Downgraded plan but still paying for admin-only accounts
description: We downgraded from Enterprise to Professional because we only need basic features now. During cleanup, I converted several users to limited admin-only accounts for audit purposes, but the bill still counts them as full paid seats. I need to know whether admin-only users are billable on this new plan and if there’s a cheaper way to retain access.

comments.message: There are 9 users who only need to manage permissions and user access, not use the product day to day. On Enterprise they used to be exempt under our agreement. After the downgrade, the current bill counts all 9 as paid seats.

Professional doesn’t support free admin-only licenses. Any enabled user, including an admin, is billable on that plan. The previous exemption was specific to your Enterprise contract and ended at downgrade.
That’s disappointing. What are our options?

You can either keep them as paid users, deactivate them and assign one or two shared admins internally, or move back to Enterprise at your prior rate if needed. Unfortunately there isn’t a role setting that changes billing on Professional. If you want, sales can discuss alternatives.

subject: Changed owner on subscription but renewal charged the wrong card after automation update
description: I transferred account ownership from our former operations manager to me yesterday and updated the renewal approvals so invoices over $5,000 route to finance automatically. This morning our annual renewal still charged the old corporate card that should have been removed, and the billing notification went to the prior owner instead of the new finance workflow. I need to switch the billing contact, reverse the charge if possible, and make sure future renewal events trigger the right approval automation.

comments.message: The account owner is now my user, but the renewal charge still hit the old Visa ending in 2044. Also, the notification email went to Jenna, who no longer works here.

If you changed the account owner, the payment method should normally update automatically with that profile, so this may just be a delay in the billing system. Please wait a few hours and see whether the email routing corrects itself.
Waiting doesn’t help with a completed charge. Ownership and payment method are separate settings in your admin panel, which is why I’m contacting support.

You’re right, and I should have clarified that. I can see the owner transfer completed, but the stored default card and billing contact were not changed. I can’t reverse the settled charge directly from support, so I’m escalating this to billing operations to review a refund and re-bill option. I’ve already updated the billing contact to your address, and your invoice approval workflow is now pointed at finance@northpeak.co for future renewal events.

subject: Deleted contacts still appearing in quarterly pipeline report
description: I deleted about 1,200 duplicate contacts from our CRM sync on Monday, and they no longer show up in the contact list. But when I run the quarterly pipeline report, the old owner counts and stage totals still include those deleted records. I already refreshed the report and tried cloning it, but the numbers are still inflated. I need to send this report to leadership today and need to know whether the deletions are supposed to update historical reporting or if something is stuck.

comments.message: I deleted the duplicates from the synced dataset, but the pipeline report still counts them. Is the report cache stuck somewhere?

Thanks for flagging this. Deleting records removes them from live views, but some reports use a materialized reporting snapshot that refreshes every 4 hours. I checked your workspace and the snapshot tied to the quarterly pipeline report has not refreshed since before Monday's cleanup.
Can that be forced? The current totals are definitely wrong for today's presentation.

Yes — I triggered a manual rebuild of the reporting index for that dataset. It should finish within 20-30 minutes, and once it completes the deleted contacts will no longer be included in current-period owner and stage totals. Historical reports exported before the rebuild won't change, but the live report will.

subject: Guest user can sign in but suddenly has admin menu
description: I invited an external consultant as a guest last week. Today she told me she can see parts of the admin menu and user list after logging in. I checked her account and it still says Guest in the user directory. I need to understand if she actually has elevated access or if the UI is wrong.

comments.message: Her email is from a vendor domain and she should only have access to one project. She can sign in fine, but she sent me a screenshot showing Settings and Users in the left nav.
Guests cannot access admin functions, so it is probably just cached navigation. Ask her to log out and back in.
She did that already. The menu is still there, and this is concerning from a permissions standpoint.

Understood. I tested this against a similar setup and there is a display issue on some guest accounts where admin navigation appears if they were added to a team with inherited configuration visibility. The menu should not grant actual User Administration rights, but that still needs verification. As a precaution, remove her from any broad teams, confirm her project-specific group assignment, and have her try opening Users directly. I’m escalating this to our permissions team with the screenshot because if the UI is exposing admin navigation to guests, it’s a product bug even if access is denied underneath.

subject: SSO login loop after domain migration
description: I’m the IT admin for our workspace, and since we changed our company email domain yesterday, about 40 users are stuck in a login loop. They authenticate through Okta, get sent back to your sign-in page, and then it asks them to log in again. I already confirmed the users are active in our account and can access other SSO apps. We have executives blocked from getting into dashboards this morning, so I need this escalated if it can’t be fixed right away.

comments.message: We switched from northbridge.io to northbridgehealth.com and now SSO keeps bouncing users back to the login page. I tested in Chrome incognito and the same thing happens. Can someone look at this urgently?

Thanks for the details. I can see successful SAML assertions reaching our side, but the return URL appears to still be tied to the old domain mapping for your workspace. This is affecting authentication handoff, so I’m escalating this to our identity engineering team as a priority. In the meantime, users who still have local password logins enabled may be able to sign in directly with email and password.
Most of our users are SSO-only, so the workaround helps only a few people. Do you need anything else from me?

I’ve attached your affected workspace ID, the domain change you mentioned, and the SAML trace notes to the escalation. No further action is needed from you right now unless the team asks for updated metadata. I’ll keep this ticket open and marked urgent until identity engineering confirms the domain mapping has been corrected.

subject: Alterei o e-mail principal da conta e os convites pararam de funcionar
description: Eu atualizei o e-mail principal do administrador da conta porque a pessoa anterior saiu da empresa. Depois disso, os convites enviados para novos usuários chegam com link inválido, e alguns convites antigos redirecionam para uma página em branco. Não sei se a troca do e-mail do administrador quebrou alguma coisa na conta ou se é coincidência, mas precisamos adicionar pessoas ainda hoje.

comments.message: Mudei o e-mail principal do administrador da conta e desde então os convites para novos usuários vêm com link inválido ou página em branco.

Olá! Obrigado por relatar. A mudança do e-mail principal não deveria afetar os convites. Você pode me enviar um exemplo de convite, com horário de envio, e informar se isso ocorre com todos os destinatários?
Sim, está acontecendo com todos. Enviei convites às 09:12, 09:18 e 09:31, todos com o mesmo problema. O link abre uma página em branco com URL de convite.

Perfeito, isso ajuda. Consegui reproduzir internamente o erro ao gerar convites em uma conta que teve o e-mail principal alterado recentemente, então parece ser um bug real relacionado à mudança de conta. Suspendi a hipótese de erro de configuração do seu lado e já abri um incidente para engenharia. Como alternativa temporária, posso orientar a adicionar os usuários por provisionamento CSV se isso atender sua urgência.

subject: User was reactivated but still missing project memberships
description: I reactivated a sales manager this morning after leave, and her login works again, but she's no longer in the regional projects she used to manage. I can add her back one by one, but there are more than 40 projects. Is there a way to restore prior memberships, or can this be escalated to someone who can recover them?

comments.message: Her user account history shows she was deactivated for only 12 days. We expected her previous access to come back automatically.

Reactivation restores sign-in access, but project memberships are not always reinstated automatically, especially if the projects are permission-scoped. There isn't a self-serve bulk restore for previous memberships. If you send me the user's email and one or two sample project names, I can escalate this to our data team to check whether her prior memberships can be recovered from audit history.
The user is ava.moreno@redcliffsales.com. Sample projects are West Enterprise Pipeline and LATAM Q4 Expansion.

I've filed the escalation with Ava's account and the sample projects, requesting a review of whether her historical memberships across the 40+ projects can be restored from audit records. If recovery isn't possible, the team should also advise on the fastest bulk reassignment option.

subject: Need to update user roles via API but requests return 403
description: I'm trying to automate role assignment for newly created users through your API. We can create the user record successfully, but when our script attempts to promote them from Viewer to Admin, the API returns 403 forbidden. I'm using an API token generated under my account, and my account is an org admin in the UI. What permission are we missing?

comments.message: The failing call is PATCH /api/v1/users/4821 with a body that updates the role field. The same token works for listing users and creating them.

A 403 on role changes usually means the token does not have enough scope. In our platform, user creation and user role management are split: tokens can have user:write without admin:write. Please go to Settings > API Tokens, edit the token, and make sure admin:write is enabled. Also note that only Owner-level admins can assign the Admin role through the API.
That explains it. My account is Admin but not Owner, and the token only had user:write. We regenerated it under our Owner account with admin:write and the same request now succeeds.

Perfect. Your automation should work going forward with the Owner-issued token for role elevation. If you want to reduce risk, you can keep your regular provisioning token separate and reserve the elevated token just for role changes.

subject: Duplicate charges after restoring archived workspace
description: I restored an archived workspace last week so we could export some historical records, and now our account has been charged for both the restored workspace and our current one. At the same time, the restored workspace is missing several customer records from March that we specifically needed for an audit. I need to understand the invoice and get the missing data issue addressed, because finance is asking why we were billed twice and compliance needs the export by Friday.

comments.message: I restored the archived workspace only temporarily to pull old records, but invoice #44821 shows an extra full-seat charge. Also, the restored workspace is missing March customer records. Can you fix the billing and help recover the missing data?

Thanks for flagging this. Restoring an archived workspace can trigger prorated billing if it becomes active with paid seats, so the extra charge may be legitimate. For the missing records, can you confirm whether those records were deleted before the archive was created?
We did not delete them. The archive was taken on April 2, and the March records were definitely there then. Also, no one added seats manually after the restore.

I reviewed the invoice timeline and I can see the restored workspace reactivated 12 seats automatically, which caused the prorated charge. I've submitted a billing adjustment request since the workspace was re-opened for export-only use. On the data side, I can't restore missing historical records from support tools, so I'm escalating this to our data operations team with the archive date and affected month. They'll verify the snapshot integrity and contact you separately. For now, I’ve placed the billing credit request on the account so finance has a record.

subject: Downgraded seats but old users still getting billed in approval workflow
description: I reduced our workspace from 25 paid seats to 18 last week because several contractors left, and I also updated our onboarding workflow to automatically deactivate users when HR marks them as terminated. The problem is that the invoice generated today still shows 25 seats, and three deactivated users were somehow re-added by an automation step tied to our Okta sync. I need to understand whether the billing is based on active seats, provisioned seats, or users touched by automations, and I need this fixed before finance closes the month.

comments.message: I changed the seat count on April 3 and expected prorated billing, but invoice INV-48211 still lists 25 seats. Our offboarding automation should remove access, yet I can see three former users back in the account as suspended members.

Thanks for the details. Billing is based on paid seats assigned during the billing period, and automated reprovisioning from your identity sync can cause seats to remain billable if the users are still mapped in Okta. I checked your workspace and can see the seat reduction request was scheduled for the next renewal instead of immediate proration.
That was not obvious in the UI. Can you make the reduction effective now and stop the workflow from adding those people back?

I’ve applied a mid-cycle adjustment for 7 seats and submitted a credit request for the difference on INV-48211. For the re-added users, your offboarding workflow deactivates them in our app, but the SCIM/Okta provisioning rule is re-creating them because they’re still assigned to the app in Okta. I disabled the ‘reinvite suspended users’ automation in your account and recommend removing that Okta assignment rule. The invoice credit should appear within 24 hours.

subject: Cannot log in after IP allowlist update and regional workspace change
description: I moved our workspace region setting to EU and tightened the IP allowlist at the same time. Now our remote team in Spain and Poland cannot log in, while office users can. I can still access the admin panel from headquarters, but the team keeps getting a generic access blocked message before they even enter MFA.

comments.message: We updated the workspace to EU hosting and narrowed the IP allowlist to our office ranges. Since then, remote employees are blocked at login with 'access denied by policy.' They don't even get to MFA.

This is expected if their home IPs are not on the allowlist. The regional workspace move is probably unrelated to the immediate block. Please review Security > Network Access and either add your VPN egress IPs, expand the allowed ranges, or disable IP restrictions temporarily so remote staff can authenticate. Also confirm the policy is applied only to admins if that was your intent.
I thought the allowlist only protected the admin area. It appears to be set to all users. If I switch it to admins only, will regular users regain access immediately?

Yes, changing the scope from 'All users' to 'Admins only' should take effect right away for new login attempts. If you still want stronger control for employees, routing them through a corporate VPN and allowlisting the VPN IPs is the safer configuration.

subject: Campo personalizado recém-criado não aparece no construtor de relatórios
description: Criei um campo personalizado novo ontem à tarde para segmentar contas por parceiro, mas ele não aparece no construtor de relatórios, embora já esteja disponível no formulário de ticket e preenchido em vários registros. Esperei a sincronização noturna e ainda assim nada. Parece que a indexação para relatórios não ocorreu. Gostaria de confirmar se isso é atraso normal ou um problema no produto.

comments.message: O campo se chama “Parceiro de Implantação”. É do tipo lista suspensa e já consigo usar normalmente na operação, mas ele não aparece nem como dimensão nem como filtro em Relatórios.
Olá! Campos personalizados costumam levar até 24 horas para aparecer em Relatórios. Como você criou ontem, eu sugeriria aguardar mais um pouco antes de concluirmos que é bug.
Já passaram mais de 24 horas e inclusive criei tickets novos preenchendo esse campo para testar. Continua invisível só no módulo de Relatórios.

Certo, então isso foge do prazo esperado. Vou encaminhar para investigação porque pode haver falha no pipeline de indexação de novos campos para o schema analítico. Se você quiser, também posso associar o ID do campo ao chamado para acelerar a análise.

subject: Former employee can still log in and we were charged after cancellation
description: We cancelled one of our add-on modules at the end of last month and removed a former employee before that, but this morning I noticed we were still billed for the add-on and the ex-employee was apparently able to log in through an old bookmark. I can’t access the admin billing page myself because my login loops back to the sign-in screen after MFA. Please help me stop the charge and make sure only current staff can access the account.

comments.message: We cancelled one of our add-on modules at the end of last month and removed a former employee before that, but this morning I noticed we were still billed for the add-on and the ex-employee was apparently able to log in through an old bookmark. I can’t access the admin billing page myself because my login loops back to the sign-in screen after MFA. Please help me stop the charge and make sure only current staff can access the account.
For the login issue, please try clearing cookies or using an incognito window. Billing charges are usually expected until the end of the cycle.
I already tried three browsers, and this doesn’t address why a removed user could still access the workspace or why the canceled add-on renewed.

I understand. I can see the add-on cancellation request was not finalized because it was left in pending state, so it renewed automatically. I’m not able to reverse that myself, so I’m escalating to Billing for a possible credit. For access, the former employee still had an active SSO session even though the app user was deactivated; I’ve now revoked all active sessions for that user. Your own MFA redirect issue appears tied to an outdated enforcement policy, and I’ve sent that to our access team to restore your admin login.

subject: Can't log in after password reset and API key disappeared
description: I reset my password because I was locked out, and after getting back into the app I noticed the API key our script uses is no longer visible in Settings. The script now fails and I also intermittently get bounced back to the login screen when trying to open the developer page. Can you help figure out whether this is expected or if my account is in a bad state?

comments.message: This morning I used the password reset link to regain access. After that, our nightly export started failing with 403 invalid key, and in the UI the API key section is blank for me. Also, when I click Developer Settings, sometimes it redirects me to sign in again.
Password resets usually revoke active sessions for security, so please log out everywhere, clear cookies, and log back in. Your API key should still be there after that.
I did that in Chrome and Firefox. I can log in, but Developer Settings still reloads and then sends me back to the sign-in page. The key is still missing.

Understood. That sounds less like expected behavior and more like an access bug on the developer settings page after password recovery. I have reproduced a similar loop in a test account with restricted developer permissions. I've escalated this for investigation; meanwhile, an org admin may be able to generate a workspace API key from Admin > Integrations if your plan includes it.

subject: Changed username format and mobile app stopped recognizing users
description: We standardized everyone’s usernames to first.last format this morning. The web app mostly reflects the changes, but the mobile app still shows old usernames and some people are getting sync failures or being logged out repeatedly. I need help figuring out whether this is just cached data or a problem with the account updates.

comments.message: Example: jsmith became john.smith. On desktop it looks correct, but iOS still shows jsmith in mentions and one user gets “token subject mismatch” when trying to sync.

The mobile app can lag behind username changes because it caches profile references. Please have the affected users force close the app, sign out, and sign back in. If they still see old usernames, reinstalling the app should clear the cached profile data.
Reinstall fixed the display issue for one person, but the user with token subject mismatch still can’t sync after reinstall.

That specific error suggests the mobile token is tied to the pre-change username identifier while the account record now expects the new one. I’ve revoked that user’s mobile sessions server-side so a fresh token can be issued on next login. If sync still fails after that, reply with the device model and app version and I’ll pass it to mobile engineering.

subject: Cannot remove deactivated user from approval workflow
description: I deactivated a former employee in our workspace yesterday, but her name is still showing up as an approver on expense requests and we can't publish an updated workflow because the system says the user is referenced in an active rule. I need to fully remove her access and get the workflow fixed today because reimbursements are stuck.

comments.message: I already checked that the account is deactivated under Admin > People, but she still appears in the Finance Approval step. Is there a way to force-remove her from all rules?

You're right that deactivating the user only blocks sign-in; it doesn't automatically remove them from workflow assignments. Please go to Settings > Workflows > Expense Approval, edit the Finance Approval step, and replace that user with an active approver or a group. If the editor won't let you save because the rule is locked by existing pending requests, I'll escalate this to our workflow support team to detach the deactivated user from the backend references.
The workflow editor shows an error when I try to save: "Cannot update approver while requests are in progress." We have 14 requests pending.

Thanks for confirming. I've escalated this to our workflow specialists because the pending requests are preventing the user reference from being cleared in-app. In the meantime, I've noted that the deactivated user must be removed from all approval paths and that reimbursements are blocked. You'll receive an update on this escalation as soon as they review the affected workflow and pending items.

subject: Import says successful, but records are missing from list views
description: I uploaded a spreadsheet of new leads and the import completed without errors, but when I open the Sales leads list I only see some of them. I’m not sure if the data failed to save, if filters are hiding the records, or if I imported them into the wrong object entirely. I need help troubleshooting and also understanding how to verify an import properly after it runs.

comments.message: The file had 1,250 rows. Import history says 1,248 created and 2 skipped. But the list view only shows 730.

That usually means the records are there but your current list has filters applied. Try clearing all filters, especially owner, lifecycle stage, and created date. Also confirm you imported into Leads rather than Contacts.
I cleared filters and I still don’t see them all. Could this be because of permissions?
Permissions wouldn’t affect list counts like that. If import history says created, they’re definitely in the system somewhere. Just refresh the browser and they should appear.

subject: Restored field values shifted into the wrong columns
description: We restored a backup after a mistaken bulk edit, and now some customer records have data in the wrong custom fields. For example, values that used to be in "Implementation Date" are showing up under "Contract Source" on a few hundred rows. This only seems to affect records created during a two-hour window yesterday. Can you investigate whether the restore mapped columns incorrectly?

comments.message: We restored a backup after a mistaken bulk edit, and now some customer records have data in the wrong custom fields. For example, values that used to be in "Implementation Date" are showing up under "Contract Source" on a few hundred rows.
I checked the restore audit and I think this happened because the field names are similar. The best fix is probably to export the affected records, correct the columns in Excel, and re-import them.
The field names are not similar, and the issue only affects records from one restore batch. Did you verify whether the restore job used field IDs or column order?

I wasn't able to verify that from my side. Since this involves restored data integrity, I'm escalating it to our database operations team with your backup job ID and a sample of affected record IDs. Please avoid further bulk edits on those rows until they review whether the restore process mismapped fields.

subject: Two people are sharing one login and now we need separate access
description: We have a bad setup that we inherited: two warehouse supervisors have been using the same login for months. We finally want to split them into individual user accounts without losing access to the dashboards and approvals tied to the shared profile. Right now, if I invite one of them separately, she can log in but doesn’t see the same permissions.

comments.message: The shared account is warehouse@brentlogistics.com. We need separate logins for Erin and Marcos, but they both need the same access the shared account had. Also, we’d like to stop anyone using the shared credential after the cutover.
The simplest approach is to change the email on the existing user to Erin’s address, then invite Marcos as a new user.
That seems risky because the approvals and audit trail would still all look like Erin. We need a cleaner way if possible.

Fair point. A better admin approach is to keep the shared account temporarily, document its current role/group memberships, and create two new named users with matching permissions in User Administration. If the shared account owns dashboards or approval queues, transfer those assets to a team-owned group or to one of the named users before deactivating it. For compliance, don’t rename the shared account to impersonate one person; that would muddy the audit history. Once both new users confirm login, reset and deactivate the shared account.

subject: Bulk deactivation removed the wrong users
description: I used the bulk user administration tool to deactivate contractors whose accounts should have expired, but the filter seems to have included several full-time employees too. Those employees lost access in the middle of the workday, and I need to restore the right people without accidentally re-enabling everyone. I’m also worried because the export before the change doesn’t clearly show which role each person had.

comments.message: I ran a bulk deactivation and it removed access for the wrong set of users. Some active employees were included along with the contractors I meant to deactivate.
You can just import the user list again and that should fix it. If the users come back, their access will return.
That’s too risky. We have users with different admin levels, and I don’t want to restore everyone blindly. Is there a way to identify exactly which accounts were changed by the bulk action?

I don’t see a direct way to isolate that from my side, so I’m escalating this to our product support team to review the bulk action logs. In the meantime, you can check the audit log manually and reactivate users one by one from the Users page, but I can’t confirm whether their previous roles will be preserved.

subject: SSO users lost CRM sync after plan downgrade
description: I downgraded our account from Enterprise to Pro last Friday, and since then two things broke at once: our Salesforce integration stopped syncing new leads, and several sales reps who normally log in through Okta are now getting a message that their accounts don’t have access. I’m also confused because we were still charged for 25 Enterprise seats on the latest invoice even though I changed the subscription in admin. Can you help untangle what plan we’re actually on and get people back in?

comments.message: I downgraded our account from Enterprise to Pro last Friday, and since then two things broke at once: our Salesforce integration stopped syncing new leads, and several sales reps who normally log in through Okta are now getting a message that their accounts don’t have access. I’m also confused because we were still charged for 25 Enterprise seats on the latest invoice even though I changed the subscription in admin. Can you help untangle what plan we’re actually on and get people back in?

I checked your workspace and can confirm the subscription change was requested, but the billing cycle has not fully rolled over yet, which is why the invoice still reflects the previous seat count. I also see that SSO enforcement and the Salesforce integration are tied to your prior Enterprise entitlement. After the downgrade, SSO-only users without password credentials can be blocked, and the Salesforce connector is set to read-only until the new plan is reconciled. I’ve corrected the seat count for the upcoming invoice, issued a prorated billing adjustment request, and temporarily re-enabled the Salesforce sync for 72 hours. For the locked-out reps, please use the attached password-reset links I’ve generated so they can log in without Okta while you decide whether to keep Pro or restore Enterprise.
The reset links worked for three reps, but two others say they never got anything. Also, will the Salesforce sync shut off again automatically in 72 hours?

Yes, the temporary sync override will expire automatically unless the account is moved back to Enterprise or the integration is reconfigured to the Pro-supported import mode. I’ve resent the access emails to the two remaining users and verified they were accepted by our mail service. If they still don’t receive them, I can convert those identities manually from SSO-only to local login and escalate the missing-mail issue to our auth team.

subject: Invoice still sent to departed employee
description: Even after we changed our team structure, invoices are still being emailed to our former office manager, and our subscription was upgraded without my approval. I need her removed from billing contacts, myself added, and clarification on why we were billed at the higher tier this month.

comments.message: Please stop sending invoices to julia.mendez@ourcompany.com. She left in February. I'm now handling finance, but I'm not listed anywhere in billing. Also, this month's invoice shows we're on the Business plan instead of Standard, which increased our charge by almost 40%. Who made that change?

I updated the invoice recipient to your address and removed Julia from billing emails. As for the plan change, it looks like someone on your account upgraded from Standard to Business on March 28. I don't have the name in front of me, but upgrades are usually triggered by user activity that exceeds plan limits.
That doesn't really answer it. We need to know who changed it, and whether we can go back to Standard with a credit because no one on finance approved the higher bill.

Downgrades are available from the billing page at any time, but charges already processed are generally final. Since I already changed the invoice contact, the rest should be manageable from your side once you log in.

subject: Can’t remove former contractor from workspace
description: I’m trying to remove a contractor who finished last month, but every time I click deactivate I get a spinning loader and then nothing changes. I also noticed her name still appears as the owner of two automation rules, so I’m wondering if that is blocking the account change.

comments.message: The contractor is still listed as Editor. I tried from Chrome and Firefox, and both show the same endless spinner when deactivating the account.

If the user owns automation rules, that can sometimes prevent deletion. Please reassign those rules to another admin first, then try again. If the spinner continues, open DevTools and let me know whether there are any network errors.
I reassigned both rules, but the deactivate button still spins forever. In DevTools I see a failed POST returning 403.
A 403 means your browser is blocking the request, likely due to an extension or stale cookies. Please disable extensions and clear cookies for our site, then try again.

subject: Archived deals reappearing in dashboard totals
description: I archived 18 closed-lost deals last week as part of our quarterly cleanup. They are gone from the Deals view when I filter for active records, but the sales dashboard still counts them in total pipeline value. I need to know if the archive job is stuck or if the reporting database is out of sync.

comments.message: I archived 18 closed-lost deals last week as part of our quarterly cleanup. They are gone from the Deals view when I filter for active records, but the sales dashboard still counts them in total pipeline value. I need to know if the archive job is stuck or if the reporting database is out of sync.

Thanks — this could be either a reporting lag or a bug in how archived records are excluded from summary queries. Please send one affected dashboard URL, the date range applied, and 3 deal IDs that are still being counted.
Dashboard URL is /dashboards/forecast-q2, date range is this quarter, and example deal IDs are D-7712, D-7734, and D-7810. All three were archived on Friday afternoon.

I reproduced the issue with those IDs. The deals are correctly flagged as archived in transactional data, but the dashboard aggregate table still has them included because the archive event did not invalidate the cached rollup. I’ve escalated this to engineering as a reporting sync bug and attached your examples. As a workaround, editing the dashboard date range and switching it back may force a refresh for some widgets, but I can’t guarantee it until engineering fixes the underlying rollup problem.

subject: Need prorated refund after disabling workflow add-on
description: I removed the advanced workflow automation add-on from our account last week because we consolidated processes into another tool, but we were still invoiced for the full month yesterday. Also, one of the old automated invoice reminder flows kept firing even after I thought the add-on was canceled. Can you confirm whether the add-on was actually terminated, whether automation should still execute after cancellation, and if we can receive a prorated refund?

comments.message: I canceled the workflow automation add-on on the 12th, but billing charged us again on the 15th for the full amount. On top of that, an automation that sends reminder emails still ran this morning.

I reviewed the account and the add-on was scheduled to cancel at the end of the current billing cycle, not immediately, which is why the charge appeared. Because the feature remained active through the cycle end, the reminder workflow could still execute. We generally do not prorate mid-cycle cancellations on monthly contracts, but I can see the cancellation is now set correctly for renewal.
That wasn’t obvious in the UI at all. We specifically disabled it to stop both charges and workflow runs right away.

I understand the frustration. I can’t issue the credit directly from support, so I’ve escalated your request to our billing team to review an exception and asked them to confirm whether they can manually deactivate the remaining automation entitlement sooner.

subject: Charged after coupon applied, checkout showed full price then discount later
description: We applied a 20% renewal discount code provided by your sales team. During checkout the page first showed the discounted total, then after I updated the billing address it flashed back to full price and spun for a long time. The final receipt charged the full amount, but the admin page now shows the coupon as active on the subscription. This feels like a bug where the coupon state was lost during checkout. Can you correct the invoice?

comments.message: We applied a 20% renewal discount code provided by your sales team. During checkout the page first showed the discounted total, then after I updated the billing address it flashed back to full price and spun for a long time. The final receipt charged the full amount, but the admin page now shows the coupon as active on the subscription. This feels like a bug where the coupon state was lost during checkout. Can you correct the invoice?

I checked the subscription and the coupon is active now, but it was not attached to the invoice that finalized yesterday. I can also see a recalculation event after the billing address change, which may have removed the discount from that transaction.
