I’m not able to conclusively determine from support whether that was expected behavior or a bug.
Can you at least refund the difference if the coupon should have applied?

I’ve sent this to our billing operations team because only they can amend a finalized invoice or issue the partial refund. I included the event logs showing the discount present before checkout and absent at invoice finalization, plus a note for engineering to inspect the address-change recalculation path. We’ll follow up once billing confirms whether the invoice can be corrected.

subject: Duplicate charge after failed seat upgrade
description: I tried to add 12 seats to our workspace yesterday. The checkout spinner hung for a while and then showed an error, so I tried again. This morning I can see two charges on our company card for the same upgrade, but our account only reflects one seat increase. I need to know whether one of these charges is going to fall off or if we were actually billed twice because of a bug in your billing flow.

comments.message: I tried to add 12 seats to our workspace yesterday. The checkout spinner hung for a while and then showed an error, so I tried again. This morning I can see two charges on our company card for the same upgrade, but our account only reflects one seat increase. I need to know whether one of these charges is going to fall off or if we were actually billed twice because of a bug in your billing flow.

Thanks for flagging this. I checked your workspace billing history and I can see one posted invoice for the seat upgrade and a second payment attempt created within 18 seconds of the first. The second transaction is still in pending status from the card processor, which often happens when the checkout page errors before we receive a final success callback. I also found a frontend timeout event tied to your upgrade action, so this does look like a bug in the billing flow rather than an intentional second invoice.
Can you confirm whether the pending charge will disappear on its own? Our finance team is asking if they need to dispute it.

You should not dispute it yet. I’ve voided the duplicate authorization on our side and attached the processor reference to your account notes. In most banks it drops within 3-5 business days. I’ve also opened a bug report with our payments engineering team for the timeout issue that let the retry create a second authorization. If the pending charge is still visible after 5 business days, reply here and we can issue a formal letter for your bank.

subject: SCIM-provisioned users are missing from role-based task routing
description: We enabled SCIM to create users automatically, and those users can sign in fine, but our workflow rules that assign onboarding tasks by role are ignoring them. Manually created users get the tasks immediately. I need to know what user setting or product configuration is missing for SCIM-created accounts to participate in automation.

comments.message: The workflow assigns equipment tasks to anyone in the 'New Hire - Sales' role. For manually created users, it works. For SCIM users, the role shows up in the profile after sync, but no tasks are generated.

Thanks — that points to a provisioning/configuration mismatch rather than a login problem. In User Administration, SCIM-created users are often left in a pending state until either license assignment or group activation completes. If the workflow is configured to run only for Active users, the automation won't create tasks until the account status changes to Active. Can you confirm whether the affected users are Active at the time the role is assigned?
I checked one example and the role arrived at 9:02, but the user didn't become Active until 9:18 after a license was applied. Our workflow trigger is only 'when role is added'.

That explains it. Update the workflow so it triggers on either 'role added' plus 'user status is Active,' or add a second automation for 'user becomes Active' where role contains 'New Hire - Sales.' That way the product configuration accounts for the provisioning sequence from User Administration. Once both conditions can be met, SCIM-created users should enter the same routing path as manually created users.

subject: CSV import keeps rejecting date field format
description: I am importing contacts from a CSV and mapping a custom renewal date field. The preview looks correct, but the import fails with a validation error on that field for about half the rows. I already reformatted the column in Excel and still cannot get it through.

comments.message: The field is configured as a date, and the CSV values are like 03/07/2026 and 11/19/2026. Some rows pass, others fail, even though the format looks the same to me.

Our importer only accepts ISO date format, so please convert the column to YYYY-MM-DD before uploading. Also check for hidden time values if the file was edited in Excel; exporting as CSV UTF-8 after changing the format usually fixes it.

I converted the dates to YYYY-MM-DD and re-exported. The import completed, but a few rows still skipped because of invalid dates. Those rows had blanks before I filled them quickly by dragging formulas.

That suggests some cells may contain formula output with whitespace or non-date text. Open the CSV in a plain text editor and inspect those specific rows, or replace the values manually. The configuration of the custom field is fine; the remaining issue is with the source data formatting.

subject: Temporary admins can edit users but automation won't recognize their approvals
description: I created a temporary admin role for regional leads so they can update user profiles during a reorg without giving them full system access. They can edit users successfully, but when they approve access-change requests, the workflow doesn't treat their approval as valid and the request stays open. I'm trying to understand whether I missed a user permission, approval setting, or workflow approver configuration.

comments.message: The approval step is configured for 'Regional Admin' approvers. I thought assigning the temporary admin role to three leads would be enough, but the audit trail says 'approval recorded from non-eligible actor' and the workflow waits for another approver.

It sounds like the temporary role grants user-edit permissions but may not include the approver capability flag tied to that workflow. In many accounts, being able to administer users and being eligible as a workflow approver are separate settings. Please check User Administration to see whether those leads are assigned to the Regional Admin approver group or only to the temporary admin permission role.
They're only on the temporary role. I don't see them in the Regional Admin approver group at all.

That would explain the behavior. Add them to the Regional Admin approver group or update the workflow configuration to recognize the temporary role as an eligible approver source. Right now the product is correctly allowing profile edits while rejecting workflow approvals from users who don't match the approver definition.

subject: Signature validation failing for some webhook events
description: We verify webhook signatures using your docs, but about 15% of events fail verification even though they appear to come from your IP ranges. The failures are intermittent and mostly affect larger payloads. I need help troubleshooting whether our HMAC implementation is wrong or if the payload is being transformed in transit.

comments.message: We compute HMAC-SHA256 over the request body using the webhook signing secret and compare it to the `X-Signature` header. Small payloads validate; larger ones often do not. We're behind an Nginx proxy if that matters.

The proxy does matter. The most common cause is hashing a parsed or re-serialized body instead of the raw byte stream. Another issue is newline normalization or gzip decompression before signature verification. Please capture the exact raw request bytes at the edge, verify before JSON parsing, and confirm you're using the timestamped format from the docs: `timestamp + '.' + raw_body` rather than just the body alone.
We were indeed hashing the parsed JSON string after middleware touched it. After moving verification earlier, most events pass, but a few still fail when the proxy decompresses the body.

Then the remaining failures are consistent with Nginx modifying the body before your app sees it. Disable request-body decompression for the webhook path or perform verification at the proxy before any transformation. If you want, send us one failing event ID and the timestamp header and I can compare the canonical payload from our delivery logs.

subject: Department admins can’t see team metrics after user reassignment
description: I reassigned several users from one department to another in User Administration, and now the old department admins say their team metrics report looks broken. The new department admins can see the users, but the totals in the report seem inconsistent depending on who opens it. I need help understanding whether the report follows current ownership, historical ownership, or admin scope, and I need something educational I can relay to both admin groups.

comments.message: We moved 18 users from Implementation to Customer Success yesterday. Today, the 'Team adoption by department' report shows different totals for different admins. One admin says the users vanished; another says they moved. Which behavior is correct?
That usually means the report is cached. Have everyone log out and back in.
They already did that and it didn’t change anything. Is this related to the department reassignment itself?

Possibly. Department-based reports often recalculate after a data refresh, and admins may also see different results based on their scoped access to departments. I don’t have enough detail to confirm whether your report uses current or historical department assignment, so I’m escalating this to our reporting team. In the meantime, you can tell both admin groups that user reassignment can temporarily affect department totals until reporting finishes processing the change.

subject: API pagination skipped users during cleanup of suspended accounts
description: I was cleaning up suspended user accounts and removing them from project memberships, but it looks like my script skipped a chunk of users between pages. After that, some suspended users were deactivated but still own imported datasets, and now our team admins can't reassign those assets in the UI. I need help figuring out the correct API flow and permissions needed to finish the cleanup.

comments.message: I paged through GET /users?status=suspended with page=1,2,3 and deactivated users as I went. When I reran the report, about 60 suspended users had never been processed. Some of the ones I did process still own datasets.

You shouldn't deactivate users while paginating with offset-based pages because the result set changes underneath you. Use cursor pagination or export the full target list first, then process by stable IDs. For the dataset ownership issue, deactivation doesn't automatically transfer owned assets, and reassignment requires either a Super Admin or a token with asset-management permissions in addition to user admin access.
That makes sense. Is there a bulk endpoint to transfer dataset ownership before we deactivate the remaining users?

Yes, but it's limited by asset type. Use the dataset ownership transfer endpoint with the current owner ID and new owner ID, then verify there are no remaining owned assets before deactivation. If your token can deactivate users but gets 403 on transfers, that's a sign the service account has user administration rights without data-management rights, so you'll need a broader admin token or a custom role update.

subject: Webhook retries are creating duplicate tasks in our approval flow
description: I'm using your API to create approval tasks from our internal procurement system, and we also trigger a workflow automation when the task status changes. Since last Thursday, whenever your webhook times out and retries, our automation treats each retry like a new event and creates duplicate approval steps. We need to know whether there's an idempotency key or event identifier we should be using, because right now managers are getting 3-4 identical approvals for a single request.

comments.message: We're posting to /v2/tasks via the API and then listening for task.updated events to move the request through our automation. If your webhook delivery gets a 5xx from us, you retry, which is fine, but the payload looks the same each time and our workflow can't tell the retry from a fresh event.

Thanks for the detail. Each webhook delivery includes a unique event_id in the JSON body and an X-Event-Id header. For workflow automation, you should deduplicate on event_id rather than task_id, because retries reuse the same event_id while separate updates generate new ones. If you're also creating tasks via API, I recommend sending an Idempotency-Key header on the POST request so duplicate client submissions don't create multiple tasks either.
I don't see X-Event-Id in the docs we were using. Is this available on all plans, and does the same event_id remain constant across all retries?

Yes, it's available on all plans. The same event_id is preserved across retries for the same webhook event. I've also confirmed your account is on the current webhook format, so you can safely key your automation off event_id and ignore repeats you've already processed. That should stop the duplicate approval steps.

subject: Trying to train admins on sandbox testing, but feature flags differ
description: I'm writing internal documentation for our admins on how to test new workflows in the sandbox before enabling them in production. The problem is that a feature available in production isn't showing in the sandbox, so I can't tell whether I'm missing a setting or if the environments are intentionally different. I need guidance I can include in the docs and, if necessary, someone to escalate this to.

comments.message: How should I explain sandbox limitations to admins, and is it normal for feature flags to be different between sandbox and production?
Sandboxes are exact copies of production, so if a feature exists in one it should always exist in the other. You can tell admins to assume parity when testing.
That doesn't sound right. I thought beta features and some account-level settings might not sync.

You're right to question that. I answered too broadly. Some features, especially betas, phased rollouts, or plan-gated settings, may not be enabled identically in sandbox environments. Since I don't want your documentation to rely on a blanket rule, I'm escalating this to our platform team to verify whether the missing feature is expected behavior in your tenant. A safer training guideline is to tell admins to validate key settings in both environments before sign-off.

subject: QuickBooks sync broken and locked-out admin after card update failed
description: I tried to update our payment card yesterday after the old one expired. The payment page errored out twice, and today our QuickBooks integration is disconnected and I can’t log in with my normal admin account anymore because it says my role was suspended. Another invoice email came through this morning showing both the failed card and a successful charge on the new card, so I need help understanding whether we’re delinquent, double charged, or both. I need admin access restored because nobody else on the team can reconnect QuickBooks.

comments.message: I tried to update our payment card yesterday after the old one expired. The payment page errored out twice, and today our QuickBooks integration is disconnected and I can’t log in with my normal admin account anymore because it says my role was suspended. Another invoice email came through this morning showing both the failed card and a successful charge on the new card, so I need help understanding whether we’re delinquent, double charged, or both. I need admin access restored because nobody else on the team can reconnect QuickBooks.

I can see one failed payment attempt on the expired card and one successful capture on the replacement card, so you were not double charged. However, the dunning workflow briefly marked the workspace past due and suspended one admin role, which in your case was also the QuickBooks integration owner. I’ve restored your admin access and cleared the delinquency flag. Please sign back in, reconnect QuickBooks from the Integrations page, and the failed sync jobs should replay automatically within the hour.
I’m back in now, but reconnecting QuickBooks says the app is still owned by a disabled user.

Thanks for checking. The account-level suspension is lifted, but the integration ownership record didn’t update with it. I’ve reassigned the QuickBooks connection to your restored admin user and queued a backfill for the last 36 hours of missed invoices. If any entries are still missing after that completes, reply here and I’ll pull the sync logs manually.

subject: Reminder workflow only sends for some form submissions
description: I configured an automation to send a reminder email 24 hours after a demo request is submitted if the owner hasn't contacted the lead yet. It's working for leads created through our website form, but not for leads coming from the webinar integration. Both should use the same pipeline. I need help figuring out whether the automation is filtered too narrowly or whether those records are missing a field the workflow depends on.

comments.message: The trigger is when a new lead is created in pipeline Inbound Demo. Wait 24 hours. If Last Contacted At is empty, send reminder to owner. Webinar leads land in the same pipeline but no reminder goes out.

Please compare one website lead and one webinar lead in Product Configuration and look at required defaults: owner assignment timing, pipeline stage, and whether Last Contacted At is initialized as blank or null. In Workflow Automation, some integrations also create the record first and assign pipeline in a second update, so a trigger based strictly on creation in a specific pipeline can miss them. A safer setup is to trigger on lead created, then add a condition after a short delay to check pipeline = Inbound Demo.
I inspected two records and the webinar lead gets the pipeline about a minute later from the integration sync. Owner is also assigned later.

That explains it. Update the workflow to start on any lead creation, wait 5 minutes, then check pipeline = Inbound Demo and owner is not empty before beginning the 24-hour reminder timer. That should catch both sources without changing your form setup.

subject: Snowflake export completed but executive KPI report is blank
description: Our scheduled Snowflake export says it completed successfully overnight, but the executive KPI report built on that data is blank for this morning's refresh. I checked the destination table and can see rows were written today, so I don't understand why the report is empty. We've got the CEO review in two hours and need either a fix or a workaround.

comments.message: The Snowflake integration job finished at 2:14 AM with a success status, and today's rows are in the warehouse. But our executive KPI report inside your app shows no data for today's refresh window. This happened once before and resolved on its own.
If the rows are in Snowflake, the integration is working. The blank report is probably just a browser cache issue. Please clear cache or open the report in an incognito window.
We tried that across three users, and the report export CSV is also blank. It doesn't seem client-side.

I see. Since the export succeeded but the in-app report dataset isn't reflecting the warehouse rows, there may be a delay or failure in the reporting ingestion layer after the integration completes. I'm escalating this to our data pipeline team because support can't re-run that internal materialization ourselves. In the meantime, if you can query Snowflake directly, that will be the safest source for your meeting.

subject: Historical report changed after parent-child hierarchy update
description: I reorganized our customer hierarchy so several branch accounts now roll up under a new parent company. The account tree looks correct in the admin view, but our historical bookings report for Q1 changed after the update, which surprised finance because they expected old periods to remain as originally booked. I need clarification on whether hierarchy edits are meant to retroactively affect reporting and whether we can preserve both current and historical rollups.

comments.message: We updated the account hierarchy, and now Q1 bookings are grouped under the new parent instead of the original branch structure. Is historical reporting supposed to change retroactively?

Yes, because parent-child hierarchy is a live attribute in reporting unless the report uses a snapshot dimension. Your Q1 report is recalculating with the current hierarchy, not the hierarchy as of booking date.
Can we revert just the report behavior without undoing the hierarchy cleanup?

Not directly from the current report. The best path is to build a snapshot-based version using hierarchy as-of date, but that requires our reporting consultants to enable the historical dimension for your account. I've documented the use case and escalated it for review.

subject: Need help understanding why deactivated users can still reach the login screen
description: We deactivated a former employee last week. Today he told us he can still get to the login page and enter his credentials, although he says he gets stopped afterward. My security lead wants to know whether deactivation is functioning correctly or if former users should be blocked earlier in the process. Can you clarify expected behavior and check whether anything is wrong with our setup?

comments.message: To be clear, he cannot reach any workspace content. He only sees a message after submitting his password that access has been disabled.

That behavior is expected. Deactivation removes workspace access but does not necessarily prevent someone from reaching the generic sign-in page, because the login page is shared across all tenants. The enforcement happens after identity verification, at which point the system denies access to the deactivated account.
Is there any way to make that less confusing for our admins? They assumed seeing the password prompt meant the account was still active.

A good practice is to pair deactivation with your IdP-side suspension if you use SSO, or to delete the user entirely if policy requires no further authentication attempts. Within our app, deactivation is working as designed, but I agree the experience can be misleading. I’ve filed product feedback requesting clearer messaging so admins understand the distinction between being able to reach the sign-in page and actually being authorized to enter the workspace.

subject: Zaproszony użytkownik nie może aktywować konta
description: Zaprosiłem nową osobę do naszego zespołu, ale po kliknięciu linku aktywacyjnego dostaje stronę logowania i komunikat, że konto nie istnieje. Adres e-mail jest poprawny. Potrzebujemy uruchomić jej dostęp jeszcze dziś.

comments.message: Nowy pracownik nie może aktywować konta. Link z zaproszenia prowadzi do logowania, a potem system twierdzi, że konto nie istnieje.

Sprawdziłem zaproszenie — zostało wysłane na poprawny adres, ale użytkownik ma status „pending” i jednocześnie Państwa workspace ma włączone ograniczenie logowania tylko dla zweryfikowanych domen SSO. Ten adres jest w subdomenie, która nie została dodana do listy dozwolonych.
Rzeczywiście, to adres w subdomenie naszej grupy. Co możemy zrobić?

Dodałem tę subdomenę do wyjątków na poziomie organizacji i ponownie wysłałem zaproszenie. Proszę, aby użytkownik otworzył najnowszy link w trybie incognito. Jeśli chcecie, mogę też zasugerować zmianę polityki, żeby podobne adresy były akceptowane automatycznie.

subject: Former employee still receiving password reset emails
description: I’m our workspace admin and I deactivated a sales rep last week, but she’s still getting password reset emails from your system and her name still appears in the people picker on some projects. I need to make sure her access is fully removed without breaking the records she owned. What should I do?

comments.message: I’m our workspace admin and I deactivated a sales rep last week, but she’s still getting password reset emails from your system and her name still appears in the people picker on some projects. I need to make sure her access is fully removed without breaking the records she owned.

Thanks for the details. If the user is deactivated but still appears in search or project history, that is usually expected for audit purposes, but they should not be able to sign in. Please go to Admin Settings > Users, open her profile, and confirm the status is Deactivated rather than Pending Invitation. Also check whether SSO is enabled, because identity-provider initiated password emails can come from your IdP rather than from us.

We do use SSO, but the email she forwarded has your branding and a reset link on your domain. In her profile I also see an old secondary email address, and the status says Deactivated. We want to keep ownership history but stop any notifications entirely.

Understood. In that case, please revoke all active sessions from the user profile, remove the secondary email address, and unassign her from any automation rules or approval steps that still reference her account. Deactivated users can remain visible in historical pickers, but they should stop receiving system emails once sessions are revoked and notification targets are cleared. If you still see new reset emails after that, reply with the timestamp and I’ll escalate it to our auth team to inspect the mail logs.

subject: Limite de taxa da API começou a bloquear importação após mudar configuração de sincronização
description: Alterámos a configuração da sincronização para correr de 5 em 5 minutos em vez de hora a hora, e desde então a importação de tickets começa bem mas depois recebe 429. Não sei se o problema é apenas volume ou se ativámos alguma opção que aumenta o número de chamadas por página. Precisamos perceber qual configuração do produto está a causar isso e como ajustar a integração.

comments.message: Alterámos a configuração da sincronização para correr de 5 em 5 minutos em vez de hora a hora, e desde então a importação de tickets começa bem mas depois recebe 429. Não sei se o problema é apenas volume ou se ativámos alguma opção que aumenta o número de chamadas por página. Precisamos perceber qual configuração do produto está a causar isso e como ajustar a integração.
O erro 429 significa que fizeram chamadas a mais. A solução é reduzir a frequência ou esperar mais tempo entre pedidos.
Certo, mas antes não acontecia. Só mudou a configuração de sincronização incremental e a expansão de comentários.

Nesse caso, a expansão de comentários provavelmente aumenta bastante o custo por pedido e, combinada com a execução a cada 5 minutos, ultrapassa o limite do plano. Não tenho acesso para alterar isso do meu lado. Recomendo desativar o parâmetro expand=comments, usar paginação menor e respeitar o header Retry-After. Se quiser, a equipa comercial pode explicar limites mais altos de API no vosso plano.

subject: API pagination skipped members during group sync
description: We sync your users nightly into our internal access review system. Since moving from manual exports to your API, some users are missing from the sync and it looks like they were all on pages after the first 200 results. We're also using the group membership endpoints to validate who should have access to a workspace. Can you confirm the correct pagination behavior so we don't miss active users?

comments.message: Right now our script uses page=1,2,3 and stops when the returned array is shorter than the limit. We noticed deleted users and suspended users are mixed in, so filtering is making this harder too.

You're likely running into cursor pagination. Our newer /users and /groups endpoints no longer guarantee stable page-number results when the directory changes during the sync. The recommended approach is to request a limit, follow the next_cursor value until it is null, and pass status=active if you only want active users. For group validation, use /groups/{id}/members with the same cursor flow.
That makes sense. We were still using the old page parameter from an outdated sample script. We'll switch to next_cursor and filter to active users before comparing memberships.
Sounds good. That should prevent skipped members during directory changes and give you a consistent source for active access reviews. If you want, I can send an example loop in Python or Node.

subject: No sé por qué Mailchimp no está sincronizando las bajas
description: Conectamos Mailchimp para pasar contactos desde la plataforma, y los nuevos se sincronizan, pero cuando alguien se da de baja aquí sigue apareciendo como suscrito en Mailchimp. No sé si es un límite de la integración o si me falta configurar algo. Necesito que me expliquen el comportamiento esperado.

comments.message: Esperábamos que las bajas se reflejaran en ambos lados. Ahora marketing está molesto porque teme enviar campañas a gente que ya se desuscribió.

Entiendo la preocupación. En muchas integraciones con herramientas de email marketing, las bajas no siempre se sincronizan de forma bidireccional por razones de cumplimiento y para evitar re-suscripciones accidentales. Déjame aclararte cómo funciona en este caso.
Sí, por favor, porque la documentación me pareció ambigua.

En nuestra integración con Mailchimp, los contactos nuevos y ciertas actualizaciones de perfil sí se sincronizan, pero el estado de baja puede depender de la dirección del flujo y de cómo se haya configurado la audiencia. Para no darte una respuesta incompleta, voy a escalar este caso al equipo de integraciones para confirmar si la baja desde nuestra plataforma debe marcarse como unsubscribed en Mailchimp o si requiere una automatización adicional. Mientras tanto, te recomiendo pausar cualquier sync de marketing para esos contactos sensibles.

subject: Changing domain name broke API authentication callback
description: We changed our company domain from oldco.com to newco.com and updated user emails in your platform. Since then, our OAuth app's redirect and some admin API operations are failing with an error about the authorized account no longer matching the installation. I think this is related to the account updates, but I need guidance on what has to be reauthorized versus what should keep working.

comments.message: After we bulk-updated all admin emails to our new company domain, our OAuth refresh flow started failing for the app our ops team installed last year. The error says the grant is no longer valid for this account. Do domain-wide account changes require re-consent?

Email-domain changes can invalidate OAuth grants if the original installing admin account is modified or deactivated, particularly when the app was authorized on behalf of a specific user. Please confirm whether the original installer still exists as an active admin, and check whether your redirect URI list still matches exactly in the developer console.
The original installer account was renamed and then deactivated. Redirect URIs are unchanged. We need the API integration back today.

In that case, the quickest path is to have an active admin reinstall or reauthorize the OAuth app so a new valid grant is issued under a current account. If you need continuity, do not delete the existing app client; just complete the authorization flow again and replace the stored refresh token. I've also seen webhook signatures continue to work without changes, but any user-bound admin endpoints should be retested after re-consent.

subject: Owner transfer fails after admin leaves workspace
description: I’m trying to transfer workspace ownership from our former IT manager to me, but every time I click Confirm the modal closes and nothing changes. I’m already a full admin, and we need to update billing contact permissions before month end. I tested in Chrome and Edge and got the same result.

comments.message: I’m trying to transfer workspace ownership from our former IT manager to me, but every time I click Confirm the modal closes and nothing changes. I’m already a full admin, and we need to update billing contact permissions before month end. I tested in Chrome and Edge and got the same result.

Thanks for the details. I checked the expected flow, and as a full admin you should be able to accept ownership if the current owner initiated the transfer. To rule out a browser issue, could you try an incognito window and confirm whether the current owner still appears as active under Settings > Members?
I tried incognito just now. Same behavior. The current owner still shows as active, but his account is deactivated through our SSO provider because he left the company.

That helps. I was able to reproduce this in a test workspace: if the current owner is deactivated via SSO, the ownership transfer dialog can fail silently instead of showing an error. I’ve logged this as a bug for engineering. As a workaround, we can manually reassign ownership from our side after verifying your admin status. I’ve started that process and attached your case to the bug report; you should get a confirmation once the backend change completes.

subject: Service account can't log in and nightly workflow imports stopped
description: Our service account that runs nightly CSV imports started failing authentication after we rotated its password. Since then, the import workflow hasn't created any records, and downstream automations didn't fire either. We need this fixed, but if service accounts are handled by another team please escalate it because operations is blocked.

comments.message: Our service account that runs nightly CSV imports started failing authentication after we rotated its password. Since then, the import workflow hasn't created any records, and downstream automations didn't fire either. We need this fixed, but if service accounts are handled by another team please escalate it because operations is blocked.

I reviewed the authentication logs and the service account is being blocked by your workspace policy requiring interactive MFA for all password-based logins. The password rotation itself succeeded, but service accounts can’t complete the new MFA challenge, so the import job never starts. Because no records are imported, the downstream automations have nothing to process. I’ve escalated this to our access policy team to confirm the safest exception path, and in the meantime I recommend switching this integration to an API token or exempting the service account from interactive MFA if your security policy allows it.
We can switch to an API token, but we need tonight's import to run. Can support create the exception temporarily?

I can’t create policy exceptions directly from support, but I flagged the escalation as time-sensitive and included your request for a temporary exemption. If you have an organization security admin, they may be able to exclude the service account from the MFA policy sooner from the admin console.

subject: Custom object import fails only when updating existing rows
description: I can create new rows in our custom object just fine from CSV, but whenever I try to update existing rows using the object's external key, the import fails with "row locked" on about 30% of records. The strange part is that the failed rows aren't being edited by anyone, and re-running the same file fails on a different subset each time. We also noticed some successful rows got their multiselect values replaced instead of appended. Could you help determine whether this is a locking bug and what import settings we should use to avoid damaging the data further?

comments.message: I can create new rows in our custom object just fine from CSV, but whenever I try to update existing rows using the object's external key, the import fails with "row locked" on about 30% of records. The strange part is that the failed rows aren't being edited by anyone, and re-running the same file fails on a different subset each time. We also noticed some successful rows got their multiselect values replaced instead of appended. Could you help determine whether this is a locking bug and what import settings we should use to avoid damaging the data further?

I reviewed the last two import jobs and the lock errors are coming from a background enrichment process that briefly touches those same records after each save. That explains why the failing subset changes between runs. The multiselect overwrite is expected unless the import is set to "append values," which I don’t see enabled in your last job. To reduce collisions, try importing in batches of 100 outside the top of the hour when enrichment runs are heaviest.
That helps, but we still need a way to update all records without babysitting retries. Is the enrichment lock behavior intended?

It’s not ideal, and I agree the importer should handle retries more gracefully. I’ve filed a bug with the import team for intermittent lock failures during background enrichment. In the meantime, the safest path is smaller batches with append mode enabled for multiselect fields, and I can send you the exact import configuration from the successful jobs if useful.

subject: Can’t access admin panel after role change
description: I was changed from Workspace Admin to Analyst by mistake, and now I can’t get into the admin panel to restore my permissions. Another admin is on leave, and our audit export is due today. I need this escalated because nobody on our side with the right access is available.

comments.message: I can still log in, but every admin page gives me an access denied message. My profile now shows Analyst. Can support put me back as admin?

I reviewed the account and can confirm your login is working, but your current role does not include admin permissions. Support cannot directly elevate a user to admin without authorization from an existing account owner. Since you mentioned no owner is available, I’m escalating this to our account integrity team to review alternative verification options.
What kind of verification do they need? I can provide our company domain email and last invoice if necessary.

That will help. I’ve noted that you can provide billing evidence and a domain-based email reply. The integrity team will likely request confirmation from the billing contact or documentation showing you’re authorized to administer the workspace. I can’t restore access from this queue, but the escalation is in progress.

subject: No entiendo cómo conectar Salesforce sin duplicar contactos
description: Estoy intentando integrar nuestra cuenta con Salesforce para que los leads del formulario entren automáticamente al CRM, pero se están creando contactos duplicados y no sé si configuré mal el mapeo. También me pierdo con la diferencia entre sincronización unidireccional y bidireccional. ¿Me pueden explicar qué debo revisar y cómo dejarlo bien?

comments.message: Conecté Salesforce ayer y desde entonces cada envío del formulario crea un contacto nuevo aunque el email ya existe. Además, activé la sincronización bidireccional porque pensé que era lo normal, pero ahora no sé si eso empeoró el problema.

Claro, te explico. La sincronización bidireccional hace que los cambios viajen en ambos sentidos, entre nuestra plataforma y Salesforce. Si solo quieres enviar nuevos leads desde formularios, normalmente conviene empezar con sincronización unidireccional hacia Salesforce. Para los duplicados, revisa en la configuración de mapeo si el campo identificador está puesto como Email y no como nombre o ID externo.
Acabo de revisar y el campo de coincidencia estaba en 'Nombre completo'. Lo cambié a Email. ¿Necesito desactivar algo más?

Sí, te recomiendo desactivar temporalmente la sincronización bidireccional mientras validas el flujo. Luego haz una prueba con un correo ya existente y confirma si actualiza el contacto en vez de crear otro. Si quieres, en el siguiente paso también puedo indicarte cómo mapear empresa, propietario y estado del lead para evitar inconsistencias.

subject: La integración con Stripe no actualiza el plan del cliente en la cuenta
description: Integramos Stripe para que cuando un cliente cambie de plan en la suscripción se actualice automáticamente su nivel de acceso en la plataforma. Los pagos entran bien y vemos eventos desde Stripe, pero la cuenta del cliente sigue con el plan anterior hasta que lo cambiamos manualmente. Creo que algo quedó mal en la correspondencia entre productos de Stripe y planes internos, o en la regla que aplica esa configuración dentro del sistema.

comments.message: Un cliente pasó de Básico a Pro en Stripe ayer. El webhook llegó, pero en nuestra plataforma siguió con permisos de Básico.

Cuando eso pasa, normalmente el evento se recibe pero no encuentra una equivalencia válida con un plan interno. Vaya a Integraciones > Stripe > Mapeo de productos y confirme que el price ID del plan Pro esté vinculado al plan correcto en la plataforma.
Encontré que el producto estaba mapeado, pero al parecer a un plan archivado con el mismo nombre. Ya lo corregí.

Perfecto, eso explicaría el comportamiento. Como además los permisos dependen de la configuración de acceso, valide en Configuración > Planes y permisos que el plan Pro activo tenga asignadas las capacidades esperadas. Los cambios futuros deberían aplicarse automáticamente; para el cliente afectado, puede forzar una resincronización desde su ficha o esperar al próximo evento de Stripe.

subject: Field-level permissions changed and now data export is missing columns
description: We tightened permissions on several sensitive customer fields in our workspace and since then our weekly export job is missing those columns entirely, even though the export template hasn't changed. We expected restricted users to lose access in the UI, but our integration account should still be able to export full records. I suspect something in the role configuration or service account assignment changed, and we need to know whether the data is gone from the export because of permissions or because the fields were removed from the schema.

comments.message: Our scheduled customer export stopped including ARR, contract end date, and health score right after we updated field visibility by role. The data still appears on some admin profiles. Why would the export file drop the columns?

Exports inherit the permissions of the user or service account that owns the export job. If those fields were set to hidden for that role, the export can omit the columns even though the fields and data still exist. Please check which role is assigned to the integration account that runs the weekly export.
It looks like the job is owned by an old operations user we downgraded from Admin to Standard last Friday.

That explains it. Reassign the scheduled export to an admin-level service account or update the current owner's role to include read access for those fields under Product Configuration > Roles & Permissions. No data needs to be restored; this is a permissions-driven export behavior.

subject: Mauvaise affectation automatique des tickets par région
description: Je souhaite configurer une règle d'affectation automatique pour envoyer les tickets francophones aux équipes régionales correspondantes, mais plusieurs demandes belges partent chez notre équipe France. Comme je dois expliquer demain à nos administrateurs comment maintenir ces règles, j'ai besoin de comprendre où le paramétrage pose problème.

comments.message: Bonjour, nous utilisons des formulaires avec un champ “Pays” et un champ “Langue”. J'ai créé des règles pour Belgique, France et Suisse, mais les tickets belges en français sont systématiquement assignés à l'équipe France. Comment configurer cela correctement sans casser les autres flux ?

Bonjour, si les tickets belges partent en France, c'est probablement parce que la règle France est plus haute dans l'ordre et utilise seulement la langue française comme condition. Il faut rendre la règle France plus spécifique ou la descendre sous la règle Belgique. Par exemple : Belgique si Pays = BE, puis France si Pays = FR, puis une règle générale Francophone si Langue = Français.
D'accord, cela a du sens. Est-ce que vous recommandez d'utiliser le pays seul, ou pays + langue pour éviter les erreurs ?

Je recommande pays + langue lorsque votre organisation traite plusieurs langues par pays, sinon le pays seul peut suffire. Pour former vos administrateurs, insistez sur deux principes : l'ordre des règles et la spécificité des conditions. Faites-leur tester des cas réels comme “Belgique + Français” et “Suisse + Allemand” avant publication. Si vous le souhaitez, je peux aussi vous proposer une structure de règles plus facile à maintenir sur le long terme.

subject: Nie mogę zmienić adresu logowania po ślubie
description: Zmieniłam nazwisko i adres e-mail w firmie, więc próbuję zaktualizować dane mojego konta. Profil pokazuje nowe imię, ale adres logowania pozostaje stary i wiadomości o resecie hasła nadal idą na nieaktualny e-mail. Nie mam uprawnień administratora.

comments.message: Czy mogą Państwo zmienić mój login z k.nowak@firma.pl na k.wisniewska@firma.pl? Sama mogłam zmienić tylko nazwę wyświetlaną, a reset hasła dalej przychodzi na stary adres.
Tak, możemy to zrobić, ale potrzebujemy zgody administratora konta lub potwierdzenia z obu adresów e-mail. Bez tego nie zmienimy loginu użytkownika ze względów bezpieczeństwa.
Administrator jest na urlopie, a do starej skrzynki mam dostęp tylko do jutra. Wysłałam też wiadomość z nowego adresu. Czy to wystarczy?

Na ten moment nie mogę wykonać zmiany bez zgody administratora. Dodałem jednak notatkę do konta i przekazałem sprawę do zespołu bezpieczeństwa z prośbą o wyjątkową weryfikację na podstawie obu adresów. Gdy otrzymamy decyzję, skontaktujemy się mailowo. Do tego czasu proszę nie usuwać starej skrzynki.

subject: SSO users are stuck in a login loop after we enforced SAML
description: We turned on SAML SSO for our account yesterday, and now several users get redirected back to the login screen after authenticating with Okta. A few admins can still get in, but most regular users cannot. I need help troubleshooting what is causing the loop, and I also want to understand what a safe rollout plan should have looked like so we don’t lock people out again.

comments.message: We turned on SAML SSO for our account yesterday, and now several users get redirected back to the login screen after authenticating with Okta. A few admins can still get in, but most regular users cannot. I need help troubleshooting what is causing the loop, and I also want to understand what a safe rollout plan should have looked like so we don’t lock people out again.

Login loops after SAML enforcement usually come from one of four issues: the users aren’t assigned to the app in Okta, the NameID/email coming from Okta doesn’t match the email on their accounts, the ACS/redirect URL is incorrect, or a domain enforcement rule is catching users before provisioning is complete. Since some admins can still log in, I’d first compare one working user and one failing user in Okta to confirm app assignment and exact email value. A safer rollout is to test with a small pilot group, keep a break-glass local admin account excluded from SSO, verify Just-in-Time provisioning or pre-provisioning, and only then enforce SSO for the full domain.

We checked one failing user and found Okta is sending their email alias, while their account in your system uses their primary email address. The admins who work can log in because their alias matches their account email.

That would explain the loop. Update the Okta attribute mapping so NameID/email sends the same address stored on the user account here, or bulk-update the user emails in our app to match the IdP if that’s your intended standard. Before re-enforcing broadly, test with 2-3 non-admin users, confirm they can complete SP-initiated login, and keep one excluded admin account as recovery access. If you want, I can also share our SAML rollout checklist for future changes.

subject: Rechnung mit falscher Firmierung
description: Ich brauche dringend eine korrigierte Rechnung für März. Auf der aktuellen Rechnung steht noch unsere alte Firmierung, obwohl ich die Rechnungsdaten bereits vor zwei Wochen im Adminbereich geändert habe. So kann unsere Buchhaltung die Rechnung nicht verbuchen. Bitte senden Sie mir eine neue Rechnung mit der korrekten Firmenbezeichnung und USt-IdNr.

comments.message: Ich brauche dringend eine korrigierte Rechnung für März. Auf der aktuellen Rechnung steht noch unsere alte Firmierung, obwohl ich die Rechnungsdaten bereits vor zwei Wochen im Adminbereich geändert habe. So kann unsere Buchhaltung die Rechnung nicht verbuchen. Bitte senden Sie mir eine neue Rechnung mit der korrekten Firmenbezeichnung und USt-IdNr.

Danke für die Nachricht. Rechnungen werden normalerweise mit den aktuell hinterlegten Daten erstellt, daher sollten Sie die Firmierung einfach im Adminbereich nochmals speichern und die Rechnung neu herunterladen.
Das habe ich bereits mehrfach gemacht. Die heruntergeladene Rechnung bleibt unverändert, und wir müssen die Frist für den Monatsabschluss einhalten.

Verstanden. In dem Fall kann ich die Rechnung nicht direkt selbst ändern. Ich habe Ihre Anfrage an unsere Abrechnungsabteilung weitergeleitet, damit dort eine manuelle Korrektur erstellt wird. Sobald ich Rückmeldung habe, melde ich mich wieder.

subject: Salesforce sync missing records from weekly pipeline report
description: I'm pulling our weekly pipeline report in your app, and the totals are way off compared with Salesforce. It looks like deals created through the Salesforce integration after last Thursday are not showing up in the report, even though I can see them on some individual account pages. I already reconnected the integration once and reran the report, but the missing records are still not included. We have our board review tomorrow morning, so I need to know whether this is a reporting issue or a sync issue and what can be done today.

comments.message: Our VP noticed that the 'Pipeline by Region' report is missing around 37 opportunities that were definitely created in Salesforce this week. The Salesforce integration page says sync succeeded at 2:14 AM. Can you check what happened?

I checked your workspace and can confirm the Salesforce connector is healthy overall, but I can also see several opportunities arriving with a blank value in the mapped 'Region' field. Because your saved report filters on Region being one of four named values, those records are excluded from the totals even though they exist in the account views. I forced a metadata refresh on the Salesforce integration and re-ran the import for the affected object.
That explains part of it, but why did this start suddenly? We didn't change the report.

It looks like the Salesforce team changed the Region field from a text field to a picklist on Thursday evening. The integration kept syncing the records, but the field mapping on our side became stale for reporting until the schema refresh. The backfill is now complete, and your weekly report shows 36 of the 37 missing opportunities. One remaining record is failing import because it references an inactive owner in Salesforce. I'm escalating that last record to our integrations team so they can correct the transform rule and update you separately.

subject: Users can sign in, but group-based app access stopped syncing
description: Our Azure AD login itself is working, but after a configuration cleanup yesterday, users are no longer getting access to the correct projects based on their SSO groups. They can enter the workspace, but many see almost nothing because their team assignments are gone. I need help understanding whether the login mapping and product permissions are connected.

comments.message: SSO login through Azure AD works, but users are not being placed into the right teams anymore. They used to get project access from their AD groups, and now everyone comes in with the default role and no project assignments.

Thanks for clarifying. Authentication and authorization are separate here: login can succeed even if group mapping fails. Did you recently change the group claim name, SCIM settings, or the team mapping rules under Access Configuration? If the app no longer receives the expected group attribute, users will authenticate but fall back to the default role and lose project assignment mapping.
We changed the token to send 'groups' instead of 'memberOf' because of another app. I did not update anything on your side.

That would do it. Update the SSO group attribute field in our SAML/OIDC configuration from memberOf to groups, then review each team mapping rule to make sure the incoming group values still match exactly. After that, affected users should sign out and back in to refresh group-based access. Historical project membership won't be rewritten unless the mapping rule applies on login, so test with one user first.

subject: Charged for custom objects we only needed for a one-time import
description: We created a temporary custom object to import event attendance data for analysis, then deleted the object after exporting the results. Our bill now includes an add-on for custom object volume. I don't understand whether deleted imported data still affects billing, and I need advice on a better way to stage one-time data loads.

comments.message: We loaded around 450,000 attendance rows into a custom object for two days, ran our report, then removed the object entirely. Why are we being billed as if we still store that data?

Custom object billing is measured from the monthly high-water mark of stored records, not only what remains at invoice time. Because the import briefly pushed your account above the included volume, the add-on was triggered for that cycle. For one-time analysis, a safer option is to use the sandbox workspace or our external warehouse connector so the raw event data stays outside production.
Can we get that charge waived? Also, if we need to do this again, how do we avoid loading duplicate attendee rows from future CSVs?

I can't approve a waiver myself, but I've submitted a request to Billing noting that this was temporary analytical data. For future imports, set a unique key such as event_id plus attendee_email and choose upsert instead of insert-only. That will update existing rows rather than creating duplicates, which helps control both data quality and billable volume. If you'd like, I can also arrange a short education session with our data onboarding team.

subject: Need guidance on purging inactive users without losing ownership data
description: We have a lot of inactive users from past acquisitions, and I want to clean up the user directory. Before I do that, I need to understand what happens to records they own and what instructions I should give our operations team so they reassign things correctly. What’s the recommended process?

comments.message: We have a lot of inactive users from past acquisitions, and I want to clean up the user directory. Before I do that, I need to understand what happens to records they own and what instructions I should give our operations team so they reassign things correctly. What’s the recommended process?

The recommended flow is to reassign ownership before deactivation, not after. Have your ops team run a report of records owned by each inactive user, then bulk reassign accounts, contacts, open tasks, and any workflow ownership rules tied to them. Once ownership is cleared, deactivate the users instead of deleting them so historical activity remains intact. For training, give the team a checklist: export owned records, reassign by object type, verify no automations reference the user, then deactivate.
Is deactivation enough to free up seats, or do we have to delete them entirely?

In most workspaces, deactivation is what frees the seat while preserving history, but it can vary by contract type. You can confirm under Billing > Seats before making changes. From a data-management standpoint, deactivation is the safer option because deleting a user can remove references your team may still need for reporting.

subject: Need to convert a contractor admin to a regular user and appoint internal owner
description: We accidentally gave our outside implementation contractor full admin access when we first launched. Now the project is over, and we need to reduce their access, make our internal IT lead the main admin, and understand what settings only the current top-level admin can change. I don't want to remove the contractor too early and lose visibility into what they configured.

comments.message: Can you tell me the safest order here? The contractor set up groups, automations, and API keys. We want to switch account control internally without breaking workflows.
Just delete the contractor's profile and create a new admin for your IT lead. The workflows should stay there.
That sounds risky if the API keys were created under their account. We also need to know whether there is an audit trail we can review first.

Good catch. Deleting the profile immediately may revoke user-scoped tokens or credentials, depending on how those integrations were configured. A safer approach is: first promote your internal IT lead to the highest available admin role, then review audit logs and integrations to identify anything tied to the contractor account, rotate any personal API keys to service-owned credentials, and only after validation reduce the contractor's role or deactivate them. Some top-level account changes may still require the current owner role, not just admin. Since this involves potential ownership transfer plus integration dependency review, I'm escalating to our technical account team so they can verify whether any account-level assets are still linked to that user.

subject: Salesforce sync creating contacts but not updating account owner
description: Our Salesforce integration is partially working: new contacts are being created from form submissions, but the account owner field and territory mapping are not updating on existing records. I need to understand whether this is a configuration issue on our side or a limitation, and what steps we should take to troubleshoot it.

comments.message: We connected Salesforce last week. New leads are coming over, but existing accounts are not getting the owner or territory updates from your platform. Where should I look first?

It sounds like the integration is on but the field mapping or update behavior may not be configured for existing records. Please check Integrations > Salesforce > Field Mapping and verify that Account Owner and Territory are mapped to writable Salesforce fields. Then open the sync rule and confirm "update existing records" is enabled, since create-only mode will skip changes on matched accounts.
I found update existing records turned off. I turned it on, but owner still doesn't change when I test with an account that already exists.

Thanks for checking. In that case, the next likely issue is permissions: if the Salesforce user connected to our app cannot edit OwnerId or the territory field, the sync will create records where allowed but silently skip restricted updates. Please review the integration user profile in Salesforce and the sync error log under Recent Jobs. If you send a sample account URL, I can point out which failed field update to look for.

subject: Webhook retries are piling up and I don't know which endpoint is failing
description: We use your webhook integration to send ticket events into our internal system. Since this morning, the retry queue has been growing and the delivery dashboard shows a mix of 200s and 500s, but I'm not sure how to isolate the bad endpoint or what the expected payload should look like after our recent workflow changes. I need both troubleshooting help and some guidance on how this integration is supposed to behave.

comments.message: Our webhook retries jumped from 0 to 1,200 today. We have three endpoints configured for different event types, and I can't tell which one is causing the backlog. Also, did the payload format change when we added custom fields to tickets?
Usually retries mean your server is rejecting requests. The payload shouldn't change, so I would start by checking your server logs.
I already checked and only one service shows intermittent 500s, but I still need to know how to identify which webhook subscription maps to which retry group in your dashboard.
I don't think the dashboard separates that level of detail. If one of your services has 500s, that's probably the cause. You may want to disable all webhooks and re-enable them one at a time.

subject: Regional access issue for users in Germany office
description: Only our Germany office is unable to sign in today. Staff in the US can log in normally. The German users enter credentials successfully, then see a blank page before being returned to the sign-in form. This started right after we enabled your new IP access policy.

comments.message: We added the Berlin office IP range to the allowlist this morning. Now everyone there is stuck in a login loop, while other offices are fine.

I reviewed the policy and it appears your Berlin IPs may be formatted incorrectly in CIDR notation. I can also see repeated blocked access events from that region. Frontline support can’t edit network access policies on behalf of customers.
Can you at least tell me the correct format? We copied the range from our firewall team.

I’ve escalated this to our security support queue and included the current allowlist entries plus recent denied login events. They can verify the syntax and, if needed, temporarily relax the rule set. If urgent, you can disable the new IP policy from a non-affected admin session to restore access while they investigate.

subject: CSV import duplicated archived contacts after field mapping change
description: I imported a CSV of about 4,800 contacts into our sandbox yesterday, and after remapping the "External ID" field, the system created duplicates for a few hundred records that were previously archived. I tried deleting and re-importing a smaller sample, but the duplicate behavior only happens when I include the lifecycle status column. Can you help me figure out whether this is a bug in the importer and how to clean up the bad records without losing the original metadata?

comments.message: I imported a CSV of about 4,800 contacts into our sandbox yesterday, and after remapping the "External ID" field, the system created duplicates for a few hundred records that were previously archived. I tried deleting and re-importing a smaller sample, but the duplicate behavior only happens when I include the lifecycle status column. Can you help me figure out whether this is a bug in the importer and how to clean up the bad records without losing the original metadata?

Thanks for the details. I checked our import logs and I can see multiple upsert attempts where archived contacts were treated as new records once the lifecycle status value was present. That does look abnormal. As a workaround, please export the affected contacts with record ID, remove the lifecycle status column, and re-import using External ID only. I can also provide a bulk delete filter for the duplicates if you share the import batch ID.
The batch ID is IMP-88241. If I remove lifecycle status, will the archived records stay archived, or will they come back as active?

I reviewed batch IMP-88241 and confirmed 312 duplicates were created from archived source records. Removing lifecycle status during the re-import will preserve the existing archived state on matched records; it should not reactivate them. I’ve flagged this as a probable importer bug to engineering and attached the duplicate record IDs to your case. You can safely bulk delete the 312 new records by filtering on created_at for that batch window.

subject: Custom renewal field won't update from quote status workflow
description: I'm trying to automate our renewals process so that when a quote status changes to Signed, a custom account field called Renewal Stage updates to Renewed. I created the field and a workflow on quote objects, but the account field never changes. The quote update itself saves fine, so I think I may have configured the action against the wrong object relationship.

comments.message: The workflow is on Quote. Trigger is Quote Status changes to Signed. Action is Update field Renewal Stage = Renewed. But nothing updates on the customer account record.

Workflows on Quote can only update fields on the Quote itself unless you explicitly use a related-record action. In Product Configuration, verify that Renewal Stage is an Account field, not a Quote field with the same label. Then edit the automation action to "Update related Account" and select Renewal Stage there. If you don't see that action, your plan may not support cross-object updates.
I checked and yes, Renewal Stage is definitely on the Account object. I also don't have an "Update related Account" action available.
Then this is probably a plan limitation. As a workaround, you could clone Renewal Stage onto Quote and report from there for now. Otherwise you'll need an upgrade.

subject: Scheduled report stopped after approval workflow change
description: I changed our deal approval workflow on Friday to add a second approver, and since then our Monday morning pipeline report never arrived. The report is supposed to email our leadership team every week, and it filters on deals that reached the final approved stage. Now the automation is moving records differently and the report run seems to be failing or returning zero rows. Can someone help figure out whether the workflow broke the report schedule?

comments.message: Our weekly scheduled pipeline report didn't send today after we updated the approval workflow. The workflow now adds a legal approval step before finance approval, and deals are no longer landing where the report expects them. I need to know if this is a reporting issue or an automation issue.

I checked the report configuration and the schedule itself is still active. The problem is that your report is filtered to 'Stage = Finance Approved,' but your workflow automation now moves qualifying deals into 'Pending Legal' first and only later into 'Finance Review.' Because of that, the report had no matching records at the scheduled send time. I recommend updating the report filter to use your final approval status field rather than the intermediate stage.
That makes sense, but some deals have fully completed both approvals and still didn't show up when I ran the report manually.

I dug a bit deeper and found the workflow also stopped populating the custom field 'Final Approval Complete' for records created via API. That's why the revised report still misses some deals. I've escalated this to our automation specialists with your workflow ID and two sample record IDs so they can review the branch handling. In the meantime, I've suggested a temporary report filter using 'Legal Approved = true' and 'Finance Approved = true' so you can pull today's numbers manually.

subject: Merged duplicate users and now mentions point to the wrong person
description: We had duplicate user accounts for one employee after changing her email and display name. I used the merge option in the admin panel, but now old comments mention the wrong profile photo and notifications are going to her inactive account. I need to know whether the merge can be corrected and what to do about the broken mentions.

comments.message: The inactive account had the old Gmail address and the active one has her company address. After merging, the activity feed looks inconsistent and she missed two notifications today.

Merging should consolidate the records automatically. For troubleshooting, please ask the user to update her avatar and notification preferences on the active profile, then wait a few minutes for the mentions index to refresh.
She did that about an hour ago and the old avatar is still shown on historical comments. New notifications are also still hitting the inactive Gmail inbox somehow.

I can see the merge completed only at the profile layer; the notification routing and mention cache did not rebuild. I’ve opened a repair task with our platform team to reindex mentions and detach the inactive email from notification delivery. Until that completes, some historical avatars may remain inconsistent, but new notifications should stop going to the old address once the repair runs.

subject: Renomeei o workspace e os relatórios ficaram inacessíveis
description: Eu alterei o nome do nosso workspace e a URL amigável da conta no painel de administração. Desde então, a área de relatórios abre sem dados para alguns administradores e para outros dá erro de permissões. Os usuários normais continuam trabalhando, então parece algo ligado à mudança da conta e não ao uso geral do sistema. Preciso saber se há como reverter ou se vocês conseguem verificar um bug.

comments.message: Depois de renomear o workspace e a URL amigável da conta, a área de relatórios ficou sem dados para alguns admins e com erro de permissão para outros.
Isso costuma acontecer quando os relatórios ainda estão reprocessando após mudanças de URL. Sugiro aguardar 24 horas e evitar acessar a área nesse período.
Nós já esperamos desde ontem de manhã. Além disso, o suporte interno viu erros diferentes para administradores distintos, o que parece estranho.

Entendo. Se passou de 24 horas, talvez haja um problema mais específico. No momento não tenho como validar logs daqui, mas você pode tentar recriar manualmente as permissões dos administradores afetados. Se não funcionar, abrimos um chamado técnico.

subject: Gelöschter Benutzer erscheint weiterhin in Zuweisungsliste
description: Ich habe einen Nutzer letzte Woche gelöscht, aber sein Name taucht immer noch in der Dropdown-Liste bei der Ticketzuweisung auf. Das sorgt für Fehlzuweisungen im Team. Ist das ein Cache-Problem oder wurde der Benutzer nicht vollständig entfernt?

comments.message: Zur Info: Der Benutzer ist in der Benutzerverwaltung nicht mehr auffindbar, nur noch bei alten Tickets und in der Zuweisungsauswahl.

Danke. Frühere Ticket-Historien behalten den Namen aus Nachvollziehbarkeitsgründen oft bei, aber in der aktiven Zuweisungsliste sollte ein gelöschter Benutzer normalerweise nicht mehr erscheinen. Können Sie prüfen, ob es sich eventuell um einen gleichnamigen Benutzer in einer anderen Gruppe handelt?
Nein, es gibt keinen zweiten mit dem Namen. Die Auswahl erscheint nur in einem bestimmten Formular.

Dann vermute ich einen veralteten Referenzwert im Formularfeld oder Index. Ich kann das nicht direkt hier bereinigen und gebe den Fall an unser Produktteam weiter. Bitte schicken Sie mir noch die Formular-ID oder einen Screenshot der betroffenen Zuweisungsliste, damit wir die Benutzerreferenz gezielt prüfen können.

subject: SSO login succeeds but API calls return unauthorized
description: I'm able to sign into the web app through Okta without any problem, but when I use the API token generated under my profile, every request to the v2 endpoints comes back 401 unauthorized. This started yesterday after we enforced SSO for everyone in our workspace. I need to know whether the token was invalidated or if there's a bug with accounts authenticated via SSO.

comments.message: We turned on mandatory Okta SSO yesterday. Since then I can still access the dashboard, but our integration using my personal API token gets 401 on every call to /api/v2/projects. I regenerated the token twice and saw the same result.

Thanks for the details. I checked our known issues list and there is an active bug where enabling enforced SSO can incorrectly mark user-scoped API tokens as inactive even though login still works. As a workaround, please create a service account that is excluded from SSO enforcement and generate a token from that account.
We don't currently have service accounts enabled on our plan. Is there anything else we can do today?

I can't fix the token state from support, so I've escalated this to engineering with your workspace ID and the timing of the SSO change. In the meantime, if an owner temporarily disables enforced SSO, your newly generated user token should work again until the bug is patched.

subject: Time tracking entries disappear after editing duration
description: Our support reps are logging time on tickets, but we've noticed that some entries disappear right after they edit the duration field. The entry is visible when first created, then after changing 30 minutes to 45 minutes and hitting save, it vanishes from the ticket history. This doesn't happen every time, but I've reproduced it on three tickets today. We use the web app only.

comments.message: Time entries are disappearing after we edit the duration. Can you help troubleshoot why the save seems to remove the entry?
Yes. To isolate it, can you tell me whether the disappearing entries were created manually or by the timer, and whether they include notes or billable status?
All three were created manually, marked billable, and two of them had notes. I just tested a non-billable one and that saved correctly after editing.

That points to a bug in the billable-entry update path rather than a general save issue. I was able to reproduce a similar failure when editing manual billable entries that already have notes attached. For now, users should avoid editing those entries directly; instead delete and recreate them with the correct duration. I've escalated this with your reproduction steps and marked it high priority because it affects billing records.

subject: Can't log in after enabling SSO
description: I turned on SAML SSO for our account this morning, and now several users can log in but I just get bounced back to the login page after authenticating with Okta. I cleared cookies and tried another browser with the same result. We need access today, so I need help troubleshooting what is misconfigured.

comments.message: I turned on SAML SSO for our account this morning, and now several users can log in but I just get bounced back to the login page after authenticating with Okta. I cleared cookies and tried another browser with the same result. We need access today, so I need help troubleshooting what is misconfigured.

Sorry you're running into this. A login loop after successful IdP authentication usually points to either the ACS URL or audience/issuer not matching exactly, or your user email coming from Okta in a different format than the one stored in our app. Can you confirm whether your Okta app is sending NameID as email, and whether you recently changed your login domain?
We did change our company email domain last week. In Okta, NameID is still using the old domain for me, but some users were already updated.

That would explain why some users can enter and you cannot. Please update the Okta username/NameID mapping for your account so it sends the same email address that exists in our platform, then try again in an incognito window. If you need both domains supported temporarily, your account team can help with a migration plan, but correcting the mismatch should restore your access first.

subject: Users can log in again, but HubSpot contacts stopped updating
description: I locked down access with Google SSO and cleaned up some user roles this morning. Now everyone can log in, but our HubSpot integration is only creating new contacts and not updating existing ones. We also found that some imported contacts lost their lifecycle stage in your platform. I need to know if this is tied to the permission changes or if the integration broke independently.

comments.message: After moving everyone to Google SSO, I removed a few old super admins and changed our integration connection to my account. Login seems fine now. But the HubSpot sync started creating duplicate contacts instead of updating matches, and lifecycle stage is empty for a bunch of today's imports.
This sounds like a cache delay after changing admins. Duplicates usually clear up on the next sync cycle. Please wait 24 hours and the lifecycle stage values should come back automatically.

We already waited through three sync cycles. New duplicates are still being created and the sync logs show 'match key unavailable' for updates. I don't want to wait another day if the wrong account permissions are the issue.

If the log says 'match key unavailable,' then it may be related to the connected HubSpot user not having access to the unique identifier property. I can't verify that from here, but you can try reconnecting the integration with a super admin and re-running the import.

subject: New VP needs admin control but we want limits on what they can change
description: Our new VP of Support wants visibility into settings and user management, but finance does not want them changing subscription or contract details. I'm trying to figure out the right role assignment and whether we can update the main account contact separately. Please explain the least risky setup.

comments.message: What role should we give the VP if they need to manage teams and settings but should not be able to touch billing or renewals?
Give them owner access so they can see everything, and then tell them not to change billing.
We need an actual control, not a trust-based workaround.

That's fair. The lower-risk approach is usually to grant the highest admin role that covers operational settings and user management, while keeping ownership, billing contact, and renewal contact with finance or an existing executive sponsor. Whether billing can be fully restricted depends on your plan's role granularity. In general, account contact records can be maintained separately from operational admins. If you want exact limits by role for your subscription, I can escalate this to the accounts team or provide the permissions matrix for your plan.

subject: Webhook test passes but live events are missing fields
description: We're sending ticket update webhooks to our internal endpoint. The test payload from the admin page includes our custom priority and region fields, but the actual live events do not. Can you look into whether the production event serializer is inconsistent, and explain the difference between test webhooks and real event payloads?

comments.message: This broke our downstream routing logic because we depend on region. We only noticed after moving from sandbox to production.
Test webhooks are sample payloads and not always identical to live events. Live events may omit empty custom fields.
The fields are not empty. I checked three tickets where both values are set, and the endpoint logs still don't show them. Are you saying that's expected?

I took a closer look and you're right, it's not expected in your examples. I can reproduce that live ticket.update events from your account are excluding two custom fields that are present in the test payload and in the ticket record. I've escalated this to engineering as a serialization bug.
