To answer your other question: test payloads are generated from a template meant to illustrate the schema, while live events are built from the actual event stream at send time, so differences can reveal real serializer issues like this one.

subject: Login loop after enabling SSO and CSV import API no longer accepts updates
description: We enabled identity-provider login for everyone and now a subset of users gets bounced back to the sign-in page in a loop unless I temporarily disable enforcement. Separately, our custom CSV import API integration used to update records by customer ID, but today it is creating new rows with empty region values instead. We also rotated API credentials during the same change window, so I'm not sure what broke what.

comments.message: About 15 users hit an infinite redirect after SSO login, though admins are fine. Also our import job that sends CSVs through the API is no longer updating existing records. It's creating duplicates, and region is blank in the imported data.

The login loop for non-admins may indicate those users aren't assigned to the SSO application or their post-login role is invalid. On the import side, if you rotated API credentials, the new token may belong to a role without access to the customer ID match field or region column. Can you confirm whether the API token was regenerated under a different user and whether the affected users share the same role?
Yes to both. The token is now under a junior ops user, and all the looping users have a custom 'field rep' role.

That explains the pattern. Assign the field rep role to the SSO app in your identity provider and verify that role is allowed to sign in to our workspace. Then recreate the API token under a service user with permission to read the customer ID key and write region values. After that, your imports should resume matching records, but you'll need to manually correct the duplicates already inserted by today's jobs.

subject: Invoice failed, account suspended, and owner account can’t get in
description: Our card on file expired, and after the last payment failed the workspace was put into restricted mode. The problem is I’m the account owner and I can’t log in to update the payment method because the login page says owner access is disabled while the subscription is past due. This is blocking our team from exporting records today. Can someone help me regain access and tell me exactly what amount is due?

comments.message: Our card on file expired, and after the last payment failed the workspace was put into restricted mode. The problem is I’m the account owner and I can’t log in to update the payment method because the login page says owner access is disabled while the subscription is past due. This is blocking our team from exporting records today. Can someone help me regain access and tell me exactly what amount is due?

I reviewed the account and found two failed payment attempts on invoice INV-88421 for $1,240.00. The suspension rule should not block the owner from updating billing, so that part is not expected behavior. I’ve generated a secure payment-update link that bypasses the normal login for 48 hours; once you update the card, the restriction should lift automatically within 15 minutes.
I used the link and updated the card. The charge went through, but the workspace still says restricted and I still can’t access billing from the normal login.

The payment has posted successfully. I manually removed the suspension flag and reset the owner login state on your profile. Please sign out, then sign back in normally. If billing access still fails after that, reply here and I’ll escalate it as an access defect, but the past-due balance is now cleared.

subject: Rotated API token while updating account alias and now scripts are inconsistent
description: I changed the name and email alias on the admin account used for our scripts, and at the same time I rotated the API token as a precaution. Some scripts work with the new token, but one older integration still says the token belongs to an inactive user. I'm not sure if that message is because of the account change, cached credentials, or the fact that we also disabled the old alias. Can you help me sort out what to check?

comments.message: We updated the admin's profile name and email alias, generated a new API token, and disabled the old mailbox alias. Most automation is fine now, but one script gets 'token belongs to inactive user' even though the admin account is active. What should I verify?

That error usually means the script is still sending an old token tied to a deactivated account, but in your case it could also indicate the token was generated before the account rename and is now invalid. Please regenerate the token again and restart the affected script host.
We already regenerated once. After checking the config, that script was still reading the previous token from an environment variable on a different server.

Thanks for confirming. Then the account alias change was probably unrelated, and the issue was stale credentials on that host. Once you update the environment variable and restart the process, the error should clear. As a cleanup step, I'd remove any old tokens and document which servers consume which credential so future account changes don't get mixed up with token rotation.

subject: Export failed after plan downgrade and some rows are blank
description: We downgraded from Enterprise to Growth at renewal, and since then our scheduled customer export stopped working. I manually exported the same dataset today, but several rows have blank email and owner fields even though those values appear in the app. Our latest invoice also includes an overage charge that I thought would stop after the downgrade. Please help sort out both the billing and the data export problem.

comments.message: After our downgrade, the scheduled export failed, and manual CSV exports have blank columns for some customer records. Also, we were billed an overage fee this month even though we changed plans. What happened?
The overage charge is normal because invoices are generated automatically. For the export issue, blank fields in CSV usually mean the browser cached an old file, so please clear cache and try again.
This was generated from your server, not my browser cache, and the blank fields are consistent across multiple downloads and users. Can someone actually check the account?

I understand. I've escalated the export issue to our technical team with examples of the blank fields. Regarding billing, I recommend contacting your account manager if you think the downgrade should have removed the overage, since support can't change invoices directly.

subject: Importei usuários com centros de custo, mas o relatório veio em branco
description: Fiz a criação em massa de usuários usando nossa planilha com a coluna de centro de custo, porque depois preciso segmentar os relatórios financeiros por esse campo. Os usuários foram criados normalmente e conseguem acessar o sistema, mas no construtor de relatórios o campo centro de custo aparece vazio para todos os novos cadastros. Já comparei a planilha com importações anteriores e não achei diferença.

comments.message: A coluna estava com o cabeçalho 'Centro de Custo' e valores como CC-204, CC-310 e CC-511. Na administração de usuários eu também não vejo esse atributo preenchido nos perfis novos.

Olá! Se o atributo não aparece nem no perfil do usuário, a importação provavelmente não mapeou a coluna para o campo personalizado correto. Em muitas contas, 'Centro de Custo' é um atributo customizado interno e precisa ser selecionado manualmente no passo de mapeamento do CSV.
Agora faz sentido. Eu deixei o mapeamento automático porque o nome da coluna era igual ao do campo. Tem como corrigir sem recriar todos os usuários?

Tem sim. Você pode fazer uma nova importação usando o mesmo identificador de usuário e mapear explicitamente a coluna para o atributo customizado 'Centro de Custo'; isso atualiza os perfis existentes sem recriá-los. Depois da atualização, o campo ficará disponível nos relatórios após a próxima sincronização.

subject: Login audit shows success, but user lands in someone else’s last page
description: One of our shared kiosk users signs in successfully, but the app sometimes opens to the previous person's last viewed record before correcting itself or throwing an authorization warning. This is obviously concerning from an access perspective. I need to know if this is just session carryover from the browser or a real bug in how your app handles login transitions on shared devices, and what settings we should use to prevent it.

comments.message: This happens on an iPad in our warehouse where multiple supervisors log in and out during a shift. They use the same browser profile.

For shared devices, our best practice is to use a private browsing window and fully sign out after each session. What you're likely seeing is the browser reusing cached state between users rather than data actually crossing accounts on the server.
I understand the best practice, but seeing another user's record for even a second seems wrong. Is that expected?

No, even on a shared browser the app should not render a prior user's protected page after a new login begins. I was able to find a similar internal bug tied to cached client-side route state on iPad Safari. Please switch the kiosk to private browsing for now and disable 'restore previous tabs' if enabled. I've escalated this as a security-sensitive bug to engineering for investigation, because the correct behavior is to clear the prior route before any new session renders.

subject: User deactivated by mistake and now their reports are missing
description: I accidentally deactivated one of our analysts while trying to remove a different contractor. I restored her account, but now she can’t log in and the team says some scheduled reports stopped running. I need to get her back in and understand what happened to her ownership settings.

comments.message: Her name is Ava Chen. After I reactivated her, she gets 'your account is not authorized to access this workspace.' In the Users page she looks active again.

When a user is reactivated, their login usually works immediately, but any content ownership transfers made during deactivation are not automatically reversed. First, open Ava’s user record and confirm she was restored to the correct workspace role, not just reactivated as a basic contact. Then check whether her scheduled reports were reassigned to another owner during the deactivation flow.
You were right — she came back as a Lite user, not Analyst. I changed the role and she can sign in now. The reports are still owned by my account though.

Glad login is working again. The report ownership transfer would need to be reversed manually; deactivation permanently reassigns owned scheduled jobs. You can either edit each report and set Ava back as owner, or from User Administration export the ownership audit and use it as a checklist. Going forward, the deactivation dialog has a review step that shows which assets will be reassigned before you confirm.

subject: Me cobraron después de cancelar y además no puedo iniciar sesión
description: Cancelé la suscripción hace dos semanas porque ya no la necesitábamos, pero hoy apareció otro cobro en mi tarjeta. Encima, al intentar entrar para descargar nuestras últimas facturas, sale un error de inicio de sesión que dice "token inválido". Necesito una explicación y acceso a la cuenta.

comments.message: Cancelé la suscripción hace dos semanas porque ya no la necesitábamos, pero hoy apareció otro cobro en mi tarjeta. Encima, al intentar entrar para descargar nuestras últimas facturas, sale un error de inicio de sesión que dice "token inválido". Necesito una explicación y acceso a la cuenta.

Lamento la molestia. Según veo, la suscripción no se canceló por completo; se programó para fin de ciclo, por eso se generó el último cobro. Ya envié la cuenta de nuevo al estado de acceso limitado para que puedan entrar a descargar facturas, pero el error "token inválido" suele resolverse restableciendo la contraseña. ¿Pueden intentarlo?

Sí, con el restablecimiento pude entrar, pero en la pantalla de cancelación se leía "cancelar ahora", no "fin de ciclo". Si ese cobro corresponde al último ciclo, necesito la factura. Si no, quiero reembolso.

Gracias por confirmarlo. Ya habilité acceso de solo lectura por 72 horas para su usuario y la factura del cobro está disponible en Facturación > Historial. Sobre la discrepancia del texto de cancelación, no puedo prometer reembolso desde soporte, pero he adjuntado su comentario a una revisión con billing para validar si la cancelación se presentó de forma ambigua.

subject: Need to update legal entity but not sure which settings matter
description: Our company merged into a new legal entity, and I need to update the account information so contracts and notifications use the new name. I found fields for organization name, billing contact, and workspace name, but I can't tell which one actually affects what customers and internal users see. Is there a recommended order for making these changes?

comments.message: Our company merged into a new legal entity, and I need to update the account information so contracts and notifications use the new name. I found fields for organization name, billing contact, and workspace name, but I can't tell which one actually affects what customers and internal users see. Is there a recommended order for making these changes?
You should probably just change the workspace name first. That usually updates everything important, including invoices and contracts.
I need to be careful here because legal and billing are separate from our app branding. Are you saying there isn't a separate field for the legal company name?
I'm not aware of one in our system. If the workspace name looks right, your users and finance team should be covered.

subject: User export shows wrong last-login dates after account merges
description: I’m cleaning up duplicate users after a domain change, and the admin export now shows several merged accounts with last-login dates from 1970 or blank values. Those same users also fail when I try to reset MFA because the admin panel says they have never logged in, which is false. I’ve tested on multiple browsers and compared against audit logs showing recent access. Can you help determine if the merge process corrupted user records?

comments.message: After merging duplicate users, the export shows wrong last-login dates and MFA reset fails because the system thinks they never logged in.

Thanks, that does sound related to the merge operation rather than browser behavior. To troubleshoot, can you confirm whether the affected users were merged from personal-email accounts into SSO-managed work-email accounts, and whether the bad dates appear immediately in the admin UI as well as the CSV export?
Yes, exactly. Personal-email accounts were merged into SSO-managed work accounts, and the bad dates appear both in the UI and export.

That pattern matches a known issue where authentication metadata is not reattached correctly after certain cross-identity merges. I’ve escalated your tenant and sample user IDs to our account-data team for repair. Until they respond, please avoid additional merges for that domain, and if a user is blocked by MFA reset, open a temporary exception by disabling enforced MFA at the group level for just that user’s team if your policy allows it.

subject: Synchronisation NetSuite dupliquée après changement de sandbox à production
description: Nous sommes passés de la sandbox NetSuite à l'environnement de production, et depuis ce basculement les factures synchronisées dans votre application apparaissent en double. J'ai bien mis à jour les identifiants OAuth et l'account ID, mais on dirait que les enregistrements de sandbox et de production ne sont pas distingués. Je voudrais éviter de purger manuellement plusieurs milliers de lignes si possible.

comments.message: Bonjour, après passage de NetSuite sandbox à production, nos factures sont dupliquées dans votre plateforme. On dirait que vous considérez les mêmes numéros comme de nouveaux objets.

Bonjour, merci pour les détails. Oui, c'est probable si la clé de correspondance utilisait seulement le numéro de facture. Lors d'un passage sandbox > production, il faut généralement rebaser l'intégration sur l'identifiant interne NetSuite + environnement source. Avez-vous conservé le même connecteur existant ou créé une nouvelle connexion ?
J'ai réutilisé le connecteur existant et juste remplacé les identifiants OAuth. Je ne voyais pas d'option pour changer la clé de correspondance.

Dans ce cas, il vaut mieux ne pas continuer à synchroniser sans correction. J'escalade le dossier à notre équipe intégrations pour qu'elle modifie la clé de rapprochement sur votre tenant et lance une fusion des doublons importés depuis la mise en production. En attendant, désactivez la synchro automatique NetSuite pour éviter d'autres doublons. Je joins à l'escalade votre ID de connecteur et la fenêtre de temps concernée.

subject: Charged after workspace merge and historical notes disappeared
description: We merged two regional workspaces into one account last month. Since then, we are still being billed for the old workspace, and historical notes from one region are missing on merged customer timelines. The records exist in counts, but the note bodies are blank in the UI and exports. We need this fixed because account managers rely on those notes, and finance wants the duplicate billing removed.

comments.message: After merging our EU workspace into our main one, we're still being charged for the old workspace. Also, historical notes from that workspace show as blank after migration. Can someone sort both issues out?

Workspace merge billing can take one cycle to settle, so the old workspace charge should disappear automatically next month. As for the blank notes, if the record count is there then the data has probably migrated correctly.
That doesn't help. Our users can see the note entries but not the actual content, and exports are blank too. We can't just assume it's fine.

I agree that blank content in both UI and export indicates a migration problem, so I'm escalating the merged note data issue to our backend team. I wasn't able to verify from support whether the legacy workspace should have terminated before invoicing, so I've also sent the billing portion to our account adjustments queue for review rather than asking you to wait another cycle.

subject: Automatische Ticketzuweisung nach Region funktioniert nur teilweise
description: Wir verteilen eingehende Support-Tickets per Workflow automatisch nach Region an verschiedene Teams. Für Deutschland und Österreich klappt es, aber Tickets aus der Schweiz bleiben seit letzter Woche in der allgemeinen Warteschlange hängen. An den Formularen wurde laut meinem Wissen nichts geändert. Können Sie mir sagen, warum die Automatisierung die CH-Tickets nicht mehr erkennt?

comments.message: Die Regel sollte bei Land = Schweiz an das DACH-Team zuweisen. Stattdessen bleiben die Tickets ohne Owner. Im Verlauf sehe ich nur "Bedingungen nicht erfüllt".

Das klingt so, als ob der Länderwert nicht exakt mit der Workflow-Bedingung übereinstimmt. Bitte prüfen Sie, ob neue Tickets den Wert "CH", "Schweiz" oder vielleicht "Switzerland" speichern. Wenn der Workflow nur auf einen dieser Werte hört, werden die anderen ignoriert.
Sie haben recht, bei den neuen Tickets steht jetzt "CH". Die Regel sucht aber nach "Schweiz".

Perfekt, dann haben wir die Ursache. Ergänzen Sie in der Verzweigung entweder beide Werte oder normalisieren Sie das Land vor der Zuweisung in einem vorgeschalteten Schritt. Danach sollte die automatische Ticketzuweisung für die Schweiz wieder greifen.

subject: Department admin added users but reports still exclude them
description: I created a new Department Admin so local managers could onboard their own staff. She successfully added six new users to the East region, but the regional staffing report still shows zero records for those employees even after sync completed. I’m trying to figure out if this is a reporting delay, a permission issue with the new admin role, or if user-created accounts need to be mapped somewhere else before they appear in analytics.

comments.message: I created a new Department Admin so local managers could onboard their own staff. She successfully added six new users to the East region, but the regional staffing report still shows zero records for those employees even after sync completed. I’m trying to figure out if this is a reporting delay, a permission issue with the new admin role, or if user-created accounts need to be mapped somewhere else before they appear in analytics.

Newly created users should appear in reporting instantly, so if they don’t, it usually means the accounts were not created correctly. Please delete the six users and recreate them under your own admin account instead of the Department Admin account.
That seems risky because they’ve already started using the system. Is there another way to check whether they’re missing a reporting attribute?
I don’t have another troubleshooting step for that. Recreating them would be the recommended path.

subject: Looping automation after adding fallback assignee group
description: I added a fallback group to catch requests when no case owner is found, but ever since then some records are bouncing between statuses and repeatedly reassigning themselves. We also recently changed user permissions for a few coordinators, so I'm not sure whether this is caused by the workflow logic or the assignee configuration.

comments.message: The workflow is supposed to assign to the record owner, and if owner is blank, send it to Operations Queue. Instead, records go to Operations Queue, a coordinator edits them, status changes to In Review, then the workflow fires again and reassigns back to the queue even though the coordinator is already the assignee.

Thanks, that's helpful. It sounds like the automation trigger is based on status change rather than only on creation or owner evaluation, so every coordinator update is re-entering the routing logic. If the coordinators also lost the permission that marks them as eligible individual assignees, the workflow may see them as invalid and fall back to the queue again. Can you confirm whether the rule includes a stop condition like 'do not run if assignee is set' and whether those coordinators still have the Case Handler role in User Administration?
There is no stop condition. And looking now, two coordinators were moved to a lighter custom role last week, so they no longer have Case Handler.

That explains the loop. Update the workflow to run only on record creation or only when assignee is empty, and restore or replace the permission needed for coordinators to be valid assignees. Otherwise the automation will keep treating their updates as unowned work and send the record back to the fallback queue.

subject: New user invite expires immediately
description: I'm trying to add two contractors to our account, but every invitation email we send says the link is invalid as soon as they click it. I removed and re-added both users three times. We are on Chrome and Edge and seeing the same result. Is there something wrong with how these user records were created?

comments.message: I'm trying to add two contractors to our account, but every invitation email we send says the link is invalid as soon as they click it. I removed and re-added both users three times. We are on Chrome and Edge and seeing the same result. Is there something wrong with how these user records were created?
Invitation links usually fail when the browser blocks cookies. Please ask them to try another browser or clear cache.
They already tried multiple browsers. Also, this is happening only for the users I created yesterday, not older invites.
In that case it may be a temporary mail delivery issue. Please wait 24 hours and send the invites again.

subject: Permissão de edição bloqueando usuários mesmo com perfil correto
description: Alguns coordenadores não conseguem editar registros de clientes, embora o perfil deles tenha permissão de edição global. Revisei os perfis e está tudo habilitado. Parece haver algum conflito de configuração, mas não consigo localizar.

comments.message: Eles conseguem abrir o registro, comentar e anexar arquivos, mas o botão de editar fica cinza.

Olá! Se o perfil permite editar mas o botão está cinza, normalmente existe uma regra adicional, como bloqueio por proprietário, status do registro ou layout em modo leitura. Esses registros estão em algum status fechado ou com bloqueio por aprovação?
Agora que você falou, todos estão com status "Aguardando aprovação financeira".

Perfeito, achei a causa provável. Em Configurações > Objetos > Regras de bloqueio, verifique se esse status está configurado como somente leitura para não administradores. Se estiver, remova a restrição ou inclua o papel Coordenador na exceção. Depois disso o botão de editar deve voltar para eles imediatamente.

subject: Password reset emails arrive, but links immediately expire
description: Several users on our team requested password resets today. The emails arrive right away, but every reset link says it has already expired, even when opened within a minute. We have one admin still logged in, but the affected users are completely blocked from accessing the platform.

comments.message: This is affecting at least nine users across two departments. We tested on different browsers and even copied the link manually, same result.
Password reset links expire after 24 hours, so they should definitely still work. It may just be a local email client issue.
It is not an email client issue if it happens in Gmail web and Outlook desktop and the links fail instantly.

You’re right, and I don’t have a direct fix from my side. I’m escalating this to the authentication team with examples from the failed reset URLs and timestamps. Please have one affected user avoid requesting additional resets for now so engineering can inspect a stable example token.

subject: File attachments fail only for drag and drop
description: Users on our account can upload attachments if they click the upload button, but dragging files into the comment box fails silently. No progress bar, no error, and nothing gets attached. This started after last week's release. We've tried PDFs and PNGs, small files and larger ones, and it's happening for multiple agents in Chrome. Since click-to-upload still works, it feels like something specific to drag and drop broke.

comments.message: Drag-and-drop attachments stopped working, although the normal upload button still works. How can we troubleshoot this?
Please open your browser console and check for any errors when you drag a file in. Also let me know whether any browser extensions are active that might interfere with the page.
We tested on two machines. Both showed a console error mentioning 'dropzone undefined' when dragging a file in, and one browser had no extensions at all.

That error is useful. It suggests a front-end regression in the drag-and-drop uploader rather than a file validation problem. I wasn't able to find a workaround besides using the upload button for now, but I've documented the console error and escalated this to the web app team as a likely bug introduced in the recent release.

subject: Automation disabled after plan change and overage invoice doesn't make sense
description: We upgraded from Growth to Scale to support more workflow runs, and at the same time I changed our default request form so new client accounts are created automatically with a regional admin owner. Since the plan change, one of our automations has been disabled for exceeding limits, but the overage invoice shows a higher run count than what the workflow dashboard reports. Also, newly created accounts are still being assigned to me instead of the regional admins we configured. I need someone to explain the invoice and fix the account assignment logic.

comments.message: Your dashboard says 41,200 workflow runs for March, but the overage invoice says 48,900. We upgraded specifically to avoid hitting caps, and the account ownership automation is still ignoring the region mapping table.

I reviewed the billing side and the invoice includes production runs plus test runs triggered in sandbox projects that are linked to your paid workspace. The dashboard widget you’re looking at appears to show production runs only, which is why the counts differ. On the automation issue, I can see your ‘Create client account’ flow still references a hard-coded fallback owner ID—your user ID.
That explains part of it, but the upgrade went through on March 10. Were we billed at the old limit for the first 10 days and the new limit after that?

Yes. The overage calculation was split across both plan periods, but I agree the invoice PDF is not clear about that breakdown. I’ve exported the daily run totals and attached them to the ticket for transparency. For the owner assignment, update step 4 to use the ‘Regional Admin Email’ variable instead of the fallback ID; I can’t edit the flow on your behalf, but that should stop new accounts from defaulting to you.

subject: Magic link opens, then says session invalid
description: Our sales reps are using magic link login because they don't have passwords set. Since this afternoon, the email link opens the app in a new tab and immediately shows 'session invalid, please sign in again.' It seems to happen mostly in Outlook desktop, but one person reproduced it in Gmail too.

comments.message: Our sales reps are using magic link login because they don't have passwords set. Since this afternoon, the email link opens the app in a new tab and immediately shows 'session invalid, please sign in again.' It seems to happen mostly in Outlook desktop, but one person reproduced it in Gmail too.
Please ask users to copy and paste the full magic link into Chrome instead of clicking it. Email clients sometimes break long URLs.
We tried that and it still fails for two users. Another rep can use the same flow without any issue.
I checked quickly and don't see a platform incident. It may just be those users' browsers. Have them clear cookies and request another link.

subject: Member login fixed, but API scope list is missing
description: After being re-invited to our workspace, I can log in again, which is great, but I still can't create the scoped API token I need for our internal tool. The token modal loads without any scope checkboxes, and saving gives a generic error. Other users say they can see the scopes normally. Can you confirm whether my access was restored completely?

comments.message: I was removed and re-added to the workspace yesterday after an email domain change. Login is working now, but under API Tokens the scope selector is empty and clicking Create returns 'something went wrong.' I need read:projects and write:comments.
Re-invites can take a few minutes to sync. Please wait an hour and try again.
It's been almost a full day. Still the same behavior.

Thanks for clarifying. I inspected your membership record and it looks like the workspace access was restored, but the API entitlement row was not recreated during the re-invite. That's why login succeeds while token scope retrieval fails. I've corrected the entitlement and verified the scope list now appears on refresh. Please log out once and sign back in before creating the token.

subject: Cobro de impuestos incorrecto y error al subir archivos
description: Somos una empresa con CIF intracomunitario válido y, aun así, en la última factura nos cobraron IVA. Además, desde ayer al subir archivos PDF de más de 20 MB el sistema devuelve "upload interrupted" y no completa la carga. Necesito que corrijan la factura y que me indiquen qué revisar del lado técnico.

comments.message: Somos una empresa con CIF intracomunitario válido y, aun así, en la última factura nos cobraron IVA. Además, desde ayer al subir archivos PDF de más de 20 MB el sistema devuelve "upload interrupted" y no completa la carga. Necesito que corrijan la factura y que me indiquen qué revisar del lado técnico.

Hola. Verifiqué su perfil de facturación y el VAT ID quedó registrado sin prefijo del país, por eso el sistema no lo validó y aplicó IVA en la factura emitida el 1 de mayo. Ya corregí el número a ESB64822109 y envié una solicitud de nota de crédito por el impuesto cobrado.

Sobre la subida de archivos, vemos fallos repetidos desde su tenant cuando la conexión usa inspección SSL corporativa. ¿Podrían probar desde otra red o desactivar temporalmente la inspección para *.uploads.saashub.com? También conviene confirmar que no haya un proxy cortando cargas largas.
Probamos con hotspot del móvil y el PDF subió sin problema. Entonces era la red corporativa. ¿La nota de crédito llegará automáticamente?

Sí, con esa prueba queda bastante claro que el bloqueo está en la inspección SSL o en el proxy de su red. La nota de crédito quedó en estado pendiente de emisión; normalmente se genera automáticamente en menos de 24 horas y recibirán la factura rectificativa por correo.

subject: Refund not issued after canceled duplicate subscription
description: Our admin accidentally created a second subscription for the same workspace during onboarding because the first payment page froze. We canceled the extra subscription within an hour, but we were still charged and no refund has appeared after six days. On top of that, the canceled subscription still shows up intermittently in the billing portal before disappearing again. Please investigate both the missing refund and the billing portal glitch.

comments.message: Our admin accidentally created a second subscription for the same workspace during onboarding because the first payment page froze. We canceled the extra subscription within an hour, but we were still charged and no refund has appeared after six days. On top of that, the canceled subscription still shows up intermittently in the billing portal before disappearing again. Please investigate both the missing refund and the billing portal glitch.

I found the duplicate subscription and can confirm it was canceled the same day. However, the refund job for that charge is sitting in a failed state because the cancellation came in before the initial payment webhook fully settled. That’s a bug we’ve seen occasionally with newly created subscriptions.
So what happens now? We need the money back and our procurement team is confused by the extra subscription appearing sometimes.

I’ve manually re-issued the refund from our billing console, and you’ll receive a credit memo by email shortly. For the portal issue, I can reproduce the canceled subscription briefly reappearing on page refresh, so I’ve escalated that to engineering with screenshots and your account IDs. The refund should post in 5-10 business days depending on your bank; the display bug is still under investigation.

subject: Alteração do alias da conta quebrou integrações mostradas no painel
description: Eu mudei o alias da conta no admin porque padronizamos o nome da empresa em todas as ferramentas. Depois disso, a página de integrações começou a mostrar dois conectores como desconectados, mas os dados continuam chegando normalmente pela API. Quando tento abrir os detalhes dessas integrações, recebo uma mensagem de erro inesperado. Não sei se a alteração da conta provocou um bug visual ou algo mais sério.

comments.message: Depois de mudar o alias da conta, a página de integrações mostra conectores desconectados, embora os dados ainda cheguem pela API. Ao abrir detalhes, dá erro inesperado.
Se os dados continuam chegando, então está tudo normal. O status da página pode demorar para atualizar depois de mudanças administrativas.
Mas já se passaram 3 dias e os detalhes da integração continuam quebrados. Parece mais do que atraso de status.
Entendi, mas no momento não vejo impacto funcional crítico. Sugiro ignorar o aviso por enquanto e monitorar. Se a API parar de enviar dados, entre em contato novamente.

subject: 2FA code accepted but access still denied
description: One of our users can enter the correct 2FA code and the system says authentication successful, but the next screen says access denied. This only happens to him. He was deactivated by mistake last week and then reactivated, so I don't know if that's related.

comments.message: One of our users can enter the correct 2FA code and the system says authentication successful, but the next screen says access denied. This only happens to him. He was deactivated by mistake last week and then reactivated, so I don't know if that's related.
If the 2FA step works, then login is working normally. Access denied usually means the user should contact their internal admin for the right permissions.
I am the internal admin, and his permissions match another analyst who can log in fine. The only difference is that accidental deactivation last week.
Understood. Please try removing and re-adding the user entirely. That often fixes access problems after reactivation.

subject: Need to transfer workspace ownership before our admin leaves
description: I'm the operations manager for our team, and our current workspace owner is leaving the company on Friday. I need to move ownership to me without breaking our SSO setup or losing access to billing and audit logs. I couldn't tell from the help center whether changing the owner also changes who receives renewal notices. Can you explain the right way to do this and what will happen after the transfer?

comments.message: I'm the operations manager for our team, and our current workspace owner is leaving the company on Friday. I need to move ownership to me without breaking our SSO setup or losing access to billing and audit logs. I couldn't tell from the help center whether changing the owner also changes who receives renewal notices. Can you explain the right way to do this and what will happen after the transfer?

Absolutely. Changing workspace ownership does not disable SSO or remove existing audit logs. The safest process is: 1) have the current owner invite you if you're not already an admin, 2) promote you to admin, 3) from Settings > Workspace > Ownership, the current owner selects Transfer Ownership and chooses your profile, and 4) you accept the transfer from the email prompt. Billing contacts and renewal notices do not automatically switch with ownership, so after the transfer you'll want to review Settings > Billing > Contacts and update those separately.
That helps. If the current owner loses access right after the transfer, can I still remove their admin role myself?

Yes. Once you accept ownership, you can manage roles for all users, including downgrading or deactivating the former owner as long as another active admin remains. I also recommend confirming your login method works after the transfer and exporting any owner-specific notifications if needed. If you'd like, I can outline a quick handoff checklist for Friday.

subject: Demande de changement du domaine autorisé pour les invitations
description: Nous avons changé de domaine de messagerie cette semaine, passant de @altius-consulting.com à @altius.eu. Depuis, les nouvelles adresses sont refusées lors de l’invitation des utilisateurs, car seul l’ancien domaine semble autorisé sur le compte. Pouvez-vous mettre à jour ce paramètre rapidement ?

comments.message: Bonjour, impossible d’inviter des collègues avec leur nouvelle adresse en @altius.eu. Le système indique que le domaine n’est pas approuvé pour notre organisation.

Bonjour, je peux m’en occuper. Les changements de domaine autorisé nécessitent une vérification de propriété pour des raisons de sécurité. Merci de m’envoyer le domaine actuel du compte, le nouveau domaine à ajouter, et idéalement une preuve de contrôle du domaine (par exemple une capture de votre console DNS ou un e-mail envoyé depuis ce domaine).

Très bien. Domaine actuel : altius-consulting.com, nouveau domaine : altius.eu. Je vous écris depuis mon adresse claire.renard@altius.eu et je joins une capture de notre gestion DNS montrant le domaine.

Merci, c’est suffisant pour lancer la mise à jour. J’ai ajouté altius.eu aux domaines autorisés et laissé l’ancien domaine actif temporairement pour éviter toute coupure. La propagation peut prendre jusqu’à 30 minutes. Si vous le souhaitez, nous pourrons supprimer altius-consulting.com dans un second temps.

subject: Stripe refund data is offset in churn report
description: Our churn report is treating several refunds from Stripe as if they happened one day later than they did in Stripe, which is moving them into the wrong month-end bucket. We use the Stripe integration and monthly reporting for board metrics, so even a small date shift is a problem. Can someone confirm whether this is a timezone issue in reporting or a bad import from Stripe?

comments.message: Refunds processed late on May 31 in Stripe appear as June 1 in your churn report. This changes our month-end churn numbers. Subscription IDs sub_91A2 and sub_91C7 are two examples.

Our reports use your account timezone, but Stripe events may arrive in UTC and then be normalized before reporting. If the refund timestamp was imported in UTC and your report buckets by local date, that could explain the shift. Are you able to confirm your workspace timezone and whether the report is grouped by processed_at or reporting_date?
Workspace timezone is America/Los_Angeles, and the report uses refund processed date. In Stripe those refunds were definitely still on May 31 local time.

Thanks. Since this could be either an integration timestamp normalization issue or a reporting bucketing issue, I'm escalating with your sample subscription IDs and timezone details. Our billing-data integrations team will inspect the raw Stripe event timestamps, and reporting engineering can confirm which date field the churn report is actually using.

subject: Former employee still appears in group sync after SCIM removal
description: We removed a user from Okta and the SCIM deprovisioning succeeded, but inside your app the user still appears in two manually managed groups and one saved audience. Team leads are worried he may still be included in announcements. I need confirmation on whether this is just a display issue or if his memberships are still active, and if necessary please escalate it.

comments.message: His status is shown as Deactivated, but the name remains in the Customer Success group and in the Q3 Rollout audience filter.
A deactivated user cannot log in, so there is nothing to worry about. The memberships will disappear automatically at some point and you don't need to take any action.
That doesn't answer whether he's still counted in audiences or notifications. We need a definite answer for compliance.

Thanks for clarifying. I can't verify audience-calculation behavior from my side, so I'm escalating this to our identity and messaging team to confirm whether deactivated SCIM users are excluded immediately from manual groups and saved audiences. I've included your compliance concern and the specific group names in the escalation.

subject: Segment membership recalculation skipped records after dedupe
description: We merged a batch of duplicate leads over the weekend, and since then one of our dynamic segments is undercounting by roughly 8%. When I inspect individual lead records, the qualifying fields are present, but the segment membership badge is missing until I manually edit and save the record. It seems like the merge changed the data correctly but didn’t trigger recalculation for some rows. Can you confirm whether segment membership should rebuild automatically after dedupe, and if not, how we can refresh the affected records in bulk?

comments.message: We merged a batch of duplicate leads over the weekend, and since then one of our dynamic segments is undercounting by roughly 8%. When I inspect individual lead records, the qualifying fields are present, but the segment membership badge is missing until I manually edit and save the record. It seems like the merge changed the data correctly but didn’t trigger recalculation for some rows. Can you confirm whether segment membership should rebuild automatically after dedupe, and if not, how we can refresh the affected records in bulk?

Segment membership should normally recalculate after a merge, so the need to manually resave records suggests the post-merge indexing event may not have fired for all leads. I can see a backlog in your workspace’s segmentation queue from Saturday night around the same time as the dedupe job. Would you like me to trigger a full segment rebuild for that object? It may take a few hours and temporarily slow list updates.
Yes, please trigger it. We have a campaign tomorrow, so even a partial fix today would help.

Done — I started a full rebuild for lead segments in your workspace and prioritized the affected segment. Early logs show records being re-evaluated, but I can’t confirm final counts yet. If the undercount remains after the rebuild completes, we’ll treat this as a dedupe-related bug and investigate the missed recalculation events further.

subject: Relatório por equipe não reflete mudança de gestor dos usuários
description: Reorganizamos as equipes e movi 23 usuários de um gestor para outro pelo painel de administração. A alteração já aparece nos perfis, mas o relatório por equipe continua atribuindo esses usuários ao gestor antigo. Isso está gerando cobrança errada entre áreas. Preciso saber se o relatório usa outro campo além do gestor atual.

comments.message: A mudança foi feita ontem às 16h. Nos perfis dos usuários já consta a nova gestora, mas no relatório exportado hoje cedo ainda veio tudo no gestor antigo.

Olá! Esse relatório usa um snapshot hierárquico diário para manter consistência histórica. Alterações de gestor feitas no módulo de administração depois do fechamento diário só entram no próximo ciclo de atualização, normalmente durante a madrugada.
Entendi. Mas eu preciso corrigir o arquivo de hoje, não o de amanhã.

Para o arquivo de hoje, a alternativa é gerar um relatório personalizado baseado em 'gestor atual do usuário' em vez de 'hierarquia no período'. Esse campo lê o cadastro mais recente. Se preferir, posso orientar como duplicar o relatório atual e trocar essa dimensão.

subject: Merged duplicate users and API calls now fail for one account ID
description: I merged two user accounts in our tenant because a colleague had duplicate logins, and after that our integration started failing when it tries to update records linked to the old user ID. Some API calls return 404 and others return a validation error saying the account reference is inactive. I need to understand how account merges affect API identifiers and whether there is a redirect or mapping we can query.

comments.message: After merging a duplicate user into the correct account, our integration can no longer PATCH resources that still reference the old user ID. Is there an endpoint to translate old IDs to the surviving account automatically?
Merges generally keep the old ID active as an alias for a period of time, so your integration should continue working. Please clear any local cache and retry the requests after 15 minutes.
We waited over an hour and the old ID still returns 404 on GET /users/{id}. The update payload also fails when that ID is included as owner_id on tickets.

Thanks for checking. I may have oversimplified that behavior. In most cases, the retired user ID is not queryable after a merge, and there isn't a public lookup endpoint for old-to-new IDs. The practical fix is to fetch the surviving user record from the UI or export, update your local mapping table, and stop sending the deprecated ID. I'm escalating this internally to confirm whether your plan includes the merge audit API, which would make that mapping easier to retrieve.

subject: Can I split one team into two without losing their history?
description: We're reorganizing our customer success department and need to split one team into two separate groups in the platform. I want to move six users to a new team, keep their past records intact, and understand what happens to dashboards and saved filters. The admin page lets me create the new team, but I'm nervous about changing memberships without knowing the downstream impact.

comments.message: We're reorganizing our customer success department and need to split one team into two separate groups in the platform. I want to move six users to a new team, keep their past records intact, and understand what happens to dashboards and saved filters. The admin page lets me create the new team, but I'm nervous about changing memberships without knowing the downstream impact.

You can create the new team and reassign the six users from Team Settings. Their historical records stay attached to the users and prior activity, so you won't lose history. The main impact is on anything filtered by current team membership: dashboards, queues, and saved views may show different results after the move. Before making the change, export or note any critical team-based reports so you can compare them after.
Will automations that route by team update automatically, or do I need to edit each rule?

Rules that target the old team won't automatically start using the new one. You'll need to review routing automations, assignment rules, and any SLA policies that reference the original team name. If you want to reduce risk, do the split after hours, move one test user first, and confirm dashboards and routing behave as expected before moving the rest.

subject: La importación desde Google Sheets falla con columnas personalizadas
description: Estoy usando la integración con Google Sheets para cargar oportunidades desde una hoja compartida. La conexión se hizo bien, pero cuando programo la importación automática, las columnas estándar entran sin problema y las personalizadas quedan vacías o se corren de lugar. Puede que haya configurado mal los nombres internos de los campos en el producto, porque cambiamos varias etiquetas hace poco.

comments.message: Las columnas "Etapa", "Origen" y "Ejecutivo" se importan bien, pero "Segmento comercial" y "Prioridad interna" llegan en blanco aunque en la hoja sí tienen datos.

Esto suele pasar cuando el nombre visible del campo cambió pero la importación sigue buscando la clave interna anterior. Vaya a Configuración > Campos de oportunidad, abra esos campos y copie el nombre API o identificador interno. Luego, en la integración de Google Sheets, remapee las columnas contra esos campos exactos.
Hice el remapeo y una de las columnas ya carga, pero "Prioridad interna" sigue entrando en blanco.

Si ese campo es de tipo selección o depende de valores permitidos, la integración solo importará opciones que coincidan exactamente con la configuración del producto. Revise en Configuración > Campos de oportunidad si los valores en la lista son idénticos a los de la hoja, incluyendo mayúsculas y espacios. Si quiere, también puede convertir temporalmente el campo a texto para validar que el problema es de opciones y no de conexión.

subject: SCIM-provisioned users are inactive but still billed
description: We use SCIM to provision users from Okta. I disabled 14 people in Okta last week, and in your app they now show as inactive, but our billing page still counts them as paid seats. Since this touches both provisioning/admin settings and billing, I need a clear answer on what state actually stops charges.

comments.message: All 14 users are marked inactive in the People page and they were deprovisioned by SCIM on the 8th. Yet Billing says 214 seats for this month. Does SCIM deactivation not release paid licenses?

SCIM deactivation removes access, but billing depends on your subscription model. For your enterprise contract, inactive SCIM users still count against the purchased seat bundle until the bundle size is amended. In other words, the admin state is correct, but the billing quantity doesn’t shrink automatically.
Then how do we reduce the bundle? I don’t see any self-service option.

Enterprise seat reductions can’t be processed in-app. You’ll need to contact your account manager at least 30 days before renewal. I agree the distinction is confusing, and I’ve passed that product feedback along, but there isn’t a mid-term adjustment available from support.

subject: Google login disabled after add-on cancellation and Stripe sync missing invoices
description: I cancelled what I thought was an unused add-on, and now I’m worried I broke multiple things. Several contractors who sign in with Google can no longer get past the access screen, and our Stripe integration stopped pulling new invoices into the dashboard. To make it worse, the latest bill still includes the add-on I cancelled plus a charge for five contractor seats that we had already removed. Can someone explain what happened and fix whatever access got turned off?

comments.message: I cancelled what I thought was an unused add-on, and now I’m worried I broke multiple things. Several contractors who sign in with Google can no longer get past the access screen, and our Stripe integration stopped pulling new invoices into the dashboard. To make it worse, the latest bill still includes the add-on I cancelled plus a charge for five contractor seats that we had already removed. Can someone explain what happened and fix whatever access got turned off?

It sounds like cancelling the add-on removed external collaborator access, which would explain why your contractors can’t sign in with Google anymore. The Stripe integration may also depend on that package. Billing changes only apply at renewal, so it is normal to still see the add-on on the current invoice.
That doesn’t sound right. The add-on was advanced audit logs, not collaborators, and Stripe was working before. Also the removed seats were deleted 3 weeks before renewal.

You’re correct, and I apologize. I checked the account more closely: the audit-log add-on cancellation did not cause the access issue. What actually happened is your workspace hit a seat true-up at renewal, and auto-deprovisioning removed five lowest-activity contractor accounts, including Google-authenticated users. At the same time, the Stripe sync paused because the service account tied to the integration was one of the deprovisioned users. I’ve restored those five users, reinstated the service account, and submitted a billing review for the disputed seat charge because the removals occurred before the renewal cutoff in your logs.

subject: Zmieniłem e-mail i straciłem dostęp do uwierzytelniania dwuskładnikowego
description: Po aktualizacji mojego adresu e-mail na koncie nie mogę się zalogować, bo kod 2FA jest powiązany ze starym profilem. Aplikacja uwierzytelniająca przestała generować poprawne kody dla tego konta. Nie mam też zapisanych kodów zapasowych.

comments.message: Czy da się zresetować 2FA i zostawić nowy adres jako główny login? Jestem jedyną osobą odpowiedzialną za eksport danych.
Tak, ale przed resetem muszę potwierdzić tożsamość. Proszę odpowiedzieć z nowego adresu firmowego i podać nazwę organizacji oraz ostatnie dwa projekty, do których miał Pan dostęp.
Wysłałem odpowiedź z nowego adresu. Organizacja to DataNorth, a projekty to Q4 Pipeline i Archiwum 2023.
Dziękuję, dane się zgadzają. Wyłączyłem 2FA dla tego użytkownika, zachowałem nowy adres jako login i wymusiłem reset hasła. Po zalogowaniu proszę ponownie skonfigurować aplikację uwierzytelniającą.

subject: CLI login succeeds but API commands fail with forbidden
description: Our team uses your CLI for automation. `cli login` completes in the browser without errors, and I can see my account in the app afterward, but any CLI command that actually calls the API fails with 403 forbidden. This started after our security team enabled group-based access in Okta. It feels like the login session and API authorization are out of sync.

comments.message: For example, `cli projects list` and `cli users list` both fail. The browser-based login flow says success, so the access token is definitely being issued.
If the login succeeds, the API should also work. A 403 usually means the CLI version is too old. Please upgrade to the latest release and try again.
We upgraded this morning. Same behavior. Also, only users in one Okta group are affected; our platform team members work fine.

Thanks, that narrows it down. The SSO login is authenticating those users, but their mapped app role is "Viewer," which doesn't include the API scopes required by the CLI commands you're running. That's why login succeeds while resource calls are forbidden. Have your Okta admin map the affected group to an admin-capable or API-enabled role, then sign out of the CLI and log back in so the new claims are picked up.

subject: API token can read users but won't deactivate them in bulk
description: I'm trying to deactivate about 140 contractor accounts through your API before month-end, but every PATCH request to set users as inactive is coming back 403. The same token can list users and pull profile data just fine. I also noticed a handful of those users are missing the external_id field in the export, which is making the bulk job messy. I need to clean up the user records and deactivate the right people today.

comments.message: We use a service account token with what I thought were admin permissions. GET /users works, but PATCH /users/{id} with active=false returns 403 forbidden. Is there a separate scope for updating users?

Yes — reading users and managing users are controlled separately. A token can have read:users without write:users. Please check the API client in Admin > Developers and confirm the service account role is User Admin or higher, and that the token has the user.write scope enabled. For the missing external_id values, those fields are nullable and won't appear for users that were created manually unless populated later.

I switched to a token from our workspace admin and now updates work for some users, but 23 users still fail with 409 conflict. Those are also the records with duplicate employee numbers in our CSV import history.

A 409 on user updates usually means the target records are tied up in a merge conflict or unique-field validation. Since you mentioned duplicate employee numbers, those users likely have conflicting values in a mapped custom field. I'd recommend exporting the affected user IDs, clearing or correcting the duplicate employee number field first, then retrying the deactivate call. If you're doing this at scale, use the bulk user update endpoint so validation errors are returned per record instead of stopping the whole run.

subject: Unable to re-enable deactivated users after SSO migration
description: I’m our workspace admin, and since we switched our team to Okta SSO yesterday, I can’t restore three previously deactivated users. They show up in User Management as inactive, but when I click reactivate I get a generic "Something went wrong" message and the page spins forever. I’ve already tried from Chrome and Safari, and one of these users needs access today. Can you help me figure out what’s broken?

comments.message: I’m our workspace admin, and since we switched our team to Okta SSO yesterday, I can’t restore three previously deactivated users. They show up as inactive, but reactivation fails with a generic error.

Thanks for reporting this. Since this started right after your SSO migration, please check whether those users already exist as provisioned identities from Okta under a different status. In Admin Settings > Users, search by email and external ID. If you see duplicate records, try removing the pending SCIM-created profile and then reactivate the original account.
I checked and two of them do have duplicate SCIM profiles in pending status. I removed one, but the original user still won’t reactivate. The third user has no duplicate at all.

That helps narrow it down. I reviewed the behavior against a known issue where legacy deactivated users can’t be reactivated after an SSO domain claim is enabled. I’ve escalated this to our identity engineering team with your tenant ID and the affected email addresses. As a workaround, for the urgent user, create a new user account with a temporary alias email and assign the same role/group access, then Okta can update the username after engineering clears the lock. I’ll keep this ticket open pending the escalation.

subject: Need to remove ex-employee admin access but invoice still includes them
description: I’m cleaning up our tenant after an admin left the company. I converted her role to inactive, reassigned all approval workflows to a new team lead, and expected our next invoice to reflect one fewer admin seat. Instead, the departed admin is still listed as billable, and two invoice approval automations are failing because they still reference her user ID in a hidden step. Can you help remove the billable seat and identify where those automations still point to the old account?

comments.message: The former admin is marked inactive in Users, but in Billing > Subscription she still appears under occupied seats. Our invoice approval workflow also errors with ‘assignee not found’ after I reassigned ownership.

Inactive users usually stop billing immediately, so if she still appears as billable it may just be a caching issue in the admin page. For the workflow failure, try cloning the automation and publishing it again.
I already waited overnight and cloned one of them. The errors continue, and finance needs the invoice corrected today.

I dug deeper and found two separate issues. First, your plan bills on provisioned admin seats, not just active status, so the ex-employee still occupied a paid admin seat until the seat assignment was removed from the subscription page. I’ve now released that seat. Second, both failing automations contain legacy custom JSON steps with her internal user ID embedded, so reassignment didn’t touch them. I’ve flagged the workflow IDs for our automation team because those hidden steps have to be edited from the backend.

subject: NetSuite-Integration importiert Rechnungen ohne Steuerbetrag
description: Wir haben NetSuite als Finanzintegration angebunden. Kundendaten und Rechnungsnummern werden synchronisiert, aber der Steuerbetrag bleibt bei importierten Rechnungen immer leer. Das ist für unsere Buchhaltung ein Problem, weil dadurch die Summen nicht stimmen. Ich brauche eine Erklärung, ob das Feld unterstützt wird oder speziell gemappt werden muss.

comments.message: Bei jeder aus NetSuite importierten Rechnung ist der Nettobetrag korrekt, der Steuerbetrag aber 0,00. In NetSuite selbst ist die Steuer sauber gepflegt.

Danke, ich habe das geprüft. In der NetSuite-Integration wird der Steuerbetrag nicht automatisch aus der Gesamtrechnung berechnet, sondern aus dem separaten Tax-Detail-Endpoint gelesen. Wenn in Ihrem Importprofil nur das Standard-Rechnungsobjekt aktiviert ist, bleibt das Steuerfeld leer.
Wo kann ich das Importprofil anpassen? Ich sehe nur die normalen Feldzuordnungen.

Die Einstellung ist etwas versteckt: Unter Integrationen > NetSuite > Datenquellen können Sie 'Tax Details importieren' aktivieren. Danach muss ein vollständiger Reimport der betroffenen Rechnungen gestartet werden. Falls der Schalter in Ihrem Tarif nicht sichtbar ist, sagen Sie bitte Bescheid — dann lassen wir ihn für Ihren Account prüfen.

subject: Need legal name updated after merger and duplicate billing fixed
description: Our company completed a merger this month, so the billing name and tax details on the account need to be updated from Alder Ridge LLC to Alder Ridge Health Group Inc. Around the same time, we seem to have been charged twice for the same subscription renewal. I need the account details changed and the duplicate charge investigated.

comments.message: We renewed on April 1, but our card was charged twice for the same annual subscription—two separate captures for $4,800. Also, our legal entity name has changed after a merger, so invoices should now be issued to Alder Ridge Health Group Inc. instead of Alder Ridge LLC. Can you update the billing profile and tell me which charge is valid?

I found the account and can update the billing company name and tax information once you send the new legal entity details. On the charges: I see one settled renewal and one pending authorization that may fall off automatically within 3-5 business days rather than a true duplicate capture.
I've attached the new legal name, billing address, and tax ID. Can you make sure future invoices use that information? And if the pending charge doesn't drop off, what happens?

I've updated the billing profile to Alder Ridge Health Group Inc., including the new address and tax ID, so future invoices should reflect that change. For the second charge, I've placed the payment under review; if the authorization is still present after 5 business days, reply here and we'll void or refund it manually depending on settlement status.

subject: Merging subsidiaries caused duplicate accounts in HubSpot sync
description: We recently merged two subsidiaries and imported a large CSV to standardize account records, then reconnected our HubSpot integration. Now we have duplicate company records, some users lost access to accounts they used to manage, and territory admins are only seeing half the imported data. I need to understand whether we can dedupe companies in bulk, preserve historical notes and owners, and fix the role-based access issue that appeared after the import.

comments.message: After a CSV import and reconnecting HubSpot, we now have duplicate company records and territory admins can't see some of the imported accounts. Can duplicates be merged without wiping notes and owner history?

Bulk company merges are possible in some cases, but it depends on whether the duplicates were created by import, by HubSpot sync, or both. Historical notes usually remain with the surviving record, though owner history may not fully merge in the UI. For visibility, imported records often default to the importing admin's territory unless territory fields are mapped during import.
The duplicates came from both sources. Our import used an operations admin account and we did not map territory because that field wasn't in the template. What should we do first?

First, pause the HubSpot sync so it doesn't recreate duplicates while cleanup is underway. Next, export the affected company IDs and identify the canonical record for each pair. Since territory wasn't mapped, reimport or bulk update the territory assignment so the right admins can see those accounts. I've also flagged this for our data services team because cross-source merges are safer when handled on the backend to preserve notes and linked activities.

subject: Depois de remover um administrador, a conta ficou com banner de erro permanente
description: Eu removi um administrador antigo da conta e atualizei o contato principal da organização. A remoção funcionou, mas desde então o painel principal mostra um banner vermelho dizendo que existe uma alteração pendente na conta, e ele nunca desaparece. Ao clicar, às vezes abre uma página vazia, às vezes volta para o dashboard. Parece ser um defeito ligado à alteração de conta.

comments.message: Removi um administrador antigo e atualizei o contato principal. Agora há um banner vermelho de alteração pendente na conta que nunca some e às vezes abre página vazia.
Olá! Esse banner normalmente indica que ainda há uma confirmação de segurança faltando. Verifique a caixa de entrada do novo contato principal e conclua qualquer e-mail de confirmação.
Já verificamos todos os e-mails e não existe pendência. O banner continua há dois dias e a página vinculada falha de forma intermitente.

Obrigado pela confirmação. Como a alteração de administrador foi concluída e o link do banner está falhando, isso sugere um bug no fluxo de pós-alteração da conta. Vou abrir uma investigação com os times responsáveis pelo painel e pelo gerenciamento de administradores. Até lá, o banner pode permanecer visível, mas não deve impedir o uso das demais funções.

subject: Need to rename a custom object without breaking existing imports
description: We created a custom object called "Installations" during implementation, but the business wants it renamed to "Assets" before rollout. My concern is that we already have import jobs, field mappings, and a few automations built against the current object. I need to know whether changing the label versus the API name will affect historical data loads and downstream references. We can tolerate cosmetic changes, but not broken syncs.

comments.message: Can I safely rename our custom object from Installations to Assets? We already imported around 60k records and have scheduled SFTP imports pointing to that object.
Yes, you can just rename it. The system should update everything automatically, including imports and automation references.
Do you mean the display label only, or the internal object key too? The UI is showing both options, and one of them warns that API references may be impacted.

You should avoid changing the internal object key if scheduled imports and automations already reference it. Renaming the display label to "Assets" is generally safe and won't alter stored data or break mappings, but changing the API/internal name can invalidate import configs, webhooks, and workflow conditions that point to "Installations." Please leave the internal key as-is unless you're prepared to update each dependency manually.

subject: New team lead can’t see the same metrics after promotion
description: I promoted one of our supervisors to Team Lead and updated her user role this morning. She now has access to manage users in her department, which is correct, but her monthly performance report looks different from before and several filters are missing. I need to understand whether the role change altered her reporting scope or whether I missed a secondary permission setting tied to user administration.

comments.message: I promoted one of our supervisors to Team Lead and updated her user role this morning. She now has access to manage users in her department, which is correct, but her monthly performance report looks different from before and several filters are missing. I need to understand whether the role change altered her reporting scope or whether I missed a secondary permission setting tied to user administration.

Role changes can affect both user-management privileges and what dimensions a user can report on. Team Lead roles typically inherit department-limited user admin rights and department-scoped reporting filters, so if she previously had broader visibility through a custom permission group, those filters may disappear. Please check her profile under User Administration to see whether a custom reporting access group was removed when you changed the base role.
You were right — the custom group got cleared when I changed the base role. If I add it back, will that interfere with her new ability to manage users only in her department?

No, those are controlled separately. You can keep the Team Lead base role for department-only user administration and re-attach the custom reporting access group for broader metrics visibility. I’d also suggest logging out and back in after the update so the report filters refresh. That should restore the missing metrics without expanding her user-management scope.

subject: Zapier dejó de enviar formularios y no veo por qué
description: Tenemos un Zap que crea leads en su plataforma cuando alguien completa un formulario de Typeform. Desde ayer los zaps aparecen como exitosos en Zapier, pero los leads no están entrando. No cambiamos nada en Typeform y en su lado no veo registros nuevos. ¿Me ayudan a diagnosticarlo?

comments.message: Probé con un envío de prueba y tampoco aparece. En el historial del zap sale 200 OK, pero en su API log no veo nada con esa hora.

Si Zapier marca 200 OK pero no hay rastro en nuestro API log, puede ser que esté enviando al endpoint equivocado o a otra cuenta mediante una clave vieja. Revise en Zapier qué subdominio y qué API key están configurados en el paso de acción. También confirme si recientemente rotaron credenciales.
Sí, rotamos la API key la semana pasada, pero en Zapier sigue puesta la anterior. La cambié y ahora los leads nuevos sí entran. El problema es que faltan todos los de ayer.

Bien, entonces encontramos la causa. Como Zapier consideró exitosas esas ejecuciones, no siempre las reintenta automáticamente si la respuesta vino de otra cuenta válida o de un entorno distinto. Le recomiendo usar la función Replay de Zapier para las tareas de ayer. Si me comparte el rango horario aproximado, también puedo verificar si esos envíos terminaron en un workspace equivocado para intentar localizarlos.

subject: New admin can log in but gets 404 when creating API token
description: I promoted one of our users to admin so she could create an API token for our warehouse sync. She can log into the product fine, but when she opens the token creation page it throws a 404 and then sometimes logs her out. My owner account can access the page normally. This looks account-specific.

comments.message: User jane@ourco.com was changed from Manager to Admin about 20 minutes ago. She can sign in and use the app, but Settings > API Access goes to a 404 page. After refreshing, she gets kicked back to login. Can you check whether the role change propagated correctly?
If she can sign in, then permissions are probably fine. A 404 usually means the browser cached an old page. Please have her use an incognito window and try again.
We already tried incognito and another browser. Same 404, same logout behavior. My account works from the same machine.

Thanks for confirming. I should have asked that first. I checked the account audit log and her admin role was applied, but the API Access feature flag did not attach to her user record after the role change. That's why login works while the token page fails. I've manually refreshed the entitlements on her account; please ask her to sign out and back in once more and then retry.
She just tested and can now create the token. Thanks.
Glad to hear it's working now. We'll also attach this to an internal bug about entitlement sync after role updates so engineering can prevent the 404/logout issue going forward.

subject: Les totaux du rapport d'activité ne correspondent pas
description: Je consulte le rapport d'activité mensuel pour mars et les totaux affichés ne correspondent pas aux chiffres exportés en CSV. Dans le tableau de bord j'ai 12 418 interactions, mais le CSV en montre 11 973. J'ai déjà essayé de changer le fuseau horaire et de refaire l'export, sans succès. J'ai besoin d'une explication rapidement pour notre revue de direction cet après-midi.

comments.message: Bonjour, le rapport d'activité de mars n'affiche pas les mêmes totaux que le fichier CSV exporté. C'est bloquant pour notre réunion aujourd'hui.

Bonjour, merci pour le signalement. Une différence entre le tableau de bord et le CSV peut venir des filtres appliqués ou du fait que le tableau inclut les conversations fusionnées alors que certains exports les détaillent séparément. Pouvez-vous me confirmer le nom du rapport, la plage de dates exacte et si vous avez activé le filtre "équipes EMEA" ?
C'est le rapport "Activité support", du 1er au 31 mars, filtre équipe EMEA activé. Nous avons aussi exclu les tickets spam. Je vois toujours 12 418 dans l'interface et 11 973 dans l'export.

Merci, je viens de vérifier le comportement connu de ce rapport. Dans "Activité support", l'interface utilise votre fuseau Europe/Paris, mais l'export CSV est généré en UTC si le rapport contient une ventilation journalière. Cela peut décaler les enregistrements du 31 mars avec le changement d'heure. Pour obtenir des chiffres alignés aujourd'hui, retirez la ventilation par jour puis relancez l'export. J'ouvre aussi un ticket interne car les deux vues devraient être cohérentes.

subject: Passwordless login disabled our warehouse sync user and imported records lost parent-child links
description: We rolled out passwordless login and removed local credentials for everyone except supposed service accounts. Since then, one warehouse sync user can no longer authenticate to the integration, and the imported account data is arriving without parent-child relationships. The sync is also creating duplicate child records instead of updating existing ones. We need a fix before tomorrow's reporting run.

comments.message: We thought we exempted all service users, but our warehouse connector started failing right after the passwordless change. It now creates duplicate child accounts, and parent account references are blank on the imported records that do come through.

That usually means the service user wasn't actually excluded from the new login policy, so the connector may be authenticating partially or using an expired token. Missing parent-child links and duplicate children suggest the sync can't read the parent external key or update by the child match key anymore. Can you verify whether the warehouse sync credentials belong to a human user or a dedicated service account?
Unfortunately it's still tied to a former engineer's account. That user was converted to passwordless yesterday and then deactivated later in the day.

That's the root problem. Create a dedicated non-human service account, exempt it from interactive passwordless login if required by your connector, and grant access to both the child match key and parent reference fields before generating new credentials. Reconnect the warehouse sync with that account. That should restore updates and parent linking going forward; for the broken records already imported, you'll likely need a repair import once the connector is healthy again.

subject: Mehrwertsteuer wird trotz VAT-ID berechnet
description: Wir sind ein Unternehmen in Österreich und haben unsere gültige UID bereits im Billing-Bereich hinterlegt. Trotzdem wurde auf der letzten Rechnung deutsche Mehrwertsteuer ausgewiesen. Das ist für uns steuerlich problematisch. Können Sie die Rechnung korrigieren und erklären, warum die Steuer überhaupt berechnet wurde?

comments.message: Wir sind ein Unternehmen in Österreich und haben unsere gültige UID bereits im Billing-Bereich hinterlegt. Trotzdem wurde auf der letzten Rechnung deutsche Mehrwertsteuer ausgewiesen. Das ist für uns steuerlich problematisch. Können Sie die Rechnung korrigieren und erklären, warum die Steuer überhaupt berechnet wurde?

Danke für die Details. Ich habe geprüft, dass Ihre UID erst nach Erstellung der letzten Rechnung im System als verifiziert markiert wurde. Deshalb wurde auf diese Rechnung noch Mehrwertsteuer berechnet. Ich habe eine Anfrage zur Ausstellung einer korrigierten Netto-Rechnung und zur Stornierung der ursprünglichen Rechnung an unser Billing-Team gestellt. Für zukünftige Rechnungen sollte die Steuer nun nicht mehr berechnet werden.

subject: Ao trocar o proprietário da conta, as permissões ficaram inconsistentes
description: Eu transferi a propriedade da conta para outra pessoa da minha equipe usando a opção de mudança de proprietário. A alteração aparentemente concluiu, mas agora eu continuo vendo algumas áreas de administrador e o novo proprietário não consegue acessar faturamento nem SSO.
