Em alguns momentos a tela mostra que a troca foi concluída, em outros parece que não. Isso começou logo após a alteração, então suspeito de erro na aplicação.

comments.message: Troquei o proprietário da conta e agora as permissões ficaram quebradas: eu ainda vejo partes de admin e o novo dono não vê faturamento nem SSO.
Entendi. Em geral, após a troca de proprietário, as permissões podem levar alguns minutos para sincronizar. Peça ao novo proprietário para sair e entrar novamente.
Isso já faz mais de 6 horas. Ele saiu, entrou, testou em outro navegador e continua sem acesso. Eu ainda tenho acesso parcial que não deveria ter.

Nesse caso, pode ser um bug na propagação de papéis após a mudança de proprietário da conta. Vou registrar a investigação com urgência porque há risco de permissões incorretas. Para avançar, preciso dos e-mails dos dois usuários e do horário exato da alteração. Enquanto isso, não recomendamos fazer novas mudanças de função manualmente.

subject: Zmiana nazwiska użytkownika i błąd przy zapisie profilu
description: Po zmianie nazwiska w firmie próbuję zaktualizować swoje dane na koncie i jednocześnie zmienić nazwę użytkownika widoczną w organizacji. Za każdym razem po zapisaniu dostaję komunikat „Nie udało się zapisać zmian”, a po odświeżeniu wraca stara wartość. Potrzebuję pomocy zarówno z aktualizacją danych konta, jak i ze zdiagnozowaniem błędu.

comments.message: Dzień dobry, próbuję zmienić nazwisko i nazwę użytkownika w profilu, ale po kliknięciu Zapisz pojawia się błąd „Nie udało się zapisać zmian”. Po odświeżeniu nic się nie zmienia.

Dzień dobry. Czy może Pan/Pani podać dokładną treść błędu, przeglądarkę oraz informację, czy problem dotyczy tylko pola nazwiska, czy też innych zmian w profilu? Proszę też sprawdzić, czy używają Państwo znaków specjalnych lub podwójnego nazwiska.
Firefox 126 i Chrome 124, w obu tak samo. Błąd jest ten sam przy zmianie nazwiska oraz nazwy wyświetlanej. Chodzi o nazwisko dwuczłonowe z myślnikiem.

Sprawdziłem i nasz formularz profilu ma obecnie ograniczenie walidacji dla nazwisk z myślnikiem w niektórych tenantach. Tymczasowo mogę ręcznie zaktualizować nazwę wyświetlaną na koncie, ale pełna synchronizacja z profilem logowania wymaga poprawki po naszej stronie. Proszę potwierdzić docelową pisownię, a zgłoszenie techniczne już utworzyłem.
Docelowa pisownia: Kowalska-Nowak. Jeśli możliwe, proszę też zaktualizować nazwę widoczną w naszych raportach.

Potwierdzam, ręcznie zaktualizowałem nazwę wyświetlaną na „Kowalska-Nowak”, więc powinna już być widoczna w aplikacji i nowych raportach. Problem z zapisem w profilu logowania pozostaje otwarty po stronie technicznej; gdy poprawka będzie wdrożona, poinformujemy Państwa e-mailem.

subject: I’m confused about API rate limits and what my developers need to know
description: I’m the operations lead, not an engineer, but our dev team asked me to check your API rate limits before they build an integration. I found the docs, but I don’t understand what they should actually do with that information. Can someone explain in practical terms what rate limits mean for planning the integration?

comments.message: They asked whether they should do real-time sync or batch jobs, and I don’t know how to answer.

Rate limits are just caps on how many API requests can be made in a given time window. In practical terms, your developers need to estimate how much data they’ll send or pull, how often, and what happens during busy periods. Real-time sync is useful when updates need to appear immediately, but it can create lots of small requests. Batch jobs are often more efficient for larger volumes because they group work together. The right choice depends on how current the data needs to be and whether the expected request volume stays comfortably under the limit.
Is there a simple rule of thumb I can share with them?
A simple rule is: use real-time only for time-sensitive events, and use scheduled batches for everything else.

subject: Custom domain SSL pending after DNS update
description: I configured our branded support portal to use support.northstar.io. DNS has been pointing to the value from your setup page for more than 24 hours, but SSL still shows as pending and the domain serves a certificate error. I want to confirm whether I missed any part of the custom domain configuration.

comments.message: We added the CNAME exactly as shown. DNS lookup now resolves correctly, but your admin page still says 'waiting for certificate provisioning.'

For custom domains, certificate issuance will stay pending if the domain was attached before DNS finished propagating or if a proxy service is sitting in front of the CNAME. Please confirm the record is DNS-only and then remove and re-add the custom domain in Admin > Branding so the system revalidates the configuration. Also make sure no AAAA record exists for the same host.
The record was proxied through Cloudflare. I changed it to DNS-only and re-added the domain. The status moved from pending to provisioning.

Perfect — that indicates the configuration is now correct and SSL issuance has started. It normally completes within an hour, though occasionally up to 24 hours. If it doesn't flip to active by tomorrow, reply here and we'll have infrastructure check the certificate job.

subject: Connexion Microsoft Teams en boucle sur l'écran d'autorisation
description: Quand j'essaie d'activer l'intégration Microsoft Teams pour envoyer des alertes dans un canal, je suis redirigé en boucle entre votre page et l'écran d'autorisation Microsoft. Je me connecte avec un compte admin global, j'accepte les permissions, puis je reviens sur votre application sans canal disponible. Même comportement sur deux navigateurs. Nous devons mettre cela en place avant demain pour notre équipe support.

comments.message: Bonjour, l'intégration Teams tourne en boucle pendant l'autorisation OAuth. À la fin, aucun canal n'apparaît dans votre interface.

Bonjour, merci pour le signalement. Ce comportement arrive souvent quand l'application Teams n'a pas reçu le consentement admin complet ou quand les cookies tiers bloquent le retour OAuth. Pouvez-vous vérifier que le consentement a bien été accordé au niveau du tenant Microsoft et refaire le test en navigation privée avec les cookies intersites autorisés ?
J'ai refait le test en privé et avec les cookies autorisés. Cette fois l'écran ne boucle plus, mais je vois "aucune équipe trouvée" alors que j'en ai plusieurs.

Parfait, on a donc éliminé le souci OAuth. Le message "aucune équipe trouvée" apparaît généralement quand l'utilisateur admin n'est pas membre des équipes ciblées, même s'il est admin du tenant. Ajoutez temporairement votre compte à l'équipe/canal concerné ou utilisez un compte qui en est déjà membre, puis cliquez sur "Actualiser les équipes". Si la liste reste vide, je peux escalader au support intégrations Microsoft avec l'ID de votre tenant.

subject: Webhooks HubSpot reçus mais non traités
description: J'essaie de connecter HubSpot à votre application pour créer automatiquement des tickets quand une propriété de contact change. Les webhooks partent bien de HubSpot, mais chez vous ils apparaissent en "reçus" puis restent en "non traités" sans message clair. J'ai déjà recréé l'abonnement webhook et vérifié l'URL de callback. Est-ce qu'il y a un format attendu particulier pour la signature ou le payload ?

comments.message: Bonjour, notre intégration HubSpot envoie bien les webhooks, mais ils restent bloqués chez vous au statut "non traités". Je ne comprends pas ce qui manque.
Bonjour, si les webhooks sont reçus, c'est probablement un souci de signature. Merci de vérifier que HubSpot envoie le header Authorization avec un Bearer token valide.
HubSpot n'envoie pas de header Authorization sur ce type d'abonnement, seulement sa signature de requête. C'est justement pour ça que je demande s'il y a une validation spécifique chez vous.

Dans ce cas, je vous conseille de désactiver la vérification de signature dans votre portail HubSpot puis de retester. Si cela ne fonctionne pas, il faudra peut-être réinstaller complètement l'intégration.

subject: Paused account reactivated, but workflow quotas didn’t reset correctly
description: We temporarily paused our subscription for budgeting reasons and reactivated it this week after paying the outstanding balance. Now our workflow automation dashboard says we have already consumed 97% of this month’s run quota even though the billing cycle just restarted on the new invoice. We need someone to verify whether usage from before the pause was carried into the new billing period and whether that’s why some automations are throttling.

comments.message: After reactivation, billing shows a new monthly period starting June 15, but workflow automation says we’ve used 11,600 of 12,000 runs already. That number looks like old usage from before the pause.

Thanks for the detail. I can confirm your account was reactivated after payment of the outstanding invoice, and I agree the automation quota should align to the active billing period. I’m seeing a mismatch between the billing cycle start date and the usage service reset timestamp.
Can you reset it manually? Our throttled workflows are delaying customer onboarding.

I can’t reset usage counters directly, but I’ve escalated this to the platform team with the billing dates attached. In the meantime, I’ve requested a temporary overage grace flag so your workflows can continue running until the quota is corrected.

subject: Auto-tagging rule fails when product family is added later
description: I'm trying to tag enterprise opportunities automatically when Product Family equals Platform and Contract Value is over 50,000. The workflow works if both fields are filled in at creation time, but it doesn't add the tag when Product Family is populated later by sales ops. I need help fixing the automation so it reacts to later field changes without tagging unrelated records.

comments.message: Current setup is trigger on opportunity created. Condition Product Family = Platform and Contract Value > 50000. Action add tag enterprise-platform. Existing opportunities that get updated later never receive the tag.

This is because the workflow only evaluates once at creation. In Workflow Automation, switch the trigger to "opportunity created or updated" and limit it with field-change criteria if available, such as Product Family changed or Contract Value changed. In Product Configuration, confirm the Product Family field is the actual source field being updated by sales ops, not a synced display-only field.
It is the source field. If I use created or updated, won't the tag action run repeatedly on every edit?

It can, depending on whether tags are idempotent in your account. To avoid noisy re-evaluation, add a condition that tag does not already contain `enterprise-platform` before the add-tag action. If your builder supports trigger filters, set it to run only when Product Family or Contract Value changes.

subject: Nie działa zmiana planu użytkownika z Viewer na Editor
description: Próbuję zmienić rolę jednego z pracowników z Viewer na Editor, bo potrzebuje edytować dashboardy. Po zapisaniu zmiana przez chwilę jest widoczna, a po odświeżeniu wraca do Viewer. Dodatkowo zakładka Uprawnienia czasami w ogóle się nie otwiera i pokazuje pustą stronę. Chciałbym ustalić, czy to problem z licencją, synchronizacją czy błędem interfejsu.

comments.message: Cześć, zmieniam pracownikowi rolę z Viewer na Editor, ale po odświeżeniu system cofa zmianę. Czasem zakładka Uprawnienia pokazuje tylko białą stronę.
Najprawdopodobniej nie mają Państwo wolnej licencji Editor. Proszę dokupić jedno miejsce i wtedy zmiana powinna przejść.
Mamy 3 wolne licencje Editor, to sprawdziłem przed zgłoszeniem. Poza tym biała strona nie wygląda na problem licencyjny.
W takim razie proszę wylogować się i zalogować ponownie. Jeśli to nie pomoże, proszę poczekać, bo czasami uprawnienia odświeżają się z opóźnieniem.

subject: No me queda claro cómo funciona el mapeo de campos con HubSpot
description: Estoy conectando HubSpot y veo opciones de mapeo estándar, campos personalizados y reglas de sobrescritura, pero no entiendo cuándo un dato se actualiza y cuándo no. Me preocupa romper información existente. ¿Pueden explicármelo de forma simple?

comments.message: Tenemos un campo personalizado de 'segmento' en HubSpot y otro parecido aquí. Si activo la sincronización, no sé cuál gana cuando los valores son distintos.
No te preocupes, eso no debería causar problemas. En general, la plataforma simplemente mezcla los datos y toma el valor más reciente automáticamente.
No veo ninguna opción que diga eso. Solo veo 'preferir valor de HubSpot', 'preferir valor local' y 'no sobrescribir si existe'.

Entonces deja la opción por defecto y debería funcionar bien. Si hay conflictos, normalmente el sistema los resuelve solo. De todos modos, puedes probar en producción con algunos contactos para ver qué pasa.

subject: GraphQL API returns partial data with field errors
description: I am querying the GraphQL API for tickets and requester details. The HTTP status is 200, but parts of the response are null and the `errors` array mentions permissions on one nested field. I need help troubleshooting whether the query shape is wrong or if our token scopes are missing something.

comments.message: Our query asks for `tickets { id subject requester { id email phone } }`. The response returns ticket data, but `requester.phone` is null and the errors array says access denied for that field.
A 200 means the query succeeded, so you can ignore the errors array. Null fields are normal in GraphQL when there is no value.
The error explicitly says `Missing scope: users:read_sensitive`. We do get email, just not phone.

Thanks for the exact message. In GraphQL, field-level authorization can return partial data with a 200 response plus errors. Your query is valid; the token just lacks the `users:read_sensitive` scope required for `requester.phone`. If you don't need phone, remove that field. If you do, generate a token with the additional scope and make sure your app is approved for sensitive user data access.

subject: Need invoice corrected after changing SMS routing numbers
description: I reconfigured our messaging setup so inbound SMS is split across two departments, and after that our invoice started showing charges for an extra phone number bundle. I thought I was only moving the routing, not adding billable resources. I need help figuring out which configuration step triggered the new charge and whether we can undo it.

comments.message: I changed our SMS configuration to assign different numbers to Support and Sales queues. Since then, billing added a second number bundle and setup fee. I didn't explicitly purchase anything — I only followed the routing wizard. Did that wizard provision a new paid number?

Yes. In the messaging wizard, selecting separate inbound routing by department automatically provisions an additional number unless you choose the shared-number option. I can see two active US numbers on your account, and billing added the second number rental plus one-time setup fee accordingly.
We don't actually need dedicated numbers; we only wanted different queues based on message content. Can we revert to one number and remove the new charges?

I've released the unused secondary number and changed the configuration to keyword-based routing on your original line. Because the second number was active for less than 24 hours and had no traffic, I've submitted a billing request to waive both the rental and setup fee. You should see the adjustment on the next invoice or as a credit memo.

subject: HubSpot connection asks to reconnect, and finance says we were billed twice
description: I’m the admin for our workspace and I can’t tell whether these issues are related, but they started the same day. Our HubSpot integration keeps showing “reauthorization required,” and at the same time I noticed we were billed twice this month for an extra block of users. One more problem: one of the added users can’t accept their invite because the login page says their email is already associated with another account. We need the sync running again and I need to know what we actually paid for.

comments.message: I’m the admin for our workspace and I can’t tell whether these issues are related, but they started the same day. Our HubSpot integration keeps showing “reauthorization required,” and at the same time I noticed we were billed twice this month for an extra block of users. One more problem: one of the added users can’t accept their invite because the login page says their email is already associated with another account. We need the sync running again and I need to know what we actually paid for.

Thanks for flagging this. Usually the integration prompt just means the HubSpot token expired, so please disconnect and reconnect it under Settings > Integrations. For the duplicate billing, that is often just an authorization hold and should disappear on its own in a few business days. If the invited user sees that message, they should try the forgot password link.

We already reconnected HubSpot twice and it still disconnects after about 10 minutes. The two charges both fully posted, they are not pending. Also the invited user used forgot password and got sent into an old trial workspace we can’t access.

In that case, I recommend waiting 24 hours for the integration cache to refresh. I’m not able to merge user records or review billing from this queue, so you may want to ask your finance team to contact their card provider if the charges remain on the statement.

subject: Changement de propriétaire du compte bloqué par une ancienne adresse
description: Je dois transférer la propriété du compte à notre nouvelle DSI, mais l’ancien propriétaire a quitté l’entreprise et son adresse n’existe plus. L’interface continue pourtant à demander une validation de cette adresse. Nous avons un audit d’accès cette semaine, donc j’ai besoin d’une procédure sûre pour débloquer la situation.

comments.message: Bonjour, impossible de changer le propriétaire du compte car le système envoie un code à une boîte mail désactivée. Nous n’avons plus aucun accès à cette adresse.
Bonjour, dans ce cas il suffit normalement de recréer temporairement l’adresse e-mail de l’ancien collaborateur pour recevoir le code, puis de refaire le transfert.
Ce n’est pas acceptable pour des raisons de sécurité et de conformité. Existe-t-il une procédure interne de vérification d’identité ?

Vous avez raison, je retire cette suggestion. Je vais escalader votre dossier à notre équipe Trust & Safety, qui gère les changements de propriété quand l’e-mail historique n’est plus accessible. Ils pourront vous demander des justificatifs d’autorité et traiter le transfert de manière sécurisée.

subject: Limite de taxa da API interrompendo carga diária
description: Nossa rotina noturna faz leitura de atividades, anexos e usuários pela API para consolidar tudo no lake. Nas últimas duas noites começamos a receber muitos 429 e a carga termina incompleta, deixando tabelas com dados faltando. Já reduzimos a concorrência, mas não está claro quais endpoints compartilham o mesmo rate limit e como retomar o processamento sem duplicar ou perder registros.

comments.message: O cabeçalho Retry-After aparece em alguns endpoints, mas em outros não. Também notei que atividades sofre mais do que usuários.

Olá! Os limites podem variar por família de endpoint e por plano contratado. Para evitar perda de dados, implemente backoff exponencial ao receber 429 e checkpoints por cursor ou updated_at para retomar de onde parou. Anexos e atividades costumam ter limites mais sensíveis por volume.
Existe alguma tabela oficial com esses limites por endpoint? Precisamos planejar a janela de processamento.

No momento não tenho uma tabela completa para compartilhar em um único documento, e alguns limites são ajustados dinamicamente. Vou encaminhar seu caso para nosso time técnico verificar os limites efetivos da sua conta e sugerir uma janela segura de throughput. Enquanto isso, mantenha concorrência baixa e trate qualquer 429 como sinal para pausar o worker daquela família de endpoint.

subject: API requests suddenly returning 401 for production script
description: Our nightly script has been calling your API for months, but starting last night every request began returning 401 Unauthorized. We didn't intentionally rotate the key, and the same script still works in our staging environment. I need to troubleshoot whether the token was revoked, expired, or if something changed on your side.

comments.message: Our nightly script has been calling your API for months, but starting last night every request began returning 401 Unauthorized. We didn't intentionally rotate the key, and the same script still works in our staging environment. I need to troubleshoot whether the token was revoked, expired, or if something changed on your side.

Thanks for the report. A 401 in production but not staging usually means the production token is invalid, scoped differently, or being sent with the wrong host. Please confirm whether the script is pointing to api.ourapp.com versus the staging endpoint, and check in Settings > API whether the production key shows last used or revoked. Also verify there isn't an extra space or line break if the key is loaded from an environment variable.
The host is correct. In Settings, the key shows revoked at 01:12 UTC, which none of us expected. We do load it from a secrets manager but haven't changed the script.

If the dashboard shows the key as revoked, our side would reject it exactly as you're seeing. The fastest fix is to generate a new production key, update the secrets manager, and rerun one request manually to confirm. After that, review your admin audit log to see who or what revoked the old token at 01:12 UTC. If no admin action appears there, reply back and we'll investigate further.

subject: Demande de restauration d’un tableau supprimé
description: Un manager a supprimé par erreur un tableau de bord partagé ce matin, et nous n’avons pas trouvé d’option de restauration en libre-service. Ce tableau est utilisé pour notre comité de direction à 14h. Si une récupération est possible uniquement en back-office, merci de l’engager au plus vite.

comments.message: Bonjour, le tableau “Pipeline Q3 - Direction” a été supprimé vers 09h10. Nous avons besoin de le récupérer aujourd’hui avant 14h.

Bonjour, je suis désolé. La restauration des tableaux supprimés n’est pas disponible depuis l’interface client et doit être vérifiée par notre équipe opérations. Je viens d’escalader votre demande en mentionnant le nom exact du tableau, l’heure de suppression et votre échéance de 14h.
Merci. Si la restauration complète n’est pas possible, un export des widgets ou des données sources pourrait déjà nous aider.

Bien noté, j’ai ajouté cette option à l’escalade pour que l’équipe cherche soit une restauration, soit une extraction partielle exploitable avant votre réunion. Je surveille le dossier et je vous tiendrai informé dès qu’un retour arrive.

subject: Campo personalizado não aparece no payload do endpoint /tickets
description: Adicionei um campo personalizado chamado "segmento_cliente" e marquei como visível no formulário. No entanto, quando faço GET no endpoint /tickets, esse campo não vem no JSON. Preciso dele para sincronizar com o nosso CRM. Já revi a configuração e não percebo se falta expor o campo na API ou se existe algum parâmetro extra.

comments.message: Adicionei um campo personalizado chamado "segmento_cliente" e marquei como visível no formulário. No entanto, quando faço GET no endpoint /tickets, esse campo não vem no JSON. Preciso dele para sincronizar com o nosso CRM. Já revi a configuração e não percebo se falta expor o campo na API ou se existe algum parâmetro extra.
Campos visíveis no formulário aparecem automaticamente na API, por isso isso não deveria acontecer. Tente limpar a cache e fazer a chamada novamente dentro de alguns minutos.
Já testei ontem e hoje, inclusive com outro token. Os campos padrão aparecem, mas o personalizado não.
Entendi. Nesse caso, provavelmente há um atraso de replicação. Aguarde até 24 horas para o campo surgir no endpoint.

subject: Can’t remove former contractor from report recipients
description: I’m cleaning up access after a contractor left. I deactivated the user successfully, but their email is still listed as a recipient on three automated reports, and the system won’t let me remove it because it says the schedule is locked by the report owner. The owner’s role was also changed last month and she no longer has admin rights. How do I update report recipients without reactivating everyone?

comments.message: I’m cleaning up access after a contractor left. I deactivated the user successfully, but their email is still listed as a recipient on three automated reports, and the system won’t let me remove it because it says the schedule is locked by the report owner. The owner’s role was also changed last month and she no longer has admin rights. How do I update report recipients without reactivating everyone?

Scheduled report recipients can usually be edited only by the current schedule owner or an admin with reporting management rights. Since the original owner lost admin rights, the cleanest path is to have a current admin transfer schedule ownership to themselves, then remove the contractor from the recipient list. You should not need to reactivate the contractor account.
I’m a current admin, but I don’t see a transfer option on the schedule. Only pause, duplicate, and delete.

Understood. In some plans the ownership transfer control isn’t exposed in the schedule drawer and has to be done from the report detail page or by our support team. I can’t complete that from chat, so I’m escalating this to our reporting specialists with the report IDs if you send them over. In the meantime, duplicating the schedule under your account and deleting the old one is a workaround if the recipients need to be updated today.

subject: Payment succeeded but SAML users blocked and Marketo sync paused
description: Our renewal payment shows as successful on the card statement, but inside the app it still says the account is restricted. Since that banner appeared, our SAML users can’t get in and the Marketo integration has paused all sync activity. I also noticed the invoice total is higher than our contracted amount because it includes seats for users we deactivated before renewal. Can you check both the billing discrepancy and whether the restriction is causing the login and integration failures?

comments.message: Our renewal payment shows as successful on the card statement, but inside the app it still says the account is restricted. Since that banner appeared, our SAML users can’t get in and the Marketo integration has paused all sync activity. I also noticed the invoice total is higher than our contracted amount because it includes seats for users we deactivated before renewal. Can you check both the billing discrepancy and whether the restriction is causing the login and integration failures?

I found the payment, but it hasn’t been matched to the invoice yet because the charge settled under a different card descriptor than usual. The restriction flag is therefore still active, and yes, that can block SAML logins and pause Marketo sync jobs. I’ve asked billing ops to manually apply the payment and opened an invoice review for the disputed seat count. I can’t remove the restriction myself from this queue, but I’ve marked the case as urgent because it is affecting both authentication and integrations.
Do you have any ETA? Our campaign launches this afternoon and nobody from marketing can log in.

I don’t want to promise a timeline I can’t guarantee. Billing ops is usually same day for payment matching, but urgent cases can still take several hours. As a temporary workaround, if you have any non-SAML local admin still active, they may be able to export the Marketo-bound data manually; otherwise we’ll need to wait for the restriction to be cleared by the billing team.

subject: Timezone correction changed activity dates on only some records
description: We ran a bulk timezone normalization on imported activities after noticing several were stored in UTC instead of local time. The job updated many records correctly, but a portion of activities now show the right timestamp in the detail view and the wrong date in timeline summaries. I need help figuring out whether two date fields got out of sync and whether the normalization script exposed a product bug.

comments.message: We ran a bulk timezone normalization on imported activities after noticing several were stored in UTC instead of local time. The job updated many records correctly, but a portion of activities now show the right timestamp in the detail view and the wrong date in timeline summaries. I need help figuring out whether two date fields got out of sync and whether the normalization script exposed a product bug.
This might be expected if browser timezone settings differ from the stored activity timezone. Please clear cache and compare in an incognito window.

We already tested with multiple users in different browsers. The detail timestamp is consistent, but the timeline summary groups the same activity under the previous day. That looks like stored summary data, not browser rendering.

You're right, that points away from a client-side display issue. Based on your description, the raw activity timestamp may have been updated while the denormalized timeline_date field stayed unchanged for part of the batch. Please send 3 affected activity IDs and the approximate time you ran the normalization job. I’m going to escalate this as a potential bug in the bulk update path for activity metadata.

subject: Need to rotate integration owner after admin left company
description: Our former system administrator left unexpectedly, and their account was suspended in Google Workspace before we realized they were the owner of our Google Sheets integration and several automated data imports. We restored access temporarily, but I want to transfer everything to a service account and remove the former admin completely. I'm worried about losing import history, failing jobs, and orphaned assets tied to that user. What should be moved first, and can we bulk identify everything owned by that account?

comments.message: A departed admin still owns our Google Sheets integration and multiple scheduled imports. We need to move those to a service account before permanently disabling the user. Is there a bulk way to find everything tied to them?

Yes. Start by searching the admin audit and scheduled jobs pages for assets owned by that user, including imports, exports, dashboards, and connected integrations. Reauthorize the Google Sheets integration with the new service account before disabling the old one, otherwise token-based jobs may fail immediately.
Can we transfer import history too, or only future ownership? We need our audit trail preserved.

Future job ownership can be reassigned, and the historical run logs will still show the original actor for completed jobs, which preserves the audit trail. If you share the user's ID, support can help identify any hidden dependencies such as API tokens or webhook subscriptions before you fully remove the account.

subject: Need help reducing storage charges from duplicate file records
description: Our storage overage doubled after a data migration from Box. We suspect many files were imported twice because the folder mapping changed mid-project. I need help identifying which records are billable, how to remove duplicates safely, and whether storage overage can be recalculated after cleanup.

comments.message: We now show 1.8 TB in the platform, but about 600 GB looks like duplicated attachments tied to the same client records. Before I delete anything, I need to know whether versions, archived files, and orphaned uploads all count toward billing.

Billable storage includes current files, previous versions, and orphaned uploads retained in the file library. Archived files count too unless they are permanently deleted after retention. To clean this up safely, export the file inventory report, group by checksum or source file ID, and review duplicates where the same binary is attached to multiple records unintentionally. I can share a step-by-step for bulk deletion using the retention manager so you don't break the primary record links.
If we delete the duplicates this week, can the overage fee on the current invoice be adjusted? The migration consultant said cleanup happened before month end, but finance was still billed.

I checked the usage snapshots and your billable peak was captured on the 30th before the duplicate cleanup job completed on the 31st. That means the current invoice is accurate under the plan terms. However, your next invoice should reflect the reduced storage. I've also attached guidance on running the duplicate file audit earlier in the month and setting an import rule to skip files with existing source IDs so this doesn't recur.

subject: Former admin still appears in approval workflow
description: I downgraded one of our admins to a regular user because she moved departments, but she still shows up as an approver in user provisioning requests. I also need to assign that approval responsibility to her replacement, who should be a user admin for only one business unit. I’m not sure if I missed a step during the role change.

comments.message: I changed Monica from Admin to Member yesterday. Today she still appears in the access approval dropdown. Her replacement is Evan, and he should only manage users for the West business unit.
That usually updates automatically within a few minutes. Please wait 24 hours and it should correct itself.
It’s already been almost 30 hours, and new requests are still routing to Monica. I need Evan assigned correctly before tomorrow morning.

I’ve reviewed the account and the role change removed Monica’s admin status, but her approval-rule assignment was left in place. I’ve now removed Monica from the provisioning workflow and assigned Evan as User Admin for the West business unit only. New requests should route to Evan, though any requests already pending may still need to be reassigned manually.

subject: Deleted rows from import file but old values are still on records
description: I thought that if I removed values from a spreadsheet and re-imported it, those fields would be cleared in the system. Instead, the old values are still on the records. I was trying to wipe out an outdated territory assignment for a set of accounts. Can you explain why that didn’t work and what the correct way is to clear data in bulk?

comments.message: I left the Territory column blank on purpose for 90 accounts and ran an update import. Nothing changed.
Blank cells in an import are usually ignored during updates, so they don’t overwrite existing values. That’s why the old territory values remained in place.
So how do I actually clear the field?
Use a bulk edit tool or import a special null value like 'CLEAR' into the Territory field.
I tried typing CLEAR on one record and now the literal word 'CLEAR' is saved in the field.

Understood. I gave you a generic method that doesn’t apply to all field types in our product. In this system, clearing values in bulk depends on the object and field configuration, and for some fields it has to be done through the bulk editor or API, not a standard CSV import. I’m going to escalate this to our technical support team so they can confirm the supported clearing method for your Territory field and advise on the safest cleanup.

subject: SCIM provisioning keeps recreating suspended users
description: We suspended several seasonal workers in your app because they should not have access until next quarter, but our SCIM sync keeps recreating them as active users with default member permissions. I need to understand whether we should suspend them in our identity provider instead, and I need a reliable way to prevent them from being re-added automatically. This has happened three times this month already.

comments.message: We suspended a batch of seasonal users in the app, but SCIM keeps recreating them as active members after sync. I need this stopped because we don’t want these users regaining access.

What you’re seeing usually happens when the source of truth is your identity provider. If those users remain assigned to the application in your IdP, SCIM can reprovision them even after they’re suspended locally. For user administration, the safest approach is to unassign or deactivate them in the IdP, then let SCIM push the change downstream. Since this has repeated multiple times, I’m escalating your case to our provisioning specialists so they can confirm whether your SCIM mappings are also applying the default member role on recreation.
That makes sense, but we also need to make sure they don’t come back with any previous elevated roles. One of them used to be a team admin.

Understood. I added that detail to the escalation because role persistence on reprovisioning needs a closer log review. As an immediate precaution, please remove those users from any admin-mapped groups in your IdP before unassigning them from the app. Our provisioning team will review whether recreated accounts inherit prior roles or are receiving a mapped default role, and they’ll advise on the cleanest long-term setup.

subject: Need help restricting dashboard access by team
description: I’m the new admin for our workspace and I’m trying to clean up who can see certain reports. Right now our sales managers can open an executive dashboard that they shouldn’t have access to, and I’m not sure if I’m supposed to change the report permissions or the user roles. I also need a simple explanation I can send to the team leads so they understand how dashboard visibility works.

comments.message: We have 3 teams in one account: Sales, Support, and Leadership. I want Leadership dashboards visible only to leadership, but our Sales Manager role can currently see them. Can you explain the right setup and tell me what to change?

Absolutely. Dashboard visibility is controlled by a combination of report folder permissions and user role access. The cleanest setup is to place the executive dashboards in a dedicated folder, then assign that folder only to a Leadership user group. After that, review the Sales Manager role to make sure it doesn’t have broad reporting access like 'View all dashboards.'
I found the folder settings, but I’m confused about user groups vs roles. If I remove reporting access from the role, won’t that also block the sales dashboards they actually need?

Correct — roles define what users are allowed to do in reporting overall, while groups determine which content they can see when folders are permissioned. So keep the Sales Manager role able to view dashboards, but limit the executive folder to the Leadership group only. You can share this with team leads: roles control capability, groups and folder permissions control visibility.

subject: Painel mostra métricas zeradas após criar novo status personalizado
description: Eu adicionei um status personalizado chamado "Aguardando validação jurídica" no fluxo de tickets. Desde então, o painel de SLA e tempo médio está aparecendo zerado para essa fila. Preciso saber se fiz algo errado na configuração ou se é bug.

comments.message: Os tickets entram e saem desse status normalmente, mas no relatório parece que eles somem das métricas.
Olá! Status personalizados nem sempre entram nos widgets padrão. Você criou esse status como aberto, pendente ou resolvido no mapeamento interno?
Marquei como status personalizado do tipo pendente.

Obrigado. Então o comportamento pode estar ligado ao mapeamento do relatório. Nos painéis padrão, alguns widgets consideram apenas os status nativos, a menos que o status personalizado esteja explicitamente incluído no filtro. Vá em Relatórios > editar widget e confira se "Aguardando validação jurídica" aparece na lista de status filtrados. Se não aparecer, isso pode indicar limitação do widget atual. Vou registrar a possibilidade e recomendo abrir uma solicitação com nosso time de produto se precisar desse status nas métricas padrão.

subject: Can’t log in after account suspension notice was removed
description: Our billing issue was resolved this morning and the suspension banner is gone, but I still can’t log in as workspace owner. Other teammates say the workspace is active again, yet my login says my account is disabled. I need owner access to finish month-end reporting.

comments.message: The workspace was suspended overnight for a failed payment, but finance updated the card and support confirmed the account was restored. Now only my owner profile seems blocked.
If the suspension banner is gone, access should be fully restored. Please try clearing cookies or using an incognito window.
I already did that on two browsers. My teammates can get in, but my specific account still says disabled.

Thanks for confirming. That suggests your owner user record may still be in a suspended state even though the workspace was reactivated. I’m escalating this to the account services team to manually review and re-enable the owner profile, since I can’t change account status from chat support.

subject: Import stalls at 83% with no error
description: I'm trying to import a CSV of about 12,000 contacts into our account. The import runs for a few minutes and then stops at 83% every time. There's no error banner, and the job just sits there until I refresh the page. I tested with two different files and got the same result. I also tried from another browser. We need this uploaded today, so I'd like to know if this is a known issue or if there's something specific I should change in the file.

comments.message: My CSV import keeps hanging at 83% with no error message. I've tried Chrome and Firefox and two different files. What should I check next?
This usually means the CSV has invalid characters. Please open the file in a text editor and remove special symbols, then retry the import.
I already exported a clean UTF-8 CSV directly from our CRM, and a smaller subset of the same file imported fine. The larger file still freezes at the same percentage.
In that case it may just be a temporary browser issue. Please clear cookies and try again in incognito mode.

subject: Email replies are creating new tickets instead of threading
description: Our support inbox started creating a brand-new ticket every time a customer replies, even when they're replying to an existing conversation. This only started happening today and it's affecting multiple agents. I need help troubleshooting whether something changed in our email channel settings or message headers.

comments.message: Our support inbox started creating a brand-new ticket every time a customer replies, even when they're replying to an existing conversation. This only started happening today and it's affecting multiple agents. I need help troubleshooting whether something changed in our email channel settings or message headers.

I can help check this. Threading usually depends on the reply retaining our reference headers or ticket ID in the reply address. Did you recently change your mail forwarding rule, secure email gateway, or sending domain? If you can share one original ticket ID and the duplicate ticket ID it turned into, that would help narrow down whether headers are being stripped.
Yes, our IT team enabled a new email security tool this morning. Original ticket was #45102 and the reply became #45189.

I reviewed the pattern and the most likely cause is that the security tool is rewriting or removing the threading headers before the reply reaches us. Please ask IT to allowlist our inbound address and preserve In-Reply-To and References headers. As a short-term workaround, customers can reply directly to the unique ticket address if it's shown in the thread. If you'd like, I can escalate with our mail team once you send a sample raw email header.

subject: SCIM-created users aren't getting into the right groups via API sync
description: We provision users from our HR system and then use your API to assign them to groups based on department. New accounts are created, but roughly 30% never get added to their expected groups, and some duplicate department values are showing up in the user profile data. I need to understand whether this is a data issue on our side or a permissions problem with the provisioning account.

comments.message: Our SCIM connection creates the user, then a follow-up API job reads the department attribute and assigns groups. The job is succeeding overall, but some users remain unassigned even though department is present.

If the users are being created, then the provisioning account should be fine. Group assignment is probably delayed because SCIM sync takes a few minutes to finish. Please wait 24 hours and see if the groups appear automatically.

We already waited overnight. These users still have no groups, and the API response for group assignment says 422 invalid target for some of them. Also, the profile shows both 'Sales' and 'sales ' in different records.

Thanks, that helps. The 422 suggests your API call is resolving a group identifier that doesn't exist for those records, and the inconsistent department values indicate the source data needs normalization. Because 'Sales' and 'sales ' are treated as different strings in your mapping logic, some users won't match a valid group key. Please trim whitespace and standardize casing in the department attribute before the assignment step, and verify the service account has permission to manage group memberships, not just provision users. If you'd like, I can escalate this with the failed payload examples.

subject: CSV export drops columns after applying saved view
description: When I open a saved view in our tickets dashboard and export to CSV, a few custom fields disappear from the file even though they are visible in the table. If I rebuild the same filters from scratch instead of loading the saved view, the export includes everything. Can you investigate whether the saved view metadata is corrupt, and also tell me how exports decide which columns to include?

comments.message: This is affecting our weekly QA audit. The missing columns are 'Escalation Reason' and 'Renewal Risk'. They show in the browser grid but not in the CSV only when the saved view is used.

Thanks for the detail. I tested with your saved view and was able to reproduce the issue: the export service is reading an older column definition attached to that saved view, not the current table configuration. So yes, this looks like a bug tied to stale saved-view metadata.
Is there anything we can do today without recreating all of our views? We have a report due this afternoon.

For today, the fastest workaround is to duplicate the saved view and re-add the missing columns before exporting; the duplicate should write fresh metadata. For context, exports are supposed to use the active view's selected columns, but in this bugged case the exporter is pulling a cached version. I've attached the issue to an existing engineering ticket and noted your deadline.

subject: Marketo field mapping changed and form submissions no longer sync
description: Our Marketo integration stopped syncing form submissions after we renamed a custom field last week. The connector still shows as healthy, but new submissions are not appearing in your platform. I need help troubleshooting whether the field mapping broke and how to restore the sync without losing data.

comments.message: The renamed field was 'Product_Interest__c' on our side. Existing records still show old values in your platform, but new submissions after the rename are missing completely.
If the connector is healthy, the sync should resume automatically. Field renames usually don't matter.
But the submissions stopped exactly after the rename, and the integration history shows validation errors if I drill in.

I checked the pattern and I need to correct my earlier note: if our mapping references the old API field name, Marketo can continue authenticating while individual submission payloads fail validation. Please remap the form field to the new API name in the connector settings and then run a backfill for the affected date range. If you want, I can hand this to our onboarding team to verify the backfill settings because I can't execute that from support chat.

subject: Automation rule not firing for one priority level
description: I set up an automation to assign urgent tickets to our on-call queue and send a Slack alert. It works for low, normal, and high priority in my tests, but not for urgent. I cannot tell whether I misconfigured the rule conditions or if urgent is handled differently by the system.

comments.message: The rule condition is basically 'when ticket is created and priority is urgent, assign queue + notify Slack.' If I create a high-priority ticket and then change it to urgent, nothing happens either.

Urgent should behave the same way, so it sounds like a rule configuration issue. First check whether the trigger is set to run only on ticket creation; if so, changing priority later would not fire it. Also confirm that 'urgent' is enabled as an allowed value in your workspace priorities, because disabled values can remain visible in old tickets but won't satisfy new automations.
Urgent was disabled in the workspace priorities because we hid it months ago, but old tickets still had it. I re-enabled it. New urgent tickets now route correctly, but Slack alerts still don't send.

Then you likely have two separate issues in the same workflow: ticket assignment from the trigger and Slack delivery from the action connection. Check whether the Slack app authorization on that rule has expired. I can confirm the product configuration around priorities was the reason the rule itself did not fire for urgent.

subject: Former owner deleted, subscription cancelled unexpectedly
description: We removed our previous IT director from the workspace after he resigned, and somehow our account now shows the subscription as set to cancel at the end of the term. We did not intend to cancel. I also need ownership reassigned to me and want to make sure our next invoice will still process normally.

comments.message: After deleting our former IT director's user account, the billing screen now says 'subscription will cancel on renewal.' He used to be the only owner on the account. I need ownership transferred to me immediately and confirmation that our May invoice will still be charged so service doesn't lapse.

When the last owner is removed, some account permissions can become restricted. I can't directly assign ownership from support without verification, but I can open an admin recovery request. About billing, if the subscription is marked to cancel, the next invoice usually won't generate unless the cancellation is reversed before renewal.
Please open whatever recovery request is needed. Our renewal is in six days, and if there is no invoice generated, that's a problem. Can you also reverse the cancellation now?

I've filed the admin recovery request as urgent and escalated it to our account access team, who will contact you after reviewing your company-domain ownership. I’m not able to reverse the cancellation flag until an authorized owner is restored, so this remains pending. If the review is completed before renewal, they should be able to reactivate billing continuity.

subject: No puedo activar la integración con SSO de Okta para provisionar usuarios
description: Estamos intentando usar Okta para aprovisionar usuarios automáticamente y además limitar el acceso según el equipo asignado, pero la integración no termina de funcionar. El inicio de sesión sí redirige a Okta, pero los usuarios nuevos no se crean y tampoco se les aplica el rol correcto cuando entran por primera vez. Sospecho que configuré mal los atributos o alguna opción de roles en el producto.

comments.message: Seguimos la guía para Okta, pegamos la URL SCIM y el token, pero ningún usuario nuevo aparece en la plataforma. Los existentes sí pueden iniciar sesión por SSO.

Entiendo. Si el SSO funciona pero SCIM no, seguramente el problema está en el aprovisionamiento y no en la autenticación. Revise en Okta que el aprovisionamiento esté habilitado tanto para "Create Users" como para "Update User Attributes". En nuestra plataforma también debe ir a Configuración > Usuarios y permisos y verificar que el aprovisionamiento automático esté activado.
Eso está activado. Mi duda es con el rol: queremos que los del grupo Ventas entren como "Colaborador" y los de Operaciones como "Administrador de solo lectura".

La asignación de roles por SCIM no se controla desde Okta directamente en todos los planes. En su caso, necesita crear reglas de asignación en Configuración > Usuarios y permisos > Reglas de acceso, usando el atributo de departamento o grupo entrante. Como no veo su plan desde este chat, voy a escalar el caso para confirmar si esa función está disponible en su suscripción y revisar por qué no se están creando los usuarios nuevos.

subject: Charged after account ownership transition request
description: I asked our IT team to deactivate my predecessor's account and move admin ownership to me, but before that was completed, your system auto-renewed our add-on package and charged the card on file. We had intended to remove that add-on before renewal. Can someone both update the account owner and review the renewal charge?

comments.message: I'm the new finance manager at North Peak Labs. We need the account ownership transferred from Darren Cole to me because he left last month. We also meant to cancel the Analytics Pro add-on before renewal, but the renewal went through yesterday for $1,200. Since I still don't have full billing access, I couldn't make the change myself. Can you help reverse the charge?

I can help with both parts. I've sent a secure ownership transfer form to your email so we can replace Darren as the billing owner after domain verification. Regarding the Analytics Pro renewal, I can see the add-on renewed automatically at 9:14 AM UTC yesterday. Our policy allows cancellation within 24 hours of renewal for annual add-ons if there has been no usage since renewal.
Great, we have not used the add-on since renewal. Please cancel it and confirm whether the $1,200 will be refunded to the same card.

Thanks for confirming. I’ve submitted the cancellation and refund request within the eligible window, and billing has approved it. The add-on is now set to terminate immediately, and the $1,200 will be refunded to the original card within 5-10 business days. Once you complete the ownership transfer form, your billing permissions will be updated as well.

subject: Zendesk ticket creation from form workflow missing attachments
description: We use a workflow that creates a Zendesk ticket whenever a high-priority support form is submitted. The ticket gets created, but file attachments from the original form are not coming over. The automation includes a file-upload field, and we specifically enabled attachment sync in the Zendesk integration settings.

comments.message: This started after we replaced the old intake form with a new one that has multiple file fields. The workflow still maps the summary and requester correctly, just not the files.

Attachment syncing in Zendesk only works if the workflow passes a single file token or an array in the expected attachment field. If your new form introduced multiple separate upload fields, the automation may not be combining them into a valid list for the integration step.
That sounds possible. Right now we have screenshot_files and log_files as separate fields in the trigger.

In that case, add a data merge step before the Zendesk action to combine screenshot_files and log_files into one array, then map that merged output to the Zendesk attachments parameter. Also make sure each file is still accessible at run time and not restricted by expiring upload URLs. If you test and some files still drop, send one sample run and we can confirm whether Zendesk rejected specific file types.

subject: Marketo leads assigned to wrong source in attribution report
description: After enabling the Marketo integration, new leads are landing in the system, but the attribution report is grouping a big chunk of them under 'Direct' instead of the mapped campaign source. We checked the Marketo activity log and the original source values are there. This is affecting our monthly reporting, so if support can't identify the mapping issue quickly please escalate it.

comments.message: After enabling the Marketo integration, new leads are landing in the system, but the attribution report is grouping a big chunk of them under 'Direct' instead of the mapped campaign source. We checked the Marketo activity log and the original source values are there. This is affecting our monthly reporting, so if support can't identify the mapping issue quickly please escalate it.
I think the issue is that attribution reports always fall back to Direct when there are multiple campaigns on a lead, so this is expected and cannot be changed.

That's not consistent with what our implementation consultant told us. It only happens for records created via the Marketo sync, and only when the source value contains a slash like 'Partner/Webinar'.

Thanks, that detail is useful. I wasn't able to reproduce a general attribution fallback rule, and your example suggests the integration transform may be normalizing that source field before reporting. I've escalated this to our reporting specialists and included the slash-delimited source values you shared. As a temporary workaround, create a custom report grouped by the raw imported Marketo source field instead of the standardized attribution dimension.

subject: Approval workflow skips new department managers
description: I set up an approval workflow so purchase requests should route to the requester's department manager, but for employees we added this week the workflow is skipping the manager step and auto-approving. I already confirmed those users are active and in the right department. I need help figuring out whether this is a configuration issue or something wrong with how manager assignments are being picked up.

comments.message: We imported 23 new users on Monday and only those new users are bypassing the manager approval rule. Older employees route correctly. Our workflow condition uses the 'Department Manager' dynamic approver.

Thanks for the details. This usually happens when the users exist but don't have a manager value populated in User Administration, even if the department field is correct. Please go to Admin > Users, open one of the affected users, and check whether the Manager field is assigned directly. The workflow can use department-based routing, but the dynamic approver still needs a resolvable manager relationship.

You were right. The department was populated from the CSV import, but the manager column was left blank. After updating the manager field for one test user, the request routed correctly. Is there a way to bulk fix the rest?

Yes — you can use the bulk user update import with email and manager email columns, then re-run provisioning for those records. Once the manager relationships are saved, the workflow will evaluate correctly for new requests. If you want, I can also share the CSV template for bulk manager assignment.

subject: New branch staff cannot activate accounts because required workspace selection is hidden
description: We added a second workspace and changed the login experience to route users by business unit. Since then, newly invited branch employees cannot activate their accounts. They click the email link, set their password, and then hit an error because no workspace is selected, but they never see a workspace picker. Existing staff with remembered sessions are unaffected.

comments.message: Newly invited users from our branch office cannot complete account activation. After setting a password they get an error saying a workspace must be selected, but there is no workspace selector on screen.

Did you recently enable automatic workspace routing or hide the workspace picker on the login page? If the routing rule depends on email domain but both headquarters and branch users share the same domain, new users may not be assigned a target workspace during first login.
Yes, we hid the picker and set routing by email domain, but both groups use the same company domain. The branch is supposed to go to the new workspace based on department.

That setup would prevent the system from determining the correct workspace during activation. Re-enable the workspace picker temporarily, or change the routing rule to use an attribute available at login, such as an SSO department claim if you're using federated auth. For invited password users, you may need to assign the correct default workspace in the invitation configuration before resending the invite.

subject: Former employee can still access shared inbox
description: One of our former employees was able to log into your platform this morning and view messages in a shared inbox even though we offboarded him last week. I removed him from our IdP, but his session seems to still work in your app on his old laptop. This is a serious access issue and needs to be escalated immediately.

comments.message: We terminated this user on Friday and removed him from Azure AD, but he opened the app today without being prompted to sign in again. I need to know how that’s possible and how to force access off now.

If you removed the user from Azure AD, they should no longer be able to log in, so this may just be a cached browser issue on that machine. Ask the employee to clear cookies and it should stop working.
He is no longer employed here, so we cannot ask him to do anything. We need you to revoke the session from your side and confirm whether active sessions survive SSO deprovisioning.

I understand. I don’t have access to revoke sessions myself, but I’ve forwarded your ticket to our security team for review. They can confirm whether the user still has an active session token and advise on forced logout options.

subject: Need a simple explanation of SSO setup for our employees
description: I’m trying to roll out SSO for our company, but I’m not very technical and your setup guide assumes I already know terms like IdP, SP, ACS URL, and claims mapping. I need someone to explain in plain English what information I need from our IT team and what steps I actually take in your app. Right now I’m stuck before I even start.

comments.message: Our IT team asked me what values they need from the app, and I don’t know what to send them.

No problem — here’s the plain-English version. Your IT team manages your identity provider (IdP), which is the system employees use to log in, like Okta or Azure AD. Our app is the service provider (SP), meaning we trust your IdP to confirm who the user is. From our side, you’ll typically give IT the ACS URL and Entity ID, which they paste into their SSO system. From their side, they’ll send you the sign-in URL, a certificate, and sometimes user attribute mappings like email and full name. In our app, go to Security > Single Sign-On, choose SAML, paste in the values from IT, and run the test mode before enforcing it for everyone.
That makes more sense. What should I ask them about user attributes so people don’t end up with duplicate accounts?

Ask them to send email as the unique identifier, since that’s what most customers use to match existing users in our platform. If they use a different unique field, confirm that with us before turning SSO on. Also ask them to send first name and last name if you want profiles filled in automatically. Before enforcing SSO, test with 2-3 existing employees to make sure the email coming from the IdP exactly matches the email already on their accounts.

subject: Locked out after SSO change and approval workflow stopped running
description: I changed our Okta SAML settings yesterday and now three admins, including me, can't log in to the workspace. Our requester approval workflow also stopped triggering for new tickets, so requests are just sitting unassigned. We need access restored and I need to know whether the automation broke because of the login change or something else.

comments.message: I changed our Okta SAML settings yesterday and now three admins, including me, can't log in to the workspace. Our requester approval workflow also stopped triggering for new tickets, so requests are just sitting unassigned. We need access restored and I need to know whether the automation broke because of the login change or something else.

I can help with both. I checked your account from the backend and can see failed SAML assertions starting at 14:12 UTC with the NameID now being sent as an email alias instead of the primary email we have on file. I’ve temporarily enabled password login for your owner account and sent a reset link so you can get back in. Separately, your approval workflow is configured to run only when the updater is an authenticated admin. Since the SSO failures prevented the approvals from being submitted by logged-in admins, the workflow never met its trigger conditions. Please log in with the reset link, confirm access, and then either revert the Okta NameID mapping or map it to primary email.

The reset link worked and I'm back in. I reverted the NameID mapping and the other admins can log in again. New approval requests are triggering now. Can you confirm if we should keep password login enabled?

Glad you're back in. Since SSO is working again, I recommend disabling fallback password login for admins unless you want it as an emergency option. I’ve escalated a short review to our identity team to verify there aren’t any lingering SAML mapping issues, but from the application side this is resolved.

subject: Guest user was converted to member but lost historical comments
description: I converted one of our guest users to a full member yesterday because she joined the company, but now her older comments in project threads show as 'Unknown User' in a few places. Her current account is active and she can log in fine. I’m worried the conversion broke the link to her old profile.

comments.message: I converted one of our guest users to a full member yesterday because she joined the company, but now her older comments in project threads show as 'Unknown User' in a few places. Her current account is active and she can log in fine. I’m worried the conversion broke the link to her old profile.

That can happen if the guest profile and member profile were not merged into the same user record during conversion. Did the email address change at the same time, or was a new member account invited separately?
Yes, both happened. She was a guest on her personal email, then HR wanted her full account on her company email, so we changed the email and upgraded her the same day.

Thanks, that sequence is helpful. It sounds like the platform may have created a second identity reference when the email changed during role conversion. I can’t repair historical authorship from chat, so I’m escalating this to our data team with the user ID and affected thread URLs. Please send 2-3 example thread links, and avoid further profile changes on that user until the review is complete.

subject: Massenänderung von Gruppenmitgliedschaften schlägt teilweise fehl
description: Ich wollte gestern etwa 120 Benutzer in eine neue Support-Gruppe verschieben. Über die Massenaktion wurden aber nur ungefähr 70 übernommen, bei den restlichen kam keine Fehlermeldung. In der Benutzerverwaltung sieht alles uneinheitlich aus und ich weiß nicht, ob ich das noch einmal ausführen darf, ohne doppelte oder falsche Zuordnungen zu erzeugen.

comments.message: Gibt es irgendwo ein Protokoll für fehlgeschlagene Änderungen an Gruppenmitgliedschaften?

Ja, in vielen Konten finden Sie unter Admin Center > Benutzeraktivität ein Audit-Protokoll für Änderungen an Rollen und Gruppen. Dort sollten fehlgeschlagene oder teilweise ausgeführte Bulk-Aktionen sichtbar sein. Wenn Sie die Aktion erneut ausführen, werden bestehende Mitgliedschaften normalerweise nicht dupliziert.
Ich sehe das Protokoll, aber nur für einzelne Benutzer, nicht als zusammenhängenden Bulk-Job. Einige Konten wurden wegen „Lizenzgrenze erreicht“ übersprungen.

Das erklärt das teilweise Verhalten. Die Gruppenzuweisung selbst ist möglich, aber wenn Ihre Zielgruppe lizenzpflichtige Funktionen oder Rollen auslöst, können Benutzer ohne verfügbare Plätze übersprungen werden. Sie können die Aktion für die verbleibenden Nutzer nach dem Freigeben zusätzlicher Lizenzen erneut ausführen; bestehende Zuordnungen bleiben dabei unverändert.

subject: Não consigo excluir registros em massa por causa de relacionamento
description: Eu preciso excluir em massa cerca de 1.800 leads de teste que foram importados no ambiente de produção por engano. Quando seleciono a exclusão, a plataforma bloqueia dizendo que existem relacionamentos com empresas e oportunidades. Eu não quero apagar as empresas reais, só os leads de teste e os links entre eles. Existe uma forma segura de fazer isso?

comments.message: Eu preciso excluir em massa cerca de 1.800 leads de teste que foram importados no ambiente de produção por engano. Quando seleciono a exclusão, a plataforma bloqueia dizendo que existem relacionamentos com empresas e oportunidades. Eu não quero apagar as empresas reais, só os leads de teste e os links entre eles.

Sim. Nesse cenário, primeiro faça uma visualização filtrando os leads de teste por origem da importação ou tag. Em seguida, use a ação em massa 'Remover associações' para desanexar empresas e oportunidades sem excluir os registros relacionados. Depois disso, a exclusão em massa dos leads deve ser liberada. Antes de executar, exporte a lista com IDs para ter um backup caso precise restaurar as associações manualmente.
Eu encontrei a opção de remover associações, mas ela só aparece item por item, não em massa.

Obrigado por confirmar. Para contas sem o módulo avançado de relacionamentos, essa remoção em massa realmente não fica disponível. A forma mais segura então é usar uma automação temporária ou a API para limpar os vínculos dos leads marcados como teste e, só depois, excluí-los. Como isso envolve muitos registros em produção, vou abrir uma escalada para nossa equipe de dados enviar o script recomendado e validar os critérios de exclusão antes de vocês rodarem.

subject: Não consigo alterar o domínio após migrar a conta
description: Eu sou administrador da conta da nossa empresa e tentei mudar o domínio de login de equipe ontem à noite, de antigaempresa.com para novaempresa.com. A opção salvou, mas hoje metade dos usuários ainda aparece com o domínio antigo e vários não conseguem entrar. Além disso, quando tento editar novamente as configurações da conta, a página de identidade fica carregando para sempre. Preciso entender se a alteração foi aplicada parcialmente ou se existe algum erro no sistema.

comments.message: Fiz a troca do domínio da conta para novaempresa.com, mas os usuários continuam misturados entre os dois domínios e a tela de configuração não termina de carregar.

Olá! Verifiquei seu relato e a mudança de domínio deveria ser imediata para todos os usuários. Tente limpar o cache do navegador e atualizar a página de identidade. Se ainda aparecer o domínio antigo, provavelmente é só atraso local.
Já fiz isso em dois navegadores e em modo anônimo. O problema continua, e no console aparece um erro 500 quando abro a página de identidade.

Obrigado pelo detalhe. O erro 500 na página de identidade indica que pode haver um problema do nosso lado após a alteração do domínio. Vou escalar para a equipe de engenharia com o ID da sua conta e o horário aproximado da mudança para investigarem o bug. Enquanto isso, evite fazer novas alterações de conta para não agravar a inconsistência.

subject: Mentions in internal notes not notifying users intermittently
description: We are seeing intermittent failures where @mentions in internal notes do not send notifications. The same user might receive one mention and miss the next one ten minutes later. Email delivery to those users works for other events, so it seems specific to mention notifications. Could you investigate whether this is a known issue?

comments.message: I have three examples from today where the notes were saved correctly, the mentioned user had access to the record, but no email or in-app notification was generated.

Thanks. I checked and there isn't a current incident posted for mentions. This could be related to notification preference evaluation or a bug in mention event generation. Can you send one affected record ID and the email of a user who missed a notification?
Record 88314, user is lpatel@brightside.io. She received a mention at 9:10 but not one at 9:23 on the same record.

I reviewed the 9:23 note event and can see the note save, but I can't confirm from support whether a mention event was emitted downstream. Since this is intermittent and not tied to access or general email delivery, I’m escalating it to our notifications team for log-level investigation. I’ll attach the record ID, timestamps, and affected user so they can compare a working mention versus the failed one.

subject: Agents can log in, but the assignment workflow is skipping our EU queue
description: Our agents can all sign in, but only after a second attempt since this morning. More importantly, the workflow that should route German-language tickets to the EU queue is skipping that branch and sending everything to General Support. I need help figuring out whether the access issue and automation issue are connected, and if not please escalate the workflow part because it's hurting response times.

comments.message: Our agents can all sign in, but only after a second attempt since this morning. More importantly, the workflow that should route German-language tickets to the EU queue is skipping that branch and sending everything to General Support. I need help figuring out whether the access issue and automation issue are connected, and if not please escalate the workflow part because it's hurting response times.

I reviewed both symptoms. The login retries appear to be caused by a temporary delay in your custom identity provider response; we’re seeing successful authentication after one timeout, but no permission errors once the session is created. That issue is likely separate from the routing automation. In your workflow history, the EU branch condition is checking 'ticket language equals de-DE' but your current language detector is populating 'de' only, so the condition never matches and the fallback queue gets used. I’ve updated the condition to accept both values and escalated the login latency to our platform team because it may require infrastructure review.
That explains the queue issue. Do we need to reprocess the tickets that already landed in General Support?

The workflow fix will apply only to new tickets. You’ll need to bulk move the existing misrouted tickets, or I can provide a filter query for them. The login latency escalation is open, and I’ll follow up through this ticket once the platform team responds.

subject: Delegated admin lost ability to suspend users in one business unit
description: I set up a delegated admin role that should allow our regional managers to suspend and restore users only within their own business unit. It works in APAC, but our EMEA manager suddenly gets an access denied error when trying to suspend anyone, even though she can still edit names and groups. I reviewed her role assignment and the business unit mapping, and nothing obvious changed. We need to disable two accounts today because of offboarding.

comments.message: Our EMEA delegated admin gets access denied when suspending users, even though other user-management actions still work.
Please confirm the delegated role includes both "Suspend users" and "View users" permissions and that the EMEA manager belongs to the correct business unit admin group.
Yes, both permissions are enabled, and she is in the right admin group. APAC works with the same role template.

Thanks. Since the role template is shared but only one business unit is affected, let’s narrow it down to scope evaluation. Have there been any recent changes to EMEA’s user attribute mapping or nested groups? If the target users are inherited through a nested group, the suspend action can fail even when edit actions still pass.
Now that you mention it, EMEA was moved under a parent region group last week, and the affected users are inherited from that nested group.

That explains the difference. Suspend/restore permissions currently don’t apply reliably through nested business-unit scopes in some tenants. For the urgent offboarding, use a full admin to suspend the two accounts now. I’m escalating the delegated-scope behavior to our authorization team and including your EMEA group structure so they can confirm whether this is the known limitation or a regression.

subject: Cannot restrict automation to only suspended users
description: I'm trying to configure an offboarding workflow that disables app access, reassigns open tasks, and notifies the person's manager when someone is marked suspended. The issue is that the automation is also firing for active users when we edit unrelated profile fields. I suspect I misconfigured either the user status mapping or the workflow trigger.

comments.message: Our HRIS sync updates user records several times a day. I created a workflow trigger that says 'when user record is updated' and condition 'status equals suspended,' but it still ran for two active employees after title changes. Those users briefly lost access.
If the status equals suspended, the workflow should only run for suspended users. I recommend removing and recreating the workflow because sometimes triggers cache old values.
I rebuilt it from scratch and it happened again. In the execution log, I can see the workflow started on profile update and then disabled the account before status was evaluated.
In that case, just move the disable-account action lower in the workflow so the system has time to catch up. That should prevent it from affecting active users.

subject: Po zmianie loginu użytkownik nie może się zalogować
description: Zmieniliśmy pracownikowi firmowy adres e-mail i próbowałem zaktualizować login w systemie. Sama zmiana wygląda na zapisaną, ale od tego momentu użytkownik nie może się zalogować ani przez SSO, ani lokalnie. W panelu administratora profil tej osoby raz pokazuje nowy adres, a raz stary. Potrzebuję pomocy z naprawą konta i znalezieniem przyczyny niespójności.

comments.message: Po zmianie loginu użytkownika z old@firma.pl na new@firma.pl pracownik stracił dostęp do konta. SSO odrzuca go, a lokalne logowanie też nie działa.

Rozumiem. Proszę potwierdzić, czy zmiana adresu była wykonana po stronie naszego panelu, czy najpierw w dostawcy tożsamości. Proszę też przesłać komunikat błędu z SSO oraz informację, czy użytkownik ma ten sam identyfikator pracownika w katalogu.
