Najpierw zmieniliśmy adres w Entra ID, potem w Waszym panelu. W SSO jest komunikat „NameID mismatch”, a identyfikator pracownika się nie zmienił.

To wygląda na rozjazd między NameID z dostawcy tożsamości a lokalnym rekordem użytkownika po zmianie loginu. Zresetowałem mapowanie logowania dla tego konta i wymusiłem ponowną synchronizację katalogu. Proszę poprosić użytkownika o ponowną próbę za 10 minut; jeśli lokalne logowanie nadal będzie nieaktywne, możemy je też ręcznie odblokować.
Po 10 minutach SSO działa, ale lokalne logowanie nadal pokazuje, że konto nie istnieje.

To akurat spodziewane po zmianie na konto zarządzane przez SSO — lokalne logowanie mogło zostać wyłączone dla nowego identyfikatora. Najważniejsze, że dostęp przez SSO wrócił. Jeśli chcą Państwo zachować też lokalne logowanie awaryjne, przekażę prośbę o ponowne włączenie tej opcji.

subject: Rows excluded from deduplication still showing in exception report
description: I ran a deduplication rule on our lead table and marked a batch of records as excluded because they were legitimate subsidiaries, not true duplicates. In the data table those records now have the exclusion flag set correctly, but the duplicate exception report is still listing them as unresolved matches. Sales ops is using that report to work the queue, so I need to know why the data state and the report don't line up.

comments.message: The exclusion flag is present on the lead records, but the duplicate exception report still lists them. Shouldn't the report remove excluded pairs automatically?
In most cases yes, but it depends on whether the report filter references the current exclusion flag or the last dedupe run output. Can you send one example record ID?
Example lead ID is L-44821. We excluded it yesterday afternoon.

I checked that record and the report is based on the static 'Potential Duplicate Status' field from the last rule execution, not the live exclusion flag. To fully clear those rows, you'll need to rerun the dedupe job or change the report filter to Excluded = false. I know that's not ideal, but that's why the mismatch exists.

subject: Nouvelle adresse de connexion administrateur et tableau de bord gelé
description: Nous avons changé de domaine e-mail cette semaine et je dois mettre à jour mon adresse de connexion administrateur. Depuis ce changement, le tableau de bord des rapports se charge sans fin lorsque j’ouvre les indicateurs de productivité sur 90 jours. J’ai essayé avec plusieurs comptes internes. J’ai besoin de savoir si le problème vient du changement de compte ou d’un incident sur les rapports.

comments.message: Bonjour, mon adresse admin est passée de @ancien-domaine.fr à @nouveau-domaine.fr. J’ai pu modifier mon profil, mais depuis, la page des rapports "productivité 90 jours" reste bloquée sur le chargement. Est-ce lié ?
Bonjour, le changement d’adresse e-mail n’a normalement pas d’impact sur les rapports. Pouvez-vous me confirmer si le problème touche uniquement votre utilisateur ou tous les admins ?
Tous les admins que j’ai testés ont le même souci sur ce rapport précis. Les autres rapports s’ouvrent, mais plus lentement que d’habitude.

Merci, cela ressemble davantage à un problème côté reporting qu’à votre changement d’adresse. Votre modification de compte est bien enregistrée, mais je vois aussi une latence anormale sur les requêtes de tableaux de bord dans votre région. J’escalade l’incident à notre équipe infrastructure/reporting avec votre identifiant de workspace. En attendant, vous pouvez utiliser l’export programmé reçu par e-mail si vous l’aviez déjà activé.

subject: Je n'arrive pas à limiter l'édition des macros à certains superviseurs
description: Nous voulons que seuls les superviseurs puissent créer et modifier des macros partagées, tandis que les agents doivent seulement les utiliser. J'ai modifié les rôles, mais plusieurs agents arrivent encore à éditer des macros globales. Je cherche à corriger cela et à préparer une courte formation d'administration.

comments.message: Bonjour, j'ai besoin d'un paramétrage propre des permissions sur les macros. Aujourd'hui, même avec un rôle agent standard, certains utilisateurs peuvent changer une macro visible par toute l'équipe. Quelles options faut-il vérifier exactement ?
Bonjour, les macros partagées sont toujours modifiables par tous les utilisateurs qui y ont accès. Ce n'est pas possible de restreindre cela autrement.
Cela me surprend, car j'avais cru voir un réglage sur les permissions avancées. Et j'ai besoin d'une réponse fiable pour notre documentation interne.

Vous avez raison de vérifier. Je me suis mal exprimé : dans les offres avec permissions avancées, vous pouvez distinguer “utiliser” et “gérer” les ressources partagées. Regardez dans Paramètres > Rôles > Contenu partagé et désactivez “gérer les macros partagées” pour les agents. Vérifiez aussi si certains agents ont un rôle personnalisé ou une appartenance à un groupe d'administrateurs de contenu. Pour votre documentation, je vous conseille d'ajouter un contrôle mensuel des rôles personnalisés, car c'est souvent là que l'écart se crée.

subject: Fusion de deux profils après changement d’adresse e-mail
description: J’ai changé d’adresse professionnelle le mois dernier et, en me reconnectant, j’ai involontairement créé un second profil au lieu de mettre à jour l’ancien. Maintenant mes accès sont répartis entre deux comptes : l’un a mes tableaux de bord enregistrés, l’autre a mes autorisations admin. J’aimerais regrouper tout cela sous ma nouvelle adresse.

comments.message: Bonjour, j’ai deux profils sur votre plateforme : ancien e-mail marion.lefebvre@hexacom.fr et nouveau e-mail m.lefebvre@hexacom.fr. Est-il possible de fusionner les comptes pour garder mes paramètres et mes droits ?

Bonjour, nous ne proposons pas de fusion complète de profils, mais nous pouvons généralement transférer le rôle d’accès principal et renommer l’adresse de connexion si l’ancien compte est encore récupérable. Pouvez-vous me préciser lequel des deux profils doit être conservé comme compte final ?
Je voudrais conserver le nouveau e-mail comme identifiant final, mais garder les tableaux de bord et préférences de l’ancien compte si possible.

Merci. J’ai transféré vos droits d’administratrice au profil m.lefebvre@hexacom.fr et demandé à notre équipe technique de copier les tableaux de bord personnels depuis l’ancien profil. En revanche, les préférences de notification ne peuvent pas être fusionnées automatiquement. Vous devrez peut-être les reconfigurer manuellement. Je vous confirme l’avancement dès que l’équipe technique termine la copie.

subject: Need to purge duplicate contacts after bad import and update owner IDs by API
description: I imported customer contacts from an old CRM and accidentally created duplicates for a few hundred users. I'm now trying to use the API to merge or delete the duplicates and reassign ownership to the correct account managers, but some calls are failing and the owners list in the export doesn't match what I see in the UI. I need a safe way to clean the data without breaking permissions for the reps.

comments.message: I exported users, identified duplicates by email domain + name, and started deleting the extras through the API. Some deletions work, but others return 400 because the user is referenced by records. I also need to update owner_id on the remaining records, ideally in bulk.

For users linked to other records, hard delete may be blocked by referential integrity checks. In those cases, merge is safer than delete if your plan includes that feature; otherwise you may need to deactivate the duplicate instead of removing it. For owner reassignment, use the bulk users import/update endpoint or PATCH each user with the new owner_id, but make sure the acting token belongs to an admin who can assign users across teams.
We don't seem to have a merge endpoint on our plan. Is deactivation enough to stop the duplicates from showing up in assignments and search?

Deactivated users are hidden from most assignment pickers, but they may still appear in historical reports and some searches. Since merge isn't available on your plan, the practical path is: export the duplicate IDs, reassign any child records to the surviving user, deactivate the duplicates, and then correct owner_id on the remaining active users. If you want the duplicates fully removed after that, billing can discuss an upgrade path to the merge feature.

subject: Google Calendar integration not importing recurring events correctly
description: Our Google Calendar integration is pulling one-off meetings into the platform, but recurring events are either missing or showing only the first instance. We rely on those events for team availability planning, so this is causing scheduling problems. Is there a known issue or setting I should change?

comments.message: The affected calendars are all shared resource calendars, not personal ones. Single events import within a minute, but weekly recurring meetings don't populate beyond the first occurrence.

Thanks — that helps narrow it down. Recurring events from shared resource calendars are handled through Google's incremental sync tokens, and when those tokens become stale, our importer may only capture the master event plus the first instance. Can you confirm whether 'Expand recurring events' is enabled in the integration settings?
Yes, it is enabled. I also noticed one resource calendar was reauthorized last month after an admin account change.

That reauthorization timing lines up with token invalidation. I can't fully repair that from chat, so I've escalated this to our integrations team to reset the calendar sync state for the affected resource calendars. In the meantime, disconnecting and reauthorizing each affected shared calendar may trigger a full resync, but I don't want to promise that as a complete fix until the team reviews the logs.

subject: Role change keeps reverting after save
description: I’m trying to downgrade one teammate from Workspace Admin to Analyst. The save button works, I get the green confirmation toast, and then after a page reload he’s still an admin. We’ve attempted this from two different admin accounts and once through the bulk editor, with the same result each time.

comments.message: I’m trying to downgrade one teammate from Workspace Admin to Analyst. The save button works, I get the green confirmation toast, and then after a page reload he’s still an admin. We’ve attempted this from two different admin accounts and once through the bulk editor, with the same result each time.
Thanks. This is sometimes caused by an external identity sync restoring the prior role. Are you using SCIM or any HRIS sync that maps roles into the platform?
Yes, SCIM is enabled, but this user is excluded from role sync. The audit log shows my role_change event succeeded and then nothing else changes it back.

Got it. Then the role shouldn’t revert, and the green toast is misleading. I suspect the frontend is acknowledging the request before the policy check finishes. Could you send the user ID and timestamp of your latest attempt so we can inspect the failed mutation? I’m escalating this because we’ll likely need engineering to confirm whether the role update is being rejected or rolled back server-side.

subject: Can’t convert a suspended user into an admin
description: I’m trying to restore one of our suspended users and promote her to admin because she’s taking over internal support. The restore button worked, but when I try to change her role the page errors out and then reverts her back to standard user. I need to get her admin access before our morning shift starts.

comments.message: The user is back in active status, but every time I switch her role from Member to Admin, I get a red banner saying the change could not be saved. I’ve tried twice.
Please clear your browser cache and try again in an incognito window. Role changes usually fail because of a local browser issue.
I already tried Chrome, Safari, and incognito. The exact user is priya.singh@halesouth.com. Also, I was able to update two other users this morning, so this doesn’t seem browser-related.
In that case, just leave her as a standard user for now and have another admin perform the tasks she needs. We don’t have a separate process for this kind of role update.

subject: Campos personalizados sumiram depois da exportação e reimportação
description: Eu exportei os tickets para CSV, ajustei algumas colunas no Excel e depois reimportei para atualizar a base. O problema é que os valores de dois campos personalizados ('segmento' e 'responsável regional') ficaram vazios em centenas de registros. Eu preciso repor esses dados sem sobrescrever notas internas e sem mexer na data de criação dos tickets. O que fiz de errado?

comments.message: Eu exportei os tickets para CSV, ajustei algumas colunas no Excel e depois reimportei para atualizar a base. O problema é que os valores de dois campos personalizados ('segmento' e 'responsável regional') ficaram vazios em centenas de registros. Eu preciso repor esses dados sem sobrescrever notas internas e sem mexer na data de criação dos tickets.

Isso normalmente acontece quando a importação trata células vazias como atualização para nulo. Para repor os dados, você pode pegar a exportação original e reimportar apenas as colunas de ID do ticket, segmento e responsável regional. Se o seu objetivo for atualizar sem afetar outros campos, use o modo 'Atualizar registros existentes' e marque a opção 'Ignorar valores vazios', se ela estiver disponível na tela de mapeamento.
Eu não vi essa opção de ignorar valores vazios. Nosso plano é Growth. Ela existe nesse plano?

Revisei aqui: no plano Growth, a opção 'Ignorar valores vazios' não aparece no importador padrão. A alternativa é preparar um CSV contendo somente os IDs e os campos que você quer restaurar, com valores preenchidos em todas as linhas. Isso não altera notas internas nem a data de criação, porque esses campos não fazem parte do arquivo. Se quiser, posso te passar um modelo de CSV para evitar que outros campos sejam zerados novamente.

subject: Deactivated user restored, but old API client ID now invalid
description: We reactivated a former employee's account because she owns an integration app in our tenant. She can now log into the UI again, but the client ID for her existing API application returns 'application not found' on the token endpoint. The app is still listed in the admin screen, so something seems inconsistent between login/access and the API backend.

comments.message: User was deactivated last month and restored today. She can sign in and open Developer Apps, but the OAuth client she created before deactivation no longer works for token exchange. The client_id is rejected even though the app appears in the UI.
When a user is deactivated, API applications are permanently deleted for security reasons. You will need to create a new app.
If that's true, why is the app still visible and editable in the UI? Also, we were not warned about deletion.

I checked with the platform logs and my initial answer was incomplete. The app record still exists, but the restore flow failed to relink it in the authorization service, so login and UI access are back while token exchange fails. I've escalated this mismatch to engineering. For the fastest path, you can create a new OAuth app now, but I've asked whether they can restore the original client ID to avoid changing your downstream configuration.

subject: Account owner left company and we can't transfer ownership
description: The only billing owner on our workspace left the company unexpectedly, and their email account has already been deactivated. We can still log in as admins, but the ownership transfer page is locked because it requires confirmation from the current owner. We need to update the owner before our renewal next week. Please escalate if this needs a manual review.

comments.message: We've got a time-sensitive renewal coming up and no way to approve changes because the former owner is gone.

Thanks for explaining the situation. Ownership transfer without access to the current owner usually requires a manual verification process by our Account Protection team, since support can't bypass that control directly.
What do you need from us to move it forward?

I've escalated this to Account Protection and requested expedited review due to the upcoming renewal. Please reply with the company domain, the former owner's email, the proposed new owner, and either a recent invoice or your contract number so they can verify authority and complete the transfer.

subject: User email changed and now API OAuth app cannot be edited
description: We changed the email and role of the employee who originally registered our OAuth application, and now nobody in our team can edit the redirect URIs. The API itself still works, but we are launching a new environment next week and need to add another callback URL. Please help determine whether this is a permission issue from the account change and escalate if support can't fix it directly.

comments.message: Our OAuth app was created by a product manager who changed departments. IT changed her email and removed her admin role, and now the Edit button for redirect URIs is disabled for the rest of us. Is app editing locked to the original creator?

If the original creator lost admin rights, that can affect who can modify app settings when the app is user-owned rather than org-owned. I can confirm your app is still attached to her user object. Unfortunately, support cannot change ownership from our side.
So what are we supposed to do? We need a new redirect URI before launch.

I've escalated this to our developer platform team to either transfer the app to an org owner or manually add the new redirect URI after verifying the target domain. Please reply with the exact callback URL and expected launch date so I can append it to the escalation and reduce turnaround time.

subject: Importação de usuários sobrescreveu telefones com valores vazios
description: Subi um arquivo para atualizar contatos existentes e agora muitos telefones ficaram em branco. No CSV, algumas linhas tinham a coluna telefone vazia porque eu só queria atualizar cargo e segmento. Achei que campos vazios seriam ignorados, não que apagariam o que já existia. Como reverter isso?

comments.message: Subi um arquivo para atualizar contatos existentes e agora muitos telefones ficaram em branco. No CSV, algumas linhas tinham a coluna telefone vazia porque eu só queria atualizar cargo e segmento. Achei que campos vazios seriam ignorados, não que apagariam o que já existia. Como reverter isso?

Se a coluna telefone estava presente no arquivo, o sistema entende o valor vazio como instrução para limpar o campo, a menos que a opção 'ignorar células vazias' esteja marcada no assistente de importação. Para reverter, você pode restaurar os valores a partir de um export anterior ou do histórico de alterações do registro, se sua edição incluir auditoria de campo.
Nossa edição tem auditoria, mas fazer isso registro por registro é inviável. Existe forma de restaurar em massa?

Existe, mas depende da disponibilidade do histórico exportável. Se em Configurações > Auditoria você conseguir exportar as mudanças do campo telefone do período, dá para montar um CSV de reversão com ID do contato + telefone antigo e reimportar apenas essas colunas, desta vez com 'ignorar células vazias' ativado. Se a auditoria exportável não estiver habilitada no seu plano, vou ser sincero: aí precisaremos escalar para ver se o suporte consegue recuperar um snapshot do banco.

subject: Search returns archived contacts even when archive filter is off
description: Our support staff are getting archived contacts in search results even though the archived filter is turned off. This is causing people to update the wrong records because active and archived contacts have similar names. I can reproduce it with several accounts in our tenant. Please investigate because it looks like the search index may be ignoring the filter.

comments.message: For example, searching "Northwind AP" returns two archived contacts above the active one, even with Archived set to No. This began sometime this week.
I wasn't able to reproduce that in my test account, so it may be expected based on relevance scoring. Archived records can still appear if they are stronger matches.
That defeats the point of the filter. Also, this definitely did not work this way last week.

I see your point. I don't have tools to inspect tenant-specific search indexing from support, so I'll hand this to our search team to verify whether the archive filter is being applied correctly in your environment. Please send 2-3 example contact names if you want me to include exact cases.

subject: Login works on web but mobile app says unauthorized
description: I can sign into the web app with no problem, but the iOS mobile app keeps returning 'unauthorized user' after I enter the same credentials. I already updated the app and reinstalled it. Our company requires SSO if that matters.

comments.message: I can sign into the web app with no problem, but the iOS mobile app keeps returning 'unauthorized user' after I enter the same credentials. I already updated the app and reinstalled it. Our company requires SSO if that matters.
If your company uses SSO, you should still be able to use the same email and password in the mobile app unless your admin disabled mobile access. Please check with your admin first.
I am the admin, and mobile access is enabled for everyone. We use Azure AD SSO and nobody enters a local password on web.

Got it. In that case I was looking at the wrong flow. The iOS app for SSO tenants needs the 'Sign in with SSO' option specifically; direct email/password will return unauthorized because there is no local credential to validate. If you've already used that option and it still fails, reply with your app version and I can escalate to mobile support.

subject: Please rename our account and tell me what users will see
description: We recently rebranded, and I need to change our company name and workspace display name in the app. Before I do that, I want to understand exactly what changes for end users. Will old shared links stop working? Does the account URL change too, or just the display name?

comments.message: We recently rebranded, and I need to change our company name and workspace display name in the app. Before I do that, I want to understand exactly what changes for end users. Will old shared links stop working? Does the account URL change too, or just the display name?

You can update the company and workspace display names from Admin Settings > General. That change affects what users see in the top navigation, email headers, and certain exported reports. Existing shared links keep working because they are tied to your workspace ID, not the display name. Your account URL only changes if you separately rename the subdomain, which is a different action and should be planned more carefully.
Good to know. If we do decide to change the subdomain later, is that something I can do myself?

Not directly from the admin panel on most plans. Subdomain changes are handled by support because we need to check custom login flows, API integrations, and bookmarked links. If you go ahead with only the display-name update now, users will see the new branding almost immediately, and no action is required on their side.

subject: Champ personnalisé Jira non mappé lors de la création de tickets
description: Nous avons relié votre plateforme à Jira Cloud pour créer automatiquement des tickets à partir des incidents critiques. La création fonctionne, mais le champ personnalisé "Service impacté" reste vide dans Jira, alors qu'il est bien rempli chez vous. J'ai essayé de refaire le mapping deux fois, mais votre interface n'affiche que les champs standards Jira. Est-ce que les custom fields sont supportés avec Jira Cloud ?

comments.message: Bonjour, notre intégration Jira crée bien les tickets, mais impossible de pousser le champ personnalisé "Service impacté". Je ne vois pas ce custom field dans votre écran de mapping.

Bonjour, les champs personnalisés Jira Cloud sont pris en charge, mais ils n'apparaissent que si notre intégration peut lire le schéma du projet et du type de ticket sélectionnés. Pouvez-vous vérifier que la connexion Jira a les droits de lecture sur le projet cible, puis cliquer sur "Actualiser les métadonnées" dans le mapping ?
Je viens de le faire. Le champ apparaît maintenant, mais sous le nom "customfield_10234" et je ne suis pas sûr du bon choix.

C'est normal avec Jira Cloud: l'API expose souvent l'ID technique. Si vous ouvrez l'écran d'administration du champ dans Jira, vous devriez retrouver le même identifiant. Mappez "Service impacté" vers customfield_10234, enregistrez, puis envoyez un incident test. Si la valeur ne passe pas, il faudra vérifier le type exact du champ (liste, texte, option) car Jira rejette silencieusement certains formats.

subject: Rabattcode wurde bei Verlängerung nicht angewendet
description: Unser Vertrieb hatte uns für die Verlängerung einen Rabatt von 15 % für die nächsten 12 Monate zugesagt. Die gestern ausgestellte Rechnung enthält aber den vollen Preis ohne Nachlass. Ich habe auch keine Position gesehen, die den Rabatt ausweist. Können Sie das bitte prüfen und korrigieren?

comments.message: Unser Vertrieb hatte uns für die Verlängerung einen Rabatt von 15 % für die nächsten 12 Monate zugesagt. Die gestern ausgestellte Rechnung enthält aber den vollen Preis ohne Nachlass. Ich habe auch keine Position gesehen, die den Rabatt ausweist. Können Sie das bitte prüfen und korrigieren?
Ich habe Ihren Account überprüft und sehe keinen aktiven Rabattcode im Billing-System. Daher wurde die Rechnung korrekt ohne Nachlass erstellt.
Ich habe die E-Mail vom Vertrieb mit dem bestätigten Rabatt vorliegen. Soll ich sie weiterleiten?

Ja, bitte senden Sie die Bestätigung hier im Anhang oder als Screenshot. Wenn der Rabatt vertraglich zugesagt wurde, lassen wir die Rechnung entsprechend prüfen und gegebenenfalls eine Gutschrift erstellen.

subject: Weekly license utilization report should go only to site admins
description: I’m setting up a weekly license utilization report and want it delivered only to our site admins for each region, not to all admins in the account. We already use user groups for regional administration, but I’m not certain whether report subscriptions can target those groups cleanly. I also need a short explanation for our internal admin handbook about how regional admin access affects reporting recipients.

comments.message: We have North America, EMEA, and APAC site admins. Each group should receive only its own region’s license report. Is the right approach to make three copies of the report, or can one report subscription handle recipients by admin group?

One subscription can usually be sent to multiple people, but region-specific delivery is cleaner with separate report versions or separate subscriptions tied to filtered views for each region. Pair each filtered report with the corresponding regional admin group so only the right site admins can access and receive it.
If a global admin owns the subscriptions, will the site admins still only see their regional data?

Not automatically. If the global admin schedules an unfiltered report, recipients may receive broader data than intended depending on how the report is configured. The safer setup is separate regional reports with saved filters, folder permissions limited to each site-admin group, and subscriptions sent only to those users. For your handbook: recipient lists and user access should align, because reporting delivery should follow the same regional boundaries as admin responsibilities.

subject: Password reset disabled after switching auth method
description: I changed my account from SSO to password login because our IdP migration is still in progress. The account page now shows password login enabled, but the "send reset email" option is greyed out and I can’t set a password. We’re trying to transition a few users manually and this one seems stuck between auth methods.

comments.message: I changed my account from SSO to password login because our IdP migration is still in progress. The account page now shows password login enabled, but the "send reset email" option is greyed out and I can’t set a password. We’re trying to transition a few users manually and this one seems stuck between auth methods.
When switching from SSO to password login, the reset option is disabled until the user logs out everywhere. Please revoke active sessions and wait a few minutes.
We revoked all sessions over an hour ago. The user also never received the activation email, and the account details panel still shows an SSO-only badge in one place and password-enabled in another.

Thanks. That inconsistency suggests the auth-mode change only partially applied. I’m opening an escalation with our authentication team because this may be a bug in the account conversion workflow. As a temporary workaround, we can create a fresh password-based account for the user and transfer their assignments if you need access restored today.

subject: Azure AD login loop after invoice became overdue and NetSuite export failed
description: Since yesterday our users have been stuck in a login loop with Azure AD, and our NetSuite export has also stopped with a permissions error. When I checked the admin area, I saw a red banner saying the invoice is overdue, which is confusing because accounting says they already paid it by ACH last week. I need to know whether the overdue invoice is what disabled access and integrations, or if something else changed.

comments.message: Since yesterday our users have been stuck in a login loop with Azure AD, and our NetSuite export has also stopped with a permissions error. When I checked the admin area, I saw a red banner saying the invoice is overdue, which is confusing because accounting says they already paid it by ACH last week. I need to know whether the overdue invoice is what disabled access and integrations, or if something else changed.

I reviewed the account and I do see an ACH payment submitted, but it is still in pending settlement, so the system temporarily placed the workspace in restricted status overnight. That status can interrupt SSO handoff and pause ERP exports like NetSuite. I’ve manually lifted the restriction for 48 hours while billing verifies the payment, and I also noticed the NetSuite token lost its role mapping during the retry attempts. Please reauthorize the NetSuite integration once from an admin account, and Azure AD logins should begin working again within 15 minutes.
Azure AD is working again now. NetSuite reconnects, but the export still fails on customer records only.

That narrows it down. The billing hold was part of the problem, but the remaining export failure is likely due to the NetSuite role lacking customer write permission on the custom segment you sync. I’m escalating this to our integrations team with your account logs and keeping the billing grace period in place until they confirm whether a connector-side mapping needs to be fixed.

subject: SCIM provisioning created deactivated users and our NetSuite import now fails
description: We enabled Azure AD SCIM provisioning to automate user access, and since then several teammates were provisioned as inactive in your app even though they can still authenticate through SSO. Around the same time, our NetSuite integration started failing on the customer sync, and the imported records that do arrive are missing subsidiary values. I'm trying to understand if the provisioning setup changed permissions on the integration user or if there is a data mapping problem on your side.

comments.message: Azure AD is creating users, but many land as deactivated and need manual reactivation. They can still get through SSO, which is confusing. Also our NetSuite sync went from healthy to failing with permission errors, and subsidiary is blank on the few customer records that make it over.

I see two separate symptoms that may share one cause. SCIM can assign a default role on provisioning; if that role lacks app access, users can authenticate but remain deactivated for product use. Separately, if your NetSuite token was tied to a user whose role changed during the access cleanup, the connector may have lost permission to the subsidiary field. Can you confirm whether the SCIM default role was recently edited and whether the NetSuite token still belongs to the original service user?
Yes, the SCIM default role was changed from Admin to a custom restricted role. And the NetSuite token now belongs to our IT manager because the old service user was removed when we audited accounts.

That lines up with what you're seeing. Please switch the SCIM default role to one that has product access, or update the custom role to include login entitlement in our app. For NetSuite, recreate the token under a dedicated service user with customer, subsidiary, and external ID permissions; then remap the customer sync fields. I can see failed imports but I can't repair the blank subsidiary values already written, so I'm opening an escalation for our integrations team to advise on backfilling those records.

subject: API key stopped working after ownership transfer
description: I transferred ownership of our workspace from me to our new IT manager yesterday, and since then our production integration has been getting 401 errors on every API call. I regenerated a key under my profile, but that still fails for some endpoints. We need to know whether the account change invalidated the old credentials and what we need to update without breaking our webhook consumer.

comments.message: We changed the workspace owner yesterday, and right after that our API started returning 401 Unauthorized for /v2/contacts and /v2/events. The old key had been working for months. Is this related to the ownership change?

Yes, ownership changes can affect API credentials if the token was created under the previous owner's user context or if admin permissions changed during the transfer. I checked your account and can confirm the original token was tied to your user, which no longer has the same role. Please create a new service account under Settings > Developers, generate a new API key there, and update both your REST client and webhook signing secret.
I created the service account key and the REST calls work again, but our incoming webhooks are still failing signature validation. We didn't rotate that secret manually.

I can see why: the webhook endpoint is still associated with the legacy app created by your old admin profile. That mapping doesn't move cleanly during an ownership transfer. I'm escalating this to our platform team to rebind the webhook subscription to the workspace-level service account without forcing a full reconfiguration. I'll attach your failing endpoint ID and mark this as production-impacting.

subject: Parent-child workflow updates are causing an automation loop
description: We automated a process where updating a parent project should update all child tasks, and then child task completion should update the parent project status. Since turning both workflows on, records keep bouncing between statuses and hitting our automation limits. I need advice on how to stop the loop without losing the sync between parent and child records.

comments.message: Workflow A: when parent project status changes to In Progress, set all child tasks to In Progress. Workflow B: when all child tasks are In Progress, update parent project to In Progress. We assumed no-op updates would be ignored, but the logs show both workflows repeatedly firing.

You've created a circular trigger pattern. Even if the visible status value doesn't appear to change, downstream updates can still count as modifications and retrigger automations. The safest approach is to add guard conditions, such as a source marker field like 'Updated by automation,' or require the new status to differ from the current stored value before writing. Many teams also separate parent-to-child sync from child-to-parent rollups so only one direction performs direct status writes.
We don't have a source marker yet, but we can add one. Is that enough by itself?

Usually it helps, but you'll want to clear the marker carefully so you don't suppress legitimate future updates. I'd recommend implementing both a marker and explicit 'only update if value changed' checks, then testing in a sandbox before re-enabling both workflows in production.

subject: Charged twice after account merge and now I can't access either workspace
description: We consolidated two workspaces into one this month, but my company card was charged on both subscriptions. At the same time, my admin login stopped working on the old workspace and the new one says I don’t have permission to manage billing. I need one of these invoices reversed and I need access to the surviving account so I can verify renewals and receipts.

comments.message: We consolidated two workspaces into one this month, but my company card was charged on both subscriptions. At the same time, my admin login stopped working on the old workspace and the new one says I don’t have permission to manage billing. I need one of these invoices reversed and I need access to the surviving account so I can verify renewals and receipts.

Merges do not normally affect billing permissions. If you can’t access billing, please ask another admin on your team to grant you access. Duplicate charges may appear temporarily and usually disappear.
There is no other admin anymore—that was part of the merge. Also, these are posted charges, not pending authorizations.
In that case, you may want to contact your bank first. I don’t see anything else to do from support right now.

subject: Webhook delivery errors affecting all production events
description: Starting around 09:20 UTC, our production webhooks began failing with 503 responses in your delivery log even though our endpoint health checks are green. Sandbox works, production does not. We have downstream automations failing for customers right now. I need this escalated to the team that can check your outbound delivery system.

comments.message: This looks like a platform-side incident. Can you confirm whether there's an issue with webhook delivery in production?
I don't see a public incident posted right now, so there may not be a broader outage. Could you verify your endpoint certificate and firewall rules?
Those haven't changed, and your logs show failures across multiple endpoints from different IPs. This needs a deeper check.

You're right that this needs internal review. I've escalated it to our Platform Engineering team with the failing event IDs, timestamp range, and the difference between sandbox and production behavior. I can't confirm a platform incident yet, but the escalation is in progress and they'll investigate the delivery path.

subject: Looking for admin guidance on deactivating users without losing history
description: I'm preparing offboarding instructions for regional admins and need to explain the right way to deactivate users. Our managers are worried that removing someone will erase task ownership history, comments, and audit trails. Can you give me a plain-English explanation of what happens when a user is deactivated versus deleted, and what we should do before processing a departure?

comments.message: I'm preparing offboarding instructions for regional admins and need to explain the right way to deactivate users. Our managers are worried that removing someone will erase task ownership history, comments, and audit trails. Can you give me a plain-English explanation of what happens when a user is deactivated versus deleted, and what we should do before processing a departure?

Deactivating a user generally blocks future login while preserving their historical activity, such as comments, ownership records, and audit entries. Deletion is more destructive and may remove profile visibility or personally identifiable details depending on your retention settings. Before offboarding, the safest steps are to transfer any active work, reassign automated ownership rules, remove sensitive tokens or integrations owned by that user, and then deactivate rather than delete unless there's a compliance reason.
This is helpful. Some local admins also asked whether deactivated users still count toward paid seats immediately.

Seat release timing depends on your subscription model and whether licenses are pooled instantly or at the next billing cycle. I can explain the offboarding best practice, but to avoid giving you the wrong billing guidance I've escalated that part to our account team. You can still proceed with the admin checklist above for the operational side.

subject: Charged for premium automations that never activated
description: We upgraded specifically to get multi-step workflow automation across departments, and we were billed for the higher plan on the 1st. The problem is that the premium automation actions still show as locked in the builder, and our live onboarding workflow can’t be published. I need this fixed or a credit, because right now we’re paying for billing access we don’t actually have in the automation module.

comments.message: We were charged for the Business plan three days ago. Billing reflects the upgrade, but the workflow builder still says multi-branch automation is unavailable on our account.
Thanks for reaching out. If the invoice is paid, the upgrade should be immediate. Can you try logging out and back in, then clearing your browser cache?
We already did that on two browsers. The banner is account-wide, not user-specific, and we have a customer launch today.
In that case it may just take a little more time for the new plan to propagate. Please wait 24 hours and check again. If it still shows locked, reply here with a screenshot.

subject: SCIM rename changed external IDs and broke API sync
description: We pushed a SCIM update from our identity provider to rename several user accounts, and now our middleware cannot reconcile some users when calling your API. It looks like the emails changed, but our sync also sees different identifiers than before. We need help understanding which account fields are stable after a SCIM-driven update and which API fields our integration should stop depending on.

comments.message: Our IdP renamed multiple users via SCIM, and our sync job now fails when trying to match API users by email. Some records also appear with a different external identifier than what we stored previously. Which field should remain stable across account renames?

The safest field to persist is the platform's immutable internal user ID returned by the API, not email, display name, or values your identity provider may rewrite. SCIM updates can change login attributes and, depending on your mapping, external IDs too. If you share one example payload and the before/after values, I can help determine whether your SCIM configuration is overwriting the external_id field.
We were indeed matching on email instead of your internal user ID. For one user, SCIM also overwrote external_id with the Azure object ID.

That would explain both issues. I'd recommend rekeying your middleware to the internal API user ID for ongoing sync, and treating external_id as IdP-managed if SCIM is configured that way. For existing mismatches, export current users from the API, rebuild the mapping table, and then rerun the failed sync in dry-run mode first.

subject: Error al conectar Google Calendar y necesito una explicación básica de permisos
description: Intenté integrar Google Calendar para que las reuniones se creen desde la plataforma, pero me apareció un aviso de permisos y no sé si di acceso de más o de menos. Además, algunos usuarios del equipo no consiguen conectar sus cuentas. Necesito entender qué permisos hacen falta y por qué no todos pueden hacerlo.

comments.message: Yo pude conectar mi calendario, pero dos compañeras reciben un mensaje de que la app necesita aprobación del administrador de Google Workspace. ¿Eso depende de ustedes o de nuestra empresa?

Depende de la configuración de Google Workspace de tu empresa. Nuestra integración solicita permisos para crear y actualizar eventos en el calendario conectado, y en algunas organizaciones un administrador debe aprobar la app antes de que otros usuarios puedan autorizarla.
¿Y cómo sé si los permisos son razonables? El equipo está preocupado por la privacidad.

Buena pregunta. Para esta integración, los permisos se limitan normalmente a ver disponibilidad básica y crear/editar eventos necesarios para la función de agenda. No usamos esos permisos para leer correos ni acceder a Drive. Lo recomendable es que tu administrador revise la pantalla de consentimiento de Google y apruebe la app para una unidad de prueba antes de desplegarla al resto del equipo.

subject: HubSpot activity report stopped updating after custom field change
description: Our marketing activity dashboard hasn't updated correctly since we changed a custom property in HubSpot on Monday. New campaign responses are visible on contact profiles, but the reporting widgets still show old totals and zeroes in segments that should have data. I need help determining whether the HubSpot integration is broken, whether the report definitions need updating, and if this has to be escalated internally.

comments.message: We renamed a HubSpot custom property from 'Lead Source Detail' to 'Acquisition Detail' and now the Campaign Performance report in your app is frozen on Monday's numbers. Can you fix it?

I reviewed the integration logs and the sync itself is still running. However, your saved report is grouped by the old property key, which HubSpot treats as a new schema reference after the rename. That's why individual contacts still show updated values while the report widgets don't.
Can you update the report without breaking our dashboards? We have six exec dashboards using that field.

I updated two affected report definitions to point to the new property and refreshed the dashboard cache, so current data should now populate going forward. The other four dashboards use locked templates owned by your admin role, and support can't edit those directly. I've escalated the remaining dashboard updates to our reporting specialists and attached the impacted widget IDs so they can batch-fix them. In the meantime, you'll see current numbers on the repaired dashboards, but historical trend lines may still look incomplete until the backfill job runs.

subject: Annual invoice still shows monthly add-on after feature was disabled
description: We removed the advanced routing package from our account configuration before renewal, but our annual invoice still includes it. The routing rules are no longer active in the admin panel, so I need to know whether billing is lagging behind the configuration change or whether the add-on was removed incorrectly.

comments.message: Our renewal processed yesterday and we were billed for Advanced Routing even though I disabled the module in settings two weeks ago. The custom queue rules are gone from the UI and we are only using standard routing now. Why is the add-on still on the invoice?

I checked the subscription history and the product configuration was turned off in the workspace, but that doesn't automatically amend the contract line item for annual billing. The add-on remains billable until it's removed from the renewal order form or canceled by our account team before the invoice is generated.
No one told us disabling it in the product wasn't enough. Is there any way to get the annual charge reversed now that the feature is no longer in use?

I've escalated this to your account manager because post-renewal adjustments on annual contracts require approval. I included evidence that the feature was disabled prior to renewal, but at the moment the invoice is still valid as issued. They can advise whether a credit or amended order is possible.

subject: Best way to onboard non-technical managers to approval workflows
description: We’re turning on approval workflows for budget requests, and the managers who need to approve items are not system-savvy. I need advice on how to introduce this without overwhelming them. Specifically, I want to know what they actually need to learn versus what only admins need to know.

comments.message: Some of them are already asking if they need to understand workflow rules and conditions.

For most approvers, no — they usually only need to know how to find pending approvals, review the request details, approve or reject, and leave comments. Workflow rules, conditions, and routing logic are admin-level concepts. A simple onboarding plan is: first show them where approval notifications appear, then do one live example of approving and rejecting, then explain what happens after their decision. Keep admin training separate so managers aren’t overloaded with setup details.
That sounds right. What’s the easiest way to prevent them from editing fields they shouldn’t touch?

You can use role-based permissions plus form settings. Set managers to an approver-style role with access to the approval action, but not configuration rights. Then make sensitive fields read-only on the form or hide them entirely from that role if your plan supports field-level permissions. If you tell me which plan you’re on, I can point you to the exact settings page.

subject: Dashboard numbers dropped after deduplicating accounts
description: After we merged duplicate accounts this morning, the totals in one of our customer health dashboards dropped by almost 12%. I expected record counts to change a little, but now some segments look completely off. I need help figuring out whether this is normal, whether data got lost in the merge, and what I should tell my team about how merged records affect reporting.

comments.message: We merged based on domain and manually reviewed a lot of them. The dashboard uses account owner, ARR, and lifecycle stage. Could merges remove values from the secondary record?

Merging can absolutely affect dashboard totals because one record is retained as the primary and the secondary record is absorbed. In many cases, the primary record keeps its existing field values unless blank-field overwrite rules or manual selections were made during the merge. So if ARR or lifecycle stage existed only on the secondary record and wasn’t carried over, your reporting can change.
Is there a report that shows what field values were kept during each merge?

Not exactly a dedicated merge-retention report in the dashboard area. You should check the merge audit entries on a sample of affected accounts and compare pre-merge exports if available. For your team, the main education point is that deduplication improves record hygiene but can change aggregates if key fields weren’t standardized first. I’d suggest exporting the merged accounts, validating ARR/lifecycle stage completeness, and then backfilling missing values with a controlled bulk update. If you want, I can escalate this to our data specialist team to review whether anything unusual happened in the merge logic.

subject: Imported date format changed and automation skipped records
description: After yesterday's CSV import, our renewal date field shows correctly on the records, but the automation that should trigger 30 days before renewal skipped about 400 customers. Looking closer, the skipped records were imported with DD/MM/YYYY while our usual file is YYYY-MM-DD. The app normalized the display, so I assumed the data was fine. Could there be a parsing bug where automations stored the raw date differently from the record view?

comments.message: After yesterday's CSV import, our renewal date field shows correctly on the records, but the automation that should trigger 30 days before renewal skipped about 400 customers. Looking closer, the skipped records were imported with DD/MM/YYYY while our usual file is YYYY-MM-DD.

I reproduced this with one of the skipped customer IDs. The import layer normalized the renewal date for display, but the automation engine stored those rows with a null next-trigger value when the source format was DD/MM/YYYY and locale detection was disabled. So yes, this is a bug affecting the downstream automation data, not just formatting. I have reprocessed the affected import batch so the trigger values are being recalculated now.
Will that also backfill the automations that should have fired yesterday?

It will recalculate eligibility, but missed actions from yesterday will not automatically replay. I've submitted a one-time backfill request to run the renewal workflow for the affected 400 records, and our automation team will confirm once that's complete.

subject: HubSpot contacts duplicated and inflating activity report
description: Since we connected HubSpot last week, our weekly activity report is basically unusable. A large number of contacts are duplicated, and every duplicated contact seems to double-count email touches in the dashboard. I merged a few manually, but the report numbers are still inflated. Can someone review the integration settings and escalate if this needs a data fix?

comments.message: Since we connected HubSpot last week, our weekly activity report is basically unusable. A large number of contacts are duplicated, and every duplicated contact seems to double-count email touches in the dashboard. I merged a few manually, but the report numbers are still inflated. Can someone review the integration settings and escalate if this needs a data fix?

This usually happens when the sync creates a new contact every time HubSpot updates a property. Please turn off your HubSpot sync and delete the duplicates, then your reports should correct themselves within an hour.

That doesn't sound right. We have thousands of contacts and can't just delete records blindly. Also, duplicates are only happening when the HubSpot contact has no company assigned. Can you confirm the actual matching logic?

I reviewed it a bit more and I don't have enough confidence to tell you to delete anything. I'm escalating this to our integrations support queue with your example records because matching behavior by company association can affect reporting deduplication. In the meantime, you can add a report filter for 'primary record only' to reduce the inflation, but this won't fully fix historical counts.

subject: New teammate can't access our workspace after invite
description: I invited a new project coordinator to our workspace yesterday, but she still can't get in. She says the link sends her to a sign-in page and then she gets a message that no account is associated with our organization. I'm also not fully sure whether I added her with the right role, because I only wanted her to view reports and comment, not edit settings. Can you help me get her access sorted out and explain the correct way to add people with limited permissions?

comments.message: I invited a new project coordinator to our workspace yesterday, but she still can't get in. She says the link sends her to a sign-in page and then she gets a message that no account is associated with our organization. I'm also not fully sure whether I added her with the right role, because I only wanted her to view reports and comment, not edit settings. Can you help me get her access sorted out and explain the correct way to add people with limited permissions?

Absolutely — it sounds like there may be two things going on: the invite may not have been accepted under the same email address you invited, and the assigned role may need adjusting. Please go to Admin Settings > Users, find her pending invite, and confirm the email address matches the one she used to sign in. If it doesn't, delete the pending invite and resend it to the correct address. For limited permissions, assign the Viewer role if she only needs to see reports, or Commenter if she should also leave comments. Neither role can edit settings.

I checked and the invite was sent to her personal Gmail, but she tried signing in with her work Microsoft account. I resent it to the work address. She can now get in, but she can still see the user management menu. I thought viewers wouldn't see that.

Glad the login issue is fixed. If she can see User Management, she was likely added as a Manager instead of Viewer or Commenter. In Admin Settings > Users, click her profile, change the role to Commenter, and save. Commenters can access shared content and leave comments but cannot manage users or workspace settings. If she still sees the menu after the role change, ask her to sign out and back in so the permissions refresh.

subject: Need reseller contact removed and direct billing reinstated
description: We recently ended our relationship with an external reseller who used to manage our subscription, but invoices are still going to them and our direct credit card was also charged this month. I need the reseller removed from the account, our internal admin rights restored, and help figuring out why both billing paths were used.

comments.message: Our former reseller, Blue Arch Systems, should no longer be attached to our account as of this quarter. Yet April's invoice was sent to them, and our company Amex was also charged directly for $2,050. Please remove the reseller contact, make me the billing admin, and explain whether we've been double billed.

I can see the account had a reseller-managed billing setup that appears to have been partially converted to direct billing. That can cause confusion, but not always duplicate charges. I'll need to escalate the reseller detachment and admin reassignment to our finance systems team because those records sync from a different backend.
Do you at least know whether the Amex charge is valid? We need to close our month-end books tomorrow.

I can confirm the direct Amex charge posted from our system, but I can't yet verify whether the reseller invoice was also collectible or just informational. I've marked the escalation as urgent and asked the finance systems team to review both billing channels and transfer billing control to you. They should respond within 1 business day.

subject: Group admin can’t add new hires to custom role
description: I manage users for our support org. A delegated group admin told me she can create users but can’t assign any of the new hires to our custom "Tier 1 Limited" role. The assign button is greyed out only for that role. I tested with my own account and saw the same thing for one department, but not another, so it seems inconsistent. We need to onboard six people before Monday.

comments.message: A delegated group admin can create users, but the button to assign our custom "Tier 1 Limited" role is greyed out. I can reproduce it too for one department only.

This usually happens when the custom role is restricted by group scope or missing a permission dependency. Please open the role in Admin > Roles and verify whether "Manage tickets" or any required base permissions were removed recently. Also check whether the affected department is mapped to a different workspace or user group.
I checked. The role still has the same permissions as last week, and the affected department is mapped to our East Support group. Users in West Support can be assigned normally.

Got it. I tested the logic you described against our role-scoping rules, and it sounds like the East Support group may have hit a stale permissions cache after the last role edit. Please remove the East Support group from the custom role, save, add it back, and then retry assigning one of the users. If that still fails, I’ll need to escalate to our access-control team because the inconsistency is likely backend rather than configuration.
I removed and re-added the group. That fixed it for two new hires, but four still can’t be assigned.

Thanks for trying that. Since the workaround only partially helped, I’ve escalated the remaining four user IDs and the role configuration to our access-control specialists. In the meantime, if onboarding can’t wait, assign those four users the standard Support Agent role temporarily and restrict them via group membership until we get a permanent fix.

subject: Trying to standardize naming conventions for account records
description: Our account list is a mess — some entries include legal entity names, some use DBA names, and some have the country appended at the end. I’m writing internal documentation for our team and need a practical recommendation for naming rules plus a way to clean up existing records in bulk.

comments.message: Our account list is a mess — some entries include legal entity names, some use DBA names, and some have the country appended at the end. I’m writing internal documentation for our team and need a practical recommendation for naming rules plus a way to clean up existing records in bulk.

I’d recommend a standard naming format like Primary Brand Name only, and then storing legal entity, DBA, and country in separate fields. That keeps account names readable while preserving reporting data. For cleanup, export accounts, decide the canonical naming rule, use formulas to generate corrected names, and then bulk update just the Account Name field. In your team documentation, include 5-10 examples of tricky cases such as subsidiaries, regional branches, and merged companies.
Should we include country in the account name for global customers with multiple branches?

Generally no, unless each branch is intentionally managed as a separate account record. It’s better to track branch or country in dedicated fields and use parent-child relationships if your data model supports them. That will make deduping and segmentation much easier later.

subject: Rows disappeared after deduplication and users are stuck in password reset loop
description: I ran the duplicate merge tool on our contacts yesterday, and now some records seem to have vanished entirely instead of being merged. At the same time, two users are stuck in a password reset loop where they can set a new password but then get prompted to reset again on next login. I need someone to help me verify whether data was deleted and why access isn't sticking.

comments.message: We merged about 2,000 duplicates. This morning our team says at least 40 customer records can't be found by search, and two users keep getting sent back to the reset password page after logging in.

For the login loop, please have the users clear their browser cache and try again. For the missing contact records, the merge tool permanently removes duplicates and keeps only the primary record, so what you're seeing may be expected.
But some of the missing names were primary records, and clearing cache didn't fix the reset loop for one user. Search by exact email returns nothing for several accounts.

Understood. This needs deeper review. I can't confirm from your note whether the dedupe job incorrectly deleted primaries or whether search indexing is delayed after large merges, and the reset loop persisting after cache clear suggests an auth issue beyond the browser. I'm escalating both the merge job audit and the affected users' authentication logs to our technical team.

subject: Não consigo atualizar prioridade via API apesar de a opção estar ativa
description: Ativei nas definições que os utilizadores da integração podem editar tickets, mas as chamadas PATCH para alterar a prioridade devolvem 200 e o valor continua igual. Consigo atualizar o assunto, mas não a prioridade. Isto pode depender da configuração do workflow?

comments.message: Ativei nas definições que os utilizadores da integração podem editar tickets, mas as chamadas PATCH para alterar a prioridade devolvem 200 e o valor continua igual. Consigo atualizar o assunto, mas não a prioridade. Isto pode depender da configuração do workflow?

Sim, pode depender. Se a prioridade estiver controlada por uma regra de workflow ou por restrições do formulário, a API aceita a chamada mas ignora o campo não editável. Verifiquei a sua conta e o campo prioridade está bloqueado quando o ticket pertence ao formulário "Suporte Enterprise". Para permitir atualização via API, precisa desativar a opção "prioridade calculada automaticamente" nesse formulário ou usar um formulário onde o campo seja editável.
Desativei a prioridade automática só no formulário de teste e agora o PATCH já altera o valor. Vamos rever isso internamente.
Perfeito. Se quiser manter a automatização e ainda assim influenciar a prioridade, uma alternativa é enviar um campo personalizado e deixar uma regra interna converter esse valor para prioridade.

subject: Bulk invite endpoint created duplicate users after retry
description: I used your bulk invite API to onboard about 120 contractors and assign them to a restricted workspace. Our script timed out, so we retried the request, and now some people appear twice in the user admin page with one pending invite and one active account. I need to understand whether there is an idempotency option or a safe way to clean this up.

comments.message: The duplicates all have the same email address pattern except a few ended up with plus-suffixed usernames in the directory. We need to keep the workspace assignments but remove the extra accounts.

Our bulk invite endpoint should ignore duplicate emails automatically, so there shouldn't be two accounts for the same person. If you see duplicates, it may just be cached data in the admin page. Try refreshing or clearing your browser cache.
I already did that, and I can also see both records through GET /users?email=. One record is pending and one is active, and both are assigned to the workspace.
In that case, please delete the extra users manually from the admin console. I don't believe the API supports idempotency keys for invites, but the UI should let you clean it up one by one.

subject: Invoice PDF download fails and late fee was added
description: I was trying to download our invoice PDF for approval, but every time I clicked Download the app returned a 500 error. Because of that delay, our payment was submitted a day later and your system added a late fee automatically. I need the late fee removed if the download failure on your side prevented us from processing the invoice on time. Can someone investigate the PDF error and the fee?

comments.message: I was trying to download our invoice PDF for approval, but every time I clicked Download the app returned a 500 error. Because of that delay, our payment was submitted a day later and your system added a late fee automatically. I need the late fee removed if the download failure on your side prevented us from processing the invoice on time. Can someone investigate the PDF error and the fee?

I found multiple PDF generation failures on your account yesterday tied to our document service, so the error you saw was real. I also verified the late fee was added overnight by the normal dunning process before your payment posted.
Okay, can you waive the fee then?

Yes. I removed the late fee and issued a revised invoice copy to your billing contact. I’ve also linked your case to the PDF-generation bug so our team can review why invoice exports failed during that period. If you still get an error downloading the updated PDF, let me know and I can send it directly.

subject: Datas foram convertidas errado no upload
description: Eu subi um arquivo com a coluna 'data de renovação' no formato DD/MM/AAAA, mas depois da importação várias contas ficaram com mês e dia invertidos. Agora contratos de 05/07 viraram 07/05 e isso bagunçou nossos alertas. Preciso corrigir os registros sem editar um por um. Há como normalizar isso em lote?

comments.message: Eu subi um arquivo com a coluna 'data de renovação' no formato DD/MM/AAAA, mas depois da importação várias contas ficaram com mês e dia invertidos. Agora contratos de 05/07 viraram 07/05 e isso bagunçou nossos alertas. Preciso corrigir os registros sem editar um por um.

Quando o importador detecta formato americano, isso pode acontecer. A correção em lote é possível: exporte apenas os registros afetados com seus IDs e a data atual, corrija a coluna em uma planilha para o padrão aceito pelo sistema (recomendamos AAAA-MM-DD) e reimporte no modo de atualização. Assim você substitui só o campo 'data de renovação'.
Como eu identifico só os afetados? Nem todas as datas ficaram erradas, apenas as ambíguas até o dia 12.

Boa observação. Filtre por datas de renovação entre os últimos valores suspeitos e compare com a coluna original do seu arquivo-fonte; foque nas datas em que dia e mês são ambos menores ou iguais a 12. Se preferir, adicione uma coluna auxiliar no Excel para sinalizar possíveis inversões. Depois reimporte somente os IDs desses registros com a data corrigida em AAAA-MM-DD. Isso evita editar um por um e não altera outros campos.

subject: Mobile app comments appear out of order after sync
description: Our field team uses the mobile app, and after syncing, comments on some records appear out of chronological order. This makes the case history confusing because a newer internal note can show above an older customer reply. We have reports from iOS and Android users, so it doesn't seem platform-specific. Can someone investigate?

comments.message: I can reproduce it on record 55192: add an internal note offline, reconnect, sync, and then the note appears above an earlier public comment from yesterday.
This is likely just a local device sort issue. Please ask the users to log out and log back in to refresh the thread order.
We already tried reinstalling the app on one device and the same record still sorts incorrectly on web afterward, so it doesn't look local.

Got it. Since the ordering issue persists on web after mobile sync, that suggests the timestamps or sequence values may be written incorrectly during sync. I’m escalating this as a probable bug to our mobile/backend team with your reproduction steps and sample record ID 55192. We’ll need their help to inspect the sync payloads.

subject: Scheduled automation sent reports at the wrong local time
description: I scheduled a workflow automation to email weekly usage reports every Monday at 8:00 a.m. local time for each account owner, but users in Australia got the email in the middle of the night while US users received it at the expected time. I need to know if the scheduler supports recipient local time or if we configured this wrong.

comments.message: The workflow is set to run every Monday at 8:00 a.m. and send a report to the account owner. We assumed that meant 8:00 a.m. for each owner's timezone, but our Sydney team got the email around 1:00 a.m. local time.

Scheduled workflows usually run in the account timezone, not each recipient's timezone. So if your workspace is set to US Eastern, everyone receives the email based on that schedule unless you build separate branches or workflows by region. To confirm, check Settings > Workspace timezone and compare it with the send timestamps.
Got it. Our workspace is set to Eastern, so that explains the send time. We'll probably need separate regional workflows.
Yes, that's the practical approach today. You can segment by owner timezone or region and schedule separate automations, though it's admittedly more manual than a true per-user local send feature.

subject: Po zmianie adresu e-mail nie mogę się zalogować
description: Zmieniłem w profilu adres e-mail na nowy służbowy i od tego momentu nie mogę zalogować się ani starym, ani nowym adresem. System mówi, że użytkownik nie istnieje. Nie mam też już aktywnej sesji w przeglądarce.

comments.message: Po zmianie adresu e-mail w profilu straciłem dostęp do konta. Oba adresy zwracają komunikat, że użytkownik nie istnieje.
Dziękuję za zgłoszenie. Takie problemy zwykle wynikają z literówki przy zmianie adresu. Proszę powiedzieć, jaki był stary i nowy e-mail.
Stary: adam.kurek@northlane.pl, nowy miał być: a.kurek@northlane.pl.

Widzę, że konto zostało zapisane jako a.kruerk@northlane.pl. Nie mogę samodzielnie poprawić loginu, bo konto jest powiązane z SSO organizacji. Przekazuję sprawę do zespołu tożsamości, żeby ręcznie skorygował adres i odświeżył mapowanie użytkownika. Otrzyma Pan aktualizację w tym wątku, zwykle w ciągu 4 godzin.

subject: Former employee still listed as security contact after admin change
description: We deactivated our old IT manager last week and promoted a new one, but I just noticed security notifications are still addressed to the former employee. I need help understanding which contact records live outside user management and how to fully update them. If this can't be done in-app, please route it to the right team.

comments.message: I assumed deactivating the old IT manager would remove them everywhere. Instead, they're still listed on security emails and maybe compliance notices too. Where do I change that?

Deactivating a user only removes their login access and assigned role; it doesn't always update separate account contacts like security, billing, or legal notifications. Those fields may live under account profile or subscription records rather than user management.
I can't find any security contact field in our admin pages. Is it hidden by plan?

It may not be self-service on your account, especially if those contacts were set during onboarding or on an enterprise agreement. Please send the replacement contact's full name, company email, and whether they should receive security-only notices or all compliance communications. I'm escalating this to our account operations team to update the non-user contact records and confirm whether any of those settings can be exposed to your admins going forward.

subject: New teammate invited but gets 'no account found' on first login
description: I added a new user from the admin console this morning. The invite email arrived, but when she clicks the link and tries to sign in, she gets 'no account found for this email.' I can see her under Users as 'Pending Invite.' I need to get her access today.

comments.message: The user is priya.kapoor@northbayops.com. I invited her as a standard member. She clicked the link from both Chrome and Safari and gets the same error. If I resend the invite, it still happens.
Sorry about that. Please ask Priya to clear cookies and try an incognito window. That usually fixes invite issues.
We already tried that. I’m more concerned that the system says 'no account found' even though I can see the pending invite in admin.

Understood. In that case, please cancel the pending invite from User Administration and send a fresh one. We sometimes see the original invitation token fail if the email was prefilled incorrectly or opened after another session. If the replacement invite still fails, reply here with the invite timestamp and I’ll escalate to our access team to inspect the token record.

subject: Seat increase approved by workflow, but account changes didn't apply before invoice
description: We set up an internal automation so any seat increase request gets manager approval and then updates our subscription through your API. A request for 12 additional seats was approved, but only 5 were actually added to the account. Despite that, the invoice billed us for all 12, and several new hires still can’t log in because the account change only partially applied. I need to know whether the workflow submitted duplicate billing events or whether the subscription update failed on your side.

comments.message: The approval workflow shows success and passed quantity=12 through the API step. In the admin portal we only received 5 extra seats, but billing charged for 12 on the same day.

I checked the API logs and found two calls: one request to add 5 seats from a manual admin action, and a later automated request to add 12. Our billing ledger recorded the 12-seat increase immediately, but the provisioning service rejected part of that update because your plan’s departmental cap allowed only 30 seats without a contract amendment.
So why were we billed for seats we never got?

That shouldn’t have happened as a final state. I’ve opened a billing correction for the 7 unprovisioned seats and escalated the provisioning mismatch to engineering. In the meantime, the automation should be changed to read the current seat cap before submitting quantity changes, otherwise approvals can create billable requests that exceed your contractual limit.

subject: Can’t remove secondary email from my account
description: I added a secondary recovery email to test something, and now I can’t remove it. The Remove button is enabled, but clicking it does nothing except briefly show a loading spinner. I’ve refreshed, signed out, and tried mobile and desktop. I need this fixed because it’s an old contractor email that should not stay attached to my account.

comments.message: I added a secondary recovery email to test something, and now I can’t remove it. The Remove button is enabled, but clicking it does nothing except briefly show a loading spinner. I’ve refreshed, signed out, and tried mobile and desktop. I need this fixed because it’s an old contractor email that should not stay attached to my account.
That sounds frustrating. Please open Developer Tools and check whether there’s any error in the console when you click Remove. If there is, send the exact text.
The console shows: "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'isPrimary')" and the network tab shows a 500 from /account/emails/remove.

Thanks, that’s useful. It looks like a backend bug related to accounts with only one verified secondary address. I removed the secondary email manually from your profile on our side, and you should see it disappear after a refresh. I’ve also attached the console error and endpoint failure to an internal bug so engineering can fix the Remove action.

subject: Locked out after too many failed attempts, reset email never arrives
description: I got locked out after entering the wrong password several times, and when I use 'Forgot password' I never receive the reset email. It's not in spam, and our mail admin says nothing from your domain is being rejected. I can't access my account at all right now.

comments.message: I got locked out after entering the wrong password several times, and when I use 'Forgot password' I never receive the reset email. It's not in spam, and our mail admin says nothing from your domain is being rejected. I can't access my account at all right now.

I can see the lockout on your account, but I don't see any successful password-reset email deliveries to your address in the last hour. Before I unlock it, please confirm the exact login email you're entering on the reset form. In many cases users request the reset for an alias while the account is stored under a different address.
You're right, I was using my old address ending in .io. The account is under .com now. I submitted the reset again with the .com address and received it immediately.

Great, that explains both symptoms. I also cleared the lockout so the reset link will work without waiting for the cooldown window. Once you've changed the password, sign in with the .com address only. If you want, your workspace admin can remove the old alias from your profile to avoid this confusion later.

subject: Dropbox import duplicates files after network timeout
