description: We're seeing duplicate documents imported from Dropbox whenever a network timeout happens during a bulk sync. The retry seems to pull the same files again instead of resuming, so our document library ends up with duplicates. Can you help troubleshoot whether deduplication is supposed to happen on retries?

comments.message: This happened twice during large folder imports over 10,000 files. The duplicate files have the same Dropbox path but different internal import IDs in your system.
Retries should not usually duplicate files. Do you know whether the connector is set to deduplicate by file hash or by source path?
I don't see that option anywhere. Under advanced settings I only see 'resume partial sync' and 'skip deleted files.'

Understood — this one is a bit clumsy on our side because the dedupe mode isn't customer-configurable for older Dropbox connectors. If the timeout occurs after file creation but before the checkpoint is committed, the retry can re-import the same source path with a new internal ID. I can't cleanly resolve that from support, so I'm escalating it and attaching your connector version. For now, reduce the batch size if possible and pause bulk imports until engineering confirms whether they can enable path-based dedupe on your tenant.

subject: No sé qué webhook debo usar para Notion
description: Quiero enviar automáticamente los tickets cerrados a una base de datos de Notion, pero no tengo claro si debo usar webhook saliente, Zapier o la API. He leído la documentación y sigo confundido. Necesito una explicación más práctica para alguien no técnico.

comments.message: Nuestra idea es que cada vez que un ticket se cierre se cree una fila en Notion con el asunto, responsable y fecha. Vi que ustedes tienen webhooks, pero no entiendo si eso por sí solo ya manda la información a Notion.

Gracias por explicarlo. Un webhook saliente solo envía los datos a una URL que tú controles; no escribe directamente en Notion. Si no tienes un desarrollador, lo más sencillo suele ser usar Zapier o Make como intermediario: nuestro sistema dispara el evento de ticket cerrado y la automatización crea el registro en Notion. Si tu equipo técnico puede programarlo, entonces sí podrían usar nuestro webhook + la API de Notion.
Entonces, ¿hay una guía paso a paso para hacerlo sin programar?

Sí. La ruta más simple es: 1) crear un disparador de 'ticket cerrado' en nuestra integración con Zapier, 2) conectar tu cuenta de Notion en Zapier, 3) mapear los campos asunto, agente asignado y fecha de cierre, y 4) probar con un ticket de ejemplo. No tenemos una plantilla específica para Notion en todos los planes, pero si me dices tu plan puedo indicarte si esa integración está disponible o si tendrías que usar webhook personalizado.

subject: Passwordless login pilot broke mobile access for field team
description: We started a passwordless login pilot for a subset of users and also changed the mobile app enforcement settings. Since then, the field team cannot access the mobile app, although browser login on desktop still works. Some users are being told their login method is not allowed on this device. I need to know what configuration to adjust without rolling back everything.

comments.message: Desktop browser access is fine, but mobile app users in our passwordless pilot get 'login method not allowed on this device.' We enabled WebAuthn and changed mobile security settings at the same time.

Can you confirm whether the mobile app currently supports the passwordless method you enforced? If 'Require passwordless for pilot group' is enabled but mobile app authentication is limited to password+MFA in your tenant settings, those users can be blocked on mobile while desktop succeeds.
That sounds right. The pilot group is set to require passwordless everywhere. We assumed the mobile app supported passkeys already.

I checked and native mobile passkey enforcement is still limited on some accounts. For now, change the authentication policy for the pilot group to 'Passwordless preferred' instead of required, or exclude mobile clients in the conditional access rule. That should restore field access without ending the desktop pilot. If you want full mobile passwordless, I can hand this to our identity team to confirm rollout status for your tenant.

subject: SCIM-provisioned users created twice
description: We turned on SCIM this morning and several employees were created as duplicate users. One copy is active with the correct role, and the other is pending invite with no team assignments. I need to know how to clean this up safely and why the provisioning created two records in the first place.

comments.message: We turned on SCIM this morning and several employees were created as duplicate users. One copy is active with the correct role, and the other is pending invite with no team assignments. I need to know how to clean this up safely and why the provisioning created two records in the first place.
Duplicate users usually mean the sync found a mismatch between the email address and the external ID. Did you have those people already added manually before SCIM went live?
Yes, most of them were manually added months ago. The duplicates seem to have the same email but different usernames.

That helps. What likely happened is SCIM matched on external identifier, not your legacy manual accounts, so it created new pending records instead of linking them. Please do not delete the active accounts yet. I’m escalating this to our provisioning team to merge or remove the orphaned pending users safely, because deleting the wrong record can break group sync. In the meantime, pause SCIM pushes for the affected group and send us 3 example user emails plus the IdP object IDs.

subject: Unexpected storage overage and missing attachments in exported cases
description: Our bill this month includes a storage overage that is much higher than expected. I started investigating and noticed that when we export closed cases older than 12 months, many attachment references are present but the files themselves are missing from the ZIP. We need to know whether archived attachments are being counted toward storage and whether those files can be retrieved. If this needs another team, please route it quickly.

comments.message: We were charged a large storage overage, but exports of old closed cases are missing many attachments. Are archived files still counted in billing, and can you recover the missing attachments?

Archived attachments do count toward storage until they are purged under your retention settings. Missing files in exports usually happen when the ZIP is too large, so please split the export into smaller date ranges.
We already tried smaller date ranges. The metadata lists the attachments, but the actual files are unavailable in every export. That sounds like a platform issue, not ZIP size.

You're right that the metadata-only behavior suggests the files may be in cold storage or failed archive state. I've escalated this to our storage engineering team to verify whether the attachment objects still exist and whether billing should exclude them while inaccessible. I can't promise a credit yet, but I've documented the disputed overage on the invoice and linked your export examples for investigation.

subject: Can't demote a user because system says they are sole team owner
description: I'm trying to remove admin privileges from a department lead who changed roles, but the app won't let me because it says he's the sole owner of two teams. We already have another org admin, but I can't find which teams are blocking the change. Please help me identify them or escalate this because we need his permissions reduced before end of day.

comments.message: The user is still active; we only want to change him from Team Owner/Admin to a standard member.
If the system says he's the sole team owner, you can usually just deactivate him first and then change the ownership later.
We do not want to deactivate him, and that sounds risky. We need him to remain active but with lower permissions.

You're right — deactivation isn't appropriate here. The permission change is being blocked by team-ownership dependencies, so I've escalated this to our user administration team to identify the teams where he's the last owner and advise the safest transfer path. They should be able to tell you exactly which teams need a new owner before the demotion can be completed.

subject: Need emergency lock on a user account showing suspicious activity
description: One of our supervisors appears to have had their account compromised. We removed their sessions through our SSO provider, but inside your app the user is still active and made changes to user groups after the reset. I need the account locked immediately and the recent admin changes reviewed. If your front-line team can't do that, please escalate urgently.

comments.message: The user is j.holloway@sterlingfield.io. We saw group membership changes around 08:14 UTC and 08:19 UTC.

I can't directly lock accounts from chat, but I can help start the process. As an immediate step, please deactivate the user under Admin > Users if you still have access. I've also marked this as a security-sensitive escalation to our account protection team so they can review the suspicious activity, confirm the lock, and audit the group changes made at 08:14 and 08:19 UTC.
We've deactivated the account now, but we need to know whether those group changes can be rolled back.

Understood. I've updated the escalation to include rollback review for the affected group membership changes and noted that the user has already been deactivated in-app. The account protection team will need to verify the audit trail and determine whether they can revert those admin actions safely.

subject: SSO login loop after enabling enforced MFA
description: I turned on enforced MFA for our workspace yesterday, and since then three of our managers are stuck in a login loop. They authenticate with Okta, get redirected back to your app, and then land on the sign-in page again with no error. I can still log in as an owner, but they cannot access anything. We need to get them back in today.

comments.message: I turned on enforced MFA for our workspace yesterday, and since then three of our managers are stuck in a login loop. They authenticate with Okta, get redirected back to your app, and then land on the sign-in page again with no error. I can still log in as an owner, but they cannot access anything. We need to get them back in today.

Thanks for the details. A login loop after enabling enforced MFA usually means the SAML response is succeeding but the users are failing the post-auth session check. Please ask one affected user to open a private browser window and try again, and also confirm whether their email address in Okta exactly matches the email on their account in our app, including any alias changes.
Private window did not help. I checked one user and found Okta has her as sarah@northpass.co, but in your app she's sarah@northpass.com. The others look correct.

That mismatch would definitely block Sarah. I also checked your tenant logs and saw the other two users are being denied because they were marked 'MFA exempt' under the old policy, which conflicts with the new enforced setting. I've cleared that stale flag on our side. Please update Sarah's email to match exactly in Okta or in our app, then have all three sign in again. If anyone still loops, send me the exact timestamp and browser.

subject: Impossible d’activer l’authentification SAML pour une filiale
description: Nous avons une instance multi-entités et l’activation SAML fonctionne pour la maison mère mais pas pour notre filiale belge. Le bouton d’activation est grisé alors que je suis super admin. Comme le déploiement sécurité est prévu cet après-midi, je voudrais savoir s’il manque un paramètre ou si cela nécessite une intervention interne.

comments.message: Bonjour, sur la filiale “Belux Services”, l’option SAML reste indisponible alors qu’elle est active sur notre entité principale. La documentation ne couvre pas ce cas multi-entités.

Bonjour, merci. J’ai vérifié rapidement et il semble que votre contrat inclue bien le SSO, mais je n’ai pas la visibilité sur l’activation par entité secondaire depuis mon outil. Je vais escalader vers notre équipe comptes/produit pour confirmer s’il s’agit d’une restriction de configuration ou d’un bug.
D’accord. Le déploiement est prévu à 15h, donc si vous pouvez leur indiquer l’urgence ce serait utile.

C’est fait : j’ai marqué l’escalade comme urgente avec votre échéance de 15h et le nom de l’entité concernée. En attendant leur retour, je vous conseille de ne pas modifier les paramètres SSO existants sur la maison mère afin d’éviter tout impact sur les utilisateurs déjà connectés.

subject: User keeps getting re-invited instead of regaining access
description: One of our designers already had an account, got locked out after too many failed attempts, and my teammate kept sending her new invites instead of unlocking the account. Now she says she has multiple emails from us and still can't get in. I need to understand the right difference between resending an invite, unlocking an account, and changing a user's role, because I think we're mixing up all three. Can you help us sort this out?

comments.message: One of our designers already had an account, got locked out after too many failed attempts, and my teammate kept sending her new invites instead of unlocking the account. Now she says she has multiple emails from us and still can't get in. I need to understand the right difference between resending an invite, unlocking an account, and changing a user's role, because I think we're mixing up all three. Can you help us sort this out?

Sure. Resend Invite is only for users who have never completed account activation. Unlock Account or Reset Password is for existing users who already activated but can't sign in. Changing a role affects permissions after login; it does not restore access by itself. For your designer, open her existing user record and use Unlock or Send Password Reset rather than inviting again.
I found her profile, but I only see Reset Password and Suspend Sessions. No Unlock option.

In many workspaces, a lockout clears automatically after the cooldown period, so Reset Password is the right action if Unlock isn't shown. Have her use the latest reset email, complete the password change, and then sign in from that original account instead of accepting any fresh invite links. After she's back in, you can ignore the duplicate invite emails; if there are any pending duplicate user records, remove those so her access and role assignment stay tied to the existing account.

subject: Invited user cannot accept invite, API webhook setup blocked
description: I'm trying to onboard a contractor who needs to log in and configure a webhook consumer for our integration. The invite link lets her set a password, but after that she lands on an error page and can't access the workspace. If I use my admin account to pre-create the webhook, the API tester says her user has no associated account. We need both the login issue and the integration side checked.

comments.message: Invite sent to contractor@vendor.io. She completed the password setup, then got 'unexpected error' and now any login attempt says no workspace membership. In our admin panel, I see her user listed as pending. The webhook test payload also references her user ID but says account missing.
That sounds like the invite was never accepted. Please delete the pending user and resend the invite.
We did that twice. Same exact result each time, and the webhook tester still sees her orphaned user ID.

Thanks, that rules out a simple bad invite. I pulled the provisioning logs and found a bug where invited users created through the vendor.io domain policy are getting authentication records without corresponding workspace membership rows. That explains why password setup completes, login fails, and API-related user lookups break. I've escalated this to the access team and attached the orphaned user IDs for cleanup.

subject: Restored user records lost team assignments
description: We restored five deactivated user profiles from the recycle bin after an HR sync mistake. Their names and email addresses came back, but their team assignments and territory mappings are blank. Before I manually rebuild everything, I need to know if restore is supposed to bring related assignment data back or if that linkage was dropped incorrectly.

comments.message: We restored five deactivated user profiles from the recycle bin after an HR sync mistake. Their names and email addresses came back, but their team assignments and territory mappings are blank. Before I manually rebuild everything, I need to know if restore is supposed to bring related assignment data back or if that linkage was dropped incorrectly.
Restoring users should generally bring back related configuration unless it was deleted separately. Can you provide one affected user ID and roughly when the restore was done?
One example is U-8821. All five were restored around 9:15 AM Eastern today from the recycle bin by our admin.

I checked user U-8821 and can see the profile record was restored, but I’m not seeing any team assignment rows recreated. I suspect the restore job only rebuilt the core user table and skipped the related mapping tables. I don’t have tooling to repopulate those links from support, so I’m escalating this to our data integrity team. In the meantime, you can manually reassign teams, but if the original mappings are needed we’ll have to see whether engineering can recover them from backups.

subject: Need help understanding permission sets before rollout
description: I'm setting up our workspace for a department-wide rollout next week, and I'm confused about how custom permission sets interact with default roles. I read the help article, but I'm still not clear on whether restricting export access at the permission-set level overrides the Manager role. We have about 80 users going live, so I want to make sure we train them correctly and avoid changing permissions one by one later.

comments.message: I'm trying to document this for our internal admins, but the examples in your knowledge base don't match what I see in our account. Can you explain the safest way to set this up before launch?

Absolutely. In general, custom permission sets add or remove capabilities independently from the default role, but certain role-based permissions can still be inherited. For export access specifically, the cleanest approach is to create a test user, assign the Manager role, then apply the permission set you intend to use and verify the effective permissions in Settings > Users > Preview Access. That preview shows the final permission outcome the user will actually have.
We don't seem to have a Preview Access option in Settings > Users. Is that only on certain plans?

Thanks for flagging that. You're right — Preview Access is only available on Enterprise plans, and I should have checked that first. Since I can't confirm from the ticket which plan you're on, I'm escalating this to our onboarding team so they can review your workspace configuration and give you the exact rollout guidance for your plan, including a recommended admin training checklist. In the meantime, the safest general practice is to test with a single pilot user before assigning roles in bulk.

subject: Service account suddenly denied login from API console
description: Our automation service account has been able to sign in to the API console for months, but since early this morning it gets “interactive login disabled.” We didn’t change its permissions. This account is tied to nightly exports, and the next run is in a few hours.

comments.message: The username and password are correct, and the account still appears active in our user list. Human users can log in fine, just not this service account.

Service accounts are not meant to use interactive login in all environments, and there was a recent policy rollout that may have enforced that flag on older tenants. I can see why this is blocking your export workflow.
So is there a fix, or did your platform just break our setup?

I’ve raised an escalation to our backend access team to confirm whether your tenant was incorrectly included in that enforcement batch. If they can’t restore interactive access, they’ll advise the supported alternative for your export job. I’ve marked the case as time-sensitive because of your scheduled run.

subject: Report subscription fires before automation finishes overnight updates
description: Every morning our executives get a report on overdue invoices, but since we added an overnight workflow that recalculates invoice status and owner assignment, the emailed report is inconsistent with what we see in the app an hour later. It looks like the report subscription is running before the automation batch completes. Can you verify timing and advise whether this should be escalated or reconfigured?

comments.message: The 6:00 AM overdue invoices report is outdated by the time people read it. Our workflow starts at 5:45 AM and updates a few thousand invoice records, including overdue status and account owner. I suspect the report snapshot is happening too early.
If the workflow starts before the report, the report should include the updates. Scheduled reports always pull live data at send time, so there shouldn't be a timing issue.
But the workflow run history shows it doesn't finish until around 6:20 AM, and the emailed totals match the pre-update counts. This seems directly tied to automation timing.

You're right, I overlooked the batch duration. The report subscription is likely querying while the workflow is still processing. I can't change execution order from support, but I have escalated this to the scheduling team and noted that your report send time likely needs to move after the automation window. As a short-term fix, please reschedule the report to 6:30 AM or later if your admin settings allow it.

subject: Importação via API criou caracteres quebrados e colunas desalinhadas
description: Fizemos uma importação para popular registros a partir de um CSV processado no nosso middleware e enviado pela API. Depois da carga, vários nomes com acento ficaram corrompidos e alguns valores foram parar em campos errados, principalmente quando havia quebra de linha em observações. Agora precisamos corrigir os dados já gravados e entender qual serialização a API espera para não repetir o problema.

comments.message: O CSV original está em UTF-8 e no nosso lado parece normal. O problema aparece só depois da criação dos registros na plataforma.

Olá! Se a importação foi feita pela API, o ideal é não enviar CSV bruto, mas objetos JSON já parseados e validados. Caracteres corrompidos normalmente indicam problema de encoding no middleware, e colunas desalinhadas podem acontecer se as quebras de linha não forem escapadas corretamente antes da transformação.
Sim, transformamos o CSV em JSON antes de enviar. Existe alguma forma de reverter em massa os registros criados ontem à tarde?

Você pode excluir os registros afetados e reenviar a importação depois de corrigir o parser. Para identificar os itens, filtre por created_at na janela de ontem e exporte os campos impactados. Se não for viável excluir, faça um update em massa com os valores corretos após regenerar o JSON usando UTF-8 normalizado e aspas escapadas nas observações.

subject: Google Ads cost import delayed and pacing report shows zero spend
description: Our Google Ads integration didn't populate today's spend, so the pacing report is showing zero for several active campaigns. The API connection still says healthy and clicks are arriving, but cost metrics aren't. Because this feeds our daily budget decisions, I need someone to investigate whether the integration is delayed or if reporting is filtering out partial data.

comments.message: Clicks and impressions from Google Ads are in the platform this morning, but spend is zero across the pacing report. The integration status page says healthy. This is affecting our paid media standup right now.
Sometimes cost data arrives later than traffic metrics from ad platforms, especially early in the day. Please wait a few hours and refresh the report.
It's already 2 PM Eastern and yesterday this data was available by 9 AM. Also the raw imported table for ad costs is empty for today.

Thanks, that's useful context. Since the traffic side of the integration is current but cost records are absent and the reporting layer is just reflecting that gap, this needs a closer look from our integrations team. I'm escalating it with urgency and noting the reporting impact on your pacing dashboard. If they confirm an upstream API delay, we'll let you know; otherwise they'll check whether today's cost import job stalled.

subject: Export job stuck in queued state after yesterday's release
description: I'm trying to export a customer activity report from our workspace, but every export I start just sits in "Queued" forever and never completes. This started right after yesterday's product update. I tested in Chrome and Edge and with two different admin accounts, so it doesn't look browser-specific. We need the report for an audit this afternoon. Can someone check whether this is a known issue and help move this along?

comments.message: Every export has been stuck in queued status for over an hour since yesterday afternoon. We have three pending exports now and none of them produced a file.

Thanks for the details. I checked our status page and don't see a platform-wide outage, but the timing after the release is concerning. Could you share one affected export ID and the workspace URL so I can investigate the job logs?
Workspace is acme-ops.app.example.com and one of the export IDs is exp_784231. There are two more with the same behavior from this morning.

I reviewed the job trace for exp_784231 and it appears the worker is failing after the request is accepted, which points to a backend bug rather than a permission or browser issue. I’ve escalated this to our engineering on-call team with your export IDs and release timing, and marked it as audit-blocking. While they investigate, I triggered one manual rerun from our side; if that succeeds you should receive the file by email. I’ll update this ticket as soon as engineering confirms root cause or workaround.

subject: Task automation creates wrong due dates after changing business week
description: We recently changed our workspace settings so the business week is Monday through Friday instead of Sunday through Thursday. Since then, a workflow that creates follow-up tasks "2 business days after case opened" is generating incorrect due dates for some teams. I need to understand whether the automation uses the global calendar, the team calendar, or cached settings from before the configuration change.

comments.message: A case opened on Thursday should now create a task due Monday, but some are getting due Sunday. I updated the workspace business hours three days ago.
Workflow timers always use UTC, so due Sunday is expected unless you manually offset for weekends.
That doesn't seem right. The step is explicitly configured as business days, not hours, and this only started after we changed the workspace week settings.

You're right, I mixed up business-hour timers with calendar days. For "2 business days after case opened," the workflow should reference the business calendar attached in Product Configuration. If some teams still get Sunday, they may be tied to older team-specific calendars. Please check whether those teams have custom support schedules overriding the global week. If they do, update or remove those overrides and republish the workflow so new executions use the correct calendar.

subject: La integración con Mailchimp no deja mapear el consentimiento de marketing
description: Estoy configurando Mailchimp para sincronizar contactos desde su plataforma y necesitamos enviar también el estado de consentimiento de marketing por razones legales. La integración conecta bien y sincroniza nombre y correo, pero el campo de consentimiento no aparece como opción para mapear. Revisé la audiencia en Mailchimp y también nuestros campos, pero no sé si ese atributo requiere alguna configuración especial en el producto.

comments.message: Necesitamos que cuando un contacto marque aceptación en nuestro formulario, ese consentimiento viaje a Mailchimp. Ahora mismo solo suben email y nombre.
Para que un campo aparezca en el mapeo, primero debe existir como campo personalizado en ambos sistemas. Cree un campo booleano llamado consentimiento y luego sincronice de nuevo el esquema.
Ya existe en ambos lados. En Mailchimp es parte del marketing permissions, no un campo personalizado normal.

Gracias, eso cambia el caso. Las marketing permissions en Mailchimp no se exponen como campos estándar en nuestro mapeador, así que no se pueden configurar desde la interfaz general. Voy a escalarlo al equipo de integraciones para confirmar si hay soporte mediante una opción avanzada o si requiere API personalizada. Mientras tanto, solo podría mantenerse manualmente o con un flujo externo.

subject: Campos personalizados sumiram da exportação
description: Eu exportei a base de empresas em XLSX para repassar ao financeiro, mas vários campos personalizados que usamos para classificação interna não vieram no arquivo. Na tela de empresas eles aparecem normalmente. Já testei duas exportações diferentes e o resultado continua incompleto.

comments.message: Eu exportei a base de empresas em XLSX para repassar ao financeiro, mas vários campos personalizados que usamos para classificação interna não vieram no arquivo. Na tela de empresas eles aparecem normalmente. Já testei duas exportações diferentes e o resultado continua incompleto.
Isso normalmente acontece quando a exportação padrão inclui apenas as colunas visíveis na visualização salva. Você pode editar a visualização, adicionar os campos personalizados e exportar novamente.
Eu já deixei as colunas visíveis antes de exportar. Inclusive um dos campos é de múltipla seleção e outro é calculado. Nenhum dos dois veio.
Nesse caso, provavelmente esses tipos de campo não são suportados em exportação XLSX. Sugiro criar campos de texto equivalentes e copiar os valores manualmente antes de exportar.

subject: Charged for premium support routing we never finished setting up
description: I started configuring premium support routing for our VIP customers, but we abandoned the setup before going live. Even so, the invoice now includes the premium routing add-on. I need to know whether simply saving the configuration draft made it billable and if the charge can be removed since the feature was never used.

comments.message: I created a VIP routing policy in admin, assigned two queues, and then stopped because we hadn't finalized our process. The policy has been in draft the whole time and no tickets have used it. Why were we billed for Premium Routing on this month's invoice?

Premium Routing becomes billable when the add-on is activated in subscription settings, not when tickets actually flow through it. I checked your audit log and the setup wizard auto-activated the add-on when the draft policy was saved for the first time. That's why billing started this month even though the policy was never published.
That's pretty misleading. Can you turn it off and get the charge removed?

I've deactivated the add-on so it won't renew next cycle. Because the feature was activated by the configuration wizard but remained unused, I've asked billing for a one-time courtesy credit. I can't guarantee approval, but I included the zero-usage logs to support the request.

subject: How do I hand off admin access during parental leave?
description: I'm the only full admin on our account and will be out on parental leave next month. I need to give my colleague enough access to manage users and settings temporarily, but I don't want to overexpose billing if I can avoid it. Can you explain which role to assign and how to reverse it when I return?

comments.message: I'm the only full admin on our account and will be out on parental leave next month. I need to give my colleague enough access to manage users and settings temporarily, but I don't want to overexpose billing if I can avoid it. Can you explain which role to assign and how to reverse it when I return?

Yes — first check whether your plan supports granular admin roles. If it does, assign your colleague a user-management or workspace-admin role without billing access, then test with them in a private browser window to confirm they can perform the needed tasks. When you return, you can edit their role back from the Users or Permissions page. If your plan only offers a single admin role, you may have to choose between full admin access or keeping them as a standard user.
I'm not sure which permission model we're on. Is there an easy way to tell?

If you open Settings and see a separate Permissions or Roles section with more than just Admin/Member, you're on the granular model. If you only see simple role choices, you're likely on the legacy model. Since permission layouts vary by plan, I'm escalating this to our account team so they can confirm exactly what your workspace supports before you make the handoff.

subject: Parent account changed, child workspaces still invoicing separately
description: We recently moved three regional workspaces under a single parent account and updated our automations so new subsidiaries should inherit the parent billing settings and owner by default. That inheritance is not happening consistently: APAC got linked correctly, but EMEA still generated a separate invoice and its new-workspace automation assigned the old regional owner instead of the parent finance admin. I need to consolidate billing and understand why the account-change automation only worked for one region.

comments.message: The goal was one invoice under the parent account, but EMEA still has its own invoice and renewal reminders. The workflow run history says ‘completed,’ so I don’t know where the inheritance failed.

I reviewed the setup and the APAC workspace was created after the parent-billing automation was published, while EMEA predates it. Existing child workspaces do not retroactively inherit billing settings from account changes unless they’re manually re-parented in backend tools.
What about the owner assignment? The EMEA workspace owner flipped back to the former regional admin after we changed it.

That happened because your workflow has two branches: one sets the parent finance admin for newly created subsidiaries, and another preserves the existing owner for legacy regions. EMEA matched the legacy branch. I’ve escalated the billing consolidation request because manual re-parenting requires our internal team, and I recommend removing that legacy owner-preservation branch if you want all future regional updates to align to the parent account.

subject: API token creator can no longer log in
description: Our integration broke because the user who created the API token can no longer log in to your platform. He gets an 'account suspended' message even though HR says he is still active, and now we can’t rotate the token or inspect the integration settings. Please escalate this because it’s blocking production data syncs.

comments.message: The user’s password was reset yesterday after suspicious activity, and today the login says the account is suspended pending review. We need access to the account or another way to manage the integration immediately.

An account suspension message means our trust system placed a hold on the login. I can’t remove that from support, and for security reasons I also can’t expose or transfer the existing API token. I’m escalating this to our trust and safety team to review the suspension and to our integrations team to advise whether the token can be re-owned or replaced safely.
Is there any temporary workaround? The sync failure is backing up orders.

If another admin can still access the integration settings, they may be able to create a new token and update the downstream system, but that depends on your role configuration. I’ve added the production impact to the escalation so the teams can prioritize the review. Until they respond, I can’t restore the login from this channel.

subject: Can’t transfer dashboard ownership from deactivated admin
description: One of our admins left the company and I deactivated his account before realizing he owned several executive dashboards. Now I can view the dashboards, but the ownership transfer option is greyed out. I need to move them to our COO without reactivating his account if possible.

comments.message: One of our admins left the company and I deactivated his account before realizing he owned several executive dashboards. Now I can view the dashboards, but the ownership transfer option is greyed out. I need to move them to our COO without reactivating his account if possible.
Dashboard ownership can only be changed by the current owner, so you will need to reactivate the former admin, log in as them, and transfer each dashboard manually.
We cannot do that for compliance reasons. Is there really no admin-side ownership transfer for deactivated users?

Not through the dashboard page. I may have oversimplified: some plans do include an admin reassignment tool under Content Administration, but I’m not certain whether it supports deactivated owners. I’m escalating this to our account administration team so they can confirm the correct path.

subject: Can you clarify why scheduled exports have a one-hour delay?
description: I'm documenting our reporting process for finance and need to explain why scheduled exports never arrive exactly on the hour. Stakeholders think something is broken, but I suspect this is expected platform behavior. Can you explain in simple terms how the export schedule window works and whether there is any way to guarantee delivery time for month-end reporting?

comments.message: I'm documenting our reporting process for finance and need to explain why scheduled exports never arrive exactly on the hour. Stakeholders think something is broken, but I suspect this is expected platform behavior. Can you explain in simple terms how the export schedule window works and whether there is any way to guarantee delivery time for month-end reporting?

Scheduled exports are queued within a delivery window rather than executed at an exact timestamp, because the system balances load across tenants. So a report set for 9:00 may be generated and delivered sometime within the following processing window instead of precisely at 9:00. For internal documentation, it's best to describe the schedule as approximate rather than minute-exact.
That makes sense. For month-end, finance wants a guarantee before 9:15 AM. Is there a setting for priority delivery?

We don't offer a guaranteed priority delivery setting for standard scheduled exports. For deadline-sensitive reporting, the usual recommendation is to trigger the export earlier, use the reporting API ahead of the cutoff, or build a buffer into the process. I'm escalating your question to our reporting specialists in case your plan includes any enhanced delivery options, but I don't want to promise exact-time delivery.

subject: Falsche Queue-Zuordnung nach neuer Routing-Regel
description: Ich habe gestern eine neue Routing-Regel konfiguriert, damit Tickets aus Österreich direkt beim DACH-Team landen. Stattdessen werden sie zufällig zwischen DACH und Global Support verteilt. Der Fehler betrifft nur deutschsprachige Formulare mit dem Feld "Land = AT". Das muss bitte schnell geprüft werden; wenn nötig eskalieren Sie es an das Routing-Team.

comments.message: Ich habe die Regelreihenfolge schon angepasst. Die Österreich-Regel steht ganz oben und prüft auf Land gleich AT und Sprache gleich de.

Danke, ich habe den Ablauf nachgestellt. In Ihrer Konfiguration greift vor der Queue-Zuordnung noch eine ältere Catch-all-Regel mit "Wenn Sprache = de, dann Round Robin DACH/Global". Diese wird durch den Modus "Weiter verarbeiten" nicht beendet. Wenn Sie möchten, ändern Sie die Österreich-Regel auf "Verarbeitung stoppen", dann sollten AT-Tickets exklusiv ins DACH-Team gehen.
Das erklärt einiges. Allerdings kann ich den Modus nicht ändern, das Feld ist bei uns ausgegraut.

Dann ist die Regel wahrscheinlich Teil Ihres gesperrten Managed-Setups. Ich habe deshalb eine Eskalation an unser Routing-Team eröffnet, damit sie die Stop-Bedingung serverseitig für Ihre Instanz anpassen oder das Managed-Paket aktualisieren. Bis dahin wäre ein möglicher Workaround, die Catch-all-Regel temporär enger zu fassen, falls das in Ihrer Umgebung erlaubt ist.

subject: Incremental sync loaded new records but report trend line is flat
description: Our nightly incremental sync brought in about 9,000 new usage events, and I can see them in the event explorer with the correct timestamps. However, the weekly adoption trend report is completely flat after last Thursday, as if no new data arrived. I'm guessing the reporting dataset may be partitioned on a different event date field than the one the sync populated. Can someone verify what field the trend report reads from and why the imported records aren't rolling up?

comments.message: New usage events are visible in the explorer, but the weekly adoption trend line hasn't moved for days. Is the report reading a different date field?

Possibly. I inspected the sync sample you sent and the new records populate Event Timestamp, but the adoption report is currently partitioned and filtered on First Seen Date, which is null for those imported rows.
That would explain it. Can we switch the report to Event Timestamp without breaking historical data?

For this report, yes. I updated the trend dataset to use Event Timestamp for new aggregation and validated that historical weeks still line up within expected variance. The chart should repopulate after the next refresh cycle in about 10 minutes.

subject: Les chiffres du tableau de bord se réinitialisent chaque matin
description: Chaque matin vers 8h, notre tableau de bord de reporting repart à zéro puis se remplit progressivement, ce qui fait paniquer la direction. Nous voulons un cumul sur la journée entière dès l'ouverture, pas un compteur qui semble perdre les données. J'ai besoin de savoir si c'est normal et comment l'éviter.

comments.message: Bonjour, nos indicateurs du tableau de bord repartent à zéro tous les matins avant de remonter. Pour la direction, ça ressemble à une perte de données.
Bonjour ! C'est normal, les tableaux de bord se recalculent une fois par jour. Il n'y a rien à faire de votre côté.
Le souci est que cela dure parfois jusqu'à 9h30, et sur certaines tuiles seulement. Avant ce n'était pas le cas.
Je comprends, mais tant que les chiffres reviennent ensuite, cela ne nécessite pas d'action. Vous pouvez simplement consulter les rapports plus tard dans la matinée.

subject: Incohérence de quotas entre contrat et interface
description: Notre contrat signé mentionne 250 licences, mais l’interface admin nous bloque à 200 et refuse toute nouvelle attribution. J’ai déjà comparé avec l’avenant de commande, donc je pense qu’il faut une vérification côté compte ou provisioning. Comme notre déploiement continue cette semaine, merci de faire le nécessaire ou d’escalader au bon service.

comments.message: Bonjour, nous avons encore 37 personnes à activer mais la plateforme affiche “quota atteint” à 200 sièges. Notre bon de commande amendé porte pourtant le total à 250.

Bonjour, merci. Je peux voir la limite appliquée à 200 dans votre tenant, mais je ne peux pas corriger moi-même une divergence entre contrat et provisioning. Je vais escalader vers notre équipe account operations avec votre avenant et demander une vérification prioritaire de la capacité.
Parfait. Avez-vous besoin d’une copie du contrat ou le PDF déjà joint au ticket suffit ?

Le PDF joint suffit pour lancer l’analyse, et je l’ai déjà référencé dans l’escalade. Si l’équipe account operations a besoin d’un document complémentaire, elle reviendra sur ce ticket ; de mon côté, le dossier est bien transmis avec priorité élevée.

subject: Need super admin restored after accidental role changes
description: I’m the only person who should be managing users for our company, but after cleaning up accounts yesterday I somehow removed my own super admin access. I can still log in, but I can’t add users, reset permissions, or see the provisioning settings. We have onboarding tomorrow morning, so this needs attention quickly.

comments.message: I accidentally changed my own role yesterday and now I can’t manage users anymore. I’m still able to sign in, but all the user administration options are gone.

I’m sorry you’re blocked. Since role restoration for the last super admin can’t be completed from the standard support queue, I’m escalating this to our account access team now. To proceed, I need the workspace URL, your login email, and the name of one additional billing or legal contact who can confirm authorization if needed.

The workspace is northbridge.opscloud.com, my login is jmarin@northbridge.com, and our finance director is Ellen Cho. There are other admins listed in the directory, but I think they only have team admin, not super admin.

Thanks, I’ve attached those details and opened a priority escalation with our account access team for super admin recovery. Because this affects user administration and there may be no active super admin left, they’ll verify ownership before changing roles. I can’t restore the permission directly from here, but the escalation is in progress and they typically respond within a few business hours.

subject: Salesforce sync missing opportunities in quarterly pipeline report
description: I'm pulling our Q2 pipeline report in your platform, and the totals are way off compared to Salesforce. Several opportunities that were created through the Salesforce integration last month are visible on the deal list, but they never show up in the report builder once I filter by owner and close date. I already forced a sync twice. We need this for tomorrow's board packet, so if this isn't something I can fix on my end please escalate it quickly.

comments.message: I'm pulling our Q2 pipeline report in your platform, and the totals are way off compared to Salesforce. Several opportunities that were created through the Salesforce integration last month are visible on the deal list, but they never show up in the report builder once I filter by owner and close date. I already forced a sync twice. We need this for tomorrow's board packet, so if this isn't something I can fix on my end please escalate it quickly.

Thanks for the detail. I checked your account and can see the Salesforce sync completed, but the affected opportunities are coming in with a blank mapped value for the custom field you're using in the report filter. That would exclude them from the report even though they exist in the deal list. As a workaround, remove the owner filter or remap the Salesforce field 'Opportunity Owner Region' to a standard text field, then rerun the report.

We don't filter on Opportunity Owner Region at all. The report is filtered on owner, close date, and stage. Also, the issue only affects records created through the integration API user. Can you please have someone investigate the sync logic?

You're right, and I dug deeper after your note. The records created by the Salesforce integration user are being tagged with the internal source 'api_import', and we have an open defect where those records can be excluded from aggregated pipeline reports when grouped by owner. I've escalated this to our integrations engineering team and attached examples from your workspace. For the board packet today, the safest workaround is an exported deals list filtered by close date and stage rather than the summary report. I'll update you as soon as engineering responds.

subject: Confused about archived projects versus deleted projects
description: I'm putting together an internal FAQ for our project leads, and people keep asking whether archiving a project removes reports, notifications, and API access the same way deleting does. The help center explanations are too short, and I don't want to give the wrong guidance. Could someone break down the practical difference in a way I can reuse with our users?

comments.message: I'm putting together an internal FAQ for our project leads, and people keep asking whether archiving a project removes reports, notifications, and API access the same way deleting does. The help center explanations are too short, and I don't want to give the wrong guidance. Could someone break down the practical difference in a way I can reuse with our users?

Yes — archiving is meant to preserve historical data while taking the project out of active workflows, whereas deleting is intended to permanently remove the project and its associated records after any retention window. In practical terms, archived projects usually remain available in reporting and audit history, but they stop appearing in normal active lists and may stop sending routine notifications. Deleted projects are removed more broadly and shouldn't be relied on for reporting continuity.
That's the kind of explanation I needed. One more thing: will archived projects still be accessible through the API for read-only reporting?

In most accounts, yes, archived items remain queryable for reporting-oriented API calls if the endpoint supports archived filters, but write actions are typically restricted. Since some endpoints handle archived records differently, I've escalated a quick confirmation request to our platform team and they'll follow up if your tenant has any exceptions. For your FAQ, it's safe to say archive preserves history; delete is for removal.

subject: SSO migration seems to have broken our API connector and user permissions on imported records
description: We migrated from email/password to SSO over the weekend. Users can authenticate now, but a few teams can only view records they previously edited, not the new ones coming in from our internal API connector. That connector also stopped updating existing accounts and is inserting duplicates with blank territory fields. We use those imports for daily handoffs, so this is becoming urgent.

comments.message: The timing lines up exactly with the SSO migration. New records created through our API are missing territory, duplicates are appearing instead of updates, and sales users say they can log in but can't see many of the new imported accounts.

The visibility issue suggests your role rules for imported records may depend on territory or owner, so blank territory could make the records effectively disappear for some teams. If the API connector lost permission to the match key after the auth changes, that would also explain the duplicates. Did you move the connector from a legacy admin token to a token from an SSO-managed user?
Yes, we retired the legacy admin token and used a token from one of our platform admins. I assumed the permissions were equivalent.

They're not always equivalent, especially if field-level permissions differ by role. Generate the API token from a dedicated service account with write access to territory and read access to your account external ID, then retry in a sandbox first. For user visibility, once territory is populated again your sharing rules should behave normally, but you'll need to fix the already imported blank records separately.

subject: Charged for add-on that fails to activate
description: We purchased the advanced audit log add-on yesterday, and the invoice posted immediately. But the feature toggle never turned on in our workspace, and the settings page throws an error saying the add-on is unavailable for our plan. Sales told us it was compatible. I need to know whether we should be refunded or whether there is a provisioning bug preventing activation after billing.

comments.message: We purchased the advanced audit log add-on yesterday, and the invoice posted immediately. But the feature toggle never turned on in our workspace, and the settings page throws an error saying the add-on is unavailable for our plan. Sales told us it was compatible. I need to know whether we should be refunded or whether there is a provisioning bug preventing activation after billing.
I checked your account and the add-on charge is valid because the purchase completed successfully. The settings error probably means the feature has not propagated yet. Please wait a few hours.
It has been almost a full day, and we still cannot use the add-on we were billed for.
In that case, you may want to ask sales whether the add-on was sold in error. Support cannot refund add-ons that have already invoiced.

subject: Members can log in but shared dashboard won't load
description: Our team can authenticate successfully, but after login the shared operations dashboard spins forever and then shows a blank page. Personal dashboards work for some people, and the issue affects both Chrome and Firefox. It started right after we renamed one of our custom fields this morning.

comments.message: Our team can authenticate successfully, but after login the shared operations dashboard spins forever and then shows a blank page. Personal dashboards work for some people, and the issue affects both Chrome and Firefox. It started right after we renamed one of our custom fields this morning.

Thanks — that helps narrow it down. Since authentication succeeds, this is likely a post-login application error rather than an access denial. Shared dashboards can fail to render if a saved widget references a field key that changed. Could you tell me which custom field was renamed and whether the dashboard has filters based on it?
Yes, we renamed the field from 'RegionCode' to 'Region'. The shared dashboard has at least two filters and one chart using that field.

That would do it. The label change shouldn't normally break the dashboard, but in your case the saved dashboard appears to be pointing to the old field identifier. I restored the original internal key while keeping the new display name, and the dashboard is loading again in my test. Please refresh and confirm. Longer term, we're logging this as a bug because renaming should not leave stale widget references.

subject: Rapport programmé reçu avec 24 heures de retard
description: Nous avons une programmation d'envoi automatique du rapport "Volumes par file" tous les jours à 6h00. Depuis trois jours, l'e-mail arrive le lendemain, avec les données de la veille. Les destinataires pensent que l'automatisation est cassée. Pouvez-vous vérifier ce qui se passe ?

comments.message: Bonjour, notre rapport programmé censé partir à 6h arrive seulement le lendemain matin. Cela se produit depuis trois jours.

Bonjour, merci. Pour les envois planifiés, le plus fréquent est un décalage de fuseau horaire entre le profil utilisateur et le compte. Pouvez-vous me confirmer le fuseau du compte et si la programmation a été créée par vous ou par un collègue ?
Fuseau du compte: Europe/Paris. La programmation a été créée par un collègue basé à Montréal, mais cela fonctionnait correctement jusqu'à cette semaine.

Je pense avoir identifié la cause: après notre changement d'heure côté Europe, les programmations héritées du fuseau du créateur peuvent conserver l'ancien offset jusqu'à leur prochaine édition. J'ai donc deux recommandations: ouvrez la planification, re-sélectionnez 6h00 puis enregistrez, ou recréez-la depuis un profil en Europe/Paris. Si le retard persiste après cela, revenez vers nous avec l'ID de la planification et nous l'escaladerons.

subject: Kündigung eingereicht, trotzdem erneut berechnet
description: Ich habe unser Abonnement vor Ablauf der Testphase gekündigt und auch eine Bestätigungs-E-Mail erhalten. Trotzdem wurde heute der erste Monatsbetrag von unserer Karte abgebucht. Bitte erklären Sie mir, warum berechnet wurde, und veranlassen Sie die Rückerstattung.

comments.message: Ich habe unser Abonnement vor Ablauf der Testphase gekündigt und auch eine Bestätigungs-E-Mail erhalten. Trotzdem wurde heute der erste Monatsbetrag von unserer Karte abgebucht. Bitte erklären Sie mir, warum berechnet wurde, und veranlassen Sie die Rückerstattung.

Wenn eine Kündigung bestätigt wurde, sollte keine Berechnung mehr erfolgen. Bitte teilen Sie mir das Kündigungsdatum und die letzten vier Ziffern der verwendeten Karte mit, damit ich die Transaktion eindeutig prüfen kann.
Die Kündigung wurde am 3. Mai bestätigt. Die letzten vier Ziffern sind 4821.

Danke. Ich konnte den Vorgang finden: Die Kündigung war zwar erfasst, aber Ihr Workspace wurde versehentlich nicht rechtzeitig auf den Teststatus zurückgesetzt. Ich habe soeben eine vollständige Rückerstattung veranlasst. Sie erhalten die Gutschrift je nach Bank innerhalb von 5 bis 10 Werktagen. Zusätzlich habe ich das Abonnement endgültig beendet, damit keine weiteren Gebühren entstehen.

subject: Nie mogę wejść po zmianie nazwiska i adresu w profilu
description: Zmieniłam nazwisko i poprosiłam o aktualizację adresu e-mail na koncie. Od tego czasu system odrzuca moje dane logowania. Nie wiem, czy konto zostało zmienione poprawnie, czy utworzyło się nowe.

comments.message: Próbuję wejść zarówno przez zwykłe logowanie, jak i przez Google. W obu przypadkach kończy się to komunikatem o braku uprawnień.
Najprawdopodobniej po zmianie danych utworzył się duplikat użytkownika. Proszę wyczyścić cookies i zalogować się starym adresem e-mail.
Ale stary adres został już wyłączony przez naszą firmę, więc nie mam do niego dostępu. Dlatego właśnie zgłaszam problem.
W takim razie proszę poprosić administratora IT o przywrócenie starej skrzynki na chwilę. Z naszej strony nie możemy nic więcej zrobić.

subject: Bulk import endpoint times out for files over 50 MB
description: I am trying to use the bulk import API for historical tickets. Smaller files upload fine, but anything around 60-80 MB hangs and eventually times out from our client after about two minutes. I need help troubleshooting whether there is a file size limit or if we should be using a different upload pattern.

comments.message: We send a multipart upload directly to `/v1/imports/bulk`. A 12 MB CSV works, but a 73 MB file times out consistently. We are calling from a job runner in AWS with a 120-second client timeout.
There isn't really a limit on that endpoint, so the timeout is probably local to your network. You can just increase your timeout and retry.
We increased it to 300 seconds and it still stalls. Is the endpoint intended for synchronous uploads?

I checked the current docs and I gave you an incomplete answer. Direct multipart uploads to `/v1/imports/bulk` are capped at 50 MB. Larger imports need the staged-upload flow: first request an upload URL, send the file to object storage, then submit the import job referencing that upload token. I don't have visibility into your previous failed uploads, but switching to the staged flow should avoid the timeout.

subject: Impossible d'ajouter un filtre dans les rapports personnalisés
description: Je veux créer un rapport personnalisé sur les tickets fermés par canal, mais l'option pour filtrer sur "Canal d'origine" est grisée dans l'éditeur. Je suis administratrice et ce problème existe sur deux navigateurs. Est-ce une limitation du produit ou un bug ?

comments.message: Bonjour, dans les rapports personnalisés je ne peux pas sélectionner le champ "Canal d'origine" comme filtre. L'option est grisée alors que je suis admin.
Bonjour, ce champ n'est probablement pas disponible dans votre forfait. Seuls les clients Enterprise peuvent filtrer sur le canal dans les rapports personnalisés.
Nous sommes justement sur Enterprise Plus depuis janvier, et je l'utilisais encore le mois dernier.
Dans ce cas je vous conseille de vider le cache du navigateur et de réessayer en navigation privée. Si ça ne fonctionne pas, il faudra attendre une prochaine mise à jour.

subject: Bulk CSV invite fails for suspended users being rehired
description: I’m uploading a CSV to restore 42 seasonal agents we had suspended last quarter, but the import stops with errors saying the email already exists or the user cannot be created. If I open those profiles individually, they show as suspended, not active. I also noticed the CSV tool says 11 of them are "new users" even though all of them existed before. I’ve checked the file formatting and retried twice. Can someone help us sort out whether this is a user state issue or a bad import process?

comments.message: Our bulk CSV import to bring back 42 suspended users fails with mixed errors like "email already exists" and "user cannot be created."

Thanks for the detail. For previously suspended users, the CSV importer won’t always reactivate them correctly if the action is set to create. Please confirm whether your file includes a status column and whether the email addresses exactly match the old accounts, including case and any alias domains.
Yes, the emails match exactly, and we included status=active. We also removed extra columns and still got the same results.

Understood. The mixed behavior suggests the importer is treating some suspended accounts as immutable records while others are being evaluated as new users. As a troubleshooting step, try reactivating one affected user manually from User Administration and then re-run the CSV with just five users to see whether the import succeeds for the rest.
Manual reactivation works for one user, but we can’t do that 42 times today. The five-user test still failed for three of them.

Thanks, that rules out a simple CSV formatting issue. I’m escalating this to our provisioning team with your sample file and the failed email list because this looks like inconsistent backend handling of suspended-user imports. While they investigate, the fastest workaround is to manually reactivate only the priority users who need immediate access, then we can bulk-correct the rest after the escalation review.

subject: Benachrichtigungen trotz deaktivierter Regel weiterhin aktiv
description: Ich habe die E-Mail-Benachrichtigung "Ticket an Anfragenden bestätigt" in unserer Instanz deaktiviert, aber Kunden bekommen die Nachricht weiterhin. Ich habe sogar eine neue Konfigurationsversion veröffentlicht. Es wirkt, als würde irgendwo noch eine zweite Regel greifen. Bitte helfen Sie mir dabei oder eskalieren Sie es, wenn Sie keinen Zugriff auf die Mail-Logs haben.

comments.message: Zur Info: Wir haben zusätzlich einen Workflow für VIP-Kunden. Ich bin mir aber ziemlich sicher, dass der nur interne Hinweise versendet.

Ich habe kurz geprüft und sehe, dass die Standardbenachrichtigung deaktiviert ist. Dann sollten keine Mails mehr rausgehen. Vermutlich stammen die Nachrichten noch aus einer Warteschlange und hören bald auf.
Die Mails gehen seit drei Tagen nach jeder neuen Anfrage raus. Das ist keine Warteschlange. Außerdem ist der Inhalt leicht anders als in der Standardvorlage.

Dann dürfte tatsächlich Ihr VIP-Workflow eine eigene ausgehende Aktion enthalten. Bitte prüfen Sie unter Automatisierungen, ob dort ein E-Mail-Schritt mit einer benutzerdefinierten Vorlage konfiguriert ist. Da ich in Ihrem Plan keine detaillierten Mail-Logs einsehen kann, kann ich nur begrenzt helfen. Wenn Sie möchten, kann ich das zur tieferen Analyse an unser Messaging-Team weitergeben.

subject: API rate limits are causing delayed workflow transitions
description: We use the API to update shipment milestones, and each milestone change is supposed to trigger an automation that moves the case to the next stage and notifies the account team. During peak hours we're hitting rate limits, some updates are retried later, and the workflow transitions happen out of order. Customers are seeing cases jump from "Delivered" back to "In Transit". How should we structure this to keep the automation sequence correct?

comments.message: Our integration sends status updates as they arrive from carriers. If a request gets a 429, we retry with backoff, but by then a later milestone may already have been accepted, so older events land after newer ones and your workflows still process them.

To avoid out-of-order transitions, don't let your workflow depend solely on arrival order of API updates. Include the carrier event timestamp or sequence number in a field, and have your integration compare it before writing. On our side, workflows fire on the final saved state, so if an older update shouldn't win, your client should reject it or write it to an audit field instead of the live status.
Can we enforce that server-side with conditional updates?

We don't currently support conditional PATCH based on another field's value in the public API. The best supported pattern is client-side ordering control plus workflow guards, for example only advancing stages when the incoming milestone rank is greater than the current one. I can send a sample automation design that uses a numeric milestone_order field to prevent backward moves.

subject: Impossible d'importer les leads depuis Google Ads via Zapier
description: J'ai configuré un Zap pour envoyer automatiquement les leads Google Ads vers votre module CRM, mais une partie des créations échoue avec une erreur de validation. Les leads sans numéro de téléphone passent, tandis que ceux avec téléphone sont rejetés. Dans l'historique Zapier, le payload semble correct et au format international. Nous avons besoin de cette intégration pour notre campagne en cours.

comments.message: Bonjour, notre Zap Google Ads > votre CRM échoue seulement pour certains leads, surtout quand le numéro de téléphone est présent. Pouvez-vous vérifier la validation côté intégration ?
Bonjour, si certains leads passent et d'autres non, le plus simple est de supprimer le champ téléphone du Zap. Comme ça l'intégration fonctionnera pour tous.
Ce n'est pas vraiment une solution, on a besoin du téléphone pour nos commerciaux. Est-ce qu'il y a une règle de format précise chez vous ?

À ma connaissance non, nous acceptons les numéros de téléphone standards. Je vous recommande de garder uniquement les chiffres dans Zapier et d'essayer plusieurs variantes. Nous n'avons pas d'autre réglage sur notre côté.

subject: Changed legal entity and invoice workflow broke after billing profile update
description: Our company changed legal entities this quarter, so I updated the billing name, tax details, and primary account owner to our new finance controller. We also have an automation that routes invoices by subsidiary based on billing profile fields. Since the change, invoices are still being generated under the old entity name, and the workflow that should tag EMEA invoices is now failing because it can’t find the updated billing profile values. We have an audit review next week and need this straightened out.

comments.message: I updated the billing profile on Monday, but invoice drafts still say ‘Larkspur Holdings LLC’ instead of ‘Larkspur Europe BV.’ The automation also stopped applying the EMEA tag after the account owner change.

Billing profile edits generally apply only to future finalized invoices, not draft documents already generated from the previous profile snapshot. I can also see your automation is reading a deprecated field called account.company_name rather than the new billing_legal_name field.
Can you regenerate the drafts with the new entity name? We already sent one internally for review.

I can request draft regeneration, but support can’t guarantee same-day turnaround. I’ve submitted that request to billing and confirmed your account owner change is complete. For the automation failure, update the condition to reference billing_legal_name and billing_country instead of the old account object fields. Until that’s changed, the subsidiary tagging logic will keep missing the new profile data.

subject: Besoin d'un modèle de configuration pour des approbations internes
description: Je veux mettre en place un processus d'approbation interne avant qu'une demande d'achat soit envoyée au fournisseur, mais je ne sais pas si je dois utiliser un workflow, un statut personnalisé ou une automatisation. J'ai aussi besoin de pouvoir expliquer simplement le fonctionnement aux responsables achats qui ne sont pas très techniques.

comments.message: Bonjour, notre cas est le suivant : un employé soumet une demande, son manager doit approuver, puis l'équipe achats traite la demande. J'ai essayé avec des statuts personnalisés et des notifications, mais on perd la trace de qui a approuvé. Quelle serait la meilleure configuration et comment la présenter aux utilisateurs métier ?
Bonjour, vous pouvez simplement créer un statut “Approuvé” et demander aux managers de laisser un commentaire quand ils valident. C'est généralement suffisant.
Ce n'est pas vraiment suffisant pour l'audit, justement. Je cherche une configuration plus structurée et des conseils de prise en main pour les équipes.

Vous avez raison. Pour un besoin d'audit, il vaut mieux utiliser un workflow avec une étape d'approbation dédiée plutôt qu'un simple statut libre. Je vous recommande : formulaire avec champ “Manager approbateur”, étape “En attente d'approbation”, action d'approbation/rejet tracée dans le workflow, puis transition automatique vers “Achats en cours”. Pour former les responsables achats, présentez-leur le processus en trois écrans maximum : soumission, approbation, traitement. Évitez de leur montrer toute la logique technique ; donnez plutôt un guide centré sur “quoi faire selon le statut affiché”.

subject: NetSuite invoices are duplicated in revenue dashboard
description: I noticed our monthly revenue dashboard shows several invoices twice after we connected NetSuite last week. The integration imported historical billing data, and now the reporting widget is overstating MRR by around 11%. I can't tell whether the duplicates are coming from two sync jobs or from the dashboard logic. Please take a look because leadership is using this dashboard in tomorrow's board prep.

comments.message: Since enabling the NetSuite integration, invoice INV-10433, INV-10441, and a bunch of others show up twice in the revenue dashboard. The underlying report totals are inflated, and I need this corrected before tomorrow morning.
This usually means the report has duplicate grouping. Please edit the dashboard widget and group by invoice number so duplicates are merged.
We already group by invoice number. The raw report export itself contains duplicate rows with different sync timestamps, so this doesn't look like a dashboard configuration problem.

Understood. In that case the integration may have created separate records during the historical import and the incremental sync. I don't have tooling to deduplicate accounting imports from support, so I'm escalating this to our integrations specialists. I've also flagged the reporting impact because your dashboard is using those imported invoice records directly. They'll review whether we can merge the duplicates or exclude one sync source.

subject: Bulk user import created orphaned manager relationships
description: We imported a new org chart with manager_email fields, and now dozens of users show managers that don't exist or belong to a different region. When I try to fix the manager_id values via API, some records update and some fail with validation errors. I also need to lock down who can edit org data because too many team leads have admin-level access right now.

comments.message: Our CSV import created users first and then attempted manager mapping. I think some manager records weren't present yet, so now we have invalid relationships in the data. The API returns 'manager not found' for certain user updates.

That usually happens when the referenced manager account is inactive or the identifier you're sending doesn't match the internal user ID. If you're updating manager relationships via API, use the platform user ID rather than email where possible. Also, only Org Admins can edit manager hierarchies in bulk, so if team leads are doing this today they may have broader access than intended.
We are currently using email, and some managers were deactivated during a restructuring. Is there a way to identify every user with an invalid manager reference?

There isn't a single built-in report for invalid manager references in self-serve, but you can export all users with user_id, manager_id, manager_email, and active status, then compare manager_id values against the active user list. Any missing or inactive manager targets should be remapped before you rerun the import. Separately, I recommend reviewing custom admin roles and removing org-edit privileges from team leads who only need reporting access.

subject: Cannot auto-close tickets after customer reply window expires
description: I need help configuring an automation that closes support tickets 5 business days after we send a pending-response message, but only if the customer has not replied. I created the workflow and a status-based rule, but tickets are either closing immediately or never closing at all. I'm not sure whether the timer is attached to the wrong event or if my status mapping is off.

comments.message: The workflow is supposed to start when status changes to Pending Customer. Then after 5 business days, if there are no public replies from the requester, it should change status to Closed. Right now some tickets close the same day.

Please check two Product Configuration items: your business hours calendar and the status transition rules. In Workflow Automation, the wait step should be configured as "wait 5 business days after entering Pending Customer," not "5 days after ticket updated." If it's tied to ticket updated, any internal note can satisfy the timer logic unexpectedly. Also add a condition right before close: requester comment count unchanged since workflow start.
I changed the wait trigger to entering Pending Customer, but I don't see an option for requester comment count unchanged. There is only "last public comment by."

Use that instead. Add an if/else condition after the wait: if "last public comment by = agent" and status is still Pending Customer, then close the ticket. One warning: if your agents send follow-up reminders publicly, that will still make the condition true even if the customer replied earlier and the status was switched back incorrectly. If you want a more reliable setup, I’d recommend escalating to your workflow specialist to build it off a custom timestamp field.

subject: Falsche Rolle an neuen Benutzern nach CSV-Import
description: Ich habe heute 43 neue Benutzer per CSV importiert. Alle wurden angelegt, aber statt „Light Agent“ haben viele die Rolle „Endbenutzer“ bekommen. Jetzt fehlen ihnen Menüpunkte und sie können nicht arbeiten. Ich brauche eine schnelle Korrektur und vor allem eine Erklärung, ob der Fehler in meiner Datei oder im Import lag.

comments.message: In der CSV stand in der Spalte role durchgehend „light agent“. Gibt es dafür eine exakte Schreibweise, die Ihr System erwartet?

Normalerweise erkennt das System Rollen unabhängig von Groß-/Kleinschreibung, daher sollte „light agent“ funktionieren. Vermutlich gab es beim Import einen temporären Anzeigefehler. Bitte laden Sie die Seite neu und prüfen Sie, ob die korrekten Rollen dann erscheinen.
Nach Neuladen ist es unverändert. Die Benutzer sind weiterhin Endbenutzer und können keine internen Notizen sehen.
Dann ändern Sie die Rollen bitte einzeln in der Benutzerverwaltung. Einen bekannten Importfehler sehe ich hier nicht. Wenn es erneut passiert, senden Sie uns gern die CSV.

subject: Notifications are being sent twice to assignees
description: Our team started receiving duplicate assignment emails yesterday afternoon. Every time a ticket is assigned, the assignee gets two identical notifications about a second apart. I checked one automation we changed this week, but I can't see anything obviously wrong. This appears to affect all users, not just one mailbox. We use Outlook if that matters.

comments.message: Assignment notifications are suddenly going out twice for every ticket. It seems system-wide. Can you help me figure out where the duplicate trigger is coming from?

Certainly. First, please check whether you recently enabled both email notifications under Admin > Notifications and a workflow automation that also sends an email on assignment. Duplicate emails often happen when both are active.
I checked that. We do have the default assignment notification enabled, but our automation only posts to Slack and updates priority. No custom email step there.

Understood. I reviewed similar cases and there is an active issue where assignment emails are duplicated if the new 'group fallback routing' setting is enabled alongside legacy assignment notifications. Please disable group fallback routing temporarily and test one ticket assignment. If you need that setting left on, I'll escalate this as a product bug with your account details.

subject: Reset hasła prowadzi do wygasłego linku
description: Za każdym razem gdy proszę o reset hasła, wiadomość przychodzi dopiero po kilkunastu minutach i link jest już nieważny. Przez to nie mogę odzyskać dostępu do konta. Czy można wysłać działający link albo jakoś inaczej zweryfikować moje konto?

comments.message: Link do resetu hasła zawsze jest wygasły, bo e-mail przychodzi z dużym opóźnieniem.
Sprawdziłem logi i rzeczywiście Państwa serwer pocztowy opóźnia dostarczenie naszych wiadomości średnio o 18 minut, podczas gdy link resetu wygasa po 15 minutach. To tłumaczy problem.
Czy da się coś z tym zrobić po Waszej stronie?

Tymczasowo wydłużyłem ważność linków resetujących dla Państwa workspace do 60 minut i wygenerowałem nowy link. Dodatkowo zalecam dodanie naszej domeny nadawczej do allowlisty po stronie IT, żeby uniknąć opóźnień. Jeśli nowa wiadomość nadal nie dotrze na czas, możemy przełączyć konto na logowanie przez link jednorazowy wysyłany przez administratora.

subject: Permissões impedem atualização de propriedade em massa
description: Eu sou administrador do workspace de operações e preciso preencher um campo obrigatório novo em milhares de registros. Porém, quando tento usar atualização em massa, o sistema diz que eu não tenho permissão para editar aquele conjunto, embora eu consiga abrir os registros individualmente. Isso parece inconsistente e está travando a governança dos dados. O que devo revisar?

comments.message: Eu sou administrador do workspace de operações e preciso preencher um campo obrigatório novo em milhares de registros. Porém, quando tento usar atualização em massa, o sistema diz que eu não tenho permissão para editar aquele conjunto, embora eu consiga abrir os registros individualmente.

Esse comportamento normalmente ocorre quando a permissão de edição em massa está separada da permissão de edição individual, ou quando o campo pertence a um objeto com política restrita por equipe. Verifique em Configurações > Permissões se o seu perfil tem 'edição em massa' para esse objeto e se o campo não está bloqueado por governança. Também vale revisar se há regras de propriedade de registro impedindo alterações fora da sua equipe.
Revisei meu perfil e a edição em massa está habilitada. O campo foi criado pelo time de TI e marcado como obrigatório no objeto Conta.
