Obrigado pelo detalhe. Se o campo foi criado recentemente como obrigatório, pode haver uma validação impedindo updates em massa quando algum registro relacionado não atende outras regras do objeto. Tente uma importação com ID + esse campo para um subconjunto de teste; se falhar, capture a mensagem completa. Como isso pode envolver política de campo e validação no backend, vou encaminhar para o suporte avançado com o nome exato do campo e o ID de uma conta de exemplo.

subject: Unklare Gutschrift nach Downgrade
description: Ich habe unser Jahresabo letzte Woche auf einen kleineren Tarif reduziert. Im Portal sehe ich jetzt eine Gutschrift, aber es ist nicht nachvollziehbar, wie dieser Betrag berechnet wurde. Außerdem wurde gleichzeitig eine neue Rechnung erzeugt. Können Sie mir bitte genau erklären, wie die anteilige Verrechnung zustande kommt?

comments.message: Ich habe unser Jahresabo letzte Woche auf einen kleineren Tarif reduziert. Im Portal sehe ich jetzt eine Gutschrift, aber es ist nicht nachvollziehbar, wie dieser Betrag berechnet wurde. Außerdem wurde gleichzeitig eine neue Rechnung erzeugt. Können Sie mir bitte genau erklären, wie die anteilige Verrechnung zustande kommt?

Ja, natürlich. Bei einem Downgrade wird immer automatisch der Restwert Ihres bisherigen Tarifs gutgeschrieben und direkt mit dem neuen Tarif verrechnet. Deshalb sehen Sie sowohl eine Gutschrift als auch eine neue Rechnung.
Das ist mir zu allgemein. Ich brauche die konkrete Berechnung, weil die Beträge nicht zu unserem Vertrag passen.

Ich habe mir die Beträge angesehen. Die Gutschrift wurde auf Basis der verbleibenden 214 Tage Ihres bisherigen Jahresplans berechnet, abzüglich des bereits genutzten Zeitraums, und dann auf die neue Rechnung angewendet. Die Differenz ergibt sich aus dem Preis des neuen Tarifs sowie der sofortigen Umstellung mitten im Abrechnungszyklus. Wenn Sie möchten, kann ich Ihnen die einzelnen Positionen als CSV aus unserem Billing-System exportieren.

subject: Downgraded seats but still charged for previous count
description: I reduced our workspace from 18 seats to 11 last week because two contractors rolled off and some managers are sharing logins temporarily. The billing page now shows 11 seats, but the invoice generated this morning still charged us for 18. I also need the owner changed from our former operations lead to me since she left the company on Friday and I can't access some of the billing controls.

comments.message: I reduced our seat count on April 3, and the admin page reflects 11 seats now. Today's invoice still billed us for 18 seats. Also, our old owner, Melissa Grant, has left the company. Please transfer billing owner/admin rights to me at this email so I can manage the account going forward.

I checked the account and the seat reduction was made after your renewal threshold for this billing cycle, so the current invoice still reflects 18 seats. The 11-seat count should apply starting next invoice. For the owner change, I can update the billing contact, but changing the account owner requires confirmation from the current owner or a domain verification step.

Melissa no longer has access to her email, so she can't confirm anything. We use the same company domain and I can provide whatever proof you need. Also, if the reduced seats only apply next cycle, can you at least confirm whether we'll get any prorated credit?

Thanks. I’ve started a domain verification request to transfer owner permissions to you; you’ll receive a verification link shortly. On billing, because the decrease happened after the renewal processed, there isn’t an automatic proration for removed seats on annual-renewing monthly-billed plans. I’ve added a note for our billing team to review a possible courtesy credit, but I can’t promise one. Once verification completes, your billing admin access should update.

subject: Jira integration failing to create issues from escalations
description: We use the Jira integration to create engineering issues from escalated tickets. Starting this morning, clicking 'Create Jira issue' throws an error and no issue is created. We recently changed our Jira project configuration, so I suspect the integration is now missing something. Can you help troubleshoot what setting is failing?

comments.message: The error shown in your app is 'Request failed with status 400'. We changed the issue type screen in Jira yesterday and added a required custom field for Severity.

That likely explains it. A 400 from Jira after a project configuration change often means the integration payload no longer satisfies the required fields for the selected issue type. Please check whether the Jira mapping in our integration includes the new required Severity field, and whether the default issue type still matches the project screen configuration.
I checked and Severity wasn't mapped. I added a default value and now issue creation works for Bugs, but not for Tasks.

Good progress. That suggests the required fields differ by issue type in Jira. Please review the field configuration for Tasks as well—there may be another required custom field or a different allowed value set for Severity. If you send the exact Jira response body for a failed Task creation, we can pinpoint the remaining mismatch.

subject: Marketo campaign sync says successful, but nurture status isn't updating
description: Our Marketo integration reports successful sync jobs, but the nurture status in your platform hasn't changed for any leads since Monday. I'm not sure whether the sync is actually writing data back, or if we misunderstood how the field mapping is supposed to work. Can someone help me troubleshoot and explain the expected setup?

comments.message: The Marketo sync job says completed successfully every hour, but lead nurture status is unchanged in your app. We expected the value from our Marketo program to flow into your customer records.

A successful job only means the connector ran; it doesn't guarantee that a mapped field changed. Please check whether nurture status is mapped as an inbound field from Marketo under Integrations > Marketo > Field Mapping, and make sure the source value actually lives on the lead record rather than only within the program member context. Our sync can read record fields directly, but not all program-scoped statuses are available without a custom sync setup.
It does look like the status we're using is only on the program membership, not the lead record.

That would explain why the sync completes without updating anything. The usual fix is to mirror the program status into a lead field in Marketo, then map that field into our platform. If you'd rather keep the current model, I can hand this off to our integrations specialists to confirm whether your plan includes the custom activity sync needed for program-level data.

subject: Pętla logowania w aplikacji mobilnej
description: W aplikacji mobilnej wpisuję poprawne dane, pojawia się na chwilę ekran główny i zaraz wracam z powrotem do ekranu logowania. Na komputerze działa normalnie. Problem występuje tylko na iPhonie.

comments.message: Mam pętlę logowania na iPhonie — po wpisaniu danych aplikacja na chwilę wpuszcza mnie do środka, a potem wylogowuje.
Czy próbował Pan odinstalować aplikację i zainstalować ją ponownie?
Tak, zrobiłem to dwa razy. W przeglądarce na laptopie mogę się zalogować bez problemu.
W takim razie to raczej problem po stronie telefonu. Proszę zaktualizować iOS do najnowszej wersji i spróbować jeszcze raz.
iOS jest aktualny. Nadal to samo.
Rozumiem. Na ten moment nie mam innych kroków. Proszę korzystać z wersji przeglądarkowej, dopóki aplikacja nie zacznie działać poprawnie.

subject: Delegation rule stopped after team lead account deactivation
description: I deactivated a team lead who left the company, reassigned their direct reports to a new manager, and expected our approval workflow to continue routing requests through the new chain. Instead, requests are getting stuck in a pending state with no approver assigned. I'm not sure whether the issue is the user admin change or the workflow configuration around delegated approvals.

comments.message: We use a rule that says if the requester's manager is unavailable, delegate to the regional director. Since the old manager account is deactivated, I thought it would fall through automatically, but the requests just sit as 'awaiting approver resolution'.

I can help check both sides. In many setups, deactivating a user does not automatically replace historical manager references already cached in open workflow definitions. First, verify in User Administration that the direct reports now show the new manager on their profiles. Second, check whether your approval step is configured as 'manager at submission time' rather than 'current manager at runtime.'

The users do show the new manager now. Looking at the workflow, the step says 'Manager snapshot on request creation.' We created one draft request before the reassignment and then submitted it after the deactivation.

That would cause it. The draft preserved the old manager relationship, and once that account was deactivated the system couldn't resolve the approver or delegation path. New submissions should use the updated manager if the profile is correct. For drafts created before the admin change, you'll need to resubmit them or have an admin manually replace the approver. If you want to avoid this in the future, switch that step to resolve the current manager at runtime.

subject: Service account lost access and nightly workflow failed
description: Our nightly automation that closes stale tickets failed for two days, and I just found out it runs under a bot account that can no longer sign in. I don't fully understand how non-human accounts are supposed to be managed in your system. Can you help us restore it and explain the recommended setup?

comments.message: The workflow audit log says 'owner authentication invalid.' The owner is a bot account we created last year for automations, but IT enforced SSO and apparently no one can log into that account now. Since then the nightly ticket cleanup workflow has stopped. What should we do?
If the bot account can't sign in, the simplest fix is to disable SSO for that user and set a permanent password so the workflow can authenticate again.
Our security team will never allow that. Is there another supported option?

Yes — and that's the better long-term path. Use a service account that is exempt from interactive login but authorized for automation ownership through API or internal system credentials, depending on your plan. Your admin should transfer the workflow to that service account, confirm it has the required automation scope, and avoid tying critical jobs to a personal or standard SSO user. I'll escalate this to our platform team to verify which non-interactive service account model is enabled in your tenant.

subject: Refund question after purging test companies from production
description: A contractor accidentally imported 12,000 test company records into our production account instead of the sandbox. We bulk deleted them the same afternoon, but we were upgraded to a higher record tier and charged more. I need to know whether deleted records can be excluded and what controls we should put in place to prevent this kind of data mistake.

comments.message: The test companies all had names like QA Dummy 001 and were never used by the sales team. Since they were obvious test data and removed immediately, can you refund the difference on this month's invoice?
If the records were deleted the same day, they generally should not count, so the invoice is probably wrong. You can ignore it for now while it updates automatically.
Finance cannot ignore a posted invoice, and the higher tier is still showing in account usage. Also, what import settings would have prevented this?
I'm not able to change posted invoices from support. To avoid this in the future, make sure imports are reviewed before running them and use your sandbox for testing.

subject: Deleted values still appear in dropdown imports
description: We removed several deprecated values from a custom dropdown field used on renewal records, but our import still accepts the old values and in some cases maps them to blanks without throwing an error. We changed the field options in admin yesterday, and the data file definitely contains retired values from our legacy system. I'm not sure whether the platform caches old option mappings in import templates or if the field configuration wasn't fully published.

comments.message: I deleted three old values from our Renewal Reason dropdown, but today's import didn't reject them consistently. Some rows went through with an empty Renewal Reason. Shouldn't invalid options fail validation?

Yes, invalid dropdown values should normally be rejected during import validation. Try deleting and recreating the import template because it may still be using cached option mappings from before your field change.
We recreated the template and got the same behavior. Rows with retired values are still imported as blank.

In that case, the importer may be configured to coerce unmatched optional dropdown values to null instead of treating them as hard errors. Check the field setting for whether Renewal Reason is required and whether the import job has "allow blank on invalid option" enabled. If you want strict rejection, you'll need to disable that tolerance or make the field mandatory during import.

subject: User removed from group still sees restricted project
description: I took one employee out of our Finance group yesterday, but she says she can still open a restricted project that only Finance should see. I already confirmed she’s no longer in the group in the admin panel. Is there a delay on permission changes, or is there another place I need to remove access?

comments.message: I took one employee out of our Finance group yesterday, but she says she can still open a restricted project that only Finance should see. I already confirmed she’s no longer in the group in the admin panel. Is there a delay on permission changes, or is there another place I need to remove access?
Group changes should apply right away, so there is no delay. If she still sees the project, then she must still be in the Finance group somewhere.
She definitely is not. I checked her profile and the group audit log. She was, however, added directly to that project months ago before we started using groups.
In that case, you can ignore it because direct project membership is overridden by group removal.

subject: SSO login loop only for users in one domain alias
description: Users signing in with our secondary email domain are stuck in an SSO login loop. They authenticate with our identity provider successfully, land back in your app, and then get sent to the login page again. Users with our primary domain can sign in normally. We changed nothing in our IdP configuration this week. Please investigate if this is an issue with domain alias handling.

comments.message: The affected alias is @acmehealth.co, while @acmehealth.com works. The same people can log in if we temporarily change their email to the .com version.

Thanks for the clear repro. I checked your SSO config and I can see the primary verified domain, but I don't have visibility into alias matching behavior at the authentication-service level. Because the issue is isolated to one verified alias and users complete IdP auth successfully, this looks like a likely product defect rather than an IdP failure. I’m escalating it to our identity team with the affected alias and your workaround.

subject: SEPA-Lastschrift nicht mehr verfügbar
description: Bis letzten Monat konnten wir unsere Rechnungen per SEPA-Lastschrift bezahlen. Jetzt wird im Abrechnungsbereich nur noch Kreditkarte angezeigt. Wir haben unsere Zahlungsart nicht aktiv geändert. Ist SEPA für unseren Account deaktiviert worden, und wenn ja, warum?

comments.message: Bis letzten Monat konnten wir unsere Rechnungen per SEPA-Lastschrift bezahlen. Jetzt wird im Abrechnungsbereich nur noch Kreditkarte angezeigt. Wir haben unsere Zahlungsart nicht aktiv geändert. Ist SEPA für unseren Account deaktiviert worden, und wenn ja, warum?
SEPA ist nur für Neukunden verfügbar, daher sehen Bestandskunden manchmal nur noch Kreditkarte.
Das ergibt keinen Sinn, wir haben SEPA ja bereits genutzt. Gibt es einen konkreten Grund für die Änderung in unserem Konto?
Möglicherweise liegt das an Ihrem Browser-Cache. Bitte öffnen Sie die Seite im Inkognito-Modus und prüfen Sie, ob SEPA dann wieder erscheint.

subject: Resposta da API não inclui macros aplicadas por automação
description: Estamos a consumir o histórico dos tickets pela API para auditoria interna. No painel, vejo que uma macro foi aplicada automaticamente quando o ticket entrou na fila de faturação, mas no endpoint de eventos isso não aparece. Já ativei o registo avançado nas configurações. Isso precisa de alguma opção adicional?

comments.message: Estamos a consumir o histórico dos tickets pela API para auditoria interna. No painel, vejo que uma macro foi aplicada automaticamente quando o ticket entrou na fila de faturação, mas no endpoint de eventos isso não aparece. Já ativei o registo avançado nas configurações. Isso precisa de alguma opção adicional?
Se a macro aparece no painel, ela deveria aparecer igual no endpoint de eventos. Talvez o vosso pedido esteja a usar paginação antiga e por isso faltem registos.
Revimos isso. O evento de mudança de estado aparece, mas não há nenhuma entrada da macro, mesmo numa chamada direta ao ticket específico.

Então pode ser uma limitação conhecida, mas não tenho confirmação agora. Vou escalar para a equipa de API porque pode depender de como a automação foi configurada no vosso tenant. Assim que tiver retorno, atualizo este ticket.

subject: Former admin still receiving system notifications
description: I changed one of our old team leads from Admin to Basic user and then deactivated the account last month, but she is somehow still getting weekly system notification emails. She cannot log in anymore, so the deactivation worked partly. I want to stop the emails and make sure there isn't some hidden admin entitlement left behind.

comments.message: I changed one of our old team leads from Admin to Basic user and then deactivated the account last month, but she is somehow still getting weekly system notification emails. She cannot log in anymore, so the deactivation worked partly. I want to stop the emails and make sure there isn't some hidden admin entitlement left behind.

If login is blocked, there isn't an active admin entitlement. Weekly system emails are often tied to watcher lists or scheduled report subscriptions, which can survive a role change and even deactivation for a short time. Please open the user profile and check Notifications, then remove any report subscriptions or ownership of scheduled exports. Also review whether that user is listed as the contact on any automation alerts.
I found two scheduled exports still owned by her, but I don't see where automation alert contacts are managed.

Go to Settings > Automations > Alert Recipients. From there you can replace inactive users on any email alerts. Once you transfer those and save, the remaining notifications should stop within the next send cycle. If you want, I can also outline a cleaner offboarding sequence for admins so these leftovers don't persist.

subject: Zendesk se conecta pero no crea tickets con la categoría correcta
description: Conectamos Zendesk para que los formularios web creen tickets automáticamente, pero todos entran sin la categoría que definimos. El resto de la información sí llega, así que parece que el fallo está solo en el mapeo de un campo personalizado o en cómo quedó configurado ese campo dentro de la plataforma. Necesito saber si es un límite de la integración o un error de configuración nuestro.

comments.message: El campo de categoría en nuestro sistema es obligatorio y tiene opciones como Facturación, Soporte técnico y Implementación. En Zendesk el ticket se crea, pero la categoría queda vacía.
Si el ticket se crea, la integración está funcionando. Puede ignorar ese campo por ahora y completarlo manualmente en Zendesk hasta que se sincronice mejor.
Pero justamente necesitamos automatizar esa categoría porque dispara flujos internos.

Entiendo, aunque no hay mucho más que revisar desde aquí. Tal vez Zendesk no soporte ese tipo de campo personalizado en integraciones estándar. Le sugiero probar con otro campo o contactar directamente a Zendesk.

subject: Zapier integration trigger fires for some records but not others
description: We use the Zapier integration to trigger follow-up workflows whenever a deal reaches Closed Won in your platform. Recently, some deals trigger the Zap immediately, while others never show up in Zapier at all. I need help troubleshooting whether the trigger is filtering incorrectly or if event delivery is inconsistent.

comments.message: There doesn't seem to be an obvious pattern, but one thing I noticed is that the missed records were bulk-updated by an automation rule instead of changed manually by a rep. We rely on this trigger for onboarding handoffs.

That difference is important. Some integrations distinguish between user-generated updates and system/automation updates, and if the Zapier trigger is subscribed only to standard record update events, bulk automation transitions may not emit the same trigger. Could you check whether the missed deals have corresponding audit log entries marked as 'automation rule' rather than 'user update'?
Yes, the missed ones all show 'automation rule' in the audit log. So does that mean Zapier just won't work for them?

At the moment, our Zapier trigger does not consistently emit for bulk automation-driven status changes, so what you're seeing is a product limitation rather than a setup issue. A workaround is to have the automation write to a secondary field and trigger off that via another integration path, or avoid bulk transitions for deals that must enter Zapier immediately. I've documented the gap for our product team.

subject: Approval workflow skips manager step for new purchase requests
description: I'm trying to automate our purchase request process, but the workflow is skipping the manager approval step for some new requests and sending them straight to finance. It only seems to happen when the requester is in our EMEA team. I need to understand why the condition isn't evaluating correctly and how to stop requests from bypassing approval.

comments.message: We have a workflow that should route any purchase request over $1,000 to the requester's manager, then to finance. For users in EMEA, the manager step is being skipped entirely even when the amount is $4,000 or more. I checked that those users do have managers set in their profiles.

Thanks for the details. I reviewed the behavior you described, and the most likely cause is that the branch condition is checking a region-specific field before the manager lookup completes. In our workflow builder, manager-based approvals rely on the requester profile being resolved first. Please edit the workflow and move the 'Amount > 1000' and 'Send to manager approval' actions ahead of any branch that references the team region. Also confirm the manager field is mapped to the system user profile field, not a custom text field.
I moved the approval step above the EMEA branch and tested with one user. That fixed it for a new request. Is there any reason it would still fail for users synced from SCIM?

Yes, if SCIM is populating manager as plain text instead of the linked user attribute, the workflow can still fail to assign the approver. Please check one affected synced profile under Directory > User attributes and verify 'Manager' is a user reference. If it's text-based, you'll need to remap that field in your SCIM configuration. Based on your test, the workflow logic itself is now correct.

subject: Teams integration test message says success but nothing arrives
description: We're setting up the Microsoft Teams integration for incident alerts. In your admin panel, the test notification returns success, but no message actually appears in the Teams channel. I need help troubleshooting whether the connector is misconfigured or if the success message is misleading.

comments.message: We pasted the webhook URL from Teams into the integration settings and saved it. The test result in your app says 'Notification sent', but the target channel remains empty. We tried two different channels with the same result.

Thanks. If our system returns 'Notification sent,' that usually only confirms the HTTP request was accepted by the webhook endpoint, not that Teams displayed it. Please verify whether the Teams workflow or incoming webhook is still active and whether your tenant has blocked connector cards. Also, if possible, paste the response body from the test log under Integration Activity.
The log response body says 'Webhook message delivery failed with error: ConversationBlockedByPolicy'. Looks like our security team changed something.

That explains it. The integration itself is functioning, but Microsoft 365 is rejecting delivery due to a tenant policy. You'll need your Teams or M365 admin to allow the incoming webhook/workflow app for that team or channel. Once policy access is restored, our existing webhook URL should work without further changes.

subject: SLA breach automation did not escalate overnight tickets
description: I expected our workflow automation to escalate any urgent support ticket that remained unassigned for 30 minutes, but several overnight tickets breached SLA without being escalated. The rule seems to work during business hours and not overnight. Can you help me understand why the timed escalation isn't firing consistently?

comments.message: The automation is configured for Priority = Urgent and Assignee is empty, then wait 30 minutes and notify the on-call manager. Tickets created at 2 p.m. escalate fine. Tickets created at 2 a.m. just sit there until morning.

Thanks — that behavior usually means the workflow is restricted to business hours. In the wait step settings, there should be an option to count only business time versus elapsed time. If it's using business time, a 2 a.m. ticket won't accumulate the 30 minutes until your support schedule begins. Switching that wait to elapsed time should make overnight escalations fire immediately after 30 real minutes.
I see the business-hours setting now. If I change it, will that affect tickets already waiting in the workflow?

Existing queued executions normally keep the timing mode they entered with, while new ones use the updated setting. If those overnight tickets are still open and need attention now, you may need to escalate them manually and monitor the next new urgent ticket to confirm the fix.

subject: Why are duplicate contacts being created from the web form?
description: Our website form is creating what looks like duplicate contacts in the CRM. I assumed the system would merge them automatically by email, but I’m now seeing separate records for the same person when they submit twice with slightly different company names. I need help understanding the matching logic and what settings we should change so our team stops calling the same lead twice.

comments.message: Our website form is creating what looks like duplicate contacts in the CRM. I assumed the system would merge them automatically by email, but I’m now seeing separate records for the same person when they submit twice with slightly different company names. I need help understanding the matching logic and what settings we should change so our team stops calling the same lead twice.
Duplicates happen when the form creates a new contact each time. You may want to clean them up manually from the contacts page.
We have thousands of submissions, so manual cleanup isn’t really workable. I’m specifically asking how matching works and whether email can be used as the primary dedupe field.

Thanks for clarifying. By default, form submissions match on the unique identifier configured for the object, which in most CRM setups is email for contacts, but only if the field is mapped consistently and normalized the same way. If the form is creating duplicates, check whether email is marked as a unique field in Data Model settings, whether the form maps to Contact rather than Lead, and whether hidden fields or an automation are creating a second record after submission. A good preventive setup is: use email as the unique key, enable duplicate warnings, and route company updates to the company record instead of using company name as a contact-level identifier.

subject: Workflow-Regel löst nach Formular-Update nicht mehr aus
description: Ich habe gestern in unserem Lead-Formular ein Pflichtfeld hinzugefügt und seitdem startet unsere Automatisierung nicht mehr. Normalerweise wird nach dem Absenden automatisch eine Aufgabe für den Vertrieb erstellt und eine Willkommensmail verschickt. Jetzt passiert gar nichts, obwohl neue Einträge im System ankommen. Ich habe die Regel nicht bewusst geändert. Können Sie prüfen, woran das liegt?

comments.message: Seit der Formularänderung werden neue Leads zwar erstellt, aber der Workflow "Inbound Follow-up" läuft nicht an. Vorher hat das problemlos funktioniert.

Danke für die Details. Wenn ein Workflow nach einer Formularänderung nicht mehr auslöst, liegt es oft daran, dass die Trigger-Bedingungen noch auf die alte Feldkonfiguration verweisen. Bitte prüfen Sie in der Automatisierung, ob das neu hinzugefügte Pflichtfeld jetzt ebenfalls eine Bedingung ist oder ob sich der interne Feldname geändert hat.
Ich sehe gerade, dass die Bedingung auf "Branche ist nicht leer" prüft. Das Feld wurde neu angelegt, weil wir das alte ersetzt haben. Der interne Name ist jetzt offenbar anders.

Das erklärt das Verhalten. Der Workflow referenziert noch das alte Feld und wird deshalb nicht mehr erfüllt. Bitte ersetzen Sie die Bedingung im Trigger durch das neue Feld "Branche" und veröffentlichen Sie den Workflow erneut. Danach sollten Aufgabenerstellung und Willkommensmail wieder wie gewohnt starten.

subject: User deactivated by mistake and restore keeps failing
description: I accidentally deactivated one of our finance users while cleaning up old accounts. When I try to restore her, the app gives a generic error and then refreshes without reactivating the profile. I also can’t assign her back to her previous group because the selector says the account is in an invalid state.

comments.message: The user needs access today for month-end reporting. I’ve tried restoring from both the People page and the audit log entry, but each time I get “something went wrong.”

Sorry about that. As a first step, please try restoring the user from a desktop browser in incognito mode and make sure no admin sessions are open in another tab. Sometimes conflicting session tokens can interfere with account state changes.
I tried incognito and a different admin account. Same error, and the browser console shows a 409 conflict on the restore request.

A 409 here usually means the user still has a conflicting membership record in a suspended state. I’ve manually cleared that stale record and restored the account. She should now be active again, and you can reassign her finance group from the People page without hitting the invalid state message.

subject: Necesito entender si la API de Zoom sirve mejor que la integración nativa
description: Queremos registrar webinars de Zoom en la plataforma y no tengo claro si la integración nativa ya cubre asistentes, cancelaciones y cambios de horario, o si necesitamos usar la API. Busco una explicación comparativa para decidir sin cometer un error de arquitectura.

comments.message: Nuestro equipo no es muy técnico, pero sí tenemos un desarrollador disponible algunas horas al mes. No queremos meternos en la API si la integración nativa ya resuelve el 80%.

En ese caso, la decisión depende del nivel de personalización que necesiten. La integración nativa suele ser mejor para casos estándar: conectar la cuenta, traer eventos básicos y evitar mantenimiento técnico. La API de Zoom conviene cuando necesitan lógica propia, campos adicionales o manejar casos específicos como reintentos, conciliación de cancelaciones o flujos entre varios sistemas.
¿Entonces la integración nativa trae asistentes y cambios de horario?
Sí, debería traer todo lo importante automáticamente, así que probablemente no necesiten la API.

subject: La integración con Jira no crea incidencias como esperaba
description: Configuré la integración con Jira pensando que cada ticket con la etiqueta 'bug' iba a crear una incidencia automáticamente, pero solo pasa a veces. No entiendo si el problema está en el disparador, en los permisos del proyecto o en el mapeo. ¿Me ayudan a entender cómo debería funcionar?

comments.message: Hice tres pruebas: una creó incidencia, otra no hizo nada y la tercera falló con un mensaje genérico. Necesito saber qué revisar primero.

Primero revisa el flujo lógico: 1) que la regla se dispare cuando se añade la etiqueta 'bug', 2) que la conexión con Jira siga autorizada, y 3) que el proyecto y tipo de incidencia de destino acepten los campos obligatorios. Si falta, por ejemplo, 'Issue Type' o 'Project Key', la creación puede fallar aunque la regla se active.
Creo que no habíamos mapeado prioridad ni componente, pero no sabía que fueran obligatorios en ese proyecto.

Eso encaja con el comportamiento intermitente. En Jira, algunos proyectos tienen validaciones propias. Como siguiente paso educativo, te sugiero abrir la configuración del proyecto en Jira y anotar todos los campos requeridos para crear una incidencia de tipo Bug. Luego vuelve al mapeo aquí y completa esos campos. Si después de eso sigue fallando, envíanos el ID de una ejecución y revisamos el log contigo.

subject: Can’t downgrade plan because active workflows use paid actions
description: We’re trying to reduce costs before renewal, but the billing portal blocks our downgrade and says we still have workflows using premium automation actions. That part is fair, except the workflows it references were disabled months ago and one belongs to a deleted sandbox. I need guidance on what exactly is preventing the billing downgrade and where to remove or convert those workflow automation dependencies.

comments.message: The billing page won’t let me downgrade from Enterprise to Pro because of active premium workflows. I only see disabled workflows, and one of the IDs in the warning doesn’t even exist in production anymore.

Downgrades can be blocked if any workflow still contains actions that are unavailable on the lower tier, even if the workflow is currently off. Sandbox assets can also be included if the subscription covers multiple environments. Please send the workflow IDs from the warning banner.
Sent. One is in our deleted sandbox and two are disabled templates we kept for reference only. We don’t want to keep paying another month just because those are lingering.

I checked the IDs and you’re right: one reference is stale metadata from the sandbox, and the other two are disabled drafts with Enterprise-only actions. I removed the stale blocker and listed the exact actions you need to replace in the drafts. Once those are edited or deleted, billing should allow the downgrade immediately.

subject: Sandbox app extension stopped loading after feature flag change
description: We enabled a new sidebar extension in our sandbox and tied it to a feature flag for just our QA team. Since flipping that flag, the extension panel is blank for everyone, including admins. I need help identifying whether the extension manifest is wrong or the rollout setting is interfering.

comments.message: The extension loaded fine before we changed targeting. Now the sidebar shell appears but the iframe content never loads in sandbox. Production is unaffected because the flag is still off there.

Blank extension panels after rollout changes are often caused by the feature flag audience excluding the service account the app uses to render metadata. Please add admins and system users to the flag audience, then republish the extension manifest so the client fetches the updated entitlement. Also verify the sandbox manifest still points to the sandbox callback URL.
We added admins but not any system users because I don't see those listed anywhere. Republishing didn't help.
In that case it's probably a caching issue in the browser. Try a hard refresh or open the ticket in a private window. If that doesn't work, wait a few hours for the flag to propagate.

subject: Changement d’administrateur principal bloqué
description: Je suis la nouvelle responsable IT de notre société et j’essaie de remplacer l’administrateur principal de notre espace, car mon prédécesseur a quitté l’entreprise. Son adresse e-mail n’existe plus, donc je ne peux pas lui faire valider le changement depuis l’interface. En plus, certaines notifications de sécurité continuent à partir vers son ancienne adresse. Pouvez-vous mettre à jour le compte administrateur principal et m’indiquer si vous avez besoin d’un justificatif ?

comments.message: Bonjour, je n’arrive pas à changer l’administrateur principal depuis les paramètres, car l’ancien admin n’a plus accès à sa boîte mail. Nous devons transférer ce rôle à mon compte avant la fin de semaine.

Bonjour, je peux vous aider. Pour ce type de changement de propriétaire de compte, nous devons vérifier l’identité du demandeur. Merci de répondre avec le nom de l’entreprise, l’URL de votre espace, l’ancienne adresse de l’administrateur, la nouvelle adresse à définir, ainsi qu’un document confirmant votre affiliation (par exemple une signature e-mail professionnelle ou un document interne).

Merci. Société : Novalys Conseil, URL : novalys.workspace.app, ancien admin : p.martin@novalys.fr, nouvel admin : s.bernard@novalys.fr. Je joins une capture de notre annuaire interne et j’écris depuis mon adresse professionnelle.

Merci pour ces éléments. J’ai lancé la procédure de changement d’administrateur principal et mis à jour l’adresse de réception des notifications de sécurité vers s.bernard@novalys.fr. Le changement sera effectif dans quelques minutes. Si vous ne voyez pas le rôle mis à jour d’ici 15 minutes, déconnectez-vous puis reconnectez-vous.

subject: User rename via API broke joins in our data pipeline
description: We updated usernames to a new email domain through the API, and that seems to have broken several downstream joins in our warehouse because some exports now show the new login while others still use the old external identifier. On top of that, only a subset of our admins can correct the bad records because the rename permission appears broader than the profile-edit permission. I need to clean up the user data and understand the right immutable key to use going forward.

comments.message: We changed about 900 users from @oldco.com to @newco.com. The API update succeeded, but our user export now has mixed identifiers across reports, and a few renamed users lost SSO access until we manually fixed them.

For integrations, you should avoid joining on mutable login fields like username or email. The stable key is the internal user ID, or external_id if you manage it consistently from your identity provider. Renaming login values can also affect SSO if the IdP and our platform are temporarily out of sync. As for permissions, many workspaces allow profile edits to delegated admins but reserve login/identity changes for a narrower admin role.
Can we mass-populate external_id now from our HRIS and then use that for future joins? We also need to know which admins can still perform renames.

Yes, you can backfill external_id via the users API or import tool, provided the values are unique. That's the right move if you need a business-owned immutable identifier for joins. To see who can perform renames, review your custom roles for identity-management or authentication-edit privileges; those are usually separate from general user profile permissions. If helpful, I can hand this to our API team for examples on a safe backfill sequence.

subject: La sincronización con SFTP reescribe fechas en formato incorrecto
description: Cada madrugada recibimos un archivo por SFTP y el sistema actualiza nuestra tabla de pedidos. Desde la semana pasada, las fechas que vienen como 03/04/2024 se están guardando como 4 de marzo para algunos registros y como 3 de abril para otros. Esto está rompiendo filtros, métricas y deduplicación por periodo. Necesito una forma segura de corregir la importación y reparar los datos ya afectados.

comments.message: Cada madrugada recibimos un archivo por SFTP y el sistema actualiza nuestra tabla de pedidos. Desde la semana pasada, las fechas que vienen como 03/04/2024 se están guardando como 4 de marzo para algunos registros y como 3 de abril para otros. Esto está rompiendo filtros, métricas y deduplicación por periodo. Necesito una forma segura de corregir la importación y reparar los datos ya afectados.

Parece un conflicto de localización entre DD/MM/YYYY y MM/DD/YYYY en el conector o en la transformación posterior. Lo primero es pausar la sincronización programada para evitar más corrupción. Después, revise la configuración del parser de fechas del origen SFTP y fuerce un formato explícito, idealmente ISO 8601 antes de cargar. Para reparar lo ya afectado, conviene identificar el rango de importaciones desde que cambió el comportamiento y hacer una actualización correctiva solo sobre esos registros.
No recuerdo haber cambiado nada en el conector. ¿Puede haber sido por una actualización de la plataforma?

Es posible, pero también puede deberse a un cambio en el archivo de origen o a una transformación heredada que empezó a interpretar según la región del workspace. He revisado incidentes abiertos y no veo uno generalizado, así que lo trataremos como caso puntual por ahora. Si me comparte una muestra del archivo y el nombre del job SFTP, escalo la revisión del parser y le indico cómo generar un parche para las filas afectadas.

subject: Sandbox-Konfiguration lässt sich nicht in Produktion übernehmen
description: Ich habe in der Sandbox neue SLA-Regeln, Ticketfelder und Automatisierungen eingerichtet. Beim Versuch, die Konfiguration in die Produktion zu übernehmen, bricht der Deployment-Check mit einer generischen Fehlermeldung ab. Da die Änderung Teil unseres Monatsendes ist, brauche ich entweder eine Lösung oder eine schnelle Eskalation.

comments.message: Die Meldung lautet nur: "Deployment validation failed on dependencies." Einen Detailbericht sehe ich nicht. Können Sie prüfen, welche Abhängigkeit fehlt?
Danke. Solche Meldungen kommen meistens von Browser-Caching. Bitte leeren Sie den Cache, melden Sie sich neu an und versuchen Sie das Deployment erneut.
Das haben wir auf zwei Rechnern ausprobiert, gleiches Ergebnis. Es betrifft offenbar nur ein bestimmtes Ticketfeld, das in mehreren Regeln verwendet wird.

Verstanden. Dann liegt es wahrscheinlich an einer Referenz auf ein Feld oder eine Gruppe, die in Produktion anders heißt oder nicht existiert. Ich kann den Validierungsbericht in Ihrem Account nicht direkt einsehen, deshalb habe ich den Fall an unser Deployment-Support-Team eskaliert. Bitte senden Sie mir noch den Namen des betroffenen Felds und den Zeitstempel des letzten Fehlversuchs, damit ich die Eskalation ergänzen kann.

subject: Shopify orders imported but refund reporting is inconsistent
description: We connected Shopify for ecommerce reporting, and order revenue looks mostly fine, but refunded orders are behaving strangely. Some refunds reduce net sales immediately, others only show up after the next full sync, and a few never hit the refund report at all even though the order timeline shows the refund event. Please investigate and escalate if this is a known integration issue because finance doesn't trust the dashboard right now.

comments.message: We connected Shopify for ecommerce reporting, and order revenue looks mostly fine, but refunded orders are behaving strangely. Some refunds reduce net sales immediately, others only show up after the next full sync, and a few never hit the refund report at all even though the order timeline shows the refund event. Please investigate and escalate if this is a known integration issue because finance doesn't trust the dashboard right now.

I reviewed the examples you sent and there are actually two behaviors mixed together. Partial refunds are reflected only after the nightly aggregation job, while full refunds usually appear after the incremental sync. The orders that never enter the refund report seem to have been refunded before the Shopify connector's 'financial status' field was added to your mapping. I've escalated those cases to integrations engineering because historical refund events may need a backfill.
That explains some of it, but can we do anything ourselves for the older missing refunds?

You can trigger a historical resync for the affected date range from the Shopify connector settings, which may repopulate the mapped financial status for older orders. If that doesn't restore them, engineering will need to run a backend backfill, and I've already noted that in the escalation so they can pick it up quickly.

subject: Unable to add a new department manager as admin
description: I’m trying to promote our new department manager to an admin role, but every time I save the change it spins for a while and then drops her back to Member. I’ve tested in Chrome and Edge, and I can edit other profile fields fine. We need her to manage users today.

comments.message: I’m trying to promote our new department manager to an admin role, but every time I save the change it spins for a while and then drops her back to Member. I’ve tested in Chrome and Edge, and I can edit other profile fields fine. We need her to manage users today.
That usually happens when the browser cache is stale. Please clear your cache and cookies, log out, and try again in an incognito window.
I already tried incognito. The role change still doesn’t stick, and I only see it happening for this one user. She was previously in a restricted contractor group if that matters.
If she was a contractor before, the safest option is to create a brand new user and assign admin there. We do not recommend changing roles on existing accounts.

subject: Benutzerdefinierte Felder erscheinen nicht im Kundenportal
description: Ich habe ein neues benutzerdefiniertes Feld "Anlagen-ID" erstellt und in der Portalkonfiguration dem Vorgangsformular hinzugefügt. Im Agentenbereich ist es sichtbar, im Kundenportal jedoch nicht. Ich habe Cache und Veröffentlichungsstatus geprüft. Das Ticket ist für uns wichtig, weil wir ohne das Feld keine Zuordnung zu unseren Geräten machen können.

comments.message: Das Feld ist als Textfeld angelegt und nicht intern markiert. Andere neue Felder erscheinen, nur dieses eine nicht.
Hallo, bitte prüfen Sie, ob der API-Name des Feldes Sonderzeichen enthält. Felder mit Bindestrichen werden im Portal manchmal nicht dargestellt.
Der API-Name ist sauber: anlagen_id. Außerdem habe ich es testweise neu erstellt, gleiches Problem.

Danke für die Rückmeldung. Dann vermute ich eine Einschränkung im verwendeten Portal-Template oder einen Indexierungsfehler nach der Veröffentlichung. Ich kann das nicht direkt auf Template-Ebene korrigieren und habe den Vorgang deshalb an unser Portal-Konfigurationsteam eskaliert. Bitte schicken Sie mir noch den Namen des betroffenen Formulars, dann ergänze ich die Eskalation.

subject: Need clarification on credit after importing and then deleting malformed orders
description: We imported a large batch of order records from our warehouse system, then discovered the date field was misparsed and every row duplicated on re-import. We deleted the bad batch and kept the corrected one, but now we're billed for the temporary spike in order objects. I need to know whether there is any chance of a credit and how to validate imports before they affect billable totals.

comments.message: This was about 220,000 rows. We noticed the issue within a few hours and removed the malformed records. Is there a pre-validation step we should have used, and do deleted bad rows still trigger billing?
There isn't really a way to preview imports before they land in production, and billing counts whatever was there at some point. So unfortunately there's nothing you could have done differently.
That doesn't sound right. I remember seeing a dry-run option somewhere, and we need actual guidance for next time.

Thanks for pushing back. You're correct: the bulk importer does have a validation-only mode that checks schema mapping, required fields, and duplicate keys before committing records. It won't catch every business logic issue, but it would have flagged your date parsing mismatch if the field format rule had been enabled. I still need to escalate the billing portion because object-volume credits require approval from Finance, but I recommend using validation mode plus a 500-row sample import into sandbox before any full load.

subject: Automation loop after PATCH requests update status
description: We built an integration that PATCHes cases through the API whenever our phone system changes the call outcome. The problem is that one of our workflow automations also updates the status and then triggers our integration again, so we end up in a loop of API updates and workflow executions. We need a safe pattern to stop the recursion without breaking the business process.

comments.message: Specifically, when our API sets status='waiting_on_customer', your automation sends an SMS and then updates a custom field called sms_sent=true. That field change triggers our middleware, which PATCHes the case again and starts the cycle over.
You should be able to solve this by excluding API updates from the workflow. Add a rule so the automation only runs when updated_by is not API.
I don't think we have an 'updated_by is not API' condition available in the builder. I only see actor type, specific fields changed, and custom fields.
In that case, the cleanest option is to have your middleware stop listening to updates from that object entirely after it sets waiting_on_customer. That will prevent the loop.

subject: Bulk update changed the wrong company owners
description: I ran a bulk update on company records and accidentally reassigned around 300 accounts to the wrong owner. I was trying to update a region field at the same time, and I think I included the owner column by mistake in my CSV. I need to know whether there’s a way to reverse the change and how to avoid this in the future because I’m nervous about doing another import.

comments.message: The import happened about an hour ago. We don’t have a backup export from right before the change, only one from last week. Is there an audit log or a way to see exactly which records were touched?

If the changes came from an import, the import history should show the job and the number of affected records, but it won’t always provide a clean rollback file. Check each company’s activity timeline for field changes, and if your plan includes audit logs, you may be able to filter changes to the Owner field from the last hour.
That helps, but I still need to fix 300 records quickly. Can support restore the previous owner values?

Support can’t directly restore previous field values on your behalf. The fastest path is usually to export the affected companies, compare against your last good export, and prepare a corrective CSV containing only the company ID and the correct owner. Before re-importing, test with 5 records first. Going forward, I strongly recommend using import templates with only the columns you intend to change and reviewing the field-mapping summary before confirming the job.

subject: Two people shared one login and now we need separate accounts
description: Our support desk had two coordinators using the same generic login for the past month, which I realize was a bad idea. We now need to split that into separate named users, make sure each person can sign in individually, and keep the old records if possible. I'm not sure whether I should rename the existing user, create two new ones, or how to avoid messing up permissions. Can you walk me through the correct setup and any best practices?

comments.message: Our support desk had two coordinators using the same generic login for the past month, which I realize was a bad idea. We now need to split that into separate named users, make sure each person can sign in individually, and keep the old records if possible. I'm not sure whether I should rename the existing user, create two new ones, or how to avoid messing up permissions. Can you walk me through the correct setup and any best practices?

Yes — don't rename the shared login into one of the individuals, because that makes the audit trail misleading. The recommended approach is to deactivate the shared account for future login, create two separate named users, and assign each the appropriate role and team membership. Historical records created by the generic account will remain attributed to that original user unless you manually reassign ownership on open items. After inviting the two new users, have each person activate their own login and enable MFA if available.
If I deactivate the shared account, can I still transfer the open tasks from it to one of the new coordinators?

Yes. Deactivation blocks sign-in but doesn't remove the account from assignment tools. From the task list, filter by owner = shared account, bulk reassign open items to the new users, and then leave closed historical items untouched so reporting remains accurate. For permissions, give both users the same Coordinator role the shared account had only if they truly need identical access; otherwise this is a good chance to reduce privileges per person.

subject: Locked out after enabling MFA and can't verify data sync status
description: I turned on MFA for our workspace this morning, and now I can log in only intermittently. When I do get in, the nightly sync page shows our product catalog import as incomplete, but I can't tell whether the missing items actually failed or if the status page is stale. I need help restoring access and confirming the data state.

comments.message: On two login attempts I got kicked back to the sign-in page after entering the OTP. Once I finally got in, the catalog sync said 'processing' for a job that started 9 hours ago. We imported around 12,000 SKUs from the API.

Sorry you're running into this. The login loop after MFA enrollment can be caused by time drift or an old session cookie. Please confirm your device clock is set automatically, then sign out of all sessions and retry in a private window. For the stuck sync, go to Data Imports and compare the imported record count with the previous successful job to see whether data actually landed despite the stale status.
Private window fixed the login issue. The import count is lower by about 1,700 SKUs, and the failed rows mention missing category IDs.

Great, that narrows it down. Your access problem was session-related after MFA enablement. For the data issue, the sync is partially complete and the missing SKUs failed validation because their referenced category IDs don't exist in the target workspace. Please re-run the import after adding or mapping those categories, and if you want, reply with one failed row sample and I can help confirm the correct format.

subject: Onboarding checklist advice and a stuck SSO mapping issue
description: I'm building an onboarding checklist for new workspace admins and want to include a section on SSO and user provisioning basics. The problem is that our role mapping from the identity provider is assigning Viewer access to people who should be Editors, so I'm hesitant to train anyone until I understand what's happening. I'd appreciate both a simple explanation we can use in onboarding and help getting this in front of the right team.

comments.message: What is the clearest way to explain SSO role mapping to non-technical admins, and why would mapped roles not match what we configured in the IdP?

The clearest explanation is: SSO confirms who the user is, and role mapping tells our app what access they should get when they sign in or are provisioned. If users are getting Viewer instead of Editor, common reasons include a mismatch in the attribute name or value, a fallback default role, or a group mapping taking priority over the direct role claim.
Is there any way for me to check which mapping actually won without editing the live setup?

There usually is, but it depends on whether your account has the identity troubleshooting panel enabled. Rather than risk giving you steps that don't exist in your tenant, I'm escalating this to our identity support specialists. They'll confirm what logging is available in your plan and help you document a safe admin onboarding process around role mapping.

subject: Changed billing contact and now API rate limits look wrong
description: I updated our account contacts and moved admin access from our finance lead to our engineering manager. Since then our API usage dashboard shows us on a lower rate limit tier, and batch imports are being throttled. I need someone to verify whether the account change accidentally altered our plan settings or API entitlements.

comments.message: After we changed the admin/contact setup this morning, our importer started hitting 429 responses much earlier than usual. The dashboard now shows 600 requests/minute instead of 2000. Did the account change downgrade our API limits?
Account contact changes shouldn't modify plan entitlements. The 429s may just be temporary load balancing. Please retry later and consider exponential backoff.
We've retried for six hours. The limit displayed in the UI is still lower, and our integration is blocked.

Understood. I don't have permission to edit API entitlements directly, and because this appears tied to an account metadata change rather than transient throttling, I'm escalating to our subscriptions/platform team. I've included screenshots from your usage page and noted that the issue began immediately after the admin/contact update.

subject: Can’t demote former admin because member page crashes
description: We need to remove admin rights from an employee who moved departments, but whenever I open his member details panel the page spins for a few seconds and then throws a generic error banner. I can still open other users fine. I’ve tried from two admin accounts, so right now we’re stuck with the wrong permissions on his account.

comments.message: We need to remove admin rights from an employee who moved departments, but whenever I open his member details panel the page spins for a few seconds and then throws a generic error banner. I can still open other users fine. I’ve tried from two admin accounts, so right now we’re stuck with the wrong permissions on his account.

Thanks for reporting this. To troubleshoot, can you confirm whether that user was originally provisioned through SCIM or invited manually, and whether the error appears after filtering by team or only from the full member list?
He was provisioned through SCIM, and the error happens from both the full list and search. We also noticed he has no department listed anymore, which seems odd.

I checked similar cases and this looks tied to a malformed team reference on some SCIM-managed profiles. That would explain why opening the record crashes before you can change the role. I’ve filed it as a bug and asked engineering to repair the broken membership object on this specific account. Until they do, we can’t safely demote him from the UI. I’ve also suggested temporarily suspending his login via your identity provider if access needs to be restricted immediately.

subject: Need a clearer process for onboarding interns
description: We're bringing on eight summer interns next Monday and last year it was chaotic. Some couldn't sign in because they used personal emails, some had too much access, and we spent half the day fixing permissions one by one. I want a simple onboarding checklist for inviting them correctly, making sure they can log in, and assigning a restricted role from the start. What do you recommend?

comments.message: We're bringing on eight summer interns next Monday and last year it was chaotic. Some couldn't sign in because they used personal emails, some had too much access, and we spent half the day fixing permissions one by one. I want a simple onboarding checklist for inviting them correctly, making sure they can log in, and assigning a restricted role from the start. What do you recommend?

A good process is: 1) confirm the exact email each intern will use to sign in, 2) create a dedicated Interns team or group, 3) bulk-invite them from Admin Settings > Users, 4) assign a restricted role such as Viewer, Commenter, or Intern Contributor depending on what they need to do, 5) add them only to the required teams/boards, and 6) ask them to complete password setup or SSO before day one if possible. To avoid over-permissioning, don't clone a full employee profile unless an intern truly needs the same access. We also recommend having one test intern account verify the invite flow before sending all eight.
This is helpful. Is there a way to resend all pending invites at once if a few miss the email?

Yes — on the Users page, filter by Pending Invitation, select the users, and use Bulk Actions > Resend Invite. If your email security sometimes quarantines invitations, you can also copy the activation links individually or ask your IT team to allowlist our sender domain. After they activate, review the Interns group once more to confirm no one inherited an admin role from a template.

subject: Regra de distribuição de tickets está mandando tudo para a fila errada
description: Configurei uma regra para encaminhar tickets com prioridade alta para a equipe Enterprise, mas desde a semana passada tudo está indo para Suporte Geral. Já revisei a ordem das regras e não consegui achar o problema.

comments.message: A condição é prioridade = alta e plano = enterprise. Antes funcionava, agora até cliente enterprise cai no time errado.
Oi! Pode ser um problema no cache das regras. Tente duplicar a regra, apagar a original e publicar novamente. Isso costuma corrigir esse tipo de comportamento.
Fiz isso e nada mudou. Continua indo para Suporte Geral.
Nesse caso, acho melhor deixar assim por algumas horas porque às vezes o roteamento demora para propagar. Se continuar amanhã, nos avise.

subject: Assinatura do webhook não valida depois de mudar segredo
description: Rodámos o segredo do webhook ontem por motivos de segurança. Desde então, o endpoint recebe as notificações, mas a assinatura HMAC já não bate com o header enviado por vocês. No painel do produto, o novo segredo aparece guardado. Preciso confirmar se a API usa imediatamente o novo valor ou se há mais alguma configuração escondida.

comments.message: Rodámos o segredo do webhook ontem por motivos de segurança. Desde então, o endpoint recebe as notificações, mas a assinatura HMAC já não bate com o header enviado por vocês. No painel do produto, o novo segredo aparece guardado. Preciso confirmar se a API usa imediatamente o novo valor ou se há mais alguma configuração escondida.

Obrigado. Fui verificar e a rotação do segredo fica ativa de imediato apenas para novos webhooks, mas existe uma fila de entregas já agendadas que pode continuar a usar o segredo antigo durante alguns minutos. Além disso, na sua configuração a opção "incluir prefixo sha256=" estava ativada e isso altera o formato esperado do header. Recomendo aceitar ambos os segredos por 15 minutos durante a transição e validar removendo o prefixo antes de comparar, ou incluindo-o do vosso lado conforme a implementação.
Faz sentido. Estávamos a comparar o hash puro e não o valor completo do header com o prefixo. Depois de ajustar isso, os eventos novos validam corretamente.
Excelente. Vou deixar anotado no ticket que o comportamento observado veio da combinação entre rotação de segredo e formato do header da assinatura.

subject: Quiero exportar solo la última versión de cada registro, pero salen historiales
description: Necesito una exportación limpia para auditoría con una sola fila por registro, usando la versión más reciente de cada uno. Sin embargo, al exportar desde la vista actual me incluye múltiples versiones históricas del mismo ID y eso infla el conteo. Ya revisé los filtros pero no encuentro cómo excluir snapshots anteriores.

comments.message: Necesito una exportación limpia para auditoría con una sola fila por registro, usando la versión más reciente de cada uno. Sin embargo, al exportar desde la vista actual me incluye múltiples versiones históricas del mismo ID y eso infla el conteo. Ya revisé los filtros pero no encuentro cómo excluir snapshots anteriores.
Para eso debe activar la opción "Solo registros activos" antes de exportar. Esa opción elimina versiones históricas y deja una sola fila por ID, normalmente la más reciente.
No veo ninguna opción con ese nombre. Solo tengo "incluir archivados" y "exportar columnas visibles".
Entonces use "exportar columnas visibles" y quite la columna de versión. Así debería salir una sola fila por registro.

subject: Conditional form section never appears in approval workflow
description: I configured a purchase request form so that when Department is IT, an extra Security Review section should appear and the workflow should route to the security approver before finance. The route to finance works, but the Security Review section never shows for requesters, and the workflow then skips the security approval because those fields are blank. I need help figuring out whether the form condition and the workflow condition are reading different values.

comments.message: Department is a dropdown field. The form rule is "show Security Review when Department = IT." The workflow branch is also checking Department = IT. But submitters only see the base form, and requests go straight to finance.

A common cause is using different Department fields in the form and workflow—one on the request template and one on the underlying record. In Product Configuration, confirm the form section visibility rule references the same field ID the workflow uses. Also verify whether the IT option's stored value is `information_technology` rather than the display label `IT`.

I checked and there are indeed two Department fields with nearly identical names. The form was using the older one, while the workflow was using the new standardized field. The new field stores `it_dept`, not `IT`.

That would explain both symptoms. Update the form visibility rule to use the standardized Department field and compare against the stored option `it_dept` if the form builder requires internal values. Then republish both the form and the approval workflow. After that, the Security Review section should appear for IT requests and the security approval branch should receive the required fields instead of skipping.

subject: Replaced canceled card after admin change, payment failed twice
description: I updated our account admin from my founder to me, then replaced the expired company card in billing. The renewal still attempted on the old card twice and now the invoice shows a late fee warning even though the new card is valid. I need the payment method and account contact corrected, and I want the failed attempts and any penalties reviewed.

comments.message: I updated our account admin from my founder to me, then replaced the expired company card in billing. The renewal still attempted on the old card twice and now the invoice shows a late fee warning even though the new card is valid. I need the payment method and account contact corrected, and I want the failed attempts and any penalties reviewed.

I can see the admin transfer succeeded, but the default payment method remained set to the founder's expired Visa until this morning. I've switched the new Mastercard ending in 4402 to default and retried the invoice successfully. The late fee warning was system-generated after the failed attempts; I've removed it before it could post as an actual charge. Your billing contact is now your profile, and future renewals will use the updated card.

subject: API webhooks duplicating events for some ticket updates
description: We're seeing duplicate webhook deliveries for ticket.updated events. Not every update is duplicated, but enough that our downstream automation is creating repeated tasks. I checked our endpoint logs and the duplicate payloads have different delivery IDs but the same event content. We need to know if this is expected retry behavior or a platform bug.

comments.message: This started two days ago and seems limited to updates made through bulk edits. Single manual edits don't always duplicate. Our webhook endpoint is returning 200 within 300ms, so it doesn't look like retries from slow responses.

Thanks, that's helpful. Different delivery IDs with identical event bodies could still happen if the source action fires more than once. Can you confirm whether the duplicated events share the same event timestamp and object revision number?
Yes, in several examples the revision number is identical. The timestamps differ by about one second.

I pulled one sample from your account and can confirm two separate deliveries were generated upstream for the same revision during a bulk edit operation. That points to a bug rather than webhook retries on your side. I’ve escalated this to engineering with your sample event IDs and noted the bulk-edit pattern. In the short term, please dedupe on revision number if possible; I realize that’s a workaround, not a fix, but it should stop the repeated tasks while engineering investigates.

subject: Mobile app crashes when opening task details
description: When I open certain task records in the iOS app, the app closes immediately and sends me back to my home screen. It doesn't happen on every task, but I've found it on at least seven records so far. Web app works fine for those same tasks. I'm on iPhone 14, iOS 17.4, and app version 6.2.1. I already reinstalled the app once, but the crash still happens.

comments.message: The iOS app crashes whenever I open some task details. Reinstalling didn't help, and it only affects certain tasks.

Sorry about that. To troubleshoot, could you share whether those tasks have anything in common, like long descriptions, attachments, or custom fields? If possible, send 2-3 task IDs that crash consistently.
Yes, the ones that crash all seem to have image attachments added from email. Here are three task IDs: T-10448, T-10463, and T-10502.

Thank you — that pattern helped. We tested one of the sample records and found the crash is triggered by inline image attachments larger than 10 MB in app version 6.2.1. This is a known bug in the mobile viewer. For now, those tasks can still be opened in the browser, and reducing or removing the large image attachment stops the crash. I've attached your examples to the engineering ticket and flagged it as impacting production use.

subject: Password reset worked, but I still can't edit the SLA automation
description: I was locked out after too many failed password attempts and used the reset email to get back in. Now I can access the workspace, but when I try to update our SLA breach automation I get a permissions error saying I need workflow admin rights. I had those rights last week. If this is a known issue, please escalate because we're changing support hours today.

comments.message: I was locked out after too many failed password attempts and used the reset email to get back in. Now I can access the workspace, but when I try to update our SLA breach automation I get a permissions error saying I need workflow admin rights. I had those rights last week. If this is a known issue, please escalate because we're changing support hours today.

I checked your login history and the lockout is resolved correctly. On permissions, I can confirm your account currently has the 'Team Lead' role, which can view automations but not edit SLA workflows. I also see your role was changed from 'Workflow Admin' three days ago, but there’s no note in the audit log explaining why. I can’t restore roles myself, so I’m escalating this to your account owner verification queue. In the meantime, if another current admin is available, they can make the SLA change today.
Our only other admin is on leave, which is why this is blocking us. Can support make the hour change if I send the exact settings?

I’m afraid we can’t apply workflow changes on behalf of customers without an authorized admin approving them from the account. I’ve upgraded the escalation priority and noted that the SLA automation is time-sensitive.

subject: Charged for inactive seats after SSO provisioning change
description: I updated our user provisioning settings so seats are created automatically from Okta, and now we're being billed for more users than actually log in. Some users were provisioned into default roles during testing and never activated. I need to know whether the configuration caused those seats to become billable and whether the invoice can be corrected.

comments.message: We changed SCIM provisioning last Friday and mapped new hires into Viewer by default. Since then, about 27 users were auto-created, but only 8 have ever signed in. Our invoice now reflects all 27 as paid seats. Is that expected?

On your Growth plan, any active provisioned user object with a product role counts toward seat billing, even if the user never logs in. I reviewed the SCIM config and the default role mapping is indeed creating billable Viewer seats at provisioning time. If you switch the default mapping to "No product access" and assign roles via group rules only, future staged users won't count as seats.
Understood. Can anything be done about the current invoice? Those extra users were created by mistake while we were testing the mapping.

I deactivated the 19 unused accounts and noted that they were generated during your provisioning test. I can't directly issue credits from support, but I've escalated invoice 77143 to billing with the SCIM audit log attached and requested a one-time adjustment for the mistaken seat creation. You'll hear back once they review it.

subject: What should I teach my team about saved views versus reports?
description: I’m preparing internal training for our operations team, and people keep mixing up saved views and reports in your app. Before I train them incorrectly, I want a simple explanation of what each one is for and when to use one instead of the other. We use both daily, but I’m not confident I can explain the difference.

comments.message: Our staff mainly want to know why they can’t build charts from a saved view.

Saved views are mainly for working the data; reports are for analyzing the data. A saved view is a filtered list you use operationally — for example, “My open high-priority tickets today.” A report is meant for trends, counts, charts, and summaries over time. That’s why views don’t usually provide chart-building options: they’re designed to help users find and act on records quickly, not do formal analysis.
Can I tell them a saved view is like a live queue and a report is like a summary?

Yes, that’s a great way to teach it. I’d phrase it as: views help you manage today’s work, reports help you understand patterns. If helpful, I can also suggest a 15-minute training agenda with examples your operations team can practice.

subject: Need help cleaning up duplicate contacts after CSV import
description: I imported a CSV of contacts into our workspace yesterday and it created hundreds of duplicates because some rows had slightly different company names. I’m not sure what the safest way is to merge or delete them without breaking our existing account history. Can you explain the right process and whether there’s a way to prevent this on the next import?

comments.message: I imported a CSV of contacts into our workspace yesterday and it created hundreds of duplicates because some rows had slightly different company names. I’m not sure what the safest way is to merge or delete them without breaking our existing account history. Can you explain the right process and whether there’s a way to prevent this on the next import?

Yes — the safest approach is to use the duplicate review tool rather than bulk deleting records. Go to Contacts > Data Quality > Potential Duplicates, review matched records, and use Merge so the primary record keeps timeline activity and associated account history. If you already know a segment is bad, you can bulk delete imported contacts by filtering on the import date, but I’d export them first as a backup. To prevent this next time, map a unique identifier like email or external ID during import and turn on duplicate detection for contacts and companies.
If I merge them, will notes and open deals from both records carry over, or do I have to reattach those manually?

Notes, activities, and open deals normally stay attached to the surviving primary record after a merge, though field values depend on which record you choose as primary. I recommend testing with 2-3 sample duplicates first so you can confirm exactly how your workspace handles associations before doing the full cleanup.

subject: Je ne peux pas comparer deux périodes dans le même rapport
description: Dans l'analyse des performances, j'essaie de comparer ce mois-ci au mois précédent, mais l'option de comparaison est absente sur certains rapports alors qu'elle existe sur d'autres. J'ai besoin d'une vue comparative pour notre présentation mensuelle. Comment l'activer ?

comments.message: Bonjour, pourquoi l'option de comparaison de périodes n'apparaît-elle pas sur certains rapports d'analyse ? J'en ai besoin pour comparer ce mois-ci au précédent.

Bonjour, l'option n'est disponible que pour les rapports basés sur des métriques temporelles compatibles. Si le rapport utilise une source temps réel ou un widget de tableau brut, la comparaison n'est pas proposée. Quel est le nom exact du rapport concerné ?
Le rapport s'appelle "Backlog par statut et priorité". C'est un rapport personnalisé avec un tableau et un graphique empilé.

Merci. Sur ce type de rapport, la comparaison n'apparaît pas si la métrique principale est un instantané de backlog plutôt qu'un volume d'événements. Vous pouvez contourner cela en dupliquant le rapport et en remplaçant la métrique par "tickets créés" ou "tickets résolus", mais pour un backlog historique il faut utiliser l'exploration Snapshot si elle est activée sur votre compte. Si vous voulez, je peux vous envoyer les étapes dans un autre message.

subject: Parent-child account links disappeared after API update
description: We ran an API script to update billing addresses on parent accounts, and afterward some child accounts no longer show a parent relationship in the UI. The child records still exist, but the hierarchy view is broken for at least 27 accounts. I need to determine whether our payload accidentally cleared relationship fields or whether your update process has a bug when writing account data in bulk.
