comments.message: We ran an API script to update billing addresses on parent accounts, and afterward some child accounts no longer show a parent relationship in the UI. The child records still exist, but the hierarchy view is broken for at least 27 accounts. I need to determine whether our payload accidentally cleared relationship fields or whether your update process has a bug when writing account data in bulk.
If the script changed the account object, it likely overwrote the parent reference. Please restore those accounts from a backup.
We only sent billing address fields and did not include parent_account_id in the payload. That’s why I’m asking whether partial updates can null omitted fields in bulk.

Our PATCH endpoint should not null omitted relationship fields, so your payload should not have removed the links by design. I can't confirm from here whether the script used PATCH or PUT, and I also can't inspect the raw request logs on your tenant. Please send one parent ID and two affected child IDs, and I’ll escalate to the API team to compare the write events against the hierarchy table updates.

subject: Exported data missing latest note entries
description: When I export our accounts to CSV, the note count and latest note date are behind by several days for a subset of records. Inside the app, I can see notes added this week, but the export reflects older values. We use the export for downstream reporting, so I need to know whether the export service is reading stale replicated data.

comments.message: When I export our accounts to CSV, the note count and latest note date are behind by several days for a subset of records. Inside the app, I can see notes added this week, but the export reflects older values. We use the export for downstream reporting, so I need to know whether the export service is reading stale replicated data.
That sounds unusual. Are you exporting from the Accounts page or from a saved report? The data sources are slightly different.
From a saved report. I tested the Accounts page export too, and that one looks correct. The issue seems limited to the saved report export.

Thanks, that narrows it down. The saved report export pulls from the reporting snapshot rather than the live account table, so this looks like a sync issue between notes activity and report materialization. I confirmed one of your recent note updates is present in the app but absent from the report snapshot. I’ve filed this as a bug for the reporting pipeline. As a temporary workaround, exporting directly from the Accounts page should give you current note fields, but the saved report export will remain stale until the snapshot refresh issue is fixed.

subject: Can’t remove last admin from a subsidiary workspace
description: I’m trying to clean up a subsidiary workspace we no longer use. The only remaining admin there transferred to our parent company team, and I want to deactivate her old subsidiary account. The system blocks me because it says she’s the last admin, but I don’t actually want anyone actively using that workspace anymore. I just need to assign a placeholder admin long enough to close out the remaining users.

comments.message: The workspace only has six users left. I need to deactivate Carla’s subsidiary account, transfer admin rights to a generic IT operations user temporarily, and then remove the remaining users over the next week.

You can’t deactivate the last admin on a workspace until another active admin exists. Create or reactivate your placeholder IT operations user first, assign it Admin, then transfer any pending user-management tasks from Carla before deactivating her account.
I created it, but the placeholder account only lets me assign Member or Analyst. I don’t see Admin as an option.

That usually means the account type doesn’t support admin access. It may be a restricted or API-only user. I can’t change that from support, so you’ll need to create a different standard user account, then promote it to Admin before Carla can be deactivated. If you want, send me the placeholder user type and I can confirm.

subject: Attachment migration created orphaned files on closed tickets
description: We used the bulk attachment migration tool to move legacy support files into closed tickets, and afterward around 90 attachments show up in storage usage but are not visible on any ticket record. The migration report marks them as successful, yet searching by filename returns nothing. We suspect the files were written but not linked correctly. Can someone investigate whether the migration dropped the ticket associations and whether those orphaned files can be reattached or removed?

comments.message: We used the bulk attachment migration tool to move legacy support files into closed tickets, and afterward around 90 attachments show up in storage usage but are not visible on any ticket record. The migration report marks them as successful, yet searching by filename returns nothing. We suspect the files were written but not linked correctly. Can someone investigate whether the migration dropped the ticket associations and whether those orphaned files can be reattached or removed?
If the migration report says successful, the files should be attached. It may just be a search indexing delay. Please wait 24 hours and check again.
It has already been 3 days. We also queried the ticket IDs directly and the attachments are not there, but storage usage still includes them.
In that case, you can delete some older attachments to free up space. I’m not able to confirm whether those specific files are orphaned from my side.

subject: Former admin removed, now app token rotation endpoint returns 404
description: We removed an old administrator from our workspace and reassigned account ownership, but now our automation cannot rotate tokens through the API anymore. The endpoint /v1/apps/{id}/rotate worked before and now returns 404 for one app and 403 for another. I need help figuring out whether app ownership changed and whether this can be repaired quickly.

comments.message: As soon as we deleted our old admin user and made our CTO the account owner, the token rotation endpoint started failing. One internal app says 404 and another says 403. Did removing that user orphan the apps?

Most likely, yes. Apps created under a user can become inaccessible if that user is removed before ownership is transferred. I checked and one of your apps is now in an orphaned state, while the other is visible but your current token lacks app:write scope. For the visible app, generate a new admin token with app management scope. For the orphaned app, I need to escalate to engineering because support cannot reassign app ownership directly.
Can you confirm whether the orphaned app's existing tokens will keep working while engineering looks at it? It's tied to our nightly sync.

I can't guarantee rotation will work, but the currently issued token should continue functioning until its scheduled expiry because runtime validation is separate from the ownership record. I've added an urgent escalation requesting reassignment before your next rotation window tonight.

subject: Página do portal do cliente quebra depois de ativar tema personalizado
description: Ativei um tema personalizado no portal do cliente e agora a página de abertura de chamados fica carregando sem renderizar os campos. Se volto para o tema padrão, funciona. Preciso identificar qual configuração do tema está causando isso.

comments.message: As alterações foram basicamente CSS e um pequeno script para esconder o campo de telefone quando o canal é e-mail.

Olá! Se com o tema padrão funciona, o problema provavelmente está no script personalizado e não na configuração do formulário. Você consegue remover temporariamente apenas o JavaScript e testar de novo?
Removi o script e a página voltou a abrir normalmente.

Ótimo, então isolamos a causa. Seu script provavelmente está executando antes de o formulário terminar de carregar ou está selecionando um elemento que mudou no novo layout. Recomendamos envolver a lógica em um evento de carregamento do portal e validar se o campo existe antes de ocultá-lo. Se quiser, envie o trecho do script em uma nova resposta que eu te ajudo a ajustar linha por linha.

subject: Can someone escalate our broken invoice workflow?
description: We're on an annual contract and our finance team can't download March or April invoices from the billing page. The buttons spin forever and then fail. I already tried multiple browsers and another admin account. We need the invoices for an audit this week, so if support can't fix it directly please escalate it to billing engineering or whoever owns the invoice service.

comments.message: This is now affecting our audit deadline. We just need the PDFs or a workaround today.
Sorry for the trouble. Have you tried clearing cache and disabling browser extensions? Invoice pages can fail to load if there's stale browser data.
Yes, as I said, we already tested multiple browsers and admins. This doesn't seem local.

Understood. I've now escalated the issue to our Billing team with your affected invoice months and account ID. I should have done that sooner given the repeated failures across browsers. While they investigate, I've also requested that they manually attach the invoice PDFs here if the generation service is stuck.

subject: Changed plan owner but refund went to old card
description: We transferred responsibility for the account from our old department head to me earlier this month. After that, we downgraded our plan and were told a credit would be issued, but the refund appears to have gone back to the previous card that has since been closed. I need the account ownership confirmed under my name and help tracing the refund.

comments.message: I took over this account from our former department head, Kevin Lowe, and I believe the ownership change was started but not fully completed. We downgraded from Enterprise to Pro on April 5, and your team said a partial refund would be issued. Our problem is the refund was sent to the old corporate card Kevin had on file, and that card account is closed. Can you confirm I'm now the billing owner and reissue the refund another way if needed?

I checked and the ownership transfer to you is still pending final acceptance, so you are not yet the billing owner in the system. For refunds, we can only send funds back to the original payment method due to card network rules. If the card is closed, the issuing bank typically reroutes the credit to the replacement account or handles it manually.
Kevin is no longer reachable, so I can't complete an acceptance flow with him. Is there another way to finalize the admin change? And can you provide proof of refund in case the bank asks?

Yes, I can convert the ownership request into a support-led verification case using your company email domain and a recent invoice copy, so I've opened that for review. I also attached the refund reference ID and ARN in the secure ticket notes; once verification is complete, I can resend those details directly to your email for your bank. The refund itself cannot be reissued to a different card unless the original credit fails back to us.

subject: Zapier trigger duplicates records and I need a safer setup
description: We built a Zapier integration so new accounts in your platform create tasks in several downstream tools. It mostly works, but we're getting duplicate tasks whenever an account is edited shortly after creation. I want help troubleshooting why the trigger fires this way, and I'd also like recommendations for a cleaner integration pattern so our ops team stops chasing duplicates.

comments.message: Our Zap is creating duplicate tasks. It seems like your "New Account" trigger fires once on creation and again when enrichment updates the account a few seconds later. How should this be configured properly?

That trigger can emit more than once if the record is updated during the initial processing window. A safer setup is to use the "Account Created" event if it's available in your Zapier app version, or add a filter/lookup step so the Zap only continues when no downstream task already exists for that account ID. We also recommend storing the source account ID in the target system as an idempotency key.
I don't see an "Account Created" trigger in our Zapier app, only "New or Updated Account." Can you enable the other one?

I can't enable it per account, and it may not be available in the older connector version you're using. For now, the practical fix is to add a deduplication step in Zapier: search the destination tool for an existing task by account ID before creating anything, and only create when none is found. If you'd like, I can escalate your request for the newer trigger to our integrations product team, but I can't promise a timeline.

subject: Transferred account to regional admin and webhook retries disappeared
description: We transferred account administration from our US team to our regional admin in EMEA. Around the same time, failed webhooks stopped retrying, even though the docs say we should get three retry attempts. We need help checking whether the admin transfer changed the app configuration or if this is a bug, and if necessary please hand this off to whoever can inspect delivery logs.

comments.message: Since moving account control to our EMEA admin, webhook failures are only logged once and never retried. This affects our ERP sync. Can you check whether retries were disabled during the account transfer?
Webhook retries are automatic and cannot be disabled, so if you see only one attempt it is probably because the receiver returned a success code.
It definitely did not. Our endpoint returned 500, and I can share logs. Also, this started exactly after the admin transfer.

Thanks — that rules out the most common cause. I reviewed the delivery summary available to support and agree the retry chain is missing. Because this may involve an app-level setting or an event pipeline issue after the admin transfer, I'm escalating to our integrations team for deeper log inspection. Please attach one recent event ID and timestamp so they can trace it.

subject: Falha ao criar ticket pela API quando o formulário exige campos condicionais
description: Temos um formulário com campos condicionais: quando a categoria é "Integração", o campo "sistema_origem" passa a ser obrigatório. Pelo painel funciona bem, mas ao criar tickets via API recebemos erro de validação mesmo enviando esse campo. Suspeito que a configuração do formulário e o nome do campo na API não estejam alinhados.

comments.message: Temos um formulário com campos condicionais: quando a categoria é "Integração", o campo "sistema_origem" passa a ser obrigatório. Pelo painel funciona bem, mas ao criar tickets via API recebemos erro de validação mesmo enviando esse campo. Suspeito que a configuração do formulário e o nome do campo na API não estejam alinhados.

Analisei um exemplo de payload seu e identifiquei a causa: no formulário, o campo tem rótulo "Sistema de origem", mas a API espera o identificador interno "origem_sistema_id" dentro de custom_fields, não o nome amigável "sistema_origem". Além disso, como a categoria é um campo single-select, o valor deve ser enviado pela chave interna "integracao" e não pelo texto exibido "Integração". Com esses dois ajustes, a validação passa.
Testámos com "origem_sistema_id" e a chave interna da categoria, e o ticket foi criado sem erro. Era exatamente isso.
Ótimo. Se ajudar, posso enviar a lista dos identificadores internos dos campos condicionais desse formulário para evitar novas tentativas por nome visível.

subject: Sincronização apagou tags antigas da base
description: Eu conectei a plataforma ao nosso sistema legado para sincronizar contatos, e desde então várias tags manuais que usávamos para segmentação desapareceram. Pelo que percebi, o conector está tratando a lista de tags como substituição completa em vez de adicionar valores novos. Preciso evitar novas perdas e recuperar o que foi removido, porque isso impacta automações e relatórios.

comments.message: Eu conectei a plataforma ao nosso sistema legado para sincronizar contatos, e desde então várias tags manuais que usávamos para segmentação desapareceram. Pelo que percebi, o conector está tratando a lista de tags como substituição completa em vez de adicionar valores novos. Preciso evitar novas perdas e recuperar o que foi removido, porque isso impacta automações e relatórios.

Entendi. Em integrações desse tipo, campos multivalorados como tags podem mesmo ser enviados em modo de sobrescrita. Como contenção imediata, pause a sincronização desse campo no conector ou desative a regra que mapeia 'tags' até confirmar o comportamento. Para recuperação, se você tiver uma exportação anterior ou histórico de auditoria, pode reimportar as tags perdidas por ID dos contatos.
Nós não temos exportação recente, e no histórico eu só vejo que o campo mudou, não a lista completa anterior.

Nesse caso, a recuperação total pode não ser possível apenas pela interface. Vou abrir uma escalada para verificar se conseguimos extrair valores anteriores dos logs da integração ou de backup interno. Enquanto isso, mantenha o mapeamento de tags desativado para impedir novas substituições, e separe alguns contatos de exemplo afetados para anexar ao chamado técnico.

subject: Kanban board columns won't load after changing custom field
description: I changed one of our deal custom fields from single-select to multi-select yesterday, and now the kanban board for that pipeline won't load. It just shows a spinner forever. List view still works. I tried switching pipelines and only the one using that field in board columns is affected. We need to use this board in a meeting later today.

comments.message: Our kanban board won't load after I changed the field type used for the columns. Is there a way to recover it?
That sounds like the browser may still have stale board data cached. Please clear your browser cache and reload the board.
I did that already, and two coworkers see the same endless spinner on different machines.
Then the board is likely still reindexing after the field change. Please wait a few hours and it should come back automatically.

subject: Need to split one shared admin account into individual users
description: Our team has been using one shared admin login for years, and we’re finally cleaning it up for audit reasons. I need to rename the shared account to one real employee, create two separate admin users for the others, and make sure user-management permissions are divided correctly by region. I’m worried about preserving activity history from the old shared account.

comments.message: The shared login is ops-admin@silverpeak.co. Going forward it should belong only to Maria. Then we need new admin-capable accounts for Ben and Aisha. Ben should manage EMEA users, and Aisha should manage APAC only. Can we do this without losing the old account history?

Yes. The cleanest option is to rename the existing ops-admin@silverpeak.co account to Maria’s individual email if she is the primary historical owner you want to retain, then create separate users for Ben and Aisha and assign regional user-administration roles. The historical activity will stay attached to the renamed account.
That’s what we want. Maria should be full Admin, Ben should be User Admin for EMEA, and Aisha should be User Admin for APAC. Can support make the account rename, or do I need to do it myself?

Support can’t perform the rename without an authorized admin request from the current account owner or billing contact, but you can do it from Admin > Users if you already have full admin rights. Once renamed, create Ben and Aisha as new users and assign the scoped roles. If you run into any validation errors during the split, send the three target emails and I can review the setup.

subject: Archived knowledge articles still appear in content usage charges
description: We migrated old help-center articles into the platform for reference, tagged them as archived, and later removed thousands that were outdated. Our content usage bill still looks inflated. I need help understanding which article states count toward billing and what data lifecycle practices we should follow for large documentation imports.

comments.message: We imported around 9,000 legacy articles with attachments, but only 2,000 are meant to stay published. Do archived articles and their files still count as billable content objects?

Published and archived articles both count toward content object limits, and attachments count toward storage whether the article is live or archived. Permanent deletion removes them from future usage calculations, but the billing tier for this cycle was based on the peak object count reached during the migration. That's why the current invoice is higher.
Understood. For the next migration wave, how should we stage the data so we can review it without causing another spike?

Use the sandbox knowledge base for the initial import and content review, then promote only approved articles to production in batches. Also, split attachments from article metadata and deduplicate files before import so the same PDF isn't uploaded repeatedly under multiple versions. I can't guarantee a credit for the current cycle, but I've documented the migration timeline and sent it to Billing for consideration.

subject: Can we transfer app ownership and keep the same API credentials?
description: I'm leaving the company next week and I currently own the private app our team uses to call your API. Security wants us to transfer everything to another admin account, but the engineering team is worried the client secret and existing tokens will stop working if we change ownership. Is there a supported way to hand off app ownership without rebuilding the integration from scratch?

comments.message: I own our private API app today, and I need to transfer it to another admin before my account is deactivated. We want zero downtime. Will ownership transfer preserve the client ID/secret?

Yes, you can transfer app ownership from the developer settings page. The client ID remains the same, but for security reasons some tenants are prompted to rotate the client secret during transfer. If your workspace shows that prompt, plan a short maintenance window because anything using the old secret will fail once the new one is saved.
We don't see a transfer option in developer settings, only 'delete app' and 'regenerate secret.' Is this feature hidden somewhere else?

You're right to call that out. In some plans there isn't a self-serve ownership transfer control for private apps. The workaround is either to keep the app in place until your account is deactivated later, or have support migrate ownership on the backend after both admins approve it. I can open that request for you if you send the app ID and the email of the receiving admin.

subject: SFTP export includes deleted users and fails after role cleanup
description: We have a nightly SFTP export that feeds our data warehouse. After we removed admin access from several users and deleted a batch of test accounts, the export started failing schema validation because those old users still appear in the file with partial records. I need help understanding whether deleted users are supposed to remain in exports for historical integrity, how to filter them out, and whether the SFTP integration credentials should be tied to an admin account or a service user.

comments.message: Our SFTP export started failing after a user cleanup. Deleted/test users still appear in the extract, and we also changed admin roles around the same time. Is that expected?

It can be expected, depending on the export type. Some data exports include inactive or deleted users as referenced entities so historical records remain relationally intact. Separately, if the export job is owned by a person whose admin role changed, permissions on the dataset may also have changed.
That makes sense. We need the warehouse feed to exclude deleted users going forward, but keep history in the app. Also, the export currently runs under my personal admin account.

Use a service account for the SFTP integration so role changes to an individual admin don't break the job. Then modify the export to filter on user_status = active if you're using the configurable dataset, or switch to the 'current state' export instead of the audit-preserving export if deleted users must be excluded. If you send the job ID, I can confirm which export mode you're on.

subject: Need credit for deleted sandbox and recovery of overwritten records
description: I deleted a sandbox environment on Monday because we were told it was no longer billable, but our new invoice still includes it. Worse, during a sync test before deletion, a batch import overwrote about 200 production contact records with old values from the sandbox export. We need help identifying whether those records can be restored and whether the sandbox charge can be reversed. This is pretty urgent because sales is working from bad data right now.

comments.message: Our invoice still shows the sandbox we deleted, and we also need help recovering around 200 production contact records that were overwritten from old sandbox data. Can you reverse the charge and restore the correct records?

I can help with both pieces. I checked the billing side first: the sandbox remained active for 3 days into the new billing cycle, so the invoice reflects a prorated environment fee rather than a full monthly charge. I've requested a courtesy credit because you were advised it would not be billable. For the overwritten contacts, I can see a bulk import job completed Monday at 10:14 AM UTC. Support cannot directly roll back production data, but I've escalated a point-in-time recovery review to our database team using that job ID.
Thanks. We only need the fields that were overwritten, not a full rollback. Is that part of the escalation?

Yes. I added that the preferred outcome is field-level restoration from the pre-import snapshot if available, to avoid affecting records updated afterward. Billing has already approved the courtesy credit, and the data recovery review is now pending with engineering. You'll get an update once they confirm whether selective restore is possible.

subject: Deaktivierter Mitarbeiter kann sich weiterhin anmelden
description: Ich bin Admin in unserem Workspace und habe gestern einen ausgeschiedenen Mitarbeiter deaktiviert. Laut Benutzerliste steht sein Konto auf „deaktiviert“, aber heute Morgen hat er sich trotzdem noch angemeldet und Kommentare in einem Ticket hinterlassen. Ich muss verstehen, warum das möglich ist und wie ich den Zugriff sofort vollständig sperre.

comments.message: Ich habe den Benutzer über Einstellungen > Benutzerverwaltung deaktiviert. Muss ich noch etwas anderes tun, damit wirklich keine Anmeldung mehr möglich ist?

Danke für die Details. Wenn ein Benutzer nur deaktiviert wird, bleiben bestehende Sitzungen unter Umständen noch kurz aktiv. Bitte wählen Sie in der Benutzerverwaltung den betreffenden Nutzer aus und klicken Sie zusätzlich auf „Sitzungen beenden“ oder „Von allen Geräten abmelden“, falls diese Option in Ihrem Plan verfügbar ist. Danach sollte kein weiterer Zugriff mehr möglich sein.
Die Option zum Abmelden habe ich gefunden und ausgeführt. Jetzt kommt er nicht mehr rein. Können Sie bestätigen, ob dadurch auch API-Tokens dieses Benutzers ungültig werden?

Ja, mit dem erzwungenen Abmelden werden aktive Web-Sitzungen beendet. Persönliche API-Tokens sollten Sie aber separat im Benutzerprofil unter „Sicherheit“ widerrufen, da diese nicht in jedem Fall automatisch entzogen werden. Ich sehe keinen Zugriff mehr über die UI, und zum vollständigen Entzug empfehle ich zusätzlich die Tokens manuell zu löschen.

subject: Refund request for failed automation import after add-on purchase
description: I purchased the workflow automation migration add-on so we could import 40 flows from our old workspace, but the import tool failed repeatedly and only brought over 11 of them. Billing still charged the full add-on fee, and now several imported workflows are missing paid actions we specifically bought the add-on to preserve. I’m requesting either a refund or for someone to complete the migration and make sure the billed automation features are actually usable.

comments.message: We bought the migration add-on this month to import our workflows, but most of the flows failed during import and some of the imported ones lost premium actions. We were charged the full fee anyway.

I’m sorry you ran into that. I reviewed the transaction and the add-on charge is valid because the migration feature was provisioned to the account. For the workflow issue, failed imports are often caused by unsupported actions or missing field mappings.
That doesn’t address the fact that we paid specifically to migrate these automations and your tool didn’t do it. Is there any credit available?

I can’t promise a refund, but I’ve escalated the case to our migrations team to inspect the failed workflow packages and to billing for a goodwill credit review. If you send the import job IDs, they can determine whether the failures were due to unsupported automation steps or an issue in the import service.

subject: Lista segmentada mostra empresas arquivadas
description: Eu criei uma lista dinâmica para campanhas com clientes ativos, mas ela continua incluindo empresas que já arquivei no mês passado. Quando abro os registros, o status aparece como arquivado, mas no segmento eles ainda contam. Já forcei a atualização da lista duas vezes. Preciso confiar nesse filtro porque o marketing vai exportar esses dados hoje.

comments.message: Eu criei uma lista dinâmica para campanhas com clientes ativos, mas ela continua incluindo empresas que já arquivei no mês passado. Quando abro os registros, o status aparece como arquivado, mas no segmento eles ainda contam. Já forcei a atualização da lista duas vezes.
Provavelmente é só atraso de cache. Aguarde algumas horas e atualize novamente, que a lista deve se corrigir sozinha.
Já faz mais de 24 horas. Inclusive exportei a lista e as empresas arquivadas ainda estão no arquivo.
Nesse caso, recomendo recriar a lista do zero. Pode haver algum problema no filtro antigo. Use 'status = ativo' e salve novamente.

subject: Tag updates from API not reflected in segment counts
description: We're using the API to update customer tags in bulk, and the record detail page shows the new tags correctly. The problem is that saved segments based on those tags still show the old counts, even after several hours. Since the raw data appears updated, I'm wondering if segmentation is reading from a stale index or if the tag writes are inconsistent.

comments.message: We're using the API to update customer tags in bulk, and the record detail page shows the new tags correctly. The problem is that saved segments based on those tags still show the old counts, even after several hours.

I reviewed your API logs and spot-checked several customer records. The tag updates were written successfully to the primary store, but the segment engine has not consumed those changes for a subset of records created before your April migration. That is a bug in our indexing pipeline, not an issue with your requests. I have restarted the affected index shard for your workspace, so counts should begin updating shortly.
Some segments updated, but one large segment is still off by about 1,200 records.

That remaining mismatch is tied to a separate backlog process for historical records. I've kicked off a full reindex, but it can take 6-8 hours on your dataset. If the count is still wrong tomorrow, reply here and I'll keep the case with engineering instead of auto-closing it.

subject: API returns 404 for users that clearly exist in the UI
description: I'm trying to automate role audits through your API. I can sign in to the app and see the users in the admin panel, but when I call GET /v1/users/{email} for several employees the API returns 404. It looks like an access problem, but the docs say admins can retrieve users. We recently moved everyone to SAML login, so I'm wondering whether account identifiers changed.

comments.message: For example, I can see maria.chen@company.com in the UI, but GET /v1/users/maria.chen@company.com returns not found. My token belongs to an admin in the same workspace.
The API may not support email addresses in that path parameter. Please try URL-encoding the @ sign and periods and see if that fixes it.
I already tried encoding. Still 404. Searching by name in the UI works, and the user definitely exists.

Understood. In your tenant, SAML is set to use immutable external IDs as the primary user key, and the /v1/users/{id} endpoint expects the platform user ID, not email. Your admin login access is fine; this is just the wrong identifier for the API route. Use GET /v1/users?email=maria.chen@company.com first, then call the detail endpoint with the returned user ID.

subject: CSV import created duplicate companies and mismatched ownership
description: I imported a cleanup file to update company records, but instead of updating existing companies, the system created duplicates for around 40 accounts. Some of the duplicates also got assigned to the wrong account owner. I need help figuring out whether this happened because of our import mapping or because matching failed on your side, and I need a safe way to merge or correct the data without losing notes and activities.

comments.message: I imported a cleanup file to update company records, but instead of updating existing companies, the system created duplicates for around 40 accounts. Some of the duplicates also got assigned to the wrong account owner. I need help figuring out whether this happened because of our import mapping or because matching failed on your side, and I need a safe way to merge or correct the data without losing notes and activities.
Usually duplicates happen when the import file contains new rows. You can just delete the extra company records and reimport with the correct owner column.

That doesn’t really answer the matching question. We mapped the external ID field specifically to avoid new records, and deleting duplicates manually could remove linked notes. Is there a way to verify whether the update key was ignored?

If the import completed, the mapping was probably fine. We don't have a way to inspect that after the fact from support, so your best option is to export everything, clean it offline, and import again.

subject: Sincronização incremental perdeu atualizações após mudança de fuso
description: Nosso job incremental usa o campo updated_at da API para carregar alterações em contas e negócios para o banco analítico. Depois da mudança de fuso do nosso servidor, percebemos que várias atualizações de uma janela de cerca de 1 hora não entraram no pipeline. Precisamos recuperar esses dados sem duplicar o restante e confirmar se a API sempre retorna timestamps em UTC ou se há alguma dependência de configuração da conta.

comments.message: Guardávamos o último horário processado em horário local e convertíamos antes da chamada. Acho que aí começou o problema.

Olá! Os timestamps da API são retornados em UTC, então o ideal é armazenar e comparar sempre em UTC no seu pipeline. Para recuperar a lacuna, reexecute a carga desde antes da janela afetada usando uma margem de segurança e faça upsert por id no banco de destino.
Perfeito. Existe risco de perder updates com o mesmo segundo exato do checkpoint?

Sim, se você usar um corte estritamente maior que o último timestamp processado. Para evitar isso, reutilize o último checkpoint com overlap de alguns minutos e deduplique por id + updated_at no destino. Se preferir, posso também compartilhar um exemplo de estratégia de watermark para evitar esse tipo de falha em cargas incrementais.

subject: Timezone normalization changed event dates in attendance summary
description: We cleaned up our event data by converting all timestamps to UTC in a bulk update, and the raw records now look standardized. Since then, the monthly attendance summary has shifted some sessions into the previous day, which changes our totals by month. I need help understanding whether the report groups by raw UTC timestamp or by the event's local timezone setting, because right now the reporting output no longer matches what operations expects.

comments.message: After normalizing event timestamps to UTC, our monthly attendance report moved some sessions into the wrong month. How does reporting bucket those dates?
Reports always use the timezone configured on the underlying event record, so changing the timestamp format alone should not affect the month grouping.
But many of these records don't have a local timezone field populated. They only have the UTC timestamp now.

I dug deeper and you found the edge case: when the local timezone is blank, the attendance summary falls back to UTC for date bucketing. That's why late-night sessions crossed month boundaries after your cleanup. You can either backfill the local timezone field or add a derived local date column and point the report to that.

subject: API-created tickets are skipping our assignment automation
description: We have a workflow that auto-assigns new tickets to a regional queue when the priority is high and the custom field "source_system" is "erp". When tickets are created manually in the UI, the workflow runs correctly. When we create the same ticket through the API, the assignment step never fires, even though the payload includes both values. Can you help us figure out what's different about API-created records?

comments.message: I compared a UI-created ticket and an API-created ticket. Both show priority=high and source_system=erp afterward, but only the UI-created one triggers the automation that routes it to EMEA Support.

This usually happens when the workflow trigger is set to 'record created' and your API request is populating fields after creation in a second call. In that case, the automation evaluates before source_system is present. Can you confirm whether you're sending everything in one POST or creating first and then PATCHing fields?
We are doing a POST to create the ticket, then a PATCH about 2 seconds later to attach metadata from our ERP once we have the mapping.

That explains it. Your current workflow only checks conditions at creation time. To cover API-created tickets, either include source_system in the initial POST or add a second automation triggered on record updated when source_system changes to erp and assignee is blank. If you want, I can share a sample rule structure for that updated workflow.

subject: Usage charges spiked after failed bulk archive job
description: We ran a bulk archive job to reduce active customer records before month-end, but the job failed midway and now our usage report shows more active records than before. Billing used that count and charged us for the higher tier. In addition, some records are stuck in a limbo state where they do not appear in normal views but still show up in exports. We need someone to untangle the data state and review the invoice.

comments.message: Our bulk archive job failed, and now we're billed for a higher active-record tier. Some records are hidden in the app but still included in exports. Can this be fixed?

A failed archive job should not increase active usage, so I checked the job logs and found multiple retry attempts that left a subset of records marked inconsistently across services. I've escalated the data repair to engineering with the affected job ID. On billing, I've requested a temporary hold on the usage overage while they confirm the correct active-record count for month-end.
Do you have any estimate? We need to close the books this week.

Engineering's SLA on data-state corrections is 2 business days for issues affecting billing. I can't guarantee completion before your close, but the invoice is now annotated as under review and finance can reference that internal note. If engineering confirms the archive failure inflated your count, billing will re-rate the charges.

subject: Rechnung als bezahlt markiert, aber Mahnung erhalten
description: In unserem Portal ist die Februar-Rechnung als bezahlt markiert. Heute haben wir trotzdem eine automatische Mahnung wegen genau dieser Rechnung bekommen. Unsere Buchhaltung ist verunsichert, ob eine weitere Zahlung notwendig ist. Können Sie klären, was hier stimmt?

comments.message: In unserem Portal ist die Februar-Rechnung als bezahlt markiert. Heute haben wir trotzdem eine automatische Mahnung wegen genau dieser Rechnung bekommen. Unsere Buchhaltung ist verunsichert, ob eine weitere Zahlung notwendig ist. Können Sie klären, was hier stimmt?

Danke für den Hinweis. Ich habe nachgesehen: Die Rechnung ist in unserem Billing-System bezahlt, aber die Mahnung wurde offenbar aus einem verzögerten Erinnerungs-Workflow versendet. Sie müssen keine weitere Zahlung leisten. Ich habe den Mahnlauf für Ihr Konto gestoppt und den Vorfall an unser Finanzteam gemeldet, damit die Automatisierung geprüft wird.

subject: Automação não dispara após mudar a etapa do funil
description: Eu configurei uma automação para enviar um e-mail quando o negócio entra na etapa "Proposta enviada", mas desde ontem nada está sendo disparado. Já revisei o fluxo e ele continua ativo. Preciso entender se é erro de configuração ou algum problema na plataforma.

comments.message: A automação está publicada e o gatilho é "quando etapa do negócio muda para Proposta enviada". Testei com 3 negócios e nenhum entrou no fluxo.

Olá! Verifiquei os pontos mais comuns. Quando a etapa é alterada por importação em massa ou por API, alguns gatilhos podem não disparar se o evento estiver configurado apenas para alterações manuais. Pode confirmar como esses negócios foram movidos de etapa?
Foram movidos por uma regra automática de pontuação, não manualmente.

Entendi. Nesse caso, o comportamento faz sentido: seu gatilho atual está ouvindo apenas mudança direta de etapa. Para funcionar com a regra automática, edite a automação e troque o gatilho para "negócio atualizado" com a condição "etapa = Proposta enviada". Também recomendo desativar e reativar o fluxo depois de salvar. Fiz um teste na minha conta interna e esse cenário dispara normalmente assim.

subject: Deleted admin account still appears in approval routing
description: I deleted a regional admin account last month, but that person still appears as an approver in our user access workflow and some requests are getting stuck. I can manage users, but I can’t remove the old admin from the approval routing screen because it says the account is inactive and locked. We need this cleaned up or escalated because approvals are backing up.

comments.message: A deleted admin is still showing up in our approval routing and requests are getting stuck with that inactive user. I can’t remove them from the workflow.
If the user is inactive, they shouldn’t affect approvals. You can ignore the old approver and the requests should move automatically.
They are not moving automatically. We have six pending requests right now waiting on someone who left the company.

Understood. I should have been more careful there. Since this appears to be a workflow reference to an inactive user admin, I’m escalating it to our workflow support team to remove the stale approver mapping and review the blocked requests. Please send one or two example request IDs so they can trace the issue faster.

subject: SSO users locked out after domain verification change
description: I updated our company domain verification yesterday, and since then about 40 employees can’t log in through SSO. They get bounced back to the login page with no clear error. I already tried reassigning a few users and clearing browser cache, but nothing changed. We need access restored today because our support team works entirely in your app.

comments.message: We changed our verified domain from old brand to new brand yesterday afternoon. Since then, users who were logging in via Okta are stuck in a loop and never reach the dashboard.

Thanks for the details. I checked your workspace and can see successful IdP handshakes for some attempts, but the application is rejecting the post-auth session for users mapped to the previous domain. This looks tied to the org-level identity configuration, which I can’t safely modify from frontline support.
Can you undo whatever changed? Our agents are blocked right now.

I’ve escalated this to our Identity Engineering team as a high-priority access incident and attached your SSO logs, affected domain names, and impacted user count. While they review, a temporary workaround is to invite critical users with password login on the new domain if your security policy allows it. I’ll update this ticket as soon as engineering responds.

subject: SCIM-created user has no product access
description: Our identity team provisioned a new employee via SCIM this morning, and the account shows up in your admin console, but she can’t access any modules after login. She gets a blank home page with no apps listed. I need to know whether this is a provisioning issue or a role assignment problem on your side.

comments.message: Our identity team provisioned a new employee via SCIM this morning, and the account shows up in your admin console, but she can’t access any modules after login. She gets a blank home page with no apps listed. I need to know whether this is a provisioning issue or a role assignment problem on your side.

Thanks — that symptom usually means the user record was created successfully, but no product role or group mapped over from SCIM. In Admin > Users, please open her account and check the Access tab for a role assignment. If it says No access, review your SCIM group-to-role mapping or manually assign a default role to confirm the issue.
You were right, it says No access. We expected everyone in Okta group 'Employees-App' to get the Standard User role, but apparently that didn’t happen for her.

That points to the SCIM mapping rather than authentication. Please verify that she is in the exact provisioning group synced to our app and that the role-mapping rule for 'Employees-App' is active. As a workaround, you can manually assign Standard User now so she can work today, then have your identity team correct the mapping for future users.

subject: New hires can’t accept invitations after domain verification
description: Since we verified our company domain yesterday, all new hire invitations are landing on the signup page with an error saying the account can’t be joined. Existing users are fine. I’m an org admin and this is blocking onboarding for six people starting this morning.

comments.message: Since we verified our company domain yesterday, all new hire invitations are landing on the signup page with an error saying the account can’t be joined. Existing users are fine. I’m an org admin and this is blocking onboarding for six people starting this morning.

Sorry about that. When this happens after domain verification, it is often because auto-join is enabled alongside invitation-only restrictions. Please go to Admin > Security and check whether both 'Allow users with verified domain to join automatically' and 'Require admin invitation' are turned on. That combination can cause invite redemption conflicts for new users.
Yes, both are on. If I turn off auto-join, will that affect current users signing in through SSO?

Current users will still be able to sign in normally through SSO. Turning off auto-join only stops new accounts from trying to self-provision during invite redemption. Please disable auto-join, resend one invitation to test, and if it works you can resend the rest. If you prefer to keep auto-join on, I can escalate this because the invite flow should handle that combination better.

subject: Okta SSO test passes, but users still get provisioning errors
description: We set up the Okta integration for SSO and user provisioning. The SSO test succeeds, but when new users are assigned in Okta they show up as failed in your provisioning log with a generic schema error. I need help troubleshooting the integration and understanding what attributes are required so we can finish the rollout.

comments.message: Login through Okta works, but SCIM provisioning fails for every new user with "invalid schema." What attributes does your app require, and where should I look?

If SSO works but SCIM fails, the issue is usually with the provisioning payload rather than authentication. Our SCIM endpoint requires a primary email, given name, family name, and an active value. In Okta, open the app's Provisioning tab and compare the attribute mappings there, especially if you've customized the username format or removed name fields from the profile mapping.
We had firstName mapped, but lastName was blank for contractors. I fixed that for one test user, and provisioning worked. Is there a way to stop the rest from failing in bulk?

Yes. After updating the mapping, run a selective push or reassign the affected users so Okta resends valid profiles. For customer education going forward, I'd recommend adding a pre-deployment check: assign a pilot group first and verify the required SCIM attributes are populated before enabling provisioning for the full org.

subject: Endpoint de utilizadores devolve resultados incompletos após ativar filtragem por equipa
description: Configurei no produto uma segmentação para que cada equipa veja apenas os seus utilizadores, e desde então a nossa integração com o endpoint /users só devolve parte da base. O problema é que a integração é administrativa e deveria continuar a ver tudo. Não encontrei uma opção clara para excluir a API dessa regra.

comments.message: Configurei no produto uma segmentação para que cada equipa veja apenas os seus utilizadores, e desde então a nossa integração com o endpoint /users só devolve parte da base. O problema é que a integração é administrativa e deveria continuar a ver tudo. Não encontrei uma opção clara para excluir a API dessa regra.

Quando ativa filtragem por equipa, isso afeta também a API. O mais comum é usar um utilizador técnico com perfil sem restrição de equipa. No seu caso, a chave atual está ligada ao perfil "Supervisor Lisboa", que herda a segmentação regional. Criei um novo perfil de serviço sem filtro e associei-o a uma nova credencial de API em ambiente de teste.
Percebi. Ainda não posso trocar a credencial hoje porque depende da equipa de segurança. Há alguma solução temporária?

Temporariamente, não consigo remover a restrição apenas para essa chave sem alterar o modelo de permissões da conta. Vou encaminhar este caso para a equipa de produto para confirmar se existe uma exceção suportada, mas a abordagem recomendada continua a ser a credencial dedicada.

subject: Workflow dupliziert Aufgaben bei erneuter Datensatzbearbeitung
description: Ich habe eine Automatisierung, die bei neuen Opportunities automatisch eine Qualifizierungsaufgabe erstellt. Wenn jemand den Datensatz später noch einmal bearbeitet, wird die Aufgabe teilweise erneut erstellt, obwohl sie nur einmal entstehen sollte. Das führt zu doppelten To-dos im Vertrieb. Können Sie mir sagen, wie ich das verhindere?

comments.message: Der Trigger ist aktuell "wenn Opportunity-Status = Neu". Sobald jemand noch ein anderes Feld ändert, kommt manchmal eine zweite Aufgabe dazu, obwohl der Status unverändert bleibt.

In dem Fall wird der Workflow wahrscheinlich bei jeder Aktualisierung neu ausgewertet und erfüllt weiterhin die Bedingung "Status = Neu". Um Duplikate zu vermeiden, sollten Sie entweder nur auf die Statusänderung nach "Neu" triggern oder vor der Aufgabenerstellung prüfen, ob bereits eine Qualifizierungsaufgabe existiert.
Eine Prüfung auf bestehende Aufgaben haben wir noch nicht. Reicht die Änderung auf "wenn Status zu Neu geändert wird" aus?

Das reduziert die Duplikate in der Regel deutlich, schützt aber nicht vor manuellen Rücksetzungen oder Re-Imports. Die sauberste Lösung ist die Kombination aus Änderungs-Trigger und einer Guard-Bedingung wie "Aufgabe noch nicht vorhanden" oder einem Marker-Feld nach erfolgreicher Erstellung.

subject: Metered overage invoice spikes after export job loop
description: Our overage invoice for API calls is about four times higher than normal. The spike lines up with a failed bulk export that kept restarting from your UI after throwing an error, and we suspect those repeated retries were counted as billable calls. The job history page is inconsistent too—sometimes it shows one failed export, sometimes eight attempts. Can you investigate whether a product bug inflated our usage billing and adjust the invoice if so?

comments.message: Our overage invoice for API calls is about four times higher than normal. The spike lines up with a failed bulk export that kept restarting from your UI after throwing an error, and we suspect those repeated retries were counted as billable calls. The job history page is inconsistent too—sometimes it shows one failed export, sometimes eight attempts. Can you investigate whether a product bug inflated our usage billing and adjust the invoice if so?

I reviewed the usage logs and can confirm there were multiple export job retries generated by the platform within a short window, not manually initiated by your team. The retry behavior appears tied to an internal error loop, and the metering service counted those calls toward overage.
That would explain the invoice. Can the excess overage be credited back?

I can’t post the credit directly, but I’ve escalated this with a recommendation to remove the retry-generated overage charges from invoice INV-48219. I also filed a bug with our data exports team including the inconsistent job-history records and metering impact. Billing ops will review the adjustment request and reply once they’ve recalculated the affected usage.

subject: Old integration user can sign in, but its API token stopped seeing all workspaces
description: We have a legacy integration user attached to multiple workspaces. The user can still log into the main app and switch between workspaces in the UI, but the API token now only returns data from one workspace and gives 403 on the others. Nothing changed in our code. We did clean up some user access assignments last week, so maybe that affected API visibility.

comments.message: The token is used with the X-Workspace header for each workspace. Since Friday, one workspace still works and the other three return forbidden. The integration user itself can see all four in the UI.

I checked the account and the integration user's login access is still valid across all four workspaces. However, the token you're using was issued before the cross-workspace API setting was deprecated. New authorization now evaluates workspace membership plus explicit API app approval per workspace.
So do we just need to mint a new token?

A new token is part of it, but not sufficient by itself. Each affected workspace owner must approve API access for that integration user under Workspace Settings > API Clients. I don't have permission to do that on their behalf, so this remains pending until those approvals are completed. Once approved, generate a fresh token and the X-Workspace calls should work again.

subject: Need example for creating records and triggering multi-step automation via API
description: We're launching a partner portal and need to create deal records through the API in a way that reliably kicks off our qualification workflow, then waits for a webhook from our scoring service, then resumes automation. I understand the individual features, but I'm struggling with the end-to-end pattern: what fields should be set on create, how to avoid race conditions, and how to resume the workflow after the external system responds?

comments.message: Today we POST the deal, then our workflow sends a webhook to the scoring service, but if the score comes back very quickly our follow-up API update can arrive before the workflow has populated some internal fields. That causes the next automation step to miss its conditions.

A robust pattern is to initialize the record through one API create call with all fields needed for the first workflow branch, including a status like scoring_pending=true. Let the workflow send the outbound webhook, and have your scoring service write back the result through the API to dedicated fields like score_status and score_value. Then trigger the next automation step off score_status changing to completed, not off timing assumptions.
Would you also recommend a correlation ID between our outbound webhook and the callback?

Definitely. Store your external request ID on the deal when you create it or when the webhook is sent, and require the callback API update to include that same correlation ID. That gives your workflow a reliable handoff point, avoids race conditions, and makes retries easier to deduplicate.

subject: Automation branch using formula field chooses wrong path
description: I have a workflow that branches based on a formula field called Risk Tier, but some enterprise customers are being sent down the low-risk path even though the formula shows High on the record. This is causing the wrong onboarding tasks to be created. I need help understanding why the workflow isn't using the value I see on the page.

comments.message: The workflow runs when an account is updated. The branch condition is Risk Tier = High, then create enhanced onboarding tasks. But in the logs, some accounts clearly showing High still went to the default branch. The field is a formula based on ARR and security questionnaire status.

Workflow evaluations can happen before formula recalculation finishes, especially when multiple dependent fields update at once. A common fix is to add a short delay before the branch or trigger the workflow off a stable field after the formula has recalculated. You can also log the formula output into a plain text or select field via a separate automation and branch on that stored value instead.
Adding a 5-minute delay worked in my test account, although it's not ideal.

That makes sense. It's a workaround rather than a perfect fix, but it confirms the issue is timing around formula evaluation. If you want, our product team can review whether this specific formula field is recalculating unusually slowly in your environment.

subject: Webhook to our ERP returns 200 but records never appear
description: We configured your outbound webhook integration to send new orders to our ERP endpoint. Your delivery log shows HTTP 200 for every request, but many of those orders never show up in the ERP. We need help troubleshooting whether the payload format or retry behavior is causing data loss.

comments.message: I compared two orders from yesterday: both show delivered on your side, but only one exists in our ERP. The missing one had a null value in the 'discount_code' field.
A 200 response means our webhook delivered successfully, so the issue is definitely on your ERP side.
Maybe, but our ERP team says their endpoint returns 200 with a warning body when optional fields are malformed. Does your system inspect response bodies or only status codes?

We only treat non-2xx status codes as failures, so warning bodies would not trigger retries. If the ERP silently rejects records when optional fields are null or incorrectly typed, that would explain the gap. As a workaround, you can update the webhook template to omit null fields, but you'll need your ERP team to confirm the accepted schema.

subject: Changement de nom de l’organisation et widgets de rapport cassés
description: Nous avons changé le nom légal de notre société et je souhaite mettre à jour le nom de l’organisation dans la plateforme ainsi que les informations du compte. Depuis ce changement dans nos systèmes internes, deux widgets de notre tableau de bord personnalisé affichent "erreur de configuration" alors que les autres graphiques fonctionnent. J’ai besoin d’aide pour la mise à jour du compte et pour réparer les rapports avant une réunion client demain.

comments.message: Bonjour, nous devons remplacer l’ancien nom de société sur le compte par notre nouveau nom légal. Depuis cette semaine, deux widgets de notre tableau de bord personnalisé ne chargent plus et affichent une erreur de configuration.

Bonjour, le changement de nom de l’organisation peut être fait depuis Paramètres > Compte si vous avez les droits admin. Pour les widgets, c’est probablement un souci temporaire d’affichage ; essayez de recréer le tableau de bord.

Je n’ai justement pas les droits suffisants pour modifier le nom au niveau du compte, seulement au niveau de mon profil. Et recréer le tableau de bord me ferait perdre les filtres et commentaires partagés.

Je comprends. Merci de m’envoyer le nouveau nom légal exact et l’URL de votre espace ; je peux demander la mise à jour côté compte si vos droits sont limités. Pour les widgets, j’ai regardé plus précisément : ils pointent vers des champs calculés liés à l’ancien nom d’organisation dans votre source personnalisée. Je n’ai pas la main pour corriger la requête moi-même, donc j’escalade au support reporting avancé. En attendant, n’effacez pas le tableau de bord existant ; exportez plutôt la vue fonctionnelle des autres widgets pour votre réunion de demain.

subject: Best practice for teaching our team to use approval rules
description: I'm the operations lead for our company and I'm onboarding a new group of approvers. I understand the basics of approval rules, but I don't know the best way to explain the difference between sequential and parallel approvals to non-technical users. Also, one of our rules seems to be skipping the finance approver occasionally, which makes me nervous about training people on the wrong process.

comments.message: Can you give me a simple explanation I can reuse in training, and can you tell me why a finance approver might be skipped?
Sure — a simple way to teach it is: sequential approvals happen one after another in a fixed order, while parallel approvals notify everyone at once and any one approval completes the request.
I don't think that second part is right for our workflow. In our test, all approvers in the parallel stage had to approve before the request moved on.

You're correct, and I misspoke. In our platform, parallel means all approvers in that stage are asked at the same time, but the stage doesn't complete until the configured approval condition is met — in most accounts that's all approvers, unless the rule is set differently. A finance approver may be skipped if the rule has conditional logic based on amount, department, or form field values. I don't want to guess which condition is causing it, so I'm escalating this to our workflow specialists to review the specific rule and send you an accurate explanation you can use in training.

subject: Dashboard widget shows different totals than underlying report
description: One of our executive dashboard widgets is counting 327 open cases, but when I click through to the report behind it I only get 311. We need to understand whether the widget cache is wrong or if the filters are applied differently. Please investigate and also explain how dashboard widgets refresh and inherit report filters.

comments.message: We changed the report filter yesterday to exclude spam and merged tickets. The report updated, but the dashboard tile still seems inflated this morning.

I confirmed the mismatch in your account. The widget is still reading a cached query definition from before yesterday's filter change, while the report itself is using the current filters. That should not happen after a report edit, so I've filed it as a dashboard caching bug.
Can we force it to refresh? Leadership uses that dashboard every morning.

Yes. Removing the widget and re-adding the same report should force a fresh query binding. For future reference, widgets normally inherit the report's saved filters at the time the widget is linked, and they refresh on the dashboard cache interval rather than every page load. After a report edit, the link is supposed to update automatically, which is the part failing here.

subject: Billed for custom objects after archiving the feature setup
description: We experimented with custom objects for asset tracking and then archived the object type and removed the related sidebar layout. Even though nobody is using it now, we're still being billed for the custom objects package. I need to know whether archiving the configuration should have stopped the charge or if the subscription is separate.

comments.message: The object type is archived and the forms referencing it are gone. Finance is asking why the add-on is still on the invoice.
Archiving the object type only hides it from active use; it doesn't cancel the custom objects package. That add-on remains billed until it's removed from your subscription settings.
Can you remove it effective this month? We don't need it anymore.

I can see the package is still provisioned, but I don't have permission to cancel mid-cycle from support. I've flagged the account for non-renewal of the add-on and sent billing a request to review whether a partial credit is possible since the configuration was archived shortly after setup.

subject: Marketo leads not appearing in attribution report after workspace merge
description: We merged two Marketo workspaces and since then our attribution report in your platform has dropped a large set of leads from one business unit. The leads are visible when searched individually, so the integration is clearly bringing in something, but the report breakdowns are no longer trustworthy. I need help understanding what changed and whether your team can restore the report or escalate it.

comments.message: Ever since our Marketo workspace merge on the 1st, the attribution report undercounts EMEA leads by about 20%. We have a quarterly review in two days.

I can see the Marketo integration switched several records to a new workspace ID after the merge. Your attribution report still filters on the old workspace dimension for EMEA, so those leads are excluded from the summary even though they're searchable.
Can you just swap the filter to the new ID?

I changed the filter on the main report and refreshed the dataset, so current EMEA totals should be much closer now. That said, multi-touch attribution for historical campaigns is still split across old and new workspace IDs, and I don't want to merge those dimensions manually from support because it could affect your audit trail. I've escalated the historical reconciliation piece to our reporting team.

subject: API token works in staging but returns 401 in production
description: I'm trying to connect our internal order system to your API. The exact same script works against our staging workspace, but in production every request to /v1/customers comes back 401 Unauthorized. I can log into the web app fine with my admin account, so I'm not sure if this is an API issue or an access issue. We rotated the token yesterday and since then production has been broken.

comments.message: We're using a server-side API token generated under my admin user. Staging still works, production does not. The error body says "token invalid or scope missing." Can you confirm whether production tokens require something different?

I checked your account and your production workspace has SSO enforced plus IP allowlisting enabled for API access. Your user can still log into the web app through SSO, but the personal token you generated yesterday is being blocked because it was created before the "Public API" permission was re-enabled on your role. Please create a new token from Production > Settings > Developers while logged into the production workspace, and make sure your role includes API:read for customers.
I created a new token and now the 401 changed to 403 on some endpoints. /v1/customers works, but /v1/users/{id}/roles says forbidden.

That means authentication is fixed and it's now a permissions issue. The token is valid, but your custom admin role doesn't include user-management API scopes in production. I've updated the role to include API:read:users and API:read:roles for your workspace owner to review. Once they approve the role change, those endpoints should work. In the meantime your integration can use /v1/customers successfully.

subject: Nie mam dostępu po połączeniu dwóch kont użytkownika
description: Mieliśmy dwa moje konta w systemie i administrator poprosił o ich scalenie pod jednym nowym adresem e-mail. Po tej zmianie nie mogę się zalogować, a dodatkowo współpracownicy nie mogą mnie dodać do projektów. Wygląda, jakby moje konto było w połowie aktywne.

comments.message: Czy możecie sprawdzić, które konto zostało zachowane i czy da się przywrócić dostęp bez utraty historii?

Widzę, że podczas scalania zachowano historię ze starszego konta, ale rekord logowania został przypięty do drugiego profilu. To powoduje konflikt dostępu. Przepnę login na właściwy rekord, ale synchronizacja członkostw w projektach może potrwać do godziny.
Najważniejsze, żebym mógł się zalogować jeszcze teraz.

Naprawiłem powiązanie loginu i wysłałem link ustawienia hasła na nowy adres. Powinien Pan już wejść do konta, natomiast brakujące projekty mogą pojawiać się stopniowo, zanim zakończy się pełna synchronizacja.

subject: Potrzebuję przenieść właściciela konta na inną osobę
description: Od przyszłego miesiąca nie będę już zarządzać naszym zespołem i muszę przekazać konto nowemu administratorowi. Dodałam go jako zwykłego użytkownika, ale nie widzę opcji przekazania własności konta. Nie chcę stracić historii ustawień ani integracji.

comments.message: Chciałabym przekazać rolę właściciela konta koledze z zespołu, adres: p.mazurek@firma.pl. Obecnie ma tylko zwykłe konto użytkownika. Gdzie mogę to zmienić?
Wystarczy, że usunie Pani swoje konto i kolega automatycznie przejmie własność konta.
To brzmi ryzykownie. Nie chcę usuwać swojego konta, dopóki nie mam pewności, że rozliczenia i integracje zostaną bez zmian. Czy jest oficjalna procedura?
Nie mamy osobnej funkcji transferu właściciela. Najlepiej zostawić jak jest albo utworzyć nowe konto dla kolegi od zera.

subject: NetSuite integration imports invoices with wrong currency
description: We connected NetSuite to sync invoices into your billing dashboard, but invoices from our UK subsidiary are coming in as USD instead of GBP. The amounts are numerically identical, so it looks like the currency code is being dropped or overwritten rather than converted. I need help troubleshooting where the mapping is going wrong.

comments.message: This affects only one subsidiary. US invoices look correct. We added a custom form in NetSuite last week for the UK team, and that may be related.

Thanks, that detail is helpful. If only one subsidiary is affected after a NetSuite form change, the integration may be reading a different transaction form or field set where the currency field is not exposed in the same way. Please confirm whether the UK invoice form still includes the standard currency field and whether your saved search or integration role can access it.
The standard currency field is on the form, but our NetSuite admin says the integration role for that subsidiary has more limited permissions. Could that make the sync default to USD?

Yes, that's plausible. If the role cannot read the subsidiary-specific currency value, the importer may fall back to the account default currency. Please have your NetSuite admin grant the integration role access to the transaction currency field for the UK subsidiary, then resync one affected invoice. If the issue persists after permissions are corrected, we'll need to inspect the raw payload from NetSuite.

subject: Imported custom fields not visible and new hires can't access workspace
description: We added several custom fields to accounts and imported values in bulk, but our new hires can't see those fields after they log in. One of them also gets bounced back to the workspace selector every time. Admins can see the imported data just fine. I need help sorting out whether this is a field permission problem or a login bug.

comments.message: The import completed successfully for custom fields like Renewal Segment and Territory Notes. New users created this week can log in, but they either don't see those fields or get stuck returning to the workspace chooser.

Thanks. Since admins can see the imported values, the data itself is likely present. For the new hires, please confirm they were assigned the same role profile as existing account managers, because custom field visibility can be role-based. The workspace selector loop can also happen if the user belongs to multiple workspaces but lacks a default one.
I checked one account and they were missing the Account Manager role group and had no default workspace. After fixing both, the fields appeared and the loop stopped.

Perfect — that confirms the import was successful and the problem was with access configuration for newly provisioned users. If you have more affected hires, assign the correct role group and set a default workspace before they next sign in.

subject: API timestamps shifted by one hour after DST change
description: Since the daylight saving time change this weekend, our integration is creating scheduled callbacks one hour late. We read timestamps from your API and assumed they were in our account time zone. Can you help troubleshoot the source of the offset?

comments.message: The API returns values like `2026-03-29T09:00:00Z`, and we display them to agents in Europe/London. After the DST transition, those callbacks show at 10:00 locally, but our code still schedules them for 09:00 local time.

The key detail is the trailing `Z`: all API timestamps are returned in UTC, regardless of your account display time zone. Around DST transitions, converting by fixed offset will be wrong. You need to parse the timestamp as UTC and then convert using an IANA time zone such as `Europe/London`, not assume the account zone is baked into the payload.
Understood. We were stripping the `Z` and treating it as naive local time. We'll change that and rerun the affected jobs.
That should fix the one-hour drift. If you need to audit past mistakes, compare the original UTC values from the API with the execution timestamps in your scheduler around the DST boundary.

subject: Annual renewal processed early and broke our approval workflows
description: Our contract wasn’t supposed to renew until next month, but finance saw the annual billing renewal go through this week. Around the same time, our approval workflows started throwing plan entitlement errors on steps that use custom conditions, as if the account was moved to a different tier. I need someone to verify the billing renewal date, confirm whether our workflow automation entitlements changed, and restore the features we had before this happened.

comments.message: We were charged for annual renewal 26 days early, and now several approval workflows say our plan does not support advanced conditions. Nothing was changed on our side.

I’m sorry about that. I can see a renewal invoice was generated after an amendment synced from your sales order, but I’m not able to verify the contract dates from support. On the automation side, the logs show entitlement checks failing for steps that require the Advanced Automation package.
We definitely had that package last week, and no one approved any downgrade.

Understood. I’ve escalated this to both Billing Operations and Account Support because it looks like the early renewal may have applied the wrong product bundle. I can’t restore entitlements manually, but I’ve marked it as urgent since your production workflows are blocked.

subject: Google Ads integration spending report delayed and inconsistent
description: Our spend report tied to the Google Ads integration has been inconsistent all week. Yesterday's campaign spend appeared this morning, then dropped again in the afternoon refresh, and some campaigns are missing conversions entirely. We use this report for daily pacing decisions, so I need a clear explanation and either a fix or an escalation path.

comments.message: The Google Ads spend report is unstable. Totals changed three times today, and campaign-level conversion columns are blank for several active campaigns.
That sounds like normal ad platform latency. Google Ads often backfills metrics, so changing totals are expected.
Expected maybe, but blank conversions for active campaigns are not normal. This only started after we added a second ad account.

I checked a sample of the imported records and it looks like the second Google Ads account is using a different conversion action schema. Our integration is syncing both accounts, but the report is still mapped to the original account's conversion fields, so the new account contributes spend without populating those conversion columns. I don't have permission to alter cross-account reporting mappings, so I'm escalating this to our ad integrations specialists. For now, use the account-specific spend report rather than the consolidated one.

subject: Need to change account email domain after acquisition
description: Our company was acquired, and all staff emails are moving from brightlane.io to northpeak.com next week. I need to understand the safest way to update user accounts in your system so people can still log in, keep their histories, and not end up with duplicate profiles. Does this need to be done one by one, or is there a bulk option?

comments.message: Our company was acquired, and all staff emails are moving from brightlane.io to northpeak.com next week. I need to understand the safest way to update user accounts in your system so people can still log in, keep their histories, and not end up with duplicate profiles. Does this need to be done one by one, or is there a bulk option?

For a domain migration, the right approach depends on whether you use password login, Google sign-in, or SSO. Individual users can often change their own email addresses from their profile, but admins may also have a bulk-edit option under user management on some plans. To avoid duplicate accounts, don't invite users under the new addresses until the old profiles are updated or mapped through your identity provider. User history stays with the profile as long as the existing account is updated rather than replaced.
We use Azure AD SSO for everyone. Should we change the emails in your app first or in Azure?

With Azure AD SSO, you generally want the identity provider changes coordinated with the app so the matching attribute remains consistent. Because the exact behavior depends on whether your SSO mapping uses email, NameID, or another immutable identifier, I'm escalating this to our identity team. They can tell you the correct sequence and whether your workspace supports a bulk remap.

subject: After MFA enrollment, API PAT creation returns 500
description: I enrolled in MFA this afternoon and now, while I can log in normally with the authenticator code, creating a personal access token throws a 500 error every time. The browser console shows a failed call to /api/me/tokens, and one attempt also forced me back to the login page. Is this a known issue?
