comments.message: MFA sign-in itself works. The problem starts after login when I go to Personal Access Tokens and click Generate. Spinner for a few seconds, then 500. Once, it redirected me to login after the error. Our CI job is blocked because I can't issue a replacement token.
500 errors on token creation are usually caused by a malformed token name. Please try a shorter name without spaces.
I tried 'ci', 'build', and even a single letter. Same 500 each time.

Understood. I checked recent reports and there is a regression where newly enrolled MFA secrets are not fully linked to the token service, causing /api/me/tokens to fail despite successful login. I've opened a bug with your request IDs. As a temporary workaround, a workspace owner can create a project-specific token for CI if that meets your needs until the MFA fix is deployed.

subject: Invoice includes API overage, but imported dataset is incomplete
description: We were billed for API overage tied to a large migration this month, but the migrated dataset in your platform is incomplete. About 15% of the accounts are missing parent-child relationships, and some custom fields are null in the imported records. If we are being charged for the migration volume, we need the data import corrected or the charges adjusted. Please escalate this if it needs engineering review.

comments.message: We got billed for API overage during our migration, but the imported data is incomplete. Parent-child relationships are missing and some custom fields came in null. Why are we paying overage for a broken import?

API overages are based on request volume, not import quality, so the charge itself is expected if your migration exceeded plan limits. For the missing relationships and null fields, please send 5 example record IDs and I can forward them.
I've attached 12 examples, plus the import batch ID. This affected far more than a few records.

Thanks, I added the sample IDs and batch ID to an escalation for our migrations engineering team. I also flagged the overage with billing so they can consider a concession if the incomplete import was caused by a platform-side processing defect rather than client-side API usage. For now the invoice remains open, but the dispute is documented.

subject: Knowledge base images are broken for some articles
description: Several images in our public knowledge base are showing as broken icons, but only on older articles. Newer articles still display images correctly. I need help troubleshooting whether the file links expired or if something changed during our recent domain update.

comments.message: Several images in our public knowledge base are showing as broken icons, but only on older articles. Newer articles still display images correctly. I need help troubleshooting whether the file links expired or if something changed during our recent domain update.
Broken images are usually a browser cache issue after a domain change. Please clear your browser cache and republish the affected articles.
I tested from multiple devices and also in private browsing. Republishing one article didn't fix the image there either.
Then please upload the images again to each article. That should restore them.

subject: Não consigo entender por que a deduplicação automática não encontrou registros óbvios
description: Ativei a deduplicação automática para contatos, mas ela não marcou vários registros que para nós são claramente a mesma pessoa. Em alguns casos o nome está igual e a empresa também, só muda que um tem e-mail pessoal e outro corporativo. Eu preciso saber quais critérios o sistema usa e se dá para ajustar.

comments.message: Ativei a deduplicação automática para contatos, mas ela não marcou vários registros que para nós são claramente a mesma pessoa. Em alguns casos o nome está igual e a empresa também, só muda que um tem e-mail pessoal e outro corporativo. Eu preciso saber quais critérios o sistema usa e se dá para ajustar.

A deduplicação automática de contatos geralmente usa identificadores fortes, como e-mail principal, telefone normalizado ou ID externo, e evita sugerir mesclagem só por nome/empresa para não unir pessoas diferentes por engano. Então dois registros com e-mails distintos podem não ser sinalizados, mesmo com nome igual.
Entendi, mas dá para deixar nome + empresa como regra adicional? Nossa operação precisa disso.

Hoje não. As regras nativas de deduplicação não permitem criar lógica customizada com nome + empresa para mesclagem automática. O caminho mais próximo é gerar uma visão com possíveis duplicados usando filtros, exportar para revisão e fazer mesclagem assistida, ou usar a API com um processo externo de matching. Se quiser, posso te passar um modelo de filtro para identificar casos suspeitos sem automatizar a união.

subject: Locked ourselves out after restricting login methods
description: I was trying to tighten up access for our workspace and disabled password login for everyone except SSO, but we have not finished configuring SAML attributes yet. Now most staff cannot get in, and new hires are also failing because the role mapping seems wrong. I need to restore access and understand which configuration I broke.

comments.message: I turned off password login and required SSO for all users, but our SAML setup is incomplete. People are seeing access denied after the identity provider step, and new users are not getting the right roles when they do come through.
You should just ask users to reset their passwords and try again. Role assignment is handled by your HR system, not by our app.
Password reset does nothing because password login is disabled. Also, the role mapping screen in your app is where I set department=ops to Manager, so I think this is related.

Understood. In that case, have an account owner sign in via the emergency local login link and turn off 'Require SSO for all users' under Security settings. Then review SAML attribute mapping in Product Configuration, especially the role claim and any default role fallback. I can't change those settings from support, but re-enabling mixed login should let users back in while you correct the mapping.

subject: Salesforce-Synchronisierung erstellt doppelte Kontakte
description: Ich habe seit gestern unser CRM mit Ihrer Plattform über die Salesforce-Integration verbunden. Seit dem ersten vollständigen Sync werden viele Kontakte doppelt angelegt, und bei einigen Deals fehlt der zuständige Owner. Ich habe den Abgleich schon pausiert, aber ich weiß nicht, ob die Dubletten durch das Mapping oder durch die Sync-Regeln entstehen. Können Sie mir sagen, wie ich das stoppe und die Ursache finde?

comments.message: Wir haben die Integration gestern aktiviert, und heute Morgen waren in Salesforce und in Ihrer Plattform jeweils mehrere hundert doppelte Kontakte zu sehen. Besonders betroffen sind Datensätze, bei denen eine private und eine geschäftliche E-Mail hinterlegt ist.

Danke für die Details. Das klingt nach einer fehlerhaften Zuordnungsregel in der Salesforce-Integration, vor allem wenn unterschiedliche E-Mail-Felder als eindeutiger Schlüssel verwendet werden. Bitte prüfen Sie unter Integrationen > Salesforce > Feldzuordnung, ob sowohl 'Email' als auch ein benutzerdefiniertes E-Mail-Feld auf das gleiche Zielfeld gemappt wurden. Deaktivieren Sie außerdem vorübergehend die Option 'Kontakte bei Update neu anlegen'.
Ich sehe tatsächlich ein benutzerdefiniertes Feld für sekundäre E-Mail-Adressen, das ebenfalls auf E-Mail gemappt ist. Aber warum fehlt dann bei manchen Deals der Owner?

Wenn beim Deal-Sync der Owner in Salesforce keinem aktiven Benutzer in unserer Plattform zugeordnet werden kann, wird das Feld leer gelassen. Unter derselben Integration können Sie im Benutzer-Mapping prüfen, ob alle Salesforce-Owner einem aktiven Teammitglied zugeordnet sind. Für die bereits entstandenen Dubletten empfehle ich, den Sync ausgeschaltet zu lassen, die falsche Feldzuordnung zu korrigieren und dann einen deduplizierenden Resync über 'Nur aktualisieren, nicht erstellen' zu starten. Wenn Sie möchten, kann ich Ihnen die genauen Schritte dafür senden.

subject: How do I verify which rows failed in a product catalog import?
description: I imported a product catalog update and the job summary says some rows were skipped, but I can’t tell which SKUs failed or why. We’re trying to clean up the catalog before a pricing change goes live, so I need to troubleshoot the bad rows quickly. I’d also like to understand what checks we should do before future imports so non-technical teammates can catch issues earlier.

comments.message: The summary says 612 updated, 37 skipped. I suspect duplicate SKU values or invalid category mappings, but the error screen is vague.

You should be able to click into the import job and download the error file. That file normally includes the original row plus a reason such as duplicate unique key, invalid enum value, or missing required field.
I found the error file, but some messages just say 'validation failed.' That’s not very actionable.

That can happen when multiple field checks fail on the same row. A practical approach is to isolate a few failed rows and compare them against rows that updated successfully—especially SKU format, category spelling, and any required price fields. For future imports, I’d recommend maintaining a template with locked column names, a dropdown list for valid categories in the spreadsheet, and a pre-import checklist for duplicate SKU detection. If you want, send over 3-5 anonymized failed rows and I can help interpret the likely validation issue.

subject: Proration charge seems wrong when deactivating users
description: We removed 23 users mid-cycle, but instead of receiving a credit on the next invoice, we were billed an extra proration amount. The usage page briefly showed the correct lower seat count, then jumped back to the old number before settling again. I am trying to understand whether the invoice engine picked up the wrong seat snapshot because of a bug. Can you verify the calculation and fix the charge if needed?

comments.message: We removed 23 users mid-cycle, but instead of receiving a credit on the next invoice, we were billed an extra proration amount. The usage page briefly showed the correct lower seat count, then jumped back to the old number before settling again. I am trying to understand whether the invoice engine picked up the wrong seat snapshot because of a bug. Can you verify the calculation and fix the charge if needed?

I looked at the invoice, and prorations can be confusing. The extra amount may be normal depending on your billing cycle. The seat count display changing is usually just delayed syncing and should not affect billing.
That doesn’t really answer the question. The charge increased right after we removed users, not added them.

You’re right, I should have been more specific. I can see a seat-audit mismatch between the entitlement service and billing snapshot on the day you deactivated those users, but I can’t adjust invoices directly from this queue. I’m escalating this to our billing specialists to recalculate the proration and to engineering to review the seat-sync discrepancy. You’ll get an update once they finish the investigation.

subject: Password resets disabled after bulk user import
description: We imported 43 new users with the CSV tool yesterday. They all appear in the directory, but multiple people are saying they can’t set a password. The reset email either doesn’t arrive or the link says expired immediately. I need to figure out whether the import created the accounts incorrectly and what I should do from the admin side.

comments.message: These are not SSO users. We imported name, email, and role only. In User Administration most of them show 'Invited,' but a few somehow show 'Active' even though they have never signed in.

Bulk-imported users should receive a standard invite flow, not a pre-set password flow. If some appear Active before first login, it may mean they were matched to existing dormant accounts. Please export the user list and check for duplicate emails or prior deactivated users that were reactivated during import.
I found several duplicates tied to old deactivated contractors. What should I do with the broken invites for the rest?

For the matched dormant accounts, reset access from each user profile so they receive a fresh activation email rather than a generic password reset. For users still in 'Invited' status, cancel the pending invite in bulk and resend from the import summary page; that generates new tokens. If mail delivery is still failing after that, send me 2-3 example addresses and I’ll have our email team check bounce logs.

subject: Password reset emails never arrive for one domain
description: All users from our @ravenwood-partners.com domain are unable to receive password reset emails from your system. Other notifications from your platform come through, just not reset emails. We have multiple locked-out users and need this escalated because it seems domain-specific.

comments.message: Spam and quarantine are clear, and our mail team says they don’t see your reset messages hitting the gateway at all. A Gmail test user got the reset instantly.

Thank you, that comparison is helpful. Since standard app notifications are arriving but password reset emails are not, this may be an issue with our transactional email provider or suppression list for your domain. I’m escalating it to our messaging team to check delivery logs and suppression status.
Can you remove the suppression today if that’s the cause? We need at least two users back in before end of day.

If the messaging team confirms the domain or addresses were suppressed, they can usually clear that the same day. I’ve marked the escalation as time-sensitive and included your note that mail gateway logs show no inbound attempts. While that’s being reviewed, an existing admin can still reset user passwords manually from the admin console if you have one available.

subject: Former employee reactivated by mistake and now we're billed again
description: One of our department managers accidentally reactivated a former employee’s account for a few hours while trying to restore another user. The seat count went up and now our monthly invoice reflects an extra paid user. I need to know whether a brief reactivation triggers billing and whether I can prevent non-admin managers from doing this.

comments.message: The user "marco.silva" was deactivated since April, then briefly reactivated on July 3 and disabled again the same day. Our seat count increased from 64 to 65 and the invoice draft includes that extra seat. Also, why can a department manager reactivate users at all?

On monthly plans, any reactivated user can increase the active seat count for the billing period if the subscription is set to true-up based on peak active seats. I checked your audit log and the reactivation lasted 3 hours, so that likely triggered the additional seat for this cycle. Separately, your custom admin role currently allows managers to restore deactivated users.
Can you remove the charge and lock that permission down?

I’ve updated the custom role so department managers can no longer reactivate users; only workspace admins can. For the billing side, I can’t remove the charge directly, but I submitted a courtesy credit request since this looks accidental and short-lived. If approved, the credit will appear on your next invoice.

subject: In-app notifications keep reappearing after being marked read
description: Our agents mark certain in-app notifications as read, refresh the page, and then the same notifications come back as unread. It seems to happen mostly with @mentions from shared channels. Can you investigate whether read state is not persisting, and also explain how notification sync is supposed to work across browser tabs and devices?

comments.message: This is causing people to miss genuinely new alerts because the unread badge never settles down. We noticed it on multiple accounts, so it doesn't seem user-specific.

I was able to reproduce the behavior with shared-channel mentions. The read action is being saved in the notification service, but the client rehydrates from a stale cache key when the page reloads, which flips those items back to unread until the next full sync. That's a bug, not expected behavior.

Normally, marking a notification as read should persist server-side and then sync to other tabs/devices through the notification stream, with local cache only acting as a temporary performance layer. Until we fix it, a hard refresh or waiting for the next sync cycle may clear the badge correctly, but I realize that's not a good workaround. I've attached your workspace to the existing issue.

subject: After role changes, users lost report access and Jira sync is overwriting record owners
description: We did a permissions cleanup after moving to SSO-only login. Some users can sign in but no longer see the reports they used to manage, and at the same time our Jira integration started overwriting record owner values with the integration user on synced tickets. We also have duplicate issue links being created on some records. Can you help sort out whether our access changes affected data permissions in the integration?

comments.message: We disabled local passwords and moved everyone to SSO-only. I also changed several custom roles. Since then, people can log in but report folders are missing for managers, and Jira-created updates are changing the record owner to the bot account. A few records now show two links to the same Jira issue.

The report visibility and Jira owner overwrite can happen if the role update removed 'view shared reports' and changed the integration's write scope. Please check whether your Jira app mapping is set to 'update owner on assignee sync' and whether the integration user was reassigned a broader role during the cleanup. Duplicate issue links usually come from retries after permission failures.
The mapping does have 'update owner on assignee sync' enabled, but that was always on before. The integration user inherited an admin role yesterday because the old custom role was deleted.

Got it. Then the likely issue is not the toggle itself but the role context changing how ownership updates are applied. Restore the integration user to a limited service role, disable the owner-write action temporarily, and re-enable report access on the manager role. For the existing duplicate issue links, you can delete extras manually from each record; we don't currently have a bulk cleanup tool for that.

subject: Automation preview shows wrong records
description: I'm building a workflow that should target open renewal opportunities, but the preview panel is pulling in closed-won records and even a few archived ones. The actual filter logic looks correct to me: stage is Renewal, status is Open, archived is false. I toggled the conditions a few times and recreated the workflow from scratch, but the preview still looks wrong. I haven't turned the automation on because I don't trust the results.

comments.message: The automation preview is showing records that don't match the filter criteria. Is the workflow logic broken, or is this just the preview?

Let's test whether the issue is in the builder or the underlying query. If you save the workflow as draft and run the same conditions in the records list view, do you get the expected open renewal opportunities there?
Yes, the list view returns the correct records. It's only the workflow preview panel that seems off.

Thanks, that helps narrow it down. We have a known bug where the preview panel can show stale sample records after filters are edited, especially when archived=false is part of the rule set. The execution engine still uses the latest saved conditions. To be safe, save the draft, refresh the page, and reopen the workflow before enabling it. If the preview remains inconsistent after that, send me the workflow ID and I'll attach it to the existing bug report.

subject: Impossible to log in from office network only
description: Nobody in our headquarters can log into the platform from the office Wi-Fi today. At home or on mobile hotspot it works. On the office network, we enter credentials and then the page times out before loading the app. We did not change anything in our tenant settings, so I'm trying to figure out whether this is on your side or ours.

comments.message: Nobody in our headquarters can log into the platform from the office Wi-Fi today. At home or on mobile hotspot it works. On the office network, we enter credentials and then the page times out before loading the app. We did not change anything in our tenant settings, so I'm trying to figure out whether this is on your side or ours.

Thanks, that's helpful troubleshooting already. Because it works off-network, the issue is likely between your office network and our authentication endpoints rather than your user accounts. Can you confirm whether your firewall or web proxy recently started blocking *.auth.ourapp.com, and whether users see a timeout before or after entering credentials?
Our network team says a new SSL inspection policy went live this morning. The timeout happens right after credentials are submitted.

That lines up with what I see: your login attempts reach the first auth step, but the token exchange to our auth subdomain never completes from your office IP range. Please have your network team allowlist *.auth.ourapp.com and exclude it from SSL inspection. As a temporary workaround, users can connect over a mobile hotspot or VPN that bypasses the office egress path.

subject: What should I show new admins about export permissions and sensitive fields?
description: I’m putting together onboarding for two new admins and want to cover safe data exports. We have some sensitive customer fields that not everyone should be downloading, but I’m not fully clear on how export permissions interact with field visibility. Can you explain this in a way I can turn into a short training guide?

comments.message: I’m putting together onboarding for two new admins and want to cover safe data exports. We have some sensitive customer fields that not everyone should be downloading, but I’m not fully clear on how export permissions interact with field visibility. Can you explain this in a way I can turn into a short training guide?
Export permissions usually follow the same visibility rules as on-screen access, so if a user can see a field they can export it. You can tell the admins just to be careful with sensitive reports.
That’s a bit too vague for training. Is there a setting to block exports entirely for some roles or to hide fields from exports?
I think there might be, but I don’t have the exact menu path. You should check the permissions section and maybe test with a non-admin user.

subject: Need training on role-based access for automation builders
description: We're expanding from one ops admin to a larger team, and I need a clear way to teach people which login roles allow them to build, test, or only view workflows. Right now some users can sign in but don't understand why they can't publish automations. Also, a test workflow someone made is accidentally emailing customers, so we need best practices fast.

comments.message: Can you explain the safest way to train new team members on workflow permissions? People can log in, but they don't understand the difference between viewing a flow, editing it, and publishing it. One junior user built a test automation and it started sending real emails.

A practical setup is to separate three capabilities: Viewer for read-only access, Builder for creating and editing drafts, and Publisher/Admin for activating changes. In training, I recommend using a sandbox workspace plus a checklist that covers trigger conditions, audience filters, and whether outbound actions are pointed to test recipients. If your plan includes approval gates, you can also require review before publication so a logged-in Builder cannot accidentally push a live workflow.
We do have a sandbox, but some people are building in production because they say the login experience is the same and they can't tell which environment they're in.

Then I'd make education part of the process as well as the tooling: rename the sandbox clearly, apply a banner theme, and restrict production workflow creation to a smaller Publisher group. For onboarding, have each new builder log in to both environments, create a harmless draft in sandbox, and walk through the publish controls so they see exactly where access changes. I can also send our admin guide on environment labeling and workflow change management.

subject: API import created orphaned records and now service accounts fail login
description: Our overnight API import created child records without parents in several projects, and this morning our integration service account can't log in to re-run the job. The error just says invalid credentials even though the password was rotated in our vault and updated in the connector. I need help troubleshooting both the bad data load and the blocked access.

comments.message: We have about 300 task records attached to missing project IDs after last night's import. When we tried to fix it, the service account started failing login with invalid credentials through the connector and the web app.

This sounds like either the account was locked after repeated attempts or the connector is still using an old token despite the password rotation. Please try logging into the web app manually with the service account, then check whether the account shows as locked in Admin > Users. For the orphaned data, review the import job error file to see whether parent projects were rejected earlier in the batch.
Manual web login says the account is locked. The import error file shows project rows failed because a required region field was missing, but the task rows still inserted.

Thanks, that's helpful. I've unlocked the service account recommendation path: an admin should unlock it and reset the password once more, then update the connector secret and test with a manual sign-in first. On the data side, the job accepted child rows even though parent rows failed validation, which is not ideal but explains the orphans. Re-import the missing project records with valid region values, then either relink or delete the orphaned tasks. If you'd like, I can also flag the partial referential validation behavior to engineering.

subject: Quarterly report skipped records after workflow archive step
description: I built a workflow to archive closed projects 30 days after completion, and now my quarterly projects report is missing a big chunk of completed work. The report used to include all completed projects for the quarter, but after the automation went live it looks like archived records are excluded even though they should still count historically. I need guidance on whether the report can include archived items or if the workflow needs to change.

comments.message: Our quarterly completed-projects report dropped by about 18% after we automated archival of completed records. I suspect the workflow is moving them into a state the report no longer reads.
Archived records are hidden from most standard reports by design, so what you're seeing is expected. If you don't want that, you should disable the archive workflow.
Disabling the workflow isn't a great option because we need the active workspace kept clean. Is there a way to report on archived projects without undoing automation?
You can try exporting all projects before they archive and combine the numbers offline. At the moment I don't have another option to offer.

subject: NetSuite invoice sync not appearing in revenue by month report
description: We sync invoices from NetSuite into your system, and the finance team noticed that April invoices are visible on the account timeline but missing from the Revenue by Month report. The integration status says successful, so I can't tell whether this is a reporting bug or a mapping issue. Please advise, and if it's not straightforward I'd like this escalated because month-end close is blocked.

comments.message: We sync invoices from NetSuite into your system, and the finance team noticed that April invoices are visible on the account timeline but missing from the Revenue by Month report. The integration status says successful, so I can't tell whether this is a reporting bug or a mapping issue. Please advise, and if it's not straightforward I'd like this escalated because month-end close is blocked.

I checked the sync logs and the NetSuite records are arriving successfully, but the invoice date field is mapped to 'Posting Date' in the integration while your Revenue by Month report is configured to bucket by 'Invoice Date'. For the April invoices you shared, 'Invoice Date' is empty, so they won't appear in that report. If you remap the integration to populate Invoice Date, future imports should report correctly.
That helps for future imports, but we need April fixed too. Will remapping backfill existing invoices or do we need a reimport?

Remapping alone won't backfill historical records. I've escalated a reimport request to our data operations team and included the affected date range so they can repopulate Invoice Date for existing NetSuite invoices. While that runs, you can create a temporary Revenue by Month report using Posting Date to reconcile April.

subject: Admin login restored, but our approval chain still fails on step 3
description: I was locked out due to MFA issues and your team helped me regain admin access yesterday. Now I can log in, but the multi-step approval workflow for vendor spend is still failing at step 3 with no notification sent to Finance. Since this process is business-critical, please escalate if you can't see why the automation is stalling.

comments.message: I was locked out due to MFA issues and your team helped me regain admin access yesterday. Now I can log in, but the multi-step approval workflow for vendor spend is still failing at step 3 with no notification sent to Finance. Since this process is business-critical, please escalate if you can't see why the automation is stalling.

I can access the workflow logs now that your admin account is active again. Step 3 is configured to notify the 'Finance Approvers' group, but that group currently has zero active members because the last two members were deactivated this week. The workflow is technically running, but it has nowhere to send the approval request, so it remains pending. I’ve added this case to our workflow queue as an escalation because the product should surface that failure more clearly, but the immediate fix is to add at least one active member back into the Finance Approvers group.
I added one person to the group and the next request went through. Can the stuck requests be resumed, or do we have to recreate them?

They can usually be resumed from the workflow run history by selecting 'retry from failed step' on each affected request. If that option isn’t available on your plan, you’ll need to recreate those requests manually. I’ve kept the escalation open for the missing warning behavior.

subject: Po zmianie domeny firmy nie działa reset hasła
description: Nasza firma przeszła z domeny abc.pl na abcgroup.pl i administrator zmienił mój login. Teraz nie mogę wejść na konto, a wiadomości do resetu hasła przychodzą chyba na stary adres, do którego już nie mam dostępu.

comments.message: Potrzebuję dostać się do konta jeszcze dziś, bo mam tam raporty dla klienta. Czy możecie przepiąć logowanie na nowy adres?

Sprawdziłem i reset hasła rzeczywiście był kierowany na poprzedni adres. Zaktualizowałem główny login na nową domenę i unieważniłem stare linki resetujące. Wysłałem nową wiadomość na Pański obecny adres.
Mail doszedł, hasło udało się ustawić, ale po zalogowaniu widzę pusty profil bez mojego zespołu.

To oznacza, że konto użytkownika zostało odłączone od organizacji podczas zmiany domeny. Już przypisałem je z powrotem do właściwego workspace. Proszę wylogować się i zalogować ponownie — dane zespołu powinny się pojawić.

subject: Duplicate Slack alerts from one escalation workflow
description: I set up an escalation automation that posts to our Slack channel when a high-priority ticket has no first response after 30 minutes. The problem is that we are getting two or three Slack alerts for the same ticket. I suspect I duplicated a trigger somewhere while configuring the workflow, but I can't find it. Can you help me narrow down what in the product setup would cause repeated executions?

comments.message: The workflow trigger is "ticket created or updated" with conditions Priority = High and First response time breached = true. Action is send Slack notification. We also have a separate reminder automation at 60 minutes.

The duplicate alerts are likely because the automation runs on every qualifying update, not just the first breach event. In Workflow Automation, change the trigger from "created or updated" to the specific SLA breach event if your account has it, or add a guard condition such as "Escalation Sent = false." Then in Product Configuration, create a checkbox field named Escalation Sent and set it to true in the same workflow after the Slack action.
That makes sense. We do have the SLA breach trigger. If I switch to that, do I still need the checkbox?

If you use the dedicated "first response SLA breached" event, you probably don't need the checkbox for this specific workflow because it should fire once per breach. Keep the checkbox only if you also have reopen scenarios that can breach again and you want to suppress repeat alerts across the full ticket lifecycle.

subject: SCIM deprovisioning is not disabling users in our app
description: I set up SCIM provisioning against your API last week so our IdP can automatically deactivate users when they leave the company. New users are being created correctly, but when I disable someone in Okta they stay active in your app and can still sign in. We need this for offboarding compliance. Can you help me figure out whether the deprovisioning call is failing or if there is another setting we missed?

comments.message: We tested with three users this morning. Okta shows the deactivation step as successful, but in your admin panel those users still appear as active members.

Thanks for the details. I checked the SCIM behavior on our side: user creation is handled by POST /scim/v2/Users, but deprovisioning in our platform only occurs when your IdP sends a PATCH with active=false or a DELETE request, depending on your connector configuration. In many Okta setups the app is configured to suspend assignments without sending active=false. Please check the Okta Provisioning tab and confirm that 'Deactivate Users' is enabled.
I just checked and that option was off. I enabled it and retried with a test user. Now I can see a PATCH request in the Okta logs and the user was automatically marked inactive in your app.

Great — that confirms the issue was the IdP deprovisioning setting rather than the API credentials. Your setup should now handle offboarding correctly. If you'd like, I can also send our SCIM attribute mapping guide so your team can verify role and group updates as well.

subject: Google Drive import keeps timing out on larger folders
description: I'm importing customer onboarding documents from Google Drive into your knowledge workspace. Small folders sync fine, but larger folders either stall at 90% or fail with a timeout after about 20 minutes. I need troubleshooting help and also some advice on the recommended way to structure imports so this doesn't keep happening.

comments.message: Our Drive integration works for folders with a few files, but one onboarding library with about 3,000 documents times out every time. Is there a size limit or a better way to do this import?

There isn't a strict document count limit, but very large first-time imports can hit timeout thresholds, especially if the folder contains many nested permissions checks or large PDFs. As a best practice, split the initial sync into smaller top-level folders by team or topic, complete the first ingest, and then let incremental sync handle later changes. Also exclude archived subfolders if you don't need them indexed.
We broke it into three folders and two completed, but the third still hangs. Most of the files there are scanned PDFs.

Scanned PDFs take longer because they require OCR before indexing, which increases processing time substantially. For that remaining folder, try reducing the batch again and disable image OCR in the import options if searchable text isn't required. If you do need OCR, I'll escalate this to our ingestion team with your connector ID so they can review the timeout thresholds for that job.

subject: Error al sincronizar contactos con HubSpot
description: Estoy intentando conectar nuestra cuenta con HubSpot para sincronizar contactos, pero desde ayer la sincronización se queda en "En curso" y nunca termina. Ya reconecté la integración y probé con otro navegador. Necesito saber si el problema es de su plataforma o de los campos mapeados porque ventas no está recibiendo los leads nuevos.

comments.message: La sincronización empezó a fallar después de que agregamos un campo personalizado obligatorio en HubSpot. En su panel no veo ningún error claro, solo que la última ejecución quedó colgada hace 14 horas.

Gracias por los detalles. Cuando se agrega un campo obligatorio en HubSpot, nuestra integración puede detenerse si ese campo no está mapeado o no recibe un valor válido. Por favor vaya a Integraciones > HubSpot > Mapeo de campos y revise si el nuevo campo aparece con advertencias. También necesito que me confirme si el error ocurre en todos los contactos o solo en los nuevos.
Revisé el mapeo y el campo nuevo ni siquiera aparece en la lista. Solo falla con contactos nuevos; los antiguos sí se siguen viendo.

Eso apunta a que el esquema en caché de la integración no se actualizó después del cambio en HubSpot. Ya forcé una actualización del conector desde nuestro lado y le pedí al sistema que vuelva a cargar los campos. Espere unos 10 minutos, luego vuelva a abrir el mapeo y ejecute una sincronización manual. Si el campo aparece, asígnelo a un valor por defecto o a una propiedad existente para que los nuevos contactos no queden bloqueados.

subject: Bulk invite created duplicate users and I can't merge them
description: I imported a CSV for 27 new hires, but three people now have duplicate profiles because their emails were already in the system under a different domain alias. One duplicate has product access and the other has the correct manager assignment. I don't see any merge option, and I need to clean this up before onboarding starts tomorrow.

comments.message: The duplicates are all showing as active users, so this is also affecting our seat count. Can I delete one and move the assignments over?

Yes, you should be able to delete the extra profile from the Users page and then reassign the manager manually on the remaining account. Once deleted, the seat count should correct itself within a few minutes.
There is no delete option, only deactivate, and deactivating doesn't move the onboarding tasks or group memberships. That's why I'm asking.

Understood. In that case this will need to be handled by our backend team because profile consolidation isn't available in the admin UI. I'm escalating the duplicate-user records for review, including the seat-count impact and the need to preserve assignments on the correct account. Please do not deactivate either profile until they confirm the merge path.

subject: Users can't save edits in one custom object
description: Our team can open records in a custom object called Vendor Profiles, but when we click Save after editing, the button spins for a few seconds and then nothing changes. This only seems to affect that object; edits in standard tickets still save normally. I need help troubleshooting whether it's a permissions issue, validation rule, or something broken in the object setup.

comments.message: Our team can open records in a custom object called Vendor Profiles, but when we click Save after editing, the button spins for a few seconds and then nothing changes. This only seems to affect that object; edits in standard tickets still save normally. I need help troubleshooting whether it's a permissions issue, validation rule, or something broken in the object setup.

I’m sorry about the trouble. Silent save failures on one custom object are often caused by either a required field hidden by layout rules or a validation rule blocking the update without surfacing clearly in the UI. Did you recently add any required fields, formulas, or uniqueness constraints to Vendor Profiles? If possible, tell me one field you're changing and whether the issue happens for admins as well as standard users.
Yes, we added a required field called Vendor Tier yesterday, but it's only visible when Region = North America. The save fails even for admins editing older records where Region is blank.

That configuration is likely the cause. If Vendor Tier is globally required but conditionally hidden, records with blank Region can fail validation because users cannot fill the required field. Please either make Vendor Tier conditionally required to match the visibility rule, or expose it for records where Region is blank and backfill a value. If you want, I can escalate this to confirm whether the UI should be surfacing a clearer validation error instead of failing silently.

subject: Automation rule creates duplicate follow-up tasks every hour
description: I set up a workflow automation to create a follow-up task 24 hours after a ticket is marked Pending, but it's creating duplicate tasks every hour until the ticket is closed. I want only one follow-up task per ticket. Can you explain what's wrong with the rule and how to prevent repeats?

comments.message: The trigger is 'when status = Pending' and then there's a wait for 24 hours, then create task 'Check in with customer.' After that, our team sees the same task created over and over each hour. We don't have any other obvious automation doing this.

This happens because the workflow re-enters each time the record is updated while still in Pending. The wait step does not prevent re-enrollment by itself. To stop duplicates, add an enrollment condition so the workflow runs only when status changes to Pending, and set a flag field like 'Follow-up task created = No' as an entry requirement. Then, after task creation, update that flag to Yes.
We don't currently have a custom flag field. Is there a simpler way without creating one?
You should be able to use the existing task due date field for that. If a due date exists, the workflow won't run again.

subject: Przez pomyłkę zmieniłem plan i liczbę użytkowników
description: Chciałem tylko sprawdzić ceny wyższego planu, ale wygląda na to, że zapisały się zmiany i teraz na koncie widzę inny pakiet oraz 25 miejsc zamiast 18. Potrzebuję wrócić do poprzednich ustawień, zanim naliczy się nowa opłata.

comments.message: Dzień dobry, chyba przypadkowo zatwierdziłem zmianę planu z Growth na Scale i zwiększenie liczby miejsc. Nie chciałem tego aktywować. Czy można cofnąć zmiany jeszcze dziś?
Jeżeli zmiana została zapisana, niestety nie ma możliwości cofnięcia.
To dziwne, bo nie podpisywałem żadnego aneksu i nie dostałem jeszcze faktury. Potrzebuję jasnej odpowiedzi, czy będzie naliczona wyższa opłata.
W takim razie proszę poczekać do wystawienia faktury i wtedy zgłosić reklamację, jeśli kwota będzie za wysoka.

subject: Zmiana kontaktu administracyjnego, ale brak maila aktywacyjnego
description: Zmieniono mnie na nowy kontakt administracyjny dla naszego konta, jednak nie mogę się zalogować, bo nie dostałem żadnego maila aktywacyjnego. Poprzedni administrator został już usunięty z firmy. Potrzebuję pilnie wejść do ustawień rozliczeń i użytkowników.

comments.message: Sprawdzałem spam i nic nie ma. Czy możecie ręcznie uruchomić aktywację albo potwierdzić, na jaki adres została wysłana?

Sprawdziłem logi i wiadomość aktywacyjna odbiła się z powodu literówki w adresie wpisanym przy zmianie kontaktu. Poprawiłem adres, ale ponieważ chodzi o konto administracyjne, muszę jeszcze przekazać sprawę do zespołu bezpieczeństwa, żeby ręcznie zatwierdzili zmianę właściciela.
Rozumiem. Czy do tego czasu da się chociaż odzyskać dostęp do zwykłego panelu użytkownika?

Niestety nie mogę nadać tymczasowego dostępu, dopóki bezpieczeństwo nie zakończy weryfikacji. Poprosiłem o priorytet i dodałem notatkę, że poprzedni administrator nie jest już dostępny. Otrzyma Pan osobną wiadomość, gdy aktywacja zostanie zatwierdzona.

subject: Salesforce contact creation is duplicating from one automation
description: I have an automation that creates a Salesforce contact when a lead is marked qualified in your system. Since Monday, every time the workflow runs it creates two contacts in Salesforce instead of one. I only see one create-record step in the workflow builder, so I am not sure whether this is an integration issue or something in the automation trigger.

comments.message: We are getting duplicate contacts for about 40 leads a day now. The workflow history shows one successful run per lead, but Salesforce shows two inserts a few seconds apart with the same email address.
Duplicate records usually mean the workflow is being triggered twice. Can you check whether you have another automation enabled on the same qualified status event?
I checked and there is only one active workflow for that event. I also exported the audit log and the lead status only changes once.

Understood. In that case, the next thing to verify is whether your Salesforce step is inside both the main path and a retry path after a wait or webhook confirmation. We have seen workflows fire the integration action twice when a record update trigger is followed by a field-sync loop from Salesforce back into our platform. Please disable the 'update source record after sync' option temporarily and add a filter so the automation only runs when Qualified changed from false to true. That should stop the loop while we investigate further.

subject: Can’t reconcile invoice after changing API limits and webhook configuration
description: We upgraded our API limit temporarily while reworking a webhook-based sync with our CRM, then scaled the webhook traffic back down after the migration. The invoice now shows both an API overage charge and the higher API package fee. I need help understanding whether the webhook configuration is still causing billable usage somewhere and how to verify the current package level in the admin settings.

comments.message: During the migration we increased API capacity and created several retry-heavy webhooks. Those have been disabled now, but billing still looks elevated.

There are two separate billing components here: the temporary API package upgrade, and usage-based overages generated while the retry webhooks were active. Disabling the webhooks now won't remove the historical overage already incurred.
Understood. How do I confirm we're back on the lower package so this doesn't continue?

Go to Admin > Developers > API to verify the current rate-limit tier shown in the banner, then compare that with Billing > Subscription > Add-ons where the upgraded API package would still appear if it hasn't been downgraded. I can confirm your overage-producing webhooks are disabled, but I can't yet verify whether the package downgrade request finished processing, so I've asked our billing ops team to confirm the effective date.

subject: Scheduled report is missing columns for delegated admins
description: I set up a usage report for our department managers, but the emailed version they receive is missing two columns that I can see when I run it manually. Those managers are delegated admins, so I’m wondering if this is a user permission issue or a reporting setting I missed. I also need to understand the logic well enough to explain it to them because they keep asking why the exports don’t match.

comments.message: The report includes license count, last login, and custom field values. When I preview it as a full admin, all columns are there. When the scheduled email goes to delegated admins, custom field values disappear. Is that expected?

Yes, that’s usually tied to the recipient’s admin scope. Scheduled reports render based on the permissions of the receiving user, so delegated admins won’t see columns tied to fields outside their scope. To confirm, open one of the missing custom fields and check whether delegated admins have visibility to it.
If that’s the case, what’s the best practice? We need those managers to get the data, but I don’t want to make them full admins.

Best practice is to either broaden delegated admin visibility only for the needed fields, or have the report scheduled from a service account with full reporting access and send it to them as recipients. For your managers, I’d explain it this way: the report is permission-aware, so recipients only receive data they’re allowed to access. If you want, I can outline the exact steps to create a reporting service account.

subject: Sandbox configuration promoted, but production data mappings don't align
description: We promoted our sandbox setup to production this weekend, including new custom fields and updated lead scoring rules. Since then, the production API ingest has been sending values into the wrong fields on some lead records, especially region and partner source. It seems like the production schema labels match sandbox, but the data mappings in the live connector may still point to older field keys. I need help understanding what should have been updated automatically versus what needs manual remapping.

comments.message: After promoting sandbox config, production leads are getting incorrect values in custom fields. The connector writes "EMEA" into Partner Type on some records. Did the promotion scramble field IDs?

Field IDs can differ between environments even if labels are identical, depending on how the fields were originally created. Promotion usually creates or updates the production schema, but external connectors that reference hardcoded field keys often need manual remapping. Which integration is posting these lead records?
It's our middleware connector through the public API. The payload still uses the old production custom field keys from before the sandbox rollout.

That would cause exactly this behavior if the target keys no longer represent the same fields. You should audit the current production field keys in the schema manager and update the middleware mapping accordingly. I can't safely confirm whether any records were miswritten without a sample payload, so I'm opening this with our integrations team to help identify the affected lead set and correct the bad mappings.

subject: Rate limit errors even though we are under the documented threshold
description: I am troubleshooting repeated 429 responses from your REST API. Based on the docs we should be well below the limit at around 200 requests per minute, but we still hit throttling during business hours. Can you help identify what we are missing?

comments.message: Our dashboard shows roughly 180-220 requests/minute from the integration. The docs mention 600 requests/minute on our plan, but we are getting bursts of 429 from `/v1/users/search` and `/v1/tickets/search`.
If you're below 600 per minute, you shouldn't be rate limited. It may just be temporary internet instability. Please wait a few minutes and try again later.
We already did that for three days. The 429 responses include `X-RateLimit-Bucket: search` and a `Retry-After` header around 20 seconds. Does search have a separate cap?

Yes, those endpoints sit in a separate search bucket with a much lower concurrent threshold than the general REST limit. I can't change that from support, so I've opened an escalation to our API platform team to confirm the exact bucket for your tenant and whether a temporary increase is possible. In the meantime, back off on 429 using the `Retry-After` value and cache repeated search queries where you can.

subject: Former employee still appears active and can log in
description: One of our former sales reps left last week, but I just noticed her account still shows as active and she was apparently able to log in this morning. I need to make sure she no longer has access, and I'd also like to understand the difference between deactivating a user and removing them entirely because I don't want to lose her historical activity. What's the right process here?

comments.message: One of our former sales reps left last week, but I just noticed her account still shows as active and she was apparently able to log in this morning. I need to make sure she no longer has access, and I'd also like to understand the difference between deactivating a user and removing them entirely because I don't want to lose her historical activity. What's the right process here?
You should be able to remove the user from the Users page and that should revoke access immediately. If you don't want them in the list anymore, deletion is the cleanest option.
I'm hesitant to delete her if that wipes out ownership on past records. Also, I don't actually see a delete option, only deactivate and suspend sessions.

Thanks for clarifying. In that case, use Deactivate and then choose Suspend active sessions to force logout. Deactivation blocks future login while preserving historical ownership, audit trail, and mentions. Permanent deletion usually isn't available for standard users specifically to avoid losing that history. For offboarding going forward, the best practice is: deactivate the user, transfer any owned assets if needed, and review group memberships or API tokens tied to that account.

subject: Webhook de integração falhando depois de alterar credenciais
description: Depois que atualizei as credenciais do nosso webhook para o ERP, os envios de pedidos começaram a falhar. A automação continua executando, mas o webhook retorna erro e o pedido não chega ao sistema externo. Preciso de ajuda para revisar a configuração.

comments.message: No histórico da automação aparece "falha na ação webhook". A URL continua a mesma, só troquei o token no cabeçalho Authorization.

Olá! Quando o token é alterado, vale revisar se o cabeçalho está no formato exato esperado pelo endpoint. Em muitos casos precisa ser "Bearer <token>" e não apenas o valor cru. Você consegue confirmar o código de resposta no log da execução?
Está voltando 401 Unauthorized. No campo eu coloquei só o token mesmo, sem Bearer.

Perfeito, isso explica a falha. Edite a ação do webhook e no cabeçalho Authorization use "Bearer " seguido do token. Depois salve e execute um teste manual a partir de um pedido de exemplo. Se o ERP também validar Content-Type, mantenha application/json. Com isso o envio deve voltar a funcionar.

subject: Former employee can still log in and edit archived records
description: We deactivated a former employee last week, but I just saw changes on archived opportunities under their name. At the same time, our archive export includes records that should have been purged already. I need to know how they still had access and whether our data retention settings are working properly.

comments.message: The user was disabled in our HR system and removed from Google, but your audit log shows edits from their account yesterday. Also, the archive export contains opportunities older than our 90-day retention window.

Thanks for reporting this. If you're using SSO, removing someone from Google does not always deactivate their app account unless SCIM or just-in-time deprovisioning is configured. Please check whether the user is still marked Active in Admin > Users. On the retention side, archived records remain exportable until the nightly purge job runs, so I'd like to know whether those records crossed the 90-day mark before last night's cycle.
They're still marked Active in the app, so we must not have deprovisioning set up correctly. Some of the records in the export are 104 days old though, so that seems separate.

Understood. I've explained the access issue correctly, but the retention gap needs investigation. Please deactivate that user directly in the app now to stop further login, and I'm escalating the purge behavior to our data platform team with your workspace ID because records older than 90 days should not still be exportable after the purge window.

subject: Le graphique des réponses par heure est décalé de deux heures
description: Sur notre rapport "Réponses par heure", tous les pics apparaissent deux heures plus tard que dans la réalité. Par exemple, le pic de 9h est affiché à 11h. Nous opérons uniquement depuis la France et le fuseau du compte est correct. J'ai besoin d'une explication car les managers remettent en cause les plannings.

comments.message: Bonjour, le graphique "Réponses par heure" semble décalé de deux heures. Nos pics de charge n'apparaissent pas au bon moment.

Bonjour, merci pour votre message. Pouvez-vous confirmer si le rapport est consulté dans le module analytique ou directement sur un tableau de bord partagé ? Et observez-vous le décalage aussi dans l'export CSV horaire ?
Oui, dans le module analytique et aussi sur le tableau de bord partagé. En revanche dans le CSV horaire les heures semblent correctes.

Merci, c'est utile. Si le CSV est correct mais pas la visualisation, cela ressemble à un problème d'affichage lié au fuseau du navigateur ou au rendu du widget. Je n'ai pas assez d'éléments pour conclure immédiatement, donc j'escalade au support technique reporting avec votre exemple. En attendant, utilisez le CSV comme source de vérité pour la présentation, car c'est le calcul brut le plus fiable.

subject: Need emergency access to paused workflow after admin departure
description: Our only admin left the company yesterday, and his account owned a workflow that paused itself after repeated errors. I can still log in as a regular manager, but I can't open the advanced settings to see what failed or resume it. We need immediate help restoring access, and I also want to understand how to avoid tying mission-critical workflows to one person.

comments.message: A customer renewal workflow is paused and the owner was our former admin. My manager account can log in, but I only see a summary view and no resume button. How can we get control back today?

Because this involves ownership of a paused workflow and a departed employee's account, we need an account-level admin or verified billing contact to authorize access changes. If you are not listed in that role, I can't transfer ownership from chat alone. What I can explain is that paused workflows usually require both workflow permissions and access to the underlying object settings to resume them.
I'm not the billing contact, but our CFO is. We just need to know what to ask her to do.

Please have the CFO contact us or update your account admins, and ask specifically for: transfer of the renewal workflow to an active shared owner, temporary admin access for an operations lead, and review of the error log for the paused run. Longer term, we recommend a shared automation owner, at least two admins with login access, and documented backup coverage for critical workflows. Once an authorized contact reaches out, we can help restore control and advise on the failure itself.

subject: Idempotency key not preventing duplicate ticket creation
description: We are retrying failed POST requests to create tickets and expected the idempotency key to stop duplicates, but we are still seeing two or three copies of the same ticket in some cases. I need troubleshooting help to understand whether we are implementing idempotency incorrectly.

comments.message: We send `Idempotency-Key: job-48392` on POST `/v1/tickets`. If the client times out after 10 seconds, our worker retries with the same payload but sometimes a second ticket is created anyway.
Idempotency should prevent duplicates globally, so that sounds like a bug on our side. I'll note it.
Can you confirm whether the key is scoped by endpoint or expires? We also route traffic across US and EU base URLs depending on tenant.

I found the likely cause: idempotency keys are scoped to the combination of region, endpoint, and API credential, and they expire after 24 hours. If your retries sometimes hit a different regional base URL than the original request, they won't deduplicate. Also, a 10-second client timeout may fire before the original request commits, making the retry race look like a duplicate. Keep retries pinned to the same base URL, use a unique key per logical create operation, and consider extending the client timeout or checking for the original request ID before retrying.

subject: Webhook test deliveries failing after rotating API key
description: I rotated the API key used in our outbound webhook configuration for ticket updates. Since then, every test delivery returns 401, but the receiving system says the new key is valid. I am not sure if I missed another setting in the webhook configuration or if the request format changed.

comments.message: We updated the Authorization header in the webhook settings with the new bearer token. The endpoint is unchanged. Test sends now fail with 401, and live events are queued but not delivered.

A 401 after key rotation is commonly caused by an outdated secret in either the header template or the environment-level connection attached to the webhook. Please open the webhook configuration and check whether it uses a shared credential object instead of the inline header you edited. If so, update the credential there as well. Also confirm there isn't an extra space before 'Bearer' in the header value.
It was using a shared credential object. I updated that too, and test deliveries are now getting through. Live events are still marked queued though.

Queued events should flush automatically once the endpoint returns success, but there can be a short delay. If they remain queued for more than 15 minutes, disable and re-enable the webhook to restart the worker subscription. Your main configuration issue was the stale shared credential.

subject: Need to split one workspace into two accounts but project access broke
description: We’re restructuring and I created a second account for our EU team. I moved several users over and changed their access, but now some of them can’t see the migrated projects in either workspace, and a few invitation links land on a blank page. I’m trying to understand if I handled the account changes incorrectly or if there’s a system issue during migration.

comments.message: I changed six users from full members in the original workspace to guest access, then invited them into the new account. Two accepted the invite but see no projects, and three others get a blank white screen from the invite email.

Thanks — that does sound like a combination of account reassignment and an access issue. As a first troubleshooting step, please confirm whether the migrated projects were explicitly shared into the new workspace, since user membership alone won’t carry project permissions. For the blank invite page, ask those users to open the link in an incognito window and confirm whether their browser is blocking third-party cookies.
Incognito fixed one invite link, but two still fail. And yes, the projects were shared, but the moved users still get “resource unavailable” when opening them.

I checked the backend logs and the affected users were left in a pending cross-workspace state after their roles changed, which is why they can authenticate but not resolve project access. I’ve reset those memberships and reissued the broken invites. The users should accept the newest email invites and should then see the shared projects normally.

subject: Need a checklist for changing our account admin after reorg
description: We had an internal reorg, and I was asked to take over platform administration from our director. Before we make any changes, I want a checklist of what to review: user roles, API tokens, billing contact, security notifications, and any settings that might still be tied to the outgoing admin. If anything requires back-end help, please let me know now so we can plan it properly.

comments.message: Can you give me a complete handoff checklist? We want to avoid missing hidden dependencies when we swap account responsibility.

Certainly. A solid admin handoff checklist includes: confirm at least two active admins; identify whether the outgoing person is the owner or just an admin; review user roles and group memberships; inventory integrations, API tokens, and any personal credentials; verify SSO/SCIM contacts; update billing, renewal, security, and legal/compliance contacts; test email sender settings and admin notifications; and document which changes are self-service versus support-assisted.
That's useful. Which of those usually need support involvement?

Common support-assisted items are ownership recovery when the current owner is unavailable, enterprise contact records stored outside the app, and in some cases subscription or legal-entity updates. Self-service items are often role changes, additional admins, branding updates, and notification settings, depending on plan permissions. My recommendation is to first promote the incoming admin, review all user- and integration-level dependencies, then submit any non-self-service contact or ownership changes together so they can be processed in one pass. If you share your plan tier, I can help narrow the checklist further.

subject: Need bulk reassignment before disabling departed users
description: I'm cleaning up our tenant after a reorg and need to deactivate 27 former employees. The problem is many of them still own dashboards, scheduled exports, and webhook-based integration flows. If I disable them one by one, I don't want those assets to disappear or our NetSuite integration jobs to fail. Is there a safe order for reassigning content and then removing access in bulk?

comments.message: We're offboarding a large group this week. Before I deactivate their accounts, I need to move their saved reports and integration-owned jobs to active managers. What's the best way to do this without losing data?

The safest sequence is: 1) export a list of the affected users and their owned assets, 2) bulk reassign dashboards/reports and any scheduled jobs to replacement owners, 3) verify integration credentials are not tied to personal accounts, and only then 4) deactivate the users. In Admin > User Management, the bulk action can disable access, but ownership transfer is handled separately from the content management area.
I found the report ownership transfer, but our NetSuite connector still shows jobs as owned by individual users. If I deactivate them, will the jobs stop?

If the connector was authorized with those users' personal OAuth tokens, yes, the jobs may fail after deactivation. Please reauthorize the NetSuite integration using a service account first, then reassign the integration jobs to that service account or another active admin. After that, you can bulk deactivate the former employees without affecting historical data.

subject: Embedded analytics panel blank for one customer segment
description: The embedded analytics panel in our app goes blank when users select the Enterprise segment, but other segments load normally. There is no error shown in the UI, just a spinner for a few seconds and then an empty panel. We can reproduce this in production and staging with the same dataset. Please help us determine whether this is a bug on your side.

comments.message: We checked browser console logs and saw a 500 on /insights/query for that segment only. Smaller segments work, and this was fine before we added two new custom fields last week.

A 500 on the query endpoint does suggest a server-side issue. I can't fix that directly from support, but I can start by gathering specifics for engineering. Can you send the workspace ID, one affected dashboard ID, and the names of the two custom fields that were added?
Workspace ws_9017, dashboard dash_44, and the new fields are Contract Tier and Renewal Owner.

Thank you. I attached those details and escalated this to our analytics engineering queue because the issue appears tied to a backend query failure for the Enterprise segment after schema changes. As a temporary workaround, try removing one of the newly added fields from the panel if possible; engineering will confirm whether one of them is causing the query planner to fail.

subject: SCIM-provisioned users can’t sign in after reactivation
description: We reactivated three employees in Azure AD and they were re-provisioned into your app through SCIM, but all three get “account inactive” when they try to log in. Their profiles show as active in our directory and visible in your user list. Our HR team needs them back in today.

comments.message: The timing is odd because new hires provision normally, but reactivated users do not. I checked group membership and licenses already.
If the users appear in the list, they should be able to access the app. Have they tried resetting their passwords?
These are SSO users. They do not use local passwords, and the error explicitly says account inactive.

Thanks, that helps narrow it down. I’m escalating this to our provisioning team because it sounds like the SCIM reactivation is not clearing the internal disabled flag on existing users. I’ve included the affected user IDs and your note that only reactivated accounts are impacted.

subject: La importación de eventos desde Calendly entra con una hora corrida
description: Integramos Calendly para crear reuniones automáticamente en su sistema, pero todas están entrando con una hora menos. Revisé la zona horaria del usuario y del evento, y en Calendly se ve correcto. El problema parece ocurrir solo desde su integración porque en el webhook original la hora viene bien.

comments.message: Estamos en horario de verano de España. Antes funcionaba bien y el desfase empezó este fin de semana.

Gracias, eso suena relacionado con el cambio estacional. Para acotarlo: ¿las reuniones se guardan con una hora menos en todos los usuarios o solo en uno? Y si revisa un evento importado, ¿nuestra plataforma lo muestra en UTC o como Europe/Madrid?
Lo probamos con tres usuarios y a todos les pasa. En el detalle del registro veo que guarda el timestamp como UTC, pero luego la conversión parece quedar mal.

Entendido. Eso apunta a un posible fallo en cómo el conector de Calendly está aplicando el ajuste de horario de verano al convertir desde UTC. No tengo una corrección inmediata desde soporte, pero ya abrí un caso con ingeniería incluyendo su ejemplo del fin de semana y la región Europe/Madrid. Mientras tanto, la única alternativa temporal es corregir la hora con una automatización posterior o editar manualmente las reuniones críticas hasta que publiquemos el parche.

subject: Não consigo dar acesso ao relatório personalizado para novos gestores
description: Sou administradora da conta e criei dois novos gestores hoje. Eles conseguem entrar no sistema, mas não conseguem ver um relatório personalizado de performance que já existe há meses. Eu já atribuí o papel de gerente e coloquei os dois no mesmo grupo dos outros gestores que veem esse relatório. Preciso liberar isso ainda hoje.

comments.message: Os usuários são a Renata e o Caio. Ambos aparecem como ativos e no grupo 'Gestores Regionais'. Mesmo assim, ao abrir Relatórios, eles só veem os relatórios padrão.

Olá! Obrigado pelas informações. Além do papel e do grupo, relatórios personalizados podem estar restritos ao proprietário original ou a uma lista de compartilhamento. Você pode abrir o relatório, clicar em 'Compartilhar' e confirmar se ele está definido para 'Todos no grupo Gestores Regionais'?
Fui verificar e estava compartilhado só com usuários específicos antigos. Mudei para o grupo. A Renata já consegue ver, mas o Caio ainda não.

Ótimo avanço. Se apenas o Caio ainda não vê, provavelmente o cache de permissões dele ainda não atualizou. Peça para ele sair e entrar novamente ou aguardar alguns minutos. Se persistir após 15 minutos, me envie o e-mail dele para eu validar a associação de grupo no backend.

subject: HubSpot install finished, but custom lifecycle field won't map
description: I'm trying to connect HubSpot and use one of our custom lifecycle fields inside your scoring workflow. The install succeeded and standard fields are syncing, but our custom property doesn't appear in the mapping dropdown. I need to know if I'm missing a setup step or if there are limits on which fields can be used.

comments.message: The HubSpot integration is active and I can see contacts syncing, but a custom property we rely on for lifecycle stage is missing from your field mapping list. How do I make it available?

Custom HubSpot properties should show up automatically after the initial metadata sync, so first click "Refresh schema" in Integrations > HubSpot. Also check that the property is created on the same object type you're mapping—contact properties won't appear in company mappings, for example.
I refreshed the schema twice. The property is on contacts, and I'm editing a contact mapping. Still not there.

In that case, please verify the HubSpot property type. Our scoring workflows only support text, number, date, and single-select fields for mapping; calculation and multi-checkbox properties are excluded from the dropdown. If your lifecycle field is one of those unsupported types, the best workaround is to mirror it into a supported custom property in HubSpot and sync that field instead.

subject: Eingeladene Benutzer erhalten keine Aktivierungs-E-Mails
description: Ich habe heute mehrere neue Kollegen über die Benutzerverwaltung eingeladen. Die Konten stehen auf „Ausstehend“, aber niemand hat eine Aktivierungs-E-Mail bekommen. Spam-Ordner sind leer. Wir müssen die Nutzer heute noch onboarden und ich brauche entweder eine schnelle Ursache oder eine alternative Methode.

comments.message: Die betroffenen Adressen liegen alle auf unserer Firmen-Domain. Einzelne externe Testadressen haben die Mail ebenfalls nicht erhalten.
Bitte prüfen Sie zunächst, ob Ihr Mailserver unsere Absenderdomain blockiert. Häufig liegt es an SPF oder DKIM.
Das hilft mir nicht wirklich, weil auch externe Gmail-Testadressen nichts bekommen haben. Gibt es bei Ihnen derzeit ein Problem mit Einladungen?

Ich habe gerade nachgesehen: Es gibt aktuell eine verzögerte Zustellung bei transaktionalen E-Mails in einem Teil unserer Region. Ich kann keine genaue Wiederherstellungszeit nennen, aber die Benutzerverwaltung selbst ist nicht betroffen. Als Workaround können Sie Benutzer vorab per SSO bereitstellen oder nach Entstörung die Einladungen erneut senden.

subject: NetSuite revenue report showing duplicate invoices
description: I'm trying to close the month and our revenue summary report in your platform is double-counting some invoices coming from the NetSuite integration. The duplicates only appear in the report export, not always in the invoice detail pages, which is making reconciliation messy. I need someone to explain whether this is due to the integration mapping or the reporting logic and help us get this fixed quickly.

comments.message: I found at least 12 invoices that appear twice in the Revenue Summary report after yesterday's NetSuite sync. They share the same invoice number but have different internal IDs in your CSV export. What's going on?
This usually happens when the report cache hasn't refreshed after a sync. Please clear your browser cache and regenerate the export.
I already did that in two browsers, and the duplicate rows are still in the CSV. Also, our finance analyst sees the same thing.

Thanks for confirming. I took a closer look and can see the NetSuite integration is ingesting both the invoice record and the credit-memo-adjusted version as separate rows because your current connector setting is keyed on internal ID instead of document number. Reporting is then counting both. I can't safely change that deduplication key from support, so I'm escalating this to our financial integrations team. As a temporary workaround, filter the report to Status = Posted and Exclude Adjustment Copies = Yes; that removes most duplicates, but not all historical ones.

subject: Exportação de relatório inclui usuários desativados
description: Desativei alguns colaboradores antigos no módulo de administração de usuários, mas eles continuam aparecendo na exportação CSV do relatório de utilização. Na tela do relatório eu marco para ver somente usuários ativos, porém no arquivo exportado os desativados ainda vêm juntos. Isso está confundindo o financeiro aqui.

comments.message: Os usuários foram desativados no começo do mês. Na visualização da tela parece certo, mas quando baixo o CSV aparecem nomes de pessoas desligadas com status vazio.

Olá! Entendi o problema. O filtro de 'ativos' na tela nem sempre é aplicado na exportação quando o relatório usa um modelo salvo antigo. Você consegue me dizer se está abrindo um relatório salvo antes de desativar esses usuários?
Sim, é um relatório salvo que usamos todo mês. Mas mesmo duplicando o modelo o CSV continua trazendo os desativados.
Obrigado por testar. Nesse caso, pode ser uma limitação da exportação do relatório legado. Como alternativa, sugiro excluir os usuários permanentemente em Administração para que não apareçam mais.
Excluir permanentemente não é opção por auditoria. Precisamos manter histórico.

Entendo. Então vou encaminhar para o time responsável por relatórios porque a desativação deveria respeitar o filtro de exportação sem exigir exclusão. Enquanto isso, a única saída prática é filtrar manualmente a coluna de status no CSV.

subject: Can't access sandbox and deployment automation is stuck in pending
description: I can log into production, but the sandbox keeps rejecting my credentials with 'account not found' even though I'm listed as an admin. We were also trying to push a workflow automation change from sandbox to production, and that deployment is stuck in pending. If these environments are out of sync, please escalate because our release window is today.

comments.message: I can log into production, but the sandbox keeps rejecting my credentials with 'account not found' even though I'm listed as an admin. We were also trying to push a workflow automation change from sandbox to production, and that deployment is stuck in pending. If these environments are out of sync, please escalate because our release window is today.

I checked and your production and sandbox environments use separate user directories unless sandbox SSO mirroring is enabled. I don’t see your user provisioned in sandbox, so the login failure is expected. The deployment is stuck because it was initiated by your sandbox user ID, which no longer resolves after a prior directory reset. I’ve manually canceled the pending deployment and escalated to our environment management team to reprovision your sandbox admin access. Once they do that, you should be able to log in and redeploy the workflow change.
We thought mirroring was enabled. Is there any way to verify that before the escalation comes back?
