From my side, the feature flag for sandbox SSO mirroring shows disabled on your account. The environment team will confirm whether that was intentionally turned off or lost during the reset, and I’ve asked them to include the timeline in their response.

subject: Campos obligatorios bloquean actualización masiva por API y por CSV
description: Estoy intentando actualizar el campo "Estado de onboarding" para 1.800 registros, pero tanto por API como por importación CSV el proceso falla porque ahora hay un nuevo campo obligatorio llamado "Propietario regional". El problema es que solo quiero actualizar un campo existente y no modificar el resto de los datos. ¿Hay alguna forma de hacer una actualización parcial sin rellenar todos los obligatorios?

comments.message: Estoy intentando actualizar el campo "Estado de onboarding" para 1.800 registros, pero tanto por API como por importación CSV el proceso falla porque ahora hay un nuevo campo obligatorio llamado "Propietario regional". El problema es que solo quiero actualizar un campo existente y no modificar el resto de los datos. ¿Hay alguna forma de hacer una actualización parcial sin rellenar todos los obligatorios?

Sí. En actualizaciones masivas, los campos obligatorios nuevos pueden bloquear tanto importaciones como llamadas API si la validación está configurada para aplicarse también en edición, no solo en creación. Revise la configuración del campo "Propietario regional" y cambie la regla a "obligatorio al crear" si ese comportamiento encaja con su proceso. Alternativamente, incluya en el CSV o payload el identificador del registro y el valor actual de "Propietario regional" para cada fila, aunque eso es más pesado.
No quiero cambiar la regla porque sí necesitamos que sea obligatorio cuando alguien edita manualmente. ¿Se puede saltar esa validación solo para importaciones?

Actualmente no hay un bypass por canal a nivel estándar. Algunas cuentas enterprise tienen una opción de validaciones diferenciadas para API/importación, pero no está disponible en todos los workspaces. Como solución práctica, puede exportar el campo obligatorio actual, combinarlo con su actualización en el CSV y volver a importar con modo "actualizar existentes". Si quiere, le indico cómo hacer el cruce por ID para no tocar otras columnas.

subject: Configuration des champs conditionnels dans le portail
description: Je tente de configurer des champs conditionnels pour que le portail affiche des questions différentes selon le type de demande, mais certains champs apparaissent dans le mauvais ordre et d'autres restent visibles même quand la condition n'est pas remplie. J'ai besoin d'une explication claire parce que je dois former notre équipe support à maintenir ce formulaire ensuite.

comments.message: Bonjour, quand un client choisit “Incident”, je veux afficher “Impact” et “Service affecté”. Quand il choisit “Question”, seul le champ “Produit” devrait rester. J'ai fait des dépendances entre champs, mais le résultat n'est pas cohérent. Avez-vous une méthode simple à suivre ?
Bonjour, dans ce cas vous devez probablement recréer entièrement le formulaire, car les champs conditionnels existants se corrompent parfois. Le plus rapide est de repartir de zéro.
Repartir de zéro sur un formulaire déjà en production n'est pas idéal. Je cherche surtout à comprendre la logique pour corriger et documenter la procédure pour l'équipe.

Je comprends. Voici une méthode moins risquée : définissez d'abord un champ parent unique, par exemple “Type de demande”, puis associez chaque champ enfant à une seule valeur du parent. Évitez de chaîner un enfant sur un autre enfant, car l'affichage devient difficile à prévoir. Ensuite, réordonnez les champs dans l'éditeur principal, car la condition contrôle la visibilité mais pas toujours la position finale. Pour la formation de votre équipe, conseillez-leur de tester chaque branche du formulaire avec un tableau simple : valeur choisie, champs attendus, champs réellement visibles. Si vous voulez, je peux vous fournir un exemple de matrice de test.

subject: Automation rule fired twice on the same ticket
description: One of our escalation automations is posting two identical internal notes and triggering the Slack alert twice, but only on some tickets. I want to know whether this is a platform bug or something misconfigured in our rule logic. Please also explain how automation evaluation works when both a workflow and a legacy rule are enabled, because I'm not confident we understand the order of operations.

comments.message: The duplicate actions happened on 14 tickets yesterday. We recently turned on the new workflow builder but left our older rule in place because we weren't sure if it still mattered.

From the audit logs, I can see both the legacy automation and the workflow builder flow matched the same condition set and each executed its own actions. So the double Slack post itself is not a processing bug; it's a configuration overlap. However, I also noticed one ticket where the workflow run was retried after a timeout, which may be why an internal note duplicated even within the new system. I'm still checking that part.
So should we disable the legacy rule, or is there a safer migration path?

Yes, I recommend disabling the legacy rule after cloning it into a test workflow and validating the trigger conditions. In terms of product behavior, automations are evaluated independently by each engine when both are active; there isn't automatic deduplication between legacy rules and workflows. The possible retry-related duplicate note is something I've sent to engineering with the affected ticket IDs, since that does appear bug-like.

subject: Owner login blocked after email alias change
description: I changed my login email from sara@northpeak.io to admin@northpeak.io last night. The confirmation email came through and now the old address no longer works, but the new address says the user does not exist. I’m the workspace owner, so I can’t ask another admin to fix this for me.

comments.message: I can still see billing receipts coming to admin@northpeak.io, so the change partly went through somewhere. I just can’t access the product.
Email changes can take a few minutes to propagate. Please wait a bit longer and try again.
It has been 14 hours. This is not a propagation delay.

Agreed. Since this is an owner access issue likely involving a partially applied identity change, I’m escalating to our account access specialists. They can inspect the user record on the backend and either complete or roll back the email update after verifying ownership.

subject: Mesclagem de empresas uniu domínios diferentes
description: Eu aceitei as sugestões de deduplicação de empresas e o sistema mesclou duas contas que não deveriam ser unidas, porque tinham nomes parecidos mas domínios e CNPJs diferentes. Agora o histórico de interações ficou todo misturado. Eu preciso separar isso de novo ou pelo menos recuperar os dados originais de cada empresa. Qual é o procedimento?

comments.message: Eu aceitei as sugestões de deduplicação de empresas e o sistema mesclou duas contas que não deveriam ser unidas, porque tinham nomes parecidos mas domínios e CNPJs diferentes. Agora o histórico de interações ficou todo misturado.

Quando uma mesclagem incorreta acontece, não é possível 'desmesclar' diretamente pela interface. O recomendado é abrir o registro resultante, revisar quais campos ficaram como vencedores e recriar manualmente a empresa que foi absorvida. Para recuperar os dados originais, consulte o log de auditoria e exportações anteriores.
Mas o histórico de notas, atividades e associações também foi combinado. Recriar manualmente não resolve tudo.

Você tem razão. Para notas, atividades e associações combinadas, a correção manual fica incompleta. Vou registrar uma solicitação para o time de plataforma verificar se ainda temos um snapshot anterior à mesclagem e se é possível restaurar os objetos afetados. Enquanto isso, evite novas mesclagens nesses registros para não dificultar a recuperação.

subject: NetSuite invoice sync times out in bulk renewal automation
description: Our renewal workflow generates invoices in NetSuite when opportunities move to Closed Won. For single deals it works, but during end-of-month bulk updates the NetSuite step times out on about half the records. We need to know whether the integration is overloaded or whether the workflow is sending bad data when many runs happen at once.

comments.message: The failures mostly happen between 5 and 6 PM when sales ops closes a batch of renewals. The error in the run log just says request timeout after 30 seconds.

This sounds like rate limiting on the NetSuite side combined with too much parallelism in the automation. Try adding a queue or delay between records so the integration step does not submit all invoice creates at once.
We do not currently have any throttle configured. Is there a recommended setting for batch operations?

For NetSuite we typically suggest limiting the workflow to 5 concurrent runs and inserting a 10-15 second delay before the invoice creation step during bulk updates. Also check whether your automation sends line-item expansions or tax lookups, because those increase payload size and push calls closer to timeout. If you need a more precise threshold, I can escalate this to our integrations team with a few failed run IDs.

subject: Integración con NetSuite fallando por token expirado
description: Nuestra integración con NetSuite dejó de traer facturas esta madrugada. En el historial aparece un error de autenticación y luego varios intentos fallidos. Ya generé un nuevo token en NetSuite, pero en su plataforma no encuentro claramente dónde actualizarlo sin romper la conexión actual.

comments.message: También me preocupa perder el mapeo de campos si desconecto todo, porque costó bastante configurarlo.

No hace falta desconectar la integración completa. En conectores con NetSuite el token se puede actualizar desde Integraciones > NetSuite > Credenciales avanzadas, y eso no borra el mapeo de campos ni las reglas de importación. Antes de guardarlo, confirme que el token nuevo tenga el mismo rol y permisos que el anterior.
Encontré la sección y actualicé el token, pero ahora sale un error distinto: "insufficient permission for search on transaction".

Eso indica que la autenticación ya funciona, pero el nuevo token o el rol asociado no tiene permiso para consultar transacciones/facturas. Como ya no es solo renovación de credenciales sino un tema de permisos en NetSuite, voy a escalarlo a nuestro equipo de integraciones para revisar la lista exacta de privilegios requeridos y validar su configuración. Mientras tanto, no desconecte el conector; el mapeo quedará intacto.

subject: Account locked after MFA reset and backup codes rejected
description: I reset MFA for one of our department managers after she got a new phone. She can enter her email and password, but then the backup codes say invalid and the page eventually tells her the account is locked for too many attempts. I'm the workspace admin, but I don't see a way to fully unlock her. Is there a bug in the MFA reset process, or is there another step I'm missing?

comments.message: I already clicked 'Reset two-factor authentication' from the admin console yesterday. She never got prompted to enroll a new device; it just kept asking for old verification methods.

If you reset MFA, the user should normally be asked to enroll again at next sign-in. Please have her clear cookies and try a different browser, as stale sessions can sometimes keep the old challenge in place.
We tried Chrome incognito and Safari on mobile with the same result. Now the account is locked so we can't test further.

Thanks for trying that. I checked our internal notes and there is an open defect where MFA resets don't fully clear remembered challenge states for locked accounts. I can't safely unlock and purge that state from chat support, so I've escalated this to our access team to manually clear the lock and reset the MFA enrollment. Once they do, she should receive the new enrollment prompt on next login. In the meantime, please do not retry backup codes, since that will keep extending the lockout window.

subject: Custom field updates are overwriting nulls and breaking user sync
description: We sync user profiles from our identity system and store cost center, region, and manager info in custom fields. Recently, our API updates started wiping out existing values whenever the source sends null, and after that several admins lost visibility into users outside their region. I need to preserve data correctly during sync and understand whether our admin scoping depends on those same fields.

comments.message: Our integration sends a full user payload every night. If cost_center is null upstream, your API seems to blank the field here too. That part is annoying but manageable; the bigger problem is that regional admins suddenly can't manage users they could see last week.

Null values in update payloads generally clear nullable custom fields unless you omit the field entirely. So for preservation, your sync should send partial updates or strip null keys before calling the API. On the visibility issue, if your admin scopes are based on region or another profile field, clearing those values can absolutely reduce what those admins are allowed to see or manage.
That matches what happened. We accidentally blanked region on 400 users. Can support restore yesterday's values?

We don't have a self-serve rollback for custom user fields from support, but if you send the affected user IDs we can ask operations whether a backup restore is possible at the field level. In the meantime, the fastest fix is usually to rerun your identity sync with the correct region values and avoid sending nulls for optional attributes.

subject: Prośba o zmianę właściciela konta i brak dostępu po migracji
description: Jestem administratorem naszego workspace i po odejściu poprzedniego właściciela konta próbuję przepisać własność na siebie. W ustawieniach widzę stary adres e-mail jako właściciela, ale ta osoba nie pracuje już w firmie. Dodatkowo po ostatniej migracji SSO nie mogę wejść w zakładkę administracyjną — strona ładuje się bez końca. Potrzebuję zmienić właściciela konta i odzyskać dostęp do ustawień.

comments.message: Dzień dobry, nie mogę zmienić właściciela konta, bo obecny owner już nie pracuje w firmie. Po kliknięciu Ustawienia > Administracja mam tylko kręcące się kółko. Sprawa pilna, bo musimy dodać nowy zespół.

Dzień dobry, rozumiem. Żeby zmienić właściciela konta, muszę najpierw potwierdzić uprawnienia. Proszę o domenę firmową, ID workspace oraz adres e-mail, na który mamy przepisać własność. W kwestii ładowania panelu proszę też sprawdzić, czy problem występuje w trybie incognito i czy przeglądarka blokuje cookies stron trzecich.
Domena to nextra.pl, workspace ID 44721, nowy właściciel: admin@nextra.pl. W incognito jest to samo, na Chrome i Edge też. W konsoli widzę błąd 403 przy /admin/bootstrap.

Dziękuję. To wygląda na problem z uprawnieniami po wdrożeniu SSO, więc przekazuję zmianę właściciela do zespołu kont. Tymczasowo proszę, aby nowy właściciel został dodany jako administrator przez innego admina, jeśli jest taka możliwość. Co do błędu 403, wygląda na konflikt mapowania ról po SSO; eskaluję to do zespołu technicznego z informacją o endpointcie /admin/bootstrap. Odezwiemy się po weryfikacji.

subject: API token works for one user but login says account suspended
description: One of our automation accounts can still call the API successfully, but when I try to log in through the UI as that same user to rotate the token, I get an 'account suspended' message. We didn't suspend it ourselves. Since the token still works, I'm worried the account status is inconsistent and could break our integration unexpectedly.

comments.message: Service user integrations@acme.io has been posting data all day through the API, but if I try to sign in as that user, the login page says the account is suspended. I need access to rotate the token before month-end.
If the account were suspended, the API would fail too, so I think the login message is probably harmless. You can just keep using the existing token until it expires.
That doesn't really help because we need to rotate credentials now, and the mismatch itself seems wrong.

You're right. I checked the backend status and the user is in a mixed state: flagged suspended in authentication, active in the API gateway cache. That's a bug, and continuing to use the token could lead to a sudden outage when caches refresh. I've sent this to the identity team for immediate correction and asked them to prioritize restoring UI access so you can rotate the token safely.

subject: Logowanie z poprawnym hasłem kończy się błędem 403
description: Od rana nie mogę wejść do panelu. Hasło jest na pewno poprawne, bo menedżer haseł je uzupełnia i na innym koncie działa. U mnie po kliknięciu „Zaloguj” od razu pojawia się błąd 403.

comments.message: Przy logowaniu na moje konto od razu dostaję 403, mimo że hasło jest poprawne.
Błąd 403 zwykle oznacza problem z uprawnieniami. Proszę wyczyścić ciasteczka i pamięć podręczną przeglądarki.
Zrobiłem to już wcześniej, także w trybie incognito. Nadal 403.

Sprawdziłem dokładniej i widzę, że adres IP, z którego Pan się loguje, trafił dziś rano na automatyczną listę blokad po serii podejrzanych prób logowania do wielu kont. To dlatego uwierzytelnienie kończy się 403 jeszcze przed pełnym wejściem do aplikacji.
Pracuję z biura i pewnie cały zespół wychodzi przez jeden adres IP. Czy da się to odblokować?

Tak, ale nie mogę zdjąć tej blokady samodzielnie z poziomu wsparcia pierwszej linii. Przekazałem zgłoszenie do zespołu bezpieczeństwa z oznaczeniem pilne i dodałem Państwa biurowy IP do wniosku o allowlistę. Do czasu decyzji proszę spróbować zalogować się z sieci komórkowej lub VPN z innym adresem.

subject: Need to train a new admin on permission sets without breaking anything
description: I’ve just taken over as system owner, and I’m trying to train a new regional admin on how permission sets work in your platform. While we were reviewing access, she accidentally removed edit rights from a small user group, so now I’m nervous about making more changes. Can you help me troubleshoot how to verify exactly what changed, and also explain a safer way to teach someone permissions management without affecting live users?

comments.message: I’ve just taken over as system owner, and I’m trying to train a new regional admin on how permission sets work in your platform. While we were reviewing access, she accidentally removed edit rights from a small user group, so now I’m nervous about making more changes. Can you help me troubleshoot how to verify exactly what changed, and also explain a safer way to teach someone permissions management without affecting live users?

Yes — first check the admin audit log for permission-set edits around the time the access changed. That should show which role or permission set was modified and by whom. Then compare the current permission set against a working one, paying particular attention to object-level edit rights, field-level restrictions, and whether the affected group inherits access from multiple roles. For training, it’s safest to use a sandbox or a test group with non-production users, document a baseline before each exercise, and make one change at a time so the effect is easy to observe and roll back.

I found the audit entry. She removed edit access on the 'Projects' object from a shared permission set that also applies to the pilot team. I restored it, but I’m still unclear on how to let her practice without giving broad admin rights.

A good middle ground is to create a limited admin role that can manage users and view permission configurations without being able to publish changes to shared production permission sets. If your plan includes sandbox admins, give her that access there first and use a copy of the pilot setup for exercises. If not, create a training permission set assigned only to test users, and walk her through permission concepts using those accounts: base role, additional permission sets, and the difference between view, edit, and admin privileges. That way she still learns the model, but mistakes won’t affect live teams.

subject: Former contractor still receives mentions after deactivation
description: We deactivated a contractor last Friday, but teammates can still @mention her name in projects and she continues to receive email notifications from those mentions. I checked that her user status is inactive and removed her from all visible groups. We’re trying to confirm whether this is a notification bug or if I missed some admin setting. This is a compliance concern, so I’d like a concrete answer rather than general docs.

comments.message: A former contractor is inactive, but people can still @mention her and she is still receiving email notifications.
If the user is inactive, they should not receive notifications. Please make sure they are removed from all projects and groups, and ask them to log out of all devices.
She’s already removed from groups, and she no longer has access to log in. The issue is that our staff can still mention her name and those emails continue going out.

Thanks for clarifying. I’d like to troubleshoot whether this is limited to cached mentions or active notification jobs. Please tell me if this happens in all projects or only older ones created before deactivation, and whether the mentions appear in the typeahead search immediately after refresh.
It happens in older projects only, and yes, her name still appears in mention suggestions after refresh.

That points to stale project membership indexes rather than a live user-session issue. I’ve escalated this to our messaging platform team because inactive users should be suppressed from mention autocomplete and notification queues. For now, please remove her from any legacy project follower lists via the project settings page if available; that may stop the emails even before engineering clears the index.

subject: Unexpected charge after switching sandbox to production
description: I moved one of our workspaces from sandbox to production last week so we could go live with our customer portal. Since then, our invoice shows an extra platform fee and a second usage line item that I wasn't expecting. I may have configured something wrong when I enabled the live environment, but I can't tell whether this is a billing bug or tied to how the workspace was set up. Can someone explain what triggered the charges and how to correct it if the production workspace was configured incorrectly?

comments.message: We had a sandbox workspace for testing, then I enabled production and connected our live domain. Our latest invoice now includes a new platform subscription plus usage charges for both environments. I thought sandbox was non-billable. Did I accidentally create a second paid instance when I changed the workspace settings?

I checked your account and I can see that when production mode was enabled, a separate billable workspace named "Northwind Live" was created because the "Create standalone production workspace" option was selected during setup. Sandbox itself remains non-billable, but the new production workspace has its own base fee and usage meter. If you intended to convert the existing sandbox instead, we can merge the configuration and credit the duplicate base charge for this cycle.

That sounds likely. We copied settings from sandbox and didn't realize that option would create a second paid workspace. If you merge them, will the custom SSO and approval rules carry over, and can the duplicate charge be removed?

Yes — I can see your SSO, approval routing, and custom fields are all attached to the live workspace config, so our billing team can issue a prorated credit for the duplicate workspace once ops merges the environments. I've opened the merge request and tagged billing to reverse the extra platform fee on invoice INV-48219. You should receive confirmation within one business day.

subject: Workflow does not send renewal reminder to contacts added after enrollment
description: I created an automated renewal reminder workflow for customer accounts 30 days before contract end. The emails are sent to the primary contact that exists when the account enters the workflow, but if we add a new billing contact later, that person never gets the reminder. I need to know whether the automation can pick up newly added contacts automatically.

comments.message: Our workflow enrolls accounts based on contract_end_date and sends a reminder email to associated billing contacts. We added two new billing contacts to an account last week, but only the original contact received the message yesterday. I expected all current billing contacts to be included at send time.
Associated records are snapshotted when the workflow starts, so later-added contacts won't be picked up. The only way around it is to clone the workflow for each contact type.
That seems pretty limiting. Is there really no way to reevaluate associations right before the email step?

I understand the frustration. At least in the standard builder, there isn't a live association refresh on delayed sends that I can offer from support here. You may want to submit this as a feature request.

subject: Doppelte Abbuchung nach Tarifwechsel
description: Ich habe gestern unseren Plan von Standard auf Pro umgestellt und heute gesehen, dass auf unserer Kreditkarte zwei Abbuchungen vom gleichen Betrag aufgetaucht sind. Im Abrechnungsbereich steht nur eine Rechnung, aber die Bank zeigt zwei Belastungen. Können Sie bitte prüfen, welche Buchung korrekt ist und die doppelte Abbuchung stornieren?

comments.message: Ich habe gestern unseren Plan von Standard auf Pro umgestellt und heute gesehen, dass auf unserer Kreditkarte zwei Abbuchungen vom gleichen Betrag aufgetaucht sind. Im Abrechnungsbereich steht nur eine Rechnung, aber die Bank zeigt zwei Belastungen. Können Sie bitte prüfen, welche Buchung korrekt ist und die doppelte Abbuchung stornieren?

Gerne prüfe ich das für Sie. Ich sehe in Ihrem Konto eine erfolgreiche Zahlung und eine zweite Autorisierung, die noch nicht gebucht wurde. Das passiert manchmal nach einem Tarifwechsel. Die vorgemerkte Belastung sollte innerhalb von 3 bis 5 Werktagen automatisch verschwinden. Ich habe zusätzlich ein Ticket an unser Billing-Team gesetzt, damit die Zahlung überwacht wird. Falls die zweite Belastung danach noch sichtbar ist, antworten Sie bitte direkt hier.

subject: Former contractor still able to sign in
description: We offboarded a contractor yesterday by removing her from our user list, but she says she can still sign in and see old project data. I need to understand whether the account was actually disabled or if some cached session is letting her back in. This is an urgent access issue.

comments.message: We offboarded a contractor yesterday by removing her from our user list, but she says she can still sign in and see old project data. I need to understand whether the account was actually disabled or if some cached session is letting her back in. This is an urgent access issue.

Thank you for flagging this. I checked your audit log and the user was removed from the workspace, but her existing session token remained valid because 'revoke active sessions on user removal' is not enabled for your tenant. I've now invalidated all active sessions for that user from our side. For future offboarding, you should enable session revocation in Security settings so access is cut off immediately.
Thanks. Is there any indication she downloaded data after we removed her?

I can see one successful login before removal and no export or download events after the removal timestamp. The last authenticated API request from her session was 14 minutes after offboarding, but it was read-only navigation to previously accessible pages. I've added the exact timestamps to the internal case notes in case you need them for audit review.

subject: Login via Google works, API key page says permission denied
description: I use Google SSO to access our account and everything in the app seems fine except the API keys page. When I open it, I get 'permission denied,' even though I'm an admin and should be able to create keys. Another admin who uses email/password can access it. We need this for a migration today, so I'm trying to tell if this is a permissions problem or some SSO bug.

comments.message: My role is Admin and I can manage users, projects, and billing. Only API Keys is blocked. The page sometimes flashes and reloads after SSO login before showing permission denied.
API key management is only available to Owners, so the permission denied message is expected.
That's not correct in our plan. Another admin in the same workspace can create them, and your docs say Admins can manage API keys on Enterprise.

You're correct; I misspoke. Looking closer, your workspace is on Enterprise and Admins should have access. Because the issue only affects Google-authenticated admins, this points to a bug in how SSO-mapped roles are being evaluated by the API key service. I've escalated it and included your comparison with the password-based admin account.

subject: User export report doesn’t match admin page counts
description: I exported a user report for our monthly audit, and the number of users in the CSV doesn’t match the count shown on the User Administration page. I need to explain the discrepancy to finance, but I can’t tell whether the report includes suspended users, archived users, or some other category. I’d appreciate both an explanation and what to check so I can fix the report if needed.

comments.message: The admin page shows 412 users total, but my export has 437 rows. I used the standard user inventory report with no custom filters. Why would the report be higher than the user admin count?
The report should always match the admin page exactly, so this sounds like a bug. Please clear your browser cache and rerun it.
I did that. Could the report be including deactivated users?

Yes, that’s possible, and I should have mentioned it earlier. Reporting datasets often include inactive or historical user records unless filtered, while the User Administration page may default to current managed users only. Please add a filter for account status and compare again. For finance, you can explain that administrative counts and reporting exports can differ when the report includes users retained for audit history.

subject: Microsoft Teams message posts before approval workflow is finished
description: We have a workflow that routes purchase requests for manager approval and then posts the final decision into a Teams channel. Since we added an auto-reminder branch, the Teams message is being sent right after submission instead of waiting for the approval outcome. The Teams integration works, but it is clearly firing at the wrong point in the automation.

comments.message: I checked yesterday’s runs and the Teams message step executes before the approval decision node. It looks like the reminder branch may have changed the order somehow.
That should not happen if the step order is correct. Please move the Teams action lower in the workflow so it appears visually after the approval node and then publish again.
I already did that. The canvas order changed, but the execution path still sends the message immediately on new requests.

I see. In our workflow builder, the visual position does not control execution — the connected branch does. The Teams action is probably still attached to the submission path rather than the Approved/Rejected outputs of the approval node. Please delete the current connector into the Teams step and reconnect it from the explicit approval result branch. If reminders are enabled, make sure the reminder path loops back only to the approval node and not to the notification action.

subject: Need to rotate API key without breaking our user provisioning jobs
description: Our security team requires us to rotate API credentials every 90 days. We use your API for creating users, disabling leavers, and updating manager relationships. I can generate a new key, but I can't tell whether both keys can remain valid during a cutover window, and I don't want our overnight provisioning run to fail mid-rotation. What's the recommended process?

comments.message: We have two separate scripts using the same key today: one for joins/moves/leavers and one for weekly access cleanup. Both touch user administration endpoints.
You should be able to rotate the key by deleting the old one and then updating your scripts with the new value immediately after. There isn't usually any downtime if you do it quickly.
That sounds risky for scheduled jobs. Is there any overlap period where both keys work, or do we need a maintenance window?

I checked our current behavior and there is a safer path: each admin can have up to two active API keys at once. Create the new key, update both provisioning jobs to use it, validate with a test user create/update/deactivate cycle, and then revoke the old key after the next successful scheduled run. If you want, I can outline a rollback plan as well.

subject: Help me explain duplicate contact prevention to sales ops
description: I'm trying to coach our sales ops team on why duplicate contacts still get created even though we turned on duplicate prevention. They assume the feature should block every duplicate, but clearly that's not happening. I need a simple explanation of what the matching rules actually do, what common gaps are, and when manual review is still necessary.

comments.message: I'm trying to coach our sales ops team on why duplicate contacts still get created even though we turned on duplicate prevention. They assume the feature should block every duplicate, but clearly that's not happening. I need a simple explanation of what the matching rules actually do, what common gaps are, and when manual review is still necessary.

Duplicate prevention compares incoming records against selected fields and applies the matching logic you've configured, such as exact email match or combinations like name plus company. It reduces duplicates, but it won't catch every case — especially if data arrives with typos, alternate emails, formatting differences, or missing fields. A good way to explain it internally is that the feature is a filter based on defined rules, not a human-level identity check.
Perfect. We also import leads from three systems, and some duplicates seem to come from one connector only. Should we just tighten the matching rules globally?

Not necessarily. Tightening rules globally can create false positives and block legitimate records from your other sources. Since one connector seems to be the outlier, I'm escalating this to our integrations team so they can review whether that source bypasses part of the normal matching flow or sends data in a format that weakens matching. In the meantime, I'd tell sales ops that manual review is still needed for edge cases and inconsistent source data.

subject: Gráfico por agente fica em branco ao aplicar comparação com período anterior
description: Quando eu abro um relatório personalizado de desempenho por agente e ativo a opção de comparar com o período anterior, o gráfico some completamente e aparece só o eixo vazio. Sem comparação ele carrega normal. Isso começou ontem e está acontecendo com outros gestores aqui também. Quero saber se é um erro conhecido e se existe alguma forma de recuperar a visualização.

comments.message: O relatório afetado é um gráfico de barras com atendimentos resolvidos por agente. Se eu marco “comparar com período anterior”, a página fica carregando e depois aparece o gráfico em branco, sem barras nenhuma.
Olá! Obrigado por reportar. Esse tipo de comportamento geralmente está ligado ao navegador. Poderia testar em outro browser e desabilitar extensões, principalmente bloqueadores?
Já testei no Chrome e no Edge, com extensões desativadas. Também pedi para outra pessoa do time abrir e aconteceu igual. No console aparece erro 500 numa chamada para /reports/compare.

Entendi. Nesse caso, não parece ser algo local. Fiz um teste rápido aqui e realmente a rota de comparação está falhando para alguns relatórios agregados por agente. Vou escalar para o time responsável por Relatórios com a evidência do erro 500. Enquanto isso, a única alternativa é duplicar o relatório e abrir dois períodos lado a lado, porque a comparação embutida está indisponível no momento.

subject: Invoice shows seats for pending invites that were never accepted
description: I invited six new hires last month, but only two of them ever accepted. The other four are still sitting in pending status in user administration. Our current invoice includes all six as billable users, which doesn’t seem right.

comments.message: Under Admin > Users, four accounts are marked "Invited" and have never logged in. Why are they counted as paid seats on invoice #88431?

Pending invites should not normally become billable until the user activates the account, unless a seat was manually reserved under your subscription settings. Let me check your workspace configuration and invoice line items.
Okay, thank you. We did import the users via CSV if that matters.

That does matter. Your workspace has "reserve seats for invited users" enabled, which was turned on during a bulk onboarding project in March. I’ve disabled that setting so future invites won’t hold paid seats, and I’ve credited the four reserved seats from invoice #88431. You’ll see the credit memo in the billing tab shortly.

subject: Need secondary admin promoted and API audit logs exported before deactivating old user
description: I'm trying to promote our security lead to admin and deactivate my own account because I'm leaving the company this week. Before we do that, we need to export API audit logs tied to my user and confirm our personal access token usage is replaced. I already tried the export endpoint, but it only returns the last 7 days instead of 90. Please advise on the proper sequence and help if this needs a specialist.

comments.message: I need to hand over admin rights and then disable my user. Our scripts still use my personal token, and we also need 90 days of API audit logs for compliance before the switch. How should we handle this?

The safest sequence is: promote the new admin, create a service account and new token for automation, validate your scripts against that token, then deactivate your personal account. For the audit logs, self-serve exports are limited to 7 days on your plan, but support can request an extended export for account transitions and compliance review.
Okay, please request that export. We need it by Thursday, and I don't want my account disabled before we have it.

Done — I've escalated an audit-log export request for the last 90 days and flagged the dependency on your account deactivation timeline. You'll receive the file through the secure download center once our compliance operations team processes it. In the meantime, keep your account active until the replacement service token is verified.

subject: Cursor da API reinicia e repete páginas no meio da sincronização
description: Estamos migrando nossa replicação de offset para cursor, mas em um volume alto de tickets o next_cursor às vezes volta para um valor já usado e a sincronização entra em loop, repetindo páginas e enchendo nossa área de staging com duplicados. Precisamos saber se isso é esperado em datasets mutáveis e qual controle de estado deveríamos adotar para manter a consistência dos dados sem interromper a integração.

comments.message: Percebemos isso principalmente quando novos tickets entram durante a leitura. Guardamos o cursor em Redis e retomamos a cada página.

Olá! O cursor não deveria voltar para um valor anterior em condições normais. Você consegue compartilhar um exemplo de resposta com o cursor repetido e o horário aproximado? Com isso consigo verificar se houve instabilidade no serviço de paginação.
Posso compartilhar, mas preciso de orientação imediata porque o processo está pausado. O que fazemos para não duplicar tudo enquanto investigamos?

Como mitigação imediata, recomendo manter um conjunto de IDs já processados por execução e gravar a última combinação estável de cursor + primeiro/último ID da página. Se detectar repetição do mesmo cursor com a mesma faixa de IDs, interrompa o ciclo e reinicie a leitura a partir de uma janela temporal menor, aplicando merge por id no destino. Vou abrir uma investigação com nosso time de API para validar esse comportamento no seu tenant.

subject: Pagination started skipping records in incremental export
description: Our nightly sync against your API is missing some records and occasionally processing duplicates. We use the incremental export endpoint with `updated_after` and paginate until `has_more` is false. This started last week after we increased the job concurrency. I need troubleshooting help because our downstream reporting is now off.

comments.message: We call `/api/incremental/tickets?updated_after=<timestamp>` from four workers in parallel. Some pages overlap and others seem to be skipped entirely. We assumed pagination tokens were interchangeable across workers.

The issue is likely the parallelization. For the incremental export API, each `next_page_token` is tied to the exact result set from the previous page and should be consumed serially. If multiple workers reuse the same `updated_after` cursor independently, you'll see duplicates and gaps when records update during the run. Please switch to a single consumer for each stream, persist the final cursor only after the job completes, and de-duplicate by resource ID plus updated_at on your side.
That explains the overlap. We changed the job to one consumer per stream and reran a backfill. Counts now line up with the UI. Thanks.
Glad that resolved it. As a safeguard, I'd also recommend logging the initial cursor, each returned `next_page_token`, and the final committed cursor so you can audit any future drift quickly.

subject: La integración con Jira crea incidencias, pero en el proyecto equivocado
description: Configuré Jira para que los bugs reportados desde nuestro portal se creen automáticamente, pero todos terminan en el proyecto interno de TI en vez del proyecto de Producto. Ya revisé la autenticación y el tipo de incidencia, aunque no encuentro dónde se fija el proyecto por defecto ni si una automatización del producto lo está sobrescribiendo.

comments.message: En la pantalla de integración elegí el proyecto PROD y guardé. Sin embargo, cada ticket nuevo aparece en ITOPS.
Es posible que haya una regla de enrutamiento a nivel de formulario. Revise la configuración del formulario desde el que salen esos bugs y vea si tiene un proyecto de Jira asignado manualmente.
Sí había una regla en el formulario, pero incluso después de cambiarla algunos tickets siguen yendo a ITOPS.

Entonces revise también Configuración > Flujos de trabajo > Automatizaciones, porque una acción posterior puede estar reasignando el destino según la categoría o el equipo. Si no encuentra nada, exporte un ejemplo con la carga útil del webhook y compárelo con la configuración del proyecto por defecto. A primera vista parece una combinación de configuración duplicada entre formulario e integración más que un fallo de conexión.

subject: Mandantenbranding wird im Hilfecenter nur teilweise übernommen
description: Ich habe unser Hilfecenter neu konfiguriert, inklusive Logo, Primärfarbe und eigener Schrift. Im Vorschaumodus sieht alles korrekt aus, live wird aber auf einigen Seiten noch das Standardlogo angezeigt und die Schrift springt zurück. Da wir morgen einen Kundenlaunch haben, brauche ich dringend Hilfe oder eine Eskalation an das Theme-Team.

comments.message: Betroffen sind vor allem die Artikelseiten und die Suchergebnisse. Die Startseite sieht korrekt aus. Ich habe das Theme bereits erneut veröffentlicht.
Danke. Das klingt nach einem CDN-Cache. Bitte warten Sie 24 Stunden, dann sollte sich das Branding überall angleichen.
Wir haben die Änderung schon vorgestern veröffentlicht. Außerdem ist nur ein Teil des Hilfecenters betroffen, deshalb glaube ich nicht an reines Caching.

Verstanden. Dann ist wahrscheinlich ein separates Artikel-Template noch auf die Standard-Assets referenziert oder das Theme-Merge war unvollständig. Ich kann die Template-Dateien in Ihrem aktuellen Paket nicht selbst anpassen, daher habe ich den Fall an unser Theme-Team eskaliert. Falls Sie Zugriff auf den Code-Editor haben, prüfen Sie bitte interimistisch die Header-Partial der Artikel- und Suchvorlage auf feste Asset-Links.

subject: I need help understanding what our analytics filters actually do
description: I’m building reports for our leadership team, but the analytics area is not very intuitive to me. I can add filters for created date, closed date, team, assignee, and tags, but I’m not confident I understand how those interact. I need someone to explain this in normal terms, especially why my ticket volume changes so much when I switch from created date to closed date.

comments.message: I thought I was looking at the same set of tickets, but the totals are completely different depending on the date field.

That’s expected, and it confuses a lot of new report builders. Created date answers, “When did tickets enter the system?” Closed date answers, “When were tickets resolved?” Those are often different populations over the same calendar range. For example, tickets created in March may not close until April, so a March report by created date and a March report by closed date won’t match. Team, assignee, and tag filters then narrow the population further based on the ticket properties attached to those records.
So if leadership wants to know how much new work came in versus how much we finished, should that be two separate reports?

Exactly. A best practice is to create one report for incoming demand using created date and another for completed work using closed date. That way the story is clearer. You can keep the other filters consistent across both reports so leadership can compare trends by team or issue type without mixing concepts.

subject: Cannot demote admin because owned automations are hidden
description: I need to remove admin rights from one of our team leads, but the system says she still owns automations and user provisioning rules. The problem is the ownership report only lists two automations, and after transferring those, demotion still fails. I’ve looked through every active workflow and SCIM rule I can see as super admin. Is there a way to identify what hidden objects are still tied to her account?

comments.message: I’m trying to demote an admin, but the product says she still owns automations and provisioning rules even after I transferred the visible ones.
Please go to Admin > Automations and sort by owner, then transfer anything under her name. After that, check Provisioning > Rules for SCIM ownership and retry the demotion.
I already did that. Only two automations appeared, both transferred, and SCIM rules show no owner column at all in our tenant.

Then there may be archived workflows or legacy rules still associated with her account. As a troubleshooting step, temporarily reactivate any archived automations in draft view and check their owner metadata. I’m also escalating this to our platform team because hidden ownership dependencies can block user administration changes and usually require backend cleanup.

subject: Bulk user import says success but only some users appear
description: I imported 240 users from CSV and got a success banner, but only 198 users were actually created. There wasn't a row-level error file, so I can't tell whether the missing records were skipped because of validation issues or if the import job silently failed. Please investigate, and also explain how bulk import handles duplicates and partial failures.

comments.message: The missing users are spread throughout the file, not just at the end. A few had the same company name, but email addresses were unique.

I checked the import job logs and found 42 rows were rejected because the role value in your CSV used display labels like 'Manager' instead of the accepted API values like 'manager'. The confusing part is that the UI still showed a generic success message and didn't surface the rejected-row summary. That's a bug in the import results screen.
That's helpful, but how should we format the file next time? The template we downloaded wasn't very clear.

For future imports, duplicates are matched primarily on email, and valid rows can still be created even if other rows fail, so imports are partially applied rather than all-or-nothing. For the role column, use the internal values documented in the CSV guide, not the human-readable labels shown in the app. I've also shared your feedback with the onboarding team because the template and success messaging clearly need to educate admins better.

subject: Workflow nach CSV-Import läuft nur für neue, nicht für aktualisierte Datensätze
description: Wir haben per CSV bestehende Firmen aktualisiert und erwartet, dass dadurch unser Reaktivierungs-Workflow startet. Bei neu angelegten Firmen funktioniert die Automatisierung, bei aktualisierten Bestandsdatensätzen jedoch nicht. Der Trigger basiert auf dem Feld "Segment = Reaktivierung". Ist das ein Fehler oder eine Einschränkung?

comments.message: Nach dem Import haben alle betroffenen Firmen das Segment "Reaktivierung". Trotzdem sind nur neu angelegte Datensätze in den Workflow eingetreten.

Danke für die genaue Beschreibung. Bei vielen Workflow-Typen gibt es einen Unterschied zwischen "Datensatz wird erstellt" und "Feldwert wird auf X aktualisiert". Wenn Ihr Trigger nur beim ersten Erstellen oder nur beim erstmaligen Erfüllen feuert, werden bestehende Firmen bei einem CSV-Update übersprungen.
Das klingt plausibel. Ich glaube, der Einstieg ist tatsächlich auf "beim Erstellen oder wenn Bedingungen erstmals erfüllt werden" gesetzt.

Dann ist das wahrscheinlich erwartetes Verhalten. Für Bestandsdatensätze brauchen Sie entweder einen Re-Enrollment-Mechanismus bei Feldänderung auf "Reaktivierung" oder einen einmaligen Bulk-Start des Workflows für eine gefilterte Liste. Wenn Sie mir sagen, welchen Workflow-Typ Sie nutzen, kann ich Ihnen die genaue Einstellung dafür nennen.

subject: Microsoft Teams-Integration zeigt keine neuen Tickets an
description: Ich habe heute die Microsoft-Teams-Integration verbunden, weil unser Serviceteam neue Tickets direkt im Kanal sehen soll. Die Testnachricht kam an, aber echte neue Tickets erscheinen nicht. Die Regel ist aktiv, und ich habe sie schon neu gespeichert. Können Sie prüfen, ob die Integration wirklich Events sendet?

comments.message: Die Verbindung zu Teams ist laut Admin-Seite erfolgreich. Nur die produktiven Ticket-Benachrichtigungen fehlen komplett, obwohl neue Tickets erstellt werden.

Danke. Wenn die Testnachricht funktioniert, ist die Verbindung zu Teams grundsätzlich korrekt. Häufig liegt es dann daran, dass die Benachrichtigungsregel nur auf einen bestimmten Tickettyp oder ein bestimmtes Team gefiltert ist. Bitte öffnen Sie die Automationsregel und prüfen Sie, ob dort Bedingungen wie 'Poste nur bei Priorität Hoch' oder 'nur für Gruppe Support' gesetzt sind.
Sie hatten recht: Es war auf die Gruppe 'Customer Success' eingeschränkt. Jetzt kommen neue Tickets im Kanal an. Allerdings erscheinen keine Updates zu bestehenden Tickets.

Das ist ein separater Trigger. In der Teams-Integration gibt es standardmäßig nur 'Ticket erstellt'. Für Statusänderungen oder neue Kommentare müssen Sie zusätzlich die Regel 'Ticket aktualisiert' aktivieren und demselben Teams-Kanal zuweisen. Damit sollten auch Updates gepostet werden.

subject: Former employee still has admin access after offboarding
description: I removed one of our IT admins from our HR system last Friday, but she can still sign into our workspace and appears to have full admin rights. We need that access revoked urgently because she should not be able to manage users anymore. I also noticed two deactivated users were automatically reactivated this morning after our directory sync ran. Can you help me lock this down and explain what happened?

comments.message: A former employee still has admin access in our account even though she was offboarded on Friday. We also had two users get reactivated after the morning sync. This is a security concern, so I need this addressed as soon as possible.

Thanks for flagging this. If the account was manually deactivated in our app but the user is still active in your identity provider, the next directory sync can restore access and prior roles. As an immediate step, please disable the user in your identity provider and remove them from any groups mapped to admin roles. I’ve also initiated an urgent escalation to our identity and access team to review the sync logs for the reactivations and confirm whether role mapping caused the admin access to persist.

We use Okta and she was removed from the employee directory, but I’m not sure whether she was removed from the admin group. I’ve now disabled her in Okta. Can you also make sure she can’t access audit logs or user provisioning while this is being reviewed?

Disabling her in Okta should block the next authentication attempt, but because this involves possible privilege persistence, I’ve marked the case as a priority security escalation. Our access team will verify whether any active sessions need to be revoked and whether the admin group mapping re-granted permissions during sync. In the meantime, please have one of your current org owners check Admin Settings > User Roles and remove any remaining org-level admin assignments for that user if visible. I’ll keep this ticket with the escalation and update you once engineering confirms the session and sync behavior.

subject: List deduplication removed the wrong primary records
description: I ran the deduplication tool on our leads table using email as the key. It did merge obvious duplicates, but in dozens of cases it kept the older incomplete record and removed the newer record that had owner, source, and lifecycle data filled in. Now those fields are missing from the surviving records. Was this an expected rule or a bug in how the merge chose which data to retain?

comments.message: I ran the deduplication tool on our leads table using email as the key. It did merge obvious duplicates, but in dozens of cases it kept the older incomplete record and removed the newer record that had owner, source, and lifecycle data filled in.

I investigated three examples from your workspace. The deduplication job correctly identified duplicates, but the merge policy applied the lowest internal record ID as the survivor instead of the record with the most recently populated fields. That's not the documented behavior for your plan. I restored the deleted lead versions from backup for those examples and opened a bug on the survivor-selection logic. Unfortunately I can't bulk reverse the whole job from support, so I've requested a data recovery task from our operations team.
Do you know if the recovery will bring back field history too, or just the latest values?

Ops should be able to restore the latest values and merge metadata, but they may not be able to reconstruct every intermediate field-history event. I've added that question to the recovery request so they can confirm before proceeding.

subject: Jira issue updates are syncing, but sprint burndown report froze
description: Our Jira integration continues to pull issue status changes, but the sprint burndown report in your app stopped updating three days ago. We can see recent issue transitions in record detail, yet the report line is flat as if no work happened. This is making engineering leadership question the dashboard during sprint review.

comments.message: Issue-level data from Jira looks current. For example, PROJ-418 and PROJ-427 both synced status changes today. But our sprint burndown report has been frozen since Tuesday and doesn't reflect any remaining story point changes.
If the issue records are current, the report should update as well. This may just be because sprint reports only refresh at the end of the sprint.
That can't be right. It updated daily throughout the last sprint, and this sprint is still active. Also the historical trend stopped exactly after we changed the Jira board mapping.

Thanks for clarifying. The board remapping is an important clue: the integration may still be syncing issue updates while the reporting dataset for burndown is tied to the prior board/sprint key relationship. I can't repair that mapping from support, so I'm escalating to our Jira integrations team and including the reporting symptom so they can check whether the burndown model needs to be rebuilt for the new board mapping.

subject: Why did enabling archived projects increase storage billing?
description: I changed our workspace settings so archived projects remain searchable for compliance, and now we are being billed for more storage than before. I assumed archived data would stay outside our active quota. Can you explain whether this configuration change made archived attachments billable and what options we have to reduce the charge?

comments.message: We enabled the "search archived projects" option because legal needed historical access. This month's bill jumped by 600 GB of storage overage. Most of that appears to be old attachments from archived projects. Does that setting move archived files into billable indexed storage?

The setting itself doesn't charge for search, but it does rehydrate archived attachments into indexed storage for fast retrieval, which counts toward your storage quota on the Professional plan. That's why you saw the overage after enabling it.
Is there a way to keep the projects searchable without pulling every attachment back into billable storage? We mostly need the metadata, not the files.
Not exactly. You could try deleting some attachments or buying more storage. I don't think there is a metadata-only option.
That seems odd because I thought there was a separate compliance archive mode. Can you confirm before we make changes?

You're right to question that. I double-checked and there is a compliance archive mode that keeps project metadata searchable while leaving attachments in cold storage, but it isn't enabled on your workspace. I can't switch that plan feature from support, so I've escalated the request to our product configuration team and asked billing to review the current overage in case they can provide a courtesy credit.

subject: Need urgent review of suspicious bulk export
description: I'm a workspace owner and noticed a very large customer export was triggered from our account at 2:13 AM UTC by a user who says they were asleep at the time. I revoked their session and reset their password, but I need help understanding what was exported and whether your security team can review the event. This may be a breach, so I don't want a generic help article.

comments.message: Please route this to whoever handles security incidents. I need audit details for the overnight export and confirmation whether any other suspicious activity occurred.

I understand the urgency. I can see the export event in your admin logs, but frontline support doesn't have access to the payload contents or the deeper forensic review tools needed for a possible account compromise.
What exactly happens next, and how quickly can someone respond?

I've escalated this to our Security Operations queue as a suspected account compromise and marked it high priority. They will review authentication events, IP history, and related admin actions, then contact you through this ticket. While they investigate, please keep MFA enforced for all admins and let me know if you'd like me to note any specific users or time ranges in the escalation.

subject: Nie mogę zalogować się po włączeniu SSO
description: Od wczoraj nie mogę zalogować się do naszego konta firmowego. Po wpisaniu adresu e-mail system przekierowuje mnie do logowania przez Google, ale po autoryzacji wracam na stronę z komunikatem „access denied”. Jestem administratorem workspace'u i muszę pilnie wejść do panelu.

comments.message: Dzień dobry, od wczoraj logowanie przez Google kończy się błędem „access denied”. Inni użytkownicy też zaczynają to zgłaszać.

Dzień dobry, sprawdziłem domenę i widzę, że wczoraj wymuszono SSO dla całej organizacji, ale mapowanie dozwolonych domen w konfiguracji SAML jest puste. To powoduje odrzucenie po poprawnej autoryzacji Google. Tymczasowo wyłączyłem wymuszenie SSO na 2 godziny, żeby mogli się Państwo zalogować hasłem lokalnym, a równolegle proszę uzupełnić domenę firmową w ustawieniach SSO > Allowed domains.
Po wyłączeniu wymuszenia udało mi się wejść. Uzupełniłem domenę i zapisałem ustawienia.

Świetnie. Włączyłem ponownie wymuszenie SSO i potwierdzam, że logowanie dla Państwa domeny przechodzi już poprawnie. Proszę dać znać, jeśli któryś użytkownik nadal widzi stary błąd — wtedy wyczyścimy cache sesji po naszej stronie.

subject: Changed my name but it still shows the old one in approvals
description: I updated my profile after getting married, but my old last name is still appearing in approval requests and some notification emails. I also want to make sure my login email stays the same. Is there another place I need to update this, or does it take time to sync?

comments.message: I updated my profile after getting married, but my old last name is still appearing in approval requests and some notification emails. I also want to make sure my login email stays the same. Is there another place I need to update this, or does it take time to sync?

Name changes can take up to 24 hours to appear everywhere, and approval workflows may cache your previous name until the next action runs. Your login email is separate, so updating your display name should not change how you sign in. You may also need to update your name in your identity provider if your company uses SSO, because some profiles resync from there.
It's already been three days, and we're on SSO. I don't know whether the name is coming from Okta or from your app.
In that case, please just change it in both places to be safe. Once both match, it should correct itself eventually.

subject: Benutzer soll nur Berichte sehen, aber keine Tickets bearbeiten
description: Ich versuche für einen Teamleiter ein Konto einzurichten, das Berichte und Dashboards sehen kann, ohne Tickets zu bearbeiten oder zuzuweisen. Mit den Standardrollen bekomme ich das nicht sauber hin. Gibt es eine Kombination aus benutzerdefinierter Rolle und Gruppe, mit der das möglich ist?

comments.message: Wir haben den Professional-Plan. In der Benutzerverwaltung sehe ich Rollen, aber ich bin unsicher, ob Berichtsrechte separat vergeben werden können.

Ja, das ist grundsätzlich möglich, wenn benutzerdefinierte Rollen in Ihrem Plan enthalten sind. Sie würden dem Benutzer eine Rolle ohne Ticket-Bearbeitungsrechte zuweisen und die Analytics-Berechtigungen separat aktivieren. Die Gruppenzugehörigkeit steuert dann eher die Sicht auf Inhalte als die Bearbeitungsrechte.
Ich finde aber keine Option für benutzerdefinierte Rollen in unserem Admin Center.

Dann sind sie in Ihrem aktuellen Tarif oder für Ihr Konto vermutlich nicht freigeschaltet. Mit den Standardrollen allein lässt sich diese Trennung oft nicht exakt abbilden. Ich empfehle, dass ich Ihren Fall an unser Account-Team weitergebe, damit geprüft wird, ob benutzerdefinierte Rollen für Ihren Vertrag verfügbar sind oder ob es eine alternative Berechtigungsstruktur gibt.

subject: Please explain why sandbox changes didn't appear in production
description: I'm training a new operations manager and she assumed that updating forms in our sandbox would automatically update production after approval. It didn't, and now she's lost confidence in the release process. I need a clear explanation of what the sandbox is actually for, what does and doesn't sync, and the right way to promote changes.

comments.message: I'm training a new operations manager and she assumed that updating forms in our sandbox would automatically update production after approval. It didn't, and now she's lost confidence in the release process. I need a clear explanation of what the sandbox is actually for, what does and doesn't sync, and the right way to promote changes.
Sandbox is basically a copy of production where you can test things first. Usually if something doesn't move over, you need to publish it.
We did publish in the sandbox. That's exactly why she's confused. I need to teach this properly, not just tell her to publish.

Thanks for clarifying. The key point is that publishing inside the sandbox usually only makes the change live within that sandbox environment; it does not by itself deploy configuration to production. Sandboxes are for safe testing and validation, while promotion to production requires the separate deployment or recreation process your account supports. Because the exact promotion path differs by feature area, I'm escalating this so our implementation team can confirm which of your form settings support deployment packages versus manual migration.

subject: Need end-user guidance for notifications, but mobile pushes are inconsistent
description: I'm preparing a help article for our employees on notification settings across email and mobile. The challenge is that mobile push notifications are arriving for some users even after they disable certain alerts on the desktop settings page. I need a simple explanation of how notification preferences are supposed to work and whether this sounds like a bug or just separate settings.

comments.message: Can you help me explain notification preferences clearly and advise what to tell users who still get mobile pushes after changing desktop settings?

A simple explanation is that notification preferences can exist at more than one level: account-wide defaults, user-level channel settings, and device-specific permissions. If someone still receives mobile pushes after changing desktop settings, it may mean the mobile app has separate notification categories or the device OS is still permitting a broader set of alerts than expected.
We looked in the mobile app and the categories don't seem to match the web settings at all. That makes it hard to write one guide.

Understood. Rather than force a single explanation that may be wrong for your app version, I'm escalating this to our mobile support team to confirm the current behavior and whether web and mobile preferences are intentionally separate in your tenant. For now, I'd advise users that web notification settings may not fully control mobile push behavior and to review both in-app and device-level settings.

subject: Need escalation for duplicate tenant creation
description: During implementation, your provisioning flow appears to have created two tenants for our company with slightly different names. Our users are split across both, and some integrations point to one tenant while SSO points to the other. We can't clean this up ourselves from the admin side. Please escalate to whoever can merge or decommission the wrong tenant without data loss.

comments.message: This is causing real confusion for our rollout. We need a safe path to consolidate the environments.

I reviewed the account and I can indeed see two active tenants tied to your organization domain. Tenant merges or reassignments aren't something support can perform because they involve data integrity checks and backend operations.
Okay, but we need guidance on what happens first so nobody loses access.

I've escalated the case to our Provisioning team and flagged the risk of data loss and SSO disruption. They will need to assess which tenant should remain primary before any merge or decommission step. Until they respond, please avoid changing SSO settings or deleting users in either tenant so they have a stable state to review.

subject: Nom de l’entreprise non mis à jour sur les notifications
description: J’ai modifié le nom de notre société dans les paramètres du compte hier, mais les invitations envoyées aux nouveaux utilisateurs affichent encore l’ancien nom. Dans l’interface, je vois bien le nouveau nom, donc je ne comprends pas si le changement n’a été appliqué qu’à moitié. Pouvez-vous vérifier ce qui bloque ?

comments.message: Bonjour, nous avons renommé notre entreprise de "DataNord" à "Datanor Groupe" dans le compte administrateur, mais les e-mails automatiques montrent encore l’ancien nom. Est-ce normal ?

Bonjour, merci pour votre message. Les changements de nom de compte sont généralement immédiats dans l’application, mais certains modèles d’e-mails gardent l’ancienne valeur en cache pendant jusqu’à 24 heures. Pouvez-vous me confirmer l’URL de votre espace et me dire si le problème concerne toutes les invitations ou seulement certaines notifications ?
L’URL est datanord.app.example. Pour l’instant, je l’ai vu sur deux invitations utilisateurs et un e-mail de réinitialisation de mot de passe.

Merci. J’ai vérifié : le nom légal du compte a bien été mis à jour côté application, mais le service d’e-mails transactionnels n’a pas encore resynchronisé le nouveau libellé. J’ai forcé la synchronisation manuellement. Les prochains messages devraient afficher "Datanor Groupe". Si vous voyez encore l’ancien nom après 2 heures, revenez vers nous et j’ouvrirai un incident.

subject: Merged accounts are double-counting in revenue by region report
description: I merged several duplicate customer accounts last week as part of a cleanup project. The account records now look correct in the main data table, but our revenue by region report is double-counting ARR for some of those merged customers. I think the child records from the old account IDs are still being referenced in the report model. Can you confirm whether merges should roll up reporting automatically or if we need to rebuild something?

comments.message: After merging duplicate accounts, the report still shows ARR under both the old and surviving account IDs. Is that expected?

It shouldn't keep both after the reporting model catches up. I reviewed one of the merged pairs and the surviving account is correct in the source data, but the report is still joining against a stale dimension table keyed on pre-merge IDs.
So is there a fix we can apply today?

I can correct part of it now by refreshing the account dimension, which should remove the duplicate region rows. However, the ARR summary cube also needs a backend rebuild from our data engineering team because it pre-aggregated revenue before the merge. I've escalated that piece and attached your examples.

subject: User moved to wrong team after directory sync
description: One employee was supposed to be added to the Finance team, but after last night's directory sync she lost access to Finance dashboards and was placed into Operations instead. I can change the team manually, but it flips back on the next sync. I'm trying to troubleshoot whether this is a mapping problem in your app or something in our user administration setup.

comments.message: One employee was supposed to be added to the Finance team, but after last night's directory sync she lost access to Finance dashboards and was placed into Operations instead. I can change the team manually, but it flips back on the next sync. I'm trying to troubleshoot whether this is a mapping problem in your app or something in our user administration setup.

When a manual team assignment is overwritten nightly, the sync source is almost always an inbound group mapping. Please check the provisioning logs for her user and compare the incoming department or group attribute values. In our app, if multiple mapped groups are present, the highest-priority team rule wins. If Operations is winning incorrectly, you may need to adjust the group-priority order under Admin > Directory Sync > Team Mapping.
I checked and she is in both the Finance and Ops groups in Azure AD because of a temporary project. I didn't realize one mapping would override the other.

That would explain it. To keep her in Finance, either remove the Ops group membership or move Finance above Operations in the mapping priority so the sync assigns the intended team. After changing that, run a manual sync for her account and confirm dashboard access is restored.

subject: Power BI export from reports no longer matching Jira integration counts
description: We use your Jira integration to feed issue data into reports and then export that data to Power BI. Since last week, the export totals don't match what your in-app report shows for open bugs by team. I need someone to help determine whether the Jira sync changed, whether the report export is dropping rows, and whether this needs escalation because our leadership deck is blocked.

comments.message: The in-app 'Open Bugs by Team' report says Platform has 84 open bugs, but the export we send to Power BI only contains 61 rows for Platform. Jira itself is closer to 84. Can you investigate?

I reviewed the report definition and the Jira integration health. Sync is completing, but your export is based on a legacy report snapshot that excludes issues updated after export generation starts. That's why the in-app dashboard and exported file differ when the dataset is changing quickly.
That seems odd. This didn't happen before, and we need the export to be stable.

You're right. I regenerated the export using the newer 'consistent snapshot' option on your account and the count now matches 83 because one Jira issue is failing sync due to an invalid project mapping. I fixed the report export setting, but I can't repair the broken Jira project mapping from support because it's tied to an admin-owned integration credential. I've escalated that final sync error to our integrations team and noted the specific issue key.

subject: Erro ao mesclar empresas: relacionamento com negócios foi perdido
description: Fiz a mesclagem de duas empresas duplicadas hoje cedo e agora alguns negócios que estavam associados à empresa secundária não aparecem mais na principal. O objetivo era justamente consolidar tudo em um único cadastro. Tem como recuperar esses vínculos?

comments.message: Fiz a mesclagem de duas empresas duplicadas hoje cedo e agora alguns negócios que estavam associados à empresa secundária não aparecem mais na principal. O objetivo era justamente consolidar tudo em um único cadastro. Tem como recuperar esses vínculos?

Normalmente a mesclagem move todos os relacionamentos da empresa secundária para a principal, mas já vimos casos em que negócios arquivados ou com vínculo múltiplo não ficam visíveis de imediato. Você consegue me informar os IDs das duas empresas e se os negócios estavam abertos ou fechados?
Os IDs são 44821 e 44790. Tinha negócios fechados e um aberto. O aberto sumiu da visão da empresa principal, mas ainda aparece na busca global.

Obrigado. Isso sugere mais um problema de indexação da associação do que perda definitiva de dados. Eu já teria que validar nos logs internos, então vou escalar para o suporte de dados com esses IDs. Enquanto isso, não recomendo desfazer manualmente criando novos vínculos, porque isso pode duplicar associações quando o reprocessamento ocorrer. Assim que o time confirmar, eles podem reanexar os negócios pela rotina de reparo de relacionamentos.

subject: Troca do nome legal da empresa travou a tela de faturamento e permissões
description: Eu atualizei o nome legal da empresa e alguns dados cadastrais da conta porque recebemos um novo CNPJ. Logo depois, a tela de faturamento passou a carregar parcialmente e a seção de permissões de equipe começou a exibir erro ao abrir. Como foram duas áreas afetadas logo após uma alteração administrativa, suspeito que exista um problema no backend relacionado à atualização da conta. Preciso disso analisado com prioridade.

comments.message: Depois de atualizar o nome legal da empresa e dados cadastrais da conta, a tela de faturamento carrega pela metade e permissões da equipe dão erro ao abrir.

Olá! Obrigado pelo contexto. Como o problema afeta mais de uma área após uma alteração cadastral da conta, isso realmente pode indicar uma falha no backend. Você consegue informar o horário da atualização e se o erro aparece para todos os administradores?
A alteração foi ontem às 16:40. Testamos com três administradores diferentes e todos veem o mesmo comportamento.

Perfeito. Com base nisso, vou tratar como possível bug relacionado à atualização cadastral da conta e encaminhar com prioridade alta para engenharia. Incluirei o horário da mudança e o fato de impactar faturamento e permissões em todos os administradores. Como medida preventiva, não façam novas alterações no cadastro até concluirmos a análise.

subject: Komunikat „Twoje konto zostało dezaktywowane”
description: Przy próbie logowania widzę informację, że moje konto zostało dezaktywowane przez administratora, ale nasz admin twierdzi, że niczego nie wyłączał. Wczoraj jeszcze normalnie pracowałem w systemie. Proszę o sprawdzenie, bo blokuje mi to pracę.

comments.message: System pokazuje, że konto zostało dezaktywowane przez administratora, choć nikt w firmie tego nie robił.

Sprawdziłem historię zmian. Konto nie zostało dezaktywowane ręcznie; zostało automatycznie zawieszone po synchronizacji SCIM z Państwa dostawcą tożsamości, ponieważ w atrybucie employmentStatus przyszła wartość „inactive”.
To błąd po stronie naszego HR, już to potwierdziłem. Czy możecie przywrócić dostęp?

Mogę przywrócić konto tymczasowo na 24 godziny, ale jeśli SCIM ponownie wyśle status „inactive”, blokada wróci. Włączyłem konto teraz i zalecam, aby administrator IdP poprawił rekord pracownika oraz wymusił ponowną synchronizację. Jeśli chcecie, mogę dołączyć nasz zespół integracji, żeby przejrzał mapowanie atrybutów.

subject: Impossible de modifier l’adresse e-mail du compte facturation
description: J’essaie de changer l’adresse e-mail associée à notre contact de facturation, mais le système refuse avec un message disant que l’utilisateur est déjà invité sur un autre espace. Nous avons pourtant besoin que toutes les factures arrivent désormais sur finance@atelierrive.fr. Je ne sais pas s’il faut fusionner un profil ou supprimer quelque chose avant.

comments.message: Bonjour, quand je veux remplacer l’adresse de facturation actuelle par finance@atelierrive.fr, j’obtiens une erreur indiquant que cet e-mail existe déjà. Comment faire le changement sans perdre l’historique ?
Bonjour, si l’adresse existe déjà, il suffit normalement de supprimer l’utilisateur en double puis de réessayer.
Je préfère éviter, car cette adresse est aussi utilisée sur un autre espace de test. Je veux seulement changer le contact de facturation sur notre espace principal, pas supprimer un utilisateur.
Dans ce cas, je vous conseille de créer une adresse complètement différente, par exemple finance2@atelierrive.fr, car notre système n’accepte pas les doublons.

subject: Need a safer process for updating subscription dates in bulk
description: We have to update renewal dates for several hundred customer records after a contract migration, but I’m worried about overwriting the wrong fields. Some records already have correct dates, some are blank, and some are in different date formats from prior imports.
