I’m looking for both troubleshooting advice on cleaning up the data and best practices so my team can do this safely.

comments.message: Can you outline a process we can follow? We’ve had mistakes before with bulk edits and I want something repeatable for our ops coordinator.

Absolutely. A safe process is: 1) export the target records first as a backup, 2) normalize the date column in your spreadsheet to YYYY-MM-DD, 3) keep only the record ID and renewal date in the import file if that’s the only field you’re updating, and 4) test with a small batch before running the full update.
What about records that already have a date? We only want to fill blanks in some cases.

Good call. To avoid overwriting good data, first create a filtered list of records where Renewal Date is blank. Export only that segment for the backfill import. For records with mixed formatting issues, fix them in the file before import rather than trying to rely on the system to interpret multiple formats. I’d also recommend documenting the mapping screen with screenshots for your ops coordinator so they know exactly which object and field to select each time.

subject: Confused about updating parent-child company relationships by CSV
description: I’m trying to upload a file to set parent-child relationships between companies after a reorg, but I’m not sure which identifiers the import needs. I attempted it once and some subsidiaries linked correctly while others didn’t. I need troubleshooting help for the failed links and a simple explanation I can share with our admin so we stop guessing when we do hierarchical updates.

comments.message: My file had Company Name and Parent Company Name columns. Some rows worked, but many didn’t, especially where names are similar.

That behavior makes sense. Name-based matching is unreliable when there are duplicates or near-matches. For relationship imports, it’s best to use unique record IDs for both the child company and the parent company whenever possible.
Can I get those IDs from an export? And do I need to include every company field again?

Yes—export the relevant companies first and include the internal Company ID. Then build an update file with just the child Company ID and the Parent Company ID if relationship assignment is the only goal. That minimizes risk. For troubleshooting the rows that failed, review whether the intended parent records actually existed in the account at import time and whether any parents were inactive or duplicated. I’d also suggest keeping a reusable hierarchy-import template for your admin team so future updates follow the same mapping every time.

subject: Deleted contacts reappeared and affected users can't sign in
description: I restored a backup from last month to recover some deleted contacts, but now we're seeing duplicate records and two team members are being told their accounts don't exist when they try to log in. I'm not sure if the restore overwrote user mappings or if the data merge failed.

comments.message: After the restore, about 800 contacts came back, but many are duplicated and linked to the wrong account owner. At the same time, two users cannot log in anymore and get 'user not found' even though they were active before.
Restores usually do not affect login records, so the access issue is probably unrelated. For the duplicates, you can export the contacts, deduplicate them in Excel, and import them again.
That doesn't really help because the ownership fields are system-managed, and these login failures started immediately after the restore. Could the restore have reverted user state or IDs?
I don't believe so. Please recreate the missing users manually and re-import the corrected CSV for the contact data. That should resolve both issues.

subject: Po aktualizacji loginu konto wpada w pętlę SSO
description: Po zmianie mojego adresu e-mail przez administratora nie mogę zalogować się do platformy. System przekierowuje mnie do naszego SSO, potem z powrotem do was i tak w kółko. Wcześniej działało bez problemu.

comments.message: Nowy adres to m.nowak@firma24.pl. Stary login był powiązany z dawną spółką i został usunięty.

Dziękuję. To wygląda na problem z mapowaniem nowego identyfikatora użytkownika w SAML. Sam nie mogę zmienić konfiguracji po stronie dostawcy tożsamości, ale widzę też niespójność po naszej stronie. Eskaluję sprawę do zespołu dostępu i dołączam stary oraz nowy login.
Czy jest jakiś obejściowy sposób, żebym dziś wszedł do konta?

Tymczasowo mogę wyłączyć wymuszanie SSO tylko dla tego użytkownika i wysłać jednorazowy link logowania na nowy adres. Zrobiłem to, więc powinien Pan móc wejść teraz przez formularz lokalny. Pełna naprawa pętli SSO będzie po stronie zespołu technicznego.

subject: Zablokowane konto po zbyt wielu próbach logowania
description: Przez pomyłkę kilka razy wpisałem złe hasło i teraz konto jest zablokowane. Opcja resetu hasła nie przychodzi na e-mail, więc nie mam jak odblokować dostępu. Czy możecie to ręcznie odblokować?

comments.message: Moje konto zostało zablokowane po kilku nieudanych próbach logowania. Mail resetujący też do mnie nie dochodzi.
Widzę blokadę bezpieczeństwa na 30 minut po 5 nieudanych próbach. Widzę też, że maile resetujące były odrzucane przez Państwa serwer z komunikatem o pełnej skrzynce.
Skrzynka jest już wyczyszczona. Czy da się to odblokować od razu?

Tak, ręcznie zdjąłem blokadę i wysłałem nowy link do resetu hasła. Link będzie ważny przez 1 godzinę. Jeśli wiadomość nie pojawi się w głównej skrzynce, proszę sprawdzić folder kwarantanny/spam, bo poprzednio serwer odbijał nasze wiadomości.

subject: API integration data present in records but absent from usage report
description: We send event data into your platform through the API integration, and I can verify the events are attached to customer records. But the monthly usage report is undercounting them by a large margin, which affects customer billing on our side. I've checked the event timestamps and they are within the expected period. Please investigate and let me know if this is a reporting bug or something about how the integration payloads are being processed.

comments.message: Our custom API integration posted about 58,000 usage events in April, but your 'Monthly Usage by Account' report only shows 41,200. The event log screen clearly has entries for the missing accounts.
If the events are visible in the log, the report should eventually catch up. Reports can take up to 24 hours to update after ingestion.
It's been 3 days. We delayed invoicing because of this.

Understood. I checked one of the missing accounts and noticed your integration is sending several event timestamps in local time without a timezone offset, while the report buckets usage strictly in UTC. Those events were ingested but assigned to the prior reporting period for some accounts. I don't have tooling to rewrite event timestamps from support, so I'm escalating this to engineering and attaching sample payloads. For now, if you run the report with a custom date range starting one day earlier, you'll see most of the missing volume included.

subject: Invoice says automation overage, but archived workflows are included
description: I’m trying to reconcile this month’s bill and the automation overage numbers don’t make sense. We archived three high-volume workflows halfway through the month, but the invoice appears to count runs from after the archive date, and one archived workflow even shows activity in the usage export. Please explain how workflow automation usage is billed after archival and correct the invoice if those runs shouldn’t be counted.

comments.message: Our invoice includes automation overage charges that seem to include archived workflows. One flow was archived on the 14th, but the export shows executions on the 19th and 20th.

Archived workflows should not accept new trigger events, but in-flight jobs and scheduled actions created before archival can still execute later and count toward usage. That said, they should appear in the logs as scheduled continuations rather than new runs. If you share the workflow IDs, I can compare them against billing usage records.
I sent the IDs in the attachment. At least one of them looks like a brand-new run, not a delayed action.

Thanks — I found one workflow where scheduled reminders explain most of the post-archive usage, but there are also 43 runs that look inconsistent with the archive timestamp. I’ve asked billing to place the overage dispute on hold while engineering verifies whether those executions were counted incorrectly.

subject: Name change stuck in pending and profile edits not saving
description: I changed my display name after getting married, but my profile has shown "pending update" since yesterday and now other account changes also don’t save. I can type a new phone number or timezone, hit save, and the page just refreshes with the old values. Is there an outage or did something break on our tenant?

comments.message: I changed my display name after getting married, but my profile has shown "pending update" since yesterday and now other account changes also don’t save. I can type a new phone number or timezone, hit save, and the page just refreshes with the old values. Is there an outage or did something break on our tenant?
Sorry about that. Usually this happens when the browser caches an older session. Please clear cookies and log back in, then try saving the profile again.
I cleared cookies and also tried a different laptop. Same result. In the browser console I can see a 409 response on /api/account/profile when I save any change.

A 409 normally means the request was blocked because your account record is locked by a previous update job. I can’t unlock that directly from support, so I’m escalating this to our platform team with the endpoint and timing you provided. In the meantime, please avoid retrying the name change repeatedly, because that can keep the record in the same state.

subject: Can someone explain SSO enforcement for new users?
description: I'm onboarding a new business unit and need to explain to our local admins what happens when SSO is enforced but users are invited before their identity provider group sync is complete. We had a few employees get stuck last week, and I want a simple explanation plus the best practice to avoid this. If there is a checklist I can share internally, even better.

comments.message: I'm onboarding a new business unit and need to explain to our local admins what happens when SSO is enforced but users are invited before their identity provider group sync is complete. We had a few employees get stuck last week, and I want a simple explanation plus the best practice to avoid this. If there is a checklist I can share internally, even better.

When SSO enforcement is enabled, invited users generally need to authenticate through your identity provider instead of creating a local password. If the group sync hasn't completed, they may exist in a pending state without the right app assignment, so they can't finish access. Best practice is to confirm app assignment and group membership in the IdP first, then send the invite after the next successful sync. A simple checklist is: verify user in IdP, confirm group membership, wait for sync, send invite, and test with one account.
That sounds like what happened. We also saw two users receive the invite email but then get an 'account not authorized' message after login. Does that mean the sync failed?

Not always. That error can also mean the SAML assertion reached us but the mapped group or domain rule did not grant product access. Because this can involve tenant-specific SSO logs, I'm escalating this to our identity team so they can review the failed sign-in events and confirm whether the issue was sync timing, group mapping, or domain restrictions.

subject: Impossible de terminer la configuration SFTP pour l'import nightly
description: Nous voulons utiliser votre connecteur SFTP pour importer chaque nuit un fichier CSV de commandes depuis notre ERP. La connexion de test passe avec l'hôte et l'utilisateur, mais au moment d'enregistrer le chemin du dossier distant votre interface renvoie "permission denied". Le dossier existe pourtant, et avec le même compte je peux y déposer des fichiers en dehors de votre outil. Nous devons mettre cet import en place avant la clôture mensuelle.

comments.message: Bonjour, le test de connexion SFTP réussit, mais dès que j'enregistre le répertoire distant pour l'import, votre intégration affiche "permission denied". Pourtant ce compte a accès au dossier.

Bonjour, si le test de connexion réussit mais pas l'enregistrement du chemin, il se peut que notre connecteur tente une opération de lecture/écriture supplémentaire dans le dossier cible. Pouvez-vous me dire si le chemin est absolu ou relatif, et si l'utilisateur SFTP a aussi les droits de listing sur le dossier parent ?

Le chemin saisi est /exports/orders, donc absolu. Je ne suis pas certain pour les droits de listing du parent, mais le compte peut déposer un fichier directement dans /exports/orders avec un client SFTP classique.

Merci. Notre connecteur valide le chemin en listant d'abord le dossier parent puis en créant un fichier temporaire de contrôle, ce qui peut échouer même si l'upload direct passe. Vérifiez les droits de listing sur /exports et les droits d'écriture/suppression dans /exports/orders. Si vous préférez, donnez-moi une fenêtre de test et j'escaladerai à l'équipe intégrations pour capturer les logs SFTP détaillés côté serveur.

subject: Search returns archived records that should be excluded
description: Since last week, our global search is showing archived customer records even though we have 'exclude archived' turned on in our workspace settings. This is confusing our support team because they keep opening outdated accounts. I tested exact name search and partial search, and both include archived results. I also had another admin confirm the same behavior from a different login.

comments.message: Global search is including archived records even though we have excluded them in settings. Is there another place this needs to be configured?
There shouldn't be another setting, but let's rule out a permissions issue. Are the archived records appearing for all roles or only admins?
I checked with an admin and a standard agent. Both see the archived records in search results.

Thanks. I checked our internal tracker and this matches a regression introduced in the latest search rollout for accounts using blended record search. The setting is being ignored at query time. I don't have a tenant-side fix to offer yet, so I'm escalating this to engineering with your workspace ID. As a temporary workaround, users can add the advanced filter status:active in search until the patch is deployed.

subject: Merged two teams and now need to rename account structure
description: We recently merged our support and success teams into one department. I want to rename our workspace, update the default sender name, and change the primary account contact without confusing our agents. I'm not sure which changes are cosmetic and which ones affect logins, notifications, or existing reports.

comments.message: Can you walk me through what happens if I change the workspace name and primary account contact? We want the branding updated, but we don't want to break dashboards or user access.

Absolutely. The workspace name change is mostly cosmetic: it updates the name shown in the app and, depending on your setup, some customer-facing areas like email headers or portal branding. It does not change user login credentials. Changing the primary account contact updates who receives account notices such as renewal reminders and certain administrative emails, but it won't remove existing admins or alter permissions by itself.
What about our reports and historical tickets? We also need to update the default sender from Support Team to Client Operations.

Historical tickets and reports remain intact after a workspace rename; they stay associated with the same account. Updating the default sender name affects outgoing notifications going forward, but it doesn't retroactively rename old messages already sent. Best practice is to make these changes during a low-volume period, notify agents about the new branding, and then verify one test notification and one dashboard after the update. If you want, I can also outline the exact admin pages to use for each change.
Yes, please send the exact steps. We plan to do this tomorrow morning.

Here are the recommended steps: 1) Go to Admin Settings > Workspace Profile to update the workspace name. 2) Go to Admin Settings > Email/Notifications to change the default sender name. 3) Go to Account > Contacts to update the primary account contact. 4) Keep at least one other full admin active during the change window. 5) After saving, run a test ticket, confirm the new sender display, and review one standard report to verify naming appears as expected. If anything looks off during the change, reply here and we can review before escalating.

subject: Workflow notification emails stopped after payment method update
description: After we changed the company card in the billing section, our workflow automation that sends approval notification emails stopped delivering. The workflows still appear to run, but the email step is marked “suppressed due to account state” in the logs. We had a short payment failure before the card update, so I’m trying to understand whether billing status can suppress workflow actions selectively and what needs to be reset.

comments.message: We had a failed renewal payment yesterday, then updated the card and the invoice is now paid. Since then, our workflows run but every email action is suppressed due to account state.
That usually means the system still thinks your account is delinquent. Since your card is updated, please delete and recreate the email action in the workflow.
That seems extreme — it’s happening across eight workflows, and non-email actions still work fine.
I understand. I don’t see anything else from my side, so recreating the steps is the best workaround for now. If that fails, you may need to contact your email provider.

subject: Reactivated user cannot see historical projects and appears twice in admin search
description: I reactivated an employee who returned from leave, but after reactivation she can only see newly assigned projects, not the projects she belonged to before deactivation. On top of that, admin search now returns two entries with the same email: one active and one inactive. I’m worried we accidentally split her account history. I’ve already had her log out and back in, and I checked that her original groups were restored.

comments.message: A reactivated user can’t see historical projects, and admin search shows two entries with the same email—one active and one inactive.

This can happen if a new profile was created during reactivation instead of restoring the original membership links. Please open both user records and compare their created dates and internal IDs. If the active profile is newer, remove project access from that duplicate and try reactivating the original inactive record instead.
The active profile is newer, but the original inactive record no longer has a reactivate button. It just says merged or replaced.

Understood. That suggests the historical project memberships remain attached to the retired record, while the user is logging into the replacement profile. I’m escalating this to our data-repair team because project ownership and user-history links may need backend consolidation. As a temporary workaround, you can add the active profile back to critical projects manually, but prior comments and membership history may not appear until the merge is corrected.

subject: Admin role changed, but user still can’t see user management
description: I promoted a team lead to User Admin about an hour ago. The audit log shows the role change succeeded, but when she logs in she still doesn’t see the user management menu. We’ve had her sign out and back in twice already.

comments.message: I promoted a team lead to User Admin about an hour ago. The audit log shows the role change succeeded, but when she logs in she still doesn’t see the user management menu. We’ve had her sign out and back in twice already.

Got it. If the audit log confirms the role change, the next things to check are whether her role is scoped to specific workspaces and whether your navigation is controlled by a feature flag. Please open her admin role details and see if it says 'User Admin (limited scope)' instead of full org-level User Admin.
It does say limited scope, and it’s scoped only to our EMEA workspace. She’s trying to manage users in the global directory.

That explains it. Limited-scope User Admins won’t see the org-wide user management area; they only get the workspace-level people view for assigned workspaces. If she needs global user management, edit her role to org-level User Admin or add a custom admin role with directory permissions.

subject: Need emergency access after account owner left company
description: Our account owner left the company unexpectedly, and none of the remaining admins can access owner-level settings anymore. We can still log in, but we’re blocked from exporting audit logs and changing retention policies. Since the owner’s mailbox is disabled, this feels like an escalation case for access recovery.

comments.message: We have two admins on the workspace, but both of us are limited to standard admin features. We need someone to transfer ownership or otherwise restore top-level access.
Ownership transfers normally have to be initiated by the current owner from within the app. If that person left, there may not be anything we can do from support.
That’s not workable. There has to be a recovery process for a departed owner in a business account. We can provide invoices, company ID, and legal contact details.

You’re right, there is a reviewed recovery path for this scenario. I’m escalating your case to our account recovery team for ownership transfer verification. They will likely request proof of company affiliation, a response from the billing contact, and details of the proposed new owner before they can update access.

subject: Can't log in to update approval workflow
description: I'm trying to update our invoice approval automation before month-end, but my account keeps saying I don't have access after I log in. I can still see dashboards, just not the workflow area. I also need a clearer explanation of who should own these automations so this doesn't keep happening every time someone changes roles.

comments.message: I can log in fine, but when I click Workflows it says access denied. We have an approval flow that routes invoices over $5,000 to finance, and I need to edit the threshold. Who is supposed to own these workflows, and why would access be different from the rest of the app?

Workflow access is controlled separately from general reporting access. In most accounts, dashboards are available to broader roles, while editing automations requires either Admin or Automation Manager permissions. Best practice is to assign business-critical workflows to a shared owner, not an individual employee, so ownership doesn't break when teams change. Can you confirm what role your profile shows under Settings > Team?
It says Ops Analyst. I used to be able to edit this workflow before IT changed our access setup last week.

Ops Analyst often loses workflow edit rights if your admin switched to a stricter role template. I can't change that from support, but your account admin can either add the Automation Manager permission to your role or move the invoice workflow under a shared operations account. I've linked our role-permission matrix and workflow governance guide below so your team can decide who should own approval automations going forward.

subject: La renovación anual salió con moneda incorrecta y no carga el dashboard
description: Nuestro contrato anual debía renovarse en EUR, pero el cargo se hizo en USD y el banco aplicó una comisión extra. Además, después de esa renovación el dashboard principal no muestra datos y se queda con el spinner infinito. Borré caché, probé en incógnito y sigue igual.

comments.message: Nuestro contrato anual debía renovarse en EUR, pero el cargo se hizo en USD y el banco aplicó una comisión extra. Además, después de esa renovación el dashboard principal no muestra datos y se queda con el spinner infinito. Borré caché, probé en incógnito y sigue igual.

Gracias por escribir. Si el cargo salió en USD, seguramente la cuenta está configurada en dólares. Sobre el spinner del dashboard, a veces ocurre cuando el navegador bloquea scripts; revisen si tienen un adblock activo.
No, nuestra orden de compra y las facturas anteriores están en EUR. Y ya desactivé adblock; además esto empezó exactamente después de la renovación.

Entendido. Entonces voy a escalar ambos puntos. No tengo visibilidad para cambiar la moneda de una renovación ya emitida, y para el dashboard necesito que ingeniería revise si la renovación dejó desincronizado el servicio de analítica del tenant. Les aviso cuando tenga novedades.

subject: SAML login works but imported customer records are missing fields
description: I'm the admin for our RevOps team. After we switched our workspace to SAML SSO yesterday, several reps can sign in, but our nightly Salesforce import is now creating duplicate customer records and not mapping the Account Owner field anymore. I also noticed one of our service accounts disappeared from the integration settings after we enforced SSO. I need help figuring out whether this is an access issue, an integration issue, or if our data got corrupted during the change.

comments.message: We enabled Okta SAML yesterday afternoon. Human users can mostly log in now, although two reps had to be re-invited. Since then, the Salesforce sync created around 200 duplicate accounts and the owner mapping is blank on new records. Our old integration user no longer appears under connected accounts.

Thanks for the detail. Enforcing SSO can disable password-based access for any non-SSO service account if it wasn't exempted, so your Salesforce connector may have lost authorization. That would explain the missing integration user and partial remapping. Please check Settings > Integrations > Salesforce and see whether the connector shows 'reauthorization required.' Also export a sample of the duplicate records with their external IDs so we can confirm whether matching keys stopped coming through.

You're right, the connector says reauthorization required. I reconnected it with a new admin account, but the sync still isn't populating Account Owner, and duplicates from last night are still in the database. The external ID column is blank on the bad records.

If the external ID is blank, the sync can't match existing records, so duplicates are expected. Reauthorizing with a different admin account may also have changed field-level permissions, which would explain Account Owner not syncing. I've checked the likely cause: the new OAuth user appears not to have read access to Salesforce Account Owner and your previous service account exemption from SSO was removed. Please create or restore a dedicated integration user, exempt it from SSO enforcement, grant it access to the external ID and Account Owner fields, then reconnect the integration with that user. The existing duplicates won't be merged automatically, so I'm escalating this to our data team to run a dedupe review on the records created after yesterday 6 PM.

subject: List filter counts changed after timezone correction
description: After we corrected our workspace timezone from UTC to Eastern, several saved lists suddenly have different counts, and some contacts are appearing in the wrong follow-up bucket. I traced it to a date-based filter on "Last activity before today," where records near midnight seem to move in and out depending on whether I view the list or export it. I need help troubleshooting whether the timezone change reindexed date values incorrectly and how to normalize the affected records.

comments.message: After we corrected our workspace timezone from UTC to Eastern, several saved lists suddenly have different counts, and some contacts are appearing in the wrong follow-up bucket. I traced it to a date-based filter on "Last activity before today," where records near midnight seem to move in and out depending on whether I view the list or export it. I need help troubleshooting whether the timezone change reindexed date values incorrectly and how to normalize the affected records.
Timezone changes can affect relative date filters, especially around midnight boundaries. Can you share one contact ID that appears in the list but not in the export, and one that does the opposite?
CT-7712 appears in the list but not the export, and CT-8841 appears in the export but not the list. Both had activity logged around 11:30 PM Eastern on Monday.

I checked those records and found the UI list is evaluating "today" in Eastern as expected, but the export service is still using UTC for the relative-date filter. So this is likely a bug introduced by the timezone change rather than corrupted contact data. For a reliable export right now, replace the relative filter with an absolute timestamp range, and I’ll report the list/export mismatch to engineering.

subject: SCIM-created users stuck pending and cannot be activated manually
description: After enabling SCIM from Azure AD, eight users were created in a pending state. As org admin, I can edit their names and groups, but I can’t activate them manually or resend invites. The Activate option is disabled, and the profile says "managed externally." I’ve verified they are assigned correctly in Azure and even removed/re-added one test user, but the status never changes. We have training tomorrow, so I need to know if this is fixable from our side.

comments.message: Eight SCIM-created users are stuck in pending, and manual activation is disabled because they’re marked managed externally.

When users are managed through SCIM, activation is generally controlled by your identity provider. Please verify the Azure AD provisioning logs show a successful create and that the app assignment includes all required attributes, especially active and userName.
The Azure logs show successful creates for all eight users, and active=true is present. That’s why I think the issue is in your platform, not Azure.
You may need to wait for the next sync cycle. Sometimes Azure updates take a few hours to reflect, so please check again later.

subject: Nie mogę dezaktywować użytkownika i przenieść jego zasobów
description: Próbuję dezaktywować konto byłego konsultanta i przenieść jego dashboardy oraz alerty do innej osoby. Kiedy klikam dezaktywację, system informuje, że użytkownik nadal ma zasoby wymagające nowego właściciela, ale ekran z listą tych zasobów się nie ładuje. Potrzebuję pomocy z samą zmianą właściciela oraz z ustaleniem, dlaczego lista pozostaje pusta.

comments.message: Chcę wyłączyć konto byłego pracownika, ale system mówi, że trzeba najpierw przenieść zasoby. Problem w tym, że lista zasobów jest pusta i kręci się w nieskończoność.

Dzień dobry, proszę podać e-mail użytkownika do dezaktywacji, e-mail nowego właściciela zasobów oraz informację, czy problem występuje w jednej przeglądarce czy we wszystkich. Jeśli może Pan/Pani, proszę też sprawdzić, czy w zakładce alertów lub dashboardów widać jakieś nieprzypisane obiekty.
Chodzi o pawel.k@acme.pl, nowy właściciel to ola.m@acme.pl. Sprawdzane w Chrome i Safari. W samych dashboardach nic podejrzanego nie widać, ale alerty Pawła nadal przychodzą na maila zespołu.

Dziękuję. To sugeruje, że ukryte zasoby to aktywne alerty powiązane ze starym kontem, a widok transferu nie renderuje ich poprawnie. Przeniosłem własność alertów na ola.m@acme.pl i zainicjowałem dezaktywację użytkownika. Błąd pustej listy zgłosiłem do zespołu frontendowego, bo powinien pokazywać te obiekty zamiast wisieć na ładowaniu.

subject: Seat transfer between departments caused unexpected overage
description: I moved four licenses from our Support department to Sales and changed the account structure so each department now has its own cost center. After that, our invoice included an overage charge for extra seats even though the total number of paid users did not increase. Can you review the account changes and explain or remove the overage?

comments.message: I moved four licenses from our Support department to Sales and changed the account structure so each department now has its own cost center. After that, our invoice included an overage charge for extra seats even though the total number of paid users did not increase. Can you review the account changes and explain or remove the overage?
Overage charges usually happen when more seats are added, so the invoice is probably correct if Sales needed additional licenses after the transfer. Cost center updates don't affect billing.
But I didn't add any net-new seats. I reassigned existing ones and the audit log shows the same total seat count before and after.
In that case, you may want to wait until next cycle to see whether it evens out automatically. I don't see a way to adjust the current invoice from here.

subject: Error al conectar Salesforce y los campos no se sincronizan
description: Estoy intentando conectar nuestra cuenta de Salesforce con su plataforma para que los leads entren automáticamente, pero aunque la autenticación parece completarse, los campos personalizados no se están mapeando bien. En concreto, el campo "Tipo de cliente" en Salesforce no aparece en la configuración del mapeo y los nuevos registros llegan sin esa información. Ya revisé la integración dos veces y no sé si me falta activar algo en la configuración del producto.

comments.message: Conecté Salesforce desde Configuración > Integraciones y la conexión figura como activa, pero al crear un lead nuevo solo se sincronizan nombre, email y empresa. Los campos personalizados no salen en el mapeador.

Gracias por el detalle. Normalmente esto ocurre cuando los campos personalizados aún no están habilitados para uso en formularios o cuando la sincronización de esquema no se ha actualizado. Por favor, vaya a Configuración > Objetos de cliente > Campos y confirme que "Tipo de cliente" esté marcado como visible y editable. Después, en la integración de Salesforce, pulse "Actualizar campos" para volver a cargar el esquema.
Hice eso y ahora sí aparece el campo en el mapeo, pero solo me deja mapearlo como texto libre y en Salesforce es una lista desplegable.

En ese caso la integración está leyendo el campo como texto porque en nuestra plataforma el campo de destino fue creado como texto. Cree o cambie el campo interno a tipo selección con los mismos valores permitidos que en Salesforce, guarde, y luego vuelva a ejecutar "Actualizar campos". Con eso el mapeo debería respetar la lista desplegable.

subject: Need to rename user emails after domain change
description: We changed our company email domain this weekend, and I updated most users manually, but now two department admins can’t log in and one of them lost admin privileges after the email change. I need to fix their account emails, restore the correct admin roles, and make sure their managed users still report to them.

comments.message: We migrated from @oldharbor.net to @harborlabs.com. For nina@oldharbor.net and jgross@oldharbor.net, I changed the login emails yesterday. Nina can’t log in at all now, and Josh came back in as a regular member instead of a department admin.

Email-domain updates can temporarily affect role sync if SSO is involved. Please confirm whether these users authenticate through SSO or password login, and tell me which department-admin role Josh should have restored.
Both are on SSO through Okta. Josh should be Department Admin for Customer Success, and Nina was already a full Admin. Their teams and direct reports should stay exactly the same.

Thanks. I can see the email changes completed, but the role sync from your identity provider did not fully update. I’ve restored Josh to Department Admin for Customer Success and re-enabled Nina’s admin access on the new harborlabs.com identities. Their user assignments and reporting structure were not changed. If Nina still can’t sign in, have your Okta admin push a fresh assignment for her new username.

subject: Locked out after enabling MFA enforcement
description: I turned on mandatory MFA for our org last night, and now I can’t get into the admin console myself. I’m the only full admin on the account. After I enter my password, it asks for a code, but I never receive the SMS. I need access restored quickly and I assume this needs to be escalated because I can’t make any changes from my side.

comments.message: I’ve tried three times and I’m not getting the MFA text message. The backup codes I thought I saved are apparently from another service. Please help me regain access.

I’m sorry you’re locked out. For security reasons, we can’t disable MFA directly from chat once enforcement is enabled on an admin profile. This requires review by our account recovery team, so I’m escalating your case now. They’ll verify account ownership before they can reset the MFA method.
How long does that usually take? We have new hires starting in an hour and I need to invite them.

Typical turnaround is 2-4 hours during business hours, though urgent admin lockouts are often handled sooner. I’ve flagged the impact on user provisioning in the escalation. If you have access to the billing owner’s email, please watch for a verification message from our recovery team, since responding quickly to that email usually speeds up the reset.

subject: Deleted employee still appears in mention picker
description: I deactivated a former employee two weeks ago and removed their license, but people can still @mention them in comments. When they click the name it shows as inactive, so the account is definitely not active. I need to understand whether this is a sync problem or if I missed a step in user administration.

comments.message: I deactivated a former employee two weeks ago and removed their license, but people can still @mention them in comments. When they click the name it shows as inactive, so the account is definitely not active. I need to understand whether this is a sync problem or if I missed a step in user administration.

Thanks for the detail. This is usually not an access issue but an indexing delay in the mention service. Deactivating and removing the license stops login, but the user can remain searchable in mentions until the directory index refreshes. Please check whether the user is still a member of any team or project groups, because group membership can keep them in the picker even while inactive.
I found they were still in one legacy project team. I removed that, but the name is still showing up right now.

That makes sense. Once the final group membership is removed, the mention index normally refreshes within 4-6 hours. I've also submitted a manual reindex request for your workspace to speed it up. If the user still appears tomorrow, send us the profile URL and we can inspect the directory cache directly.

subject: Data sync duplicates after changing field mapping
description: I reconfigured our Salesforce sync so the account owner maps to a custom user field in your app. Since making that change, new accounts are syncing twice and some records are missing the mapped owner value. I need help figuring out whether I broke the mapping or if there is another sync setting involved.

comments.message: Before the change, sync worked fine. After remapping account owner, every new Salesforce account creates two company records in your system. One has the owner field empty, the other has partial data.

That sounds like the mapping may now point to a non-unique field, causing the sync to create a new record instead of updating the existing one. Please check the sync configuration for the record matching rule and make sure it still uses Salesforce Account ID as the unique external key, not owner email or company name. Also verify the custom owner field is set to accept the same data type being sent by Salesforce.
The matching rule was accidentally changed to company name during testing. I set it back to Account ID. The duplicates stopped, but existing duplicate records are still there.

Great, that should prevent new duplicates. Existing duplicates won't merge automatically, so you'll need to clean those up manually or through our bulk merge tool. If you want, I can send the article for deduping, but the sync configuration itself should now be correct.

subject: Changement de devise de l’entité et ventilation par région erronée
description: Nous avons renommé une entité interne et mis à jour plusieurs paramètres du compte, mais depuis ce changement les rapports exécutifs affichent la mauvaise région pour une partie des données. Je dois aussi faire modifier les paramètres administratifs de cette entité car l’ancien responsable n’est plus dans l’entreprise. Je ne sais pas si la mauvaise ventilation vient du changement d’entité ou d’un mapping cassé dans les rapports.

comments.message: Bonjour, depuis la réorganisation de notre entité Benelux/France, les rapports exécutifs envoient certains dossiers France dans la région "Autres". En parallèle, je dois reprendre les paramètres administratifs de cette entité car l’ancien responsable a quitté la société.

Bonjour, pour la partie compte, je peux vous aider à faire mettre à jour le responsable d’entité si vous m’envoyez le nom de la société et votre e-mail professionnel. Pour les rapports, la catégorie "Autres" apparaît souvent quand le champ région n’est pas rempli sur les fiches.
Le champ région est bien rempli, justement. Le problème a commencé juste après qu’on a renommé l’entité et déplacé plusieurs équipes sous cette structure.

Merci, c’est utile. Je lance la demande de changement de responsable d’entité avec vérification de domaine. Côté reporting, cela ressemble plutôt à un mapping figé dans votre rapport exécutif : il utilise probablement encore l’ancien identifiant d’entité malgré le nouveau nom. Je transmets à notre équipe reporting pour correction du dataset et je vous conseille d’utiliser temporairement le filtre par identifiant d’équipe plutôt que par région agrégée.

subject: Le rapport affiche des agents désactivés comme utilisateurs actifs
description: Dans notre rapport d'adoption, deux anciens agents partis en février apparaissent encore comme utilisateurs actifs en avril. Cela fausse notre suivi interne. J'ai vérifié qu'ils sont bien désactivés dans l'administration. Est-ce que le reporting met du temps à se mettre à jour ?

comments.message: Bonjour, deux agents désactivés apparaissent encore comme utilisateurs actifs dans notre rapport d'adoption du mois d'avril.
Bonjour, oui, un léger délai est possible. Normalement les rapports se mettent à jour sous quelques heures après la désactivation.
Cela fait plus de six semaines, donc ce n'est pas un simple délai. Ils n'ont aucune activité récente, mais ils sont comptés comme actifs.

Merci pour la précision. Dans ce cas, il est probable que le rapport "adoption" utilise la dimension "membres provisionnés" plutôt que "utilisateurs ayant eu une activité". Si c'est un rapport standard, je ne peux pas modifier la logique moi-même. Je vais transmettre votre cas à l'équipe produit pour confirmer si c'est attendu ou non, et je vous recommande en attendant d'ajouter un filtre sur "dernière activité" pour exclure ces profils.

subject: Zendesk ticket updates not reflected in team performance dashboard
description: We integrated Zendesk so our support managers could use your team performance dashboard, but reopened tickets and assignee changes from Zendesk are not updating in the report. The raw ticket record eventually changes, but the dashboard keeps the old owner and status for days. Can you look into whether the integration is failing to trigger report refreshes, and escalate if this needs engineering?

comments.message: We integrated Zendesk so our support managers could use your team performance dashboard, but reopened tickets and assignee changes from Zendesk are not updating in the report. The raw ticket record eventually changes, but the dashboard keeps the old owner and status for days. Can you look into whether the integration is failing to trigger report refreshes, and escalate if this needs engineering?

I checked a few synced Zendesk tickets in your workspace. The integration is updating the underlying record, but your team performance dashboard is using a snapshot dataset that refreshes every 72 hours rather than real-time ticket data. That's why the raw record changes before the dashboard catches up. You can shorten the lag by editing the dashboard dataset schedule to daily, but sub-daily refresh isn't available on your current plan.
Daily helps, but it still won't explain why some tickets have the original owner even after four days. Those examples were reassigned last week.

Good catch. Since the delay exceeds the normal snapshot window, I've escalated those sample ticket IDs to engineering to check whether reassignment events from Zendesk are being excluded from the dataset rebuild. In parallel, I recommend building a temporary report directly on the live ticket object if you need accurate owner metrics before the dashboard issue is confirmed.

subject: Jira issue transition action fails only in scheduled workflow
description: I built a scheduled workflow that finds overdue onboarding tasks and transitions the linked Jira issues from In Progress to Blocked. The search step works and returns records, but the Jira transition step fails for every item in the scheduled run. If I test the same Jira action manually on a single record, it succeeds.

comments.message: The error says 'transition not available for this issue,' but these issues definitely have Blocked as a valid status in Jira. This only happens when the automation runs on the nightly schedule.

Thanks for the details. Scheduled workflows run under the workflow owner’s integration permissions, while manual tests use the current user session. If the Jira connection belongs to someone who no longer has project transition rights, the automation can fail even though your manual test passes.
That might be it. The workflow owner left the company last month, and her Jira account was deactivated. Is there a way to switch the connection without rebuilding the automation?

Yes — open the workflow, go to the Jira step, and use 'Change connected account' to reauthorize with an active Jira user that has permission to transition issues in that project. After saving, rerun the schedule or use 'Run now' to validate. If the old owner appears on multiple automation steps, updating each Jira action is important because they do not all inherit the new credential automatically.

subject: Can't use magic link login and Marketo sync stopped matching leads
description: Our contractors use magic link login because they are outside our SSO directory. Since yesterday, magic links are failing for that group after we tightened domain access rules. At the same time, our Marketo sync began inserting duplicate leads instead of updating existing ones, and source campaign values are blank in newly imported records. I need to get people back into the app and stop the bad data from piling up.

comments.message: We restricted access to approved domains and turned on SSO enforcement for employees. Contractors are supposed to stay on magic links, but now they get 'access method not allowed.' Separately, the Marketo connector is creating duplicate leads and source campaign is empty on all records since this morning.

For the login issue, it sounds like the domain restriction policy is catching contractor emails before the magic-link exception is applied. Add their domains to the allowed list or create an access policy group that permits passwordless login. On Marketo, duplicates plus blank source campaign usually mean the sync user lost visibility to the dedupe key and campaign field. Did the Marketo connection use one of the employee accounts now forced onto SSO?
Yes, the Marketo connection was under a former sales ops employee account that is now suspended. We can add the contractor domains, but we need the import fixed too.

Understood. Reconnect Marketo using a non-human service account that is excluded from SSO and has access to the lead external key plus source campaign field. Once reconnected, run a dry-run sync first to confirm updates are matching instead of creating new leads. That should stop new duplicates, but the duplicate leads already created will need separate cleanup.

subject: Erro 403 ao usar chave de API em workspace recém-criado
description: Acabei de criar um novo workspace para a equipa de suporte em Portugal e ativei o acesso à API. Mesmo assim, qualquer chamada para /v1/users retorna 403. A mesma chave funciona no workspace antigo. Não sei se falta alguma permissão na configuração do produto ou se a API não herda as definições entre workspaces.

comments.message: Acabei de criar um novo workspace para a equipa de suporte em Portugal e ativei o acesso à API. Mesmo assim, qualquer chamada para /v1/users retorna 403. A mesma chave funciona no workspace antigo. Não sei se falta alguma permissão na configuração do produto ou se a API não herda as definições entre workspaces.

Obrigado pelo detalhe. As chaves de API não herdam permissões entre workspaces; cada workspace tem o seu próprio escopo. No novo workspace, o acesso à API estava ativado, mas o perfil associado à chave não tinha a permissão "Ler utilizadores" nas definições de funções. Ativei essa permissão e recomendo gerar uma nova chave, porque a atual foi criada antes da alteração de função.
Gerei a nova chave e agora o /v1/users responde 200. Não sabia que as permissões eram separadas por workspace.
Sim, isso costuma causar confusão em contas com vários ambientes. Já agora, confirme também se os restantes endpoints que usa precisam de permissões adicionais no mesmo perfil, para evitar novos 403.

subject: Proration question after splitting one workspace into two brands
description: We reconfigured our account from one workspace with two brands into two separate brand workspaces mid-cycle. The invoice now shows prorated subscription changes and duplicate automation charges that I don't fully understand. I need someone to map the billing lines back to the configuration change so I can approve the invoice internally.

comments.message: On the 12th, we split our single workspace into Brand A and Brand B workspaces so each team could manage its own forms and automation rules. This month's invoice has prorated credits, two new workspace charges, and what looks like duplicate automation add-ons. Can you explain which line corresponds to what?

I reviewed the subscription timeline. You originally had one workspace with the Automation Suite add-on. When the split was performed on the 12th, the original workspace was credited for the unused portion of the month, and two new workspace subscriptions were prorated from that date forward. The Automation Suite is billed per workspace, so after the split it appears twice — once for Brand A and once for Brand B.
That helps. We only need advanced automation on Brand A, not both. We cloned the configuration during the split and forgot to remove it from Brand B. Can that be fixed this cycle?

Yes. I've disabled Automation Suite on Brand B effective today, so next month's bill will only include it for Brand A. For the current prorated charge, I submitted a billing adjustment request noting that the add-on was inherited during the workspace split. If approved, you'll receive a partial credit for Brand B's automation line.

subject: Okta-SCIM legt deaktivierte Benutzer erneut an
description: Wir verwenden Ihre Okta-SCIM-Integration für das Benutzer-Provisioning. Wenn ein Mitarbeiter in Okta deaktiviert wird, wird er in Ihrer Plattform erst gesperrt, taucht aber nach dem nächsten Gruppenabgleich wieder als aktiver Benutzer auf. Das ist sicherheitsrelevant. Können Sie erklären, warum die Integration das macht?

comments.message: Wir haben das jetzt bei drei ehemaligen Mitarbeitern gesehen. Deaktivierung in Okta klappt zuerst, aber nach einigen Stunden sind die Accounts in Ihrer Plattform wieder aktiv.

Das klingt ernst. Bei der Okta-SCIM-Integration passiert so etwas meistens, wenn neben dem SCIM-Deprovisioning noch ein Gruppen-Sync aktiv ist, der Benutzer mit zugewiesener App-Mitgliedschaft erneut provisioniert. Können Sie in Okta prüfen, ob die betroffenen Nutzer zwar deaktiviert, aber weiterhin der Anwendung oder einer zugeordneten Gruppe zugewiesen sind?
Ja, die App-Zuweisung war noch über eine Standardgruppe vorhanden. Heißt das, wir müssen Benutzer immer erst aus der Gruppe entfernen?

Genau. Solange die Gruppenzuweisung aktiv bleibt, interpretiert der nächste Abgleich den Benutzer als erneut bereitzustellenden Account. Empfohlen ist die Reihenfolge: erst App- oder Gruppenzuweisung entfernen, dann Benutzer deaktivieren. Ich sende Ihnen zusätzlich unseren Leitfaden zur SCIM-Deprovisionierung mit Okta, damit das künftig sauber läuft.

subject: Azure AD group sync removed licenses but data exports kept running
description: We use Azure AD to manage access. After changing group membership rules, several users were downgraded from full seats to viewer access, which is expected, but a few of them still have scheduled data exports and Slack notifications firing from their old setup. I thought removing the license would stop that. I'm trying to understand whether these automations are stored with the user, whether admins can transfer them, and why the integration is still posting on behalf of people who no longer have the right role.

comments.message: We changed Azure AD group mappings and some users lost full access, but their scheduled exports and Slack alerts are still active. Can admins transfer or disable these in bulk?

When users are downgraded, personal automations do not always stop immediately. Scheduled exports and notification rules are separate objects from licensing, and some integrations continue until the underlying token is revoked or the schedule is disabled. An admin can review automations from the governance page and reassign certain items.
I don't see a governance page in our plan, and I need to stop these today. Is there a direct admin tool for all users' exports?

In that case, you should ask each user to log in and turn off their own schedules before they're downgraded. There isn't really an admin-level way to manage that globally, and Slack messages should stop on their own soon.

subject: Erreur après changement du nom affiché de l’utilisateur
description: J’ai voulu mettre à jour mon nom affiché après un changement de nom de famille, mais depuis la modification certains collègues me voient toujours sous mon ancien nom dans les commentaires, tandis que mon nouveau nom apparaît dans mon profil. Je voudrais aussi savoir si vous pouvez corriger mon historique pour éviter la confusion.

comments.message: Bonjour, j’ai changé mon nom dans les paramètres du compte hier. Mon profil affiche bien "Camille Arnaud", mais dans les discussions de projet on voit encore "Camille Morel". Est-ce que vous pouvez harmoniser cela ?

Bonjour, le changement de nom met à jour le profil actuel, mais les anciens commentaires conservent souvent le nom au moment de la publication pour l’historique. Nous pouvons vérifier s’il s’agit seulement d’un délai de synchronisation ou d’un comportement normal. Depuis quand voyez-vous cette incohérence ?
Depuis hier après-midi. Même sur un nouveau commentaire ajouté ce matin, un collègue m’a dit voir encore l’ancien nom dans une notification e-mail.

Merci, dans ce cas cela ressemble à un cache partiel entre le profil et les notifications. J’ai rafraîchi votre fiche utilisateur côté système. Les nouveaux commentaires et notifications devraient maintenant afficher "Camille Arnaud". En revanche, l’historique ancien restera probablement sous l’ancien nom pour des raisons de traçabilité.

subject: Zahlung fehlgeschlagen trotz gültiger Karte
description: Seit heute erscheint bei uns im Konto "Zahlung fehlgeschlagen", obwohl die hinterlegte Firmenkarte aktiv ist und laut Bank nicht abgelehnt wurde. Unser Workspace ist jetzt im schreibgeschützten Modus, und das blockiert unser Team. Bitte helfen Sie schnell, damit der Zugang wieder freigeschaltet wird.

comments.message: Seit heute erscheint bei uns im Konto "Zahlung fehlgeschlagen", obwohl die hinterlegte Firmenkarte aktiv ist und laut Bank nicht abgelehnt wurde. Unser Workspace ist jetzt im schreibgeschützten Modus, und das blockiert unser Team. Bitte helfen Sie schnell, damit der Zugang wieder freigeschaltet wird.
Das klingt nach einem Problem mit dem Zahlungsanbieter. Bitte entfernen Sie die aktuelle Karte und hinterlegen Sie dieselbe Karte erneut. Danach sollte das System automatisch noch einmal abbuchen.
Das habe ich gerade gemacht. Jetzt sehe ich zwei fehlgeschlagene Zahlungsversuche, aber der Workspace ist weiterhin eingeschränkt.
Dann warten Sie bitte einfach 24 Stunden. Das System entsperrt Konten in der Regel automatisch, sobald eine Karte vorhanden ist.

subject: Best way to organize custom fields before importing a new client list
description: I’m onboarding a new sales ops manager next week, and before we import about 20,000 client records I want a clear explanation of how we should structure custom fields. Right now people have been putting renewal dates, regions, and account tier into random text fields. Can you walk me through how to set this up properly so the team understands what belongs where?

comments.message: I’m onboarding a new sales ops manager next week, and before we import about 20,000 client records I want a clear explanation of how we should structure custom fields. Right now people have been putting renewal dates, regions, and account tier into random text fields. Can you walk me through how to set this up properly so the team understands what belongs where?

Absolutely. Before importing, it helps to define each field by data type and business use. Renewal date should be a date field so you can filter and automate reminders. Region is better as a controlled dropdown to avoid spelling variations, and account tier should also be a single-select field if each client only has one tier. I’d avoid free-text fields except for notes. A good rollout is: 1) document each field’s purpose, 2) create naming conventions, 3) import a small sample file, and 4) give the team a short data-entry guide with examples of valid values.
That makes sense. Is there a way to stop users from creating more one-off fields later?

Yes — if you’re an admin, you can limit field creation permissions to admins only under workspace settings. I’d also recommend maintaining a simple data dictionary in a shared doc so new managers know which field to use instead of requesting duplicates.

subject: Subscription paused, but automated reactivation triggered a charge and ownership change
description: We paused our subscription during a temporary shutdown and built a workflow that should reactivate the account only after a finance approval and an owner review. Somehow the account was reactivated automatically during testing, a monthly charge posted, and the workspace owner switched from our CFO to an implementation manager account used in the workflow. We need the accidental charge reviewed and an explanation for how the automation was allowed to make both the billing and ownership changes without the required approval.

comments.message: The reactivation flow was in test mode. It should not have resumed billing, and it definitely should not have reassigned account ownership to the implementation manager service user.
Test automations can’t affect live billing, so the charge was probably generated by the normal renewal cycle rather than your workflow. Ownership changes also require a direct admin action in the UI.
That doesn’t line up with the audit logs. They show an API token from the implementation manager account reactivated the subscription and then called Update Owner within the same minute.

Thanks for pointing that out. I reviewed the token activity and you’re correct: the workflow was connected to a live API credential even though the automation itself was marked test mode, so the actions executed against production. I’m escalating the charge dispute to billing for possible reversal and have revoked that token to prevent another accidental reactivation. The owner has also been restored to your CFO account, but engineering will need to investigate why your approval gate was bypassed in that flow.

subject: Zmiana roli superadmina blokuje się na ekranie potwierdzenia
description: Próbuję przekazać rolę superadmina innej osobie w firmie, ale po wpisaniu kodu potwierdzającego ekran tylko odświeża formularz i nic się nie dzieje. Testowałem kilka razy, różne przeglądarki i różne kody. Potrzebuję pilnie zmienić uprawnienia, bo od jutra jestem poza firmą. Proszę o pomoc z przeprowadzeniem zmiany i diagnozą problemu z potwierdzeniem.

comments.message: Muszę dziś przekazać rolę superadmina, ale ekran potwierdzenia po wpisaniu kodu tylko się odświeża. Nie ma błędu, po prostu wraca do tego samego formularza.
Proszę wyłączyć blokowanie wyskakujących okienek i spróbować ponownie. To zwykle pomaga przy ekranach potwierdzenia.
To nie są wyskakujące okienka. Sprawdzałem nawet na czystym profilu przeglądarki i telefonie. Czy możecie ręcznie przepisać superadmina?
Ręczna zmiana superadmina nie jest możliwa z poziomu supportu. Mogę jedynie zasugerować ponowną próbę później albo użycie innego urządzenia.

subject: Webhook deliveries returning 401 after rotating API key
description: I rotated our production API key yesterday and updated it in our integration service, but since then every webhook retry and our follow-up API calls to fetch ticket details are failing with 401 Unauthorized. The same code worked before the rotation. I need help figuring out whether we're using the new key correctly or if something else changed on your side.

comments.message: We rotated the key at about 18:00 UTC. Since then POSTs to /v1/events/ack and GETs to /v1/tickets/{id} both return 401. We already confirmed the new key is what our secret manager is serving.

I checked our auth logs and can see requests from your account still presenting a key ending in 7d9a, which is the old revoked credential. Your newly issued key ends in c184. In many cases this happens when one worker pool keeps an old env var after rotation. Please restart all workers and verify the Authorization header is exactly `Bearer <new_key>` with no extra quotes or whitespace.

You were right. Two background workers had not restarted and were still using the old secret. After cycling them, the GET requests work again, but webhook acknowledgements are now returning 403 instead of 401.

Progress at least. The 403 on `/v1/events/ack` is because that endpoint requires the `events:write` scope, and your replacement key was created with read-only access. I’ve regenerated a scoped key template in your admin panel instructions. Create a new key with `tickets:read` and `events:write`, update the service, and the acknowledgements should succeed.

subject: Mudança de nome da organização duplicou membros desativados
description: Eu alterei hoje o nome da organização e também atualizei o identificador interno da conta porque passamos por um rebranding. Depois disso, a lista de membros começou a mostrar usuários desativados em duplicidade, e ao clicar em alguns perfis a tela dá erro. Parece ter acontecido logo após a alteração da conta, então quero saber se isso é um bug conhecido e como corrigir sem perder histórico.

comments.message: Depois de mudar o nome da organização e o identificador da conta, começaram a aparecer usuários desativados duplicados e alguns perfis nem abrem.
Olá! Esse comportamento não é esperado. Consegue me confirmar se a alteração foi feita pelo painel de administração ou via API? Também preciso de 2 ou 3 exemplos de usuários afetados.
Foi pelo painel de administração. Exemplos: marcia@antigaempresa.com, tiago@antigaempresa.com e suporte@antigaempresa.com. Todos aparecem duas vezes, mas só um registro abre.

Obrigado. Analisei os exemplos e vejo que os registros duplicados surgiram logo após a atualização do identificador da conta. Isso sugere um bug de indexação da lista de membros depois de alterações cadastrais da organização. Como correção temporária, a visualização pode ficar inconsistente, mas os dados principais parecem preservados. Já abri uma investigação para engenharia e anexei os usuários citados. Avisaremos quando o reparo estiver em produção.

subject: Help me explain usage limits to our managers
description: I'm putting together a quick guide for our managers on workspace usage limits and overage alerts. I thought I understood how the monthly active usage metric works, but our numbers jumped after a bulk import and now I don't know how to explain it accurately. I don't want to train people with a simplified answer that's actually misleading.

comments.message: Can you explain usage limits in a way managers will understand, and should a bulk import affect monthly active usage?

You can describe it as: usage limits track how many people actively interact with the workspace during the billing period, and alerts warn admins when they're nearing the plan threshold. A bulk import can affect the metric if it creates or activates users in a way that counts toward your plan, but whether it does depends on the exact contract terms and the type of import performed.
So is there a standard rule, or do I need to tell managers 'it depends'?

For managers, 'it depends' isn't very helpful, so I'd suggest telling them that any mass user change can impact reported usage and should be reviewed with an admin first. I don't have access to your contract details from here, so I'm escalating this to our account support team to confirm how your specific plan treats imported or reactivated users. They can give you wording that's accurate for your guide.

subject: Comparativo trimestral soma janeiro duas vezes no dashboard
description: Meu dashboard executivo com relatório comparativo trimestral está somando janeiro em duplicidade no cartão de receitas por cliente. Fevereiro e março parecem normais, mas o total do trimestre fica inflado exatamente pelo valor de janeiro repetido. Já revisei os filtros, a fórmula calculada e o período, e não encontrei erro de configuração. Preciso saber se vocês conseguem investigar isso com urgência.

comments.message: O widget afetado é o cartão “Receita líquida por cliente - trimestre”. Quando abro o detalhamento por mês, janeiro aparece duas vezes na composição do total trimestral, embora a tabela abaixo liste janeiro só uma vez.
Olá! Talvez exista um problema na sua fórmula calculada, especialmente se estiver usando soma acumulada trimestral. Você pode recriar a métrica em vez de reutilizar a existente?
Eu já recriei a métrica do zero e o resultado continua igual. Inclusive outro dashboard usando a mesma fonte apresenta a mesma duplicidade só no cartão, não na tabela.

Entendi. Nesse cenário, é mais provável que o bug esteja na renderização/agregação do componente de cartão para comparativos trimestrais, e não na sua fórmula. Vou abrir um incidente com o time de produto de Relatórios e anexar que a tabela base não duplica janeiro, apenas o cartão resumido. Se puder, mantenha a tabela como fonte de verdade até corrigirmos o widget.

subject: Double billed after enabling regional data residency add-on
description: I enabled the EU data residency option in our account configuration because our legal team required it, but our latest invoice makes it look like we were billed twice for the same add-on. I'm not sure if I misconfigured the region settings across two workspaces or if the charge is wrong. Please help me understand what happened.

comments.message: We have one parent account with two workspaces. I turned on EU residency for our customer support workspace and later saw two separate residency add-on charges on the invoice. I may also have toggled the same setting while testing in the operations workspace. Should that create two billable add-ons?
Usually add-ons are charged per account, not per workspace, so this sounds like a duplicate invoice line. Can you send a screenshot?
Attached. The invoice has one line for "EU Data Residency - Support" and another for "EU Data Residency - Ops". Both workspaces are under the same subscription, which is why I'm confused.

Thanks, I see the issue now. I was mistaken earlier: on your Enterprise contract, regional data residency is billed per workspace when the storage region is changed at the workspace level. Your support and ops workspaces are each configured for EU storage, so billing generated two add-on lines. If the ops workspace was only used for testing, disable EU residency there before the next renewal and I can ask billing whether they will waive this month's second charge, but I can't promise it.

subject: Cannot limit dashboard visibility to one department
description: I built an executive dashboard and tried to make it visible only to our Finance department group. Even after changing the sharing setting, managers from Sales can still see it in the dashboard list. I am not sure if group-based sharing needs another config step.

comments.message: The dashboard is set to 'shared with specific groups' and I selected Finance only. A Sales manager sent me a screenshot showing it still listed in their account this morning.

Dashboard permissions are controlled entirely by the share setting, so if Finance is the only selected group, Sales users should not see it. Please ask the Sales manager to log out and back in, because it's probably an old cached permission.
They logged out and back in. It still appears, though they get an error when opening it.
Then that means the restriction is working as intended. Seeing the dashboard name in the list can happen temporarily and isn't something we can change from support.

subject: Merged two teams and now duplicate users distort reporting
description: We merged our Support East and Support Central teams and updated several users to a new manager and department. Since then, our team performance reports are double-counting some people, and one supervisor who should now manage both groups can only edit users from the old East team. I suspect we have a mismatch between user administration hierarchy and reporting attributes after the account changes, but I’m not sure where to correct it.

comments.message: We merged our Support East and Support Central teams and updated several users to a new manager and department. Since then, our team performance reports are double-counting some people, and one supervisor who should now manage both groups can only edit users from the old East team. I suspect we have a mismatch between user administration hierarchy and reporting attributes after the account changes, but I’m not sure where to correct it.

What you’re describing does sound like two separate layers being out of sync: reporting may still have historical department assignments or duplicate active records, while the supervisor’s admin scope may still be tied to the old team mapping. Start by checking whether those users have both old and new team memberships in their profiles, and whether the supervisor is assigned to a permission group limited to East.
A few users do show both old and new team memberships, and the supervisor still has the East-only admin group. If I remove the old memberships, will that fix the report counts immediately?

It should stop future double-counting once the reporting model refreshes, but it may not correct reports built on historical snapshots until the next data update or unless the report filters exclude inactive memberships. Update the supervisor to the new combined admin group, remove the stale team memberships from affected users, and then rerun the report after the next refresh window. If the duplicates remain after that, send us the report ID and we’ll investigate the reporting side further.

subject: Record merge tool spins forever when moving notes between owners
description: When I try to merge duplicate company records and select the option to keep notes from both owners, the merge modal just spins forever after I click Confirm. If I uncheck notes transfer, the merge finishes normally. I tested this on three duplicate pairs, and it only seems to fail when one of the records has more than 200 historical notes. Is there a data limit here, or is the note migration step breaking?

comments.message: When I try to merge duplicate company records and select the option to keep notes from both owners, the merge modal just spins forever after I click Confirm. If I uncheck notes transfer, the merge finishes normally. I tested this on three duplicate pairs, and it only seems to fail when one of the records has more than 200 historical notes. Is there a data limit here, or is the note migration step breaking?

There isn’t a documented limit at 200 notes, so I tried one of your examples in the backend logs. The merge request is timing out during note reassignment after 240 seconds, which points to a performance bug rather than a user error. As a workaround, you can complete the merge without notes, then export the notes from the duplicate record before merge and reattach critical ones manually to the surviving company.
That workaround is pretty rough. Is there any way support can move the notes in bulk instead of us copying them manually?

We can’t bulk reassign notes from support tools today. I’ve escalated the timeout behavior to engineering with your examples, including the record pairs where note counts were 213, 247, and 301. If they provide a script-based remediation, I’ll update this ticket, but for now the issue remains open.

subject: Manager-based reminders not sending after permission cleanup
description: We cleaned up old admin permissions and disabled several inactive accounts, and now our reminder workflow for overdue approvals is not sending messages to managers for one business unit. The requests are still being created, but the escalation reminders never go out. I need to understand whether this is tied to user records, notification settings, or the workflow itself.

comments.message: Only the Manufacturing unit is affected. The reminder step is configured to notify the requester's manager after 48 hours. For impacted users, the requests stay pending and no reminder appears in the audit log.
Please check whether email notifications are enabled globally under Settings. If they're off, reminders won't send.
Email notifications are enabled globally, and other business units are still getting reminders. It seems specific to manager routing for Manufacturing users.
Then it may be a temporary delay in the notification service. Please wait 24 hours and see if the reminders appear.

subject: Need help understanding workspace permissions before rollout
description: I'm setting up our first department-wide rollout and I'm confused about how workspace roles, custom permissions, and team access all interact. I don't want to accidentally give managers edit access to dashboards they should only view. I read the help article, but it still isn't clear which setting takes precedence. Can someone explain the safest way to set this up for about 80 users?

comments.message: I'm setting up our first department-wide rollout and I'm confused about how workspace roles, custom permissions, and team access all interact. I don't want to accidentally give managers edit access to dashboards they should only view. I read the help article, but it still isn't clear which setting takes precedence. Can someone explain the safest way to set this up for about 80 users?

Absolutely. The simplest way to think about it is: workspace role sets the broad baseline, and any custom permission set can further restrict or expand what that user can do within allowed areas. Team access then controls which specific resources they can see. For your rollout, the safest approach is to assign managers a Viewer workspace role first, confirm team membership is correct, and then test one pilot user before bulk assigning all 80 users.
That helps, but one of our managers needs to edit only one dashboard folder and stay view-only everywhere else. I don't see how to do that with the standard roles.

You're right that standard roles alone won't handle that exception cleanly. I can explain the intended model, but this specific mixed-access setup usually needs review to confirm whether your plan supports folder-level edit exceptions in your current subscription and permission framework. I'm escalating this to our product support team so they can verify the exact configuration options and recommend the best rollout pattern.

subject: Webhook test succeeds but live events never arrive
description: I'm setting up a webhook to our internal endpoint. The built-in test payload reaches us successfully, but actual live events from ticket updates never show up. I've been troubleshooting our firewall and don't see anything blocked, so I need help figuring out why test delivery works but production events do not.

comments.message: I'm setting up a webhook to our internal endpoint. The built-in test payload reaches us successfully, but actual live events from ticket updates never show up. I've been troubleshooting our firewall and don't see anything blocked, so I need help figuring out why test delivery works but production events do not.

A successful test means the endpoint is reachable, so the next place to check is whether the webhook is actually subscribed to the event types you expect and whether the rule is enabled for the right workspace objects. Please open the webhook configuration and confirm ticket.updated is selected, not just test or ticket.created. Also check whether any filter condition limits it to a specific queue or status.
You found it — it was only subscribed to ticket.created, and we were expecting updates and comments. I changed it to include ticket.updated and ticket.commented.

Great, that would explain the mismatch between test success and missing live traffic. You should now receive future matching events, but events missed while the subscription was incomplete will not be replayed automatically. If you want, trigger a small ticket update now to verify end-to-end delivery.

subject: After MFA reset, API calls from our script started failing
description: I had my MFA reset by our IT team because I got a new phone. After that, I was able to log back into the web app, but our Python script that uses my API token began getting 401 responses. I didn't intentionally change the token. Is the MFA reset related, or is this a separate API problem?

comments.message: The script uses a token I created a few months ago under my admin account. Browser login is fine now after reenrolling MFA, but the API rejects every request.
MFA changes do not usually affect API tokens. Since you can log in, I would expect the token to still work. Please verify your script isn't sending the wrong header format.
