We didn't change the code, and the timing lines up exactly with the MFA reset.

I reviewed the audit log more closely and I was mistaken earlier. Your org has a policy to revoke all active API tokens when a user's authentication factors are reset, which is why your web login recovered but the script did not. Please generate a new token under your account and update the script. If you'd rather avoid future user-access events breaking the integration, move it to a dedicated service account.

subject: Shopify-Bestellungen werden nicht mehr importiert
description: Seit zwei Tagen zieht Ihre Shopify-Integration keine neuen Bestellungen mehr in unser System. Alte Bestellungen sind noch sichtbar, aber alles ab gestern fehlt. Wir haben keine Änderungen vorgenommen und der Shop läuft normal weiter. Können Sie prüfen, ob das an der API-Verbindung liegt?

comments.message: In Shopify selbst sehen wir die Bestellungen ganz normal. In Ihrer Plattform endet der letzte Import vorgestern um 23:14 Uhr.
Danke. Ich sehe in solchen Fällen oft, dass das API-Token der Shopify-Integration abgelaufen ist oder dass ein Scope fehlt. Bitte trennen Sie die Verbindung einmal und verbinden Sie den Shop neu.
Das würde ich ungern sofort tun, weil wir dann vielleicht historische Zuordnungen verlieren. Können Sie sehen, ob es konkrete Fehler beim Import gibt?

Ich kann hier leider keine detaillierten Import-Logs einsehen. Ich habe den Fall an unser Integrations-Team weitergegeben, damit die Shopify-Webhook- und API-Fehler für Ihren Account geprüft werden. Bitte lassen Sie die Verbindung vorerst bestehen; sobald wir eine Rückmeldung haben, melden wir uns wieder.

subject: Google Calendar integration only syncs one direction
description: I connected our team calendar so events from your app should appear in Google Calendar, and external updates in Google should sync back. Right now events created in your app show up in Google, but changes made in Google Calendar never update in your platform. I'm trying to understand whether this is a known limitation or a sync issue we can fix.

comments.message: For example, if I move a customer onboarding call by 30 minutes in Google Calendar, your app still shows the old time even after manual sync. This started after I reconnected the integration last Friday.

Our Google Calendar integration supports two-way sync, so that behavior is not expected. When you reconnected it, did you grant the app full calendar read/write access, or only event creation permission? If incremental sync tokens were invalidated, reconnecting can restore outbound sync but break inbound updates until the subscription is recreated.
I granted whatever Google prompted for, but I don't remember the exact wording. Is there somewhere I can see whether the subscription was recreated?

Not currently in the UI. The fastest test is to fully disconnect the Google Calendar integration, reconnect it, and then create a brand-new event and edit it from Google to see whether the change comes back. If that still fails, send us the affected calendar email address and approximate edit times so we can check whether the inbound webhook subscription was re-registered.

subject: Changed plan owner, but invoice still going to former employee
description: Our finance manager left the company, so I updated the account owner and billing contact to myself two days ago. The renewal invoice still got emailed to her old address, and our card was charged before we could swap payment methods. I need the account contact fixed and want to know whether that charge can be reversed and re-run on the new card.

comments.message: Our finance manager left the company, so I updated the account owner and billing contact to myself two days ago. The renewal invoice still got emailed to her old address, and our card was charged before we could swap payment methods. I need the account contact fixed and want to know whether that charge can be reversed and re-run on the new card.

Thanks for reaching out. I updated the primary account owner field, so future notifications should go to you. For the charge, we normally can't reverse successful renewals once processed. You can add the new card under Billing Settings for future invoices.
I already changed both the owner and billing contact before renewal, so I'm trying to understand why the old address still received it. Also, I need a receipt with my name on it for reimbursement.

It may have used cached contact information from before the update. I can't change the name on a finalized receipt from chat support, but I recommend downloading the invoice PDF and adding a note for your finance team. The contact on file now appears to be your profile.

subject: Trying to split one workspace into two business units
description: Our company now has two business units, and leadership wants one of them to operate under a separate admin structure. I need guidance on whether we can split our current workspace, move a subset of users to a new account, and assign a different account owner for that unit. I also need to understand what data and settings would not carry over automatically.

comments.message: Can we carve out one department into its own workspace and give their director control, or do we need a brand new account from scratch?
You can probably just rename one of your teams and assign a new admin. That should be the same as splitting it.
No, they need separate administration, separate branding, and eventually separate renewals. That doesn't sound like the same thing.

Understood, and you're right. A separate business unit with independent administration usually means a new workspace or account rather than a team rename. What can be moved varies: users can often be re-invited, branding can be recreated, and some configuration may need to be rebuilt manually; historical data, automations, IDs, or reporting often do not migrate cleanly by default. Because you're asking about account restructuring, ownership assignment, and potential data separation, this needs a scoped review. I'm escalating to our account and migrations team so they can confirm whether your plan supports a split and what the safest transition path would be.

subject: New hires can access the app URL but see a blank page after login
description: We onboarded six new hires this morning. They can all authenticate with Google SSO and the URL changes to our workspace, but then the screen stays blank with a spinner forever. Existing employees can log in fine. We need to know whether this is a provisioning issue or a bug with first-time login for new users, and if there’s anything we should do differently when adding people.

comments.message: All six were bulk-invited from the People page yesterday and assigned the Analyst role. Browser console shows a failed request to /bootstrap with a 500 error for one of them.
A 500 on /bootstrap usually indicates a browser extension or cached asset problem. Please ask the users to disable extensions and clear cache before trying again.
We tried incognito with no extensions on two of the accounts and they still get the same spinner. Older users in the same browser profile are fine.

Got it. Since it only affects first-time Analyst users and you captured a server-side 500 on bootstrap, this points to a backend bug in the initial workspace setup for that role rather than a local browser issue. As a workaround, edit one affected user and temporarily change their role to Editor, have them log in once, then switch them back if needed. I’ve also escalated the bootstrap failure to engineering with the role-specific pattern you found. For future onboarding today, avoid bulk-creating Analyst users until we confirm the fix.

subject: Bedingte Verzweigung ignoriert leere Felder im Renewal-Workflow
description: In unserem Renewal-Workflow sollen Accounts ohne hinterlegten Ansprechpartner an das Customer-Success-Team gehen. Stattdessen laufen sie in den Standardpfad, als ob ein Kontakt vorhanden wäre. Das passiert vor allem bei älteren Datensätzen. Ich verstehe nicht, warum die Bedingung für leere Felder nicht greift.

comments.message: Die Verzweigung lautet sinngemäß "wenn Ansprechpartner leer ist, weise CSM zu". Bei alten Accounts ohne sichtbaren Kontakt geht der Workflow trotzdem auf den normalen Pfad.

Möglicherweise sind die Felder nicht wirklich leer, sondern enthalten Altwerte wie Leerzeichen oder einen verknüpften, inzwischen archivierten Datensatz. Bitte öffnen Sie einen betroffenen Account und prüfen Sie in der Rohansicht, ob das Feld null ist oder noch eine ID enthält.
In der Rohansicht sehe ich tatsächlich bei einem alten Account noch eine archivierte Kontakt-ID.

Dann bewertet die Automatisierung das Feld korrekt als nicht leer, auch wenn im UI kein aktiver Ansprechpartner sichtbar ist. Sie können den Workflow anpassen, damit archivierte Kontakte separat behandelt werden, oder per Massenupdate die alten Referenzen bereinigen. Wenn Sie möchten, schicke ich Ihnen die Filterlogik für "Kontakt ist leer oder archiviert" an unser Success-Team zur Ausarbeitung.

subject: Cobro duplicado y acceso bloqueado al panel
description: Veo dos cargos este mes por la misma suscripción Pro y, además, desde ayer el panel se queda cargando en blanco después de iniciar sesión. Ya probé en Chrome y Edge y sigue igual. Necesito que revisen tanto la facturación como el problema técnico porque mi equipo no puede trabajar.

comments.message: Veo dos cargos este mes por la misma suscripción Pro y, además, desde ayer el panel se queda cargando en blanco después de iniciar sesión. Ya probé en Chrome y Edge y sigue igual. Necesito que revisen tanto la facturación como el problema técnico porque mi equipo no puede trabajar.

Gracias por reportarlo. Revisé la cuenta asociada a admin@novared.es y efectivamente hay un cobro duplicado del 3 de mayo: uno automático y otro generado al actualizar la tarjeta. Ya inicié el reembolso del cargo extra; debería verse en 5 a 10 días hábiles.

Sobre la pantalla en blanco, encontré errores por caché de la app web después de la última versión. Les pedí cerrar sesión, abrir una ventana incógnita y entrar con este enlace de limpieza de sesión. Si prefieren, también puedo invalidar todas las sesiones activas.
Entré en incógnito y ya carga. ¿Pueden invalidar las sesiones de todos modos? Tenemos 12 usuarios y varios seguían con el fallo.

Listo, acabo de invalidar las sesiones de los 12 usuarios del workspace. Ya deberían poder entrar normalmente. El reembolso quedó confirmado con el ID RF-18427. Si mañana alguno sigue viendo la pantalla en blanco, envíenme una captura de la consola del navegador y lo reviso.

subject: Bulk tag removal only updated half the selected records
description: I selected 1,246 customer records and removed the tag 'legacy-webinar' in bulk. The job showed as completed, but when I filter by that tag today, 612 records still have it. I need help determining whether the bulk update silently failed on part of the batch and whether rerunning it could create more inconsistent data.

comments.message: I selected 1,246 customer records and removed the tag 'legacy-webinar' in bulk. The job showed as completed, but when I filter by that tag today, 612 records still have it. I need help determining whether the bulk update silently failed on part of the batch and whether rerunning it could create more inconsistent data.
Bulk updates can take some time to propagate. Please wait 24 hours and check again.
It has already been over 24 hours. Also, the activity log shows one bulk action entry, but the remaining records were never touched. That makes it look like the job stopped mid-batch.

Understood. I should have asked that first. Based on what you described, it does sound like the batch may have processed only part of the selected IDs. Please send the bulk job ID if you have it, plus 5 record IDs that kept the tag, and I’ll verify whether the worker hit an error threshold or skipped a shard. Do not rerun the action yet if you need a clean audit trail.

subject: Auto-upgrade to annual invoicing after enabling advanced SLA settings
description: I was adjusting our SLA configuration and business hours, and around the same time we accepted an in-app prompt for 'better pricing.' Our latest invoice is now annual instead of monthly, which our finance team did not approve. I'm trying to understand whether changing those advanced support settings triggered a billing plan change or whether this was a separate subscription action.

comments.message: We enabled multi-policy SLA rules and custom schedules, then shortly after got a yearly invoice. The timing makes it look connected.

Advanced SLA settings are part of product configuration and wouldn't by themselves switch billing cadence. The annual invoice likely came from accepting a subscription quote in the billing prompt, though I understand why it looked tied to the feature setup.
No one on my team realized that prompt would finalize an annual commitment. Can we revert to monthly?

I can see an accepted annual order tied to an admin login on your account, but reversals for committed term changes need approval from our contracts team. I've escalated the case with the screenshot of the acceptance event and noted your dispute. Please don't pay the invoice until the review is complete if your finance process allows that.

subject: Admin can't reset password for a user in another region
description: I'm one of the admins for our EMEA team and I tried to help a colleague in APAC who is locked out. I can see her name in the directory but the option to resend invite or reset login isn't available to me. She definitely has an account, and I expected admins to be able to manage any user. Can you explain why I can't do this and what level of admin access controls cross-region user management?

comments.message: I'm one of the admins for our EMEA team and I tried to help a colleague in APAC who is locked out. I can see her name in the directory but the option to resend invite or reset login isn't available to me. She definitely has an account, and I expected admins to be able to manage any user. Can you explain why I can't do this and what level of admin access controls cross-region user management?

If you can see the user but not manage login actions, you're probably a scoped admin rather than a global user admin. Scoped admins can manage users only within their assigned teams or regions. Cross-region actions like password resets or invite resends usually require either Global User Admin or Workspace Admin, depending on your plan's admin model. You can confirm your scope under Settings > My Role or by checking the Admin Roles page.
That makes sense. We want regional admins to handle only their own users, but still have a backup process when someone is out. What's the best practice?

Best practice is to assign at least two scoped admins per region and one central Global User Admin for overflow cases. Document which teams or regions each admin controls, and test quarterly that each backup admin can actually reset access and deactivate users in their scope. If you'd like, I can share a sample regional admin matrix your team can adapt.

subject: Microsoft Teams bot posts to wrong channel after tenant migration
description: Since our Microsoft 365 tenant migration, your Teams integration is posting workflow notifications into an old channel instead of the new one we selected. The integration page shows the correct team and channel names, but the messages keep landing in the archived project space. Can you help me troubleshoot how the mapping got stuck?

comments.message: We selected the replacement channel last Friday and the save action completed without errors. The old channel was renamed before being archived, which might matter.
It might matter, but channel names are usually enough for routing. Please delete the archived channel from Teams if you don't want posts going there.
We can't delete it yet for compliance reasons, and the UI already shows the new channel selected. That sounds more like an ID mapping issue than a naming problem.

You're probably right. Teams routing uses the channel ID under the hood, and after tenant migrations or channel renames we sometimes retain the stale ID even when the label updates in the UI. I've opened an escalation for our integrations team to refresh the stored Team/Channel IDs on this connector. Until they do that, changing the visible selection alone may not redirect messages correctly.

subject: Need to transfer workspace ownership after admin left
description: I’m the remaining admin for our workspace, and our previous owner left the company last week. I can still manage users, but I can’t change the billing contact or some security settings because they’re locked to the owner role. We need ownership moved to me today so I can finish onboarding two new managers and deactivate the former owner’s account.

comments.message: Our workspace owner, Daniel Reeves, is no longer with the company. I’m listed as an admin, but not the owner. I need ownership transferred to my account and I also need to remove Daniel from the workspace without breaking our current user groups.

I can help with that. Since this is an ownership change, I need to verify the request before updating account roles. Please reply from your company domain with the workspace URL, Daniel’s email, your email, and confirmation from an executive or billing contact approving the transfer.

I’m already writing from my company email. Workspace URL is northstar-ops.acmeapp.com, Daniel’s email was dreaves@northstar.io, and mine is lchen@northstar.io. Our finance director, Maya Patel, is copied and approves the transfer. Once that’s done, please deactivate Daniel and keep his direct reports assigned to the same teams.

Thanks, I’ve verified the domain and approval. I’ve transferred workspace ownership to lchen@northstar.io, deactivated dreaves@northstar.io, and preserved all existing team memberships and reporting assignments for the affected users. You should now be able to update billing contact and security settings from Admin Console > Workspace Settings.

subject: Automation disabled after account suspension and I still can't access admin settings
description: Our card failed over the weekend, the account was briefly suspended, and after payment went through I can log in again but only as a regular agent. I used to manage the ticket routing workflows and now the round-robin automation is marked inactive. I need my admin access restored, but if this needs another team please route it quickly because support volume is piling up.

comments.message: Our card failed over the weekend, the account was briefly suspended, and after payment went through I can log in again but only as a regular agent. I used to manage the ticket routing workflows and now the round-robin automation is marked inactive. I need my admin access restored, but if this needs another team please route it quickly because support volume is piling up.

Thanks for reporting this. I can see the subscription was reactivated today, but I don’t have permission to restore account roles after a suspension event. I also see the round-robin workflow is inactive, though I’m not able to tell from my tools whether it was disabled during suspension or manually edited. I’m escalating this to our billing and account recovery specialists to restore your admin access and review the automation state.
Can you at least turn the workflow back on while they review the role issue? Tickets are all going to the default queue.

I’m sorry, but I can’t modify inactive workflows unless I’m acting with an active admin on the account, and your admin role restoration is still pending. I’ve marked the escalation as urgent and included the impact on routing so the specialist team can prioritize it.

subject: Why are suppressed contacts counted in our billable audience?
description: We cleaned our database and marked about 18,000 contacts as suppressed because we cannot legally email them anymore. Even though they are not sendable, our audience-based bill still reflects the full count. I need an explanation of which contact states are billable and guidance on how to manage compliance data without paying for records we should not message.

comments.message: We need to retain suppression history for compliance, but we also need to cut costs. Do suppressed, bounced, and unsubscribed records all count the same for billing?

On your current plan, billable audience includes all stored contact profiles except permanently deleted records. Suppressed, bounced, unsubscribed, and archived contacts remain billable because they occupy profile storage and compliance history. The only contact state that removes them from audience billing is deletion, or moving them to an external suppression list outside the profile store.
We can't just delete them because we need proof of consent withdrawal. Is there a recommended workflow?

Yes. Many customers export the minimum required compliance fields—email, consent status, timestamp, source, and legal basis reference—to an external archive, then delete the full marketing profile from the platform. Another option on our enterprise plan is the compliance ledger add-on, which stores suppression evidence separately from billable audience profiles. I can't promise a retroactive credit, but I've asked Billing to review whether your recent cleanup effort qualifies for one.

subject: Modification du contact principal refusée sans explication
description: Je souhaite remplacer le contact principal de notre abonnement par notre directrice administrative, mais lorsque j’enregistre le changement la page revient simplement à l’état précédent sans message d’erreur. J’ai testé sur plusieurs postes. J’aimerais savoir s’il y a une restriction liée aux rôles ou si quelque chose est bloqué sur notre compte.

comments.message: Bonjour, le changement de contact principal ne se sauvegarde pas sur notre compte. Je sélectionne la nouvelle personne, je clique sur enregistrer, puis rien n’est pris en compte.

Bonjour, cela peut arriver si la personne choisie n’a pas encore accepté son invitation ou si elle n’a pas un rôle administrateur compatible. Pouvez-vous me donner l’adresse e-mail du contact actuel et celle du nouveau contact principal, ainsi que confirmer si le nouveau contact est bien utilisateur actif ?
Contact actuel : achats@meridia-sa.fr. Nouveau contact : delphine.ragot@meridia-sa.fr. Oui, elle est active et a déjà le rôle administratrice de facturation.

Merci. Je vois effectivement un échec silencieux sur votre compte lors de la mise à jour du contact principal. Ce n’est pas lié à ses droits. J’ai ouvert un ticket technique avec nos ingénieurs et appliqué un contournement temporaire pour définir delphine.ragot@meridia-sa.fr comme contact de facturation secondaire. Dès que l’équipe corrige le blocage, nous finaliserons le remplacement du contact principal.

subject: Need refund after duplicate user accounts inflated our seat count
description: Our HR team imported users twice, which created duplicate accounts for several employees with slightly different email aliases. I merged or removed the duplicates in user administration, but we were already billed for 11 extra seats this month. Can those charges be reversed?

comments.message: Examples are john.doe@company.com and jdoe@company.com. The duplicates were active for about five days before we caught them. I’ve deleted the extra accounts now, but invoice preview still includes 11 additional seats.

I reviewed the audit trail and can confirm the duplicate users were created through two separate CSV imports. Because your plan bills on the highest active seat count reached during the cycle, the seat spike is reflected correctly on the invoice.
So even obvious duplicates can’t be adjusted?

Support can’t automatically waive usage-based charges, but I do think this is a reasonable refund request. I’ve sent the billing team the user IDs removed in the merge and asked them to review an exception for the 11 excess seats. No guarantee, but they’ll reply by email.

subject: Criação em massa de usuários falhou e o relatório ficou duplicado
description: Fiz uma importação em massa de usuários por CSV ontem à noite. Alguns usuários foram criados duas vezes com e-mails alternativos e agora o relatório de adoção mostra 128 contas, quando deveriam ser 121. Eu já desativei os duplicados, mas o relatório continua contando todos. Como corrigir isso sem perder o histórico dos usuários válidos?

comments.message: Os duplicados foram gerados porque a planilha tinha uma coluna de e-mail secundário e o importador aparentemente aceitou como novo login. Hoje cedo desativei 7 contas repetidas.

Olá! Obrigado pelo contexto. Quando usuários duplicados são desativados depois de terem sido sincronizados com o relatório de adoção, a contagem total pode continuar considerando registros históricos até a próxima recomputação.
Existe alguma forma de forçar essa recomputação? Preciso do número correto para apresentar amanhã.

Sim. Vá em Relatórios > Adoção > Configurações e clique em 'Reconstruir métricas'. Isso recalcula usuários ativos e exclui desativados da contagem atual, mantendo o histórico de eventos. O processo pode levar até 2 horas. Se quiser uma solução mais rápida para amanhã, filtre a métrica por 'Status do usuário = Ativo' no período atual.

subject: Facturation en double sur notre renouvellement annuel
description: Je constate deux prélèvements pour le même renouvellement annuel sur notre carte société. Je n’ai pas la main pour corriger cela depuis le portail, et comme la clôture comptable est cette semaine, j’ai besoin soit d’un remboursement soit d’un avoir immédiatement. Merci de traiter cela rapidement.

comments.message: Bonjour, nous avons été débités deux fois de 4 800 € le 3 de ce mois pour le même abonnement. J’ai comparé les numéros de facture et je ne vois qu’un seul renouvellement légitime dans l’interface.

Bonjour, je suis désolé pour ce désagrément. Je peux confirmer qu’il y a bien une anomalie potentielle entre le paiement capturé et la facture affichée, mais les remboursements ne peuvent pas être déclenchés depuis le support général. J’escalade immédiatement votre dossier à notre équipe facturation avec priorité élevée et j’ajoute votre contrainte de clôture comptable cette semaine.
Merci. Pouvez-vous aussi indiquer qu’un justificatif provisoire nous aiderait pour la comptabilité aujourd’hui ?

Oui, je viens de l’ajouter dans l’escalade. En attendant leur validation, je vous ai envoyé par e-mail un récapitulatif du ticket mentionnant la suspicion de double débit et la demande de document provisoire ; notre équipe facturation reviendra vers vous directement sur ce dossier.

subject: Free trial converted to paid while my admin account was locked
description: Our team was testing the product on a free trial and it converted to a paid subscription over the weekend. I would have canceled it, but my admin account got locked after too many failed login attempts and the unlock emails never came through. We have not actually used the account beyond setup, and I need both access to the account and help reversing the charge if possible.

comments.message: Our team was testing the product on a free trial and it converted to a paid subscription over the weekend. I would have canceled it, but my admin account got locked after too many failed login attempts and the unlock emails never came through. We have not actually used the account beyond setup, and I need both access to the account and help reversing the charge if possible.

Trial conversions are automatic unless canceled before the end date. I’ve manually unlocked your admin account and resent the verification email. If you can now sign in and cancel, that will prevent future charges. I can’t guarantee a refund for the first paid month.
I’m in now and have canceled, but I still need to know whether this first charge can be refunded since the account was inaccessible when the trial ended.

Given the lockout and minimal usage, I submitted a refund review to our billing queue and attached the login failure evidence from your account history. You’ll get a final decision by email within 2 business days. Access is restored, but the billing outcome is still under review.

subject: Cannot update primary admin because the old owner is unreachable
description: Our original account owner left over a year ago, and no one on the team can log in as them anymore. We still have admin access to the workspace, but certain account changes are blocked because the platform says only the owner can complete them. We need to update ownership and understand what verification you need from us.

comments.message: We're stuck because our admins can manage users and content, but not the account-level settings we need to change. How do we reclaim owner access when the old owner is gone?
You should be able to reset the old owner's password and use that account to make the changes.
We don't have access to that person's email inbox, and that doesn't sound like a compliant process anyway.

You're absolutely right; we can't advise accessing a former employee's account that way. When the owner is unreachable, ownership recovery typically requires verification from current authorized admins and/or an officer at your company, often using your company domain and account details. Since this is a sensitive account-change request, I'm escalating it to our account security team. Please reply with your workspace URL, your role, the name of the proposed new owner, and a secondary company-domain contact who can verify the request. They will review the ownership transfer options and any documentation needed.

subject: Manager role can edit reports but shouldn’t manage users
description: I inherited a messy permissions setup and I’m trying to separate reporting access from user management. Our regional managers need to build and edit their own reports, but they definitely should not be able to invite users or change anyone’s account settings. I’m not sure how to configure that correctly, and I need a straightforward explanation for our operations director before I make changes.

comments.message: Right now the Regional Manager role has both report editing and user admin privileges. If I clone the role and turn off user management, will they still be able to save dashboards and schedule reports for their teams?

Yes, as long as the cloned role keeps reporting permissions like create/edit reports and manage dashboard subscriptions, removing user administration permissions won’t affect their ability to work in reporting. I’d recommend cloning the role, removing invite/edit/deactivate user privileges, and testing with one pilot user before rolling it out.
Do I need to move them into a different group too, or is role change enough?

Role change handles the admin capabilities, but group membership may still affect which report folders and dashboards they can access. So if their content access should stay the same, keep the same groups; if not, adjust both. For your director: roles govern what managers can do, while groups control which reporting content they can see.

subject: Jira integration failing after annual renewal and invited users denied access
description: We renewed annually this week and something is off. The invoice amount is higher than our quote, our Jira integration started failing on project lookups, and three newly invited users get “access denied” immediately after setting their passwords. We use role-based provisioning but these were standard licenses, so I’m not sure why billing, integration permissions, and login access all broke at once.

comments.message: We renewed annually this week and something is off. The invoice amount is higher than our quote, our Jira integration started failing on project lookups, and three newly invited users get “access denied” immediately after setting their passwords. We use role-based provisioning but these were standard licenses, so I’m not sure why billing, integration permissions, and login access all broke at once.

Thanks for the details. I found that your renewal moved the workspace onto the new annual packaging, which changed both seat entitlements and integration scopes. The invoice overage appears to be from an automatic true-up added during renewal, though I agree it should be compared to your quote. The Jira app is failing because the service account lost the project-admin scope during the package migration, and the invited users are landing in a pending-access state because there are no unassigned paid seats left under the new limit. I’ve added three temporary seats so those users can log in now and opened an internal request to review the renewal billing and restore the Jira scope mapping.
The new users can log in now, but Jira is still failing and our admin page shows 6 temporary seats, not 3.

I mistakenly applied the seat patch twice, so I’ve corrected that back to 3 and noted the error on the billing review so you aren’t charged for the extra temporary seats. The Jira scope issue still requires our integrations team because the OAuth grant has to be regenerated on the backend after the renewal migration. I’ve escalated it with high priority.

subject: Bulk deactivation request after team restructure
description: We just merged two departments and need to deactivate 14 former contractors by end of day, but we also have to reassign three of them as temporary user managers on a different team before their old accounts are closed. I’m not sure whether to do this in two passes or if support can handle the account and admin changes together without losing audit history.

comments.message: I have a CSV ready with the 14 contractor accounts to deactivate. Before that happens, three of those users need to be moved under our Vendor Ops team and given user-admin rights for only that team. We need to keep their login history for compliance.

Yes, we can guide the sequence. First, do not deactivate the three users who still need access. Upload the CSV for the remaining 11 deactivations under Admin > Users > Bulk Actions. Then manually move the three active users to Vendor Ops and assign the Team User Admin role. Deactivation will preserve audit history for all affected accounts.
That helps. If I deactivate the other 11 first, will the three reassigned users keep their existing SSO mapping, or do I need to recreate them after moving teams?

Their SSO mapping will remain intact as long as those three accounts stay active. Team reassignment and changing them to Team User Admin won’t require recreating the accounts. If you want, reply with the three emails and I can confirm the role scope before you make the change.

subject: Importación duplicó contactos y cambió campos personalizados
description: Subí un CSV con unos 4.200 contactos y después de la importación aparecieron registros duplicados y varios campos personalizados quedaron cruzados. Por ejemplo, el campo "Región" ahora muestra valores del campo "Segmento" en cientos de filas. Intenté revertirlo desde el historial, pero no veo una opción para deshacer esa carga masiva. Necesito saber cómo corregir esto sin perder las notas y actividades que ya tenían esos contactos.

comments.message: Subí un CSV con unos 4.200 contactos y después de la importación aparecieron registros duplicados y varios campos personalizados quedaron cruzados. Por ejemplo, el campo "Región" ahora muestra valores del campo "Segmento" en cientos de filas. Intenté revertirlo desde el historial, pero no veo una opción para deshacer esa carga masiva. Necesito saber cómo corregir esto sin perder las notas y actividades que ya tenían esos contactos.

Gracias por el detalle. Esto suele pasar cuando en la importación no se usa un identificador único para actualizar registros existentes y cuando el mapeo de columnas cambia entre campos personalizados. No hay un botón general para deshacer una importación completa si ya se aplicó, pero sí podemos minimizar el impacto. Primero, exporte los contactos afectados filtrando por fecha de creación de la importación. Luego, revise si tiene un campo único como email o ID externo para consolidar duplicados con una importación de actualización. Para los campos cruzados, puede hacer otra importación corrigiendo el mapeo y sobrescribiendo solo "Región" y "Segmento".

El problema es que también se duplicaron empresas asociadas y algunos contactos nuevos heredaron actividades del registro equivocado. Si hago otra importación, me preocupa empeorarlo. ¿Hay forma de restaurar solo los objetos creados ayer?

En ese caso no le recomiendo seguir importando hasta revisar la integridad de las relaciones. He escalado su caso a nuestro equipo de gestión de datos para validar si pueden revertir parcialmente los registros creados en la ventana de ayer sin afectar notas ni actividades históricas. Mientras tanto, no edite los contactos afectados. Si me comparte el ID de la importación o un archivo con 10 ejemplos de registros cruzados, lo adjunto a la escalación.

subject: Modification de l’e-mail principal impossible et historique des rapports tronqué
description: Je dois remplacer l’adresse e-mail principale associée à notre compte car elle pointe vers une boîte qui n’existe plus. En essayant de le faire, j’obtiens un message indiquant que l’adresse est utilisée par un utilisateur archivé. De plus, lorsque j’ouvre les rapports sur 12 mois, l’historique s’arrête à février alors que nous avons des données bien avant. Cela nous bloque pour l’audit interne.

comments.message: Bonjour, impossible de mettre à jour l’e-mail principal du compte : le système dit que l’adresse souhaitée existe déjà, mais je pense qu’elle appartient à un ancien utilisateur archivé. En plus, nos rapports annuels ne montrent que les données depuis février.

Bonjour, si l’adresse est déjà liée à un utilisateur archivé, il faut d’abord la libérer en anonymisant ou renommant cet utilisateur, ce que je peux demander. Pour l’historique tronqué, pouvez-vous me dire si cela concerne tous les tableaux de bord ou seulement un rapport spécifique ?
Principalement le tableau de bord de performance globale, mais même l’export annuel s’arrête au même mois. Nous n’avons pas changé de plan récemment.

Parfait, j’ai soumis la libération de l’adresse e-mail afin que vous puissiez la définir comme contact principal du compte. Pour les rapports, je vois que votre politique de rétention n’a pas changé ; en revanche, l’espace a été migré vers le nouveau moteur analytique en mars et l’index historique semble incomplet. J’ouvre une reconstruction des données antérieures à février. Ce n’est pas immédiat, mais vous recevrez une mise à jour dès que le backfill sera terminé.

subject: Can I give a temporary admin access without changing our whole setup?
description: Our operations manager is covering for me next week and needs to be able to add users and reset access if someone gets locked out, but I don't want to leave her with permanent admin rights afterward. I'm not seeing an obvious temporary role option. Is there a safe way to do this, and what exactly would she be able to do while she has that access?

comments.message: Our operations manager is covering for me next week and needs to be able to add users and reset access if someone gets locked out, but I don't want to leave her with permanent admin rights afterward. I'm not seeing an obvious temporary role option. Is there a safe way to do this, and what exactly would she be able to do while she has that access?

Yes — the safest approach is to promote her to Workspace Admin now and then manually downgrade her later. Workspace Admins can add users, reset access, update authentication settings, and make most administrative changes.
That seems broader than I want. I definitely don't want her changing authentication settings or billing while I'm away.

Understood. A better fit would be Team Admin if her work is limited to user access for specific teams, or User Admin if your plan includes granular admin roles. User Admin can invite users, deactivate accounts, resend invites, and reset login access without billing permissions. Since there isn't a built-in expiration timer on role assignments, set a calendar reminder to revert the role after your leave. If you'd like, I can also outline a least-privilege setup based on whether she needs to manage all users or just one department.

subject: Changement d’administrateur et rapports mensuels manquants
description: Je suis la nouvelle responsable opérations chez notre société et j’ai repris le compte après le départ de l’ancien administrateur. J’ai bien été ajoutée comme utilisatrice, mais je n’arrive pas à me définir comme administratrice principale ni à modifier l’adresse e-mail de facturation. En plus, dans la section Rapports, les rapports mensuels d’activité sont vides depuis le début du mois alors que nos équipes utilisent bien la plateforme tous les jours. J’ai besoin de récupérer l’accès administrateur complet et de comprendre pourquoi les données de reporting ne remontent plus.

comments.message: Bonjour, je remplace notre ancien admin qui a quitté l’entreprise vendredi. Je peux me connecter, mais je n’ai pas les droits pour changer l’administrateur principal ni mettre à jour l’e-mail de contact du compte. Et depuis le 1er du mois, notre rapport mensuel affiche 0 activité.

Bonjour, merci pour ces précisions. Pour le changement d’administrateur principal, il faut qu’un admin existant fasse le transfert depuis Paramètres > Équipe. Concernant les rapports vides, cela arrive souvent quand le fuseau horaire du workspace a été modifié. Pouvez-vous vérifier ce point ?
Justement, l’ancien admin n’est plus joignable, donc je ne peux pas faire le transfert de cette manière. Et je n’ai pas accès aux paramètres avancés du workspace pour vérifier le fuseau.

Compris. Dans ce cas, je vais lancer une vérification manuelle de propriété du compte pour vous promouvoir administratrice principale. Merci de répondre à ce ticket avec votre domaine d’entreprise et une facture récente ou le numéro de contrat. Pour les rapports, je vois un changement de source de données sur votre espace le 2 de ce mois ; je transmets aussi au support reporting car cela peut expliquer les graphiques à 0. Je vous reviens dès que l’escalade est prise en charge.

subject: New branch users cannot trigger branch-specific onboarding automation
description: We launched a new branch and imported users into a new organizational unit. Their profiles look correct, but our onboarding workflow that creates branch-specific checklists and assigns local admins never runs for them. Existing branches still work. I need help checking the org-unit setup, user assignments, and workflow conditions.

comments.message: The workflow condition says 'if branch is one of North, South, East, West' then create the local onboarding tasks and assign the branch admin. We added 'Central' as a new branch in User Administration yesterday, but hires there get only the generic checklist.

This is likely because the product configuration for the workflow still references only the original four branch values. Adding Central in User Administration makes it available on user profiles, but it does not automatically update existing workflow conditions or branch-admin lookup tables. Did you also add Central to the branch-specific condition and map a Central admin user?
I added Central to the user field options, but I didn't realize the automation had its own lookup table. And no, there isn't a Central admin mapped yet.

That would prevent the branch-specific path from running. Please update the workflow condition to include Central and add a valid Central branch admin in the routing table. Once both the user attribute and automation mapping exist, new Central users should receive the correct onboarding tasks. If you'd like, I can send the exact navigation steps for both screens.

subject: Exportação CSV está cortando caracteres acentuados
description: Eu exportei nossa base de contatos para CSV para tratar dados fora da plataforma, mas nomes com acento como 'João', 'Márcia' e 'Gonçalves' saem corrompidos quando abro o arquivo. Além disso, a coluna de observações quebra em várias linhas por causa de quebras de texto. Preciso de uma exportação utilizável para limpeza e reimportação. Como faço isso sem perder caracteres?

comments.message: Eu exportei nossa base de contatos para CSV para tratar dados fora da plataforma, mas nomes com acento como 'João', 'Márcia' e 'Gonçalves' saem corrompidos quando abro o arquivo. Além disso, a coluna de observações quebra em várias linhas por causa de quebras de texto.

Isso costuma acontecer por causa da codificação do arquivo e de como o Excel abre CSV por padrão. Exporte novamente em UTF-8, se essa opção estiver disponível, e importe o arquivo no Excel via 'Dados > De Texto/CSV' escolhendo origem UTF-8 em vez de dar duplo clique no arquivo. As quebras de linha nas observações devem vir entre aspas no CSV; isso é esperado e não significa corrupção dos dados.
Eu não vejo opção de UTF-8 na tela de exportação, só CSV padrão.

Nesse caso, o sistema provavelmente já gera o CSV em UTF-8, mas o problema é a abertura direta no Excel. Abra-o pelo assistente de importação do Excel ou use Google Sheets/LibreOffice para validar os caracteres. Se você pretende reimportar depois, evite salvar novamente em um formato legado como ANSI. Para a limpeza de dados, também posso sugerir exportar em XLSX se essa opção existir na sua conta, porque lida melhor com acentos e quebras de linha.

subject: Cannot download receipts because my account lost admin rights after seat reduction
description: I reduced our seats this month and expected a lower charge, but the invoice total looks unchanged. When I tried to check the receipt details, I discovered my account no longer has billing permissions even though I used to manage the subscription. I can still log in to the workspace, just not to the billing section. I need the correct invoice and restored access to receipts for month-end close.

comments.message: I reduced our seats this month and expected a lower charge, but the invoice total looks unchanged. When I tried to check the receipt details, I discovered my account no longer has billing permissions even though I used to manage the subscription. I can still log in to the workspace, just not to the billing section. I need the correct invoice and restored access to receipts for month-end close.

Seat reductions only apply at renewal, so the unchanged invoice total may be normal depending on timing. I also see your role was changed from Billing Admin to Workspace Admin during a permissions sync last Friday, which explains the access issue. I can restore billing access if you confirm you should still be the billing contact.
Yes, I’m still the billing contact. We’re closing the books tomorrow, so I need this today if possible.

Understood. I restored your Billing Admin role, and you should be able to access receipts after signing out and back in. On the invoice amount, your reduction was submitted mid-cycle, so pricing won’t change until the next renewal on the 1st. I’ve annotated the account so your finance team has a record of that timing.

subject: Sandbox API returns 500 when creating tickets with custom fields
description: I am testing ticket creation in your sandbox and every request that includes our custom dropdown field returns a 500 Internal Server Error. The same request without that field succeeds. I have already checked that the JSON is valid, so I need help troubleshooting whether the field configuration is broken.

comments.message: The request posts to `/v1/tickets` with `custom_fields: {"region":"emea"}`. Without `custom_fields`, the ticket is created. With it, we get a 500 and request ID `req_91ac4`.
A 500 usually means invalid JSON or an outage. Please confirm your body is well formed and try again in a few minutes.
The body is valid JSON; removing only the custom field makes it work. The sandbox dropdown definitely exists in the UI.

I checked the request ID and your sandbox field `region` still has an old option value mapping. The UI label is 'EMEA', but the API currently expects the internal option key `reg_2`. Passing `"region":"emea"` triggers a server-side bug instead of a validation error. As a workaround, send `"region":"reg_2"`. I've also filed a defect for the 500 response because it should return 422 with the accepted values.

subject: Réattribution du compte après départ du fondateur et KPI divergents
description: Je reprends l’administration de l’outil après le départ de notre fondateur, qui était l’unique propriétaire du compte. J’ai besoin que la propriété soit transférée à mon nom rapidement. En parallèle, les KPI dans le rapport "direction" ne correspondent pas aux chiffres du rapport "opérations" pour la même période, notamment sur les tickets résolus. Merci de nous aider à sécuriser l’accès et à comprendre quelle source utiliser.

comments.message: Bonjour, notre fondateur a quitté la société et son compte est encore le seul propriétaire. Il faut transférer la propriété à mon nom. Nous constatons aussi 512 tickets résolus dans le rapport direction contre 476 dans le rapport opérations pour avril.

Bonjour, je peux vous aider pour les deux sujets. Le transfert de propriété nécessite une vérification de sécurité : merci d’envoyer depuis votre domaine professionnel le numéro de contrat ou une facture récente. Pour l’écart de reporting, ces deux rapports n’utilisent pas forcément la même définition de "résolu" : l’un peut compter la date de fermeture, l’autre la date de première résolution.
D’accord, je joins le numéro de contrat et une facture. Pouvez-vous me dire lequel des deux rapports est recommandé pour notre comité mensuel ?

Merci, la demande de transfert de propriété est bien lancée auprès de notre équipe conformité. Pour votre comité mensuel, utilisez plutôt le rapport opérations si vous voulez mesurer la production réelle sur la période, car il est basé sur la date de résolution effective. Le rapport direction agrège plusieurs statuts de clôture et peut inclure des réouvertures. Je vous ai aussi envoyé l’article expliquant les définitions, en attendant la finalisation du changement de propriétaire.

subject: User email update caused webhook signing confusion
description: We changed the login on the admin account that created our API/webhook integration, and now our team is debating whether the webhook secret and API token need to be rotated too. Events are still arriving, but one developer claims the signature header changed format after the account update. Can you clarify what account changes actually affect developer credentials?

comments.message: We updated the admin email tied to our integration account. API requests with the existing bearer token still work, but we think webhook signatures may have changed. Does changing the account email rotate the webhook secret or token automatically?

No, changing the user's email address alone does not normally rotate either the API token or the webhook signing secret. If events are arriving and signature verification is failing, it's more likely a parsing issue, an endpoint change, or a secret that was manually regenerated. Could you compare a recent delivery against the previously stored secret?
We checked and the signature verifies with the same secret as before. It looks like our dev was reading the wrong header name in a new framework adapter.

Great, then the account change itself wasn't the cause. You shouldn't need to rotate credentials just because the user email changed. I'd still recommend documenting whether your integration depends on a personal admin account; if so, moving it to a dedicated service account later will reduce this kind of risk.

subject: Round-robin assignment automation is overloading one rep
description: I built a workflow to auto-assign inbound leads to our sales reps in round-robin, but one rep is getting almost everything while the others barely receive any. We expected an even split across five people. I need help figuring out whether the workflow logic or queue settings are causing this imbalance.

comments.message: The workflow triggers when a lead is created and then uses the round-robin assignment action for our SDR team. We noticed Mark received 18 of the last 22 leads. All five reps are active users, but two are in a different timezone if that matters.

Timezone usually doesn't affect round-robin. This sounds like some reps may not meet assignment eligibility at the moment the workflow runs. Please check whether vacation status, capacity limits, or role-based availability filters are enabled on the SDR queue. Also, if the workflow has any preceding branch like region or company size before the assignment action, that could be narrowing the pool before round-robin executes.
Capacity limits are enabled, and three reps were already at their cap when most of those leads came in. I hadn't realized the round-robin only considers currently eligible reps.

That explains the skew. The workflow itself is likely functioning as designed: round-robin only rotates across eligible users at execution time. If you want a more even distribution over the week, you could raise the capacity caps, reset them more frequently, or route overflow leads to a holding queue first.

subject: SSO login loop after enforcing domain restriction
description: I turned on Google SSO and restricted access to users from our company domain yesterday. Since then, three teammates can log in, but everyone else gets sent back to the sign-in page after authenticating. I need to know whether I misconfigured something in our workspace settings or if this is a bug.

comments.message: We enabled Google SSO, checked 'require SSO for all users,' and added our domain. Now most users authenticate with Google and then land right back on the login screen. Admins can still get in.

This usually happens when the identity provider is sending an email domain that doesn't exactly match the allowed domain in your authentication settings. Please go to Admin Settings > Security > SSO and confirm the allowed domain list only includes your primary domain, without spaces. Also verify that affected users are assigned to an active team in your workspace, because unassigned users can be redirected back to login.

I found that our domain list had both acme.com and Acme.com entered separately from testing, and the affected users had not been provisioned into teams yet. After fixing both, two users can log in, but one still loops.

Good catch. For the remaining user, ask them to clear cookies for our app and retry, then check whether their Google account email matches the exact email in their user profile. If it still loops after that, send us the user's email and a HAR capture and I'll escalate it, but the workspace configuration issue appears to be mostly resolved.

subject: Usunięty pracownik nadal widnieje jako menedżer zespołu
description: Chcę zmienić menedżera zespołu po odejściu pracownika, ale system nie pozwala przypisać nowej osoby, bo stary użytkownik nadal jest powiązany z kilkoma automatyzacjami. Gdy wchodzę w ustawienia zespołu, lista użytkowników ładuje się bardzo długo, a czasem kończy błędem 500. Potrzebuję wskazówki, jak poprawnie przepiąć rolę i co powoduje ten błąd.

comments.message: Nie mogę zmienić menedżera zespołu, bo były pracownik nadal jest przypisany w tle. W dodatku strona z ustawieniami zespołu raz się ładuje, raz pokazuje błąd 500.

Rozumiem. Proszę podać nazwę zespołu, adres e-mail starego i nowego menedżera oraz godzinę, o której ostatnio pojawił się błąd 500. Czy problem występuje tylko w ustawieniach zespołu, czy też np. przy edycji automatyzacji?
Zespół Sprzedaż PL, stary menedżer: marek@firma.pl, nowy: joanna@firma.pl. Błąd był dziś około 10:14. W automatyzacjach też czasem nie da się otworzyć reguł, które należały do Marka.

Dziękuję, to pomaga. Wygląda na to, że konto byłego pracownika jest nadal właścicielem kilku reguł, co blokuje zmianę menedżera. Po naszej stronie odpinam przypisania właściciela od automatyzacji, a błąd 500 przekazuję do analizy z podanym timestampem. Na teraz proszę spróbować ponownie za około 30 minut; jeśli nadal się nie uda, dokonamy zmiany menedżera ręcznie.
Po 40 minutach udało się otworzyć ustawienia, ale przy zapisie zmiany dostałem komunikat, że „użytkownik nie ma pełnej synchronizacji z katalogiem”.

To wskazuje na opóźnienie synchronizacji katalogu użytkowników. Ręcznie przepisałem menedżera zespołu na joanna@firma.pl, natomiast pełne uporządkowanie starych powiązań i źródła błędu 500 nadal analizujemy. Jeśli zobaczy Pan/Pani jeszcze stare dane w regułach, proszę dać znać.

subject: New hires can log in but onboarding workflow never starts
description: We set up an onboarding automation that should create tasks as soon as a new employee signs in for the first time. The new hires are able to authenticate, but nothing gets triggered, and I don't know if we configured the event correctly. Can someone explain how first-login triggers are supposed to work and why ours isn't firing?

comments.message: Our workflow is supposed to send a welcome task list when a user logs in for the first time. Three new hires signed in today and got into the platform, but no onboarding tasks were created. Did we miss a setting?

First-login automations only fire on the 'user activated' event, not every successful authentication. If the users were pre-provisioned through SCIM or bulk import, their first SSO session may not count as activation because the account was already marked active. In the workflow builder, can you check whether the trigger is 'First successful login' or 'User activated'?
It is set to 'First successful login.' We imported everyone from HRIS before they signed in.

Thanks, that helps. With pre-provisioned users, that trigger can be unreliable depending on the identity flow. A better setup is to trigger on 'User created' or on an HRIS field change, then add a condition for department or start date. For the three users who already signed in, the workflow won't retroactively run, so you would need to launch the onboarding playbook manually for them. If you want, I can share a template for onboarding automations that avoids login-dependent triggers.

subject: Microsoft Teams envía notificaciones, pero con enlaces rotos al registro
description: Activamos la integración con Microsoft Teams para recibir avisos cuando se crea una cuenta nueva, y los mensajes sí llegan al canal correcto. El problema es que el enlace incluido en la tarjeta abre una URL antigua de nuestro entorno sandbox en vez de producción. Yo ya revisé la integración, pero no encuentro dónde cambiar esa base URL ni si depende de una configuración general del producto.

comments.message: Las tarjetas llegan bien a Teams, pero al hacer clic en "Ver registro" me manda a app-sandbox en lugar de nuestra instancia productiva.
Eso normalmente toma la URL base definida en la configuración del workspace. Revise en Configuración general > Dominio de la cuenta si todavía figura el subdominio de sandbox como predeterminado.
Sí, estaba el dominio viejo en una sección avanzada que nadie había visto. Ya lo cambié, pero las tarjetas nuevas siguen saliendo con el enlace viejo.

Gracias por confirmarlo. En ese caso puede haber caché en la app de Teams o una instalación previa de la integración usando la URL antigua. Desinstale y vuelva a instalar la conexión de Teams desde Integraciones, y luego genere un evento de prueba. Si persiste después de reinstalarla, lo pasamos a ingeniería porque ya sería un problema del generador de enlaces.

subject: Merged two workspaces and now paying for both subscriptions
description: We consolidated our marketing workspace into our main company account last Friday and reassigned those users under the primary subscription. However, I still see the legacy workspace listed under my account settings, and we were billed for both subscriptions this morning. Please help close the old account properly and stop the extra billing.

comments.message: We consolidated our marketing workspace into our main company account last Friday and reassigned those users under the primary subscription. However, I still see the legacy workspace listed under my account settings, and we were billed for both subscriptions this morning. Please help close the old account properly and stop the extra billing.

I found the old marketing workspace still in active status because the subscription was never canceled after the user migration. I’ve now scheduled it for immediate cancellation and disabled auto-renew. Since today’s charge had already posted, I submitted a refund request for that duplicate subscription. You’ll keep access to historical data in read-only mode for 30 days from the old workspace login.

subject: Impossible de renommer des équipes et export CSV incomplet
description: J’essaie de réorganiser notre compte en fusionnant deux équipes et en renommant plusieurs groupes, mais les modifications ne sont pas prises en compte. Par ailleurs, lorsque je génère un export CSV depuis les rapports de performance, il manque la colonne des propriétaires de dossier alors qu’elle apparaît bien à l’écran. Nous sommes en pleine revue trimestrielle, donc c’est bloquant.

comments.message: Bonjour, quand je change le nom de nos équipes dans les paramètres du compte, le nouveau nom apparaît pendant quelques secondes puis revient à l’ancien. En parallèle, l’export CSV des rapports de performance est incomplet : la colonne "propriétaire" n’y figure pas.

Bonjour, j’ai regardé rapidement et le renommage d’équipe est probablement lié au cache du navigateur. Je vous invite à vider le cache et à réessayer. Pour le CSV, l’export ne contient pas forcément toutes les colonnes visibles dans le rapport, c’est un fonctionnement normal.
J’ai testé sur deux navigateurs et avec un autre administrateur, même résultat. Et nous avons déjà eu cette colonne dans les exports le mois dernier.

Merci. Après vérification plus poussée, le renommage échoue parce que vos groupes sont synchronisés depuis votre annuaire SSO, donc les noms sont réécrits automatiquement. Il faut modifier la source côté synchronisation SCIM ou désactiver la synchro pour ces groupes. Pour le reporting, vous avez raison : la colonne propriétaire a été retirée par erreur d’un modèle d’export sur certains comptes. J’ai ouvert un bug produit et, en attendant, vous pouvez récupérer cette donnée via le rapport "Dossiers détaillés" plutôt que "Performance équipe".

subject: CSV import mapped dates correctly, but dashboard shows zero renewals
description: I imported a CSV of renewal opportunities this morning and all 438 rows appear in the table with the right close dates and amounts. The issue is that our renewals dashboard still shows zero for this month, even though the underlying records are there. I suspect something is wrong with the date field or how the report filters are reading the imported data. Can you help me figure out why the data exists but the reporting widget is blank?

comments.message: The imported opportunities are visible in the dataset, but the renewals dashboard says 0 for June. We need this fixed before our customer success review.
Usually that means the import didn't work. Please try re-uploading the CSV and make sure the dates are in YYYY-MM-DD format.
The import did work — the rows are there, and the dates are already in YYYY-MM-DD. The report filter uses the 'Renewal Date' field. Could that field type be the problem?
If the rows are visible, reporting should pick them up automatically. There isn't anything else to check on our side, so a re-import is still the best option.

subject: Approval workflow skips the manager step for contractors
description: I'm trying to set up an onboarding workflow so that every new hire gets an approval from their department manager before the account is created. Full-time employees go through the approval step correctly, but contractors are skipping straight to account creation. I configured a branch based on employment type and I can't tell if I misconfigured the condition or if the workflow engine is ignoring that field.

comments.message: For contractors, the workflow jumps from "Collect details" straight to "Create account". The "Manager approval" step is only firing for employees marked full-time. We use the custom field Employment Type with values Full-Time, Part-Time, and Contractor.

This usually happens when the branch condition is checking the display label instead of the stored value. In Product Configuration, please open the Employment Type field and confirm whether Contractor is stored as `contractor` or something else. Then edit the workflow branch so the condition matches the stored value exactly. Also make sure the approval step is connected inside the contractor branch, not only in the default path.

You were right that the stored value is `vendor`, not `contractor`. I updated the condition and now the approval fires. But existing contractor records created before today are still bypassing it when edited.

That part is expected if your automation trigger is set to run only on record creation. Change the workflow trigger to "on create and when Employment Type changes," or create a second workflow for existing records entering the contractor state. After publishing, test with an older record by changing Employment Type away and back to `vendor`. That should make the manager approval step run for those records too.

subject: Anexos baixados pela API sem vínculo confiável com os registros
description: Estamos extraindo anexos pela API para um repositório central, mas o endpoint de arquivos nem sempre traz de forma clara a qual ticket ou empresa cada arquivo pertence. Em alguns casos só temos o file_id e o nome do arquivo, o que dificulta a governança e a retenção correta dos dados. Precisamos montar um inventário consistente e entender qual relacionamento deve ser usado para mapear os anexos ao objeto de origem.

comments.message: Nossa auditoria pediu rastreabilidade completa de anexos para titularidade e retenção. Sem esse vínculo, o processo fica incompleto.

Olá! Normalmente o relacionamento vem pelo endpoint do objeto de origem, não pelo endpoint de arquivos. A prática recomendada é listar os anexos a partir de tickets, comentários ou registros pai, e não tratar o arquivo como entidade principal para reconciliação.
Mas já temos milhares de arquivos históricos baixados apenas pelo endpoint de arquivos. Como reconstruímos o vínculo agora?

Para o histórico, você pode cruzar pelo nome do arquivo e pela data de criação com os comentários e registros que contenham anexos no mesmo período. Não é perfeito, mas costuma resolver a maioria dos casos.

subject: Users can't access imported records after SSO login change
description: I updated our team's SSO settings yesterday and since then a few users can log in, but they either land on a blank dashboard or can't see the customer records we imported last week. Admins still see everything. I need help figuring out whether this is a permissions issue, a bad sync, or something broken in the import.

comments.message: Three sales reps can sign in through Okta, but after login the Customers page is empty and one of them gets stuck on a loading spinner. These records were imported via CSV on Friday and are visible to me as an admin.

Thanks for the details. This can happen when new SSO users are mapped to a default role without dataset access. Please check whether those reps were assigned to the correct team and workspace after the SAML change. Also ask one affected user to open the browser console and confirm whether the spinner appears only in Chrome or in an incognito window.

You were right about the role mapping. The SSO update created them as basic users in the wrong workspace. I fixed two accounts, but one rep still gets the spinner in Chrome and can't open imported customer records.

That suggests two issues: access mapping and a local client problem. Since the records now appear for the corrected users, the data import is intact. For the remaining rep, have them clear cached site data for our app or try incognito; we've seen stale workspace tokens cause endless loading after role changes. If that doesn't work, send me their user ID and I'll escalate the session logs review.

subject: User group sync promoted members to admins
description: After we changed our directory groups this week, several regular users were promoted to admin inside the platform. I’ve already removed the extra admins manually, but I’m not confident the next sync won’t add them back. We need help identifying which group mapping is causing this and whether you can disable that mapping temporarily.

comments.message: Several standard users were unexpectedly promoted to admin after our latest group sync. I removed the permissions manually, but I need to know why this happened and stop it from happening again.

Admin roles can be assigned through group-based role mapping if a synced directory group is tied to an admin permission set. Please send the names of the affected users, the groups they belong to, and whether you use Okta, Azure AD, or another provider. I’m escalating this to our directory integration team because they’ll need to inspect your mapping configuration and recent sync events.
We use Azure AD. The affected users are in a new Operations Managers group that was copied from an old group, and I suspect the old one used to map to admin.

That’s helpful. I’ve passed that context to the escalation and noted the likely inherited mapping from the copied group. While the integration team reviews it, please remove the app assignment from the Operations Managers group or change the mapping on your identity provider side if you can do so safely. We can’t disable the mapping from frontline support, but the escalated team can verify which group granted admin and advise whether a temporary suspension of sync or mapping change is appropriate.

subject: Email update says successful but login still tied to old address
description: I updated my login email in account settings last week because our company changed domains. The app showed a success message, but I still only receive login codes at the old address and the Users page still lists the old email for my profile. I’m trying to avoid getting locked out once the old mailbox is shut off.

comments.message: I updated my login email in account settings last week because our company changed domains. The app showed a success message, but I still only receive login codes at the old address and the Users page still lists the old email for my profile. I’m trying to avoid getting locked out once the old mailbox is shut off.
If the old email still appears, it sounds like the change may not have fully propagated. Please try changing it once more from Settings > Account and then sign out of all sessions.
I already did that twice. The banner says updated, but after refresh it reverts. Also, our audit log shows "email_changed" events for the new domain, which is confusing.

In that case it should be fine, and the UI is probably just delayed. Once propagation completes, the login codes will start going to the new address automatically. Please wait 24 hours and check again.

subject: Accès bloqué après migration de domaine
description: Je suis administratrice de notre compte et depuis que nous avons changé le domaine de notre entreprise hier, trois utilisateurs ne reçoivent plus les e-mails d’invitation et je ne peux pas terminer la configuration SSO. J’ai déjà vérifié les adresses et le dossier spam. Comme nous devons onboarder une nouvelle équipe demain matin, j’ai besoin d’une solution rapide.

comments.message: Bonjour, notre domaine est passé de nordexis.fr à nordexis.eu et depuis ce changement, les invitations restent en attente. Je dois aussi reconnecter le SSO mais l’écran me renvoie une erreur générique.

Bonjour, merci pour les détails. J’ai vérifié votre espace et je vois des rejets liés à la vérification du nouveau domaine. La mise à jour du domaine et du SSO nécessite l’intervention de notre équipe identité, je vais donc escalader ce ticket en priorité avec les adresses concernées et la capture de l’erreur SSO. En attendant, pouvez-vous me confirmer le nouveau domaine exact et l’heure du basculement ?
Oui, le nouveau domaine exact est nordexis.eu et le changement a été fait hier vers 16h20. Les utilisateurs bloqués sont m.lefevre@nordexis.eu, a.renaud@nordexis.eu et s.klein@nordexis.eu.

Parfait, merci. J’ai transmis ces informations à notre équipe identité avec une demande de traitement urgent compte tenu de votre onboarding demain. Vous recevrez une mise à jour dès qu’ils auront validé le domaine et relancé les invitations ; en attendant je laisse le ticket escaladé et suivi de près.

subject: Confusing login error after domain enforcement was turned on
description: I enabled domain enforcement so only users from our company domain can sign in. Since then, one legitimate employee keeps getting 'You do not have access to this workspace' even though her email ends with our approved domain and she was already an active user. I need help understanding what this setting actually checks and whether there’s a defect here.

comments.message: Her email in our HR system is maya@northbridge.com, but inside your app her profile shows maya@northbridge.co because that was her old address before the domain migration. She signs in through Microsoft using the new address.

Thanks, that detail is important. Domain enforcement checks the identity returned at login against the user record stored in the workspace. If the workspace still has her old .co address while Microsoft authenticates her as .com, access can be denied even though she is the same person. Please update her user record in the app to the current .com address or re-invite her under that address.
I updated the email, but she still got blocked on the first retry.

That can happen because the old identity mapping may persist briefly after the profile edit. Ask her to start a fresh login from an incognito window, and if it still fails after 15 minutes, remove and re-add her user under the .com address. I agree the error message is misleading in this case, so I’m logging feedback for the product team to clarify that the denial can be caused by a stored email mismatch rather than just missing workspace membership.

subject: Unexpected charges after nightly sync keeps creating duplicate accounts
description: Our ERP sync appears to create a new company record every night instead of updating the existing one when the legal name changes slightly. That has pushed us over our account volume limit, and finance is asking for a credit. I also need help understanding how to structure the import keys so records update correctly rather than multiplying.

comments.message: Examples are 'Acme Ltd' and 'ACME Limited' ending up as separate accounts with the same tax ID. We have thousands now. Can billing reverse the overage since this was caused by bad matching?

I reviewed your sync logs and the connector is matching on company name, not tax ID or ERP account ID. That's why each naming variation is being inserted as a new record and counted toward your account volume. To stop growth, change the primary match field to a stable external ID and run a deduplication merge based on tax ID. I can credit one week of overage as a courtesy while you clean up the duplicates.
Can you outline the cleanup steps? We don't want to lose child contacts or opportunity history when merging.

Use the account export first to identify duplicate clusters by tax ID, choose a surviving master record, and run the merge tool with 're-parent associations' enabled so contacts, deals, notes, and files move to the master. Do this in batches because merges are irreversible. I've also sent our connector guide showing how to map ERP account ID as the upsert key, which will prevent future duplicate creation and unnecessary billing.

subject: Past-due notice sent after autopay succeeded
description: Our customer portal shows that autopay succeeded on Monday, and the card statement also shows the charge. Even so, we received two past-due emails and a warning inside the app that features would be limited. When I open the invoice, the timeline lists both Payment received and Payment failed within the same minute. Please look into whether a billing event bug triggered the collections notices incorrectly.

comments.message: Our customer portal shows that autopay succeeded on Monday, and the card statement also shows the charge. Even so, we received two past-due emails and a warning inside the app that features would be limited. When I open the invoice, the timeline lists both Payment received and Payment failed within the same minute. Please look into whether a billing event bug triggered the collections notices incorrectly.
That usually happens when the bank declines a payment and then retries later. If the charge is on your statement, the warning should go away automatically. Please wait 24 hours.
It has already been more than 48 hours, and the warning is still there. Our finance team wants confirmation that this won’t affect service.

I’m sorry about that. Looking deeper, I do see a duplicated webhook sequence that marked the invoice both failed and paid, which incorrectly triggered the dunning notice. I can’t suppress the collections state from this interface, so I’ve escalated this to our payments platform team for manual correction. Your service is not scheduled for suspension while the ticket is open, but the warning may remain until they clear the bad event state.

subject: Unable to save custom ticket form with conditional fields
description: I am trying to configure a custom intake form for our support portal. The form has a dropdown for issue type and conditional fields that should appear based on the selection. The draft saves until I add the third condition, then I get a generic error and nothing publishes.

comments.message: I can create the form and add one or two conditional rules, but as soon as I add a third rule tied to the same dropdown, the Save button spins and then shows 'Something went wrong.' I already tried another browser.

Conditional fields can fail to save if one of the dependent fields is marked required while also hidden for other branches. Please review each conditional field and make sure only fields shown in all paths are globally required. Also check that none of the option labels in your dropdown are duplicated, even with different capitalization.
You were right about a hidden required field. I changed that, and now the error message is different: it says one rule conflicts with another. I don't understand which one.

When multiple rules use the same source dropdown, each value can only belong to one visibility condition per target field. The quickest fix is to remove all conditions from the affected field and rebuild them one at a time. Unfortunately I can't see the conflicting rule from my side without screenshots, but the issue is with the form configuration rather than publishing.

subject: Renamed user can’t log in with updated email
description: I changed one of our employees to a new corporate email after a domain migration, but now she can’t sign in. The old email no longer works, and password reset links sent to the new address don’t arrive.
